PN CN210403912-U
TI Carbon dioxide recycling-based household PEMFC combined heat and power system, has water vapor reforming device provided with natural gas desulfurizing device, and selective oxidation reaction device and PEMFC fuel cell provided with separating device.
AU YU P
   WEI T
   SUN B
AE SHANDONG MINGYU NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202037129A
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a heat-electricity cogeneration technology field, especially relates to a CO2-based recovery of household PEMFC the combined heat and power system. comprises a natural gas desulfurization device are connected with the natural gas storage device, a steam reforming device for natural gas reforming hydrogen by the water-gas shift device for removing CO and hydrogen production, selective oxidation reaction device for further removing and PEMFC fuel battery of the CO selective oxidation reaction device and the PEMFC, the fuel battery is further provided with a CO2 separating device. the system is not only suitable for household, and can prevent CO2 of the PEMFC fuel cell damage, and it will not pollute the environment.
DC X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC X16-C01C; X16-K03

PT P
PN CN111004639-A
TI Producing coke by dry distillation treating of raw coke coal, anthracite coal, coke powder, asphalt and petroleum coke and comprises mixing raw materials evenly and carrying out die-casting treatment to press into briquette.
AU CHEN J
   LIN X
AE XUZHOU DONGXING ENERGY CO LTD (XUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202032292X
AB    NOVELTY - Producing coke by dry distillation treating of raw coke coal, anthracite coal, coke powder, asphalt and petroleum coke, is claimed.
   USE - The method is useful for producing coke.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has uniform specifications, large block size, reaching 120-130 mm, and high strength, drop strength not greater than 95%; reduces the damage rate of coke during transportation and reduces the loss of coke; has low porosity. The coke: is not easily oxidized when placed in the air; has small reaction capacity, low reactivity, and high reaction heat intensity; does not generates carbon monoxide due to carbon dioxide in the furnace; and reduces the wide temperature range and high temperature zone formed in the smelting furnace.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The raw material is 10-20 pts. wt., preferably 15 pts. wt. raw coke coal, 50-80 pts. wt., preferably 60 pts. wt. anthracite, 4-10 pts. wt., preferably 8 pts. wt. coke powder, 5-12 pts. wt., preferably 9 pts. wt. asphalt and 5-10 pts. wt., preferably 7 pts. wt. petroleum coke. Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (1) providing above raw materials, (2) mixing the raw materials evenly and carrying out die-casting treatment to press into briquette and (3) heating briquette, dry distillating and carbonization treating to obtain the product. In the step (3), during the carbonization process of the retorting process, the briquette is heated by program, where the program heating comprises (3-1) heating the briquette to 350-400 degrees C at a heating rate of 8-12 degrees C/minute for 8-10 hours, (3-2) heating up the temperature to increase the briquette to 400-550 degrees C at a heating rate of 5-10 degrees C/minute for 8-10 hours, and (3-3) heating up the temperature will raise the briquette to 550-1050 degrees C at a heating rate of 3-5 degrees C/minute for 6-8 hours. In the step (1), passing steam into the mixed raw materials and heating the raw materials under stirring, performing the original heat treatment to prepare the blended coal mixture, and performing die casting treatment. Preferred Components: In the step (1), the particle size of the: raw coking coal is 40-60 mesh; anthracite coal is 50-80 mesh; coke powder is 100-150 mesh; asphalt is 20-40 mesh; and coke is 60-80 mesh. In the step (2), the relative pressure during the die casting process is 20-30 MPa.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)
MC H09-A
IP C10B-055/02; C10B-057/16

PT P
PN CN110983529-A
TI Manufacturing degradable polybutylene adipate            terephthalate-bulked continuous filament yarn comprises            slicing process, screw process, spinning process, side            blowing process, oil drawing process, expanded network            process and winding process.
AU GAO J
   SU F
   SHAN J
AE SHANDONG LIYUAN FIBER CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020316587
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing degradable polybutylene adipate                terephthalate-bulked continuous filament yarn                comprises slicing process, screw process, spinning                process, side blowing process, oil drawing process,                expanded network process and winding process, where                the slicing step includes adding the crystallized                PBTA slice, and blowing at air 15 plus minus 2                degrees C, the oiling drawing process includes                controlling the pre-drawing roller temperature at                70 degrees C, the main drawing roller temperature                at 90 degrees C, the draft ratio is 3.5, the                deformer temperature in the expansion network                process is 130 plus minus 1 degrees C, optimizing                spinnability and adding 2% stable oil.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing                degradable polybutylene adipate                terephthalate-bulked continuous filament                yarn.
   ADVANTAGE - The product is biodegradable within 180 days,                and generates carbon dioxide and water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  maintaining crystallization temperature of PBTA                  slice after crystallization at 110 degrees C. In                  the screw process, the screw temperature is 170                  degrees C in zone 1, 210 degrees C in zone 2, 220                  degrees C in zone 3, 223 degrees C in zone 4, 224                  degrees C in zone 5, 225 degrees C in flange                  zone, and the temperature of the box is                  maintained at 222 degrees C. In the winding                  process, the winding machine is wound into drum                  to obtain final product. The method comprises                  providing protective layer on the outside of the                  PLA-BCF yarn body. Preferred Components: The                  stable oil agent is 1-2 parts per thousand ethoxy                  ester and 1-2 parts per thousand anionic                  antistatic agent. The polybutylene adipate                  terephthalate-bulked continuous filament yarn                  yarn body is provided, and woven from plurality                  of monofilaments. The monofilaments are provided                  with substrate, the substrate is polybutylene                  adipate terephthalate, and the monofilament cut                  surface structure is trilobal type graphics, and                  multiple monolithic nanoparticles of catalytic                  masterbatch are uniformly distributed throughout                  the monofilament. The polybutylene adipate                  terephthalate-bulked continuous filament yarn                  body is cross-woven with four monofilaments. The                  monofilament cross-sectional structure is a                  triangular pattern, and multiple catalytic mother                  nanoparticles are uniformly distributed in the                  monofilament body. The structure of the                  monofilament cut surface is hollow irregular                  pattern, and multiple catalytic mother                  nanoparticles are evenly distributed in the                  monofilament body.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).); F02 (Yarns - mechanical finishing of yarns or ropes; warping or beaming (D02, D07).)

PT P
PN CN110975886-A; CN110975886-B
TI Preparation of porous two-dimensional cadmium-zinc            sulfide nanosheets for, e.g. water decomposition,            involves solvothermally reacting zinc            sulfide-ethylenediamine precursor, cadmium salt, and            organic solvent, and hydrothermally reacting.
AU LIU E
   XUE W
   FAN J
   HU X
   BAI X
   MIAO H
   ZHANG D
AE UNIV NORTHWEST (UYXB-C)
GA 202030475L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of porous two-dimensional                cadmium-zinc sulfide nanosheets, involves mixing a                zinc salt, ethylenediamine aqueous solution, and a                sulfur source, solvothermally reacting at 140-180                degrees C for 6-24 hours to obtain a zinc                sulfide-ethylenediamine precursor, mixing the                precursor, a cadmium salt, and an organic solvent,                solvothermally reacting at 140-180 degrees C for                0.5-16 hours to obtain a cadmium-zinc                sulfide-ethylenediamine precursor, and                hydrothermally reacting the precursor with water at                120-160 degrees C for 3-6 hours.
   USE - Preparation of porous two-dimensional                cadmium-zinc sulfide nanosheets used as                photocatalyst for decomposition of water to produce                hydrogen, carbon dioxide reduction, and oxidation                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides nanosheets having uniform                surface pore distribution, large number of reactive                sites, and stable performance                characteristics.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zinc salt is zinc                  chloride, zinc acetate, and/or zinc nitrate. The                  cadmium salt is cadmium chloride and/or cadmium                  nitrate. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio                  of zinc salt and sulfur source is 1:1-6. The                  molar ratio of cadmium salt and zinc                  sulfide-ethylenediamine precursor is                  1:0.5-20.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sulfur source is                  thiourea and/or thioacetamide.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN110896995-A; CN110896995-B
TI Prolonging storage period of Asparagus used for            preserving fruits and vegetables, involves performing            ultrasonic and preservative treatment on Asparagus and            carrying out modified atmosphere packaging.
AU FAN L
   WANG J
   LI J
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
GA 2020242881
AB    NOVELTY - Method of prolonging storage period of                Asparagus involves performing ultrasonic and                preservative treatment on Asparagus and carrying                out modified atmosphere packaging, where the gas                composition in the modified atmosphere packaging is                5-15% oxygen and 5-15% carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving fruits and                vegetables enhancing nutritional quality and                providing anti-mold soft rot, anti-yellowing and                anti-lignification effect (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method suppresses the decay of Asparagus                and total number of surface colonies, inhibits the                respiration of Asparagus, retains soluble solids,                maintains the quality of Asparagus and extends the                storage period to 40 days, is reasonable,                economical and suitable for industrial                production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for improving nutritional quality of Asparagus                involving subjecting the Asparagus to ultrasonic                treatment and 1-methylcyclopropene fumigation and                carrying out modified atmosphere packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves                  ultrasonically treating for 5-15 minutes at                  100-360 W. Preferred Components: The preservative                  comprises composite preservative of acetic acid                  and gibberellin or 1-methylcyclopropene                  preservative. Preferred Composition: The                  concentration of acetic acid in the composite                  preservative of acetic acid and gibberellins is                  10-30 ml/l and gibberellin is 25-50 mg/l. The                  concentration of 1-methylcyclopropene is 0.1-1 g                  per 5 kg Asparagus and addition amount of                  1-methylcyclopropene is 0.002-0.02 %mass.                  Preferred Method: The method involves (i)                  selecting superior Asparagus with stem length of                  25 cm, diameter of 16-18 mm, and with no bending,                  mechanical damage, scratches and decay, (ii)                  immersing the Asparagus obtained in step (i) in a                  ultrasonic generator containing water,                  ultrasonically treating at 100-360 W for 5-15                  minutes, removing and draining, (iii) immersing                  the Asparagus treated in step (ii) in a                  preservative or soaking for 5-15 minutes, taking                  out and draining quickly, (iv) subjecting the                  Asparagus obtained in step (iii) to modified                  atmosphere packaging using modified atmosphere                  packaging machine, where the gas composition in                  the modified atmosphere packaging contains 5-15%                  oxygen, 5-15% carbon dioxide and 80-100%                  nitrogen, and (v) refrigerating the Asparagus                  packaged under modified atmosphere at 2-5 degrees                  C and relative humidity of 80-90%. Preferred                  Property: The method involves using low density                  polyethylene antibacterial preservative bag for                  packaging with the thickness of 2-10 mm.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PT P
PN CN110841595-A
TI Preparing nitrogen-doped graphite carbon material, comprises e.g. mixing tannic acid, urea and zinc chloride in water as solvent, then placing obtained suspension in oven for certain time, and calcining under nitrogen gas atmosphere.
AU ZHOU Q
   ZHU X
   GU Z
   LIANG W
   ZHOU Z
   ZHU M
AE ELECTRIC POWER RES INST STATE GRID JIANG (SGCC-C)
   NANJING INST TECHNOLOGY (NANJ-C)
GA 202020118C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nitrogen-doped graphite carbon material, comprises mixing tannic acid as carbon source, urea as nitrogen source and zinc chloride as activator in water as solvent to obtain suspension, and then placing the suspension in oven for certain period of time, and calcining under nitrogen gas atmosphere to obtain final product.
   USE - The material is useful as absorbent for absorbing gases including carbon dioxide and organic matter including dyes (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon material: has rich pore structure and active sites for surface absorption; and utilizes environmentally friendly tannic acid and urea as raw materials. The method: has simple process and low cost, and requires low temperature and simple equipment; and is easy to carry out.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) dissolving urea and tannic acid in a mass ratio of 0.5-2.5 in 20 ml water containing 16-18 g zinc chloride to obtain suspension, and magnetically stirring for 20-50 minutes, (ii) transferring the suspension into oven and reacting at 110 degrees C for certain time to obtain black solid mixture, then heating the black solid mixture at a heating rate of 5 degrees C/minute to 700 degrees C under high-purity nitrogen atmosphere, and then calcining at a constant temperature for 1 hour, and (iii) activating the product prepared in step (ii) with 3 mol/l nitric acid, then washing with deionized water, and drying to obtain nitrogen-doped graphite carbon material. In step (ii), the reaction time is 16-24 hours. In step (iii), the prepared product is washed with deionized water to a neutral pH, and dried at 80 degrees C in oven. In step (i), urea and tannic acid in a mass ratio of 0.5-1 and 18 g zinc chloride are dissolved in 20 ml water to obtain suspension, and then the suspension is magnetically stirred for 30 minutes.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC D04-A01; D04-A01F3; D04-A01P1; D04-B06; D04-B06B; D04-D; E05-U06; E11-A01; E11-A02; E11-F07A; E11-Q02B; E11-W; E21-B05; E25-E01; E25-E02; J01-D01; J01-E02B3; J01-E03C3; J04-X

PN CN110698730-A
TI Preparation of easily degradable foam packaging material involves heating mixture containing starch, sodium benzoate, dicumyl peroxide, fatty acid glyceride, and isopentane, extruding, pressing extruded sheet-shaped foam blank and cooling.
AU ZHANG J
   WANG J
AE ZHANGZHOU BAOLIANG IND & TRADE CO LTD (ZHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202008633M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of easily degradable foam packaging material involves mixing starch, polypropylene, p-toluenesulfonylurea, sodium benzoate, 2,5-dimethyl-2,5-di-tert-butylperoxane or dicumyl peroxide, fatty acid glyceride, and isopentane in mixer uniformly, stirring, feeding mixture into screw extruder with T-shaped head and injection port on barrel, heating mixture in barrel at different stages, extruding, injecting isopentane or carbon dioxide from injection port, conveying extruded sheet-shaped foam blank to conveyor with several rollers and roll pressing, cooling shaped foam to obtain sheet-shaped foam product, feeding sheet-shaped foam product into machine to make packaging products.
   USE - Preparation of easily degradable foam packaging material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides easily degradable foam packaging material using biodegradable starch, which solves problems of slow and difficult degradation of polypropylene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The process involves using sodium benzoate as nucleating agent, 2,5-dimethyl-2,5-di-tert-butylperoxyhexane as crosslinking agent, fatty acid glyceride as surface active and coupling agent and two blowing agents to increase the viscosity and strength of molten mixture, so that molten mixture is easy to foam. The p-toluenesulfonylurea is chemical blowing agent, and isopentane or carbon dioxide is physical blowing agent. The biodegradable and naturally differentiable components account for about 50% of total components, and when the biodegradable and naturally differentiable components are degraded and differentiated, the polypropylene in material can be broken into powder.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The starch can be biodegraded, and polypropylene can be quickly degraded by light, heat and oxygen. Due to use of starch and polypropylene, the packaging material has functions of biodegradation and rapid light, heat and oxygen degradation. The sheet-shaped foam packaging material is laminated and bonded to required thickness and cutting according to required packaging shape to make packaging product.

GA 202005459R

The pyrolysis carbonization temperature is controlled between 500-700 degrees C and the pyrolysis carbonization time is controlled to 30-45 minutes. The activation process uses a physical activation method between 750-900 degrees C with carbon dioxide and water vapor as the activation medium, and the activation time is controlled within 2-3 hours.

PN CN110467943-A; CN110467943-B
TI Natural gas production method using coal, olefine and coal tar involves utilizing oxygen-enriched steam from pressurized and gasified raw coal, feeding raw coal into pressurized gasification bed, and heating and drying.
AU LI N
   LI K
AE CHENGDU JUSHI ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOGY (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201999017B
AB    NOVELTY - A natural gas production method using coal, olefine and coal tar comprises utilizing oxygen-enriched steam from pressurized and gasified raw coal, oxygen-enriched water gas washing and coal tar separation, oxygen-rich gas acid gas removal, methanol synthesis, methanol separation, methanol exhaust gas methane separation, and olefin production methanol dehydration; feeding raw coal into pressurized gasification bed, heating and drying using hydrocarbon-rich hydrogen gas, removing water, carrying out pyrolysis and dry distillation, obtaining hydrocarbon-rich hydrogen gas, gasifying, obtaining methane-rich hydrogen gas, and oxidizing and combusting residual carbon.
   USE - Natural gas production method using coal, olefine and coal tar.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is low-cost, environment-friendly, has improved energy converting efficiency, reduced water consumption, and has reduced carbon dioxide emission.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Methods: The natural gas production method using coal, olefine and coal tar comprises feeding hydrocarbon-rich hydrogen gas into hydrogen distributor, heating, moving into coke hydrogenation layer, reacting to generate methane-containing hydrogen gas, heating, generating high temperature methane hydrogen gas, feeding into semi-coke hydrogenation layer, performing rapid gasification reaction, feeding high-temperature methane-rich hydrogen gas into pyrolysis carbonization layer, generating hydrocarbon-rich hydrogen gas, moving into raw coal drying layer, drying, converting adsorbed water and chemical water into steam, and discharging, removing tar dust, cooling, separating oil and water, performing acid-hydrogen separation process, moving natural gas into methane anaerobic olefin/aromatics plant, generating olefins/aromatics, separating hydrogen, boosting into hydrogen cycle machine, re-entering into the furnace to produce hydrocarbon-rich hydrogen gas, feeding gasifying agent comprising oxygen and water vapor, heating at 600 degrees C, gasifying, generating carbon dioxide, releasing heat, transferring into hydrogenated residual coke, performing endothermic water vapor gasification reaction, converting carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas in crude gas at 700 degrees C, removing raw coal gas, feeding heat into waste heat steam boiler and into water gas scrubbing waste heat recovery device, gasifying, carrying out carbon monoxide shift reaction with catalyst, obtaining transformed gas as shift gas, performing pressure swing adsorption separation process, feeding desorbed gas into gas turbine exhaust gas boiler or low-calorific value gas boiler, converting combustible components into steam energy for recycling, and utilizing 30-80% oxygen as gasifier stripping medium. The pressurized coal gasification bed process is carried out under 1-3 MPa, 3-6 MPa and/or 6-10 MPa, and outlet pressure of hydrocarbon-rich hydrogen gas of methane anaerobic to olefin production is 0.95-0.6 times, 0.6-0.3 times, 0.3-0.1 times and/or 0.1-0.05 times.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

   WANG G

GA 201996273A
AB    NOVELTY - Pure physical preserving method of fruits and vegetables comprises using natural fruit and vegetable with 90% maturation degree as raw material, classifying fruit and vegetable by size, cleaning, drying, and removing inappropriate portion; subjecting to cold and wet air convection processing and dehydrating; standing for 1-3 hours; performing non-adhesive independent quick-freezing; and freezing at lower than degrees C-18 and storing.

PN CN110269088-A
TI Preserving environmentally-friendly healthy roasted chicken, comprises e.g. cooling the roasted chicken to room temperature, spraying cooled roasted chicken with grape seed extract preservative, vacuumizing and packaging.
AU HUANG M
   GUO Y
   HUANG J
   ZHOU X
   YANG J
   LI X
AE NANJING HUANGJIAOSHOU FOOD TECHNOLOGY (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 201982618U
AB    NOVELTY - Preserving environmentally-friendly healthy roasted chicken, comprises (a) cooling the roasted chicken to room temperature; (b) spraying the cooled roasted chicken obtained in the step (a) with the grape seed extract preservative, and then placing into a modified atmosphere packaging box; (c) vacuumizing the modified atmosphere packaging box of the roasted chicken obtained in the step (b) by a modified atmosphere packaging machine and then filling with a mixed gas of carbon-dioxide and nitrogen; and (d) packaging the roasted chicken under air conditioning atmosphere and preserving at a low temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving environmentally-friendly healthy roasted chicken.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not require synthetic food additives, and the shelf life of roasted chicken can reach more than 21 days, and produces product having color protection, conformal shape, taste preservation, anti-fat oxidation and natural healthcare effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: In the step (b), 2 ml grape seed extract preservative is sprayed per 300-350 g of cooled roasted chicken. The grape seed extract preservative is prepared by adding grape seed extract powder to sterile water at a ratio of 0.5%. The grape seed extract powder has proanthocyanidin content of greater than or equal to 95%. In the step (b), the mixed gas of carbon-dioxide and nitrogen, carbon-dioxide content accounts for 40%, nitrogen content accounts for 60% and the ratio of the mixed gas of carbon-dioxide and nitrogen to the volume of roasted chicken is 2.54. Preferred Method: In the step (c), the temperature of the low-temperature freezing is 4 plus minus 1 degrees C.

PN WO2019143878-A1; US2021061732-A1; US11208366-B2; US2022073439-A1
TI Converting a base gas to a first gas, comprises exposing the base gas having alkane to an iridium(IV) oxide-based catalyst and forming the first gas comprising an alkene, an alkyne, an alcohol and/or an aldehyde.
AU WEAVER J F
   BIAN Y
AE UNIV FLORIDA RES FOUND (UYFL-C)
   UNIV FLORIDA RES FOUND INC (UYFL-C)
GA 201964669B
AB    NOVELTY - Converting a base gas to a first gas, comprises exposing the base gas to an iridium(IV) oxide-based catalyst and forming the first gas, where the base gas is an alkane and the first gas comprises an alkene, an alkyne, an alcohol and/or an aldehyde.
   USE - The method is useful for converting base gas to first gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides efficiently and selectively convert ethane to ethylene at lower temperature would have significant economic and environmental benefits; and utilizes the iridium(IV) oxide(110) that promotes ethane dehydrogenation to ethylene just above room temperature and exhibits controlled prehydrogenation of the iridium(IV) oxide(110) surface that is an effective approach for increasing selectivity toward ethane conversion to ethylene rather than carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The iridium(IV) oxide-based catalyst comprises iridium(IV) oxide(110). The iridium(IV) oxide-based catalyst comprises iridium metal oxides (IrxMyOz) (I) or iridium halo oxides (Irx1Xy1Oz1). The iridium(IV) oxide-based catalyst comprises iridium(IV) oxide deposited onto a support. The support comprises an oxide support comprising silica, alumina, titanium dioxide, magnesium oxide, calcium oxide, cerium oxide and/or zeolites. The support is a non-oxide support.M = Ru, Ti, Re, Nb, Ta, Os, Pt, Pd, Cu, Ag, Au, Rh, Cr, Mn, Ni, Fe and/or Co;z = 1-2;x+y = greater than or equal to 1;X = halo, S, Se and/or Te;x1 = greater than or equal to 1; andy+z = 1-2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Exposing comprises exposing the alkane to the iridium(IV) oxide-based catalyst at 200-400 degrees K. The method further comprises prehydrogenation of the iridium(IV) oxide-based catalyst prior to exposing the alkane to the iridium(IV) oxide-based catalyst. Preferred Components: The alkane comprises 1-5C alkane. The first gas comprises 1-5C alkene, 1-5C alkyne, 1-5C alcohol and/or 1-5C aldehyde. The base gas is ethane or methane and the first gas is ethylene, methanol and/or formaldehyde.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Forest unattended monitoring device utilizing method, involves comparing data transmitted by conversion module with environmental standard index by identification and comparison module, obtaining monitoring result, and issuing alarm.
AU XU S
AE NANJING ZHONGYUANTONG COMMUNICATION TECH (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 201954820M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving control instruction transmitted by a remote monitoring terminal through a light intensity sensor, a temperature and humidity sensor, a carbon dioxide concentration sensor and a PM2.5 detection sensor. An environmental index of a forest is collected. Index data is transmitted to the remote monitoring terminal through a wireless network. A standard index of a forest environment is set to the light intensity sensor and the temperature and humidity sensor by the remote monitoring terminal. PN CN109769925-A
TI Preserving mulberry sprouts comprises picking mulberry sprouts, pre-cooling the picked mulberry sprouts, sealing the pre-cooled mulberry sprouts in air-conditioning preservation bag and storing in refrigerator.
AU CHEN F
   CHEN Y
   WANG L
   LUO Z
   YIN J
   DAI F
   ZHONG J
AE SERICULTURE & AGRI-FOOD RES INST GAAS (SERI-Non-standard)
GA 201947082H
AB    NOVELTY - Preserving mulberry sprouts comprises (a) picking mulberry sprouts, (b) pre-cooling the picked mulberry sprouts to 4-8 degrees C, (c) sealing the pre-cooled mulberry sprouts in an air-conditioning preservation bag under the oxygen permeability of 5625-11643 cm3/m2 for 24 hours at 0.1 Mpa pressure, carbon-dioxide permeability of 2800-5821 cm3/m2 for 24 hours at 0.1 Mpa pressure and water vapor transmission rate of 17.81-62.58 g/m2 for 24 hours and (d) storing the sealed preservation bag in a refrigerator at 2 plus minus 8 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving mulberry sprouts.
   ADVANTAGE - The method comprises physical refrigeration for preservation, and does not use any chemical reagents in the whole process; and s simple and safe.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The mulberry sprouts are picked at 6-9 in the morning and spreading to dry if there are water drops on the surface. The time interval between the picking and the pretreatment of the mulberry sprouts does not exceed 4 hours, preferably not greater than one hour. The mulberry sprouts are piled at a height of 8-15 cm for pre-cooling. The preserving mulberry sprouts further comprises removing the pests and damaged mulberry sprouts after picking the mulberry sprouts and before the pre-cooling.

AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing gradient structure high durability concrete product comprises (a)mixing a part of the cementing material with a dense admixture to obtain concrete; (b) adding concrete into a mold, and then curing and demolding to obtain a concrete test piece; (c) subjecting surface of the concrete specimen to surface drying treatment, and then placing in an autoclave for carbonization curing under a carbon dioxide atmosphere and carrying out standard maintenance to obtain a highly durable concrete product with a gradient structure.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing gradient structure high durability concrete product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the corrosion resistance of the concrete material, high strength of the outer layer and deformation resistance of the test piece; and extends the service.

Preferred Method: In the step (a) the cementing material is ordinary portland cement. In the step (a) method comprises substituting a part of the cementitious material with a dense admixture, where the substitution rate is 15-30%. In the step (b) curing temperature is 20 plus minus 2 degrees C, humidity is not less than 95% and curing time is 24 hours. In the step (c) surface drying treatment condition has relative humidity of 20 plus minus 5%,temperature 50 plus minus 10 degrees C and drying treatment time of 24 hours. In the step (c) relative humidity is 70% or more, temperature is 0-30 degrees C, concentration of carbon dioxide in the autoclave is 90% or more in an autoclave, total gas pressure is 0.2-0.5 MPa and the curing time is 4-12 hours. In the step (c) relative humidity is greater than 95%, temperature is 20 plus minus 5 degrees C to 28 days old, or placed in water at 20 plus minus 5 degrees C to 28 days old.

PN CN109622043-A; CN109622043-B
TI Preparing polyimide/silver aerogel composite material used as a photo-catalyst used e.g. in photolysis of water, comprises e.g. using diamine and dianhydride as precursor, adding silver nitrate solution and crosslinking agent.
AU ZHAO X
   YI X
   TIAN S
   ZHANG J
   LIU B
   JU W
   FAN H
   LIU X
AE NEW MATERIALS INST SHANDONG ACAD SCI (UYQQ-C)
GA 2019387305
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyimide (PI)/silver (Ag) aerogel composite material comprises (i) using diamine and dianhydride as a precursor of the reaction, adding silver nitrate solution, crosslinking agent, catalyst dehydrating agent, and reducing solvent, and preparing PI/Ag wet gel by sol-gel method, and (ii) aging the wet gel obtained in the step (i) in ethanol and drying by ethanol supercritical technique.
   USE - The polyimide/silver aerogel composite material is used as a photo-catalyst used in photolysis of water, degradation of organic pollutants and antibiotics, reduction of carbon dioxide, and selective organic synthesis (all claimed), used in the device of photo-catalysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple. The aerogel material is light and flexible, and has good industrial application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for polyimide/silver aerogel composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the diamine monomer is 2, 2'-diamino diphenylamine or 4, 4'-diamino diphenyl ether, the dianhydride monomer is biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride or pyromellitic dianhydride, the crosslinking agent is 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenoxy)benzene, the catalyst is pyridine, the dehydrating agent is acetic anhydride, and the solvent is N, N-dimethylformamide, N-methylpyrrolidone or formamide. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the molar ratio of the diamine, dianhydride, silver nitrate, crosslinking agent, catalyst and dehydrating agent is 1-2:1-2:0.01-0.1:0.01-0.1:8-16:8-16. In the step (ii), the specific method of the aging process is as follows: (1) gelatinizing the PI/Ag wet gel for 0.5-2 hours (preferably 1 hours), soaking in an ethanol solution of N-methyl pyrrolidone (preferably 75% N-methylpyrrolidone) for 23-25 hours (preferably 24 hours), (2) soaking the wet aerogel obtained in the step (1) in a solution of N-methyl pyrrolidone (preferably 25% N-methylpyrrolidone) in ethanol for 23-25 hours (preferably 24 hours), and (3) aging the wet aerogel obtained in the step (2) in an ethanol solution for 23-25 hours (preferably 24 hours). In the method, the circulating operation of step (iii) is performed 3-5 times (preferably 4 times). In the method, the specific conditions of the ethanol supercritical drying method is as follows: the heating rate in the supercritical process of ethanol is 10 degrees C/minutes, heat preserving temperature is 536-553 K (preferably 536 K), the pressure is 6.0-9.0 MPa (preferably 9.0 MPa), and the heat preservation time is 1-4 hours (preferably 3 hours).

AE NANJING INST TECHNOLOGY (NANJ-C)
GA 201930925L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves de-dusting flue gas in a furnace smoke chamber based on cyclone dust removing process. Flue gas cooling operation is performed in a heat exchanger after de-dusting operation, where the heat exchanger comprises an inner pipe and an outer pipe. Cold air is utilized during cooling operation to exchange heat. Flue gas desulfurizing operation is performed after completing the flue gas cooling operation. The desulfurized flue gas is introduced into calcium hydroxide solution for performing de-carbonation process. The flue gas is re-introduced into a cupola furnace after removing the carbon dioxide for full-combustion.

PN CN109394684-A
TI Injection composition comprises phenylephrine hydrochloride, sodium chloride and edetate disodium.
AU LI X
   MIAO J
AE SHANDONG WEIFANG HAPPY PHARM LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201922818K
AB    NOVELTY - Injection composition comprises 100 g phenylephrine hydrochloride, 50-80 g sodium chloride, and 0.7-1 g edetate disodium in 10000 ml total amount.
   USE - Injection composition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the injection composition in a closed environment, which involves:
   (A) adding 90 wt.% hot water for injection in the preparation container, cooling to 30 degrees C temperature, passing carbon dioxide to saturation;
   (B) dissolving disodium edetate with a small amount of hot water for injection;
   (C) adding sodium chloride, phenylephrine hydrochloride and disodium edetate solution into the water for injection and stirring until dissolved;
   (D) adding water for injection to the total amount, stirring uniformly;
   (E) sampling the solution, determining 2.8-4.8 pH value of solution, filtering with a 0.45 microns, 0.22 microns filter membrane;
   (F) potting the solution into the potting operation procedure; and
   (G) filling inside of ampoule with nitrogen gas passing to obtain the finished product, and subjecting to high temperature sterilization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: Injection composition comprises 8-12 g sodium metabisulfite in 10000 ml total amount. Preferred Conditions: The process involves adding wt.% water for injection in the preparation container, cooling to 30 degrees C temperature, and passing carbon dioxide for 15 minutes to make it saturated, adding sodium chloride, sodium metabisulfite, phenylephrine hydrochloride, and the disodium edetate solution into the water for injection. The method is carried out in sterilization production process.

PN CN109368625-A
TI Preparation of detonation of dispersible graphene quantum dot, involves modifying oxygen-containing functional group on edge of graphite particles, and adding water or organic solvent and ultrasonically treating.
AU MIAO Z
   TANG F
   JIANG Y
AE UNIV YANCHENG TEACHERS (UAET-C)
GA 2019204308
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of detonation of dispersible graphene quantum dot, involves (1) draining inside of the explosion container, using carbon dioxide, argon or nitrogen as a shielding gas, igniting fuel column, exhausting exhaust gas after the product is settled, collecting nano-scale graphite powder on the inner wall and the bottom of the container, washing with deionized water until neutral, drying and storing in a drying dish, (2) adding graphite powder and sodium chlorate to concentrated sulfuric acid, stirring in an ice bath, modifying the oxygen-containing functional group on the edge of the graphite particles, centrifuging the reaction product, adding water or an organic solvent and ultrasonically treating.
   USE - Preparation of detonation of dispersible graphene quantum dot.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation process has easy control, simple steps, high yield, has reduced energy consumption, and reduces the generation of polluting gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The vacuum pressure in the step (1) is less than 100 Pa. The nano-sized graphite particles in the step (1) are mainly distributed at 1-20 nm. In the step (2), the stirring time is 6 hours in an ice bath. The ultrasonic treatment time in the step (2) is 0.5-12 hours. Preferred Compositions: The mass ratio of sodium chlorate to graphene in the step (2) is 2-8.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent in the step (2) includes N-methylpyrrolidone, an aldehyde, organic acid, dimethylformamide, dimethylacetamide, chlorobenzene, and/or dichlorobenzene.

TI Co-producing methanol and ammonia involves providing hydrocarbon feed stock, preparing separate hydrogen stream and separate oxygen stream by electrolysis of water, preparing separate oxygen stream and separate nitrogen stream.
AU HAN P A
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 201912283Y
AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing methanol and ammonia involves providing hydrocarbon feed stock, preparing separate hydrogen stream and separate oxygen stream by electrolysis of water, preparing separate oxygen stream and separate nitrogen stream by separation of air, and introducing portion of separate oxygen stream and unit of separate oxygen into autothermal reformer. The hydrocarbon feed stock to methanol synthesis gas which comprises hydrogen and carbon oxides. The methanol synthesis gas is converted to raw methanol in methanol synthesis stage. The portion of separate hydrogen stream and the separate nitrogen stream are introduced into ammonia synthesis loop and nitrogen and hydrogen stream is converted to ammonia.
   USE - Method for co-producing methanol and ammonia.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to co-producing methanol and ammonia that reduces carbon dioxide emission.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The module (M is equal to subtraction of hydrogen stream and carbon dioxide stream divide by addition of carbon monoxide stream and carbon dioxide stream) of methanol synthesis gas is adjusted to a value of 1.9-2.2 by adding portion of the separate hydrogen stream into the methanol synthesis gas. The separate hydrogen stream and the separate nitrogen stream are introduced into the ammonia synthesis loop in amounts to provide molar ratio of the hydrogen to the nitrogen of 2.7- 3.3. The electrolysis of water and/or the separation of air is powered by renewable energy.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

N'-hexadecyl-N,N-dimethylethyl hydrazine, 200-300 pts. wt. water, 14-16 pts. wt. enzymolysis gelatin dispersion, 3-5 pts. wt. glycoprotein and 2-4 pts. wt. preservative. The preparation method of leveling agent involves (a) weighing the above mentioned raw materials, (b) first mixing the polyimide resin and the diluents to obtain polyimide diluent, and (c) adding polyallylamine, N'-hexadecyl-N,N-dimethylethyl hydrazine, enzymolysis gelatin dispersion, glycoprotein and preservative sequentially in water, stirring and mixing, adding polyimide diluent, introducing carbon dioxide gas under stirring, discharging and filling to obtain the product.

   ADVANTAGE - The agent has excellent dyeing effect and color fastness.

Preferred Method: The enzymatic gelatin dispersion is prepared by mixing and stirring gelatin and deionized water having an isoelectric point of 6 in a mass ratio of (1:8)-(1:15), leaving and swelling, adding 1-5 %mass endopeptidase in gelatin mixture, stirring at constant temperature, carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis to the mixture and heating to inactivate enzyme.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyimide resin is semi-aromatic polyimide or aromatic polyimide.

   HUANG R
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201895524W
AB    NOVELTY - A coagulating agent is prepared by milling phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier to obtain raw material, calcining into calcining kiln to obtain clinker, dissolving in water, solid-liquid separating, floating precipitate, separating sulfide, calcining separated sulfide, catalytic reacting flue gas generated by calcination by vanadium pentoxide, absorbing using concentrated sulfuric acid to obtain sulfuric acid, adding carbon dioxide to liquid in solid-liquid separation, precipitating, drying, crushing to obtain aluminum hydroxide, heating and mixing aluminum hydroxide and sodium hydroxide solution, cooling to room temperature, adding citric acid solution, stirring to obtain mixed solution, and dripping hydrochloric acid solution to transparent colorless.

The mass ratio of aluminum hydroxide and citric acid solution is 1:0.3-0.4. The pH value of hydrochloric acid solution is 2-3. Preferred Method: Calcining is done at 1000-1200 degrees C for 2-5 hours. Drying is done at 100-120 degrees C. Heating and mixing is done at 70-90 degrees C for 1-2 hours. Stirring is done for 0.5-2 hours.

PN CN108855064-A
TI Binary alloy-doped titanium dioxide visible light catalytic material comprises alloy material containing gold-doped metal compound.
AU XU H
   LIU F
   OUYANG S
   ZHAO M
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 201895694H
AB    NOVELTY - A binary alloy-doped titanium dioxide visible light catalytic material comprises an alloy material containing gold-doped metal compound (I).
   USE - Binary alloy-doped titanium dioxide visible light catalytic material.
   ADVANTAGE - The binary alloy-doped titanium dioxide visible light catalytic material effectively optimizes the visible light catalytic water decomposition hydrogen production/oxygen generation and carbon dioxide photoreduction activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A binary alloy-doped titanium dioxide visible light catalytic material comprises an alloy material containing gold-doped metal compound of formula: Au-M (I).
   M = Cu, Ru, Ag or Pt.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the binary alloy-doped titanium dioxide visible light catalytic material, which involves reducing the corresponding metal inorganic salt precursor with a chemical reagent in the absence of a surfactant or a polymer as a dispersing agent, and supporting the binary alloy nanoparticles on the surface of the titanium dioxide by a one-step hydrothermal method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method involves: (1) adding a gold-containing precursor and a M-precursor to titanium dioxide suspension in a stoichiometric ratio; (2) slowly adding a reducing agent solution to the suspension obtained in the step (1), stirring the solution and then transferring the solution to a PTFE-lined hydrothermal kettle; and (3) placing the hydrothermal kettle in the step (2) in an oven, reacting at 120-150 degrees C for 1-2 hours, washing with water after the reaction is completed, and drying. Preferred Components: The metal inorganic salt precursor includes a gold-containing precursor and an M precursor. The gold-containing precursor is one of gold chloride, chloroauric acid and potassium chloroaurate. The M-containing precursor is one of copper chloride, copper nitrate, barium chloride, silver nitrate, platinum chloride, chloroplatinic acid and potassium chloroplatinate. The reduction chemical reagent is one of sodium borohydride, hydrazine hydrate, sodium hypophosphite, ascorbic acid and hydrogen peroxide. The mass ratio of alloy to titanium dioxide is 0.5-1.5%. The molar ratio of reducing agent to titanium dioxide in the step (2) is 5:1-1:1. The titanium dioxide is anatase, rutile or brookite.
DC E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN CN108841789-A
TI Activating mouse T lymphocytes in vitro by obtaining lymphocytes, selecting cluster of differentiation 3 (CD3) epsilon lymphocytes, coating plate with anti-mouse CD3 epsilon and CD28 antibody, adding medium and culturing T lymphocytes.
AU YANG J
   LIU M
   WANG X
   LI C
   SONG Y
AE UNIV PLA THIRD MILITARY MEDICAL SECOND (UAMT-C)
GA 201894322C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for activating mouse T lymphocytes in vitro involves (a) obtaining lymphocytes of mouse spleen, peripheral lymph nodes, mucosal epithelium or peripheral blood, (b) selecting cluster of differentiation 3 epsilon + (CD epsilon +) lymphocytes and checking the purity, (c) coating 48-well cell culture plate with biologically active anti-mouse CD3 epsilon and CD28 antibody, (d) adding medium and the activated T lymphocytes into the cell culture plate obtained in the step (c) to resuspend the cells and (e) culturing the cell plates obtained in step (d) in a carbon dioxide incubator for 8-12 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for activating mouse T lymphocytes in vitro.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves activation rate of T lymphocytes up to 90% in a short time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves sacrificing 6-10 weeks old clean-grade mice by cervical dislocation, soaking in 70-85 vol.% ethanol solution for 3-10 minutes, aseptically removing the spleen, peripheral lymph nodes or mucosal epithelium and placing in a petri dish having a diameter of 10 cm and containing 18 ml phosphate buffer and 2 ml fetal calf serum. The step (b) involves screening CD epsilon + lymphocytes using the magnetic beads and performing flow cytometry assay. In step (c), the concentration of the anti-mouse CD3 epsilon and CD28 antibody mixture for coating the 48-well cell culture plate is 10 mu g/ml and 2 mu g/ml, respectively. The step (c) involves adding phosphate buffer solution having a pH of 7.6 and 100 mu l antibody mixture to each well. The method involves coating anti-mouse CD3 epsilon and CD28 antibody mixture on a 48-well cell culture plate, covering with a culture plate cover, and uniformly shaking the culture plate to cover the bottom of the cell culture well of the culture plate. The step (d) involves seeding the cells in a 48-well plate, where the concentration of the inoculated cell suspension was 1x 106/ml and inoculating 500 mu l cell suspension per well.In step (d), the activated T lymphocyte medium is based on Roswell Park Memorial Institute (RPMI)-1640 medium supplemented with 10 vol.% fetal bovine serum, 1x 104 mu mol/l 4-hydroxyethylpiperazineethanesulfonic acid, 20 mu mol/l L-glutamine, 10 mu mol/l sodium pyruvate, 1 mu mol/l non-essential amino acid solution, 50 mu mol/l beta -mercaptoethanol and 1x 105 units/l recombinant mouse interleukin-2 protein.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

TI Oil composition used in working fluid for e.g.            refrigerator, comprises oil mixture of naphthenic            mineral oil with polyol ester oil and/or polyvinyl            ether oil together with sorbitan compound and/or            glycerin fatty acid ester.

GA 2018896975
AB    NOVELTY - A refrigerator oil composition comprises an                oil mixture of a naphthenic mineral oil with a                polyol ester oil and/or a polyvinyl ether oil                together with a sorbitan compound and/or a glycerin                fatty acid ester.
   USE - Oil composition is used in working fluid                (claimed). Uses include but are not limited to                refrigerator, freezer, water heater,                air-conditioning equipment and exhaust heat                recovery apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The refrigerator oil composition suppresses                the adhesion of naphthenic mineral oil to an inner                surface of a heat exchanger, and can maintain                performance of different types of freezing                apparatus for a long period of time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for working                fluid, which comprises the composition, and at                least one refrigerant chosen from hydrofluorocarbon                refrigerant, hydrofluoroolefin refrigerant and                carbon dioxide refrigerant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The kinematic                  viscosity of naphthenic mineral oil at 40 degrees                  C of 5-460 mm2/second. At least one of polyol                  ester oil and polyvinyl ether oil has kinematic                  viscosity at 40 degrees C of 5-350 mm2/second.                  Preferred Composition: The composition contains a                  mixed oil of naphthenic mineral oil, polyol ester                  oil and sorbitan compound, or a mixed oil of                  naphthenic mineral oil and polyvinyl ether oil                  and 0.5-5 %mass at least one of sorbitan compound                  and glycerin fatty acid ester, and further an oil                  discharge suppressing agent for suppressing                  discharge of mixed oil from a compressor. The                  mass ratio of naphthenic mineral oil, polyol                  ester oil and polyvinyl ether oil is 60-90:40-10.                  The sorbitan compound is compound of                  polyoxyethylene, sorbitan, and fatty acid.

TI Method for detecting gallic acid, andrographolide and dehydroandrographolide, involves taking standard product of gallic acid, andrographolide standard, and andrographolide standard and adding methanol, followed by extracting with methanol.
AU LUO D
   ZHANG Y
   SUN L
   CHEN Q
AE WUZHOU FOOD & DRUG CONTROL INST (WUZH-Non-standard)
   WUZHOU FOOD & DRUG CONTROL INST (WUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201883071G
AB    NOVELTY - A gallic acid, andrographolide and dehydroandrographolide detecting method involves taking standard product of gallic acid, andrographolide standard and dehydrated andrographolide standard, adding methanol to make standard solution, taking a sample of the compound andrographis paniculata into a powder, and extracting with methanol to obtain a test solution, taking the standard solution and test solution on the supercritical chromatogram to obtain the content of gallic acid, andrographolide, and dehydroandrographolide in the test solution, where the chromatographic column is silica gel column, taking 0.1% carbon dioxide-phosphoric acid solution as mobile phase, the detection wavelength of andrographolide is 225 nm, the detection wavelength of dehydroandrographolide is 254 nm, the detection wavelength of gallic acid is 273 nm, and the injection volume of 5 mu l.
   USE - Method for detecting gallic acid, andrographolide and dehydroandrographolide in tablet.

PN CN108545944-A; CN108545944-B
TI Preparation method of brick, involves forming blank, drying, spraying glaze, performing ink jet printing, drying, spraying skid-proof glaze, firing, temperature programming, cooling, sintering, temperature programming, cooling and grinding.
AU WU Z
   FAN X
   LI L
   CHENG G
   LIN J
AE FOSHAN SHIWAN EAGLE BRAND CERAMIC CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201875864T
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of brick involves (A) forming a blank, and drying to obtain brick blank, spraying glaze on a bottom surface of the brick blank, performing ink jet printing, and drying, (B) spraying skid-proof glaze with thickness of 0.5-1 mm to obtain prefabricated brick, (C) placing the prefabricated brick in a kiln furnace for firing, and temperature programming to 1100 degrees C, (D) cooling to below 100 degrees C, placing into the kiln furnace for sintering, and temperature programming to 1200 degrees C, and (E) cooling to room temperature in a nitrogen atmosphere containing carbon dioxide to obtain semi-finished product, grinding and edging.
   USE - Preparation method of brick (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of skid-proof brick with excellent thermal resistance and antifouling property, and strong flexural strength.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Conditions: The temperature programming in the step (C) is performed by increasing the temperature to 200 degrees C, maintained for 10 minutes, increasing the temperature to 800 degrees C, maintaining for 25 minutes, increasing the temperature to 1100 degrees C, and maintaining for 15 minutes. The temperature programming in the step (D) is performed by increasing the temperature to 200 degrees C for 5 minutes, increasing the temperature to 600 degrees C for 20 minutes, and increasing the temperature to 1200 degrees C for 45 minutes. The drying temperature is 100-180 degrees C and 180 plus minus 0.1 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In the step (E), the content of carbon dioxide is 10-20 vol.%. The skid-proof glaze comprises a frit including dry particles (A), dry particles (B) and dry particles (C). The mass ratio of the dry particles (A), the dry particles (B) and the dry particles (C) is (2-5):(1-3):1. The dry particles (A) comprises 51-53 %mass silicon dioxide, 18-20 %mass aluminum oxide, 0.08-0.10 %mass ferric oxide, 10-11 %mass calcium oxide, 3-4 %mass magnesium oxide, 5-6 %mass potassium oxide, 5-6 %mass sodium oxide, 3-4 %mass zinc oxide, and 0.15-0.20 %mass lanthanum oxide. The dry particles (B) comprises 54-56 %mass silicon dioxide, 15-17 %mass aluminum oxide, 0.05-0.10 %mass ferric oxide, 8-9 %mass calcium oxide, 2-3 %mass magnesium oxide, 7-8 %mass potassium oxide, 7-8 %mass sodium oxide, 2-3 %mass zinc oxide, and 0.20-0.25 %mass cerium(III) oxide. The dry particles (C) comprises 50-52 %mass silicon dioxide, 23-24 %mass aluminum oxide, 0.05-0.06 %mass ferric oxide, 13-14 %mass calcium oxide, 3-4 %mass magnesium oxide, 3-3.5 %mass potassium oxide, 2.5-3 %mass sodium oxide, 2-2.5 %mass zinc oxide, and 0.30-0.35 %mass praseodymium oxide. Preferred Properties: The particle size of dry particles (A), dry particles (B) and dry particles (C) is 120-300 mesh.

AU JIN X
   LI Y
   PAN J
   LI L
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2018667412
AB    NOVELTY - Increasing activated carbon yield involves preparing dehydrating agent solution, soaking crushed dried biomass raw material, dipping and stirring, filtering, drying, heating, pre-carbonizing under vacuum or inert atmosphere, carbonizing, grinding carbonized products into powder, adding water to stir, washing, and drying to obtain biomass activated carbon material.
   USE - Method for increasing activated carbon yield.
   ADVANTAGE - The method retains carbon in biomass, improves product yield, utilizes dehydrating agent to promote decomposition and dehydration of biomass raw materials, prevents the action of water and carbon and its chain reaction during high temperature reaction, inhibits carbon to form carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methanol, methane and carbon-containing volatile components including formaldehyde, and reduces resources waste, production cost and air pollution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dehydrating agent is phosphorus dehydrating agent or boron dehydrating agent. The phosphorus dehydrating agent comprises ammonium polyphosphate, ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, diammonium hydrogen phosphate, disodium hydrogen phosphate, sodium dihydrogen phosphate, poly-sodium phosphate and/or sodium hexametaphosphate. The boron dehydrating agent comprises boric acid or borax. The mass concentration of dehydrating agent solution is 1-15%. The inert atmosphere is nitrogen atmosphere or argon gas atmosphere.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The biomass raw material comprises coconut, grapefruit peel, husk, silk, cotton, bagasse, shell, bran, chestnut, walnut shell or seed shell. Preferred Method: Dipping and stirring is done for 0.5-5 hours. Drying is done at 60-20 degrees C for 2-8 hours. Pre-carbonizing is done at 250-500 degrees C for 30-150 minutes. Carbonizing is done at 700-1000 degrees C for 60-180 minutes.

   GUAN W

   SU Y
   CUI W
   SHEN W
AE UNIV NANJING AGRIC (UYNA-C)
GA 201866092P
AB    NOVELTY - Increasing the resistance of crops using hydrogen sulfide donor sodium hydrosulfide comprises increasing the seed germination rate under drought stress or salt stress by immersing the seed in a sodium hydrosulfide solution having a concentration of 0.01-1.6 mmol/l at 25-30 degrees C for 6-24 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for increasing the resistance of crops using hydrogen sulfide donor sodium hydrosulfide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases germination rate, plant biomass, regulates chlorophyll content of plant photosynthesis, and plants caused by stress, changes water loss, photosynthetic rate, stomatal conductance, intercellular carbon dioxide concentration and transpiration rate to improve crop tolerance according to different plants at different growth stages and different periods of stress, different application methods and sodium hydrosulfide solution concentration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The method uses the crop seedling stage includes the root solution treated with a sodium hydrogen sulfide solution at a concentration of 0.01-1.6 mmol/l at a temperature of 25-30 degrees C. The rooting treatment is to change the solution for every 6-24 hours, and continuously replace the mixture for 2-5 times. The sodium hydrosulfide solution used per plant is 20-50 ml. The method comprises using of hydrogen sulfide to improve the stress-resistant growth of crops in the seedling stage in the seedling stage includes using sodium hydrosulfide mixture with a concentration of sodium thiosulfate of 0.01-1.6 mmol/l in different stress periods and using spraying treatment. The different stress periods includes initial, middle and late stages of stress, and foliar application of sulfur in each period, where the amount of the sodium hydride mixture is kept the same, and an average of 30-100 ml per plant is sprayed, and spraying is performed for 1-3 times per period. The sodium hydrosulfide mixture is an aqueous solution of sodium hydrosulfide having a concentration of 100 mmol/l and is prepared by mixing with Tween-80 (RTM: Non-ionic surfactant (5) and SILWET 408 (RTM: Trisiloxane ethoxylate) at a volume ratio of 500-1000:1:1. The method uses hydrogen sulfide to improve the stress-resistant growth of crops in the seedling stage in the crop seedling stage at a temperature of 25-30 degrees C, with a concentration of 0.05-0.4 mmol/l sodium hydrosulfide solution root irrigation. The method uses sodium hydrosulfide mixture with a concentration of sodium thiosulfate of 0.05-0.1 mmol/l in different stress periods and subjecting to spraying treatment.

PN CN108281726-A; CN108281726-B
TI Method for extracting lithium hydroxide from lithium phosphate-containing waste residue, involves mixing lithium phosphate-containing waste residue with alkaline earth metal oxide, followed by evaporating purified liquid and filtering.
AU CHEN Y
   GAN H
   CAO L
   GUAN J
   CHEN B
   SHI X
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 2018564975
AB    NOVELTY - A lithium hydroxide extracting method involves mixing lithium phosphate-containing waste residue with alkaline earth metal oxide and/or hydroxide powder, adding water to adjust the slurry, heating and grinding after leaching, cooling after the reaction is completed, filtering and washing the filter cake with clean water to obtain filtrate. The filtrate is mixed with washing solution to obtain extract. The extract is mixed added with soluble carbonate and/or carbon dioxide, stirred, reacted completely and filtered to obtain a purified liquid. The purified liquid is evaporated, crystallized and filtered to obtain lithium hydroxide monohydrate.
   USE - Method for extracting lithium hydroxide from lithium phosphate-containing waste residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables extracting lithium hydroxide with rational design, high resource utilization rate and high purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline earth metal oxide and/or hydroxide are selected from the group consisting of calcium oxide, barium oxide, strontium oxide, calcium hydroxide, barium hydroxide and strontium hydroxide. The soluble carbonate is selected from sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate and ammonium carbonate.

   JAPAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY AGENCY (JSTA-C)
GA 2018260671
AB    NOVELTY - A composite material comprises a porous                coordination polymer compound which is supported                within the pores of a porous material including                network structure of silicon-oxygen bonds obtained                by copolymerizing a dialkoxysilane and a                trialkoxysilane.
   USE - Composite material is used in agent for                adsorbing gas (claimed) e.g. carbon dioxide gas,                nitrogen gas, hydrogen gas, carbon monoxide gas                which is byproduct gas at steel mills and                petrochemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material has high filling rate                while maintaining or improving the properties of                the porous coordination polymer compound.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                manufacture of composite material, which involves                copolymerizing the porous material with the                dialkoxysilane and the trialkoxysilane in a                solvent, such that porous coordination polymer                compound is supported within the pores of the                porous material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The organic ligand is                  a compound including a carboxyl group, a pyridyl                  group, or an imidazolyl group. The solvent is                  chosen from methanol, ethanol, 1-propanol,                  2-propanol, 1-butanol, 2-butanol, benzene,                  hexane, acetaldehyde, acetone, cyclohexane and                  N,N-dimethylformamide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The ratio of the                  volume of the composite material with respect to                  the volume of the porous material is 1 or less.                  The porous material is a compound having partial                  structures of formulae (I) or (II), preferably                  chosen from structures of formulae (Ia)-(If). The                  porous material is obtained by copolymerizing by                  alkoxysilane compounds of formulae (III) and                  (IV). The porous coordination polymer compound is                  compound of formula: (M(OOC-Y1-COO))2, and                  includes a structure in which an organic ligand                  is coordinated to a metal ion having valence of                  2-4.A1-A3=functional group chosen from vinyl,                  cyano, 1-5C alkyl, amino, mercapto, fluoro, aryl                  or epoxy;M=Cu2+, Zn2+, Ru2+, Rh2+, Mo2+, Fe3+, Al3+,                  Ti4+ or Co3+;andY1=divalent aromatic group in which two                  adjacent COOH groups are in meta-position.Preferred Properties: The porous material                  has porosity of 50 vol.% or more, average pore                  diameter of 5-20 mu m. The composite material has                  complex introduction rate (ratio of the content                  of the porous coordination polymer compound with                  respect to the total content of the composite                  material), of 40 %mass or more. The volume                  expansion rate of the porous material after                  contacting the porous material with a liquid                  containing the porous coordination polymer                  compound and the solvent, and before contacting                  the porous material, is 1.2-2.

TI Continuous steel plate heat treatment furnace comprises heating stand placed on heat treatment furnace, heating unit placed on heating zone and object flows into heating zone and heat-treated by heating unit.
AU NOH D S
   LEE D K
   LEE M J
   LEE E K
   KIM S G
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 201821300F
AB    NOVELTY - Continuous steel plate heat treatment furnace comprises a heating stand (11) installed on the heat treatment furnace, a heating unit (1) installed on a heating zone, and an object (90) flows into the heating zone and heat-treated by the heating unit. The heating unit is a porous combustor.
   USE - Used as continuous steel plate heat treatment furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The furnace improves energy efficiency, productivity and quality; contributes to environmental protection by reducing emission of nitrogen oxides and carbon dioxide generated during heat treatment; uniformly radiates heat energy on object; prevents backfire and object from being corroded; reduces operation cost; increases heat transfer rate, reaction rate and driving stability; and smoothly flows fuel and air.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the continuous steel plate heat treatment furnace.
   Heating unit (1)
   Heating stand (11)
   Object (90)

PT P
PN CN107649175-A; CN107649175-B
TI Preparation of gallium-germanium-doped manganese            oxide-SAPO molecular sieve catalyst by soaking SAPO-11            molecular sieve in manganese salt, adding ammonium            carbonate, stirring, aging, soaking in            gallium-germanium salt, drying, and roasting.
AU ZHAO X
AE SHANGHAI GETONG IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201811563F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of gallium-germanium-doped                manganese oxide-SAPO molecular sieve catalyst                comprises soaking SAPO-11 molecular sieve in                manganese salt solution for 2-8 hours, adding                ammonium carbonate solution while stirring, aging                for 30-40 minutes, filtering, drying, and baking,                where amount of manganese element is 16-20%; and                soaking in gallium-germanium salt, drying, and                roasting, where molar ratio of gallium, germanium,                and manganese is 0.02-0.05:0.1-0.2:1.
   USE - Method for preparing gallium-germanium-doped                manganese oxide-SAPO molecular sieve                catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst can be used in the low                temperature and low temperature ammonia selective                catalytic reduction-denitration system of                automobile exhaust and has maintained good                temperature operating window, good resistance to                sulfur dioxide, carbon dioxide and water poisoning                ability, and good catalytic activity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The SAPO-11 molecular                  sieve is prepared by mixing aluminum source,                  silicon source, phosphorus source, fluoride and                  template diisopropylamine into water, stirring at                  room temperature, adding carbon black, stirring                  at room temperature, reacting at 80-130 degrees C                  for 3-6 hours, crystallizing to 200-220 degrees C                  for 8-24 hours, cooling to 150-170 degrees C for                  1-2 hours, cooling to room temperature and at 0-5                  degrees C, filtering, triturating using deionized                  water to neutral, drying, and roasting, where                  molar ratio of silica, aluminum oxide, carbon,                  phosphorus pentoxide, sodium fluoride,                  diisopropylamine, and water is 1.0: 2.1:1.2:                  2.1:0.11:3.1:200. The drying process is done for                  4-10 hours at 100-120 degrees C, preferably 5-20                  hours. The roasting process is done for 3-5 hours                  at 650-750 degrees C, preferably 500-600 degrees                  C. Preferred Components: The mass concentration                  of ammonium carbonate solution is 10-15%. The                  molar ratio of ammonium bicarbonate to manganese                  salt solution is 1:10-15. The aluminum source is                  pseudoboehmite. The silicon source is silica sol.                  The phosphorus source is phosphoric acid.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC J04-E04; J04-E11
IP B01D-053/56; B01D-053/86; B01J-029/85; B01J-037/02; B01J-037/08

   USE - Hydrogen production system based on solar fuel methanol water reforming.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogen production system achieves zero carbon dioxide emissions throughout the entire industrial chain and realizes sustainable development of human society and construction of ecological civilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for methanol reforming, which involves:
   (A) sending methanol and water in the solar fuel methanol synthesis system to the methanol-water reforming module, and methanol and water undergo a reforming reaction under the action of a methanol reforming catalyst to obtain hydrogen, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and unreacted water vapor mixture I;
   (B) passing mixed gas I through the carbon dioxide and hydrogen separation module, entering separated hydrogen gas to the hydrogen gas collection module, and entering other gasses to the water vapor conversion module to obtain the mixed gas II; and
   (C) passing mixed gas II through the carbon dioxide separation module, entering carbon dioxide to the carbon dioxide collection module, and entering unreacted gas to the carbon dioxide and hydrogen separation module again to be separated.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of hydrogen production system based on solar fuel methanol water reforming. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solar fuel methanol refers to methanol synthesized using a renewable energy route. The solar fuel methanol includes methanol synthesized by renewable energy generation, hydrogen production by electrolyzed water, and hydrogenation of carbon dioxide. The renewable energy includes one of light, water and wind. The carbon dioxide and hydrogen separation module uses a palladium membrane separation technology, and the working temperature range is 340-450 degrees C.

A third washing cooler is set to cool the third washing liquid. The combustion exhaust gas discharged from the second cleaning unit is washed with a third cleaning liquid in a third cleaning unit. The amine accompanying the combustion exhaust gas is recovered. A portion of the second cleaning liquid discharged from the second cleaning part is mixed into the third cleaning liquid.
