TI Two-stage flash vapor recovery system for low-temperature methanol washing flash tower, comprises pretreatment system comprises low temperature methanol, liquid carbon dioxide recovery system, ammonia synthesis and recovery methane system.
AU YAN J
   SONG S
   LV C
AE HENAN JINMEI TIANQING COAL CHEM CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 2018560902
AB    NOVELTY - Two-stage flash vapor recovery system for low-temperature methanol washing flash tower, comprises pretreatment system, liquid carbon dioxide recovery system, ammonia synthesis and recovery methane system, where pretreatment system comprises low temperature methanol washing flash tower gas buffer unit, where carbon dioxide gas compressor and water cooling unit are released flash gas in second stage of low-temperature methanol washing flash tower, and flash gas body entered in first-stage inlet of carbon dioxide gas compressor (3) through the gas buffer unit, and passed through carbon dioxide gas compressor. The stage is compressed to an outlet pressure of 1.1-1.3 mega Pascal and then entered in water-cooling unit. The liquid carbon dioxiderecovery system comprises a fine desulfurization tower, a purification tower, a carbon dioxidegas compressor, a pre-cooler (7), an ammonia condenser, a separation tower, and a product carbon dioxidestorage tank.
   USE - Two-stage flash vapor recovery system for low-temperature methanol washing flash tower.
   ADVANTAGE - The two-stage flash vapor recovery system device is realizes sorting and recycling flashed vapor, saves energy and protects environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The flash gas is entered in fine desulfurization tower after passing through the water cooling unit, and flash gas after desulfurization is removed to purification tower (6). The flash gas after the purification tower is entered second stage of carbon dioxidegas compressor and is pressurized to 3.4-3.6 mega Pascal and then entered in precooler. And then to the ammonia condenser for cooling and liquefaction, and finally into separation column. The liquid carbon dioxide is separated from non-condensable gas, and liquid carbon dioxidecoming out from bottom of the separation column are entered in product carbon dioxidestorage tank. The ammonia-forming and recovery methane system includes a coal gas-making unit, a carbon monoxide converter, a low-temperature methanol-eluting sulfur decarburization unit, a liquid nitrogen-washing separation methane carbon monoxide unit, an ammonia plant, an ammonia product storage tank, and a methane gas product storage tank. The gas produced in the gas enters the carbon monoxide converter first, then enters the low-temperature methanol-eluting sulfur decarburization unit, and then entered in liquid nitrogen-washed separation methane carbon monoxide unit. The separated carbon monoxide is sent back to the carbon monoxide converter, and the separated methane entered in methane gas product storage. The remaining gas entered in ammonia plant, and the prepared ammonia product entered in ammonia product storage tank. The carbon monoxide converter outlet pipe is connected to separation tower (9) in liquid carbon dioxiderecovery system, and the non-condensable gas in separation tower entered in carbon monoxide converter outlet pipe, and then entered in low temperature methanol elutes sulfur decarburization unit and is incorporated into synthetic ammonia production system (10).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of two-stage flash vapor recovery system device.
   Carbon dioxide gas compressor (3)
   Purification tower (6)
   Pre-cooler (7)
   Separation tower (9)
   Production system (10)

Preferred Condition: The flash gas pressure released in second stage of low-temperature methanol washing flash column is 0.35 mega Pascal. The flash gas is compressed by carbon dioxide gas compressor to a discharge pressure of 1.2 mega Pascal and then entered in water cooling device. The purification tower is regenerated, and non-condensable gas separated by the separation column which is introduced for regeneration. The flash gas is purified by purification tower and then pressurized into second stage of carbon gas compressor at 3.5 mega Pascal in pre-cooler.

TI Vulcanizable rubber composition used in pneumatic tire, comprises diene-based elastomer and carbon dioxide-generated carbon reinforcement produced by mixing first gas stream containing carbon dioxide and second gas stream containing gaseous reducing agent to form reaction gas mixture.
AU MAZUMDAR A
   DENNIS-PELCHER R V
AE GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER CO (GOOD-C)
   GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER CO (GOOD-C)
   GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER CO (GOOD-C)
GA 2022739341
AB    NOVELTY - Vulcanizable rubber composition comprises 100 parts by weight elastomer (phr) of at least one diene-based elastomer, and 1-100 phr of a carbon dioxide-generated carbon reinforcement. The carbon dioxide-generated carbon reinforcement is produced by mixing a first gas stream containing carbon dioxide and a second gas stream containing a gaseous reducing agent to form a reaction gas mixture. The reaction gas mixture is supplied to a reaction zone and the carbon dioxide is reacted with the gaseous reducing agent in the reaction zone in the presence of an iron-containing catalyst to form water and the solid carbon product. The water formed in the reaction zone is separated from the reaction gas mixture during the reaction of the carbon dioxide with the gaseous reducing agent.
   USE - Vulcanizable rubber composition used in pneumatic tire (claimed) such as race tire, passenger tire, aircraft tire, agricultural, earthmover, off-the-road, truck tire, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The vulcanizable rubber composition has improved recycled nature and sustainability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide-generated carbon reinforcement includes single-wall carbon nanotubes, multi-wall carbon nanotubes, carbon nanofibers, graphite platelets, graphene, carbon black, and amorphous carbon. The carbon dioxide-generated carbon reinforcement includes agglomerations of particles of solid carbon on an iron-containing catalyst. The solid carbon is selected from graphite, graphene, carbon black, amorphous carbon, fibrous carbon, and buckminster fullerenes. The sulfur-containing organosilane is selected from bis(trialkoxysilylalkyl) polysulfides, mercaptosilanes, and blocked mercaptosilanes. The sulfur containing organosilicon compounds is selected from 3,3'-bis(triethoxysilylpropyl) disulfide, 3,3'-bis(triethoxysilylpropyl) tetrasulfide and 3-(octanoylthio)-1-propyltriethoxysilane. The diene-based elastomer is selected from styrene-butadiene rubbers, polybutadiene rubbers, natural rubbers, synthetic polyisoprenes, and functionalized versions. Preferred Conditions: The entangled agglomerations of particles of solid carbon are clustered with a characteristic dimension of less than 1 millimeter. The solid carbon is formed by reacting carbon dioxide with a gaseous reducing agent in the presence of an iron-containing catalyst, at least some of the particles of solid carbon bonded to a particle of the iron-containing catalyst, the catalyst particle having a dimension between about 1.3-1.6 times a dimension of the particle of solid carbon associated with the catalyst particle. Preferred Compositions: The vulcanizable rubber composition further comprises 1-150 phr of silica and 1-20 phr of a sulfur-containing organosilane. The amount of carbon dioxide-generated carbon reinforcement ranges from 5-80 phr.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).); A12 (Polymers of di-and higher olefins; acetylenics; nitroso compounds.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A95 (Transport - including vehicle parts, tyres and armaments.); Q11 (Wheels, tyres, connections (B60B-D))

TI Preparing supercritical carbon dioxide foaming            degradable material comprises mixing polyglycolic acid,            nucleating agent, castor oil mixture and filler,            extruding, granulating, drying to obtain modified            material, and processing modified PGA material in            supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.
AU XING Q
   XU J
   LIU W
   CHEN X
AE HAINAN SHINER IND CO LTD (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 202209855P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing supercritical carbon dioxide foaming                degradable material comprises mixing 75-85 pts. wt.                polyglycolic acid, 0.2-0.6 pts. wt. nucleating                agent, 10-20 pts. wt. castor oil mixture and 4-10                pts. wt. filler, extruding and granulating, and                drying to obtain modified polyglycolic acid (PGA)                material, where the nucleating agent is polylactic                acid and/or polycaprolactone, the filler is nano                silicon dioxide and/or nano calcium carbonate, the                castor oil mixture is mixture of castor oil and                methanol with the purity of 88-93%, processing the                modified PGA material in supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid to obtain supercritical carbon                dioxide foaming degradable material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                supercritical carbon dioxide foaming degradable                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: is light weight, and fully                biodegradable foam material with low cell size and                high cell density; has excellent heat insulation                and heat preservation properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                supercritical carbon dioxide foaming degradable                material, prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the                  castor oil to the methanol is 7.5-8.5:2. The                  weight average molecular weight of the                  polyglycolic acid is 200,000-300,000 g/mol, and                  the melting point is 200-220&#8451;. The                  polylactic acid is D-polylactic acid with                  weight-average molecular weight of 90,000-110,000                  g/mol. The weight average molecular weight of the                  polycaprolactone is 100,000-120,000 g/mol. The                  supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is a carbon                  dioxide fluid with purity of greater than                  99.9%.Preferred Method: The pressure of                  supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is greater                  than or equal to 8 MPa; and the time for                  supercritical carbon dioxide fluid treatment is                  10-15 minutes. During the extrusion and                  granulation, the temperature of the feeding                  section is 160-175&#8451;, the temperature of                  the mixing section is less than or equal to                  200&#8451;, and the temperature of the                  extrusion section is 180-195&#8451;.

TI Large coal-fired power plant carbon dioxide capturing integral system disturbance suppression method, involves collecting output quantity of coal-fired power plant carbon dioxide capturing integral system.
AU LI M
   LIAO P
AE JIANGSU SHUNGAO INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021A8302G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting a controlled variable and a control variable of a large coal-fired power plant carbon dioxide capturing integral system. A relative state space model of the large coal-fired power plant carbon dioxide capturing integral system is established. An infinite time domain performance index satisfying Lyapunov stability condition is established. A controller related parameter is set. Target function is constructed. An upper bound infinite time domain performance index is optimized. An amplification state space equation is formed. A current time control variable is corrected. A current time control quantity is output. An output quantity of the coal-fired power plant carbon dioxide capturing integral system is collected.
   USE - Large coal-fired power plant carbon dioxide capturing integral system disturbance suppression method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effectively processing noise and undetectable disturbance problem in an industrial process, so that actual demand of an industrial field can be satisfied.

PT P
PN CN110693063-A; CN110693063-B
TI Preparing blueberry extract useful for tobacco,            comprises e. g. selecting fresh blueberry and blueberry            leaf and sending into super-critical carbon dioxide            extraction device and sending blueberry extract into            high-pressure sealed container.
AU ZHOU X
   CHEN Y
   ZHAO T
AE CHINA TOBACCO HUBEI IND LLC (CNTA-C)
GA 202008781H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing blueberry extract comprises (i)                selecting fresh blueberry and blueberry leaf and                sending into super-critical carbon dioxide                extraction device, sending the obtained                super-critical carbon dioxide fluid containing                blueberry extract directly into a high-pressure                sealed container, decreasing the temperature and                pressure to liquefy the super-critical carbon                dioxide, stirring and crushing the blueberries and                making the blueberry paste uniformly distributed in                the liquid carbon dioxide, reducing the pressure,                evaporating a portion of the liquid carbon dioxide,                and forming the remaining liquid carbon dioxide                into dry ice containing paste-type blueberry,                crushing the dry ice and heating with hot air, and                sublimating the dry ice to obtain mixture; (ii)                preparing immobilized lactic acid bacteria; (iii)                fermenting the mixture with the immobilized lactic                acid bacteria; and (iv) filtering the fermentation                product.
   USE - The extract is useful for tobacco (claimed)                for reducing throat irritation.
   ADVANTAGE - The extract has rich and complete nutrition                and rich fragrance, and increases fruit sweet                fragrance of tobacco, and improves taste and                reduces the effect of residue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  temperature is lowered below 30 degrees C, and                  the pressure is lowered below 7 MPa to liquefy                  the super-critical carbon dioxide, the pressure                  is further reduced to normal pressure so that the                  remaining liquid carbon dioxide forms dry ice                  containing paste-type blueberry, the dry ice is                  heated by hot air flow at 300-340 degrees C. In                  the step (iii), the mixture is mixed with the                  immobilized lactic acid bacteria in the mass                  ratio of 2-5:1, the fermentation is performed at                  20-28 degrees C and the stirring speed is 1-5                  revolutions/minute for 48-96 hours, the step of                  ultrasonically extracting the fermentation                  product by mixing the fermentation product with                  ethanol with mass fraction of 50-95% in a                  material ratio of 1:3-8, the then at the                  ultrasonic frequency of 45-65 Hz and extracting                  for 30-120 minutes at 30-50 degrees C. In the                  step (iv), the ultrasonic extraction solution is                  filtered through a filter membrane of 0.6-0.9 mm.                  The extraction solution obtained by filtering                  through a filtration membrane is under pressure                  of -0.01 to -0.1 Mpa and temperature of 45-55                  degrees C, the concentrated under reduced                  pressure to relative density of 1.25-1.35 and                  adding 20% of the mass of the concentrated                  solution of propylene glycol. In the step (ii),                  the method for preparing the immobilized lactic                  acid bacteria by mixing lactic acid bacteria and                  sodium alginate solution with mass fraction of                  3-6% in a volume ratio of 3-5:100, the obtained                  mixed solution is added drop-wise into calcium                  chloride solution with mass fraction of 3-6%,                  calcium alginate gel beads are formed, and the                  calcium chloride solution and the calcium                  alginate gel beads are stored at -4 degrees C for                  12-24 hours, taking out the small ball, rinsing                  with sterile water, and drying to obtain                  immobilized lactic acid bacteria. The preparation                  of the lactic acid bacteria comprises inoculating                  lactic acid bacteria in broth culture medium,                  culturing at 35-40 degrees C for 40-50 hours                  under stirring speed of 45-55 revolutions/minute,                  and centrifuging the culture solution at                  7000-10,000 revolutions/minute.

PN CN110465197-A
TI Separation of stable carbon isotope involves preparing ionic liquid using heterocyclic organic compound, chemically exchanging ionic liquid with carbon dioxide gas, and desorbing ionic liquid which is absorbed with carbon dioxide.
AU WANG T
   ZHANG F
   FEI W
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 2019990783
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of stable carbon isotope involves mixing an organic base with a nitrogen-containing heterocyclic organic compound or an organic acid compound at room temperature to prepare an ionic liquid and purifying the ionic liquid, chemically exchanging the ionic liquid with an initial raw material carbon dioxide gas under normal temperature and pressure, or under normal pressure, and desorbing the ionic liquid which is absorbed with carbon dioxide at 353-383 K under normal pressure to obtain a highly-abundant carbon dioxide (13-CO2) gas.
   USE - Separation of stable carbon isotope.
   ADVANTAGE - The method separates stable carbon isotope using ionic liquid having high carbon dioxide absorption capacity and absorption rate, under normal temperature operation. Purification of the ionic liquid serving as the chemical-exchange solvent is economical, safe and reliable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) screening of ionic liquid in the separation of stable carbon isotope process; and
   (2) chemical-exchange solvent composition, which comprises the organic base and nitrogen-containing heterocyclic organic compound or organic acid compound, and optionally a solvent. The composition does not precipitate solids after saturated absorption of carbon dioxide.

TI Method for constructing all-atom model of polymer            and ultra-critical carbon dioxide mixed system,            involves adding polymer to ultra-critical carbon            dioxide mixed system, and establishing stable atomic            model of polymer and mixed system.
AU YAN Y
   ZHANG J
   ZHAO M
   DAI C
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM HUADONG (UYPE-C)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 201926400L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves combining first                topological relationship between first monomers and                second topology relationship between second                monomers into a sequence relationship to generate a                complete topology relationship of the polymer                according to an arrangement mode between the first                and second monomers in the polymer. Energy of                high-molecular configuration is minimized by using                Gromacs software and a selected all-atom force                field. Energy optimal configuration process of the                polymer is realized. The energy-optimized polymer                is added to an ultra-critical carbon dioxide mixed                system. High-temperature annealing process of the                polymer and the ultra-critical carbon dioxide mixed                system is executed by the Gromacs software. A                stable atomic model of the polymer and the                ultra-critical carbon dioxide mixed system is                established.
   USE - Method for constructing all-atom model of a                polymer and ultra-critical carbon dioxide mixed                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing influence rule of                the polymers on micro-dynamics of ultra-critical                carbon dioxide application and development.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                polymer.

AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) determines the state of photosynthesis of the plant (23) based on a temperature difference between the leaf vein portion and leaf blade portion of the plant, and controls the supply amount of carbon dioxide into a facility for growing the plant based on the state of photosynthesis of the plant. PN CN114907187-A; CN217594572-U
TI Method for directly preparing methanol from            pure/rich carbon dioxide and hydrogen comprises mixing            pressurized hydrogen/carbon dioxide gas with            pressurized compressed circulating gas, preheating,            transferring to first reaction bed, reacting, carrying            out gas-liquid separation and flash evaporation.
AU REN W
   SI J
   ZHENG Y
   ZHANG G
   WANG S
   WANG T
   JIANG B
   XU B
   ZHOU F
AE CHINA CHENGDA ENG CO LTD (CNCE-C)
GA 2022A9000M
AB    NOVELTY - A method for directly preparing methanol from                pure/rich carbon dioxide and hydrogen comprises (i)                mixing a pressurized hydrogen/carbon dioxide raw                material gas with a pressurized compressed                circulating gas to obtain a raw material mixed gas,                preheating and transferring to the first reaction                bed of the carbon dioxide hydrogenation reactor as                a bed inlet gas to react, (ii) taking first                reaction bed outlet gas as a heat source for                preheating the mixed gas of raw material, carrying                out gas-liquid separation to the heat exchanged                first reaction bed outlet gas and transferring the                separated liquid to next methanol flash evaporation                process, (iii) using the gas separated from the                gas-liquid separation as the raw material gas of                the next bed layer, preheating, reacting, cooling                the outlet gas and separating and (iv) separating                the outlet gas of last reaction bed layer by                gas-liquid separation, transferring the separated                liquid into next step of methanol flash evaporation                process.
   USE - The method for directly preparing methanol                from pure/rich carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problems of low carbon                dioxide conversion rate and high power consumption                in existing methods and the carbon dioxide one-way                conversion rate is high, greatly reduces the loop                circulation amount and the investment is low.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A method for directly preparing methanol from                pure/rich carbon dioxide and hydrogen adopting a                carbon dioxide hydrogenation reactor provided with                multiple reaction bed layers comprises (i) mixing a                pressurized hydrogen/carbon dioxide raw material                gas with a pressurized compressed circulating gas                to obtain a raw material mixed gas, preheating the                raw material mixed gas and transferring to the                first reaction bed of the carbon dioxide                hydrogenation reactor as a bed inlet gas to react,                (ii) taking first reaction bed outlet gas after the                reaction as a heat source for preheating the mixed                gas of raw material, carrying out gas-liquid                separation to the heat exchanged first reaction bed                outlet gas and transferring the separated liquid to                next methanol flash evaporation process, (iii)                using the gas separated from the gas-liquid                separation in step (ii) as the raw material gas of                the next bed layer, preheating, reacting, cooling                the outlet gas and separating and (iv) separating                the outlet gas of last reaction bed layer by                gas-liquid separation, transferring the separated                liquid into next step of methanol flash evaporation                process, compressing the separated gas as the                compressed circulating gas and mixing with the                pressurized hydrogen/carbon dioxide raw material                gas to obtain the raw material mixed gas and                performing the reaction again.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (iv), a part of                  the separated gas is extracted to form a                  relaxation gas for export and the remaining gas                  is compressed and used as a compressed                  circulating gas. The raw mixed gas is first                  protected by the annular gap of the carbon                  dioxide hydrogenation reactor and preheated into                  the first reaction bed. The gas-liquid separation                  of each step enters the methanol flash                  evaporation tank to obtain a crude methanol and                  flash steam. The method involves preheating the                  bed gas to 210-240&#8451; by the inlet and                  outlet heat exchangers.Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide                  hydrogenation reactor is operated at 5.0-10.0 MPa                  and the operating temperature is                  210-290&#8451;. The pressure of the                  pressurized hydrogen/carbon dioxide raw material                  gas is 5.0-10.0 MPa. The pressure of the                  pressurized compressed circulating gas is                  5.0-10.0 MPa.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  hydrogen/carbon dioxide is 3:1 in the pressurized                  hydrogen/carbon dioxide raw material gas. The                  dosage ratio of the pressurized compressed                  circulating gas to the pressurized                  hydrogen/carbon dioxide raw material gas is                  2-4:1.Preferred Device: The reaction bed layers in                  the carbon dioxide hydrogenation reactor are                  distributed axially or radially.

PN WO2022031726-A2; WO2022031726-A3
TI System for producing polycarbonate polymer, has            carbon dioxide reduction electrolyzer having membrane            electrode assembly having ion conductive polymer layers            and cathode catalyst for chemical reduction of carbon            dioxide to carbon monoxide, intermediate reactors and            polycarbonate synthesis reactor.
AU FLANDERS N H
   KUHL K P
   CAVE E R
   MA S
   HUO Z
   HAINES C S
   BEKKEDAHL T A
   CORP K L
   MISHRA A D
   IZETT E
AE OPUS 12 INC (OPUS-Non-standard)
GA 202221680H
AB    NOVELTY - The system (501) has: a carbon dioxide                reduction electrolyzer comprising a membrane                electrode assembly, which comprises one or more ion                conductive polymer layers and a cathode catalyst                for facilitating chemical reduction of carbon                dioxide to carbon monoxide; intermediate reactors                collectively configured to receive carbon monoxide                produced by the carbon dioxide reduction                electrolyzer and produce one or more intermediate                chemicals; and a polycarbonate synthesis reactor                configured to receive the one or more intermediate                chemicals or its one or more derivatives and                synthesize polycarbonate polymer. The intermediate                reactors have a Fischer Trosch reactor configured                to produce naphtha. The gas fermentation reactor is                configured to produce a product chosen from                naphtha, phenol, and acetone. The carbon dioxide                reduction electrolyzer is configured to produce                hydrogen and the carbon monoxide in a molar ratio                of at least 0.5:1.
   USE - The system is used for producing polycarbonate                polymer, producing a metal formate, producing one                or more chemical compounds, and producing liquid                hydrocarbons from carbon dioxide (all claimed). The                system and the method are used for carbon dioxide                reactor control, and for production of carbon                monoxide and/or other carbon-containing products                (carbon-containing species). The produced metal                formate is used as enzyme stabilizers in liquid                detergents, where the metal formate includes                sodium, potassium, or cesium formate, or an alkali                earth formate such as calcium or barium formate.                The chemical compounds include ethylene glycol                (monoethylene glycol or MEG), acetic acid,                diisocyanate, polymer polyurethane and oxalic                acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The system achieves high tolerance to                impurities and/or dilute carbon dioxide inputs (as                compared to other carbon dioxide reactors), such as                tolerance to poisoning by impurities in the reactor                input(s) and/or to inputs diluted by species such                as methane, carbon monoxide, oxygen, and/or                nitrogen. The method achieves impurity and/or                dilutes input tolerance (always selecting such                process conditions, and selecting such process                conditions in response to a current and/or                anticipated state of the reactor input, such as an                impure and/or dilute state). The system and/or                method can additionally or alternately exhibit any                suitable tolerance to impure and/or dilute inputs                or exhibit no such tolerance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method of producing a polycarbonate                polymer;
   (2) a system for producing a metal                formate;
   (3) a method of producing a metal                formate;
   (4) a system for producing one or more                chemical compounds; and
   (5) a system for producing liquid hydrocarbons                from carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                system including a carbon dioxide electrolyzer                configured to produce syngas.
   System (501)
   Carbon dioxide electrolyzer (503)
   Downstream gasification unit or partial                oxidation unit (507)

PN US11219860-B1; EP3995204-A1; WO2022096644-A1; EP3995204-B1
TI Scrubbing gas comprising carbon dioxide to deplete            gas of carbon dioxide for large-scale power plants,            involves feeding first spent aqueous scrubbing liquid            to anode chamber, regenerating by electrolysis and            withdrawing regenerated liquid.
AU JAKOBSEN J S
AE ESTECH AS (ESTE-Non-standard)
   ESTECH AS (ESTE-Non-standard)
   ESTECH AS (ESTE-Non-standard)
GA 202207108S
AB    NOVELTY - Scrubbing a gas comprising carbon dioxide to                deplete the gas of the carbon dioxide (CO2),                involves scrubbing the gas in a scrubber with a                first alkaline, aqueous scrubbing liquid to                dissolve carbon dioxide as hydrogen carbonate                and/or as carbonate in the first scrubbing liquid                to provide a first spent aqueous scrubbing liquid                comprising hydrogen carbonate and/or carbonate,                feeding the first spent aqueous scrubbing liquid to                an anode chamber of an electrolytic cell comprising                the anode chamber and a cathode chamber separated                by a membrane, regenerating the first spent aqueous                scrubbing liquid in the electrolytic cell by                electrolysis to increase the pH of the first spent                aqueous scrubbing liquid in the cathode chamber and                to deplete the first spent aqueous scrubbing liquid                of hydrogen carbonate and carbonate in the anode                chamber by decreasing the pH, and withdrawing                regenerated alkaline, aqueous scrubbing liquid from                the cathode chamber and re-circulating it to the                scrubber.
   USE - Method for scrubbing a gas comprising carbon                dioxide to deplete the gas of the carbon dioxide                for large-scale power plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables optimizing a complete,                energy and cost-efficient system for depleting gas                of carbon dioxide driven by electrical power, by                utilizing less heat for carbon dioxide                capture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Scrubbing a gas comprising carbon dioxide to                deplete the gas of the carbon dioxide (CO2),                involves scrubbing the gas in a scrubber with a                first alkaline, aqueous scrubbing liquid to                dissolve carbon dioxide as hydrogen carbonate                and/or as carbonate in the first scrubbing liquid                to provide a first spent aqueous scrubbing liquid                comprising hydrogen carbonate and/or carbonate, and                having a pH of 7-9, feeding the first spent aqueous                scrubbing liquid to an anode chamber of an                electrolytic cell comprising the anode chamber and                a cathode chamber separated by a membrane,                regenerating the first spent aqueous scrubbing                liquid in the electrolytic cell by electrolysis to                increase the pH of the first spent aqueous                scrubbing liquid in the cathode chamber and to                deplete the first spent aqueous scrubbing liquid of                hydrogen carbonate and carbonate in the anode                chamber by decreasing the pH, and withdrawing                regenerated alkaline, aqueous scrubbing liquid from                the cathode chamber and re-circulating it to the                scrubber, where the regeneration process further                comprises generating gaseous hydrogen in the                cathode chamber and a gaseous mixture of oxygen and                carbon dioxide in the anode chamber by                electrolysis, the step of the scrubbing of the gas                is performed in a first stage and in a second                stage, the regenerated alkaline, aqueous scrubbing                liquid withdrawn from the cathode chamber, is fed                as a second alkaline, aqueous scrubbing liquid to                the second stage of scrubbing downstream of the                first stage of scrubbing, and a second spent                scrubbing liquid resulting from the second stage of                scrubbing, is partly fed as the first scrubbing                liquid to the first stage of scrubbing upstream of                the second stage of scrubbing, the pH of the second                scrubbing liquid is higher than the pH of the first                scrubbing liquid, the gaseous hydrogen is withdrawn                from the cathode chamber, and the gaseous mixture                of oxygen and carbon dioxide is withdrawn from the                anode chamber.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                100 for scrubbing gas comprising carbon dioxide to                deplete the gas of the carbon dioxide, which                comprises a scrubber 200 configured to scrub the                gas with first scrubbing liquid, the electrolytic                cell, and a regeneration arrangement 300 configured                to regenerate the first scrubbing liquid in the                electrolytic cell.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a process scheme of a                system for scrubbing flue gas.
   System (100)
   Scrubber arrangement (200)
   First buffer tank (230)
   Second buffer tank (240)
   Regeneration arrangement (300)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises separating the gaseous mixture of                  oxygen and carbon dioxide into a first stream                  rich in oxygen and/or depleted of carbon dioxide,                  and a second stream rich in carbon dioxide and/or                  depleted of oxygen, withdrawing an aqueous stream                  still comprising some hydrogen carbonate from the                  anode chamber, concentrating the withdrawn                  aqueous stream comprising some hydrogen carbonate                  to provide a concentrated stream comprising                  hydrogen carbonate, and re-circulating the                  concentrated stream comprising hydrogen carbonate                  to the electrolytic cell. The regenerated                  alkaline, aqueous scrubbing liquid withdrawn from                  the cathode chamber is mixed with a part of the                  second spent scrubbing liquid to provide the                  second scrubbing liquid, where the pH of the                  second alkaline, aqueous scrubbing liquid is                  lower than the pH of the regenerated scrubbing                  liquid withdrawn from the cathode chamber, and/or                  a part of the second spent scrubbing liquid is                  mixed with a part of a first spent scrubbing                  liquid resulting from the first stage of                  scrubbing, to provide the first alkaline, aqueous                  scrubbing liquid and the pH of the first                  scrubbing liquid is higher than the pH the first                  spent scrubbing liquid. The carbon dioxide and/or                  oxygen withdrawn from the anode chamber is                  compressed into liquid carbon dioxide and/or                  compressed oxygen. The carbon dioxide is                  dissolved as the hydrogen carbonate and the                  carbonate in the first scrubbing liquid, to                  provide the first scrubbing liquid comprising                  hydrogen carbonate and carbonate. The                  concentrating step is achieved by reversed                  osmosis. The part of the regenerated scrubbing                  liquid is diluted by an aqueous stream and then                  re-circulated to the cathode chamber.Preferred Components: The gas is flue gas or                  exhaust gas. The pH of the second scrubbing                  liquid is 12-14 and the pH of the first scrubbing                  liquid is 8-10. The hydrogen withdrawn from the                  cathode chamber is used as a fuel to provide                  electricity. The fuel is provided in a fuel cell.                  The first alkaline, aqueous scrubbing liquid                  comprises a dissolved metal hydroxide comprising                  dissolved potassium hydroxide, dissolved sodium                  hydroxide and dissolved lithium hydroxide. The                  system further comprises a separator to separate                  the gaseous mixture of oxygen and carbon dioxide                  into a first stream rich in oxygen and/or                  depleted of carbon dioxide, and a second stream                  rich in carbon dioxide and/or depleted of                  oxygen.

PN EP4019488-A1; WO2022137138-A1
TI Providing product composition comprising methane            used for producing synthesis gas composition comprising            carbon monoxide and hydrogen, by contacting gaseous            mixture comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide with            supported methane synthesis catalyst comprising cobalt            to provide methane selectivity.
AU PATERSON A J
AE BP PLC (BRPE-C)
   BP PLC (BRPE-C)
GA 2022850749
AB    NOVELTY - Process for providing a product composition                comprising methane, involves contacting a gaseous                mixture comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide with                a supported methane synthesis catalyst, where the                supported methane synthesis catalyst comprises 1-35                wt.% cobalt on an elemental basis to provide the                product composition with a methane selectivity of                at least 75%.
   USE - The process is used for providing product                composition comprising methane for producing                synthesis gas composition comprising carbon                monoxide and hydrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process can efficiently convert carbon                dioxide to methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for producing a synthesis gas                composition comprising carbon monoxide and                hydrogen, which involves reforming a reforming feed                comprising methane with water and/or oxygen to                produce a reforming product stream comprising                carbon monoxide and hydrogen, where at least a                portion of the methane is produced by the process;                and
   (2) a Fischer-Tropsch process, which involves                contacting a hydrocarbon synthesis mixture                comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen with a                Fischer-Tropsch hydrocarbon synthesis catalyst to                produce a hydrocarbon composition with a                selectivity for 5C+ hydrocarbons of at least 50%                and/or a selectivity for oxygenates of at least                20%, where at least a portion of carbon monoxide                and/or hydrogen is produced by the process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The gaseous mixture                  comprises not greater than 5 wt.% carbon                  monoxide. The molar ratio of hydrogen and the                  carbon dioxide is 1:1-4:1, e.g. 1:1-3.5:1 or                  1:1-3:1 or 1.5:1-4:1, or 1.5:1-3.5:1, or                  1.5:1-3:1, or 2:1-3:1, or 2:1-3.5:1, or 2:1-3:1.                  The at least 50 vol.% of the gaseous mixture is                  hydrogen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen, e.g., at                  least 60 vol.%, at least 70 vol.%, at least 80                  vol.%, or at least 90 vol.%. The supported                  methane synthesis catalyst comprises 2-20 wt.%,                  e.g., 2-15 wt.%, or 2-10 wt.%, or 5-20 wt.%, or                  5-15 wt.%, or 5-10 wt.%, or 7-20 wt.%, or 7-15                  wt.%, or 7-12 wt.%, or 10-20 wt.%, or 10-15 wt.%                  cobalt on an elemental basis. The supported                  methane synthesis catalyst further comprises                  0.5-15 wt.%, or 0.5-10 wt.%, or 0.5-7 wt.%, or                  0.5-5 wt.%, or 1-20 wt.%, or 1-15 wt.%, or 1-10                  wt.%, or 1-5 wt.%, or 2-20 wt.%, or 2-15 wt.%, or                  2-10 wt.%, or 2-5 wt.%, or 5-20 wt.%, or 5-15                  wt.%, or 5-12 wt.%, or 5-10 wt.%, or 7-20 wt.%,                  or 7-15 wt.%, or 7-12 wt.% manganese on an                  elemental basis. Preferred Components: The                  supported methane synthesis catalyst comprises a                  support material comprising alumina, zirconia,                  titania, silica, zinc oxide, and/or ceria. The                  hydrogen comprises green hydrogen (e.g., hydrogen                  generated through electrolysis, wherein the                  electrolysis is powered, at least in part, by                  renewable energy), and/or the carbon dioxide                  comprises captured carbon dioxide or carbon                  dioxide from biomass gasification. Preferred                  Conditions: The contacting is performed at                  150-325 degrees C (e.g., 150-300 degrees C, or                  150-275 degrees C, or 150-250 degrees C, or                  175-325 degrees C, or 175-275 degrees C, or                  175-250 degrees C, or 200-325 degrees C, or                  200-275 degrees C, or 200-250 degrees C). The                  selectivity for methane is at least 80% (e.g., at                  least 85%, or at least 90%, or at least 95%). The                  carbon dioxide is reacted with a 5C+ selectivity                  of not greater than 10%, e.g., not greater than                  8%, or not greater than 7%, or not greater than                  5%, or not greater than 4%, or not greater than                  3%. The product composition is provided with a                  carbon dioxide conversion of at least 5%, e.g.,                  at least 25%.

PN CN114604910-A
TI Obtaining nickel carbonate by mineralizing carbon            dioxide with magnesium and nickel solution used in e.g.            metallurgy, involves injecting magnesium-containing            solution into reactor and introducing ammonia gas into            solution to adjust pH value.
AU LIU Q
   JIN X
   HE M
   LIANG Y
   GAO Y
   LIU W
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2022834708
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining nickel carbonate by mineralizing                carbon dioxide with magnesium and nickel solution,                involves (a) injecting the magnesium-containing                solution into the reactor, introducing ammonia gas                into the magnesium containing nickel solution to                adjust the pH value, and fully stirring to make the                solution be alkaline, (b) continuously introducing                CO2 into the mother liquid, reacting for a certain                time at a certain temperature, filtering to obtain                the magnesium carbonate (or magnesium carbonate                hydrate) solid and solution rich in nickel ammonium                complex, (c) heating the solution containing nickel                ammonium complex to steam ammonia to decompose the                nickel ammonium and reduce the pH of the solution                to obtain nickel carbonate (or nickel carbonate                hydrate) precipitation and precipitation mother                liquor, and (d) returning the ammonia gas separated                from the ammonia distillation for use.
   USE - Method for obtaining nickel carbonate by                mineralizing carbon dioxide with magnesium and                nickel solution used in metallurgy, mechanical                manufacturing, traffic, national defense and                aerospace fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The obtained nickel carbonate has good                ductility, corrosion resistance, high temperature                resistance, high strength and other excellent                properties. The method enables good extraction                effect without using extraction agent, and realizes                separation of magnesium and nickel by utilizing the                pH change, so that to avoid environmental pollution                caused by an organic extractant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pH of the                  mineralized mother liquor is 8-12. The                  mineralization reaction temperature, after                  introducing CO2 is 20-100degrees Celsius, and the                  reaction time is 10-180 minutes. The ammonia                  distillation reaction temperature is                  30-100degrees Celsius.

   DING L
   WU Z
AE UNIV ANHUI NORMAL (UYAI-C)
GA 202264287C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of potassium-doped copper selenide nanosheet array structure material involves obliquely placing foam copper in a mixed solution containing selenium source, alkali source, reducing agent and alkali metal salt, and hydrothermally reacting in a reaction kettle.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing potassium-doped copper selenide nanosheet array structure material used as electrocatalyst for carbon dioxide reduction (all claimed) to ethanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases the electrochemical active area of the catalyst and exposes more catalytic active sites by doping monovalent potassium ions; enhances the conductivity of the catalyst and increases the rate of interfacial charge transfer by doping with monovalent potassium ions; can effectively adjust the electron migration between atoms after doping monovalent potassium ions into copper selenide nanosheets, and potassium transfers electrons to selenium to generate electron-rich selenium sites, so that linear-type carbonyl asteriskCO(asteriskCOL) and bridge-type carbonylasteriskCO(asteriskCOB) intermediates have excellent adsorption effect, thus promoting carbon-carbon coupling to form carbonyl-aldehyde (asteriskCOCHO) intermediate; and improves the catalytic activity of electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction to ethanol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the potassium-doped copper selenide nanosheet array structure material, which has a nanosheet array shape with a lateral size of 300-1300 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the selenium source and the alkali metal salt is 3:(1-3). PN CN113548649-A
TI Low-temperature adsorption process for purifying helium gas, comprises e.g. purifying and treating hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, water, mercury in natural gas, extracting crude helium gas using obtained natural gas, refining, introducing refined gas to activated carbon filter.
AU CHEN Z
   ZHOU L
AE GUANGZHOU YIGAS GASES CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021C30037
AB    NOVELTY - Low-temperature adsorption process for purifying helium gas, comprises (i) purifying and treating hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, water and mercury in natural gas, where the first step is purifying hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide by alcohol amine method, and then using molecular sieve to purify water and mercury, (ii) subjecting the natural gas obtained in step (i) to high pressure condensation to separate alkane and helium in natural gas to obtain crude helium gas, (iii) performing catalytic oxidative dehydrogenation on crude helium gas obtained in step (ii) to obtain refined helium gas, and (iv) introducing the refined helium gas obtained in step (iii) through a filter with a built-in activated carbon filter to obtain high-purity helium gas.
   USE - The low-temperature adsorption process is useful for purifying helium gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts chemical reactions and physical reactions, which is used to purify helium in natural gas and purifying and processing the other components in natural gas; can recycle the purified alkane and meets environmentally friendly benefits; has simple process flow, and certain economic benefits; and is suitable for mass production of helium gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the alcohol amine method includes introducing natural gas into a weak alkaline aqueous solution of alcohol amine-containing desulfurizing tower and adopting chemical reaction to remove hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide; the molecular sieve is divided into dehydrated molecular sieves and mercury-removed molecular sieves, which dehydrates and removes mercury from natural gas; and in the finally obtained natural gas, the residual hydrogen sulfide is required to be less than or equal to 0.004%, the residual carbon monoxide is required to be less than or equal to 0.1% and the residual water is required to be less than 0.001%. The step (ii) requires to be performed in a separation device, which includes a pressure regulating component and a temperature regulating component and requires a pressure of 19-21 MPa and a temperature of -160 to 170degrees Celsius and the alkane is in liquid state. In step (ii), the liquid alkane is discharged from the bottom of the device by drainage method. The step (iii) is required to be carried out in catalytic device, and the catalytic oxidative dehydrogenation includes catalytic dehydrogenation or oxidative dehydrogenation, the catalytic dehydrogenation and oxidative dehydrogenation both requires to be carried out under high temperature conditions and the temperature is determined based on the different oxidants or catalysts. The method further comprises (v) cooling the high-purity helium gas obtained in step (iv) to less than -268degrees Celsius to liquefy the high-purity helium gas, and then storing the liquid high-purity helium gas in storage tank.Preferred Components: In step (ii), the atmospheric liquid methane, atmospheric liquid nitrogen or negative pressure liquid methane is used as cold source.

TI Integrating ammonia desulfurization and            decarbonization involves using desulfurization            circulating liquid to removing portion of sulfur            dioxide, carbon dioxide and free ammonia in process            gas.
AU ZHANG J
   LUO J
AE JIANGSU XINSHIJI JIANGNAN ENVIRONMENTAL (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGNAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION GROUP (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021993790
AB    NOVELTY - Integrating ammonia desulfurization and                decarbonization involves using desulfurization                circulating liquid to removing portion of sulfur                dioxide in the process gas, using the                decarburization circulating liquid to removing                portion of the carbon dioxide in the process gas,                using the desulfurization circulating liquid to                removing the portion of the free ammonia in the                process gas, and returning the desulfurization                circulating liquid to the desulfurization                unit.
   USE - Method for integrating ammonia desulfurization                and decarbonization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device                for implementing the ammonia desulfurization and                decarbonization, which comprises ammonia                desulfurization functional zone, an ammonia                decarbonization functional zone, an ammonia washing                functional zone, an ammonium sulfate post-treatment                system and an ammonium bicarbonate post-treatment                system, using ammonia as desulfurizing and                decarburizing agent, the process gas firstly enters                the desulfurization function area for                desulfurization, generating ammonium sulfate                fertilizer, the process gas after desulfurization                enters into the decarburization function area,                removing the carbon dioxide in the process gas,                generating ammonium bicarbonate fertilizer, the                process gas after decarburization contains free                ammonia, entering into the ammonia washing function                area, using the desulfurization circulating                solution to washing, using process water to washing                after washing, the desulfurization solution                containing ammonia and process aqueous solution and                returning to the desulfurization functional area as                absorbent of desulfurization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide                  removal rate is 30-98%, preferably 40-80%, more                  preferably 45-60%. The volumetric flow rate of                  the process gas in the removal of carbon dioxide                  is 0.5-99% based on the volume of the process gas                  removal of sulfur dioxide, preferably 1-70%, more                  preferably, 5-50%. The pH of the concentrated                  circulating fluid is 1-6, preferably, 2-4.5, the                  concentration of ammonium sulfite is 0-0.2%,                  preferably, 10-60%. The pH of the absorption                  circulating fluid is 4.5-6.5, preferably,                  4.8-6.2, and the concentration of ammonium                  sulfite is 0.1- 3%, preferably, 10-38%. The pH of                  the decarburization circulating liquid is 7-11,                  preferably, 8-9.5, the ammonium bicarbonate                  concentration is 10-22%, and the ammonia/carbon                  dioxide molar ratio is 1.8-3, preferably, 2-2.                  The desulfurization absorption temperature is                  5-55 degrees C, preferably, 20-40 degrees C. The                  decarburization absorption temperature is 5-40                  degrees C, preferably 10-30 degrees C. The                  concentration of the concentrated ammonium                  sulfate solution is controlled at 10-38%,                  preferably, 12-30%, and the pH is controlled at                  2.5-7.5, preferably, 3-5.5. The concentration of                  the second dilute ammonium sulfate solution is                  controlled at 0-5%, preferably 0.02-2%, and the                  pH is controlled at 3-7.

PT P
PN WO2021105726-A1; US2021284585-A1; US11352302-B2; CN114901611-A; EP4065535-A1
TI Diffusing carbon dioxide within concrete mixture            by mixing non-recycled aggregate material with carbon            dioxide gas in pretreatment chamber of concrete            preparation system, and mixing aggregate material with            cement and water.
AU HAMMAD A D
   AL HUNAIDY A S
   FADHEL B A
   AMR I T
   AL H A S
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2021598207
AB    NOVELTY - Diffusing carbon dioxide (CO2) within a                concrete mixture, involves mixing a non-recycled                aggregate material with a CO2 gas in a pretreatment                chamber of a concrete preparation system to form a                CO2 adsorbed aggregate material; transferring the                CO2 adsorbed aggregate material from the                pretreatment chamber into a cement mixing chamber                of the concrete preparation system; and mixing the                CO2 adsorbed aggregate material with cement and                water at 30-60 degrees C to form the concrete                mixture. Mixing the CO2 adsorbed aggregate material                with cement and water releases CO2 from the CO2                adsorbed aggregate material and diffuses CO2 into                the concrete mixture to form a carbonated concrete                mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for diffusing CO2 within                concrete mixture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The systems and methods can carbonate concrete                mixtures with increased CO2 penetration that is                uniformly distributed throughout the concrete                mixture. By uniformly distributing CO2 throughout                the carbonated concrete mixture, the carbonated                concrete mixture may produce hardened concrete                products having increased mechanical strength,                higher CO2 uptake for permanent sequestration, and                expedited production time when compared to concrete                products formed with steam or water as the curing                agent and when compared to concrete products formed                with a CO2 curing agent that is unevenly                distributed throughout the carbonated concrete                mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Method: Alternatively, diffusing                  CO2 within concrete mixture involves mixing a                  non-recycled aggregate material comprising a                  pozzolan material with a CO2 gas in a                  pretreatment chamber of a concrete preparation                  system to form a CO2 adsorbed aggregate material;                  transferring the CO2 adsorbed aggregate material                  from the pretreatment chamber into a cement                  mixing chamber of the concrete preparation system                  through a chamber connection pipe; and mixing the                  CO2 adsorbed aggregate material with cement and                  water at 30-60 degrees C to form the concrete                  mixture, where mixing the CO2 adsorbed aggregate                  material with cement and water releases CO2 from                  the CO2 adsorbed aggregate material and diffuses                  CO2 into the concrete mixture to form a                  carbonated concrete mixture comprising a                  distribution of carbon that varies by less than                  or equal to 10% throughout the volume of the                  carbonated concrete mixture. Mixing the                  non-recycled aggregate material with the CO2 gas                  in the pretreatment chamber involves directing                  the non-recycled aggregate material into the                  pretreatment chamber; and directing the CO2 gas                  from the CO2 source into the pretreatment                  chamber. The CO2 gas is directed from the CO2                  source into the pretreatment chamber at a flow                  rate of 300-1400 ml/minute. Mixing the                  non-recycled aggregate material with the CO2 gas                  in the pretreatment chamber further involves                  agitating the non-recycled aggregate material in                  the pretreatment chamber. The concrete                  preparation system further comprises a cement                  source coupled to the cement mixing chamber by a                  cement input pipe and a water source coupled to                  the cement mixing chamber by a water input pipe;                  and mixing the CO2 adsorbed aggregate material                  with the cement and the water in the cement                  mixing chamber involves directing the cement from                  the cement source into the cement mixing chamber;                  and directing the water from the water source                  into the cement mixing chamber. The CO2 adsorbed                  aggregate material is transferred from the                  pretreatment chamber into the cement mixing                  chamber through a chamber connection pipe.                  Preferred Parameters: The carbonated concrete                  mixture comprises a distribution of carbon that                  varies by less than or equal to 10% or less than                  or equal to 5% throughout the volume of the                  carbonated concrete mixture. The pozzolan                  material comprises CO2 adsorption rate or a peak                  CO2 adsorption rate of greater than or equal to                  6.0x 10-4 mole/s; and a CO2 adsorption capacity                  of greater than or equal to 0.4 millimole/g.                  Preferred Components: The non-recycled aggregate                  material comprises a pozzolan material. The                  pozzolan material comprises a natural pozzolan                  material.

   ZHAO J
   WANG Y
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 202151670J

The silicon sheet divides the terahertz wave after beam expanding into a reflected wave (4a) and a transmission wave (4b). The reflected wave is transmitted to a measured sample (5). The reflected wave is reflected by a gold-plated 60 degree off-axis parabolic mirror (6) and forms a spherical reference light wave (6a) to propagate into a pyroelectric detector (7).

PN US2021102040-A1; WO2021071736-A1; CN114555668-A; US11407874-B2; EP4041792-A1
TI Polyol premix useful for producing closed-celled            and rigid polyurethane foam, comprises polyol            composition comprising polymer polyol, catalyst,            physical blowing agent composition, and carbon dioxide            generating chemical blowing agent.
AU ADKINS R L
   PARKS B W
   APRAHAMIAN S L
   MILLER C C
   CONNOR E E
AE COVESTRO LLC (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO LLC (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO LLC (CVST-C)
GA 202135055P
AB    NOVELTY - Polyol premix comprises (a) a polyol                composition comprising a polymer polyol having a                hydroxyl number of greater than 260 mg potassium                hydroxide/g, (b) a catalyst, (c) at least 50 wt.%                physical blowing agent composition based on the                total weight of the physical blowing agent                composition of a hydrocarbon, and (d) a carbon                dioxide generating chemical blowing agent.
   USE - The polyol premix is useful for producing a                closed-celled and rigid polyurethane foam as a                thermal insulation medium in construction of                refrigerated storage devices, in construction                industry or for thermal insulation of long-distance                heating pipes and containers, and composite article                for refrigeration apparatus, such as a                refrigerator, freezer, or cooler with an exterior                metal shell and interior plastic liner.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyurethane foam exhibits improved                thermal insulation properties, physical and                processing properties, such as compressive                strength, dimensional stability, flow, reactivity                and/or de-mold characteristics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   A closed-celled and rigid polyurethane foam                comprising the reaction product of a reaction                mixture comprising a polyisocyanate, the polyol                composition, the catalyst, the physical blowing                agent composition and the carbon dioxide generating                chemical blowing agent; and
   A process for producing the polyurethane foam,                involving mixing the polyisocyanate with the polyol                composition at an isocyanate index of 90-150 or                120-150.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer polyol                  comprises polymer particles comprising a reaction                  product of a reaction mixture comprising (i) a                  base polyol, (ii) an ethylenically unsaturated                  compound, optionally (iii) a preformed                  stabilizer, and (iv) a free radical initiator.                  The base polyol comprises a polyether polyol                  having a functionality of 2-6 and hydroxyl number                  of at least 300 mg potassium hydroxide/g. The                  ethylenically unsaturated compound comprises                  styrene and acrylonitrile at a weight ratio of                  80:20-20:80. The polyol composition comprises                  10-40 wt.% aromatic amine-initiated polyether                  polyol having a functionality of 2-6 and a                  hydroxyl number of 300-500 mg potassium                  hydroxide/g, or 1-40 wt.% aromatic polyester                  polyol having a hydroxyl number of 150-410 and a                  functionality of 1.5-3 or 1.9-2.5.Preferred Composition: The content of                  ethylene oxide units in the base polyol based on                  the total weight of the base polyol is not                  greater than20 wt.%. The polymer polyol has                  hydroxyl number of at least 280 mg potassium                  hydroxide/g. The polymer polyol is present in an                  amount of at least 30 wt.% based on the total                  weight of the polyol composition. The hydrocarbon                  is present in an amount of at least 90 wt.% based                  on the total weight of the physical blowing agent                  composition.
   EXAMPLE - Typical composition comprised: 42.02 pts. wt.                sucrose and glycerin-initiated polyether polyol;                29.94 pts. wt. ortho-toluenediamine initiated                polyether polyol; 9.82 pts. wt. aromatic polyester                polyol; 2.45 pts. wt. TEGOSTAB B 8465 (RTM:                Silicone surfactant); 0.33 pt. wt. POLYCAT 5 (RTM:                N,N,N',N',N"-pentamethyldiethylenetriamine); 0.78                pt. wt. POLYCAT 8 (RTM:                N,N-dimethylcyclohexanamine); 0.39 pt. wt. POLYCAT                41 (RTM:                N,N',N'-Tris(dimethylaminopropyl)-s-hexahydrotriazine                catalyst); 1.82 pts. wt. water; and 12.45 pts. wt.                cyclopentane.

PN CN212535779-U
TI Safety seal for supercritical carbon dioxide cycle power generation expander, has static ring fixed between sealing shell and main shaft and movably connected with main shaft, where end of static ring is provided with spring.
AU TANG D
   BAO X
   ZHOU Z
   LI K
AE CHENGDU YITONG SEAL CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202118996X
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a special safety seal for supercritical carbon dioxide cycle power generation expander, which is installed on the main shaft box between the expander dry gas seal and the bearing, comprising a sealing shell, a ring groove, a static ring and a movable ring. sealing the normal operation; the sealing gas enters and fills the ring groove between the static ring and the sealing cavity through the connecting hole; at this time, the thrust of the sealing gas to the static ring is greater than the spring force; there is no joint between the static ring and the movable ring. when the sealing occurs leakage, leakage gas through the end face leakage, the sealing cavity pressure is reduced, the thrust of the sealing gas to the static ring is less than the thrust of the spring to the static ring; the spring pushes the static ring to close to the movable ring; the final dynamic and static ring contact, realizing the leakage of the sealing gas, avoiding the high temperature carbon dioxide leakage into the bearing box; protecting the bearing box, so as to avoid the damage range is enlarged. The sealing device is simple in structure, safe and reliable.

AB    NOVELTY - Energy-saving MDEA decarbonization device for                natural gas comprises an absorption tower for                purifying raw gas. The regeneration tower that                desorbs the rich amine liquid, carbon dioxide                compressor for pressurizing desorption gas, and                purified gas cooler for cooling the purified gas. AB    NOVELTY - Mouthpiece (10) comprises (a) a U-shaped                material (20) configured to seat against the outer                face of the dental arch of a person when the                mouthpiece is in the mouth of the person, (b) an                opening (55) defined in U-shaped material in                communication with the oral cavity of the person                when the U-shaped material is seated against the                outer face of the dental arch, and (c) a buccal                retention portion (14) configured for frictional                engagement with the lateral buccal portions (inner                cheeks) of the person.
   USE - The mouthpiece is useful in breathing                apparatus for increasing partial pressure of                carbon-dioxide in blood of subject (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mouthpiece has specific width that is                large enough to prevent dislodgment or removal of                the device from the mouth, is easily-manually                manipulated to effect insertion and removal of the                device, and is easily retained in a person's mouth                without need to clench the teeth which lead to jaw                fatigue, temporal-mandibular joint (TMJ)                dysfunction/pain and other orthodontic                problems.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                breathing apparatus for increasing partial pressure                of carbon-dioxide in blood of a subject.

   Buccal retention portion (14)
   Duct portion (16)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Product: The U-shaped material                  comprises opposite arms arranged to-be-positioned                  between the dental arch and the lateral buccal                  portions of the person. The buccal retention                  portion comprises a bulbous protrusion on each of                  the opposite arms, protruding laterally outward                  from the U-shaped material into contact with the                  lateral buccal portions of the person. The                  mouthpiece has a lateral width spanning the                  bulbous protrusions, where lateral width is                  greater than the inter-commissural distance of                  the person's mouth when the mouth is at least                  partially open. The bulbous protrusion comprises                  friction enhancing surface features defined on an                  outer surface of protrusions facing the lateral                  buccal portion of the person. The surface                  features include treads that are vertically                  arranged relative to a lateral width of the                  mouthpiece spanning the bulbous protrusions, and                  are fore-aft spaced apart in relative to the                  lateral width of the mouthpiece to define valleys                  between them. The U-shaped material defines an                  opening in each of the opposite arms in fluid                  communication with the valleys defined between                  the treads. The bulbous protrusions further                  define a fore-aft channel through the treads. The                  mouthpiece further comprises (d) a duct portion                  extending from U-shaped material away from the                  dental arch when the mouthpiece is in the mouth                  of the person, where duct portion defines at                  least one channel in fluid communication with                  opening and extending to a second opening outside                  the mouth of the person, and (e) a tooth                  engagement portion arranged to contact one or                  more teeth of the person when the device is                  inside the person's mouth. The duct portion                  includes (i) a septum extending along a length of                  the duct portion to define two adjacent channels,                  (ii) a curved portion between opening and second                  opening, where curved portion curved downward                  from opening over the chin of the person when the                  mouthpiece is in the mouth of the person, and                  (iii) an interface portion between opening and                  curved portion, where interface portion sized to                  extend the curved portion away from the chin of                  the person when the mouthpiece is in the mouth of                  the person. The U-shaped material, buccal                  retention portion and duct portion are integrally                  formed as one piece. The U-shaped material and                  buccal retention portion are integrally formed as                  one piece. The U-shaped material is formed of a                  resiliently pliant material so that the opposite                  arms are manually pinched towards each other. The                  material is silicone. The U-shaped material                  includes opposite arms arranged to be positioned                  between the dental arch and the lateral buccal                  portions of the person. The tooth engagement                  portion includes a wing arranged on each of                  opposite arms and projecting laterally inward                  into the oral cavity of the person when the                  device is inside the person's mouth, and wing                  arranged on the U-shaped material to contact at                  least one of the molars of the person.
