PT P
PN CN111434382-A
TI Supported vanadium oxide promoted rhodium-based            catalyst used for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide and            carbon monoxide to produce ethanol comprises metal            rhodium as active component, and metal vanadium as            auxiliary agent supported on carrier.
AU GONG J
   WANG G
   MU R
   ZHAO Z
   ZENG L
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 202071106T
AB    NOVELTY - A supported vanadium oxide promoted                rhodium-based catalyst comprises 1-5 wt.% metal                rhodium as active component, and not greater than 1                wt.% metal vanadium as auxiliary agent supported on                carrrier. A rhodium-oxygen-vanadium bond is formed                between oxygen and metal rhodium forming                rhodium-vanadium oxides interface site.
   USE - The supported vanadium oxide promoted                rhodium-based catalyst is used for hydrogenation of                carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide to produce                ethanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst remains stable for a long time,                and has higher ethanol selectivity than traditional                catalyst, low operating pressure, improved catalyst                activity and the ethanol selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the catalyst comprising                soaking carrier in solution containing metal                rhodium and vanadium, taking out, and calcining                under air atmosphere at 400-600 degrees C for 1-6                hours; and
   (2) application of catalyst comprising                pressing catalyst into 20-40 mesh granular                catalyst, adding to fixed bed reactor, subjecting                to reduction reaction with 10-15 vol.% nitrogen and                hydrogen mixture at 400-500 degrees C for 1-2                hours, adding nitrogen gas, pressurizing to 30-50                bar, adjusting temperature to 200-300 degrees C,                adding reaction gas, and reacting.
   The molar ratio of carbon dioxide and hydrogen                is 1-5:1. The balance gas is nitrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The solution containing                  metal rhodium and vanadium is prepared by                  dispersing soluble rhodium salt and vanadium salt                  in deionized water. The method further comprises                  naturally cooling calcined product to 20-25                  degrees C. Preferred Components: The carrier is                  MCM-41 molecular sieve, mesoporous silica,                  alumina and/or zeolite. The soluble rhodium salt                  is rhodium trichloride. The soluble vanadium salt                  is ammonium metavanadate.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN111205927-A
TI Extracting peony essential oil involves collecting fresh peony petals, washing, freeze-drying and crushing to obtain peony powder, adding peony powder to the mixed solvent and mixing well to obtain a mixture.
AU LI W
   ZHENG J
AE HEBEI RUILONG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 202049777H
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting peony essential oil involves collecting fresh peony petals, washing, freeze-drying and crushing to obtain peony powder, adding peony powder to the mixed solvent and mixing well to obtain a mixture. The mixed solvent comprises 0.01-0.03 pts. wt. glacial acetic acid, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. diethyl ether, 1.2-3.5 pts. wt. polydimethylsiloxane, 2.5-6.2 pts. wt. dichloromethane. The mixture is continuously extracted with supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, the extraction temperature is 35-40 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 25-30 mega pascal, the flow rate is 22-25 kg/h, and the extraction time is 1-1.5 hours. The extracted carbon dioxide fluid is separated, the flow rate of the carbon dioxide fluid is 5 to 13 kg/h, and the separator pressure is 6 to 7 mega pascal. The temperature is 38-42 degrees C, and extraction for 1.5-2.5 hours. The extract is discharged from the bottom of the separator, after standing, the peony essential oil is obtained by oil-water separation.
   USE - Method for extracting peony essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to extract peony essential oil that solves the low yield of extracting essential oil of Mudanhua by the existing technology.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The ratio of the mixed solvent to the peony pollen is (1.7-3): 10. The ratio of the mixed solvent to the peony pollen is 2.3:10. The freeze-drying temperature is (-)10-(-)25 degrees C.

PN CN111068646-A; CN111068646-B
TI Preparing zinc tin oxide catalyst for formic acid            production by carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction,            involves dissolving dimethylimidazole in methanol to            obtain solution A, dissolving zinc nitrate in methanol            to obtain solution B.
AU WU J
   LIU G
   GUO X
   LI B
   ZI Z
AE QINGDAO INST BIOENERGY & BIOPROCESS TECH (CAQD-C)
GA 2020381261
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing zinc tin oxide catalyst involves                dissolving dimethylimidazole in methanol to obtain                solution A, dissolving zinc nitrate in methanol to                obtain solution B, and adding A to B dropwise at                20-50 degrees C, controlling the temperature at                20-70 degrees C and stirring for 0-8 hours, cooling                and reducing the temperature, filtering the product                with suction, washing and drying to prepare ZIF-8.                The stannous salt and prepared ZIF-8 are mixed, and                then placed in muffle furnace from room temperature                to 400-600 degrees C, heating rate is 1-20 degrees                C/minute, calcined for 2-8 hours, then slowly                reduced to room temperature to obtain zinc tin                oxide catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing zinc tin oxide catalyst                for formic acid production by carbon dioxide                electrochemical reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare zinc tin oxide                catalyst that is beneficial to increase application                efficiency, has better formic acid selectivity,                reduces energy consumption, production costs in                large-scale applications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for producing formic acid by carbon dioxide                electrochemical reduction using zinc-tin oxide                catalyst, which involves mixing the zinc tin oxide                catalyst, membrane solution and isopropyl alcohol                ultrasonically, connecting to carbon cloth and                connecting with electrode clip as working                electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The concentration of                  the solution A is 5-10g/100ml. The concentration                  of the solution B is 3-6g /100ml. The molar ratio                  between zinc, tin and oxygen is measured by                  ZnxSn(1-x)Oy, where x is 0.95-0.01, y is                  2-x-1.

PT P
PN CN109759109-A; CN109759109-B
TI Nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst preparation for catalyzing hydrogenation of nitrobenzene compounds to aniline compounds, involves taking activated carbon containing carbon dioxide leaving group.
AU LU C
   ZHOU Y
   ZHANG X
   NIE J
   LIU Q
   FENG F
   ZHANG Q
   ZHAO J
   XU X
   GUO L
   LV J
   MA L
   CEN J
   LI X
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201946503C
AB    NOVELTY - Nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst preparation involves taking activated carbon containing carbon dioxide leaving group, and then treating activated carbon with a treating agent. The treatment agent is a mixture of perchloric acid, sodium chlorate, and nitric acid to obtain pretreated activated carbon. The primary amine organic substance is dissolved in water, mixed with activated carbon and then placed in a hydrothermal kettle for hydrothermal reaction in a carbon dioxide atmosphere. The sample is separated after completing the reaction, and then washed and dried to obtain nitrogen modified activated carbon. The noble metal is supported on the obtained nitrogen-modified activated carbon by ultraviolet light reduction to obtain the nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst. The noble metal is palladium or platinum.
   USE - Method for preparing nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst for catalyzing hydrogenation of nitrobenzene compounds to aniline compounds, preferably nitrobenzene compound selected from nitrate-based benzene, o-nitrotoluene, m-nitrotoluene, p-nitrophenyltoluene, p-nitrophenol, m-nitrophenol or o-nitrophenol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with high conversion rate, good selectivity, good stability and fast hydrogenation rate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic transmission electron microscopy image of the prepared catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The activated carbon is uniformly dispersed into the treatment agent solution to obtain suspension, which is gradually heated at 25-50 degrees C under stirring for 5-15 hours. The obtained suspension is cooled to room temperature, suction filtered, and washed. The primary amine organic substance contains two amine groups, preferably 1,2-propylenediamine and diamine or hexamethylenediamine.

TI New polymer comprising fused bicyclo ketone repeat            units derived from 2-adamantan-1-yl-oxirane compounds            and carbon dioxide, used in sacrificial polymer            composition.
AU SETO K
   BELL A
   BEER A
AE PROMERUS LLC (RORU-C)
   PROMERUS LLC (RORU-C)
   SUMITOMO BAKELITE CO LTD (SUMB-C)

GA 2019373022
AB    NOVELTY - Polymer comprising fused bicyclo ketone repeat                units (IV) is new, where the repeat unit is derived                from 2-adamantan-1-yl-oxirane compounds (I) and                carbon dioxide.
   USE - The polymer is useful in sacrificial polymer                composition (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polymer comprising fused bicyclo ketone repeat                units of formula (IV) is new, where the repeat unit                is derived from 2-adamantan-1-yl-oxirane compounds                of formula (I) and carbon dioxide.
   Either R1, R2 = H, methyl, ethyl, linear or                branched (3-12C)alkyl, (6-10C)aryl,                (6-10C)aryl(1-12C)alkyl, (5-10C)heteroaryl or                (5-10C)heteroaryl(1-3C)alkyl; or
   R1CR2 = (3-12C)cycloalkyl ring optionally                containing at least one N, O or S; and
   R3-R5 = H, methyl, ethyl, linear or branched                (3-12C)alkyl, (3-12C)cycloalkyl,                (6-12C)bicycloalkyl, (7-14C)tricycloalkyl,                (10-14C)tetracycloalkyl, (6-10C)aryl,                (6-10C)aryl(1-12C)alkyl, (5-10C)heteroaryl,                (5-10C)heteroaryl(1-3C)alkyl, (1-12C)alkoxy,                (3-12C)cycloalkoxy, (6-12C)bicycloalkoxy,                (7-14C)tricycloalkoxy, (10-14C)tetracycloalkoxy,                (6-10C)aryloxy(1-3C)alkyl,                (5-10C)heteroaryloxy(1-3C)alkyl, (6-10C)aryloxy,                (5-10C)heteroaryloxy, (1-6)acyloxy or halo.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing (IV); and
   (2) a sacrificial polymer composition                comprising the polymer, a photoacid generator (PAG)                and/or photo-base generator (PBG), and a                solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): Preparation of (I)                  comprises reacting (I) with carbon dioxide in the                  presence of a ligand-supported metal                  catalyst;
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: (I) is                  2-(adamantan-1-yl)oxirane,                  2-(adamantan-1-yl)-3-methyloxirane,                  2-(adamantan-1-yl)-3-phenyloxirane,                  2-(3-methyladamantan-1-yl)oxirane,                  2-(3-ethyladamantan-1-yl)oxirane and                  2-(3-fluoroadamantan-1-yl)oxirane. The weight                  average molecular weight of the polymer is                  1000-300000. The polymer exhibits a glass                  transition temperature of at least 100 degrees C.                  The polymer further comprises at least one repeat                  units of formula (-C(=O)-O-CH2-C(R6)(R7)-O-) (V)                  derived from a corresponding epoxide of formula                  (II) and carbon dioxide, preferably (II) is                  2-methyloxirane, 2-ethyloxirane,                  2-isopropyloxirane, 2-(tert-butyl)oxirane,                  2-(tert-pentyl)oxirane, 2-cyclopentyloxirane,                  2-cyclohexyloxirane, 2-cycloheptyloxirane,                  2-cyclooctyloxirane, 1-oxaspiro(2.5)octane,                  1-oxaspiro(2.7)decane or                  2-norbornaneoxirane.Either R6, R7 = H, methyl, ethyl, linear or                  branched (3-12C)alkyl, (3-12C)cycloalkyl,                  (6-10C)aryl, (6-10C)aryl(1-12C)alkyl and                  heteroaryl; orR6CR7 = (3-12C)cycloalkyl ring optionally                  containing at least one N, O or S.Preferred Process: The process includes a                  halogenated solvent. The ligand-supported metal                  catalyst is chromium, manganese, iron, cobalt,                  nickel, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, iridium,                  platinum or zinc. The catalyst is a cobalt metal                  complex of formulae (VI) or (VII). The process                  further comprises a co-catalyst, which is                  N,N-dimethylpyridin-4-amine.R = methyl, ethyl, linear or branched                  3-12C-alkyl, 3-12C-cycloalkyl or 6-10C-aryl,                  preferably tert-butyl; andY1 = halo (preferably Cl), acetate,                  trifluoroacetate, benzoate, tosylate, triflate,                  mesylate, C6F5CO2 or azide.Preferred Composition: The composition                  further comprises a thermally activated acid                  generator (TAG); and optional additives selected                  from the group consisting of sensitizers,                  adhesion promoters, antioxidants, antioxidant                  synergists and fillers. The solvent is anisole,                  n-butyl acetate, dimethylacetamide,                  cyclopentenone, cyclohexanone, gamma                  -butyrolactone and/or                  propyleneglycol-monomethylether acetate. The PAG                  or PBG is e.g.                  (4-isopropylphenyl)(p-tolyl)iodonium                  (perfluorophenyl)trihydroborate and/or                  2,3,4,6,7,8-hexahydro-1H-pyrimido(1,2-a)pyrimidin-9-ium                  2-(3-benzoylphenyl)propanoate. The optional                  additives are 1-chloro-4-propylthioxanthane,                  isopropyl thioxanthone, phenothiazine,                  benzoquinone or 1,1,1-tris(hydroxymethyl)propane.                  The PAG or PBG loading is 0.15-10 phr polymer                  inclusive.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1, R2 = H, methyl, ethyl or phenyl; and
   R3-R5 = H, methyl, ethyl or F.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN107935058-A; CN107935058-B
TI Preparation of battery-grade cobalt sulfate by stirring crude cobalt carbonate with purified water, carbon dioxide gas assisted ultrasonically washing, filtering, adding purified water and reacting with concentrated sulfuric acid.
AU HUANG Y
   XU D
   ZHONG H
   HE Y
   HUANG T
AE GUANGDONG JIANA ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   HUNAN JIANA ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 201832788G
AB    NOVELTY - A battery-grade cobalt sulfate is prepared by stirring crude cobalt carbonate with purified water, carbon dioxide gas assisted ultrasonically washing, filtering to obtain filter residue, adding into dissolution crystallization tank, adding purified water and slurry, adding concentrated sulfuric acid to react to obtain cobalt sulfate solution, opening cooling water circulation system, cooling and crystallizing cobalt sulfate solution, and filtering to obtain cobalt sulfate crystal and crystallization mother liquid.
   USE - Method for preparing battery-grade cobalt sulfate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids traditional extraction high cost, long process, low efficiency and high energy consumption caused by traditional evaporating and crystallizing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A battery-grade cobalt sulfate is prepared by stirring crude cobalt carbonate with purified water at speed of 100-300 revolutions/minute (rpm), carbon dioxide gas assisted ultrasonically washing for 0.5-2 hours, filtering to obtain filter residue, adding into dissolution crystallization tank, adding purified water and slurry according to liquid-solid ratio of 0.5:1-1.5:1, adding concentrated sulfuric acid to react to reaction end point of 1.5-3.0 to obtain cobalt sulfate solution, opening cooling water circulation system, cooling and crystallizing cobalt sulfate solution, and filtering to obtain cobalt sulfate crystal and crystallization mother liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Washing is done at liquid to solid ratio of 3:1-6:1, carbon dioxide amount of 0.5-2.0 L/minute, ultrasonic wave frequency of 20 KH and wave intensity of 0.5-2 W/cm3, and sending filter liquid to wastewater plant for softening. Reaction is done at stirring speed of 200-500 rpm.

   USE - Preparation of carbon disulfide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of carbon                disulfide, improving utilization rate of raw                material and enhancing separation effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of carbon disulfide, involves                quantitatively adding sulfur to carbon sulfide                reactor after melting, adding metal sulfide                catalyst to reactor, introducing dried carbon                monoxide into the reactor, mixing with sulfur vapor                and heating at 350-450 degrees C to obtain carbonyl                sulfide, removing unreacted sulfur, cooling,                washing with alkali and removing impurities to                obtain carbonyl sulfide and extracting carbonyl                sulfide condensate, increasing the pressure of                rectification column and distilling carbonyl                sulfide to obtain gaseous carbonyl sulfide,                controlling the temperature of tower at 50-250                degrees C and disintegrating to obtain carbon                disulfide and carbon dioxide, separating carbon                disulfide and carbon dioxide to obtain pure carbon                disulfide, passing carbon dioxide to carbonate                compound production plant and separating unreacted                carbonyl sulfide.

PN CN114307992-A
TI Spherical solid amine carbon dioxide absorbing material with good high swelling rate and improved the wet carbon dioxide adsorption capacity, formed by taking polyethylene imine as substrate, then cross linking and curing with cross linking agent.
AU CHEN Y
   CHEN S
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 202256235A
AB    NOVELTY - Spherical solid amine carbon dioxide absorbing material, formed by taking polyethylene imine as substrate, then cross linking and curing with cross linking agent, where the substrate comprising a temperature-sensitive group introduced by Michael addition, the grain diameter D50 of the spherical solid amine carbon dioxide absorbing material is 2-5 microns, and the swelling rate is 110-270%.
   USE - Used as spherical solid amine carbon dioxide absorbing material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has good high swelling rate, capable of reducing the mass transfer resistance of carbon dioxide in the presence of water, so as to improve the wet carbon dioxide adsorption capacity, and capable of fast desorption at a lower temperature; reduces the regeneration energy consumption of the adsorption material. The method is simple, and convenient, and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing spherical solid amine carbon dioxide absorbing material comprising (1) adding polyethylene imine and N-alkyl acrylamide substance into water, mixing uniformly and performing Michael addition reaction to obtain aqueous solution containing modified polyethyleneimine, (2) adding the liquid paraffin, Span-80 (Is a biodegradable surfactant based on a natural fatty acid (oleic acid) and sugar alcohol sorbitol) into the aqueous solution in the step (1), mixing uniformly, then adding cross linking agent for crosslinking reaction, finishing reaction, centrifuging, washing, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The temperature-sensitive group comprises N-alkyl acrylamide substance and N-isopropyl acrylamide. The cross linking agent comprises ethylene glycol diglycidyl ether, polyethylene glycol diglycidyl ether, butanediol diglycidyl ether, polyhexanediol diglycidyl ether, hexanediol diglycidyl ether, and/or neopentyl glycol diglycidyl ether. The mass ratio of the polyethyleneimine to the N-alkyl acrylamide is 1: (0.1-1). The mass ratio of the polyethyleneimine to the cross linking agent 1: (0.5-1.5). The mass ratio of the polyethylene imine to liquid paraffin is 1: (1.8-2.2). The mass ratio of the polyethyleneimine to Span-80 (Is a biodegradable surfactant based on a natural fatty acid (oleic acid) and sugar alcohol sorbitol) is 1: (0.07-0.08).Preferred Method: The method cross linking at 50-80degreesC for 60-75 hours, preferably 50-70degreesC for 2.5- 4 hours. The step (ii) comprises uniformly mixing and stirring at a rate of 400-600 revolutions/minute for 12-18 minutes.

TI Hydrogenation of carbon dioxide for producing oxo-synthesis syngas-containing composition, involves contacting copper-zinc-zirconium oxide catalyst with feed stream containing carbon dioxide to produce stream containing carbon monoxide.
AU MAMEDOV A
   REA C
   SHAIKH S
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   MAMEDOV A (MAME-Individual)
   REA C (REAC-Individual)
   SHAIKH S (SHAI-Individual)
GA 201809033X
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, involves contacting copper-zinc-zirconium metal oxide catalyst (112) with reactant feed comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide. The reaction occurs at a temperature of at least 550 degrees C and a pressure of at least 0.5 MPa to produce a product stream comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide. The volume ratio of hydrogen to carbon dioxide in the feed stream, is at least 0.5:1. The molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide is 0.7-1.4.
   USE - Hydrogenation of carbon dioxide for producing oxo-synthesis syngas-containing composition which is used as intermediate or feed material in hydroformylation reaction to form oxo-product e.g. 2C or more alcohol, alkyl ether, and dimethyl ether (all claimed), formaldehyde, acetic acid, ammonia, and fertilizers. Can also be used for aliphatic oxygenate production, olefin synthesis, carbonylation of methanol, carbonylation of olefins, and reduction of iron oxide in steel production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently produces highly pure water-free syngas-containing composition.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce syngas using a combined carbon dioxide- and hydrogen-containing reactant feed gas and copper-zinc-zirconium mixed metal oxide catalyst.
   System (100)
   Combined reactant gas source (102)
   Reactor (104)
   Collection device (106)
   Inlet (108)
   Copper-zinc-zirconium mixed metal oxide catalyst (112)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further involves removing carbon dioxide from the product stream. Preferred Conditions: The reaction temperature is preferably 580 degrees C, and the reaction pressure is preferably 2.5-3.5 MPa. The hydrogenation process involves passing hydrogen gas at a flow rate of 30-70 mL/minute, and carbon dioxide gas at a flow rate of 30-70 mL/minute. The carbon dioxide conversion rate is at least 20%. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide in the syngas-containing composition is preferably 0.9:1-1.1:1. The syngas-containing composition preferably comprises 60-65 mol% carbon dioxide, 4-5 mol% methane, 16-17 mol% carbon monoxide, and 15-16 mol% hydrogen. The copper-zinc-zirconium metal oxide comprises 50-60 wt.%, preferably 55.19 wt.% copper oxide, 20-30 wt.%, preferably 24.9 wt.% zinc oxide, and 15-25 wt.%, preferably 19.9 wt.% zirconium dioxide. The volume ratio of hydrogen to carbon dioxide is 0.5:1-2:1, preferably 0.5:1-1:1.

PN CN114959148-A
TI Electric furnace iron-making method used in steel            industry, involves e.g. adding intermediate frequency            electric heating induction coil, uniformly mixing            reactant, adding adhesive, and forming continuous            production of feeding on top of furnace and molten iron            at bottom of furnace.
AU LIU S
AE LIU S (LIUS-Individual)
GA 2022B5278V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves: (i) adding intermediate                frequency electric heating induction coil around                blast furnace bottom furnace cylinder, matching                intermediate frequency conversion device, heating                and melting iron in furnace cylinder by electric                heating, and providing high temperature environment                in furnace; (ii) adjusting according to requirement                of carbon content and sulfur content in molten                iron, uniformly mixing reactant, adding adhesive,                and drying; (iii) pre-filling bottom intermediate                frequency induction furnace cylinder with pig iron,                filling prepared furnace material from blast                furnace, heating and melting pig iron in furnace                cylinder, preheating furnace material by waste                heat, generating iron and carbon dioxide gas by                chemical reaction, and discharging carbon dioxide                gas; and (iv) forming continuous production of                feeding on top of furnace and molten iron at bottom                of furnace.
   USE - Iron-making method of electric furnace used in                steel industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The electric furnace iron-making method is                environment-friendly, safe and reliable, convenient                for operation processing, and has good                popularization prospect and commercial value. The                method does not need to build sintering system and                hot-blast stove system, and realizes low device                investment, less carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide                gas emission.

PN CN114811624-A
TI Purification of dimethyl formamide-containing            waste gas and production of nitrous oxide, involves            mineralizing dimethyl formamide, carbon and hydrogen            into carbon dioxide and water, and converting nitrogen            into nitrous oxide using lanthanum-manganese aluminate            material loaded with precious metal.
AU CHENG J
   ZHAO T
   HAO Z
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV CHINESE ACAD SCI (UCAS-C)
GA 2022A21505
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of dimethyl formamide                (DMF)-containing waste gas and production of                nitrous oxide involves completely mineralizing DMF                in the exhaust gas, mineralizing carbon and                hydrogen into carbon dioxide and water, and                converting nitrogen into nitrous oxide using                catalyst and oxygen in the air as oxidant. The                catalyst is lanthanum-manganese aluminate material                loaded with 0.1-2 %mass precious metal.
   USE - Purification of dimethyl formamide-containing                waste gas and production of nitrous oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not produce ammonia, nitric                oxide, nitrogen dioxide, organic matter and other                impurities during conversion of nitrogen into                nitrous oxide, effectively reduces the difficulty                and cost of nitrous oxide purification in the later                stage, and is safe and                environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purification of dimethyl formamide                (DMF)-containing waste gas and production of                nitrous oxide involves completely mineralizing DMF                in the exhaust gas, mineralizing carbon and                hydrogen into carbon dioxide and water, and                converting nitrogen into nitrous oxide using                catalyst and oxygen in the air as oxidant. The                catalyst is lanthanum-manganese aluminate material                loaded with 0.1-2 %mass precious metal and is of                formula: M-LaMnxAlyO19 where, x is 1-4, y is 8-11                and M is precious metal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  DMF is 0.1-2%, preferably 0.1-1%, gas space                  velocity is 1000-9000 hour-1, preferably                  1000-6000 hour-1and the reaction temperature is                  200-250&#8451;.Preferred Process: The catalyst is prepared                  by (s1) dissolving nitrate of lanthanum,                  manganese and aluminum in a molar ratio of                  1:1-4:8-11 into water at 50-70&#8451;,                  preferably 70&#8451;, (s2) adding the                  solution into saturated ammonium carbonate                  solution, maintaining pH at 8.5-9.5, stirring and                  aging for 3-6 hours, preferably 5 hours, and                  centrifuging, (s3) drying the sample at                  130&#8451; for 12-15 hours, preferably                  120&#8451; for 15 hours, grinding, placing in                  a corundum crucible, raising the temperature to                  500-600&#176;C, preferably 600&#176;C at                  5&#8451;/minute for 3-5 hours, preferably 3                  hours, and raising the temperature to                  1200-1400&#176;C, preferably 1300&#8451;                  from 600&#8451; at 2&#8451; /minute for                  4-6 hours, preferably 5 hours, and roasting to                  obtain lanthanum-manganese-aluminum oxide                  (LaMn1-4Al8-11O19) composite metal oxide                  material, (s4) loading 0.1-2 %mass, preferably                  0.1-1 %mass noble metal nitrate to                  lanthanum-manganese-aluminum-oxide by                  isovolumetric impregnation method and roasting at                  500-600&#8451;, preferably 500&#8451; for                  2-6 hours, preferably 2 hours.

PT P
PN CN114759195-A
TI Non-agglomerant cathode composite material useful            as cathode in lithium-carbon dioxide batteries, where            cathode material is composite material in which nickel            oxide is anchored on nickel foam.
AU ZHANG C
   WU Y
   HE F
   QIAO C
   REN M
   LIU Q
   YAO J
   LIU W
   JI Y
AE UNIV QILU TECHNOLOGY (UYQQ-C)
GA 202294925H
AB    NOVELTY - Non-agglomerant cathode composite material, is                claimed. The cathode material is a composite                material in which nickel oxide is anchored on                nickel foam. The composite material is prepared by                soaking foam nickel in diluted hydrochloric acid,                removing surface oxide layer, preparing oxalic acid                solution as electrolyte solution, using                two-electrode system, using electrochemical working                station to apply constant voltage to the electrode,                performing electrochemical oxidation to the foam                nickel and oxalate solution to obtain solid A,                washing solid A with water and ethanol, drying in                vacuum, and calcining foam nickel loaded nickel                oxide composite material by high temperature                calcining.
   USE - The composite material is useful as cathode in                lithium-carbon dioxide batteries (claimed), e.g.                rechargeable lithium-carbon dioxide battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation, green and                environment-friendly. The electrode does not need                to add any agglomerant, which avoids the covering                of part of the active site, improves the catalytic                performance of the electrode, avoids the side                reaction caused by the decomposition of the                adhesive agent under the high voltage, and improves                the stability of the battery. The non-agglomerant                composite material electrode prepared by the method                can be recycled, which reduces the manufacturing                cost and avoids the waste of the resource.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparation method of non-agglomerant cathode                composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The two-electrode                  system is the working electrode is nickel foil or                  graphite, and the counter electrode is foamed                  nickel. The solid A is nickel foam supported                  nickel (II) oxalate composite material.

TI Membrane assembly for use in e.g. carbon dioxide            separation applications, has porous membrane and            gas-affinity material which is non-continuously            deposited and/or is of irregular thickness, and is            suitable to dissolve gas to be separated.
AU ASHIROV T
   COSKUN A
AE UNIV FRIBOURG (UYFR-Non-standard)
   UNIV FRIBOURG (UYFR-Non-standard)
GA 2022850704
AB    NOVELTY - The assembly has a porous membrane and a                gas-affinity material which is non-continuously                deposited and/or is of irregular thickness, and                which is suitable to adsorb or dissolve a gas to be                separated from a mixture of gases. The porous                membrane is in contact with or deposited on the                porous substrate. The porous substrate is                preferably permeable to the gases in the mixture,                where the assembly exhibits saturation when a                specific amount of the gas is adsorbed, absorbed,                or dissolved and a permeate ratio of the membrane                during operation changes when the saturation is                reached.
   USE - Membrane assembly for use in gas separation                applications such as carbon dioxide separation                applications, hydrogen separation applications and                natural gas purification application. Can also be                used for water purification applications, solvent                treatment applications and biomedical                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane assembly is provided with                high-energy efficiency, low-cost, robustness,                reliability, and scalability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a method for producing a membrane                assembly;
   (2) a method for separating gases from a                mixture of gases; and
   (3) a method for treating a membrane assembly                to an initial separation capacity of the                membrane.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The transition metals                  (d block) are ferric, copper, zinc, manganese,                  ruthenium, gold, silver, iridium or platinum. TI Catalyst for copolymerization of carbon dioxide and epoxy compound used in preparation of polycarbonate applied in agricultural mulch film, is a ternary composite catalyst of chitin-supported diethylzinc and yttrium trifluoroacetate.
AU CHEN Q
   QIAN J
   CHEN S
   JI Z
   HE M
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 202230075F
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst is a ternary composite catalyst of chitin-supported diethylzinc and yttrium trifluoroacetate, and is a complex formed by the reaction between diethylzinc and the hydroxyl group on chitin, and then complexed with yttrium trifluoroacetate.
   USE - Catalyst for copolymerization of carbon dioxide and epoxy compound used in preparation of polycarbonate applied in fields such as agricultural mulch film and disposable packaging (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ternary copolymerization of cyclohexane oxide and ethylene oxide has good catalytic activity, and can improve the adhesion of traditional rare earth ternary catalysts while maintaining a high molecular weight and ester link content. The catalyst can remove most of the catalyst only by filtration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a preparation of the catalyst, which involves under the nitrogen atmosphere, adding diethylzinc, yttrium trifluoroacetate, chitin and tetrahydrofuran into a closed reactor with magnetic stirring, stirring the reaction, distilling and concentrating;
   (2) An application of ternary composite catalyst of chitin loaded diethyl zinc and yttrium trifluoroacetate prepared by the preparation method in preparing polycarbonate by catalyzing carbon dioxide, epoxy cyclohexane and ethylene oxide;
   (3) The polycarbonate obtained by the application, comprises the weight-average molecular weight of more than 100000, and the content of the ester segment chain is more than 90%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the diethyl zinc, the yttrium trifluoroacetate and the chitin is 20:1:5-20. In the tetrahydrofuran, 20 ml of tetrahydrofuran is added for every 0.01 mole of diethylzinc. The molar ratio of the epoxy cyclohexane, ethylene oxide and the diethyl zinc added to the preparation catalyst is 20:5:0.25-2. The 5 ml of tetrahydrofuran is correspondingly added to each 0.1 mole of epoxy cyclohexane.Preferred Conditions: The conditions of the stirring reaction are stirring at 200-400 rpm at 60degrees Celsius for 2.5 hours.Preferred Process: The application involves under nitrogen atmosphere, adding the ternary composite catalyst of the chitin-loaded diethylzinc and yttrium trifluoroacetate, epoxycyclohexane, ethylene oxide and tetrahydrofuran into a dry 300-ml autoclave, introducing carbon dioxide, and adjusting the pressure to 4 MPa, stirring the reaction at 1000 rpm at 80 degrees Celsius for 9 hours to obtain polycarbonate.

TI Curable composition for forming cured fluoropolymer product, contains filler containing surface-reacted calcium carbonate which is reaction product of natural ground or precipitated calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide.
AU WELKER M
   RENTSCH S
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 202216774G
AB    NOVELTY - A curable composition comprises crosslinkable fluorine-containing polymer and a filler containing surface-reacted calcium carbonate and/or ultrafine calcium carbonate. The surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction product of natural ground calcium carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide and one or more oxonium ion donors. The carbon dioxide is formed in situ by oxonium ion donor treatment and/or is supplied from external source.
   USE - Curable composition used for forming cured fluoropolymer product e.g. articles such as O-ring seals, shaft seals, gaskets, tubing, valve stem seals, fuel hose, tank seals, diaphragms, flexi liners for pumps, mechanical seals, pipe coupling, valve lines, military flare blinders, electrical connectors, fuel joints, roll covers, firewall seals and clips for jet engines (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The curable composition produces cured fluoropolymer product having increased tear resistance and elongation at break.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) cured fluoropolymer product, which is formed from the curable fluoropolymer composition;
   (2) method for producing cured fluoropolymer, which involves providing crosslinkable fluorine containing polymer, a filler chosen from surface reacted calcium carbonate, ultrafine calcium carbonate or their mixtures, combining the fluorine containing polymer and curing the curable fluoropolymer composition; and
   (3) use of a filler for reinforcing the cured fluoropolymer product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The surface-reacted calcium carbonate has volume median particle size of 1.5-15 mu m, a volume top cut particle size d98 of 3-30 mu m, and specific surface area of 30-100 m2/g measured using nitrogen and BET method. The ultrafine calcium carbonate has volume median particle size d50 of 0.3-0.5 mu m, a weight top cut particle size d98 of 1-6 mu m and BET specific surface area of 8-20 m2/g measured using nitrogen and BET method. The tear resistance and/or the elongation at break of the cured fluoropolymer product is increased compared to a cured fluoropolymer product containing an equivalent volume of carbon black N550 as filler, preferably the tear resistance and/or the elongation at break of the cured fluoropolymer product is increased by at least 5%, preferably by at least 20%. Preferred Components: The natural ground calcium carbonate is selected from marble, chalk, limestone, and their mixtures and the precipitated calcium carbonate is selected from precipitated calcium carbonates having an aragonitic, vateritic or calcitic crystal form or their mixtures. At least one oxonium ion donor is selected from hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid, phosphoric acid, citric acid, oxalic acid, an acidic salt, acetic acid, formic acid, and their mixtures at least partially neutralized by a cation selected from lithium, sodium, phosphorus, phosphate ion, magnesium (2+), or calcium (2+), and their mixtures. The ultrafine calcium carbonate is selected from ultrafine ground calcium carbonate, ultrafine precipitated calcium carbonate, ultrafine dolomite, and their mixtures. The filler comprises 0.11-3 mg/m2 surface-treatment layer on at least a portion of the filler surface. The surface-treatment layer is formed by contacting the filler with 0.07-9 mg/m2, preferably 0.11-3 mg/m2 surface-treatment agent(s). The additional filler is selected from carbon black, silica, graphite, clay, talc, diatomaceous earth, barium sulfate, titanium dioxide, wollastonite, and their mixtures.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The surface treatment agent is selected from mono- or di-substituted succinic anhydride containing compounds, mono- or di-substituted succinic acid containing compounds, mono- or di-substituted succinic acid salts containing compounds, saturated or unsaturated fatty acids, salts of saturated or unsaturated fatty acids, unsaturated esters of phosphoric acid, salts of unsaturated phosphoric acid esters, functionalized poly- and or perfluorinated alkyl compounds, abietic acid, salts of abietic acid, and their mixtures, and reaction products, preferably maleated polybutadienes, saturated fatty acids, poly(hexafluoropropylene oxide)s having a terminal carboxyl group placed on the terminal fluoromethylene group, their mixtures and reaction products.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The crosslinkable fluorine-containing polymer is a copolymer of vinylidene fluoride and at least one monomer selected from hexafluoropropylene, tetrafluoroethylene, fluorinated vinyl ether, perfluoroalkylvinylether, chlorotrifluoro-ethylene, propylene, ethylene, bromine- or iodine-containing fluoroolefins, and their mixtures, preferably a crosslinkable fluoroelastomer selected from FKM fluoroelastomer, FFKM fluoroelastomer, FEPM fluoroelastomer, and their mixtures, as defined by ASTM D1418-17. The composition further comprises a crosslinking agent selected from an amine, a diamine, a bisphenol, a peroxide (preferred), and their mixtures, acid acceptor, accelerator, coloring pigment, dyes, wax, lubricant, oxidative- and/or UV-stabilizer, antioxidant, additional filler, processing aid, plasticizer, additional polymer, and their mixtures. Preferred Properties: The crosslinkable fluorine-containing polymer has a specific gravity of 0.5-5, preferably 1-3, measured according to ASTM D297, and/or a fluorine content of 53-71 wt.%, preferably 66-71 wt-%, based on the total weight of the polymer.

PN WO2022010344-A1
TI Offshore renewable energy storing and recovering method, involves storing renewable energy by causing portion of carbon dioxide to flow from reservoir to another reservoir via well, ducting structure and another well.
AU VAN HIJFTE L G
   STIGTER H T
AE FIZZY TRANSITION VENTURES BV (FIZZ-Non-standard)
GA 202207507H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves storing and recovering renewable energy, which is generated offshore, comprises using a first reservoir (5) connected to a first well (3), and a second reservoir (4), a ducting structure, an electrically driven compressor, a turbine, and an electric generator. Each of the first reservoir and the second reservoir is an offshore underground reservoir from which natural gas or oil was previously extracted and which has been made ready for injection and storage of C02. The C02 is defined as being a mixture of pure carbon dioxide and other substances, where the share of carbon dioxide is 50%.
   USE - Method for storing and recovering offshore renewable energy. Uses include but are not limited to electrical energy, solar radiation, geothermal heat and tidal movement.

TI Breathing gas remixer for modifying gas mix inhaled by user, comprises mixing unit that generates gas mix by obtaining exhaled gas from gas reservoir, and delivers gas mix to mouthpiece through inhalation gas transport tube.
AU JOHNSON B D
   DOWNS J H
AE EMERCENT TECHNOLOGIES LLC (EMER-Non-standard)
GA 2021B5004K
AB    NOVELTY - The breathing gas remixer comprises a mouthpiece having a mask, coupled to a gas reservoir through an exhalation gas transport tube and to a mixing unit (400) through an inhalation gas transport tube. The mixing unit generates a gas mix by obtaining exhaled gas from the reservoir, measuring a carbon dioxide level of the gas using a mixing sensor (407), determining a target level using a breathing model stored using a microcontroller, obtaining fresh air through a fresh air intake port (404), and mixing the gas and the fresh air using an impeller to generate the mix until the mix comprises carbon dioxide at approximately the target level as specified in the model.
   USE - Breathing gas remixer for modifying a gas mix inhaled by user.
   ADVANTAGE - The breathing gas remixer can accurately adjust inspired carbon dioxide and/or oxygen levels independently to a desired level. The quality, efficiency, and speed of devices to provide breathing gas mixes can be improved.

PN US11059030-B1; WO2021154911-A1; US2021229076-A1
TI Bi-reforming over an enhanced-acidity modified red mud catalyst composition involves providing methane feed in presence of carbon dioxide and reacting in bi-reforming reaction, and neutralizing pH of unmodified red mud waste material , Bi-reforming over an enhanced-acidity modified red mud catalyst composition involves providing methane feed in presence of carbon dioxide and reacting in bi-reforming reaction, and neutralizing pH of unmodified red mud waste material.
AU ALSOLAMI B H
   FADHEL B A
   ALBUALI M A
   BAMAGAIN R
   ALSOLAMI B
   FADHEL B
   ALBUALI M
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202179486V
AB    NOVELTY - Bi-reforming over an enhanced-acidity modified red mud catalyst composition involves providing a methane feed in the presence of carbon dioxide and steaming to react in a bi-reforming reaction over the enhanced-acidity modified red mud catalyst composition. A pressure between about 5 bar and 20 bar to produce synthesis gas comprising hydrogen (H2) and carbon monoxide (CO). The enhanced-acidity modified red mud catalyst composition prepared by a homogeneous precipitation process, which involves providing an unmodified caustic red mud waste material produced from an alumina extraction process from bauxite ore, and dissolving in water the unmodified caustic red mud waste material. The pH of the unmodified red mud waste material is neutralized with an acid comprising hydrochloric acid, and combined nickel oxide. The Group VIB metal oxide present at between about 1-30 wt.% of the enhanced-acidity modified red mud catalyst composition.
   USE - Method for bi-reforming over an enhanced-acidity modified red mud catalyst composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides cost-effective and durable unit for bi-reforming.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The Group VIB metal oxide comprises at least one metal selected from the group consisting of chromium, molybdenum, and tungsten. The enhanced-acidity modified red mud catalyst composition includes at least one component selected from the group iron oxide (Fe2O3), aluminum oxide (Al2O3), silicon oxide (SiO2), sodium oxide (Na2O) and titanium oxide (TiO2). Preferred Conditions: The increased temperature is about 750?oC. The increased pressure is between about 10 bar and about 15 bar. The gas hourly space velocity of the methane feed in the presence of carbon dioxide and steam is between about 1000-10000 per hour. A majority of particles of the enhanced-acidity modified red mud catalyst composition have a particle size of less than about 70 micrometer. The conversion percentage of methane reaches up to about 35%. A molar ratio of methane:carbon dioxide:steam is about 3:1:2. The produced H2 from the bi-reforming reaction is at least about 4 mol. % of produced products from the bi-reforming reaction for at least about 5 hours. The Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area of the modified red mud catalyst composition is between about 50 m2/g and about 90 m2/g.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN112960685-A
TI Preparing nano calcium carbonate useful for            filling rigid polyvinyl chloride comprises e.g.            calcining limestone to obtain quicklime, placing            quicklime and water for digestion reaction to obtain            calcium hydroxide, sieving and suspending.
AU LI H
   ZHU Y
   ZHAN Q
   MA J
   HUANG H
AE ANHUI XUANCHENG HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHN (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021686398
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano calcium carbonate comprises (i)                calcining limestone at 1000-1200 degrees C to                obtain quicklime, placing quicklime and water for                digestion reaction to obtain calcium hydroxide,                sieving and allowing to stand and then suspending                calcium hydroxide with a solid content of 6.0-12%                liquid, (ii) adding a crystal form control agent to                the calcium hydroxide suspension, passing in a                mixed gas with a carbon dioxide concentration of                20-40% for carbonation reaction and controlling the                flow of carbon dioxide mixed gas to 0.005-0.02                m2/h, when the suspension PH is less than or equal                to 7to9, stopping the introduction carbon dioxide,                (iii) performing surface modification of suspension                by adding sodium stearate solution to the                suspension, stirring for 30-60 minutes, then adding                the calcium salt solution to the suspension,                stirring for 1-3 hours, then adding the zinc salt                solution to the suspension, stirring for 1-3 hours,                and (iv) carrying out press filtration, drying and                sieving.
   USE - The nano calcium carbonate is useful for                filling rigid polyvinyl chloride (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nano calcium carbonate: improves the                mechanical property and whiteness of hard polyvinyl                chloride; and inhibits the yellowing, aging and                decomposition of hard polyvinyl chloride                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  mass content of magnesium carbonate in the                  limestone is less than or equal to 0.3%. The mass                  ratio of quicklime to water is 1:5-10. The                  crystal form control agent comprises sucrose,                  glucose and maltose and the added amount is                  0.2-0.5% of calcium carbonate on dry basis. The                  mass concentration of the sodium stearate                  solution is 20-70% and the added amount of                  carbonic acid is 20-50% of the mass of calcium on                  dry basis. The calcium salt solution is a mixture                  of calcium chloride, calcium gluconate, calcium                  dihydrogen phosphate and/or calcium nitrate. The                  mass concentration of calcium salt solution is                  20-70% and the adding amount is 10-20% of calcium                  carbonate on dry basis. In the step (iii), the                  zinc salt solution is a mixture of zinc nitrate,                  zinc chloride, zinc sulfate and/or zinc acetate.                  The concentration of the zinc salt solution is                  20-70% and the added amount is 15-30 wt.% of the                  calcium carbonate on dry basis. Preferred Method:                  In the step (ii), the carbonation reaction                  control temperature is 30-40 degrees C and the                  stirring speed is 500-1000 rotations/minute. In                  the step (iii), the surface modification control                  temperature is 70-90 degrees C and the stirring                  speed is 300-500 rotations/ minute (RPM). In the                  step (iv), the drying temperature is 100-130                  degrees C and the time is 18-22 hours.

PN CN111961207-A; CN111961207-B
TI Citric acid-catalyzed synthesis of caprolactone            and dimethylsiloxane triblock copolymer for            L-polylactic acid polymerization in carbon dioxide,            involves stirring anhydrous citric acid and            polydimethylsiloxane, and adding caprolactone.
AU ZHAN S
   WAN Z
   WANG J
   ZHAO Q
AE UNIV DALIAN (UYDV-C)
GA 2020B8027H
AB    NOVELTY - Citric acid-catalyzed synthesis of a triblock                copolymer of caprolactone and dimethylsiloxane                involves adding anhydrous citric acid and PDMS in a                two-necked flask, stirring and warming the mixture                under the protection of nitrogen until the citric                acid is completely dissolved. A certain amount of                &#949; -caprolactone is placed in the constant                pressure funnel, slowly dropped into the flask                under nitrogen atmosphere, and heated at 40-                80&#176; C for 12-24 hours. The obtained                product is separated and purified, filtered with                suction and dried to obtain the stabilizer                polycaprolactone-polydimethylsiloxane-polycaprolactone                (PCL-PDMS-PCL), where the molar ratio of the                anhydrous citric acid to &#949; -caprolactone                is 1:4 to 1:10, the mass ratio of &#949;                -caprolactone to PDMS is 1:(0.5-1.5).
   USE - Citric acid-catalyzed synthesis of                caprolactone and dimethylsiloxane triblock                copolymer for dispersion polymerization of                L-polylactic acid in supercritical carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts citric acid as a catalyst,                which can avoid the use of toxic organic solvents                in the prepared biomedical materials, and is more                green and safer, and has shorter reaction time and                milder reaction temperature. The polymerization                process is a promising preparation of biomedical                materials method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for using                triblock copolymer of caprolactone and                dimethylsiloxane in the dispersion polymerization                of L-polylactic acid in supercritical carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of                  &#949; -caprolactone to PDMS is 1:0.5, 1:1 or                  1:1.5. The molar ratio of the catalyst anhydrous                  citric acid to -caprolactone is 1:7, the reaction                  temperature is 65&#176; C, and the reaction                  time is 16 hours. The &#949; -caprolactone is                  slowly dripped into the flask at a rate of 1                  drop/second under a nitrogen atmosphere.                  PCL-PDMS-PCL is a triblock copolymer with a mass                  ratio of &#949; -caprolactone and PDMS of                  1:1. Preferred Composition: The addition amount                  of the stabilizer PCL-PDMS-PCL is 3-15 wt.%,                  preferably 5 wt.%.

PN CN111662447-A
TI Synthesizing porous functional ionic polyurea material used as catalyst to catalyze reaction of carbon dioxide cycloaddition, by heating urea and zinc chloride, adding tris(4-aminophenyl)amine, putting in blast drying box and drying.
AU LIU Q
   GAO Y
   ZHAO G
   ZHAO Q
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 202092175V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for synthesizing porous functional ionic polyurea material by solvothermal method, involves (a) adding urea and zinc chloride in a molar ratio of 16:1-20:1, placing on the heating module and raising the temperature to 80 degrees C and stirring, until the solid powder in the tube is completely melted into a colorless and transparent eutectic solvent, (b) adding tris(4-aminophenyl)amine, shaking, continuing to heat up and reacting on the heating module for 0.5-2 hours, (c) after the reaction is over, putting in a blast drying box to continue the reaction for 3-13 days, and (d) after the reaction, taking out the pressure tube, immersing the product in deionized water for washing, performing suction filtering, and placing in a freeze dryer for drying for 48-72 hours to obtain a blue-gray product, preferably a porous functional ion polyurea material.
   USE - The method is used for synthesizing porous functional ionic polyurea material, which is used as a catalyst to catalyze the reaction of carbon dioxide cycloaddition to propylene carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The synthesized porous functional ionic polyurea material has the characteristics of strong thermal stability, high specific surface area and excellent adsorption capacity, and improves the utilization of carbon dioxide resources.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The reaction time in step (b) is 1.5 hours. The reaction time in step (c) is 7 days. The drying time in step (d) is 72 hours. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of urea to zinc chloride in step (a) is 16:1.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing simulated flue gas activated and                expanded biomass charcoal comprises (i) placing the                biomass in a tube furnace, and calcining and                carbonizing at 300-400 degrees C under nitrogen                atmosphere for 2-6 hours to obtain carbonized                biomass, and (ii) grinding the carbonized biomass                obtained in the step (i), placing in the tube                furnace again under the condition of introducing                simulated flue gas, and reacting and activating at                650-850 degrees C for 3-5 hours to obtain a biomass                charcoal product with porous structure, where the                simulated flue gas comprises carbon dioxide and                nitrogen components in a volume ratio of                10-90:90-10.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing simulated                flue gas activated and expanded biomass                charcoal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: achieves the purification and                desulfurization of flue gas; makes the gas quality                meet the national flue gas emission standards;                simulates the proportion of the main components in                the flue gas that meets the national emission                standards; provides way of thinking for the use of                factories to process the carbon dioxide in the flue                gas; reduces the carbon dioxide content in the                exhaust gas; burns the generated carbon monoxide as                fuel gas again; saves energy; and reduces emissions                while reducing fuel costs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  biomass is bamboo, peanut shells and/or corn                  stover, preferably peanut shells. In the step                  (ii), the volume ratio of carbon dioxide and                  nitrogen in the simulated flue gas is                  25-75:75-25.

PT P
PN CN110624531-A
TI Preparing bismuth titanate photocatalyst used for photoreduction of carbon dioxide, by dissolving bismuth (III) nitrate pentahydrate, dripping titanium tetrachloride, dissolving sodium hydroxide, transferring to PTFE reactor and cooling.
AU ZHANG X
   WANG Y
   ZHANG C
   REN G
   LI R
   LIU J
   FAN C
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 202002644K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a bismuth titanate photocatalyst, involves (i) dissolving 2-5 g bismuth(III) nitrate pentahydrate in 20-40 ml deionized water or dilute nitric acid to form solution (A), (ii) dripping 0.1-1 ml titanium tetrachloride or titanium (IV) butoxide to 20-40 ml deionized water to form solution (B), dissolving 4-10 g sodium hydroxide in 30-60 ml deionized water to form solution (C), and sequentially dripping solution (B) and solution (C) to solution (A) to form solution (D), and (iii) stirring solution (D) at room temperature for 0.5-1 hour, transferring to PTFE reactor, placing in oven, taking out at 150-250 degrees C for 12-24 hours, cooling reactor to room temperature, repeatedly washing the precipitate with absolute ethanol and deionized water until the surface is neutral, drying in oven at 70 degrees C to obtain sample, and obtaining bismuth titanate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing bismuth titanate photocatalyst used for photoreduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The process prepares high-efficiency photocatalytic material, is mild, controllable, simple and easy to operate. The band structure of bismuth titanate matches the redox potentials of the valence and conduction bands required for photo-driven carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide which exhibits excellent activity, selectivity and stability.

PN CN109749013-A
TI Preparing high-strength anti-wear polypropylene printing film involves carrying first polymerization reaction using vinyl acetate, allyl glycidyl ether and initiator to obtain vinyl acetate-allyl glycidyl ether copolymer.
AU FANG W
   LIU W
   JIANG L
   LIU Z
   LUO C
AE GUIZHOU GETTEL NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 201947663U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-strength anti-wear polypropylene printing film involves carrying first polymerization reaction using vinyl acetate, allyl glycidyl ether and an initiator to obtain a vinyl acetate-allyl glycidyl ether copolymer, modifying portion of vinyl acetate-allyl glycidyl ether copolymer to obtain a silica modifier. The first polypropylene resin and the silica modifier are sequentially melt-blended to obtain a polypropylene material. The polypropylene material is placed in a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid containing a polar monomer and an initiator, soaked for 6 hours to 3 days at a pressure of 10 Mega Pascals and a temperature of 50 degrees C, taken out at room temperature and pressure for 1-4 hours. The mass ratio of the polar monomer to the carbon dioxide in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is 5:100. The mass ratio of the initiator to the polar monomer is 6:100. The polypropylene material treated is grafted.
   USE - Method for preparing high-strength anti-wear polypropylene printing film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enbales to prepare high-strength anti-wear polypropylene printing film, which greatly improve surface polarity and printability of polypropylene materials, has higher solubility, improve surface printability and the scratch resistance of the printed layer while maintaining the excellent comprehensive performance of the polypropylene material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The polar monomer is maleic anhydride, acrylic acid, methacrylic acid, vinyl acetate, hydroxyethyl acrylate, methacrylate hydroxyethyl ester. The initiator is dicumyl peroxide, di-tert-butyl peroxide, diisopropyl peroxydicarbonate, dibenzoyl peroxide, diphenyldodecyl peroxide, azobisisobutyronitrile, azobisisoheptanenitrile, or benzophenone, 4,4'-dimethylaminobenzophenone, diethoxyacetophenone, dichloroacetophenone. The supercritical carbon dioxide fluid includes carbon dioxide, a mixed system of acetone, chloroform, ethyl acetate, toluene, cyclohexane and carbon dioxide, the acetone, chloroform, ethyl acetate, toluene, cyclohexane and carbon dioxide. The amount accounts for 2-20% of the mass of the supercritical carbon dioxide system. The grafting treatment is a heat treatment, or an ultraviolet irradiation treatment, or a combination treatment of heating and ultraviolet irradiation. The heat treatment temperature is 60-130 degrees C, and the time is 10-60 minutes. The ultraviolet light has a wavelength of 320-450 nanometer, and the ultraviolet irradiation time is 1-10 minutes. The vinyl acetate-allyl glycidyl ether copolymer is divided into a first portion and a second portion, and the silica is subjected to the first portion of the vinyl acetate-allyl glycidyl ether copolymer. modified. The second polymerization of the second polypropylene resin and the second portion of the vinyl acetate-allyl glycidyl ether copolymer is performed and reacted to obtain a compatibilizer. The compatibilizing agent, the silica modifier, and the polypropylene resin are sequentially subjected to the melt blending and the biaxial stretching treatment to obtain the modified polypropylene film.

   USE - Polymeric hollow fibers for preparing of                carbon molecular sieve hollow fibers membrane for                separating mixture of gases e.g. carbon dioxide and                methane, hydrogen sulfide and methane, carbon                dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and methane, carbon                dioxide and nitrogen, oxygen and nitrogen, nitrogen                and methane, helium and methane, helium and                nitrogen, helium and sulfur hexafluoride, hydrogen                and ethylene, ethylene and ethane, propylene and                propane, or ethane/propane and ethylene/propylene                (all claimed).

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of carbon molecular sieve                hollow fibers membrane; and

The core layer and the sheath                  layer are free of high performance polymer. The                  core layer preferably comprises 2-30 wt.% several                  nanoparticles which are hydrophobic. The                  nanoparticles are polyhedral oligomeric                  silsesquioxane silica nanoparticles. The support                  layer comprises 90-98 wt.% polymeric hollow                  fibers. Preferred Properties: The support layer                  has radial thickness of 100-500 mu m. The skin                  layer has radial thickness of 0.05-2 mu m. The                  carbon molecular sieve hollow fibers membrane has                  carbon dioxide-methane has selectivity of more                  than 20, preferably more than 75, and carbon                  dioxide permeance of more than 200 GPU.

PN CN108893451-A
TI Culturing large-scale of human parainfluenza type I virus involves inoculating human parainfluenza type I virus is on Vero cells overgrown with single layer, and then culturing under carbon dioxide, and virus titer in supernatant.
AU ZHOU W
   ZHOU L
   SUN J
   ZHAO Q
   LI G
   FU G
   WU X
AE ZHENGZHOU YIMEINUO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201897151S
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing large-scale of human parainfluenza type I virus involves inoculating human parainfluenza type I virus is on Vero cells overgrown with a single layer at 0.001-0.01mol, and then culturing at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide. The virus titer is subjected in the supernatant, after inoculation, which is detected by enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay every day, and the virus proliferation curve is drawn. The culture supernatant is collected until the seventh day, the virus culture supernatant is collected, the cells are digested with trypsin digest, and the passage of the toxic cells is carried out at 1:4-5, and then cultured at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide. The collect the virus solution performed the above step, re-add the virus culture solution to carry out the passage amplification of the toxic cells to the large-scale.
   USE - Method for culturing large-scale of human parainfluenza type I virus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to culture large-scale of human parainfluenza type I virus, which has good cell survival ratio, reduces cell preparation quantity, shortens the culture time, improves working efficiency, and satisfies the requirement for in-depth research and production of the human parainfluenza type I virus vaccine.

PT P
PN KR1902958-B1
TI Module-type multi-functional composite sensor for            use in e.g. home, has alert portion determining room            occupation when movement is sensed through motion            detection and carbon dioxide concentration detected            through coupling stoker.
AU KIM T S
AE TONGYANG BMS (TONG-Non-standard)
GA 2018777516
AB    NOVELTY - The sensor (1000) has a module mounting                portion (1200) mounted with multiple module units.                A controller (1400) distinguishes the module units                mounted on the module mounting portion and assigns                a memory area for receiving a signal. A transceiver                (1110) wirelessly transmits information from the                controller to an outside. A fire determining unit                determines fire break out through a fire sensing                signal. A coupling stoker determines carbon dioxide                concentration. PN US2018043279-A1; US10500524-B2
TI Removing N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP) from solvent mixture comprising NMP and ethylene glycol in chamber, comprises exposing the mixture to supercritical fluid stream of carbon dioxide, and collecting removed NMP in stream exiting chamber.
AU QUINLAN G L
AE QUINLAN G L (QUIN-Individual)
   QUINLAN PROPERTIES LLC (QUIN-Non-standard)
GA 201813582U
AB    NOVELTY - Removing at least 75 wt.% or vol.% of N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP), based on the total weight of a solvent mixture or based on the total volume of the solvent mixture respectively, from the solvent mixture comprising NMP and ethylene glycol in a chamber (110), comprises exposing the solvent mixture comprising the NMP and the ethylene glycol to a supercritical fluid stream of carbon dioxide at a temperature and pressure to remove at least 75 wt.% or vol.% of the NMP, and collecting the removed NMP in the supercritical fluid stream of carbon dioxide exiting the chamber.
   USE - The method is useful for removing at least 75 wt.% or vol.% of N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP), based on the total weight of a solvent mixture or based on the total volume of the solvent mixture respectively, from the solvent mixture comprising NMP and ethylene glycol in a chamber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method separates at least two solvents in a solvent mixture, and removes at least 95% of one of the solvents from the solvent mixture, and is time saving by achieving increased recoveries of the solvent components.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of an open system.
   System (100)
   Chamber (110)
   Pump (120)
   Collection vessel (130)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises providing the supercritical fluid stream at a pressure to be miscible with the NMP. The method further comprises: depressurizing the removed NMP in the supercritical fluid stream to collect the removed NMP; collecting the removed NMP in a closed vessel; collecting the removed NMP in an open vessel; recycling the supercritical fluid stream; providing the recycled supercritical fluid stream to additional solvent mixture; removing additional amounts of the NMP from the solvent mixture to remove a total of at least 99 wt.% or vol.% of the NMP from the solvent mixture, where exposing the collected NMP to the recycled supercritical fluid stream; reusing the supercritical fluid stream to separate solvent mixture; adding a carrier to the supercritical fluid stream to increase the amount of the NMP recovered compared to the amount of NMP recovered using the same supercritical fluid without the carrier; detecting the NMP in the supercritical fluid stream; drying the supercritical fluid after collecting the NMP; providing the dried supercritical fluid back to the chamber; and configuring the supercritical fluid stream to be supercritical carbon dioxide at a pressure and temperature effective to be miscible with the NMP and the ethylene glycol.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
