PN WO2018232507-A1; CA3068082-A1; AU2018288555-A1; IN201917054847-A; SG11201912759-A1; EP3642170-A1; JP2020524103-W; US2020282595-A1; EP3642170-A4; AU2022201059-A1; SG11201912759-B
TI Preparing a concrete mix involves adding concrete            materials to a mixer, adding mix water to mixer, where            mix water comprises carbonated concrete wash water, and            mixing water and concrete materials to produce a            concrete mix.
AU MONKMAN G S
   SANDBERG P J
   CAIL K
   FORGERON D P
   MACDONALD M
AE CARBONCURE TECHNOLOGIES INC (CARB-Non-standard)
   MONKMAN G S (MONK-Individual)
   SANDBERG P J (SAND-Individual)
   CAIL K (CAIL-Individual)
   CARBONCURE TECHNOLOGIES INC (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBONCURE TECHNOLOGIES INC (CARB-Non-standard)
   MONKMAN G S (MONK-Individual)
   SANDBERG P J (SAND-Individual)
   CAIL K (CAIL-Individual)
   CARBONCURE TECHNOLOGIES INC (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 201900067Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a concrete mix involves adding                concrete materials to a mixer, adding mix water to                mixer, where mix water comprises carbonated                concrete wash water, and carbonated mix water has                specific gravity of more than 1.10, and mixing the                water and the concrete materials to produce a                concrete mix.
   USE - Method for preparing a concrete mix.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare a concrete mix,                which injects carbonated water at a downward                location in the tank, and materials from one batch                is recycled into another batch or batches, allows                less material such as cement to be used, and                decreases or eliminates costs associated with                disposing of wash water materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) an apparatus for carbonating wash water                produced in production of concrete in a wash water                operation, which comprises a source of carbon                dioxide, a first unit operably connected to the                source of carbon dioxide that runs to a wash water                container, where wash water comprises cement and/or                supplementary cementitious materials (SCM), and                wash water container contains wash water from a                concrete production site, an unit having one or                more openings positioned to deliver carbon dioxide                at or under the surface of wash water in the                container to produce carbonated wash water, and a                system to transport carbonated wash water to a                concrete mix operation where carbonated wash water                is used as mix water in a concrete mix;
   (2) an apparatus for preparing a concrete mix,                which comprises a first mixer for mixing concrete                materials and water, a second mixer for mixing                concrete materials and water, a first water                container holding water that comprises carbonated                concrete wash water, a second water container,                different from the first, holding water that is not                carbonated concrete wash water, a first system                fluidly connecting the first water container with                the second mixer and a second system fluidly                connecting the second water container with the                first mixer;
   (3) a method for treating waste concrete in a                concrete mixer, which involves adding water to the                mixer to wash out mixer and adding carbon dioxide                to the mixer to produce carbonated wash water in                the mixer;
   (4) a method for treating wash water from a                concrete production process, which involves                exposing wash water to carbon dioxide to produce                carbonated wash water; adding a portion of the                carbonated wash water to a concrete mixer so that                carbonated wash water mixes with a cement mix in                the mixer, where carbonated wash water comprises                solids and not more than 80% of solids are removed                from the carbonated wash water before its use in                the mix;
   (5) a carbonated concrete wash water                composition, which comprises a wash water from the                concrete, carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide                reaction products with the wash water;
   (6) a method for sequestering carbon dioxide                and/or offsetting carbon dioxide produced in                production and use of concrete at a concrete                production facility, which involves contacting                water that comprises concrete wash water produced                at facility that comprises solids having                cementitious material with carbon dioxide to                produce carbonated wash water; and re-using a                portion of carbonated wash water and associated                solids in production of concrete at the concrete                production facility for sequestering and/or                offsetting an amount of carbon dioxide from the                production and using concrete from the facility,                where production and use comprises carbon dioxide                produced in producing cement used in the                concrete;
   (7) a method for treating concrete wash water                with carbon dioxide, which involves contacting                concrete wash water carbon dioxide; determining a                value for one characteristic of a pH of the wash                water, a temperature of the wash water, and/or a                carbon dioxide content of air above the surface of                the wash water; and modulating contacting of the                concrete wash water with carbon dioxide based on                portion on determined value;
   (8) an apparatus for contacting concrete wash                water with carbon dioxide, which comprises a                container containing concrete wash water, where                wash water contains solids, a system configured to                agitate the wash water in container sufficiently to                prevent solids from settling, and a sensor                configured and located to determine a pH of the                wash water, a sensor configured and located to                determine a carbon dioxide concentration in air                above the wash water, and/or a sensor configured                and located to determine a temperature of the wash                water, a system configured to contact the wash                water with carbon dioxide, and a controller, where                controller is operably connected to the sensor or                sensors and to actuator, and controller receives                information from sensor or sensors and is based on                a portion of information, and then sends a signal                to actuator to modulate the flow of carbon                dioxide;
   (9) a method, which involves exposing a                concrete mix to one or more set retarders; and                exposing the concrete mix to carbon dioxide;
   (10) a method, which involves exposing                concrete wash water to carbon dioxide to produce                carbonated wash water; exposing the carbonated wash                water to an admixture, and where admixture is such                that after exposure the solids in the carbonated                wash water remain suspended with little or no                agitation;
   (11) a method, which involves exposing                concrete wash water to carbon dioxide to produce                carbonated wash water; mixing carbonated wash water                with cement powder to produce a wet cement mix; and                exposing wet cement mix to carbon dioxide to                produce a carbonated wet cement mix;
   (12) an apparatus, which comprises a holding                tank for holding concrete wash water, a first unit                operably connected to the holding tank, where first                unit comprises an inlet from the holding tank to                admit wash water to the unit and an outlet to                replace wash water back in the holding tank after                it has circulated through the unit, and comprises                one or more openings for introducing carbon dioxide                into wash water pumped through the unit; and
   (13) a composition, which comprises a                hydraulic cement, and mix water for the hydraulic                cement, where mix water comprises carbonated                concrete wash water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Conditions: The carbonated                  concrete wash water comprises 10%, preferably 40%                  of the total mix water. The mix water comprises a                  first portion of water that is not carbonated mix                  water and a second portion of mix water that                  comprises carbonated mix water, where first batch                  of mix water is added to the concrete materials                  before second batch of mix water. The first                  portion of water is added at a first location and                  second portion of water is added at a second                  location, where first and second locations are                  different. The second location is the drum of a                  ready-mix truck. The second portion of mix water                  is added 2 minutes after the first portion. The                  carbonated concrete wash water has a density of                  1.10g/cm3. The carbonated concrete wash water is                  held for 1, preferably 3 days after rinsing from                  a concrete truck. The concrete mix is                  sufficiently workable for its intended use, and                  carbonated wash water is of an age that same mix                  made with wash water of the same age in same                  proportions is not sufficiently workable for its                  intended use. The mix water comprises carbonated                  wash water in an amount that results in a                  concrete mix that is 5% stronger at a time after                  pouring than same concrete mix made without                  carbonated wash water. The time after pouring is                  1 day, 7 days, 28 days, or its combination. The                  mix water comprises carbonated wash water in an                  amount that allows concrete mix to contain 5%                  less cement than, and retain a compressive                  strength after pouring of 5% same concrete mix                  made without carbonated wash water and with the                  extra 5% cement. The apparatus further comprises                  one or more sensors that monitor one or more                  characteristics of the wash water and/or                  carbonation of wash water in the container, where                  one or more sensors is operably connected to                  controller and delivers information regarding                  characteristic of the wash water and/or wash                  water operation to the controller. The                  characteristics is pH of wash water, rate of                  delivery of carbon dioxide to the wash water,                  total amount of wash water in the wash water                  container, temperature of the wash water,                  specific gravity of the wash water, concentration                  of one or more ions in the wash water, age of the                  wash water, circulation rate of the wash water,                  timing of circulation of the wash water, or its                  combination. The concrete mix is prepared by                  adding concrete materials to a mixer, adding mix                  water to mixer, where mix water comprises                  carbonated concrete wash water in an amount that                  total carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide reaction                  products supplied by the carbonated mix water to                  concrete mix not more than 3.5 wt.% cement, and                  mixing water and the concrete materials to                  produce a concrete mix. The sensors are                  configured to monitor two of pH of the wash                  water, rate of delivery of carbon dioxide to the                  wash water, total amount of wash water in the                  wash water container, temperature of the wash                  water, specific gravity of the wash water,                  concentration of one or more ions in the wash                  water, age of the wash water, circulation rate of                  the wash water, timing of circulation of the wash                  water, appearance of bubbles at surface of the                  wash water, carbon dioxide concentration of the                  air above the wash water, electrical conductivity                  of the wash water, and optical characteristics of                  the wash water. The concrete mix is prepared by                  using carbonated wash water having a specific                  gravity of 1.03, preferably 1.05. The portion of                  carbon dioxide added to mixer is added as carbon                  dioxide dissolved in wash water for the mixer.                  The concentration of carbon dioxide in the wash                  water is 2g/L water, preferably 4g/L water. The                  portion of carbon dioxide added to the mixer is                  added as solid carbon dioxide. The mixer is a                  transportable mixer. The mixer is a drum of a                  ready-mix truck. The method further involves                  transporting a portion of the carbonated wash                  water to a wash water treatment system. The wash                  water treatment system treats wash water having                  carbonated wash water to remove aggregates. The                  wash water treatment system adds additional                  carbon dioxide to the wash water having                  carbonated wash water. The carbonated wash water                  comprises 20%, preferably 40% of total mix water                  for the cement mix, and not more than 60% of the                  solids are removed from carbonated wash water                  before its use in the mix. The amount of carbon                  dioxide and carbon dioxide reaction products in                  wash water composition is 0.5 wt.% solids in the                  wash water composition. The pH of carbonated wash                  water is less than 9.0, preferably less than 8.5.                  The mix water comprises carbonated concrete wash                  water in an amount such that total carbon dioxide                  or carbon dioxide reaction products supplied by                  the carbonated mix water to concrete mix is 0.01                  wt.%, preferably 0.05 wt.% cement. The re-used                  carbonated wash water and associated solids                  replaces a portion of cement that is used in                  produced concrete. The portion of cement replaced                  is 0.1%, preferably 0.5% of cement that is used.                  The amount of carbon dioxide sequestered and/or                  offset is 0.1%, preferably 0.5% of total carbon                  dioxide produced in manufacture and use of                  concrete produced at the concrete production                  facility. The method further involves contacting                  concrete mix produced at concrete production                  facility with carbon dioxide to produce a                  carbonated concrete mix. The carbonated concrete                  mix utilizes less cement that an uncarbonated                  concrete mix of the same mix design. The                  carbonated concrete mix utilizes 0.1%, preferably                  0.5% less cement that an uncarbonated concrete                  mix of the same mix design. The method involves                  determining a value for two characteristics of pH                  of the wash water, a temperature of the wash                  water, and/or a carbon dioxide content of air                  above the surface of the wash water. The concrete                  wash water is in a holding tank and is agitated                  sufficiently to prevent settling of solids. The                  agitation is accomplished by pumping the wash                  water. The agitation is accomplished by use of an                  augur. The portion of wash water is removed to a                  location away from the holding tank to be                  contacted with the carbon dioxide. The value for                  each of the characteristics determined is                  compared to a predetermined range of values. The                  comparison to range of values is used in portion                  to determine a degree of modulation of the                  contacting of the wash water with carbon dioxide.                  The concrete mix is a concrete wash water. The                  concrete mix is exposed to set retarder in the                  drum of a ready-mix truck. The method further                  involves mixing aggregate with the cement powder                  and water to produce a wet concrete mix.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PT P
PN EP3995205-A1; WO2022101294-A1
TI Preparing sodium carbonate by providing carbonator reactor containing sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, supplying gas stream comprising carbon dioxide to reactor, stopping supply of gas stream comprising carbon dioxide to reaction and discharging reaction mixture containing sodium carbonate.
AU LOPEZ MONTERO J A
   NIETO SEPULVEDA F J
   ALONSO MORENO C
   CANALES VAZQUEZ J
   GARCIA YUSTE S
   ZAFRILLA RODRIGUEZ J E
   GUEDDARI AOURIR A
AE UNIV CASTILLA-LA MANCHA (UYCA-Non-standard)
   ALCOHOLES TOMELLOSO SA ALTOSA (ALCO-Non-standard)
   UNIV CASTILLA-LA MANCHA (UYCA-Non-standard)
   ALCOHOLES TOMELLOSO SA ALTOSA (ALCO-Non-standard)
GA 202265077Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing sodium carbonate involves (a) providing at least one carbonator reactor containing a sodium hydroxide aqueous solution; (b) supplying a gas stream comprising carbon dioxide to the at least one carbonator reactor provided in step (a), where the gas stream is introduced in the carbonator reactor above the surface of the sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, preferably the flow of the gas stream comprising carbon dioxide is equal to or higher than the absorption capacity of the system and the reaction mixture is continuously stirred following a turbulent agitation regime, (c) stopping the supply of the gas stream comprising carbon dioxide to the carbonator reaction and (d) discharging the reaction mixture containing sodium carbonate from the at least one carbonator reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sodium carbonate. The carbonator reactor is used for the preparation of sodium carbonate by capture of carbon dioxide in a sodium hydroxide aqueous solution (claimed). The method is used for heating or cooling processes and carbonation process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method decreases carbon dioxide emissions to the atmosphere, eliminates greenhouse gas emission such as carbon dioxide, provides simple and economic method, is easy to implement at industrial scale, reduces negative carbon dioxide emissions, allows capture of highly concentrated carbon dioxide, provides great versatility, and reduces the requirements for transport and storage of reagents, dimensions of the installation, energy consumption and the water footprint. The carbonator reactor provides efficient heat transmission, improves thermal efficiency, provides simple construction and low cost. The method exhibits safe reaction stoppable at any moment and enhances heat transfer to outside improving the control of the temperature inside the carbonator reactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the carbonator reactor comprising a body comprising a nozzle, at least an inlet adapted for introducing liquids, solids or their mixtures, an outlet adapted for discharging a liquid phase or slurry, means for regulating the temperature and means for regulating the temperature pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonator reactor further comprises stirring system comprising impeller comprising inclined blades, preferably the blades make an angle from 5-60 degrees in respect to the vertical axis, and at least one baffle, preferably the width of the baffle from 1-15% internal diameter of a cylindrical shell comprised in the body of the carbonator reactor. Preferred Composition: The sodium hydroxide aqueous solution contained in the carbonator reactor provided in step (a) has a concentration greater than or equal to 10 wt.%, preferably 35-55 wt.%. The amount of carbon dioxide comprised in the gas stream is greater than or equal to 30 wt.%, preferably 95-99.9 wt.%. The gas bulk comprises at least 3% maximum internal volume of the body of the carbonator reactor. Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide stream is generated in an alcoholic fermentative process. The supply of the gas stream comprises carbon dioxide to the carbonator reactor is carried out at a temperature equal to or lower than 160 degrees C and a pressure equal to or lower than 20 barg. The supply of the gas stream comprises carbon dioxide to the stirred reactor is carried out at atmospheric pressure and temperature of less than or equal to 80 degrees C, preferably 60-80 degrees C. The method involves stopping the supply of the gas stream comprising carbon dioxide to the carbonator reactor, when the pH of the reaction mixture achieves a value from 8-13. The method further involves separating sodium carbonate monohydrate from the discharged reaction mixture, preferably by filtration or centrifugation. Preferred Properties: The turbulent agitation regime has a reynolds number 5000-300000. The turbulent agitation regime has a speed from 50-400 rpm, and preferably the reaction mixture has a liquid free surface renewal speed of at least 0.06 m/s. Preferred Condition: The absorption capacity per unit of geometric area is 10-46 m3/hours m2, at a temperature of less than or equal to 80 degrees C and a pressure of less than or equal to 0.35 barg.
   EXAMPLE - The example section describes the general preparation of sodium carbonate that does not support the claims.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Selectively producing ethanol used for removing            carbon dioxide from atmosphere by contacting a gas            mixture comprising carbon dioxide and methane in            presence of water with a catalyst comprising            permanently polarized hydroxyapatite.
AU TURON DOLS P
   SANZ BELTRAN V
   RODRIGUEZ RIVERO A M
   ALEMAN LLANSO C
   PUIGGALI BELLALTA J
   REVILLA-LOPEZ G
   SANS J

AE BRAUN SURGICAL SA B (BINT-C)
   UNIV CATALUNYA POLITECNICA (UYCL-C)
   BRAUN SURGICAL SA B (BINT-C)
   UNIV CATALUNYA POLITECNICA (UYCL-C)
   BRAUN SURGICAL SA B (BINT-C)
   UNIV CATALUNYA POLITECNICA (UYCL-C)
GA 2021C2195L
AB    NOVELTY - An ethanol is selectively produced by                contacting a gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide                and methane in presence of water with a catalyst                comprising permanently polarized                hydroxyapatite.
   USE - Selective production of ethanol used for                removing carbon dioxide from atmosphere                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The efficiency of the reaction can be                increased by using a coated permanently polarized                hydroxyapatite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: The permanently                  polarized hydroxyapatite has a crystallinity of                  greater than or equal to 65%, preferably 65-99%,                  and/or proportion of amorphous calcium phosphate                  is less than or equal to 18 wt.%, preferably                  0.1-17 wt.%, based on the total weight of                  permanently polarized hydroxyapatite, and/or                  proportion of beta -tricalcium phosphate is less                  than or equal to 36 wt.%, preferably 0.1-35 wt.%,                  based on the total weight of the permanently                  polarized hydroxyapatite, and/or bulk resistance                  of 107-105 Omega .cm2, preferably 106-105 Omega                  .cm2, where the bulk resistance increases by                  4-73%, preferably 4% after 3 months, and/or                  surface capacitance decreasing by less than or                  equal to 8%, preferably 5-3% after 3 months.                  Preferred Process: The permanently polarized                  hydroxyapatite is obtained by (a) preparing                  samples of hydroxyapatite, (b) sintering the                  samples prepared in step (a) at 700-1200 degrees                  C, (c) applying a constant or variable direct                  current (DC) voltage of 250-2500 V for greater                  than or equal to 1 minute at 900-1200 degrees C                  or applying an equivalent electric field of                  1.49-15 kV/cm for greater than or equal to 1                  minute at 900-1200 degrees C or applying an                  electrostatic discharge of 2500-1500000 V for                  less than or equal to 10 minutes at 900-1200                  degrees C or applying an equivalent electric                  field of 148.9-8928 kV/cm for less than or equal                  to 10 minutes at 900-1200 degrees C, and (d)                  cooling the samples obtained in step (c)                  maintaining the DC voltage or the equivalent                  electric field or cooling the samples obtained in                  step (c) maintaining or without maintaining the                  electrostatic discharge or the equivalent                  electric field. The permanently polarized                  hydroxyapatite is obtained by (a) preparing                  samples of hydroxyapatite, (b) sintering the                  samples prepared in step (a) at 1000 degrees C                  for 2 hours, (c) applying an equivalent electric                  field of 3 kV/cm at 1000 degrees C for 1 hour,                  and (d) cooling the samples obtained in step (c)                  maintaining the equivalent electric field for 30                  minutes. Contacting step is carried out in the                  presence of liquid water. Contacting step is                  carried out with a volumetric ratio of the                  permanently polarized hydroxyapatite to water,                  preferably liquid water, of 1000:1-0.01:1,                  preferably 300:1-350:1. Contacting step is                  carried out with a volumetric ratio of carbon                  dioxide to methane of 200:1, preferably 1:1.                  Contacting step is carried out under a total                  pressure of 0.1-100 bar, preferably 6 bar.                  Contacting step is carried out under a partial                  pressure of carbon dioxide of 0.035-90 bar,                  preferably 3 bar, and/or under a partial pressure                  of methane of 0.00017-5 bar, preferably 3 bar.                  Contacting step is carried out under UV                  irradiation or UV-Vis irradiation having a                  wavelength of 200-850 nm, preferably 253.7 nm.                  Contacting step is carried out under UV                  irradiation and/or visible light irradiation, in                  particular having a irradiance of 0.1-200 W/m2,                  preferably 2-10 W/m2. Contacting step is carried                  out at a temperature of 25-250 degrees C,                  preferably 95 degrees C. Contacting step is                  carried out without UV irradiation and at a                  temperature of 25-250 degrees C, preferably 140                  degrees C. Contacting step is carried out                  continuously or discontinuously and/or for                  0.0001-120 hours, preferably 48-72 hours.

PN EP3789100-A1; WO2021043767-A1; US2022339619-A1
TI Desorption of carbon dioxide from anion            exchangers, involves heating at least one anion            exchanger containing polystyrene copolymer with amine            functional group, by increasing temperature by using            microwave radiation and reducing pressure.
AU NEUMANN S
   OCHSMANN C
AE LANXESS DEUT GMBH (LNXS-C)
   LANXESS DEUT GMBH (LNXS-C)
   LANXESS DEUT GMBH (LNXS-C)
GA 202123755Y
AB    NOVELTY - Desorption of carbon dioxide involves heating                at least one anion exchanger containing polystyrene                copolymer with amine functional group (I), by                increasing the temperature by using microwave                radiation and optionally reducing pressure.
   USE - Desorption of carbon dioxide from anion                exchangers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient desorption of                carbon dioxide from anion exchangers containing                polystyrene copolymer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Desorption of carbon dioxide involves heating                at least one anion exchanger containing polystyrene                copolymer with amine functional group of formula                (I), by increasing the temperature by using                microwave radiation and optionally reducing                pressure.
   R1 = 1-6C alkyl or H.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The desorption is                  carried out at temperatures of 50-180 degrees C,                  and pressure of 10-3 mbar to normal pressure. In                  the adsorption process, gases containing carbon                  dioxide are adsorbed. Preferred Components: The                  anion exchanger contains 0.2-3.0 mol carbon                  dioxide per kg of dried anion exchanger. The                  gases contain 0.1-60 vol.% carbon dioxide, and                  0.1-20 vol.% water, based on the total volume of                  the gases used. Preferred Conditions: The                  microwave radiation is carried out at energy of                  1-1000 kW.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Processes: The anion exchanger (I)                  is obtained by reacting an anion exchanger                  containing polystyrene copolymer with amine                  functional group of formula (II) with carbon                  dioxide. The adsorption of the carbon dioxide on                  the anion exchanger containing polystyrene                  copolymer with functional group (II) is performed                  at temperature of 0-30 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: The polystyrene copolymer structure                  with functional group (I) is                  styrene/divinylbenzene crosslinked copolymer. The                  anion exchanger has a bead polymer with pore                  diameter of 100-550 Angstrom . The anion                  exchangers containing polystyrene copolymer with                  functional groups (II) comprises 1-20 wt.% water                  based on the total mass of the anion                  exchangers.

PN CN110498406-A
TI Preparing graphene by continuous spray coating of graphite powder liquid carbon dioxide suspension, comprises e.g. adding liquid carbon dioxide with ultra-low viscosity, ultra-strong fluidity and depending on specific gravity.
AU DONG L
   DONG T
AE DONG T (DONG-Individual)
GA 2019A2337C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene by continuous spray coating of graphite powder liquid carbon dioxide suspension, comprises utilizing a sealed tank, a sealed barrel, a stirrer, a feeding port, an output pipe, a linear nozzle, a roller, a roller and a graphene sheet, adding liquid carbon dioxide with ultra-low viscosity, ultra-strong fluidity, super-strong permeability, super-absorption, specific gravity 1.101, boiling point -78.5 degrees C, and nano-level, specific gravity 2.25 graphite powder to the precise measurement in a sealed tank with a stirrer, the graphite powder broken down into free carbon atoms and liquid carbon dioxide to form a uniformly mixed suspension during the stirring disturbance, during continuous stirring, the output tube and linear nozzles located in the middle and lower part of the side wall of the sealed tank evenly spraying on the surface of the rotating roller, depending on the specific gravity difference of the carbon atoms in the liquid carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing graphene by continuous spray coating of graphite powder liquid carbon dioxide suspension.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and is economical. The graphene has comprehensive and complete physicochemical properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing graphene by continuous spray coating of graphite powder liquid carbon dioxide suspension, comprises a sealed tank, a sealed barrel, a stirrer, a feeding port, an output pipe, a linear nozzle, a roller, a roller and a graphene sheet, adding liquid carbon dioxide with ultra-low viscosity, ultra-strong fluidity, super-strong permeability, super-absorption, specific gravity 1.101, boiling point -78.5 degrees C, and nano-level, specific gravity 2.25 graphite powder to the precise measurement In a sealed tank with a stirrer, the graphite powder broken down into free carbon atoms and liquid carbon dioxide to form a uniformly mixed suspension during the stirring disturbance, during continuous stirring, the output tube and linear nozzles located in the middle and lower part of the side wall of the sealed tank evenly spraying on the surface of the rotating roller, depending on the specific gravity difference of the carbon atoms in the liquid carbon dioxide, the carbon that reaches the roller surface first atoms in a three-dimensional equilibrium environment without the kinetic energy of the upper and lower atoms, completing the two-dimensional combination in the lateral direction to form a two-dimensional crystal, liquid carbon dioxide quickly evaporates due to the huge temperature difference in the room temperature environment, forming a graphene membrane attached to the surface of the roller, which is peeled off under the flush of liquid carbon dioxide at the linear nozzle connected to the sealed barrel, wound on a roller to complete the graphene production.

PN CN114894833-A
TI Method for effectively measuring complete uniform            temperature of carbon dioxide-enriched fluid inclusion,            involves executing heating program to measure complete            homogeneous temperature data of carbon dioxide-rich            fluid inclusions.
AU BAO Z
   DING Z
   ZHANG P
   SONG M
   WANG M
   ZHANG L
   LI S
   LI J
AE UNIV HEBEI GEO (UYHE-Non-standard)
GA 2022A7099B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting ore sample                containing more transparent hydrothermal minerals.                The inclusion sheet is unloaded from a glass slide.                A sharp blade is used to cut the transparent                minerals on the inclusion sheet into small pieces.                Small fragments containing more carbon dioxide-rich                fluid inclusions are selected as a target sample                for experimental research. A sample chamber of a                hydrothermal diamond pressure chamber is cleaned                before loading the sample. A position of a metal                rhenium sheet is fixed. The experimental sample of                carbon dioxide-rich fluid inclusions is loaded.                Bubble disappearance temperature in the sample                chamber is determined after checking the tightness                of the sample chamber. Heating rate is fixed. A                heating program is executed to measure the complete                homogeneous temperature data of the carbon                dioxide-rich fluid inclusions after completing the                measurement experiment of the complete homogeneous                temperature of the carbon dioxide-rich fluid                inclusions.
   USE - Method for effectively measuring complete                uniform temperature of carbon dioxide-enriched                fluid inclusion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables obtaining accurate and                reliable temperature data of inclusion complete                uniformity, which can provide scientific judgment                basis for clarifying the metallogenic temperature                and metallogenic mechanism of carbonatite type rare                earth deposits.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a method for effectively measuring complete uniform                temperature of carbon dioxide-enriched fluid                inclusion. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - The transparent minerals include quartz,                  calcite, barite, and fluorite.

PT P
PN CN113773483-A
TI Carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol used for slow-rebound polyurethane foam, is prepared by using dimethyl carbonate and phosphazene catalyst dual-catalytic system for catalyzing polymerization of carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide.
AU GONG W
   ZHANG D
   WANG T
   YU T
   SUN Z
AE SHANDONG INOV NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021E9567J
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol is prepared by using dimethyl carbonate and phosphazene catalyst dual-catalytic system for catalyzing polymerization of carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide, where the polycarbonate ether polyol has functionality of 2-3 and hydroxyl value of 32-38 mg/g.
   USE - The carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol is used for slow-rebound polyurethane foam.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol reduces the use of petrochemical resources alkylene oxide, reduces the costs, is environmentally-friendly, forms slow-rebound polyurethane foam with excellent mechanical properties and biodegradability, and is prepared by simple and easy preparation method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol, involving (a) adding small molecule initiator and dimethyl carbonate catalyst to pressure-resistant reaction kettle, uniformly mixing, replacing with carbon dioxide, removing water, introducing propylene oxide for initiating polymerization reaction, and adding propylene oxide and ethylene oxide for reacting to obtain intermediate polycarbonate ether polyol, (b) adding intermediate polycarbonate ether polyol and phosphazene catalyst to pressure-resistant reaction kettle, performing nitrogen replacement, heating, adding propylene oxide and ethylene oxide, performing polymerization reaction, and adding ethylene oxide for end-capping polymerization reaction to obtain crude polycarbonate ether polyol, and (c) adding phosphoric acid, water and adsorbent to crude polycarbonate ether polyol for post-treatment, and performing suction filtration to obtain polycarbonate ether polyol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In polycarbonate ether polyol, the concentration of carbonate groups is 5-15 %mass. In step (b), the added amount of phosphazene catalyst is 0.08-0.40 %mass based on total mass of crude polycarbonate ether polyol.Preferred Components: The small molecule initiator comprises polyether polyols with functionality of 2-3 and molecular weight of 100-1000 g/mol. The dimethyl carbonate catalyst comprises zinc-cobalt double metal cyanide complex catalyst, and the concentration of dimethyl carbonate catalyst is 0.01-0.08% based on total mass of intermediate polycarbonate ether polyol.Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the mass of propylene oxide that initiates polymerization reaction is 5-15 %mass based on total mass of initiator. The step (a) involves reacting at 100-160degrees Celsius, and using carbon dioxide pressure for reaction under 0.5-7 MPa. The step (a) involves feeding propylene oxide and ethylene oxide in fixed ratio, and the concentration of copolymerized ethylene oxide is 70-80 %mass based on total mass of alkylene oxide. The step (b) involves passing propylene oxide and ethylene oxide in fixed ratio, where the concentration of copolymerized ethylene oxide is 70-80% based on quality of alkylene oxide, the mass of ethylene oxide in end-capping polymerization is 2-15 %mass based on total mass of crude polycarbonate ether polyol.
DC A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.)

   ZHAO J

The carbon                dioxide aeration unit comprises swirl aeration                component.
   USE - Gasification ash water aeration removing                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device reduces hardness and turbidity of                gasification ash water, removes suspended solids by                carrying out carbon dioxide aeration, flocculation                and sedimentation and other operations of                gasification ash water, improves utilization rate                of carbon dioxide to make total hardness of                effluent not less than 300 mg/L, turbidity not less                than 5 Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU) and                suspended solids content not less than 20 mg/L,                decreases scaling and fouling tendency of                gasification ash water system, has long-term stable                operation of gasification system, high hardness                removal efficiency, less sludge production, high                degree of automation and small floor area.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for aerating                gasification ash water to remove hardness by device                comprising introducing carbon dioxide to                gasification ash water to be treated for aeration                to form flocs, mixing gasification ash water with                flocculant, performing flocculation sedimentation                to obtain supernatant and precipitating                supernatant.

   First stage flocculation sedimentation unit                (2)
   Two stage flocculation sedimentation unit                (3)
   Precipitation separation unit (4)
   Ph regulator (5)

   Sludge treatment unit (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The material of swirl                  aeration component is stainless steel, carbon                  steel, ethylene propylene rubber, polypropylene                  and/or polyethylene, preferably stainless steel. PN CN111286011-A
TI Carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate polyester            copolymer is a copolymer of carbon dioxide and an epoxy            compound, an epoxy compound having a double bond/ester            group functional group in a side group, and a lactide            monomer.
AU XIE D
   ZHANG Z
   CHEN Y
   WU L
   ZHANG C
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 202056483A
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate polyester                copolymer is a copolymer of carbon dioxide and an                epoxy compound, an epoxy compound having a double                bond/ester group functional group in a side group,                and a lactide monomer. The copolymer has a number                average molecular weight of 6000 to 100,000 and a                molecular weight distribution of 1.1-1.5, the molar                percentage of polycarbonate segments in the                copolymer is 40-80%, the total molar percentage of                double bonds and ester group functional groups                contained in the side groups of the copolymer is                15-40.
   USE - Carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate polyester                copolymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate                polyester copolymer has good hydrophilicity and                water dispersibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate                polyester copolymer, which involves:
   (A) adding epoxy compounds and lactide                monomers with double bond/ester functional groups                in the side groups to the autoclave, with or                without solvent;
   (B) filling the autoclave with carbon dioxide                to maintain the pressure in the autoclave at                1.0-5.0 mpa, where polymerization reaction is                carried out at 20-90 degrees C for 10-48                hours;
   (C) adding large amount of hydrochloric acid                methanol solution to terminate the reaction, after                the completion of the polymerization reaction;                and
   (D) washing resulting product with a large                amount of methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The epoxy compound is                  propylene oxide or epoxycyclohexane. The epoxy                  compound having a pendant group containing a                  double bond/ester functional group is an alkene                  propyl glycidyl ether, glycidyl methacrylate,                  tert-butyl 3,4-epoxybutyrate, o-nitrobenzyl                  2,3-epoxypropionate, 1,4-cyclohexadiene-1 ,                  2-epoxide, 1,2-epoxy-4-vinylcyclohexane, limonene                  epoxide and 3,4-cyclohexene epoxide-1-carboxylic                  acid tert-butyl ester. The structural formula of the metal                  porphyrin catalyst is of formula (I).M=Cr or Co or Al;R2, R3 and R4=hydrogen, fluorine, chlorine,                  bromine, chlorine or substituted or                  bromo-substituted aliphatic groups; andX=Cl, -OCH2CH3, -OCH(CH3)2, Br, CCl3COO-,                  -CF3COO, 2,4-dinitrophenoloxy, 3,5-di One of                  nitrophenoloxy and 2,4,6-trinitrophenoloxy.. The cocatalyst is                  bis-(triphenylphosphoryl) ammonium chloride,                  4-dimethylaminopyridine, 2,6-dimethyl pyridine.                  The solvent is one of dichloromethane, toluene,                  tetrahydrofuran or 1,4-dioxane. Preferred                  Conditions: The molar ratio of metal porphyrin                  catalyst and a cocatalyst is 1:0.5-2. The molar                  ratio of the metalloporphyrin catalyst to the                  lactide monomer 1:50-1000. The molar ratio of                  epoxy compound to lactide monomer is 1-20:1. The                  molar ratio of the epoxy compound and epoxy                  compound in which the pendant group contains a                  double bond/ester functional group is 0.1-1:1.                  The surface water contact angle is lower than 45                  degrees , and the particle size of the latex                  particles is less than 350nm in a 1g/l aqueous                  dispersion.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a production                stream having a gas phase and a liquid phase, the                production stream including oil, hydrocarbyl gas,                carbon dioxide, and water. The water stream is pressurized that                contains carbon dioxide absorbed. The operating pressures of                the various streams are at relatively high                pressures to thereby retain appreciable amounts of                carbon dioxide within the water phase, during the                process of separating targeted hydrocarbons from                the other constituents within the production                stream, such as the water.

   ADVANTAGE - The system improves auxiliary loop starting stability in running process at very low ambient temperatures, realizes auxiliary loop evaporation applying process in a cooling-gas cooler to reduce main loop outlet temperature and reduces energy consumption rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a trans-critical carbon dioxide parallel compressing system capacity ratio determining method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit diagram of a trans-critical carbon dioxide parallel compressing system.
   Carbon dioxide main circuit compressor (1)
   Gas cooler (2)
   Cold evaporator (3)
   Evaporator (5)
   Carbon dioxide auxiliary loop electronic expanding valve (7)
   Water pump (8)
   Fan (9)

TI Performing semi-batch process for making            polycarbonate polyol, involves combining            hydroxyl-containing starter compound, carbonate            catalyst and alkylene oxide per mole of            hydroxyl-containing starter compound in reaction            vessel.
AU NIKBIN N
   BABB D A
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 201838797R
AB    NOVELTY - Performing semi-batch process involves                combining hydroxyl-containing starter compound,                carbonate catalyst and 0.25-5 moles of alkylene                oxide per mole of hydroxyl-containing starter                compound in reaction vessel, pressurizing reactor.                The carbon dioxide is introduced into reaction                vessel. The polymerization of the carbon dioxide                and alkylene oxide is initiated by subjecting                hydroxyl-containing starter compound, carbonate                catalyst, alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide in the                reaction vessel at 40 degrees C and pressure of 138                kilopascal.
   USE - Method for performing semi-batch process for                making polycarbonate polyol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform semi-batch                process that is safe.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing semi-batch process involves                combining hydroxyl-containing starter compound,                carbonate catalyst and 0.25-5 moles of alkylene                oxide per mole of hydroxyl-containing starter                compound in reaction vessel, pressurizing reactor.                The carbon dioxide is introduced into reaction                vessel. The polymerization of the carbon dioxide                and alkylene oxide is initiated by subjecting                hydroxyl-containing starter compound, carbonate                catalyst, alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide in the                reaction vessel at 40 degrees C and pressure of 138                kilopascal. The additional alkylene oxide and                carbon dioxide is feed to the reaction vessel under                polymerization at 150 degrees C without removal of                product such that the alkylene oxide and carbon                dioxide copolymerize to form polycarbonate. The                alkylene oxide is fed to reaction vessel, product                polycarbonate polyol is recovered from reaction                vessel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The reaction vessel is                  pressurized with carbon dioxide. The alkylene                  oxide and carbon dioxide are fed to reaction                  vessel. The alkylene oxide is fed to reaction                  vessel in ratio of 0.005-1.0 moles of carbon                  dioxide per mole of alkylene oxide. The alkylene                  oxide is stored in and fed into the reaction                  vessel from a container vessel that has an                  atmosphere that is 50 mole% carbon dioxide and                  not greater than 10 mole% nitrogen. Preferred                  Components: The carbonate catalyst is represented                  by (I).R1 and R2=hydrogen, halide, nitro group,                  nitrile group, imine, amine, ether group, silyl                  ether group, or acetylide group or optionally                  substituted alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl, haloalkyl,                  aryl, heteroaryl, alicyclic or heteroalicyclic                  group;R3=substituted alkylene, alkenylene,                  alkynylene, heteroalkylene, heteroalkenylene,                  heteroalkynylene, arylene, heteroarylene or                  cycloalkylene, where alkylene, alkenylene,                  alkynylene, heteroalkylene, heteroalkenylene and                  heteroalkynylene, aryl, heteroaryl, alicyclic or                  heteroalicyclic;R4=Hydrogen, substituted aliphatic,                  heteroaliphatic, alicyclic, heteroalicyclic,                  aryl, heteroaryl, alkylheteroaryl or                  alkylaryl;R5=Hydrogen, substituted aliphatic,                  heteroaliphatic, alicyclic, heteroalicyclic,                  aryl, heteroaryl, alkylheteroaryl or                  alkylaryl;E1=Carbon;E2=oxygen, Sulfur or NH;X=OC(O)Rx, OSO2Rx, OSORx, OSO(Rx)2, ORx,                  phosphinate, halide, nitrate, hydroxyl,                  carbonate, amido or optionally substituted                  aliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic,                  heteroalicyclic, aryl or heteroaryl;Rx=hydrogen, or optionally substituted                  aliphatic, haloaliphatic, heteroaliphatic,                  alicyclic, heteroalicyclic, aryl, alkylaryl or                  heteroaryl;G=neutral or anionic donor ligand which is a                  Lewis base; andM=Zn(II), Cr(II), Co(II), Mn(II), Ti(II),                  Mg(II), Fe(II), Cr(III)-X, Co(III)-X, Mn(III)-X,                  Fe(III)-X, Ca(II), Ge(II), A1(III)-X, Ti(III)-X,                  V(III)-X, Ge(IV)-(X)2 or Ti(IV)-(X)2.Preferred Components: The carbonate catalyst                  comprises metal complex of tetradentate ligand                  and double metal cyanide complex. The reaction                  vessel further contains at least one compound in                  which magnesium, Group 3-Group 15 metal, or                  lanthanide series metal ion which is bonded to                  one alkoxide, aryloxy, carboxylate, acyl,                  pyrophosphate, phosphate, thiophosphate,                  dithiophosphate, phosphate ester, thiophosphate                  ester, amide, siloxide, hydride, carbamate or                  hydrocarbon anion. The alkylene oxide is                  propylene oxide. Preferred Condition: The                  temperature is 40-110 degrees C. The pressure is                  275 kilopascal-1.1 megapascal gage.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AE GUANGDONG HYDROGEN ENERGY SCI & TECHNOLO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201805700M
AB    (CN206878109-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a self-draught beer machine, reforming reaction device for water to the methanol reforming reaction to generate hydrogen and carbon dioxide, the carbon dioxide produced by conveying to the pressurizer. pressurizing the beer in the beer delivery to refrigerator, using the existing methanol water reforming hydrogen production mode to obtain the processed raw material with carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide, the beer machine get rid of the dependence on the carbon dioxide bottle, beer on draft machine also can process beer without carbon dioxide bottle, it avoids the various problems caused by using carbon dioxide gas, and further by setting the fuel cell, hydrogen generated by the reforming reaction device for electrochemical reaction in the fuel cell and oxygen to produce electricity and water, then energy produced by respectively supplying refrigerator, a pressurizer and a reforming reaction device and realize the self-supply of the beer, the beer machine also can work without external power supply, it is more convenient to use.

EA    NOVELTY - The machine has a gas supply device for conveying carbon dioxide to a beer keg. A refrigerator is connected with the beer keg. The gas supply device is provided with a pressurizer that is connected with the beer keg. A carbon dioxide reforming reaction device is connected with the pressurizer. A compression device is provided with a fuel cell. A hydrogen outlet of the reforming reaction device is provided with a power supply end to supply the electric energy to the pressurizer and the reforming reaction device. A separating device separates hydrogen and carbon dioxide adsorbent. A vaporizing over-heater is equipped with a conduction oil.
   USE - Self-generating beer machine.
   ADVANTAGE - The machine is convenient to use, and can process beer without carbon dioxide bottle so as to avoids various problems caused by carbon dioxide gas, and utilizes the fuel cell to generate hydrogen by the reforming reaction device during electrochemical reaction in the fuel cell so as to produce electricity. The pressurizer is connected with the reforming reaction device to realize beer self-supply process.

TI Method for predicting carbon dioxide emission of target object, involves determining incidence relation between power energy consumption and fossil energy consumption according to consumption data corresponding to types of specified energy.
AU WANG L
   TANG J
   HOU X
   BAI W
   ZHOU J
   ZHENG X
AE SINOCARBON INNOVATION & INVESTMENT CO (SINO-Non-standard)
GA 2021C45940
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining first consumption data of multiple types of appointed energy sources in multiple preset historical time periods, where appointed energy sources comprise an electric power energy source and a fossil energy source, and the first consumption data is utilized for representing consumption amount of specified energy. Incidence relation between the power energy consumption and the fossil energy consumption is determined according to the consumption data corresponding to types of specified energy. Second consumption data is obtained for real-time power energy of a target object. Carbon dioxide emission of the target object is predicted in real time based on the second consumption data and association relationship.
   USE - Method for predicting carbon dioxide emission of a target object.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables obtaining accurate high time resolution power data, and obtaining the carbon dioxide emission result in real time so as to improve time resolution of the carbon dioxide emission data. The method enables performing regression analysis for multiple proportional coefficients by allowing a prediction module corresponding to the preset historical time period to obtain the analysis result so as to determine association relation of power energy consumption amount and fossil energy consumption according to consumption data and association relationship, thus predicting carbon dioxide emissions of the target object in real-time with higher timeliness in an efficient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (a) a device for predicting carbon dioxide emission of a target object;
   (b) an electronic device comprises a processor and a memory for predicting carbon dioxide emission of a target object;
   (c) a computer readable storage medium for storing a set of instructions for predicting carbon dioxide emission of a target object.

AU CARDON G
   GREGORY R
   TRUEBA A
   VALENTIN S
   GUILLAUME C
   RAPHAELLE G
   ANTONIO T
   SOLENE V
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2021B7026Q
AB    NOVELTY - A biogas production installation consists of a digester which is fed by biomass, comprising a compartment (C1) allowing the anaerobic digestion of the biomass, a compartment (C2) allowing the desorption of the carbon dioxide included in the biomass, two passages allowing the biomass to circulate between the two compartments, and an unit for allowing air to be injected into the compartment (C2).
   USE - Installation used for forming biogas (claimed) e.g. biomethane used as natural gas of fossil fuels for filling station for vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The installation includes the digester which is capable to produce the gas stream enriched in methane, containing at least 60% methane and gas stream enriched in carbon dioxide. The digester produces two gas streams which can be easily purified. By contacting the air with the biomass for the desorption of carbon dioxide, effectively, the carbon dioxide is desorbed and the biogas is enriched in methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of producing biogas, which involves introducing the biomass into the compartment (C1) of the digester, circulating of at least portion (P) of the biomass from the compartment (C1) to the compartment (C2) of the digester, introducing air into the compartment (C2), performing desorption of carbon dioxide included in portion (P) of the biomass located in the compartment (C2), recovering carbon dioxide-enriched air and portion of the carbon dioxide-depleted biomass, circulating the portion of the biomass depleted in carbon dioxide from the compartment (C2) to the compartment (C1), performing anaerobic digestion of biomass in the compartment (C1), and recovering biogas enriched in methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The biogas production installation consists of an agitator which is provided to agitate the biomass in the compartment (C1), an unit for heating the biomass in the compartment (C1) to a temperature of 30-70 degrees C, an air injection nozzles which are placed below the compartment (C2), a pump for circulating the biomass through the passages located between the two compartments, an unit for extracting air enriched in carbon dioxide and an unit for extracting biogas enriched in methane. The compartment (C1) is a cylindrical enclosure surrounded by another cylindrical enclosure constituting the compartment (C2).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step of introducing air into the compartment (C2), the air is an enriched air, preferably air containing at least 22% oxygen.

PN US2020284945-A1; US11353621-B2
TI Method for carbon dioxide sequestration in geologic reservoir minimizes caprock uplift and maximizes volume of carbon dioxide sequestered by adjusting number of injection wells, array formation and injection pressure at each injection well.
AU KHAN S
   KHULIEF Y A
   AL-SHUHAIL A
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 202087935Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining changes in porosity, horizontal stresses, pore pressures, permeabilities, pressure wave velocities and shear wave velocities based on each injection pressure, determining amount of caprock uplift and location of caprock uplift at each injection pressure based on changes, and determining each volume of carbon dioxide sequestered in reservoir (112,114) at each injection pressure after a period of time by a computer. The computer correlates injection pressure at each injection well after the period of time to amount of caprock uplift and volume of carbon dioxide sequestered. The caprock uplift is minimized and the volume of carbon dioxide sequestered is maximized by adjusting the number of injection wells (116a,116b), the array formation and the injection pressure at each injection well.
   USE - Method for carbon dioxide sequestration in geologic reservoir.
   ADVANTAGE - The pore pressure and effective stresses are used to predict subsequent uplift of the caprock, leakage into the subsurface layers and long term stability of the reservoir. Reduces pore pressure build-up and effective stresses and maximizes the reservoir storage capacity by varying the number of carbon dioxide injection wells along with their placement arrangement in naturally fractured carbonate reservoir.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an alarming system for leakage in a geologic reservoir sequestering carbon dioxide; and
   (2) a non-transitory computer readable medium.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a geological model illustrating locations of the Biyadh and Arab Jubaila target locations for carbon dioxide injection and geological layers above and below the Biyadh reservoir.
   Reservoir (112,114)
   Injection wells (116a,116b)
   Caprock (122)
   Subsurface layers (124,126,128,130,132,134,136)
   Tuwaiq mountain (138)

PN CN110437203-A; CN110437203-B
TI Large-scale continuous countercurrent extraction and supercritical carbon dioxide extraction nicotine combined process comprises e.g. drying tobacco waste including tobacco, discarded tobacco or tobacco stems and crushing and extracting.
AU DENG X
   HU W
   YANG D
   SU X
AE HUNAN XINWENTIAN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2019968488
AB    NOVELTY - Large-scale continuous countercurrent extraction and supercritical carbon dioxide extraction nicotine combined process comprises e.g. drying tobacco waste including tobacco, discarded tobacco or tobacco stems and crushing, using high concentration of ethanol solution as an extraction medium and extracting nicotine in tobacco waste powder using continuous countercurrent extraction device, concentrating nicotine extract extracted into nicotine-containing smoke extract, placing smoke extract in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device for nicotine extraction to obtain nicotine primary product, concentrating nicotine primary product, purifying to obtain higher purity nicotine product, loading nicotine-containing cigarette paste with solid powder carrier before placing in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device comprising three extraction vessels, separation vessel 1 and separation vessel 2, and volume of the extraction vessel is 1000 l.
   USE - The method is useful for large-scale continuous countercurrent extraction and supercritical carbon dioxide extraction nicotine combined process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Large-scale continuous countercurrent extraction and supercritical carbon dioxide extraction nicotine combined process comprises (a) drying tobacco waste including tobacco, discarded tobacco or tobacco stems and crushing into 40-200 mesh powder; (b) using high concentration of ethanol solution as an extraction medium and extracting nicotine in tobacco waste powder using continuous countercurrent extraction device; (c) concentrating nicotine extract extracted in step (b) into nicotine-containing smoke extract; and (d) placing smoke extract obtained in step (c) in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device for nicotine extraction to obtain nicotine primary product and concentrating nicotine primary product and purifying to obtain a higher purity nicotine product, loading nicotine-containing cigarette paste with solid powder carrier before placing in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, where supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device comprises three extraction vessels, separation vessel 1 and separation vessel 2, and volume of the extraction vessel is 1000 l.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (b) volume concentration of ethanol solution is 70-98%, preferably 88-92%. In the step (b) pH of ethanol solution ranges from 2-6, preferably 3-5. The mass ratio of ethanol solution to tobacco waste is 6-12:1 and preferably 6-8:1. The solid powder carrier is at least one of kaolin, bentonite, silica, natural tobacco powder, powder of extracted tobacco extract, rice husk powder, wood powder and natural tobacco powder, preferably powder of extracted tobacco extract. The extracted products in separation vessel 1 and separation vessel 2 are collected together and acidified to adjust the pH to 3-6 and acid used in the acidification treatment is one of sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, and citric acid. Preferred Method: In the step (b) continuous countercurrent extraction device extracts nicotine for normal temperature extraction or warm extraction and temperature range for warm extraction is 25-60 degrees C. In the step (b) continuous countercurrent extraction device extracts nicotine by ultrasonic or microwave assisted extraction. In the step (d) adjusting pH of nicotine-containing paste to 9-11 before placing in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device has a working pressure range of 12-60 MPa, extraction temperature range of 32-90 degrees C and extraction time of 60-180 minutes, optimal extraction working pressure ranges from 24-30 MPa, extraction temperature ranges from 50-75 degrees C and extraction time is 90-120 minutes, where supercritical carbon dioxide extraction apparatus uses supercritical carbon dioxide superfluid and an extraction cosolvent for nicotine extraction, supercritical carbon dioxide superfluid is used in an amount of 10-50 times the mass of the smoke extract, and extraction cosolvent is an ethanol solution. The volume concentration of the ethanol solution is 80-98% and amount of extraction cosolvent is 5-50% of the mass of the smoke.

TI Cement industry carbon dioxide excessive discharge comprehensive evaluation method, involves calculating comprehensive index according to weight of index layer and grey association degree of bottom event to determine key risk factor.
AU SI Y
   YANG Q
   LIU J
   LI H
   WANG Q
   HUANG W
AE ZHONGKE YUNYAO DONGGUAN TECHNOLOGY CO (ZHON-Non-standard)
   UNIV DONGGUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDU-C)
GA 2022749529
AB    NOVELTY - The comprehensive assessment method involves building a cement industry carbon dioxide excess discharge accident tree in cement industry. The carbon dioxide excess emission accident tree is converted into a level analysis model and constructed a pair of comparison matrixes. The weight of an index layer is calculated in a hierarchical analysis model. A grey association degree of a bottom event is calculated. A comprehensive index is calculated according to the weight of the index layer and the gray association degree. A key risk factor is determined.

PT P
PN CN112551552-A
TI Preparation of sodium hydroxide using carbon            dioxide, involves mixing calcium oxide and sodium            sulfate solids to obtain mixed powder, adding calcium            hydroxide solution, filtering to obtain calcium            carbonate precipitate and mixed solution.
AU WANG W
   ZHANG W
AE XUANCHENG KAIANG CHEM CO LTD (XUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202136864Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of sodium hydroxide using carbon                dioxide, involves (1) mixing, grinding, and sieving                calcium oxide and sodium sulfate solids to obtain a                mixed powder, (2) adding the mixed powder to the                water and stirring continuously, (3) introducing                carbon dioxide gas while stirring to obtain a mixed                solution, (4) adding calcium hydroxide solution to                the mixed solution, after reacting completely to                obtain turbid solution, (5) allowing the turbid                solution described in step (4) to stand and then                filtering to obtain a calcium carbonate precipitate                and a mixed solution of calcium sulfate and sodium                hydroxide, and (6) adjusting the temperature of the                mixed solution of calcium sulfate and sodium                hydroxide to gradually precipitate calcium                sulfate.
   USE - Preparation of sodium hydroxide using carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method absorbs carbon dioxide produced by                automobile exhaust, improves the absorption rate of                carbon dioxide, produces higher-priced sodium                hydroxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  calcium oxide to calcium sulfate is greater than                  28:71. The concentration of the calcium hydroxide                  solution is 0.015-0.2 mol/L. Preferred                  Conditions: The pressure of the system when the                  carbon dioxide gas is passed through in step (3)                  is 0.5-0.7 MPa. The temperature of the system                  when the carbon dioxide gas is passed through in                  step (3) is 15-20 degrees C. The stirring speed                  of step (2) is 150-180 rpm. The stirring time                  described in step (2) is 50-100 minutes.

MC E11-K03; E11-W; E33-A02

PT P
PN CN112409578-A; CN112409578-B
TI Preparing high molecular weight polybutylene            adipate terephthalate by supercritical carbon dioxide            induced polybutylene adipate terephthalate            crystallization/solid phase polycondensation comprises            e.g. replacing with carbon dioxide gas.
AU XU X
   ZHEN C
AE GOLDEN POLYMER TECHNOLOGY NINGBO CO LTD (GOLD-Non-standard)
GA 202126503D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high molecular weight polybutylene                adipate terephthalate (PBAT) by supercritical                carbon dioxide induced PBAT crystallization/solid                phase polycondensation comprises (1) adding PBAT                raw materials into the reactor, and replacing with                carbon dioxide gas, (2) supercritical carbon                dioxide comprises PBAT crystallization, (3)                discharging pressure relief and discharging, (4)                recrystallizing the product obtained in the step                (3), and (5) solid-phase polycondensating.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high                molecular weight polybutylene adipate terephthalate                by supercritical carbon dioxide induced PBAT                crystallization/solid phase polycondensation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high molecular weight, and                molecular weight of the product PBAT can be                controlled and synthesized in the range of                1.75-2.30x 105 according to actual needs, product                molecular weight distribution index is narrow (PDI                1.55-1.80), excellent mechanical properties, lower                residual monomer content, no odor, excellent color                (white), and excellent hydrolysis resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (2), the                  supercritical carbon dioxide induced PBAT                  crystallization is specifically by introducing                  sufficient carbon dioxide gas into the reactor,                  stirring, raising the temperature to 70-80                  degrees C, increasing the pressure to 13-15 MPa,                  and inducing the crystallization time for 60-120                  minutes. In the step (3), the pressure relief                  specifically comprises maintaining pressure                  relief temperature of 70-100 degrees C, pressure                  relief rate of 1-1.2 MPa/minute, and pressure                  relief to slightly positive pressure of 1.05-1.1                  ATM. In the step (4), recrystallization includes                  grinding, crushing, sieving and drying. The 30-40                  mesh particle sieve is sieved, the drying time is                  1-2 hours, and the drying temperature is 80-90                  degrees C. In the step (5), the solid-phase                  polycondensation reaction temperature is 100-140                  degrees C, the solid-phase polycondensation                  reaction pressure is 10-80 KPaG, and the                  solid-phase polycondensation reaction time is                  2-18 hours. The solid-phase polycondensation                  reaction is carried out in fluidized state.                  Preferred Components: In the step (3), the                  crystallinity of PBAT obtained is 25-30%. In the                  step (5), the fluidizing gas used in the                  solid-phase polycondensation reaction is carbon                  dioxide gas and/or nitrogen. The flow rate of the                  carbon dioxide gas is 3-50 l/minute when the                  fluidizing gas used in the solid-phase                  polycondensation reaction is carbon dioxide gas.                  The weight average molecular weight Mw of the                  PBAT obtained by the solid-phase polycondensation                  is 1.75-2.30x 105, the molecular weight PDI is                  1.55-1.80, and the density is 1.23 plus minus                  0.02 g/cm3. In the step (1), the shape of the                  PBAT raw material added to the reactor is                  granular, flake and/or strip shape. In the step                  (2), the supercritical carbon dioxide is used as                  the inducer, the temperature is greater than 31.1                  degrees C, and the pressure is greater than 7.4                  MPa.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)
MC A05-E04C; A08-M10; A11-A04; A11-B02; A12-S09A

   USE - Preparation of light-burned magnesium oxide by magnesite cracking method.
