AE INST NUCLEAR ENERGY RES ATOMIC ENERGY CO (ATEN-C)
GA 201956633Y
AB    NOVELTY - The present invention provides a carbon dioxide separation system, which comprises a gas supply unit, a pressure boosting unit, a condensing unit and a storage unit. The gas supply unit supplies a gas to be processed, wherein the gas to be processed contains carbon dioxide and nitrogen. The pressure boosting unit is connected to the gas supply unit to receive the gas to be processed. The pressure boosting unit pressurizes the gas to be processed so as to form a pressure-boosted gas with a first pressure. The condensing unit is connected to the pressure boosting unit to receive the pressure-boosted gas. The condensing unit condenses the pressure-boosted gas to reach a first temperature so as to form a carbon dioxide liquid and a processed gas. The storage unit is connected to the condensing unit to receive and store the carbon dioxide liquid.

AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide fracturing fluid thickener                comprises 50-70 wt.% low-molecular-weight polyether                polymer, 6-15 wt.% modified polydimethylsiloxane,                9-15 wt.% vegetable oil, 11-18 wt.% lower alcohol                and 2-4 wt.% tocopherol.
   USE - Carbon dioxide fracturing fluid thickener for                fracturing water-sensitive reservoir.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide fracturing fluid thickener                is inexpensive, environmentally-friendly, reduces                the interfacial tension of crude oil rock, improves                the surface wettability of the formation rock,                saves water resources, has no three wastes                emissions and can be used to improve the carbon                dioxide flooding effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing                carbon dioxide fracturing fluid thickener, which                involves mixing low-molecular-weight polyether                polymer with vegetable oil, then adding lower                alcohol, heating water bath at 40-50 degrees C,                stirring and mixing, then adding modified                polydimethylsiloxane, finally adding tocopherol,                stirring and cooling to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide                  fracturing fluid thickener is mixed with                  supercritical carbon dioxide or liquid carbon                  dioxide, tested at 40 degrees C and 22 MPa, and                  controlled at viscosity of 5.0-9.3                  mPa.second.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The vegetable oil is                  selected from linseed oil, sesame oil, sunflower                  oil, soybean oil, rapeseed oil, olive oil, rice                  bran oil, tung oil, castor oil and/or waste                  cooking oil. The lower alcohol is selected from                  methanol, ethanol, propanol, isopropanol and/or                  butanol. The tocopherol is dihydrobenzopyran-6-ol                  or its derivative. The modified                  polydimethylsiloxane is selected from                  hydroxyl-terminated polydimethylsiloxane,                  amino-terminated polydimethylsiloxane or                  polyether modified polydimethylsiloxane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The                  low-molecular-weight polyether polymer is a                  mixture of polyethyl vinyl ether and                  polypropylene glycol monobutyl ether in a mass                  ratio of (40-55):(11-20).

PN CN114045182-A; CN114045182-B
TI Pyrolysis gas reflux and secondary adsorption            carbon sequestration and emission reduction method            involves converting biomass into biocarbon and liquid            gas, catalytically pyrolyzing gas portion, drying,            adsorbing dried gas, and reacting with carbon dioxide            absorbent to reduce carbon dioxide content.
AU ZHANG P
   HUO L
   JIA J
   YAO Z
   XIE T
   ZHAO L
AE INST ENVIRONMENT & SUSTAINABLE DEV IN AG (CAGS-C)
   CAAS ENVIRONMENT & SUSTAINABLE DEV AGRI (CAGS-C)
GA 2022339394
AB    NOVELTY - Pyrolysis gas reflux and secondary adsorption                carbon sequestration and emission reduction method                involves: (S1) preparing biomass, pretreating and                thermochemical conversion, converting biomass into                biocarbon and liquid gas, refluxing gas portion in                high temperature carbonization zone of pyrolysis                device for catalytic pyrolysis, condensing                generated gaseous product to obtain non-condensable                gas, refluxing the non-condensable gas in the                carbonization zone, and achieving catalytic                conversion of carbon dioxide by using the                conditions of high temperature carbonization in the                carbonization zone to convert it into carbon                monoxide gas; (S2) primary adsorption of the gas                obtained in step (S1), pressurizing the gas and                then adsorbing, drying, and separating the gas                after physical adsorption; and (S3) secondary                adsorption of the dried gas obtained in step (S2),                and using chemical adsorption method to react the                gas with the carbon dioxide absorbent to reduce the                content of carbon dioxide.
   USE - Pyrolysis gas reflux and secondary adsorption                carbon sequestration and emission reduction                method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of high carbon                dioxide content in the pyrolysis gas in the biomass                pyrolysis process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Pyrolysis gas reflux and secondary adsorption                carbon sequestration and emission reduction method                involves: (S1) biomass pyrolysis by preparing                biomass, pretreating and thermochemical conversion,                converting the biomass into biocarbon, and liquid                gas, refluxing gas portion in high temperature                carbonization zone of pyrolysis device for                catalytic pyrolysis, condensing the gaseous product                generated by pyrolysis to obtain non-condensable                gas, where the reflux gas is non-condensable gas,                refluxing the non-condensable gas in the high                temperature carbonization zone, and achieving                catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide by using the                conditions of high temperature carbonization in the                carbonization zone to convert it into carbon                monoxide gas with high calorific value; (S2)                primary adsorption of the gas obtained in step                (S1), using physical adsorption method to                pressurize the gas and then using an adsorbent for                adsorption, drying, and separating the gas after                physical adsorption; and (S3) secondary adsorption                of the dried gas obtained in step (S2), and using                chemical adsorption method to react the gas with                the carbon dioxide absorbent to reduce the content                of carbon dioxide. The secondary adsorption method                involves using the gas with high carbon dioxide                content after primary adsorption as carrier gas,                pressurizing and atomizing, fully mixing with the                carbon dioxide absorbent, adsorbing the carbon                dioxide, separating and secondary drying the gas                after chemical reaction adsorption. The gas after                secondary drying is high calorific value gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pretreatment of the                  biomass involves drying and crushing, drying                  until the water content of the biomass is less                  than 10%, and pyrolyzing at 550degrees Celsius                  for 30 minutes. The gas, liquid or solid obtained                  by separation in steps (S2) and (S3) are                  recycled. The step (S3) involves detecting the                  concentration of carbon dioxide by introducing                  the pyrolysis gas after removing carbon dioxide                  and drying into carbon dioxide concentration                  monitor for detection, adjusting the                  concentration of the atomized carbon dioxide                  absorbent by feeding back to pressurizing                  atomization system when the carbon dioxide                  concentration monitor detects that the                  concentration is greater than 15%, and adjusting                  the pressure to increase the flow rate of the                  carbon dioxide absorbent.Preferred Conditions: The pressurizing                  pressure is 0.101-0.405 MPa.Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide                  absorbent is 0.0224 mol/L saturated calcium                  hydroxide solution, and is atomized and then                  chemically reacted with the gas to adsorb.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The adsorbent is                  methanol solution.

TI Preparing methanol involves adjusting the carbon dioxide gas concentration in the raw material mixed gas by boosting the pressure of the raw material synthesis gas, raising the temperature of the boosted raw material mixed gas.
AU HIROTANI K
   KANDA K
   YOSHIDA N
AE TOYO ENG CORP (TOXS-C)
GA 2020325555
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing methanol involves adjusting the carbon dioxide gas concentration in the raw material mixed gas by boosting the pressure of the raw material synthesis gas, raising the temperature of the boosted raw material mixed gas, introducing the heated raw material synthesis gas into a methanol synthesis reactor equipped with a methanol synthesis catalyst, obtaining a gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and methanol, cooling the obtained gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and methanol to condense methanol to obtain liquid crude methanol. The unreacted gas is seperated, and discharged portion of the separated unreacted gas as a purge gas, and mixed the remaining unreacted gas obtained by removing the purge gas from the unreacted gas with the pressurized raw material synthesis gas as a circulation gas to obtain final prtoduct, where the obtaining the raw material mixed gas, raising the temperature of the raw material mixed gas and the hydrogen.
   USE - Method for preparing methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare methanol reduces the discharge|emission amount of carbon dioxide gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing methanol involves adjusting the carbon dioxide gas concentration in the raw material mixed gas by boosting the pressure of the raw material synthesis gas, raising the temperature of the boosted raw material mixed gas, introducing the heated raw material synthesis gas into a methanol synthesis reactor equipped with a methanol synthesis catalyst, obtaining a gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and methanol, cooling the obtained gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and methanol to condense methanol to obtain liquid crude methanol. The unreacted gas is seperated, and discharged portion of the separated unreacted gas as a purge gas, and mixed the remaining unreacted gas obtained by removing the purge gas from the unreacted gas with the pressurized raw material synthesis gas as a circulation gas to obtain final prtoduct, where the obtaining the raw material mixed gas, raising the temperature of the raw material mixed gas and the hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide of obtaining a gas containing methanol and methanol, the heated raw material mixed gas is introduced into the preliminary methanol synthesis reactor to obtain a gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and methanol, and adding carbon dioxide gas for adjustment to the raw material synthesis gas, or adding carbon dioxide gas for adjustment upstream or downstream of the mixing the circulating gas with the raw material synthesis gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method for producing methanol involves increasing the pressure of the raw material synthesis gas to 5-15 MPa abs, mixing the raw material synthesis gas pressurized with a circulating gas obtained in a subsequent to obtain a raw material mixed gas, raising the temperature of the raw material mixed gas obtained to 200-350 degrees C, introducing the raw material mixed gas into a methanol synthesis pre-reactor equipped with a methanol synthesis catalyst to obtain a first reaction mixed gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and methanol, introducing the first reaction mixed gas obtained into a main reactor for methanol synthesis equipped with a methanol synthesis catalyst, and second reaction mixture containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and methanol obtaining gas. The second reaction mixed gas obtained is cooled, methanol is condensed to obtain a gas-liquid mixture containing the crude methanol and unreacted gas, sepearted a liquid containing condensed crude methanol and an unreacted gas, discharging portion of the unreacted gas, and send the remaining unreacted gas as a circulating gas, added carbon dioxide gas for adjustment to the raw material synthesis gas, added carbon dioxide gas for adjustment after the raw material synthesis gas and the circulated gas are mixed, adjusted the carbon dioxide gas concentration in the raw material mixed gas by adding a carbon dioxide gas for adjustment to the circulating gas recycled. The raw material synthesis gas is a synthesis gas produced by steam reforming of a gas containing a hydrocarbon or a reformed gas derived from a coke oven gas. The method involves heating a heater to raise the temperature of the raw material mixed synthesis gas to 200-350 degrees C. The method involves cooling the first reaction mixed gas to a temperature at which methanol in the first reaction mixed gas is not condensed. The carbon dioxide gas so that the composition of the raw material mixed gas after mixing the carbon dioxide gas for concentration adjustment. The R and M are the following parameters calculated from the gas composition. R = (H2 mole% -CO2 mole%) / (CO mole% + CO2 mole%) M = (H2 mole%)/(2CO mole% + 3CO2 mole%). The catalyst with the same activity is used as methanol synthesis catalyst used by methanol synthesis preliminary-reaction device of process. The carbon dioxide gas for adjustment is a carbon dioxide gas recovered from a flue gas of a steam reforming furnace by a chemical absorption method, a membrane separation method or a pressure fluctuation adsorption method (PSA method), a carbon dioxide gas recovery of an ammonia plant. The carbon dioxide gas is obtained by the method, carbon dioxide gas recovered from the blast furnace gas or coke oven gas produced as a by-product in steel mills by the chemical absorption method, membrane separation method or pressure fluctuation adsorption method (PSA method), at thermal power plants Carbon dioxide gas recovered from the exhaust gas from which the fuel is burned by the chemical absorption method, the membrane separation method or the pressure fluctuation adsorption method (PSA method), and the chemical absorption method, the membrane separation method or the pressure fluctuation adsorption method (from the refuse incineration facility). The portion of raw material mixed gas is bypassed from the methanol synthesis preliminary reactor and introduced into the methanol synthesis main reactor. The methanol synthesis catalyst amount of the methanol synthesis preliminary reactor is such that the design temperature of the methanol synthesis main reactor is near the maximum reaction rate line in the temperature profile of the methanol synthesis main reactor and does not exceed the maximum temperature reaching point.R=1.9-3.0; andM=0.9-2.5.

TI Sulfate/metal oxide-loaded filler used for            desorbing carbon dioxide from amine solution, comprises            porous ceramic filler and catalytically active            components attached to porous ceramic filler, where            catalytically active components comprise sulfate and            metal oxide.
AU CAI M
   CHENG G
   YANG S
   ZHAO H
   YANG C
   LI Y
   GUO Z
   FU K
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 2021A42873
AB    NOVELTY - Sulfate/metal oxide-loaded filler comprises                porous ceramic filler and catalytically active                components attached to the porous ceramic filler,                where the catalytically active components comprise                sulfate and metal oxide.
   USE - The sulfate/metal oxide-loaded filler is used                for desorbing carbon dioxide from amine solution                which is used in a desorption tower of amine                solution desorption of carbon dioxide in a carbon                dioxide capture device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sulfate/metal oxide-loaded filler                effectively solves the various problems caused by                arranging catalysts and packing in the absorption                tower.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Sulfate/metal oxide-loaded filler comprises                porous ceramic filler and catalytically active                components attached to the porous ceramic filler,                where the catalytically active components comprise                sulfate and metal oxide of formula: MxOy.                INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing sulfate/metal                oxide-loaded filler for desorbing carbon dioxide                from amine solution comprising (a) pretreating                porous ceramic filler, (b) obtaining a metal (M)                nitrate solution with a concentration of 5-20                %mass, obtaining an ammonium sulfate solution with                a concentration of 5-20 %mass, (c) immersing the                porous ceramic filler in the metal nitrate                solution, performing ultrasonic impregnation,                drying and calcination, and naturally cooling to                room temperature, (d) immersing the porous ceramic                filler treated in step (c) in an ammonium sulfate                solution, performing ultrasonic impregnation,                drying and calcining, and naturally cooling to room                temperature to obtain the product; and
   (2) the sulfate/metal oxide-loaded packing                used for amine solution desorption of carbon                dioxide in a desorption tower of amine solution                desorption of carbon dioxide in a carbon dioxide                capture device, and the operating temperature of                the amine solution in the desorption tower for                desorption of carbon dioxide is 80-95 degrees                C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The type of porous                  ceramic packing is pall ring, Raschig ring,                  ceramic ball and/or corrugated plate. The metal                  oxide is zinc oxide, copper oxide, aluminum                  oxide, zirconium oxide or cerium oxide. Preferred                  Method: The step (a) involves removing the                  impurities on the surface of the porous ceramic                  packing, using a 50% oxalic acid solution to                  clean the porous ceramic packing, condensing,                  refluxing and boiling for 1 hour, after cooling,                  washing with deionized water until the pH of the                  washing solution of 7, and drying at 110 degrees                  C for 12 hours. The step (c) involves immersing                  the porous ceramic filler in the metal nitrate                  solution, and then ultrasonically immersing and                  drying for 2-4 times, where the ultrasonic                  immersion time is 1-3 hours, the drying                  temperature is 110 degrees C, and the drying time                  is 12 hours, calcining in an inert gas atmosphere                  at a temperature of 500-700 degrees C for 2-4                  hours, and naturally cooling to room temperature.                  The step (d) involves immersing the porous                  ceramic filler treated in step (c) in an ammonium                  sulfate solution, and performing ultrasonic                  immersion and drying for 2-4 times, where the                  ultrasonic immersion time is 1-3 hours, and the                  drying temperature is 110 degrees C, the drying                  time is 12 hours, and increasing the temperature                  to 300-500 degrees C at a heating rate of 5                  degrees C/minute under an inert gas atmosphere,                  and calcining at a constant temperature for 2-4                  hours, and naturally cooling to room temperature                  to obtain the product.

   ENI SPA (ENIE-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Mineralization of carbon dioxide involves                reacting carbon dioxide with a natural mineral                phase comprising a prevalent alkaline-earth metal                silicate, in which alkaline-earth metal is                magnesium, and/or calcium, in the form of fine                particulate material, in an aqueous slurry                containing 35 wt.% finely ground mineral phase and                an alkali metal carbonate or bicarbonate,                preferably sodium carbonate or bicarbonate and/or                potassium carbonate or bicarbonate, at temperature                of 50-300 degrees C and carbon dioxide pressure of                1.0 MPa or more, preferably 2.0 MPa or more, and                washing the solid product obtained from the                reaction with water until removal of the alkaline                metal to obtain a solid carbonated material                to-be-used as an additive for cements.
   USE - Mineralization of carbon dioxide discharged                from capture processes from coal, natural gas and                other fuel combustion fumes, and fumes of                industrial processes of cement, refinery and                petrochemical plants, used for forming solid                carbonated material as additive for cements such as                Portland cement for building material (all claimed)                for construction applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables mineralization of carbon                dioxide by simple post-treatment by carrying at                room temperature and pressure. The carbonated                material has excellent pozzolanic properties and                can be conveniently used as supplementary cement                material in cement formulation, with small                environmental impact in terms of carbon dioxide                emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) solid carbonated material, which comprises                an amorphous silica and at least one alkaline-earth                metal carbonate, and is obtained by mineralization                of carbon dioxide; and
   (2) building material, which comprises 35-99                wt.%, preferably 60-95 wt.% Portland cement and                1-65 wt.%, preferably 5-40 wt.% the solid                carbonated material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves (i)                  preparing a slurry (a) of the natural mineral                  powder phase with diameter of 300 mu m in aqueous                  solution in presence of 35 wt.% or less alkaline                  carbonate or bicarbonate, preferably sodium                  carbonate or bicarbonate and/or potassium                  carbonate or bicarbonate, with respect to the                  weight of the slurry (a), (ii) reacting the                  slurry (a) in a suitable reactor, with carbon                  dioxide maintained at a pressure of 2 MPa or                  more, preferably constant, and at temperature of                  50-300 degrees C, to obtain a slurry (b), (iii)                  discharging the slurry (b) and separating the                  solid phase, optionally recycling the mother                  liquors into step (i) for the preparation of the                  slurry (a), (iv) washing the solid phase obtained                  in step (iii) with water until removal of the                  alkali metals residues, preferably sodium and/or                  potassium, and separating to obtain the solid                  carbonated material and (v) optionally drying the                  obtained solid material. The solid phase in stage                  (iii) or solid phase in step (iv) is separated by                  filtration, decantation or centrifugation.                  Preferred Components: The natural mineral phase                  is olivine. The concentration of the alkaline                  carbonate or bicarbonate in the aqueous slurry or                  in slurry (a) of the stage (i), is 0.1-2.0 M. The                  slurry comprises 25-35 wt.% mineral phase. The                  solid carbonated material comprises 2 wt.% or                  less, preferably 0.5 wt.% or less sodium and/or                  potassium with respect to the total weight of the                  solid material dried in air at 120 degrees C for                  2 hours. Preferred Conditions: The temperature in                  stage (ii) varies between 120-170 degrees                  C.

AB    NOVELTY - The device has a water locking module, a                shearing vibration module and a water storage                mixing module (20) that are arranged in a steel                pipe. The cylinder I is fixed on the cylinder II                which is fixed on the cylinder III fixed on the                capillary orifice plate. The water storage mixing                module is provided with several capillary water                storage orifices arranged in sequence along the                gas-liquid flow direction. A row of water storage                ball stick models is formed between every two                adjacent capillary water storage orifice plates.                The water storage ball stick model is provided with                several cube-shaped ball stick units. The                cube-shaped ball and stick unit is provided with a                funnel-shaped liquid inlet, spherical water storage                grid and capillary discharge tube. The capillary                discharge tube is communicated with the capillary                channel on the capillary water storage orifice                plate.
   USE - Ultra-dry carbon dioxide foam generating                device in ultra-dry carbon dioxide foam system for                continuous oil pipe (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device can work stably under the severe                environment of high temperature and high pressure,                and can reduce the difficulty and complex degree of                the carbon dioxide foam generation. PT P
PN CN111578463-A
TI Method for adjusting opening of fresh air valve of variable air-volume air conditioning system by electronic device, involves adjusting opening of valve based on difference between real-time and preset standard carbon dioxide concentrations.
AU YANG J
   GUO J
   YANG L
AE ENN SHUNENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ENNS-Non-standard)
GA 202084690R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining steady-state temperature and supply air temperature of designated space corresponding to a variable air volume air conditioning system. Corresponding steady-state air supply volume is calculated according to steady-state load of the designated space when the designated space enters into steady state. Amount of carbon dioxide generated in the designated space is determined. Corresponding steady state carbon dioxide concentration is calculated according to total air supply carbon dioxide concentration, the carbon dioxide generation amount and the steady state air supply volume when the designated space enters into the steady state. Real-time carbon dioxide concentration in the designated space corresponding to maximum value of the steady-state carbon dioxide concentration is detected. Opening of a fresh air valve is adjusted according to difference between the real-time carbon dioxide concentration and preset standard carbon dioxide concentration.
   USE - Method for adjusting opening of a fresh air valve of a variable air-volume air conditioning system by an electronic device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables satisfying load requirements of each appointed space, improving air quality of the designated space.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a device for adjusting a fresh air valve of a variable air-volume air conditioning system by an electronic device; and
   (2) a computer readable storage medium for storing a set of instructions for adjusting a fresh air valve of a variable air-volume air conditioning system by an electronic device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for adjusting opening of fresh air valve of variable air-volume air conditioning system by electronic device. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

TI Producing Artemisia princeps extract with reduced fragrance component, useful as e.g. medicine composition for preventing and improving inflammation, comprises performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction in Artemisia princeps extract.

   CHOI W
   PARK D W
   LEE S
AE KOREA TESTING & RES INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202265584B
AB    NOVELTY - Producing Artemisia princeps extract with reduced fragrance component and enhanced anti-inflammatory ingredient content, comprises performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction in Artemisia princeps extract.
   USE - The method is useful to produce Artemisia princeps extract as medicine, health functional food and cosmetic composition for preventing and improving inflammation by inhibiting production of nitric oxide and production of pro-inflammatory cytokines (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has reduced strong fragrance component, has excellent palatability, is non-toxic, increases anti-inflammatory activity, and contains natural product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for medicine composition comprising Artemisia princeps extract produced by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anti-inflammatory active ingredient includes chlorogenic acid, 3,4-di-O-caffeoylquinic acid, 3,5-di-O-caffeoylquinic acid, 4,5-di-O-caffeoylquinic acid, jaceosidin and/or eupatilin. Preferred Method: The Artemisia princeps extract is Artemisia princeps ethanol ultrasonic extract which is obtained by ultrasonic treatment using an ethanol at 40-50 degrees C, with an output amount of 280-290 W for 80-100 minutes. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is carried out under 300-400 bar, at 45-55 degrees C for 2-4 hours. The method further comprises re-extracting with an ethanol solvent.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Nitric oxide synthase inhibitor; Prostaglandin-endoperoxide synthase 2 inhibitor; Prostaglandin E2 inhibitor; Interleukin 1- beta inhibitor; Interleukin-6 inhibitor; Tumor necrosis factor- alpha inhibitor.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of the composition is 0.001-1000 mg/kg/day by oral or parenteral route.
   EXAMPLE - The example provides details of preparation of Artemisia princeps extract that do not support claims.

PN FR3114943-A1
TI Powder composition useful for carbonating beverages comprises first component comprising bicarbonate and second component comprising organic acid, where components react chemically in solution to release carbon dioxide to carbonate beverage.
AU GILLAIN A
   GINEBRE J
AE BUBBLE IT DRINKS (BUBB-Non-standard)
GA 2022516692
AB    NOVELTY - Powder composition comprises a first component comprising at least one bicarbonate or a mixture of bicarbonates, a second component comprising at least one organic acid or a mixture of organic acids. The components are adapted to react chemically in solution to release an amount of carbon dioxide which is sufficient to carbonate the beverage. The first component comprises less than 50 wt.% sodium bicarbonate.
   USE - The powder composition is useful for carbonating beverages.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is packed in boxes comprising several sachets, each of which is packaged a dose of formulation to prepare a given quantity of drink.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 60 wt.% first component and 40 wt.% second component. Preferred Components: The second component comprises at least 50 wt.% malic acid. The first component comprises at least 50 wt.% potassium bicarbonate. The first component comprises mixture of sodium bicarbonate and potassium bicarbonate. The first component has a sodium bicarbonate/potassium bicarbonate weight ratio of 0.3-0.4, preferably 1-3. The quantity of second component is more than the molar quantity necessary for a stoichiometric reaction with the first component. The quantity of second component is greater than 5% of the stoichiometric quantity of component, preferably more than of the order of 10%. The stoichiometric molar ratio between second component and first component is of the order of 0.44, molar ratio of the components in the formulation is 0.50. The composition comprises at least one additional ingredient, preferably natural flavoring and/or a soluble natural fiber. The composition is packaged in unit doses of the order of 20 g to gasify the order of 1 l drink. The first and second components are packaged separately to be mixed extemporaneously at the time of the preparation of the carbonated drink. The formulation is packaged in a dose capable of releasing 5-10 g, preferably 6-7 g carbon dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN CN112820553-A; CN112820553-B
TI Preparation of cellulose proton-type ionic liquid            composite gel electrolyte for capacitor, involves            dissolving cellulose in carbon dioxide/organic alkali            dissolving system, derivatizing, adding organic            synthetic polymer, and crosslinking.
AU XU Q
   ZHU A
   XIE H
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2021586253
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cellulose proton-type ionic                liquid composite gel electrolyte involves                dissolving cellulose in a carbon dioxide/organic                solvent/organic alkali dissolving system, adding a                compound containing an acid anhydride functional                group to perform a derivatization reaction,                sequentially adding an organic synthetic polymer                and a crosslinking agent, uniformly stirring, and                performing crosslinking reaction.
   USE - Preparation of cellulose proton-type ionic                liquid composite gel electrolyte used for capacitor                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly and                economical, and produces cellulose proton-type                ionic liquid composite gel electrolyte having high                ion conductivity at room temperature, wide                electrochemical window, and excellent energy                storage performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The organic solvent                  is at least one of dimethyl sulfoxide,                  N-methylpyrrolidone, tetramethylurea,                  tetraethylurea, N,N-dimethylimidazolinone,                  N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-diethylacetamide,                  pyrrolidone, 2-azahexanone,                  N,N-dimethylpropenylurea, and sulfolane. The                  organic base is chosen from                  2H,3H,4H,6H,7H,8H,9H,10H-pyrimido(1,2-a)azepine                  of formula (II),                  2H,3H,4H,6H,7H,8H-pyrrolo(1,2-a)pyrimidine of                  formula (III),                  2,3-N',3-N',3-N,3-N,5-N',5-N',5-N,5-N-nonamethyl-2,4,6-triaza-3,5-diphosphaocta-3,5-diene-3,3,5,5-tetramine                  of formula (IV), imidazole compound of formula                  (V), 1,1,3,3-tetramethylguanidine compound of                  formula (VI), and                  1,3-dimethylimidazolidin-2-imine compound of                  formula (VII). The                  acid-anhydride-functional-group-containing                  compound is chosen from 24 compounds, e.g.                  compounds of formulae (VIII)-(XII). The                  crosslinking agent is an epoxy compound.R1 = H or 1-6C alkyl;andn = not defined.N.B: n is not defined. Preferred Property:                  The organic base has an acid-base dissociation                  constant of more than 20.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: Preparation of the                  cellulose proton-type ionic liquid composite gel                  electrolyte preferably involves mixing 0.1-30                  %mass cellulose, an organic solvent, and 0.1-50                  %mass organic base, charging carbon dioxide at a                  pressure of 0.1-15 MPa into the mixed system for                  reaction at 50-150 degrees C for 1-24 hours to                  obtain a light-yellow transparent liquid, adding                  the compound containing an acid anhydride                  functional group to the light-yellow transparent                  liquid, performing derivatization reaction with                  stirring at 20-100 degrees C for 1-24 hours,                  adding organic synthetic high-molecular polymer                  to the reaction product, stirring and dissolving                  at 20-100 degrees C for 0.1-3 hours, adding the                  crosslinking agent to the solution, evenly                  stirring, transferring to a mold, and reacting at                  20-100 degrees C for 1-24 hours. Preferred                  Composition: The cellulose raw material is one or                  more of plant cellulose separated from                  microcrystalline cellulose, alpha -cellulose,                  cotton, wood pulp, bamboo pulp, or agricultural                  and forestry lignocellulose waste. The cellulose                  is of formula (I). The molar ratio of total                  amount of compound containing acid anhydride                  functional group to glucose unit in the                  light-yellow transparent liquid is 0.5:1-5:1. The                  mass ratio of organic synthetic polymer to                  cellulose is 0.1:1-10:1. The organic synthetic                  polymer includes one or more of polyvinyl                  alcohol, polyethylene glycol, polyacrylic acid,                  polyacrylamide, polystyrene maleic anhydride                  copolymer, polymethoxyethylene maleic anhydride                  copolymer, styrene and acrylic acid copolymer,                  polymethylmethacrylate, polyacrylonitrile,                  polyvinylidene fluoride, and polyvinylidene                  fluoride-hexafluoropropylene. The molar ratio of                  crosslinking agent and cellulose is 0.5:1-1:10.                  The mold is a polytetrafluoroethylene mold, a                  glass mold or a stainless steel mold.p = 50-1000                  (excluding 50 and 1000).

PN JP2021062882-A
TI Package used for packaging time indicator, comprises hygroscopic laminated film containing sealant layer.
AU NAKATA K
   WATANABE M
AE DAINIPPON PRINTING CO LTD (NIPQ-C)
GA 202141865G
AB    NOVELTY - A package reacts with carbon dioxide and water and changes its color tone according to the time history of absorbing carbon dioxide, and comprises at least a hygroscopic laminated film (10) comprising a sealant layer (4) and a gas barrier layer (3) in this order from the inner layer side, and further containing a hygroscopic layer (5) on the inner layer side of the gas barrier layer.
   USE - Package used for packaging time indicator (claimed) and time indicator loose-sample product.
   ADVANTAGE - The package contains hygroscopic layer containing economical hygroscopic resin excellent hygroscopicity, and hygroscopic layer having excellent strength and flexibility.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the package.
   Protective layer (2)
   Gas barrier layer (3)
   Sealant layer (4)
   Hygroscopic layer (5)
   Hygroscopic laminated film (10)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The moisture absorbing layer is arranged between the sealant layer and the gas barrier layer. The sealant layer also serves as the moisture absorbing layer. The hygroscopic laminated film comprises the moisture absorbing layer on the inner layer side of the sealant layer, and the moisture absorbing layer which is arranged in a region other than the joint portion. The package is a bonded portion of two films, and at least one of the two films is the hygroscopic laminated film. The package further comprises a container component containing a storage portion and a flange portion for accommodating the time indicator, a lid material, at least one of the container component and the lid material containing the hygroscopic laminated film, and an external patch with a time indicator which is stored and sealed, a release layer and the moisture absorbing layer on the time indicator side arranged adjacent to the indicator layer in this order. The time indicator is housed and sealed. The time indicator is a base material and an indicator layer arranged on one surface of the base material, and containing a time indicator side moisture absorbing layer arranged adjacent to the indicator layer. The indicator layer comprises calcium hydroxide, an indicator dye, and a binder resin. The external patch with the time indicator comprises the indicator layer, a support layer, an adhesive layer containing a drug and an adhesive base. The indicator dye is a compound whose color tone changes when the pH changes from the alkaline region to the neutral region.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The hygroscopic layer contains a hygroscopic resin. The hygroscopic layer further contains a resin and a hygroscopic agent.

PT P
PN WO2020231970-A1
TI Producing winterized Cannabis oleoresin involves providing crude cannabis extract from cannabis bio-material by either liquid alkane-based solvent or super-critical carbon dioxide, crude cannabis extract contains cannabinoids and lipids.
AU TENNANT N
AE PRECISION EXTRACTION SOLUTIONS (PREC-Non-standard)
GA 2020B4919J
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a winterized Cannabis oleoresin involves providing a crude Cannabis extract from Cannabis bio-material by either a liquid alkane-based solvent or super-critical carbon dioxide, the crude Cannabis extract contains at least cannabinoids and lipids, and then combining the crude Cannabis extract with methanol to produce a crude winterized solution, the lipids precipitating out of the crude winterized solution, filtering the crude winterized solution to remove the lipids and produce a winterized extract solution as the filtrate, and removing the methanol from the winterized extract solution to produce a winterized Cannabis oleoresin.
   USE - Method for producing winterized Cannabis oleoresin (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The liquid alkane-based solvent includes n-heptane. The cannabinoids are based on a combined amount of CBD, CBDA, CBGA, THC, CBC, and THCA. Preferred Composition: The liquid alkane-based solvent consists of at least 95% of n-heptane by volume percentage. The crude Cannabis extract has a relative concentration of chlorophyll to cannabinoids, as by units of mass per unit volume is less than or equal to 0.01, preferably, less than or equal to 0.005, more preferably, less than or equal to 0.01. The composition of matter of a crude Cannabis extract, or a winterized Cannabis oleoresin produced from the crude Cannabis extract, including chlorophyll and cannabinoids, with a relative concentration of chlorophyll to cannabinoids, as by units of mass per unit volume, is less than or equal to 0.01, preferably, less than or equal to 0.005. Preferred Conditions: In the combining step, the crude Cannabis extract and the methanol are both at a temperature of 50 degrees F to 90 degrees F. The step of maintaining the crude winterized solution is performed at 50 degrees F to 90 degrees F for a dwell time of 1 minute to 24 hours, the dwell time ending at initiation of the filtering of the crude winterized solution. The dwell time is 1 minute to about 8 hours. In the step combining, the crude Cannabis extract and the methanol are both at a temperature of 50 degrees CF to 90 degrees CF. In the step combining, the crude Cannabis extract and the methanol are both at a temperature of not less than 34 F. Preferred Process: The step of providing the crude Cannabis extract includes combining the Cannabis bio-material and the liquid alkane-based solvent to provide a crude Cannabis mixture, followed by removing the liquid alkane-based solvent from the crude Cannabis mixture to produce the crude Cannabis extract, preferably, includes combining the Cannabis bio-material and the supercritical carbon dioxide to provide a crude Cannabis mixture, followed by removing the supercritical carbon dioxide from the crude Cannabis mixture to produce the crude Cannabis extract. The method for producing a winterized Cannabis oleoresin, involves combining n-hexane and Cannabis bio-material to provide a crude Cannabis mixture, the n-heptane dissolving at least cannabinoids and lipids from the Cannabis bio-material, then removing the n-heptane from the crude Cannabis mixture to produce a crude Cannabis extract that contains at least the cannabinoids and lipids, combining the crude Cannabis extract with methanol to produce a crude winterized solution, the lipids precipitating out of the crude winterized solution, then filtering the crude winterized solution to remove the lipids and produce a winterized extract solution as the filtrate, and removing the methanol from the winterized extract solution to produce a winterized Cannabis oleoresin.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D18 (Skins, hides, pelts, leather and chemical treatment of tobacco.); P15 (Tobacco (A24).)

AB    NOVELTY - Treatment agent comprises 60-70 pts. wt. light calcium carbonate, 5-10 pts. wt. ferrous sulfate, 15-20 pts. wt. manganese sulfate and 15-20 pts. wt. metatitanic acid.
   USE - The treatment agent is used for waste gas containing polychloroaromatic hydrocarbon (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The agent has low cost, high hydrolytic activity and combustion activity of organic components, realizes dechlorination of PAHs and catalytic combustion of by-products without organic components at 300-350 degrees C, converting into calcium chloride, carbon dioxide and water, is easy to reach emission standard, harmless and easy to be discarded and treated and does not affect long-term storage performance, conversion capacity and dechlorination capacity by carbon dioxide contained in waste gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of treatment agent comprises placing 90-110 pts. wt. water to reactor, adding ferrous sulfate and manganese sulfate to reactor, mixing, adding metatitanic acid, crushing, adding light calcium carbonate powder, reacting at 80-95 degrees c, aging for 2-3 hours to obtain slurry containing calcium sulfate fiber, placing light calcium carbonate powder in kneading machine, adding Sesbania powder, mixing, sprinkling slurry containing calcium sulfate fiber, kneading, extruding strip, drying extruding strip and roasting in air at 420-450 degrees C for 2-4 hours. Preferred Components: The metatitanic acid is intermediate in production process of titanium dioxide by sulfuric acid method. The ferrous sulfate heptahydrate is byproduct of the Russian Japanese sulfuric acid titanium dioxide plant. The exhaust gas comprises not greater than 5 wt.% oxygen and 3-5 wt.% water vapor.

TI Separation of carbon isotope for producing            concentrated carbon isotope used as tracer in e.g.            chemical application, involves photodissociating cooled            formaldehyde gas to produce mixed gas containing carbon            isotope-containing carbon dioxide.
AU JEONG D
   LEE L
   KIM Y

   KWANG-HOON K
   KIM T S

AE KOREA ATOMIC ENERGY RES INST (KAER-C)
   KOREA ATOMIC ENERGY RES INST (KAER-C)
   KOREA ATOMIC ENERGY RES INST (KAER-C)
GA 202045554B
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of carbon isotope involves cooling                a formaldehyde gas to 190-250 K, and                photodissociating the cooled formaldehyde gas to                produce a mixed gas including a carbon                isotope-containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and                residual formaldehyde.
   USE - Separation of carbon isotope (13C) for                producing concentrated carbon isotope (claimed)                used as tracer in chemical application, biochemical                application, and environmental application.
   ADVANTAGE - The method separates carbon isotope with high                selectivity and high energy efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                concentration of carbon isotope, which involves                obtaining a mixed gas comprising carbon                isotope-containing carbon monoxide and hydrogen by                performing separation of carbon isotope.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: Cooling is performed                  in a cooling bath or a cooler including a mixture                  of ethanol and dry ice. Photodissociation is                  carried out under a pressure of 0.01-5 Torr using                  an optical fiber laser having a wavenumber of                  28396.1-28401.3 cm-1. Preferred Process:                  Separation of carbon isotope further involves                  separating the residual formaldehyde by cooling                  and condensing the mixed gas and residual                  formaldehyde at 181 K (-92 degrees C) or                  less.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing a soil carbon sample with biological carbon in a transparent culture bottle. The transparent culture bottle is filled with sodium hydroxide solution and placed in a non-transparent screw mouth bottle. The non-transparent screw mouth bottle is sealed in a thermo tank. PN CN110643065-A; CN110643065-B
TI Preparing composite rubber additive by adding intermediate product of 2-mercaptobenzothiazole to pressurizing tank, heating and introducing carbon dioxide, regulating pressure and temperature, and performing condensing granulation.
AU XU C
   XIN Z
   ZHOU R
   CHEN C
   LIU J
   ZHANG S
   LV G
   SHAN X
   YAO X
AE SHANDONG SUNSINE CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 202004885W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing composite rubber additive involves adding an intermediate product of 2-mercaptobenzothiazole (MTB) in aniline high-temperature high-pressure method to a pressurizing tank, heating and introducing carbon dioxide, regulating the pressure in the pressurizing tank to 72.9 atm or above, maintaining the temperature for a certain time after the molten material reaches the equilibrium state, taking out the material and performing condensing granulation.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing composite rubber additive (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite rubber additive is porous, easy-to-disperse and multifunctional, has improved dispersibility in rubber matrix and poor scorch resistance, and improves mechanical properties of rubber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The temperature when the MBT intermediate product is added to the pressurized tank or the temperature of the material in the pressurized tank is 31.26-231.26 degrees C, preferably 80.26-131.26 degrees C. In the method, the pressure in the pressurized tank is adjust to 72.9-300 atm. The temperature is maintained for 5-20 minutes. The condensing granulation is performed by a rotating steel belt type condensing granulator. Preferred Method: The rotary steel belt type condensing granulator is used to prepare the material into flake or hemispherical samples. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of carbon dioxide and MBT intermediate product is 1:1000-1:5, preferably 1:500-1:100.

PN CN110586183-A; CN110586183-B
TI New covalent organic framework material useful in preparing titanium dioxide/covalent organic framework photocatalytic material as catalyst by photo-catalysis.
AU LIU L
   ZHANG J
   SHI J
   ZHANG B
   CHEN G
   YANG G
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI CHEM INST (CSCI-C)
GA 2019A76978
AB    NOVELTY - Covalent organic framework material i.e. nitrogen substituted biphenyl compounds (I) are new.
   USE - (I) is useful in preparing titanium dioxide/covalent organic framework photocatalytic material as catalyst by photo-catalysis (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes supercritical carbon dioxide which ensures control synthesis of TiO2/COF hollow sphere heterojunction photocatalytic materials; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Covalent organic framework material i.e. nitrogen substituted biphenyl compounds of formula (I) are new.
   A = biphenyl-4,4'-diyl.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) preparing titanium dioxide/covalent organic framework (TiO2/COF) photocatalytic material comprising combination of (I) and supercritical carbon dioxide; and
   (3) photocatalytic composition comprising TiO2/COF photocatalytic material and noble metal nanoparticles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises mixing pyromellitic formaldehyde and biphenyldiamine in solvent and then carrying out amine-aldehyde condensation reaction to obtain (I). Preferred Components: The molar ratio between pyromellitic formaldehyde and biphenyldiamine is 0.9:1-1.5, preferably 0.9:1.35; the solvent is mixed solution of dioxane and aqueous acetic acid solution in a volume ratio of 50:9, the mass ratio between pyromellitic formaldehyde and dioxane is 0.9:10 mmol/ml, the reaction temperature is 100-150 degrees C, preferably 120 degrees C, and reaction time is 72 hours. Preferred Method: The preparation method of TiO2/COF photocatalytic material comprises dispersing titanium dioxide nano-particles uniformly on the inner and outer surfaces of (I). The method further comprises taking tetrabutyl titanate (TBT), water, ethanol and (I) as raw material and performing hydrolysis reaction in presence of supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain final product. The mass ratio between TBT and (I) is 1:1-2; the hydrolysis reaction temperature is 100-150 degrees C, preferably 120 degrees C, hydrolysis reaction time is 12-24 hours, and pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide is 0-5.52 MPa. The photo-catalysis reaction is photocatalytic water cracking visible light catalyzed reaction, where visible light has wavelength of 380-780 nm, the noble metal nano-particles are platinum or gold nano-particles, reaction time is 4-12 hours and the reaction temperature is room temperature, preferably 25 degrees C.

   ADVANTAGE - The mesoporous polyionic liquid catalyst has typical polycation framework, rich and good thermal stability of halogen, recycle stability, excellent catalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Mesoporous polyionic liquid catalyst is represented by formula (I).. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing mesoporous polyionic liquid catalyst, which involves performing solvothermal reaction of N-vinylimidazole and dibromomethane to obtain dinuclear ionic liquid polymerizable monomer bis-(3-vinyl-1-imidazole)methylenebisbromo salt (C1DVIM)Br, followed by polyethylene, adding initiator to alcohol-water solvent system, polymerization monomer complete free radical self-polymerization reaction to prepare mesoporous polyionic liquid catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The initiator is azobisisobutyronitrile or 2,2'-azobis(2-methylpropylguanidine) dihydrochloride. The epoxy compound substrate is styrene oxide, epichlorohydrin, phenyl glycidyl ether, 1,2-epoxyhexane, 1,2-epoxydecane, 1,2-epoxydodecane 1,2-epoxytetradecane. Preferred Conditions: The ratio of N-vinylimidazole to dibromomethane is 1.5-2.5:1. The ratio of polyethylene glycol to monomer (C1DVIM)Br is 10-50:1, ratio of polyethylene glycol to water is 5-50:1, and amount of initiator monomer is 5-20%. Preferred Process: The metrhod further involves synthesizing dinuclear ionic liquid polymerizable monomer bis-(3-vinyl-1-imidazole)methylenebisbromo salt (C1DVIM)Br, dissolving N-vinylimidazole and dibromomethane in tetrahydrofuran and stirring at room temperature, placing in oven for solvent heat treatment, after cooling to room temperature, yellow crystalline product is obtained, washing to obtain pure monomer (C1DVIM)Br, synthesizing mesoporous polyionic liquid catalyst monomer (C1DVIM)Br, adding polyethylene glycol and initiator to reaction tube, followed by addition of cosolvent deionized water, stirring at room temperature, and static in oil bath, open heating to complete free radical self-polymerization, curing mixture to wash and dry to obtain mesoporous polyionic liquid catalyst. The epoxy compound is used as substrate raw material, and under catalysis of the mesoporous polyionic liquid catalyst, reflux reaction is carry out under normal pressure condition with condenser connect to carbon dioxide balloon, and after reaction is completed, catalyst is recovered by filtration and reuse.

PN CN109010133-A
TI Oil-soluble rose fruit extract useful for cosmetic            whitening agent and/or an antioxidant, prepared by            drying fresh rose fruit raw material, pulverizing dried            rose fruit, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide            extraction and separating.
AU YANG Z
AE SHENZHEN XIANDI COSMETICS CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A54496
AB    NOVELTY - Oil-soluble rose fruit extract is claimed. The                preparation of oil-soluble rose fruit extract                comprises (i) drying fresh rose fruit raw material                under vacuum until water content is 5-8%; (ii)                pulverizing dried rose fruit to 20-60 meshes; (iii)                adding crushed rose fruit material into the                extraction tank; and (iv) carrying out                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and                separating.
   USE - The oil-soluble rose fruit extract is useful                for cosmetic whitening agent and/or an antioxidant                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The oil-soluble rose fruit extract: is safe                and does not produce allergic condition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The rose fruit                  comprises rose fruit, rose seed, rose leaf and                  rose flower with weight content of 85 wt.% or                  less than 85 wt.%. The auxiliary extracting agent                  comprises at least one acetone, ethanol and ethyl                  acetate. The proportion of the pulverized rosehip                  material in extraction tank in feeding step is                  60-80%, and the auxiliary extractant is 5-10 wt.%                  in extraction tank. Preferred Method: The drying                  is carried out at lower than 70 degrees C, higher                  than -10 degrees C; and pulverizing is carried                  out using lower than 70 degrees C, higher than                  -10 degrees C. The feeding step further includes                  introducing an auxiliary extracting agent into                  the extraction tank. The extraction is carried                  out at less than or equal to 60 degrees C,                  greater than or equal to -10 degrees C at 25-35                  MPa, extracting for 3-5 hours and controlling                  carbon dioxide flow rate at 200-300 kg/h.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The separating in                  extracting step, comprises a first separating                  tank and a second separating tank, where the                  first separating tank temperature is lower than                  70 degrees C, higher than -10 degrees C, the                  pressure is 7-20 MPa, and the second separating                  tank temperature is lower than 50 degrees C,                  higher than -10 degrees C at pressure is 1-6                  MPa.

   SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
GA 201859146P
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing formaldehyde oxidation catalyst, involves (a) preparing solvents with different polarities according to certain ratio, (b) adding precipitating agent and surfactant to special solution to obtain mixed solution, (c) adding aqueous solution of transition metal salt to obtain precipitate, washing the obtained precipitate and drying to obtain catalyst precursor, (d) calcining the catalyst precursor to obtain formaldehyde oxidation catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing formaldehyde oxidation catalyst for catalytic oxidation of formaldehyde to water and carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of cubical shaped formaldehyde oxidation catalyst with high activity and removes formaldehyde in the indoor air at room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The transition metal salt is inorganic salt of transition metal element or organic salt of transition metal element.    TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The solvent is selected from water, ethanol, cyclohexane, dodecane and/or butanol. The solvent with different polarities is a combination of a medium-high polar solvent and a low-polar solvent. The medium-high polar solvent is selected from water, ethanol and/or butanol. The low-polar solvent is selected cyclohexane and/or dodecane. The molar ratio of precipitating agent to transition metal salt is 1:(1-3). The molar ratio of surfactant to transition metal salt is 100:(1-10). Preferred Conditions: The step (c) comprises drying at 40-130 degrees C. The step (d) comprises calcining at 200-800 degrees C.

PN US2022002889-A1; CA3130915-A1
TI Reducing carbon dioxide emission in production of            chemical compound or product, involves selecting            reagent required for production, and replacing the            reagent with an equivalent reagent.
AU RAU G H
AE PLANETARY HYDROGEN INC (PLAN-Non-standard)
   PLANETARY HYDROGEN INC (PLAN-Non-standard)
GA 202207145E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting a reagent                required for production; and replacing the reagent                with an equivalent reagent produced so that the                total amount of carbon dioxide (CO2) emitted in the                production and use of the chemical compound is                smaller than the predetermined amount.
   USE - Method for reducing carbon dioxide (CO2)                emission in a production and use of a chemical                compound or product during which a predetermined                amount of CO2 is emitted to the atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The method helps in reducing net CO2emissions                of fossil fuels in an ecofriendly manner and cost                effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for reducing carbon dioxide emissions, which                comprises unit for production and use of a chemical                compound, during which a predetermined amount of                carbon dioxide is emitted to the atmosphere, and                unit for producing the reagent so that an amount of                carbon dioxide emitted in the production of the                chemical compound is smaller than the predetermined                amount.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reagent is                  selected from hydrogen (H2), oxygen (O2) and                  chlorine. The chemical compound is one of a                  chemical compound containing hydrogen atoms, a                  chemical compound containing oxygen atoms, a                  chemical compound containing chlorine atoms, and                  a chemical compound containing a reduced metal.                  The chemical compound is selected from                  hydrocarbons, organic compounds, acids, ammonia,                  peroxide, and water. The gas is hydrogen, oxygen                  or chlorine gas. The metal hydroxide contains                  metal ions from group 1 through group 6 elements                  of the periodic table. The carbon dioxide is                  derived from one or more of atmosphere, biomass,                  soil or the ocean, and a fossil source of the                  carbon dioxide. The metal hydroxide is sodium                  hydroxide (NaOH) or potassium hydroxide (KOH),                  and the another metal hydroxide is magnesium                  hydroxide (Mg(OH)2) or calcium hydroxide                  (Ca(OH)2). Preferred Conditions: The chemical                  compound is obtained by a process selected from                  metal oxide smelting, oil refining, hydrogenation                  of margarine, and oxidation or partial oxidation                  of compounds. The reagent is a gas produced in an                  electrochemical cell. The metal hydroxide                  co-formed in the cell is contacted and reacted                  with carbon dioxide to form one or more of the                  metal bicarbonate and the metal carbonate, for                  sequestering the carbon dioxide from the                  atmosphere. Preferred Process: The method further                  involves substituting the gas with a                  negative-CO2-emission gas for reducing the                  predetermined amount of CO2emissions. The                  negative-CO2-emission gas is produced by                  supplying a direct current from an electrical                  source at a predetermined voltage to an                  electrolytic container having an anode, a                  cathode, an electrolyte solution comprising a                  metal salt, an anode region adapted to generate                  an oxidative gas and an acidic solution, and a                  cathode region adapted to generate hydrogen gas                  and a dissolved metal hydroxide solution. The                  metal in the dissolved metal hydroxide solution                  is derived from the metal salt of the electrolyte                  solution. The metal silicate soluble in the                  acidic solution is supplied from a source                  disposed externally to the electrolytic                  container. The acidic solution is removed from                  the anode region to another container outside the                  electrolytic container, for reacting the removed                  acidic solution with the metal silicate to                  generate a metal salt solution. The metal is                  derived from the metal silicate. The metal salt                  solution is reacted with the dissolved metal                  hydroxide solution to produce a reaction solution                  and generate another metal hydroxide. The another                  metal hydroxide is separated from the remaining                  reaction solution and exposed to carbon dioxide                  from air causing a reaction with the carbon                  dioxide to form one or more of the metal                  bicarbonate and the metal carbonate for removing                  and sequestering the carbon dioxide from the                  atmosphere.

TI Preparing nickel ion-modified bismuth oxygen            sulfur photocatalyst used for reducing carbon dioxide            to methane, by hydrothermally reacting using bismuth            nitrate pentahydrate, thiourea, water, and nickel            nitrate hexahydrate.
AU ZHANG G
   JIANG L
   LI Y
AE UNIV WUHAN POLYTECHNIC SHENZHEN RES INST (UYWU-Non-standard)
GA 202168600K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing nickel ion-modified                bismuth oxygen sulfur photocatalyst, involves using                bismuth nitrate pentahydrate, thiourea, lithium                hydroxide monohydrate, water, and nickel nitrate                hexahydrate as raw materials, and performing                hydrothermal reaction to obtain the nickel ion                modified bismuth oxygen sulfur photocatalyst.
   USE - The method is used for preparing nickel                ion-modified bismuth oxygen sulfur photocatalyst                for reducing carbon dioxide to methane                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation, safety, and                low cost. The nickel ion-modified bismuth oxygen                sulfur photocatalyst has high photo-generated                carrier separation efficiency, ability to adsorb                and activate carbon dioxide, excellent                photoreduction of carbon dioxide, photocatalytic                performance of methane and cycle stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the use                of the nickel ion-modified bismuth oxysulfide                photocatalyst, which involves using the nickel ion                modified bismuth oxysulfide photocatalyst under                full-spectrum light irradiation with 99% carbon                dioxide and air atmosphere to reduce carbon dioxide                to methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves (i)                  dissolving bismuth nitrate pentahydrate and                  thiourea in water and thoroughly stirring, (ii)                  adding lithium hydroxide monohydrate to the mixed                  solution obtained in step (i), and completely                  stirring, (iii) adding nickel nitrate hexahydrate                  to the mixed solution of step (ii), and                  completely stirring to obtain a mixed solution,                  and (iv) transferring the mixed solution obtained                  in step (iii) to a hydrothermal kettle, and                  performing hydrothermal reaction to obtain the                  nickel ion-modified bismuth oxygen sulfur                  photocatalyst. Preferred Conditions: The                  hydrothermal reaction is carried out at 200                  degrees C for 72 hours. Preferred Composition:                  The molar ratio of nickel/bismuth of the nickel                  nitrate hexahydrate and the bismuth nitrate                  pentahydrate is 0.16-0.32, preferably 0.16, 0.24,                  and 0.32.
