PT P
PN CN108181201-A
TI Processing experimental method for producing shale            gas by carbon dioxide replacement comprises e.g.            placing the shale rock sample in a triaxial stress core            holder, pressurizing, and heating the shale rock            sample.
AU LONG Y
   LIU J
   SONG F
   XU L
   HUANG X
   WANG R
   YIN D
   WANG Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG OCEAN (UYZO-C)
GA 2018503012
AB    NOVELTY - Processing experimental method for producing                shale gas by carbon dioxide replacement comprises                e.g. (i) placing the shale rock sample in a                triaxial stress core holder, pressurizing, heating                the shale rock sample and maintaining at constant                temperature, pumping out the air in the triaxial                stress core holder, and bringing the vacuum in the                triaxial stress core holder to a preset value, (ii)                injecting methane into the triaxial stress core                holder at constant pressure, until the shale rock                sample adsorbs methane, and replacing methane                adsorbed by shale rock samples with carbon dioxide,                (iii) measuring the mass of the mixed gas of carbon                dioxide and methane in the triaxial stress core                holder by the fluid collection metrology system and                filtering, absorbing carbon dioxide in the mixed                gas, and measuring the mass of methane in the mixed                gas, (iv) collecting and recording each parameter,                and (v) changing the permeability of the shale rock                sample or changing the injection pressure of                methane.
   USE - The method is useful for processing                experimental method for producing shale gas by                carbon dioxide replacement.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing experimental method for producing                shale gas by carbon dioxide replacement comprises                (i) placing the shale rock sample in a triaxial                stress core holder, pressurizing the triaxial                stress core holder to preset pressure, heating the                shale rock sample and maintaining at constant                temperature, pumping out the air in the triaxial                stress core holder, and bringing the vacuum in the                triaxial stress core holder to a preset value, (ii)                injecting methane into the triaxial stress core                holder at constant pressure, until the shale rock                sample adsorbs methane, injecting carbon dioxide                into the triaxial stress core holder at constant                pressure, and replacing methane adsorbed by shale                rock samples with carbon dioxide, (iii) measuring                the mass of the mixed gas of carbon dioxide and                methane in the triaxial stress core holder by the                fluid collection metrology system and filtering the                mixed gas, absorbing carbon dioxide in the mixed                gas, and measuring the mass of methane in the mixed                gas, (iv) collecting and recording each parameter                in the steps (i) to (iii) to calculate the recovery                factor, and (v) changing the permeability of the                shale rock sample or changing the injection                pressure of methane, and the magnitude of the                carbon dioxide injection pressure, and repeating                the steps (i) to (iv). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also                included for experimental device for producing                shale gas by carbon dioxide replacement, comprising                using triaxial stress core holder for placing shale                rock samples, and forming environment simulation                system, controlling the environment in the triaxial                stress core holder, simulating the shale rock                sample in the formation environment, using vacuum                pumping system for pumping gas from triaxial stress                core holder, using fluid injection system for                injecting carbon dioxde, and methane into a                triaxial stress core holder, using fluid collection                metering system for measuring the amount of carbon                dioxide and/or methane discharged from triaxial                stress core holder, using data acquisition system                for collecting and recording triaxial stress core                holders, a formation environment simulation system,                a fluid injection system, and a fluid collection                metering system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), when                  the triaxial stress core holder, and pressurizing                  to preset pressure, axial pressure chamber in a                  triaxial stress core holder, and the confining                  pressure chamber is alternately pressurized to                  preset pressure. The triaxial stress core holder                  comprises gripper cylinder, a rubber sleeve, a                  front end plug, a rear end plug and a rear end                  sealing cap. The rubber sleeve is disposed in the                  holder cylinder, a pressure chamber is arranged                  between the holder cylinder and the rubber                  sleeve. The front end plug seals the front end of                  the rubber sleeve. The rear end plug is slidably                  inserted into a rear end of the rubber sleeve.                  The sealing cap is connected to the rear end                  plug. A sealing chamber is formed between the                  sealing cap and the rear end plug. The rubber                  sleeve has a placement cavity for placing a shale                  rock sample. The formation environment simulation                  system includes a back pressure loading pump for                  adjusting the back pressure of the triaxial                  stress core holder, and a shaft pressure loading                  pump for adjusting the pressure in the axial                  pressure chamber of the triaxial stress core                  holder. A gas heating device for heating carbon                  dioxide and methane and incubator for maintaining                  constant temperature in the triaxial stress core                  holder, and fluid injection system includes                  carbon dioxide cylinder for carbon dioxide for                  triaxial stress core holder and a gas buffer tank                  for storing carbon dioxide or methane, and                  injecting into the triaxial stress core holder.                  The fluid injection system has mechanism for                  separately adjusting carbon dioxide cylinder,                  pressure regulating valve for injection pressure                  of methane cylinder. The fluid collection                  metering system includes a gas mass flow meter                  and a balance for measuring the mass of a mixed                  gas of CO2 and CH4 in a triaxial stress core                  holder of the system, and the balance is used to                  weigh carbon dioxide or methane in a gas buffer                  tank quality. The data acquisition system                  includes a computer, a pressure sensor, and a                  temperature sensor. The computer collects                  parameters measured by a pressure sensor, a                  temperature sensor, and a balance. The computer                  is also used to collect the mass of the mixed gas                  of carbon dioxide and methane measured by the                  fluid collection and metering system. Preferred                  Method: In the step (ii), when the constant                  pressure is injected into the triaxial stress                  core holder, injecting the methane, and the back                  pressure of the triaxial stress core holder is                  atmospheric pressure, and discharging the                  methane, measuring the amount of methane                  discharged and calculating the amount of methane                  remaining in the triaxial stress core holder,                  maintaining constant temperature in the triaxial                  stress core holder, and promoting adsorption of                  methane by shale rock samples. In the step (ii),                  before injecting carbon dioxide into the triaxial                  stress core holder at constant pressure, reducing                  the back pressure of the triaxial stress core                  holder, until the shale rock sample desorbs                  methane. In the step (iv), where each parameter                  includes a triaxial stress core holder confining                  pressure chamber, pressure and temperature in the                  shaft pressure chamber, injection pressure of                  methane, carbon dioxide, and temperature of shale                  rock sample, measuring the amount of methane and                  carbon dioxide, injecting into the triaxial                  stress core holder and the amount of the mixed                  gas of carbon dioxide and methane measured by                  fluid collection.

PT P
PN CN112044261-A
TI Method for capturing carbon dioxide and synergistically removing nitric oxide comprises mixing absorbent active components, biomass and binder to prepare new calcium-based absorbent particles.
AU CHEN H
   LIANG X
   LIANG C
   LI X
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 2020C4857N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for capturing carbon dioxide and synergistically removing nitric oxide comprises mixing absorbent active components, biomass and binder to prepare new calcium-based absorbent particles, where calcium-based absorbent particles are used in the reactor to capture carbon dioxide in the discharged flue gas and simultaneously remove nitric oxide.
   USE - The method is useful for capturing carbon dioxide and synergistically removing nitric oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces nitric oxide in the flue gas and the cost of system; and realizes the combined removal of carbon dioxide and nitric oxide from industrial source flue gas, as well as the utilization of biomass resources.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active component of the absorbent is limestone, dolomite or industrial waste calcium carbide slag after being crushed and sieved to form particles with a particle size of less than 250 mu m. The biomass is forestry biomass or agricultural biomass. The biomass is wood chips, coconut shell, coconut shell charcoal, and/or peanut shell, and the addition amount is 15-50%. The binder is high-alumina cement and/or high-alumina fly ash and the addition amount is 10-30%. Preferred Method - The active components of the absorbent are mixed, before preparing new calcium-based absorbent particles from biomass and binders by washing the biomass with 15-30% acetic acid solution, drying after filtration to reduce the influence of alkali metal ions in the biomass on the anti-sintering performance of the absorbent. The preparation process of the calcium-based absorbent particles is based on the uniform mixing of the active components of the absorbent, where the biomass and the binder, adding tap water or deionized water, stirring uniformly to form a wet powder with uniform humidity, extruding cylindrical material in the extruder, dry, then cutting round by a spheronizer, and drying it naturally. The method comprises using reactors including fixed bed, fluidized bed, and fast bed and subjecting calcium-based absorbent to carbonation reaction in the reactor at 600-725 degrees C to capture carbondioxide in the flue gas and at the same time reduce nitric oxide in the flue gas and the method for capturing carbondioxide and synergistically removing nitric oxide, where the exhaust flue gas is oxy-fuel combustion flue gas produced by air combustion of coal, biomass, and solid waste. In the method, the reduction of nitric oxide is based on reducing gases (CO, H2, CxHy) and biomass coke generated by biomass pyrolysis to achieve reduction of nitric oxide. The particle size of the calcium-based absorbent is 0.6-1.5 mm.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. AU CHEN D

AU COADY C
   MAGUIRE-BOYLE S J
   SOUTHWELL J E
AE NISSAN CHEM AMERICA CORP (NISC-C)
GA 2022A8606E
AB    NOVELTY - Process for removing hydrogen sulfide from a                stream, involves (A) adding a fluid comprising (i)                a dispersion of colloidal nanoparticles having                surface functionality comprising copper, zinc,                iron, or manganese, and (ii) a triazine, where the                stream is chosen from oil streams, gas streams,                carbon dioxide point source purification streams                and geothermal energy system streams.
   USE - The process is used for removing hydrogen                sulfide from a stream from oil streams, gas                streams, carbon dioxide point source purification                streams and geothermal energy system streams.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The process involves (A)                  adding a fluid comprising (ia) colloidal                  dispersion of copper species coated silica                  nanoparticle nanoparticles, and (iia) a                  triazine.
   EXAMPLE - 1000 ml Snowtex ST-30(RTM: Aqueous alkaline                colloidal silica dispersion, 30 wt.% silicon                dioxide solids, 10-15 median particle size) was                placed into a 2000 ml 4 neck glass reactor                assembled with addition funnel, thermometer,                heating mantle connected to voltage regulator, and                mixer with 2 inch diameter trifoil mixing blade.                Mixing was activated at 150 rpm and silicasol was                brought to 50&#176; C. Into the addition funnel                was weighed 49.98 g aminoethyl aminoethyl                aminopropyl trimethoxysilane. Addition funnel was                assembled to reactor top and silane was slowly                added to stirring silicasol at a drop rate of 2                drops per second. After all organosilane had been                added to reaction the mixture was allowed to stir                at 50&#176; C for a period of 3 hours. Finished                surface-treated alkaline silica was poured off to a                2 l nalgene bottle for storage and use. Into a 1000                ml Nalgene bottle was placed 300 g distilled water,                300 g propylene glycol monomethyl ether solvent,                and 300 g fluid. Contents was mixed thoroughly by                shaking container vigorously for 30 seconds.

PN JP2022049185-A
TI Producing lithium silicate and silicon from powder of silicon by using powder of lithium carbonate, powder of silicon dioxide and silicon in same system so that exclusion of carbon dioxide takes precedence over formation of silicon carbide.
AU SUENAGA H
AE OSAKA TITANIUM TECHNOLOGIES CO LTD (OSAN-C)
GA 2022578821
AB    NOVELTY - Lithium silicate (SiLixOy) and silicon (Si) are produced from powder of silicon by using powder of lithium carbonate (Li2CO3), powder of silicon dioxide (SiO2) and silicon in the same system so that the exclusion of carbon dioxide (CO2) takes precedence over the formation of silicon carbide (SiC).
   USE - Production of lithium silicate and silicon from powder of silicon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of reducing the generation of silicon carbide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the manufacturing apparatus of lithium-containing silicon oxide powder.
   Vapor deposition apparatus (100)
   Crucible (110)
   Heater (120)
   Vapor deposition drum (130)
   Scraper (141)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: A mixture of powder of lithium carbonate, powder of silicon dioxide and powder of the silicon is heated at greater than or equal to 400 degrees C and less than the melting point of lithium carbonate in a reduced atmosphere. The powder of lithium carbonate is located between the layer of the powder of lithium carbonate and the layer of the powder of silicon, and the powder of the silicon dioxide and powder of silicon in layered form are heated at the temperature equal to or higher than the melting point of the lithium carbonate. The mixed powder layer of the powder of lithium carbonate and the powder of silicon dioxide and the layer of the powder of silicon are adjacent, and the mixed powder of silicon in a layered state is heated at a temperature higher than the melting point of the lithium carbonate. The layer of silicon powder is the lowest layer. During the heating of the mixed powder of lithium carbonate and silicon dioxide, the powder of silicon is thrown in the system.

PN CN113880708-A; CN113880708-B
TI Preparing 4-cyclohexyl benzoic acid comprises preparing 1-bromo-4-cyclohexylbenzene into Grignard reagent, reacting Grignard reagent with carbon dioxide in an organic solvent, and acidifying to obtain 4-cyclohexylbenzoic acid.
AU SHEN Q
   GUO S
   YU C
   CHEN J
AE NANJING HUAGUAN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202211063X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 4-cyclohexyl benzoic acid comprises (1) preparing 1-bromo-4-cyclohexylbenzene into Grignard reagent, (2) reacting Grignard reagent with carbon dioxide in an organic solvent, and (3) acidifying to obtain 4-cyclohexylbenzoic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 4-cyclohexyl benzoic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has easily obtained raw material, low cost and stable process, designs heating temperature, uses magnesium powder and other conditions, realizes preparation of Grignard reagent of 1- bromo-4-cyclohexyl-benzene, overcomes technical deficiency that expensive catalysts or expensive ligands are required to prepare 4-cyclohexyl benzoic acid, and has significant progress. The 4-cyclohexyl-benzoic acid has high chemical purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the substrate for preparing Grignard reagent is at least one of bromide, chloride and iodide. The material is at least one of magnesium powder, isopropyl magnesium chloride, isopropyl magnesium chloride and lithium chloride. In the step (2), the organic solvent is at least one of methyl tertiary butyl ether, tetrahydrofuran and 2-methyltetrahydrofuran.Preferred Method: In the method, the mol ratio of 1-bromo-4-cyclohexyl and material is 1:(1-3). In the step (2), the temperature of introducing carbon dioxide is 40-20degrees Celsius. The method specifically comprises adding magnesium powder and tetrahydrofuran into reaction flask, replacing with nitrogen three times, stirring and heating to 60degrees Celsius, adding iodine particles, adding part of the tetrahydrofuran mixed solution of 1-bromo-4-cyclohexylbenzene, stirring and heating to 60degrees Celsius, initiating the Grignard reaction, dropping remaining tetrahydrofuran solution of 1-bromo-4-cyclohexylbenzene at 60-65degrees Celsius, completing dropping, controlling the temperature at 60-65degrees Celsius for 2 hours, cooling reaction solution to -20degrees Celsius, passing dry carbon dioxide into the system, controlling the temperature to -20 to -10degrees Celsius until no heat is generated by ventilation, ventilating and stirring for 1 hours, when the temperature is controlled not less than 0degrees Celsius, dropping water into the system, completing the addition, adding hydrochloric acid under the temperature control of 0-20degrees Celsius, completing the addition, and post-processing to obtain the product.

PT P
PN DE102019111071-A1
TI Catalytically producing formamide, comprises e.g. performing conversion of carbon dioxide and hydrogen in methanol phase in presence of ruthenium-phosphine complex as catalyst and acid to obtain methyl formate and water.
AU MAKHYNYA Y
   EL-HAWARY T
   GLOTZBACH C
   TENHUMBERG N
   LEITNER W
   KLANKERMAYER J
   SCHUMACHER H
AE RHEINISCH WESTFAELISCHE TECH HOCHSCHULE (RWTH-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
GA 2020A61639
AB    NOVELTY - Catalytically producing formamide, comprises (a) performing conversion of carbon dioxide and hydrogen in methanol phase in presence of ruthenium-phosphine complex as catalyst and acid to obtain methyl formate and water, and (b) reacting the methyl formate with ammonia to form formamide and methanol.
   USE - The method is useful for catalytically producing formamide using ruthenium-phosphine complex as catalyst in catalytic production of methyl formate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves yield of formamide using ruthenium-phosphine complex.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of ruthenium-phosphine complex as catalyst for catalytic production of methyl formate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ruthenium-phosphine complex includes at least one monophosphine, diphosphine, triphosphine or a compound with more than three phosphine groups. The monophosphine is monophosphine based compound of formula (PR1R2R3) (I). The ruthenium-phosphine complex includes non-phosphine ligands including carbene, amine, amide, phosphite, phosphoamidite, phosphorus-containing ethers or esters, sulfide, trimethylene methane, cyclopentadienyl, allyl, methylallyl, ethylene, cyclooctadiene, acetylacetonate, acetate, hydride, halide, phenolate and/or carbon monoxide, preferably trimethylene methane, cyclopentadienyl, allyl, methylallyl, ethylene, cyclooctadiene, acetylacetonate, acetate, hydride, halide, phenolate and/or carbon monoxide. The ruthenium-phosphine complex is ruthenium-triphosphine complex, where the triphosphine is triphosphine based compound of formula (R7-C(-CH2-R5R6P)(-CH2-R3R4P)-CH2-R1R2P) (II), preferably 1,1,1-tris(diphenylphosphinomethyl)ethane. The ruthenium-phosphine complex is 1,1,1-tris(diphenylphosphinomethyl)ethane based compound of formula ((A)Ru(L)3) (III), preferably (ruthenium(1,1,1-tris(diphenylphosphinomethyl)ethane)(trimethylene methane)). The concentration of ruthenium-phosphine complex in the catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the methanol phase is 0.1-5 mol.%, preferably 2-0.1 mol.% based on the molar amount of carbon dioxide. The acid is a Lewis acid or a protic acid, preferably organoaluminum compounds, organoboron compounds, sulfonic acids, scandium compounds or perfluorinated copolymers having at least one sulfo group. The acid is Lewis acid used as cocatalyst in combination with the ruthenium-phosphine complex.R1, R2 and R3 = optionally substituted alkyl, aryl or heteroaryl, where R2 and R3 is phenyl;R1-R6 = optionally substituted aryl or heteroaryl, preferably optionally substituted phenyl;R7 = H, alkyl, cycloalkyl or aryl;A = 1,1,1-tris(diphenylphosphinomethyl)ethane (II); andL = monodentate ligands, where at least two monodentate ligands L are replaced by bidentate ligand or three monodentate ligands L are replaced by a tridentate ligand, and the monodentate ligands are preferably trimethylene methane, cyclopentadienyl, allyl, methylallyl, ethylene, cyclooctadiene, acetylacetonate, acetate, hydride, halide, phenolate and/or carbon monoxide.Preferred Method: The catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the methanol phase is carried out at 20-150 degrees C, preferably 80-120 degrees C under pressure of 40-220 bar, preferably 100-180 bar. The method comprises separating hydrogen and carbon dioxide from the reaction mixture containing methyl formate by lowering the pressure and optionally lowering the temperature, and separating methyl formate from the reaction mixture by distillation, mixing separated methyl formate with ammonia and reacting with the formamide and methanol, or adding ammonia into the reaction mixture to react the methyl formate with the ammonia to form formamide and methanol. The reaction of the methyl formate with ammonia to form formamide and methanol is carried out at 20-100 degrees C, preferably 60-80 degrees C under atmospheric pressure of 70 bar, preferably 45 bar. The methanol phase used for the catalytic conversion is produced by adding hydrogen into a methanol phase loaded with physically bound carbon dioxide by scrubbing a synthesis gas containing carbon dioxide to remove carbon dioxide using a methanol phase, preferably methanol, or using as scrubbing liquid or by gas scrubbing of carbon dioxide containing synthesis gas to remove carbon dioxide using a scrubbing liquid which is not a methanol phase, performing desorption of the carbon dioxide from the loaded scrubbing liquid and performing absorption of the desorbed carbon dioxide into a methanol phase. The gas scrubbing of a synthesis gas to remove carbon dioxide from the synthesis gas with methanol as scrubbing liquid is carried out at less than or equal to -20 degrees C under pressure of 20-50 bar, where the synthesis gas is a synthesis gas for ammonia synthesis or a possibly processed gas including coke oven gas, a blast furnace gas or exhaust gas from cement works as synthesis gas and/or the gas scrubbing is carried out on a synthesis gas obtained from the steam reforming and/or subsequent water gas shift reaction. The method comprises mixing the methanol phase loaded with carbon dioxide with a partial stream of the hydrogen-containing synthesis gas after the gas scrubbing or with partial stream of the hydrogen-containing synthesis gas before the gas scrubbing. The catalytically produced methyl formate is reacted with ammonia to form formamide and methanol.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN111333904-A; CN111333904-B
TI Preparing sound-absorbing energy-absorbing composite material comprises e.g. carrying polymerization reaction on diamine A and diamine B with carbon dioxide to obtain polyurea elastomer; and dissolving polyurea elastomer in solvent.
AU CHENG H
   WU P
   ZHAO F
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 202061148T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sound-absorbing energy-absorbing composite material comprises (1) carrying polymerization reaction on diamine A and diamine B with carbon dioxide to obtain polyurea elastomer; where the diamine A is isophorone diamine; the diamine B is C4-C10 diaminooxane; (2) dissolving the polyurea elastomer in solvent to obtain elastomer solution; and (3) impregnating polyurethane foam with elastomer solution, drying, obtaining polyurethane foam composite material loaded with polyurea elastomer.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sound-absorbing energy-absorbing composite material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for sound-absorbing energy-absorbing composite material is prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The diamine B is 1,5-diamino-3-oxapentane, 1,8-diamino-3,6-dioxaoctane, 1,10-diamino-4,7-dioxadecane, 1,12-diamino-4,9-dioxadodecane, and/or 1,13-diamino-4,7,10-trioxatridecane. The molar ratio of diamine A to the diamine B is 1:(1-2.5). In the step (3), in composite materials, the loading amount of polyurea elastomer is 30-100 wt.% mass of polyurethane foam. The mass concentration of the elastomer solution is 10-100 g/l. The solvent includes ethanol, methanol and/or N,N-dimethylformamide. Preferred Method: The step (1) comprises (i) reacting diamine A and the diamine B under pressurized condition of carbon dioxide gas to obtain a prepolymer; and (ii) reacting the prepolymer under atmospheric pressure of carbon dioxide gas to obtain polyurea elastomer. In the step (i), the carbon dioxide gas is pressurized to 1-20 MPa, the reaction temperature is 120-200 degrees C, and the reaction time is 2-48 hours; in the step (ii), the reaction temperature is 120-300 degrees C, and the reaction time is 0.5-10 hours. In the step (i), after the reaction, the method further comprises cooling and drying; the drying is vacuum drying; the drying temperature is 40-100 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN110934889-A
TI Extracting amber comprises taking Baltic amber, grinding, sieving, adding amber powder in extraction kettle, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, extracting, eluting and separating crude amber extract, and vacuum distilling.
AU ZHANG X
AE JIAXING YICUI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAX-Non-standard)
GA 202026489D
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting amber comprises (i) taking Baltic amber, grinding by using grinder and sieving through more than 100 mesh sieve to obtain amber powder; (ii) adding amber powder in extraction kettle of supercritical extraction device, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid and extracting at 40-80 degrees C under extraction pressure of 30-70 MPa to obtain crude amber extract; and (iii) eluting and separating crude amber extract by using macroporous adsorption resin column, and vacuum distilling the eluate.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting amber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents the effective components in amber from destroying due to high temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The flow rate of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is 20-30 kg/hour. In the step (ii), the extraction is carried out for 1-3 hours. The elution and separation is carried out by (a) wet-packing macroporous adsorption resin, where the pore diameter of macroporous adsorption resin is 13-14 nm, and pre-processing for removing oligomers and porogens contained in macroporous adsorption resin; (b) adding crude amber extract to ethanol and stirring until dissolving to obtain sample; and (c) adding sample into macroporous adsorption resin column, eluting by ethanol and collecting eluate. The pre-processing comprises washing macroporous adsorption resin using 95 wt.% ethanol at flow rate of 2 bed volume/hour until the effluent is clear and transparent, washing with water with at flow rate of 2 bed volume/hour for 1-3 hours, washing with 4 wt.% hydrochloric acid solution at flow speed of 4 bed volume/hour and washing with water for removing hydrochloric acid until pH of effluent is 6-8. In the step (b), the mass of ethanol is 2-3 times the crude amber extract. In the step (c), the column height of macroporous adsorption resin column is more than 10 times the diameter macroporous adsorption resin column. The vacuum distilling is carried out at 100-120 degrees C for 10-20 minutes under vacuum degree of 40-60 Pa.

TI Preparing nanoChinese herbal medicine, by washing, crushing and sieving Chinese herbal medicine and dispersing in water, mixing dispersion liquid with e.g. fluorocarbon solution, filling carbon dioxide, dispersing and freeze-drying product.
AU HUANG G
   YANG H
   HUANG C
   KONG P
AE UNIV SHANGHAI MEDICINE & HEALTH SCI (USMH-C)
GA 201880480W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing nanoChinese herbal medicine, involves (a) washing, crushing and sieving Chinese herbal medicine and dispersing Chinese herbal medicinal powder in water, (b) mixing the dispersion liquid obtained in step (a) with fluorocarbon solution and surfactant solution, filling carbon dioxide, sealing, performing ultrasonic dispersion, leaving and separating to obtain medicinal liquid, and (c) freeze-drying medicinal liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nanoChinese herbal medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares product which has high solubility, reduces risk of oxidation, and avoids high temperature processing of Chinese herbal medicine, and loss of active ingredients, and is easy to absorb.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The Chinese herbal medicine comprises pseudoginseng, rhizoma gastrodiae, fructus amomi, elecampane, Angelica, tuckahoe, dendrobe and Cordyceps sinensis. The method involves sieving crushed product using a sieve of 500 mesh size. The concentration of traditional medicinal powder in the dispersion liquid is 30-70 %mass. The fluorocarbon solution is an aqueous solution of a fluorocarbon compound chosen from perfluorocyclic ether, perfluorooctyl bromide, perfluorodecalin and perfluoromethylcyclohexylpiperidine, where the concentration of fluorocarbon solution is 5-8 %mass. The surfactant solution comprises cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, dodecyltrimethylammonium bromide, dioctadecyldimethylammonium bromide, sodium dodecyl sulfate, sodium dodecyl sulfate, sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, polyethylene glycol octyl phenyl ether, polyoxyethylene sorbitan monooleate, sorbitan monostearate, cocamide diethanolamine, fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, lauryl ethoxylate, polyoxyethylene 80-polyoxypropylene 30-polyoxyethylene 80, polyoxyethylene 100-polyoxypropylene 70-polyoxyethylene 100 or polyoxyethylene 20-polyoxypropylene 70-polyoxyethylene 20. The volume ratio of dispersion liquid, fluorocarbon solution and surfactant solution is 1-10:1:1, where the concentration of carbon dioxide is 0-30 ml/m3. The ultrasonic dispersion is performed at 500-1200 watt for 30-60 minutes. The freeze-drying is performed at -60 degrees C to -30 degrees C, and vacuum degree of 20-100 Pa for 10-50 hours.

GA 2022C1552Q
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions from                fluid catalytic cracking process involves providing                chemical looping system comprising                reducer/regenerator reactor, an oxidizer reactor                and combustor reactor. The catalyst coke particles                and metal oxide particles are fed into the                reducer/regenerator reactor of the chemical looping                system to produce multiple streams comprising a                regenerated catalyst stream, a CO2/H2O stream and a                reduced metal oxide particle stream. The stream of                reduced metal oxide particles and a stream of water                vapor are fed into the oxidizer reactor of the                chemical looping system to produce multiple streams                comprising hydrogen stream and a stream of oxidized                metal oxide particles. The stream of oxidized metal                oxide particles and stream of air are fed into the                combustor reactor of chemical looping system to                produce heat and stream of metal oxide particles.                The carbon dioxide of CO2/H2O stream is sequestered                to reduce the CO2 emissions from the                reducer/regenerator reactor.
   USE - Method for reducing carbon dioxide (CO2)                emissions from a fluid catalytic cracking                process.

The reduced metal                  oxide particles comprise iron and iron oxide                  particles. The iron oxide is ferrous oxide. PT P
PN CN110540637-A
TI Preparing a cross-linked poly(propylene carbonate) by using maleic anhydride copolymer-propylene oxide-carbon dioxide copolymerization in one-pot method involves taking propylene oxide and carbon dioxide as first monomer and second monomer.
AU GAO L
   HUANG M
   FENG J
   XIAO Z
   LIANG X
AE UNIV LINGNAN NORMAL (ULNN-C)
GA 2019A5176F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a cross-linked poly(propylene carbonate) by using a maleic anhydride copolymer/propylene oxide/carbon dioxide copolymerization in a one-pot method involves taking propylene oxide and carbon dioxide as first monomer and second monomer. The maleic anhydride copolymer is used as third monomer, and zinc glutarate is used as a catalyst to perform the polymerization reaction to obtain a crosslinked poly(propylene carbonate) in one pot.
   USE - Method for preparing a cross-linked poly(propylene carbonate) by using a maleic anhydride copolymer/propylene oxide/carbon dioxide copolymerization in a one-pot method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare a cross-linked poly(propylene carbonate) by using a maleic anhydride copolymer/propylene oxide/carbon dioxide copolymerization in a one-pot method, which has less synthetic steps, simple preparation process, short polymerization time, adjusts cross-linking degree and physical property of cross-linked PPC, increases catalytic efficiency, reduces carbon dioxide pressure, enhances thermal stability, mechanical strength and dimensional stability of PPC, overcomes problem of easy decomposition of PPC material and easy deformation product, good application prospect in field of plastics and rubber, and is suitable for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The zinc glutarate and maleic anhydride/tetrahydrophthalic anhydride copolymer are added in a high-pressure reaction kettle, vacuum dried at 80 degrees C for 24 hours, air-cooled, filled with nitrogen to replace air for three times, evacuated, and propylene oxide is filled with carbon dioxide to maintain polymerization reaction temperature and carbon dioxide pressure, and reacted under stirring condition. The polymer is dissolved in dichloromethane, 5% hydrochloric acid is added to dissolve zinc glutarate catalyst, and washed with distilled water to neutral pH value. The polymer dichloromethane solution is precipitated with ethanol and dried under vacuum at 80 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain a polymer. The mass ratio of zinc glutarate and propylene oxide is 1:100-500. The mass ratio of maleic anhydride copolymer and propylene oxide is 1-10:100. The polymerization reaction is performed at a temperature of 60-80 degrees C and carbon dioxide pressure of 4.5MPa for 15-25 hours. The average molecular weight of maleic anhydride copolymer is 600-2000. Preferred Components: The third monomer is a maleic anhydride copolymer. The maleic anhydride copolymer is maleic anhydride/styrene copolymer, maleic anhydride/vinyl acetate copolymer, maleic anhydride/methyl acrylate copolymer, maleic anhydride/methyl methacrylate copolymer, maleic anhydride/acrylamide copolymer, maleic anhydride/vinyl methyl ether copolymer or maleic anhydride/1,2,3,6-tetrahydrophthalic anhydride copolymer.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.)
MC A02-A07; A04-F05; A05-E06A; A10-B01; A10-D

PN CN110093479-A; CN110093479-B
TI Smelting stainless steel by using bottom blowing carbon dioxide comprises e.g. adding dephosphorized molten iron to converter, blowing oxygen and carbon dioxide, adding furnace material, alloying, and slagging.
AU LI J
   LIU W
   DI J
   ZHAO J
AE SHANXI TAIGANG STAINLESS STEEL CO LTD (TYGT-C)
GA 2019698416
AB    NOVELTY - Smelting stainless steel by using bottom blowing carbon dioxide comprises (i) adding dephosphorized molten iron to a converter, blowing oxygen at the top portion of lance, and blowing a mixture of oxygen and carbon dioxide at the bottom of the lance, (ii) adding a furnace material to the smelting process, and alloying and slagging, (iii) when the carbon content of the converter molten pool is 0.2-0.5 wt.%, the oxygen gun and the bottom gun stop blowing oxygen, and the bottom gun continues to blow carbon dioxide underneath, and (iv) adding a reduction material to a molten pool, reducing, when the molten steel composition meets the requirements, transferring to a vacuum oxygen decarburization (VOD) furnace, and refining.
   USE - The method is useful for smelting stainless steel by using bottom blowing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes carbon dioxide in the inert gas blown at the bottom of the converter that reduces the smelting cost; avoids the increase of the nitrogen element content in the molten steel caused by the bottom blowing nitrogen; and ensures the stability of the physical and chemical properties of the stainless steel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The furnace material comprises lime, high carbon ferrochrome, pure nickel, nickel iron alloy, and/or high carbon ferromanganese. The reducing material comprises ferrosilicon, aluminum and/or fluorite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The converter is a KOBMS furnace or an AOD furnace.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the ratio of oxygen and carbon dioxide blown by the primer is gradually adjusted from the initial 2-3:1-1:3-4 as the carbon content of the molten iron is lowered. The dephosphorized molten iron is obtained by injecting lime powder and iron phosphorus into blast furnace molten iron in a pretreatment tank. The VOD furnace refining comprises selecting a VOD furnace, vacuum pumping, when the vacuum degree reaches the requirement, the top gun blows oxygen decarburization, the bottom gun blows argon stirring, then the material is added in the molten iron, and when the content of each component is satisfied, performing steel tapping. Preferred Components: The material comprises ferrotitanium, low carbon ferrochrome and/or nickel beans. The stainless steel comprises 409L, 436, 444, 321 or 316Ti stainless steel.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)

PT P
PN CN109251349-A
TI Producing graphite extruded board using carbon dioxide blowing agent involves weighing graphite polystyrene particles, ordinary polystyrene particles, carbon dioxide blowing agent, nucleating agent, and flame retardant.
AU MU Z
   LI Y
   SUN D
   GUO W
   WANG Q
AE MU Z (MUZZ-Individual)
GA 2019104368
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a graphite extruded board using a carbon dioxide blowing agent involves weighing 20-30 pts. wt. graphite polystyrene particles, 60-70 pts. wt. ordinary polystyrene particles, 2.5-3.5 pts. wt. carbon dioxide blowing agent, 1-2 pts. wt. nucleating agent, and 3-5 pts. wt. flame retardant. The graphite polystyrene particles, ordinary polystyrene particles, nucleating agent and flame retardant are fed into the extruder through the feeder, fully plasticized in the extruder, and then injected into the carbon dioxide foaming agent. Then it is extruded through a die, and the pressure is quickly released after the die is extruded. The foaming agent wrapped by the plastic rapidly expands to form a closed cell, and the polystyrene plastic forms a honeycomb-like closed cell under the action of the cell. The foaming board is then shaped into the required thickness and width by a shaped tractor, which can be cut and formed.
   USE - Method for producing a graphite extruded board using a carbon dioxide blowing agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce a graphite extruded board using a carbon dioxide blowing agent, which makes the product, that fully meet the national standard of the thermal insulation board.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide blowing agent is prepared by mixing ethanol, isopropanol and distilled water in a volume ratio of 3:2:2 to obtain a mixture. The carbon dioxide and the mixture are added to a high-pressure mixer at a mass ratio of 2:1, where the pressure is 20 megapascal, and the mixture is shunted, merged, rotated, divided, recombined, repeated action, and fully mixed. The nucleating agent is alumina, and the flame retardantis melamine.

PN RD700047-A
TI Production of gray hydrogen involves using            electrified steam methane reformer and fired heater in            which pressure swing adsorption off-gas is burnt, and            using heat from fired heater in steam methane reformer            plant for feed preheating and/or steam generation.
AU 
AE ANONYMOUS (ANON-C)
GA 2022A15280
AB    NOVELTY - Production of hydrogen (gray hydrogen)                involves using an electrified steam methane                reformer and a fired heater in which a pressure                swing adsorption off-gas is burnt, and using the                heat from the fired heater in the steam methane                reformer plant for feed preheating and/or steam                generation. In order to reduce the amount of                pressure swing adsorption off-gas, the production                method is optionally combined with a concept of:                (i) low water-gas shift outlet temperature (reduced                carbon monoxide slip), (ii) recycling portion of                the off-gas to the water-gas shift reactor to                convert more carbon monoxide with water to hydrogen                and carbon dioxide, (iii) recycling portion of the                pressure swing adsorption off-gas to the reformer                to convert more methane to syngas, (iv) recycling                portion of the pressure swing adsorption off-gas to                a pressure swing adsorption (A2) or to a pressure                swing adsorption (A1) to recover more hydrogen, and                (v) high sulfur/carbon molar ratio of 2.4 or                more.
   USE - Production of hydrogen (gray hydrogen).
   ADVANTAGE - The method lowers carbon dioxide content of                the pressure swing adsorption off-gas and limits                carbon dioxide emissions of the fired heater, by                separating carbon dioxide from the shifted process                upstream of the pressure swing adsorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of hydrogen (gray hydrogen)                involves using an electrified steam methane                reformer and a fired heater in which a pressure                swing adsorption off-gas is burnt, and using the                heat from the fired heater in the steam methane                reformer plant for feed preheating and/or steam                generation. In order to reduce the amount of                pressure swing adsorption off-gas, the production                method is optionally combined with a concept of:                (i) low water-gas shift outlet temperature (reduced                carbon monoxide slip), (ii) recycling portion of                the pressure swing adsorption off-gas to the                water-gas shift reactor to convert more carbon                monoxide with water to hydrogen and carbon dioxide,                (iii) recycling portion of the pressure swing                adsorption off-gas to the reformer to convert more                methane to syngas, (iv) recycling portion of the                pressure swing adsorption off-gas to a pressure                swing adsorption (A2) or to a pressure swing                adsorption (A1) to recover more hydrogen, (v) high                sulfur/carbon molar ratio of 2.4 or more (reduced                methane slip from reformer and carbon monoxide slip                in shift), and (vi) high reformer outlet                temperature of more than 900 degrees C, preferably                up to 950 degrees C (reduced methane slip from                reformer). The fired heater can also be operated                with oxygen instead of air (oxyfuel) to ease carbon                monoxide capture.

PN CN114481201-A
TI Reducing silver-modified copper-loaded zeolite            (Ag/Cu-TS-1) light-driven carbon dioxide comprises            dissolving copper(II) nitrate trihydrate, adding            zeolite TS-1, dissolving silver nitrate, irradiating            with xenon lamp, dissolving in acetone, dispersing and            suspending in potassium bicarbonate solution.
AU YAN L
   WANG J
   LI B
   LIU W
   SUN W
   LI G
   SUN Y
AE UNIV HAINAN NORMAL (UHNN-C)
GA 202270851A
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises (a) using one-step                hydrothermal method to synthesize zeolite TS-1, (b)                dissolving copper(II) nitrate trihydrate in the                solution of deionized water and tetrapropyl                ammonium hydroxide, adding zeolite TS-1, stirring,                then drying and removing nitrate to obtain Cu-TS-1,                (c) dissolving silver nitrate in the deionized                water, adding methanol suspension of Cu-TS-1                prepared in step (b), stirring and irradiating with                xenon lamp, collecting grey powder, washing,                freezing and drying to obtain silver modified                copper loaded zeolite Ag/Cu-TS-1, (d) dissolving in                acetone and aqueous solution, ultrasonically                dispersing and mixing suspension to obtain catalyst                slurry, then coating catalyst slurry on a                carbonized paper, drying to obtain carbon paper                coating catalyst, (e) suspending in potassium                bicarbonate solution, taking platinum sheet and                Ag/AgCl as counter electrode and reference                electrode, adding bubbler carbon dioxide gas into                potassium bicarbonate solution, opening xenon                lamp.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing                silver-modified copper-loaded zeolite (Ag/Cu-TS-1)                light-driven carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The zeolite: has super-light absorption                performance and separation efficiency of light                generated charge; realizes efficient and stable                catalytic conversion of CO2 to methanol and ethanol                under the ultraviolet visible light.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reducing silver-modified copper-loaded zeolite                (Ag/Cu-TS-1) light-driven carbon dioxide comprises                (a) using one-step hydrothermal method to                synthesize zeolite TS-1, (b) dissolving copper(II)                nitrate trihydrate in the solution of deionized                water and tetrapropyl ammonium hydroxide, adding                zeolite TS-1, stirring for 4 hours at                80&#176;C, then drying and removing nitrate to                obtain Cu-TS-1, (c) dissolving silver nitrate in                the deionized water, adding methanol suspension of                Cu-TS-1 prepared in step (b), stirring and                irradiating with xenon lamp, centrifuging and                collecting grey powder, washing for three times,                freezing and drying for 24 hours to obtain silver                modified copper loaded zeolite Ag/Cu-TS-1, (d)                dissolving the silver modified copper loaded                zeolite Ag/Cu-TS-1 in acetone and aqueous solution,                ultrasonically dispersing and mixing suspension for                2 hours to obtain the catalyst slurry, then coating                the catalyst slurry on a carbonized paper, drying                at 80&#176;C for 4 hours to obtain the carbon                paper coating catalyst, (e) suspending carbon paper                coating catalyst in potassium bicarbonate solution,                taking platinum sheet and Ag/AgCl as counter                electrode and the reference electrode, adding                bubbler carbon dioxide (CO2) gas into the potassium                bicarbonate solution, opening the xenon lamp,                realizing the photo-electrochemical reduction of                CO2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises                  (a1) adding sodium carbonate (Na2CO3) in the                  deionized water, then adding the purity of 25                  wt.% tetrapropyl ammonium hydroxide, magnetically                  stirring at 40&#176;C for 4 hours, then                  slowly adding metasilicic acid (H2SiO3), (a2)                  dropping titanium(iv) sulfate (Ti (SO4) 2) and                  deionized water into the solution of step (a1),                  vigorously stirring for 6 hours at 40&#176;C,                  then transferring the solution to the                  polytetrafluoroethylene high pressure kettle,                  keeping for 12 hours at 150&#176;C, (a3)                  centrifuging and separating the solution after                  the reaction of the high pressure kettle, washing                  with deionized water for 3 times, air drying at                  100&#176;C for 8 hours, and (a4) calcining                  the product obtained in step (a3) at                  550&#176;C for 4 hours to obtain the white                  powder zeolite TS-1. The step (c) comprises                  stirring suspension for 30 minutes, and                  irradiating Xe lamp for 30 minutes.Preferred Components: The ratio of Na2CO3                  and deionized water is 1 g: 80 ml. The mass ratio                  of tetrapropylammonium hydroxide and Na2CO3 is                  144:1. The mass ratio of H2SiO3 to Na2CO3 is                  39:1. The mass ratio of Ti(SO4)2 to Na2CO3 in                  step (a1) is 2.4:1. The mass ratio of copper(II)                  nitrate trihydrate (Cu(NO3)2.3H2O) to Na2CO3 in                  step (a1) is 1:1. The molar ratio of Ti(SO4)2 and                  deionized water is 0.48:3. The mass ratio of Cu                  (NO3) 2. 3H2O, zeolite TS-1, deionized water and                  tetrapropyl ammonium hydroxide is 0.5:0.3:20:1.                  The concentration of the KHCO3 is 0.1 M.

PN KR2254812-B1
TI Quantifying tritium and radioactive carbon from spent activated carbon involves burning the waste activated carbon sample in the sample zone of the high-temperature combustion furnace to generate a combustion product.
AU SEOK O J
   LEE K H
AE RADSOL CO LTD (RADS-Non-standard)
   HI-AIR KOREA CO LTD (HIAI-Non-standard)
GA 202159867R
AB    NOVELTY - Quantifying tritium and radioactive carbon from spent activated carbon involves burning the waste activated carbon sample in the sample zone (212) of the high-temperature combustion furnace (210) to generate a combustion product, passing the generated combustion products through the catalyst zone (214), collecting tritium water contained in the combustion product by the first bubbler solution filled in the first bubbler (220), collecting radioactive carbon dioxide contained in the combustion product by the second bubbler solution filled in the second bubbler (222), preparing a first mixed solution by mixing the first bubbler solution and the first scintillation solution in which tritium water is collected, and preparing a second mixed solution by mixing the second bubbler solution and the second scintillation solution in which the radioactive carbon dioxide is collected.
   USE - Method for quantifying tritium and radioactive carbon from spent activated carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The radioactive carbon contained in waste activated carbon generated in a nuclear power plant and tritium can be quantitatively analyzed quickly and accurately by the liquid scintillation counting method. The treatment cost of the waste activated carbon generated in the air purification system of a nuclear power plant is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Quantifying tritium and radioactive carbon from spent activated carbon involves burning the waste activated carbon sample in the sample zone (212) of the high-temperature combustion furnace (210) to generate a combustion product, passing the generated combustion products through the catalyst zone (214), collecting tritium water contained in the combustion product by the first bubbler solution filled in the first bubbler (220), collecting radioactive carbon dioxide contained in the combustion product by the second bubbler solution filled in the second bubbler (222), preparing a first mixed solution by mixing the first bubbler solution and the first scintillation solution in which tritium water is collected, preparing a second mixed solution by mixing the second bubbler solution and the second scintillation solution in which the radioactive carbon dioxide is collected, preparing a first blank solution by mixing the first bubbler solution and the first scintillation solution in which tritium water is not collected, preparing a second blank solution by mixing a second bubbler solution that does not collect radioactive carbon dioxide and a second scintillation solution, quantifying tritium using the first mixed solution and the first blank solution, and quantifying radioactive carbon using the second mixed solution and the second blank solution.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of an analysis device for quantitative analysis of tritium and radioactive carbon using the high-temperature combustion furnace.
   Combustion furnace (210)
   Sample zone (212)
   Catalyst zone (214)
   First bubbler (220)
   Second bubbler (222)

AB    NOVELTY - Method for purifying hydrogen, involves (a) adding natural gas and water vapor to sealed total reaction kettle at same time, and passing water vapor through high-temperature processing, (b) removing first-stage mixed gas reacted by primary (a) total reaction kettle to S-shaped cooling pipeline and conveying primary mixed gas to primary cooler, cooling, and removing water vapor in first-stage mixed gas, (c) introducing constant-temperature secondary mixed gas to methane absorption tank using U-shaped tube, inserting one end of U-shaped tube to bottom of methane absorption tank, and removing methane, (d) introducing tertiary mixed gas to carbon dioxide reaction kettle using U-shaped tube, and removing carbon dioxide from tertiary mixed gas, and (e) repeating second-stage water vapor removal process, and introducing third-stage mixed gas to secondary cooler, cooling four-stage mixed gas, discharging high-purity hydrogen gas, and introducing high-purity hydrogen gas to gas collecting tank.
   USE - Method for purifying hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures hydrogen with purity of 99.5-99.9%, highly targets and removes one by one in purification process, adopts simple and economical purification equipment, exhibits cost of purifying hydrogen for small and medium enterprises with low, and is easy to promote.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for purifying hydrogen, involves (a) adding natural gas and water vapor to sealed total reaction kettle at same time, setting the internal temperature to 600-800 degrees C under pressure of 0.1013 pan/minute, where the reaction is intermittent operation reaction, and passing water vapor through high-temperature processing of 500-600 degrees C before introducing, (b) removing first-stage mixed gas reacted by primary (a) total reaction kettle to S-shaped cooling pipeline and conveying primary mixed gas to primary cooler, cooling the temperature of primary mixed gas to 23-28 degrees C, and removing water vapor in first-stage mixed gas, (c) introducing constant-temperature secondary mixed gas to methane absorption tank using U-shaped tube, inserting one end of U-shaped tube to bottom of methane absorption tank, and removing methane in secondary mixed gas, (d) introducing tertiary mixed gas to carbon dioxide reaction kettle using U-shaped tube, where one end of U-shaped tube is inserted to bottom of carbon dioxide reaction kettle, and removing carbon dioxide from tertiary mixed gas, and (e) repeating second-stage water vapor removal process, and introducing third-stage mixed gas to secondary cooler, cooling four-stage mixed gas to 1-5 degrees C, discharging high-purity hydrogen gas, and introducing high-purity hydrogen gas to gas collecting tank.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The chemical equation of total reactor involves reacting methane with water to obtain carbon monoxide and hydrogen, and reacting carbon monoxide with water to obtain carbon dioxide and three hydrogen atoms, where the catalyst is nickel, the first-stage mixed gas comprises methane, water, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and the hydrogen concentration out of device is 70-75%. The water vapor in step (a) is overcharged and 1.3-1.8 times amount of natural gas, the ratio of initial natural gas and water vapor reaction is 1:1, and passing excess natural gas by subsequent charging. The methane absorption tank is filled with 2/3 carbon tetrachloride solvent. The carbon dioxide reactor is filled with 2/3 limestone solution, and the reaction equation involves reacting calcium carbonate with water and carbon dioxide to obtain calcium bicarbonate. In method, each time hydrogen purification is performed, the pipeline and inside of equipment need to be vacuumed. Preferred Components: The first-level mixed gas comprises methane, hydrogen, carbon dioxide and large amount of water vapor, the second-level mixed gas comprises methane, carbon dioxide, hydrogen and small amount of water vapor, the third-level mixed gas comprises carbon dioxide, hydrogen and small amount of water vapor, the four-stage mixed gas comprises hydrogen and very small amount of water vapor, and the hydrogen concentration is 99.5-99.9%.

TI Synthesizing intermediate bisacetylferrocene            comprises e.g. dissolving ferrocene and acetic            anhydride in toluene in four-necked bottle, after fully            dissolved, transferring to supercritical device,            passing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.
AU LIU K
   WANG F
AE XUZHOU SHUNPING BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XUZH-Non-standard)
   XUZHOU BANGBO INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202065032K
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing intermediate bisacetylferrocene                comprises (1) dissolving 4 g ferrocene and 5 g of                acetic anhydride in 80 ml toluene in a four-necked                bottle, after fully dissolved, transferring to a                supercritical device, passing supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid at a rate of 0.5 degrees C/minutes,                increasing the temperature, adding 8 g of alluminum                chloride supported alluminum-mobil Composition of                Matter No. 41 (Al-MCM-41) catalyst, stirring at 40                degrees C and 7.5 MPa for 5 minutes, (2) adding 30                ml chloroform under ultrasonic vibration conditions                to fully disperse, dissolving the catalyst, and                connecting the reactor with an exhaust gas                absorption device that can prevent back suction,                (3) continuously feeding the supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid to the reaction device, maintaining 0                degrees C and 7.5 MPa, after 15 minutes the                solution is blood red, and completing the                acetylation reaction when the solution is                purple-black.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing                intermediate bisacetylferrocene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the product selectivity                and yield under Al-MCM-41 shape-selective                catalysis, to achieve the improvement of                intermediate production efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing intermediate bisacetylferrocene                comprises (1) dissolving 4 g ferrocene and 5 g of                acetic anhydride in 80 ml toluene in a four-necked                bottle, after fully dissolved, transferring to a                supercritical device, passing supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid at a rate of 0.5 degrees C/minutes,                increasing the temperature, adding 8 g of alluminum                chloride supported Al-MCM-41 catalyst, stirring at                40 degrees C and 7.5 MPa for 5 minutes, (2) adding                30 ml chloroform under ultrasonic vibration                conditions to fully disperse, dissolving the                catalyst, and connecting the reactor with an                exhaust gas absorption device that can prevent back                suction, (3) continuously feeding the supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid to the reaction device,                maintaining 0 degrees C and 7.5 MPa, after 15                minutes the solution is blood red, and completing                the acetylation reaction when the solution is                purple-black, (4) slowly pouring the purple-black                liquid after the reaction into crushed ice made                from deionized water, the liquid will separate into                layers, washing an upper aqueous phase three times                with a small amount of chloroform, and washing                lower organic phase three times with water, (5)                combining all organic phases, placing in a                distillation unit, and performing distillation to                obtain crude products, and recovering the distilled                chloroform, (6) adding deionized water to the above                crude product, heating to boiling, filtering while                hot, and placing the filtrate in a rotary                evaporator to evacuate and evaporate, dissolving                the resulting solid in absolute ethanol and                allowing to naturally evaporate to obtain refined                intermediate bisacetylferrocene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  the alluminum chloride supported alluminum-mobil                  Composition of Matter No. 41 (Al-MCM-41) catalyst                  comprises (1) dissolving silicon dioxide,                  alluminum trioxide, cetyltrimethylammonium                  bromide, and water in the ratio of 1 :0.0165:                  0.14:80, and 0.014 mol the template                  cetyltrimethylammonium bromide in deionized water                  and stirring at 60 degrees C, (2) adding ethyl                  orthosilicate dropwise, the solution gradually                  turns into a white turbid suspension, , gradually                  adding aluminum isopropoxide, and adjusting the                  pH of the solution with 10% concentration of                  tetraethylhydroxide to adjust the pH = 11, (3)                  stirring the solution at 60 degrees C for 3                  hours, and transferring the solution to a                  polytetrafluoroethylene-lined stainless steel                  reactor for crystallization at 150 degrees C for                  48 hours, (4) filtering, washing with water,                  ethanol and water in turn, drying, and finally                  placing in a muffle furnace, slowly heating up to                  550 degrees C for 8 hours, removing the organic                  template, and obtaining Al-MCM-41, and (5) taking                  alluminum-mobil Composition of Matter No. 41                  (Al-MCM-41 ) and alluminum trioxide with a mass                  ratio of 3.5:1, dissolving in a 10% strength                  hydrochloric acid solution, placing in a round                  bottom flask, stirring vigorously at room                  temperature for 8 hours, filtering and drying the                  solid in an oven at 110 degrees C for 10 hours to                  obtain acidified alliminum trichloride supported                  Al-MCM-41 catalyst.

PN CN108813426-A
TI Drying bream fish by slaughtering fish, adding salt, inserting ultrasonic vibrating rod, placing material in drying chamber, introducing carbon dioxide and promoting powder smoke.
AU PEI L
   WU X
   PEI X
AE ANHUI HAOZAILAI FOOD CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201892095M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for drying bream fish involves (a) slaughtering the bream fish, removing viscera and gills, and cleaning, (b) placing the cleaned fish in salt bath, inserting ultrasonic vibrating rod into salt layer, ultrasonically vibrating the salt layer and using brush to remove salt from fish, (c) hanging bream fish on drying rod in drying chamber, introducing carbon dioxide into drying rod, discharging air in the drying chamber from window, stopping carbon dioxide, opening the window of drying chamber to control the air humidity in drying chamber, (d) air-drying the bream fish, burning the pre-configured powder in the air to dry, burning the powder, regulating the combustion position of the powder to promote the powder smoke to enter the room along with wind, preparing powder by drying and crushing fennel, peppermint, Chrysanthemum, rhizoma ligustici wallichii, turmeric, dried pomegranate skin, pine needle and honeysuckle, sieving and uniformly mixing garlic.
   USE - The method is useful for drying bream fish.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for drying bream fish involves (a) slaughtering the bream fish, removing viscera and gills, and cleaning the fish, (b) placing the cleaned fish in salt bath and buried with salt, inserting ultrasonic vibrating rod into salt layer during salt treatment process, ultrasonically vibrating the salt layer for 12-14 hours and using brush to remove salt from fish, (c) hanging bream fish on drying rod in drying chamber, controlling the hanging distance between the bream fish is 35-50 cm, introducing large amount of carbon dioxide into drying rod, discharging air into drying chamber from window, stopping carbon dioxide, opening the window of drying chamber to control the air humidity in drying chamber to 20-25%, and the wind speed through drying chamber is 1.5-2.5 m/second, (d) air-drying the bream fish for 46-50 hours, burning the pre-configured powder in the air to dry, burning the powder to produce powder smoke in accordance to the wind direction, regulating the combustion position of the powder to promote the powder smoke to enter the room along with wind, air-drying until the moisture content of the bream fish is less than 45%, where the powder comprises 20-30 pts. wt. garlic, 15-18 pts. wt. fennel, 12-18 pts. wt. peppermint, 2-4 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum, 2-3 pts. wt. rhizoma ligustici wallichii, 5-8 pts. wt. turmeric, 2-4 pts. wt. dried pomegranate peel powder, 5-8 pts. wt. pine needle and 3-4 pts. wt. honeysuckle, preparing powder by drying and crushing fennel, peppermint, Chrysanthemum, rhizoma ligustici wallichii, turmeric, dried pomegranate skin, pine needle and honeysuckle, sieving through 50-60 mesh sieve fine powder and uniformly mixing garlic.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In step (b), placing the cleaned fish in salt bath and buried with salt, inserting ultrasonic vibrating rod into salt layer during salt treatment process, ultrasonically vibrating the salt layer for 13 hours and using brush to remove salt from fish. In step (c), hanging bream fish on drying rod in drying chamber, controlling the hanging distance between the bream fish is 45 cm, introducing large amount of carbon dioxide into drying rod, discharging air in the drying chamber from window, stopping carbon dioxide, opening the window of drying chamber to control the air humidity in the drying chamber to 22%, and the wind speed through drying chamber is 2 m/second. In step (d), air-drying the bream fish for 48 hours, burning the pre-configured powder in the air to dry, burning the powder to produce powder smoke in accordance to the wind direction, regulating the combustion position of the powder to promote the powder smoke to enter the room along with wind, air-drying until the moisture content of the bream fish is less than 45%, where the powder comprises 25 pts. wt. garlic, 16 pts. wt. fennel, 18 pts. wt. peppermint, 3 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum, 2.5 pts. wt. rhizoma ligustici wallichii, 7 pts. wt. turmeric, 3 pts. wt. dried pomegranate peel powder, 6 pts. wt. pine needle and 3.5 pts. wt. honeysuckle, and preparing powder by drying and crushing fennel, peppermint, Chrysanthemum, rhizoma ligustici wallichii, turmeric, dried pomegranate skin, pine needle and honeysuckle, and sieving through 60 mesh sieve fine powder and uniformly mixing garlic.

TI Antibacterial water-absorbing pad for fresh-keeping cold fresh meat in trays, comprises antibacterial water absorbing bag body, cold fresh meat and tray that are wrapped by using sealing cover film of breathable film or air permeability.
AU ZHAO C
   FAN X
   LI J
   MENG L
   LIU W
   CHEN H
   WANG H
AE UNIV HEILONGJIANG BAYI AGRIC (UYON-C)
GA 2021C0645A
AB    NOVELTY - Antibacterial water-absorbing pad comprises antibacterial water-absorbing bag body 1 made of nonwoven fabric or special sanitation strong. The antibacterial water-absorbing bag body is divided into two parallel connected independent bags 2, where one independent bag is filled with carbon dioxide releasing agent and other independent bag is filled with water absorbent, where each gram of water absorbent absorbs 15-25 ml. The antibacterial water-absorbing bag body and cold fresh meat are placed on tray. The antibacterial water-absorbing bag body, cold fresh meat and tray are wrapped in-tray by using sealing cover film with piece of breathable film or air permeability.
   USE - Antibacterial water-absorbing pad is used in the field of food preservation for fresh-keeping cold fresh meat in trays, i.e. hot frozen meat and frozen meat.
   ADVANTAGE - The antibacterial water-absorbing pad uses carbon dioxide release agent combined with super absorbent resin combined with shallow tray packaging or tray modified atmosphere packaging to make cold fresh meat in a closed packaging space, prolong the shelf life and improve the appearance of the cold meat product through the antibacterial and humidity control atmosphere formed in the environment, sensory quality of fresh meat is maintained relatively good, have obvious odors and have a huge impact on the sensory indicators of meat taste, and antibacterial water-absorbent pad has good water absorption, good anti-corrosion effect, and convenient preparation and use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing an antibacterial water-absorbing pad for fresh-keeping chilled meat on a tray, which involves:
   a. weighing certain weight of water-absorbing agent into beaker, adding sterile water to beaker based on 20 ml per gram of water-absorbing agent, allowing standing for 1-2 minutes to make it uniformly absorb water, placing water-absorbing agent into independent bag of antibacterial water absorbing bag body, and using heat-sealing machine to perform sealing treatment;
   b. calculating weight of carbon dioxide release agent, where carbon dioxide release agent is composed of sodium bicarbonate and citric acid, mixing sodium bicarbonate and citric acid are uniformly and placing into another independent bag of antibacterial water-absorbing bag body, and using heat-sealing machine to perform sealing treatment;
   c. matching size of antibacterial water-absorbing bag, weight of water-absorbing agent, and weight of carbon dioxide release agent with size of tray, so that stacking thickness of water-absorbing agent is less than 1 cm to avoid occupying more internal space of the tray.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of antibacterial water-absorbing pad.
   Antibacterial water absorbing bag body (1)
   Independent bags (2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide release agent is composed of sodium bicarbonate and citric acid.Preferred Conditions: The anti-bacterial water-absorbent bag body is laid flat on the tray, where thickness of the water-absorbing agent after water absorption is less than 1 cm to avoid occupying more space inside the tray.Preferred Process: The sealing cover film is provided with certain degree of vacuum when the antibacterial water-absorbent bag body, cold meat, and tray are wrapped with a sealing cover film by using modified atmosphere packaging machine to vacuum, so that the air pressure in the sealed cover film is lower than the normal pressure by 0.1 megapascal (MPa). The water-absorbing agent is a super-absorbent resin, and water-absorbing agent absorbs 20 ml per gram of water-absorbing agent before placing in the separate bag, where amount of water absorbing agent is mass ratio of chilled meat to water absorbing agent is 55:1-65:1.
