PT P
PN WO2021124224-A1
TI Removing water from mixture containing water and compound, by prearranging treatment container, feeding mixture into container, feeding stream of treatment gas, maintaining mixture, extracting gas stream and withdrawing concentrate.
AU GARCIA M D L A
   GARCIA M F
AE OMEGA DI MARIO ALBERTO DELLOMODARME & C SAS (OMEG-Non-standard)
   INGAR DI FERNANDO HORACIO GARCIA & C (INGA-Non-standard)
GA 2021712495
AB    NOVELTY - Process for removing water from a mixture containing water and a compound, involves prearranging a treatment container defining a target mass ratio (Rt) between the water and the compound, feeding the mixture into the treatment container, feeding a stream of a treatment gas containing carbon dioxide into the treatment container and causing the treatment gas to contact the mixture, in such a way that a part of the carbon dioxide reacts with the water of the mixture forming carbonic acid, maintaining the mixture at a predetermined treatment temperature, extracting an exhaust gas stream from the treatment container, maintaining and extracting, repeating the steps of determining and comparing until a final concentrate of the compound is obtained having the predetermined final ratio (Rt) between the water and the compound: withdrawing the concentrate as a final concentrate from the treatment container.
   USE - The process is useful for removing water from mixture containing water and a compound.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for removing water from a mixture containing water and a compound, where the mixture has a pH value less than or equal to 7 and being at an initial mass ratio (Ri) between the water and the compound, involves prearranging a treatment container defining a target mass ratio (Rt) between the water and the compound, where the target mass ratio is lower than the initial mass ratio (Ro), feeding the mixture into the treatment container, feeding a stream of a treatment gas containing carbon dioxide into the treatment container and causing the treatment gas to contact the mixture, in such a way that a part of the carbon dioxide reacts with the water of the mixture forming carbonic acid, maintaining the mixture at a predetermined treatment temperature, extracting an exhaust gas stream from the treatment container, where the exhaust gas stream comprises water and carbon dioxide, so as to progressively remove water from the mixture while the stream of the treatment gas is supplied, and the exhaust gas stream is extracted in/from the treatment container, and the mixture containing the compound is concentrated, where a step is provided of determining a current mass ratio (R) between the water and the compound in the mixture, comparing the current mass ratio (R) with the target mass ratio (Rt), if the current mass ratio (R) is lower than the target mass ratio (Rt) continuing the steps of feeding, maintaining and extracting, repeating the steps of determining and comparing, until a final concentrate of the compound is obtained having the predetermined final ratio (Rt) between the water and the compound: withdrawing the concentrate as a final concentrate from the treatment container.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The carbon dioxide has a concentration higher than 50%, preferably higher than 70% in the treatment gas, where the treatment gas comprises substantially pure carbon dioxide. The treatment temperature is higher than 30 degrees C, preferably higher than 40 degrees C, more 45-75 degrees C. The compound comprises a mineral and/or organic acid, where the acid is sulfuric acid and the treatment temperature is set between 45-75 degrees C until a mass ratio between the water and the sulfuric acid is reached corresponding to a sulfuric acid concentration lower than 85 wt.%, and is progressively increased up to a temperature lower than the boiling temperature of the mixture until a mass ratio between the water and the sulfuric acid is reached corresponding to a sulfuric acid concentration between 85-98%, preferably the treatment temperature is increased up to 190 degrees C. The mixture further comprises cations of a metal. The compound is a salt having a saturation concentration in water, where the mixture is a homogeneous solution of the salt and the steps of feeding, maintaining and extracting are continued until the target mass ratio (Rt) is attained corresponding to a concentration higher than the saturation concentration to crystallize the salt in the treatment container. The mixture comprises a wet solid, preferably a waste sludge from an industrial process, where the water is an imbibition water of the solid. The mixture is a vegetal juice. Preferred Process: The steps of feeding, maintaining and extracting are continued until the target mass ratio (Rt) is attained corresponding to a predetermined residual amount of moisture per mass unit of the compound, until the compound is substantially dry, where the compound is a salt obtained in an anhydrous state at the end of process. The steps are provided of condensing and receiving the water of the exhaust gas stream, conveying a portion of the carbon dioxide contained in the exhaust gas stream, which has been separated as an incondensable gas, back to the treatment container. The steps of determining and comparing are performed according to a predetermined time program during the steps of feeding, maintaining and extracting, preferably decreasing-length time intervals are provided between consecutive steps of determining and comparing. The steps of determining and comparing are performed once a predetermined time has elapsed since the beginning of the steps of feeding, maintaining and extracting. The step of determining a current mass ratio R between the water and the compound present in the mixture is performed indirectly, by any other equivalent weight or volume ratio, or an equivalent ratio involving both weight and volume amounts, and the step of comparing the current mass ratio (R) is performed by comparing the other equivalent ratio with a corresponding target ratio equivalent to the target mass ratio (Rt). The step of comparing is performed indirectly between current values of equivalent mass or volume equivalent amounts, and target amount values of the water or of the mixture, where the equivalent amount values can comprise the mass of the mixture, whose current value can be determined by weighing the treatment container for instance, where the target or final value being known which the mixture will have upon reaching the target conditions, the volume of the mixture, whose current value can be determined by measuring the level of the mixture i.e. present in the treatment container, the mass or the volume of the water extracted from the treatment container along with the exhaust gas mixture, whose current value can be determined by weighing or reading the volume of the water condensed in an exchanger and collected in a reservoir, where the amount of exhaust gas mixture extracted from the treatment container and the amount of treatment gas supplied to the treatment container, and computing the difference of such amounts being provided. The process is performed by a continuous flow of the mixture, and the steps of determining and comparing are made in line along a flow of the treated mixture, preferably at the exit of the treatment container, or a tubular treatment container, a step being provided of conveying the treated mixture back into the container itself, preferably into a feed tank of the treatment container, as long as or in any case when the step of comparing indicates that the content of water accompanying the compound is higher than the target value, and the step of extracting the treated mixture being provided when the step of comparing indicates that the content of water accompanying the compound is lower than or equal to the target value (Rt).

PN CN110982247-A
TI Preparing film-type carbon dioxide indicator in transportation or storageprocess of sodium bicarbonate, involves adding polyvinyl chloride and polyvinylidene fluoride to mixed solution comprises dimethylacetamide and tetrahydrofuran.
AU SU D
   REN D
AE NANJING HANERSI BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020314830
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing film-type carbon dioxide indicator involves adding polyvinyl chloride and polyvinylidene fluoride to the mixed solution comprises dimethylacetamide and tetrahydrofuran, stirring and dissolving at 40-50 degrees C, then slowly adding water-based polyurethane emulsion dropwise, and controlling the speed to 100-120rpm, returning to room temperature after stirring for 30-50 minutes to obtain blended emulsion A, mixing disodium hydrogen phosphate and agar at elevated temperature, melting and mixing, pouring into the spray equipment, passing compressed nitrogen gas to spray the mixed molten liquid into atomized droplets, solidifying under cold air and sieving to obtain spherical binary porogen, adding initiator and filler to dimethyl formamide, stirring and mixing, and oscillating ultrasonically for 5-15 minutes to obtain solution B, adding methyl red and bromothymol blue to deionized water to prepare a solution C with a certain mass concentration for use.
   USE - Method for preparing film-type carbon dioxide indicator used in transportation or storageprocess of sodium bicarbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare film-type carbon dioxide indicator is convenient to use, has high mechanical strength, and good indication effect, and high sensitivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing film-type carbon dioxide indicator involves adding polyvinyl chloride and polyvinylidene fluoride to the mixed solution comprises dimethylacetamide and tetrahydrofuran, stirring and dissolving at 40-50 degrees C, then slowly adding water-based polyurethane emulsion dropwise, and controlling the speed to 100-120rpm, returning to room temperature after stirring for 30-50 minutes to obtain blended emulsion A, mixing disodium hydrogen phosphate and agar at elevated temperature, melting and mixing, pouring into the spray equipment, passing compressed nitrogen gas to spray the mixed molten liquid into atomized droplets, solidifying under cold air and sieving to obtain spherical binary porogen, adding initiator and filler to DMF, stirring and mixing, and oscillating ultrasonically for 5-15 minutes to obtain solution B, adding methyl red and bromothymol blue to deionized water to prepare a solution C with a certain mass concentration for use, adding solution B and solution C to the blended emulsion A dropwise at the same time and stirring at 60-80rpm for 5-15 minutes, adjusting the speed to 100-120rpm and adding the spherical binary porogen to continue stirring 30-50 minutes, adjusting the speed to 200-400rpm and adding sodium carboxymethylcellulose to continue stirring for 30-50 minutes to coating liquid after defoaming under vacuum, placing clean and drying release paper on a smooth glass substrate, applying the coating liquid on the release paper with a film applicator, standing at room temperature and solidifying for 1-3 hours, placing oven out after drying at the temperature for 5-15 minutes, and standing to cool and scrape the film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The weight ratio of polyvinyl chloride and polyvinylidene fluoride is 20-30:1. The volume ratio of dimethylacetamide and tetrahydrofuran in the mixed solution is 1:1. The weight ratio of disodium phosphate and agar is 1: 0.3-1. Preferred Components: The initiator is benzoyl peroxide, dibenzoyl peroxide, peroxide Lauroyl oxide, di-tert-butyl peroxide, dicumyl peroxide, diisopropyl peroxide, dicyclohexyl dicarbonate, azobisisobutyronitrile, azobisisoheptanonitrile. The filler is nano zinc oxide and nano titanium dioxide in a weight ratio of 1-5:1-5. The weight ratio of methyl red and bromothymol blue is 3:2. The mass concentration of the solution C is 3-8 wt.%. The oven temperature is 35-40 degrees C.

PN CN108950593-A
TI Preparing copper nanowire-supported tin catalyst electrode for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide comprises e.g. self-growing layer of copper nanowires on surface of the copper electrode by redox method and ultrasonic cleaning.
AU YE D
   CHEN G
   ZHU X
   CHEN R
   ZHANG B
   LI J
   LIAO Q
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2018A0465J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper nanowire-supported tin catalyst electrode for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide comprises e.g. (a) self-growing layer of copper nanowires on the surface of the copper electrode by redox method, (a.i) placing the copper electrode in hydrochloric acid for ultrasonic cleaning, washing with deionized water, ultrasonically washing in a mixed solution of ethanol and acetone, rinsing with deionized water and drying, (a.ii) placing the copper electrode obtained in the step (a.i) into the obtained reaction solution, where the reaction solution comprises sodium hydroxide solution and ammonium sulfite solution, removing and allowing to stand, washing with deionized water, naturally drying the air to obtain copper(II) hydroxide nanowire electrode, (a.iii) placing copper(II) hydroxide nanowire electrode obtained in the step (a.ii) in a sodium borohydride solution for reduction reaction, taking out, washing with deionized water, and drying under vacuum.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing copper nanowire-supported tin catalyst electrode for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; has excellent performance; improves surface area of the electrode; increases contact area between catalyst and electrolyte; and improves electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing copper nanowire-supported tin catalyst electrode for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide comprises (a) self-growing layer of copper nanowires on the surface of the copper electrode by redox method, (a.i) placing the copper electrode in hydrochloric acid for ultrasonic cleaning, washing with deionized water, ultrasonically washing in a mixed solution of ethanol and acetone, rinsing with deionized water and drying, (a.ii) placing the copper electrode obtained in the step (a.i) into the obtained reaction solution, where the reaction solution comprises sodium hydroxide solution and ammonium sulfite solution, removing and allowing to stand, washing with deionized water, naturally drying the air to obtain copper(II) hydroxide nanowire electrode, (a.iii) placing copper(II) hydroxide nanowire electrode obtained in the step (a.ii) in a sodium borohydride solution for reduction reaction, taking out, washing with deionized water, and drying under vacuum to obtain copper nanowire electrode, (b) electroplating tin catalyst on a copper nanowire electrode, and preparing electrode having nanowire structure, (b.i) dissolving sodium citrate in deionized water, dissolving tin(II) chloride in a sodium citrate solution to obtain plating solution, stirring the plating solution for more than 10 hours before plating, (b.ii) placing copper nanowire electrode obtained in the step (a.iii) in the plating solution obtained in the step (b.i), where the counter electrode is platinum plate electrode, and the rotor is appropriately stirred, electroplating by constant current plating, washing with deionized water and vacuum drying to obtain copper nanowire-supported tin catalyst electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The copper plate electrode is foamed copper electrode. The electrode is a copper plate electrode. The surface of the copper electrode is provided with a copper nanowire. The surface of the copper nanowire is plated with a tin catalyst.

PN CN108169223-A; CN108169223-B
TI Determining alkali value of dimethyl sulfoxide recovered from carbon fiber wastewater comprises e.g. using bromocresol green as indicator, using carbon dioxide-free water in test and titrating using hydrochloride standard solution.
AU LV C
   LI Y
   ZHAO Y
   WEI Y
   SHAO W
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 201849051W
AB    NOVELTY - Determining alkali value of dimethyl sulfoxide recovered from carbon fiber wastewater comprises e.g. using bromocresol green as an indicator, using carbon dioxide-free water in the test, titrating using hydrochloride (HCl) standard solution until the solution turns yellow-green and keeping without discoloration, repeating titration volume value with obtained HCl standard solution, taking arithmetic mean of remaining parallel titration results from highest and lowest values of finally consumed HCl standard solution and recording as V0, subjecting carbon fiber wastewater to a distillation method for recovering dimethyl sulfoxide, mixing rectified and recovered dimethyl sulfoxide and carbon dioxide-free water thoroughly, mixing evenly, adding bromocresol green indicator, titrating with HCl standard solution until solution turns yellow-green and keeping without discoloration which is the final point of titration and repeating titration of the volume value of obtained HCl standard solution.
   USE - The method is useful for determining alkali value of dimethyl sulfoxide recovered from carbon fiber wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method detects alkalinity value of recovered dimethyl sulfoxide accurately and rapidly and is simple in operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Determining alkali value of dimethyl sulfoxide recovered from carbon fiber wastewater comprises (i) using bromocresol green as an indicator, using carbon dioxide-free water in the test, titrating using hydrochloride (HCl) standard solution until the solution turns yellow-green and keeping for 30 seconds without discoloration, repeating titration volume value with obtained HCl standard solution for 5 times, taking arithmetic mean of remaining 3 parallel titration results from highest and lowest values of finally consumed HCl standard solution and recording as V0; (ii) subjecting carbon fiber wastewater to a distillation method for recovering dimethyl sulfoxide, mixing rectified and recovered dimethyl sulfoxide and carbon dioxide-free water thoroughly at a ratio of 1:1-6, mixing evenly, adding bromocresol green indicator, titrating with HCl standard solution until solution turns yellow-green and keeping for 30 seconds without discoloration which is the final point of titration, repeating titration of the volume value of obtained HCl standard solution for 5 times, taking arithmetic mean of the remaining 3 parallel titration results from highest and lowest values volume of finally consumed HCl standard solution and recording as V; and (iii) calculating rectified dimethyl sulfoxide alkalinity value according to the formula as specfiedi, where volume of the HCl standard solution consumed by V-titration to the end point is in ml, V0 is the volume of the HCl standard solution consumed by the titration blank in ml, concentration of HCl standard solution is mol/l and V1 is volume of dimethyl sulfoxide recovered by distillation in the test in ml.

PN KR1840813-B1
TI Multi-gas detecting apparatus has gas correlation filter that is provided with reference cells for generating reference beams in order to measure carbon dioxide and methane gas with respect to light passed through disk band filter.
AU EO I S
AE UNIV HONAM IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYHO-Non-standard)
GA 201824717X
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) includes a light source (110) which is fixed to a frame (101) for emitting the infrared ray. A gas correlation filter (150) is provided with reference cells for generating the first and second reference beams in order to measure the carbon dioxide and the methane gas with respect to the light passed through the filter domains of a disk band filter (130). An output unit (195) controls a sub-motor (185) through a driving unit (194) so as to move to a movement circulation position when the number of rotations of the main motor reaches a set number of rotations.
   USE - Multi-gas detecting apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - Two or more measurement values for the carbon dioxide and methane gas are obtained in one rotation of a disk-shaped wavelength filter, thereby the measurement accuracy and the measurement speed are increased.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A main motor (171) rotates a rotation center axis. The optical cavity structure is arranged so that the measurement target gas is loaded and unloaded and the light passing through the correlation filter passes. The output unit produces the concentration of the carbon dioxide and methane gas within the optical cavity structure from the first reference beam, first measurement light, second reference beam and second measurement light detected by the optical detector. First and second reference cells are charged with the nitrogen or the inert gas. First and second measurement cells are charged with the carbon dioxide and the methane gas. An optical detector (191) detects the light passing through an optical cavity structure (160). The optical cavity structure includes an upper reflective plate (162) and a reflective plate (164). The light coming out from the light source is inclined with respect to the upper reflective plate. A main motor position adjusting portion (180) varies the separation distance between the optical axis of the light emitted from the light source and the rotation center axis of the main motor along the orthogonal direction. The adjusting portion includes sub-motor which is installed in a main motor housing. A pinion gear (186) is rotated with the sub-motor. A rack gear (187) is mounted on the frame and is engaged with the pinion gear. The driving unit operates the sub-motor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the multi-gas detecting apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN US2018020700-A1; WO2018022671-A1; CA3031814-A1; US10674749-B2; MX2019001067-A1
TI Production of carbonated beverage involves introducing carbon dioxide into flowing stream of product blend containing water, syrup and dissolved oxygen and deaerating carbon dioxide-containing product blend into vented atmospheric vessel.
AU LUCAS L M
   LUCAS L
AE BEVCORP LLC (BEVC-Non-standard)
   BEVCORP LLC (BEVC-Non-standard)
GA 201807319N
AB    NOVELTY - A carbonated beverage is produced by introducing carbon dioxide (CO2) into flowing stream of product blend containing water, syrup and dissolved oxygen, such that CO2 is dissolved in product blend, deaerating CO2-containing product blend by introducing blend into vented atmospheric vessel in which interior is at ambient pressure with headspace maintained above surface of liquid within vessel and dissolved oxygen is released from product blend and vented from vessel, and pumping deaerated product blend from vessel.
   USE - Production of carbonated beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - More stable product is produced by reducing amount of dissolved air. Faster filling of container is achieved by lowering amount of dissolved air. Production losses due to spillage and short fills from product foaming during packaging are prevented. Shelf-life is increased due to lower oxygen levels in product as well as reduced corrosion in case of aluminum containers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbonated beverage is produced by introducing carbon dioxide (CO2) into flowing stream of product blend containing water, syrup and dissolved oxygen, such that CO2 is dissolved in product blend, deaerating CO2-containing product blend by introducing blend into vented atmospheric vessel in which interior is at ambient pressure with headspace maintained above surface of liquid within vessel and dissolved oxygen is released from product blend and vented from vessel, pumping deaerated product blend from vessel in which deaerated blend includes dissolved CO2 at intermediate carbonation level less than final carbonation level, and carbonating deaerated product blend to final carbonation level downstream of vented vessel to produce carbonated beverage for subsequent packaging. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) system for producing carbonated beverage comprising carbon dioxide (CO2) injector which controllably injects CO2 into flowing stream of blend of water and syrup containing dissolved oxygen, atmospheric deaerating vessel which is located downstream of CO2 injector and vents to atmosphere and maintains liquid level and headspace above liquid contained in which deaerating vessel receives flowing stream of water/syrup blend, and carbonator which is located downstream of deaerating vessel and carbonates deaerated blend of water and syrup received from deaerating vessel; and
   (2) production of beverage which involves introducing nitrogen gas (N2) into flowing stream of blend of water and syrup containing dissolved oxygen, such that N2 is dissolved in blend, deaerating N2 containing blend by introducing blend into vented atmospheric vessel in which interior is at ambient pressure with headspace maintained above surface of liquid within the vessel, and dissolved oxygen is released from blend and vented from vessel, and pumping deaerated blend from vessel in which deaerated blend includes dissolved N2.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the deaeration system.
   Gas mass flow meter (8)
   Control valve (9)
   Static mixer (11)
   Tank (12)
   Level sensor (15)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Parameters: Intermediate carbonation level is 2-4 volumes of carbon dioxide (CO2) less than final carbonation level, or 0.6 -1.45 volumes of CO2 per volume of product blend. Temperature of product blend during the steps of injecting and deaerating is 50-90 degrees F. Preferred Process: Temperature of product blend during deaeration is measured. Amount of CO2 introduced during carbonating step is adjusted based upon temperature. Temperature is measured by measuring temperature within vented atmospheric vessel, measuring temperature of product blend entering vented atmospheric vessel, or measuring temperature of product blend leaving the vented atmospheric vessel. Deaerating step reduces dissolved oxygen in product blend to less than or equal to 200 ppb, preferably less than or equal to 100 ppb. Deaerating step reduces dissolved nitrogen. Released oxygen and portion of CO2 injected upstream of vessel is vented from vessel during deaerating step. Carbon dioxide (CO2) is injected in form of bubbles having size of less than or equal to 5 mu m in diameter. CO2-containing product blend is introduced into vented atmospheric vessel at a height within 1 foot of height of surface of liquid within the vessel, and CO2 is injected sufficiently upstream of vented vessel so that contact time of greater than or equal to 1 s is provided. Preferred Components: Product blend further contains dissolved nitrogen. Amount of dissolved CO2 retained in deaerated product blend pumped from vented vessel is within 0.1 volumes of full CO2 saturation at atmospheric pressure at measured product blend deaeration temperature.

TI Carbon dioxide seal address and safety monitoring            system has composite modulation demodulation instrument            that is connected with embedded armoured spiral cable            or armored distributed three component seismic signal            sensing cable, first and second underground monitoring            armored cables.
AU WU J

   WANG X

AE OPTICAL SCI & TECHNOLOGY CHENGDU LTD (OPTI-Non-standard)
GA 2022C2815K
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a metal sleeve pipe (3) whose                outer side is fixed with a first underground                standard distributed pressure sensor array. An                outer side of a carbon dioxide injection pipe (4)                in a carbon dioxide monitoring well is connected                with a second underground standard distribution                pressure sensor array. A ground three-component                detector is provided with a wired or wireless node                type according to a three-dimensional mode. A                composite modulation-demodulation instrument (15)                is placed near a well opening of the carbon dioxide                monitoring well. The composite modulation                demodulation device is connected to an embedded                armored spiral cable or an armored distributed                three component seismic signal sensing cable, a                first underground monitoring armoured cable (5) and                the second underground monitoring armored cable                (6).
   USE - Carbon dioxide seal address and safety                monitoring system based on optical fiber sensing                technology. Uses include but are not limited to                military, national defense, aerospace, industrial                and mining enterprises, energy and environmental                protection, industrial control, medicine and                health, measurement and testing, construction, and                household appliances.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can use the well-ground combined                collecting three-dimensional three-component ground                well seismic data to perform high precision                amplitude-preserving construction offset imaging                processing and multi-amplitude processing seismic                data attribute extraction, so that high precision                construction off-set imaging result can be fully                studied. The fault distribution characteristic and                rule in the underground back inclined structure of                the carbon dioxide sealing area can be evaluated                comprehensively. The influence and damage degree of                the underground structure suffering strong                structure movement can be comprehensively                evaluated. The buried depth and thickness of the                reservoir for carbon dioxide sequeasagging,                extension range, porosity, permeability,                connectivity and other important reservoir                parameters can be selected safely and suitable                carbon dioxide seal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                carbon dioxide seal address and safety monitoring                method based on optical fibre sensing                technology.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic plan view of                the distribution of carbon dioxide injection wells                and monitoring wells in the carbon dioxide                injection and geological storage area.

PN CN110684575-A
TI Removing carbon dioxide in high-pressure natural gas by introducing carbon dioxide-containing natural gas into absorption tower, subjecting absorption liquid to flash evaporation, heating, performing thermal flash evaporation and cooling.
AU MAO S
   ZHU D
   ZHOU Z
   YU Y
   CHEN Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 2020074056
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing carbon dioxide in high-pressure natural gas involves (i) introducing high-pressure and high-content carbon dioxide-containing natural gas into an absorption tower to be subjected to countercurrent contact with an absorption liquid for absorption and respectively obtaining purified gas and carbon dioxide-enriched rich liquid from the tower top and the tower bottom of the absorption tower, (ii) subjecting the carbon dioxide absorption liquid to a high-pressure flash evaporation to remove dissolved methane and other hydrocarbons, (iii) introducing the absorption liquid of a flash evaporation of hydrocarbon into a low-pressure flash evaporation tank and simultaneously carrying out a hot flash steam stripping in the low-pressure flash evaporation tank, (iv) heating the absorption liquid after the low-pressure flash evaporation, (v) subjecting to a thermal flash evaporation and (iv) cooling the absorption liquid after the thermal flash evaporation and recycling.
   USE - The method is useful for removing carbon dioxide in high-pressure natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can reduce system energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for removing carbon dioxide in high-pressure natural gas involves (i) introducing high-pressure and high-content carbon dioxide-containing natural gas into an absorption tower to be subjected to countercurrent contact with an absorption liquid for absorption and respectively obtaining purified gas and carbon dioxide-enriched rich liquid from the tower top and the tower bottom of the absorption tower, (ii) subjecting the carbon dioxide absorption liquid to a high-pressure flash evaporation to remove dissolved methane and other hydrocarbons, where the high-pressure flash pressure is 0.8-1.2 MPa, (iii) introducing the absorption liquid of a flash evaporation of hydrocarbon into a low-pressure flash evaporation tank and simultaneously carrying out hot flash steam stripping in the low-pressure flash evaporation tank, where the low-pressure flash evaporation pressure is normal pressure to 0.08 MPa, (iv) heating the absorption liquid after the low-pressure flash evaporation to increase the temperature of the absorption liquid to 3-15 degrees C, (v) subjecting the heated absorption liquid to a thermal flash evaporation, where the thermal flash evaporation pressure is 0.01-0.09 MPa and (iv) cooling the absorption liquid after the thermal flash evaporation and recycling to the absorption tower.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves introducing the absorption liquid having temperature of 60-75 degrees C into the absorption tower. The method involves carrying out the hot flash steam stripping at the same time of the low-pressure flash evaporation, where the pressure of the low-pressure flash evaporation is 0.01-0.08 MPa. The method involves heating the absorption liquid after the low-pressure flash evaporation at 5-10 degrees C and performing the thermal flash evaporation, where the thermal flash pressure is 0.01-0.08 MPa. The method involves using a flash vapor of hot flash as a stripping gas for the low pressure flash evaporation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The absorption liquid is a methyldiethanolamine solution with the addition of an activator. The absorption liquid activator is diethanolamine or piperazine and its derivative or hindered amine. The absorption liquid contains a defoaming agent or a corrosion inhibitor.

AB    NOVELTY - Monitoring flammability of various streams                during vinyl acetate production, involves (a)                reacting using a feed stream comprising acetic                acid, ethylene, oxygen, carbon dioxide, alkanes,                and water in a reactor to produce a crude vinyl                acetate stream comprising vinyl acetate, acetic                acid, water, ethylene, carbon dioxide, and alkanes,                (b) cooling the crude vinyl acetate stream in a                heat exchanger, (c) separating the crude vinyl                acetate stream into a tail gas stream, a flash gas                stream, and a vinyl acetate stream, where the tail                gas stream comprises ethylene, carbon dioxide,                alkanes and oxygen, (d) heating the tail gas stream                in the heat exchanger, (e) removing at least a                portion of the carbon dioxide from the flash gas                stream to produce carbon dioxide removal overheads                stream, (f) mixing vaporized acetic acid with the                tail gas stream in a vaporizer to produce a                vaporized stream, and (g) adding oxygen to the                vaporized stream to produce the feed stream, and                measuring and/or determining.
   USE - The method is useful for monitoring                flammability of various streams during vinyl                acetate production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has improved efficiencies at more                than one location in the vinyl acetate production                process.

PT P
PN CN112844476-A
TI Biomass carbon material loaded with nano-nickel catalyst for reforming catalytic reaction with methane-dioxide feed gas, is obtained by removing impurities from biomass rich in cellulose, and adding nickel ion-containing nitrate solution.
AU HAN J
   YANG Q
   QIN L
   ZHAO B
   ZHANG Q
AE UNIV WUHAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UWSC-C)
GA 202161972D
AB    NOVELTY - Biomass carbon material loaded with nano-nickel catalyst is obtained by removing impurities from the biomass rich in cellulose and lignin, adding nickel ion-containing nitrate or carboxylate solution to the biomass-containing aqueous solution, obtaining the nickel-containing biochar by hydrothermal treatment together, reducing the catalyst precursor in sodium borohydride, obtaining a biomass carbon nano-nickel catalyst containing oxygen-containing functional groups, where nickel is the active component, the loading of nickel is 5-7 %mass, the proportion of nickel particles with a particle size of less than 4 nm is not greater than 12%, the pore diameter of the catalyst is 1-4 nm, and the volume of the micropores with a pore diameter less than 4 nm is 0.273-0.366 cm3/g.
   USE - The biomass carbon material loaded with nano-nickel catalyst is used for reforming catalytic reaction with methane-dioxide feed gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst maintains at least 500 hours without deactivation in the carbon dioxide reforming stability experiment, stabilizes the conversion rates of methane and carbon dioxide at 80-92% and 90-98%, respectively, has high activity and stability, and produces almost no carbon deposits during the reaction process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the catalyst involves (a) using biomass rich in cellulose and lignin as raw material, after dehydration and crushing, mixing with acid solution in the ratio of 10-20 g:100 ml, soaking for 12 hours at room temperature, washing the filtered mixture with deionized water until the supernatant pH of 7, finally, taking the filtrate and drying in an oven at 100-110 degrees C for 12-24 hours to obtain a solid, (b) placing the solid obtained in step (a) above in a beaker and dispersing in an aqueous solution containing a nickel compound, transferring to a hydrothermal reactor to undergo hydrothermal carbonization reaction, and washing several times with deionized water, (c) using the chemical reduction method, placing the solid obtained in step (b) in a magnetic stirrer and stirring, dripping sodium borohydride solution, and continuously stirring for 1-2 hours, transferring to a vacuum drying oven for drying at 85 degrees C to obtain the product; and
   (2) use of the catalyst in reforming catalytic reaction with methane-dioxide feed gas at a reaction temperature of 650-850 degrees C and a space velocity of 10-20 l/(g.hours) in an inert gas atmosphere, and the reaction time is 0-500 hours, to prepare synthesis gas containing hydrogen and carbon monoxide, the feed gas is configured according to methane: carbon dioxide is 1:1, the conversion rate of methane and carbon dioxide can be 80-87% and 88-95%, respectively, and the gas volume ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide in the synthesis gas is 0.86-0.98.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nickel-containing compound is nickel nitrate hexahydrate, nickel chloride hexahydrate, tetrahydrate, and/or nickel acetate. The molar ratio of borohydride ion to nickel ion in the step (c) is 6-4:1. Preferred Conditions: The conditions for the hydrothermal carbonization reaction in the second step are temperature: 100-250 degrees C for 5-10 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of the acid solution used in the step (a) is 0.01-2 mol/l. The acid used is any one of aqueous solutions of nitric acid, sulfuric acid, and/or phosphoric acid. The biomass used in the step (a) comprises bagasse, corn stover, wheat stover, peanut hulls, and/or sunflower hulls.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E31-A02C; E31-N03C; E31-N05B1; J04-E04A; J04-E11; N02-C01; N06-E01; N06-F; N07-J

PN CN111632587-A; CN111632587-B
TI Indium-indium trioxide heterogeneous nanosheet            composite material electrocatalyst for electrocatalytic            carbon dioxide activation, has heterogeneous structure,            and clear boundary between crystalline and amorphous            regions.
AU ZHANG B
   SUN W
   LIANG Y
   HUANG Y
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 202089626F
AB    NOVELTY - An indium-indium trioxide heterogeneous                nanosheet composite material electrocatalyst has                heterogeneous structure and thickness of 1-3                microns, and is partially amorphous after                reduction, and has clear boundary between                crystalline and amorphous regions. The preparation                of indium-indium trioxide heterogeneous nanosheet                composite material involves dispersing indium                nitrate in deionized water, adding urea,                dispersing, placing in hydrothermal reactor,                reacting, washing, drying to obtain indium                hydroxide, placing indium hydroxide, heating,                calcining to obtain indium trioxide, dispersing                indium trioxide in mixed electrolyte solution of                ethanol and 5% Nafion (RTM: perfluorinated resin                solution), diffusing on carbon paper as working                electrode, reducing content of indium trioxide in                0.4-0.6 mol/L sodium bicarbonate in three-electrode                system consisting of working electrode, saturated                calomel reference electrode and platinum counter                electrode using linear sweep voltammetry, and                scanning.
   USE - Indium-indium trioxide heterogeneous nanosheet                composite material electrocatalyst for                electrocatalytic carbon dioxide activation                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The indium-indium trioxide heterogeneous                nanosheet composite material electrocatalyst is                economical, has high product purity, large specific                surface area, and excellent carbon dioxide                activation performance, and is prepared at low                temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An indium-indium trioxide heterogeneous                nanosheet composite material electrocatalyst has                heterogeneous structure and thickness of 1-3                microns, and is partially amorphous after                reduction, and has clear boundary between                crystalline and amorphous regions. The preparation                of indium-indium trioxide heterogeneous nanosheet                composite material involves dispersing indium                nitrate in deionized water, adding urea,                dispersing, placing in hydrothermal reactor,                reacting at 100-180 degrees C, preferably 120-150                degrees C for 12-24 hours, preferably 15-20 hours,                centrifuging, washing, drying to obtain indium                hydroxide, where mass volume ratio of indium                nitrate, urea and water is (451-452) mg:(360-361)                mg:(20-40) mL, placing indium hydroxide in air                atmosphere, heating to 450-650 degrees C,                preferably 500-600 degrees C at heating rate of 2-6                degrees C/minute, preferably 3-5 degrees C/minute,                calcining for 1-4 hours, preferably 2-3 hours to                obtain indium trioxide, dispersing 1-3 mg indium                trioxide in mixed electrolyte solution of 0.5-2 mL                ethanol and 8-12 mu L 5% Nafion (RTM:                perfluorinated resin solution) for 15-25 minutes,                diffusing on carbon paper as working electrode,                reducing in three-electrode system consisting of                working electrode, saturated calomel reference                electrode and platinum counter electrode, where                linear sweep voltammetry is used to reduce content                of indium trioxide in 0.4-0.6 mol/L sodium                bicarbonate, and scanning 8-12 linear sweep                voltammetry curves at voltage of -0.9 V to 2.2 V,                preferably -0.7 V to 1.9 V for stability.                INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of the indium-indium trioxide                heterogeneous nanosheet composite material                electrocatalyst; and
   (2) use of electrocatalyst for                electrocatalytic carbon dioxide activation, which                involves producing formate with high Faraday                efficiency and excellent product selectivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass-volume ratio                  of indium nitrate, urea and water is preferably                  (451.1-451.3) mg:(360.3-360.5) mg:(25-35) mL. The                  amount of indium trioxide is preferably 1-2 mg.                  The amount of ethanol added is preferably 1-2 mL.                  The amount of 5% Nafion (RTM: perfluorinated                  resin solution) solution added is preferably 8-10                  mu L.

PT P
PN CN111424183-A
TI Extracting and preparing lithium carbonate from sulfate type and chloride type salt lake brine involves concentrating brine, adding alkaline solution, magnesium ions, extractant and diluent, feeding carbon dioxide, extracting, crystallizing.
AU MENG Y
   BA T
AE MENG Y (MENG-Individual)
GA 202069400D
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises concentrating brine of the salt lake to a suitable concentration of lithium ion, adding alkaline solution, subjecting to magnesium ions, passing an extractant and diluent, stirring, extracting, repeating the extraction to obtain an extracted organic phase containing lithium ions and small amount of magnesium(II) ions, feeding carbon dioxide and pure water into extracted organic phase, optionally the multi-stage operation of further extraction and stripping can achieve the requirement of low magnesium-lithium ratio by adjusting the pH value of the stripping solution, adding alkali to the stripping solution to precipitate and remove a small amount of magnesium ions in the form of magnesium hydroxide, heating lithium bicarbonate aqueous solution obtained after magnesium removal and decomposing by heating to produce lithium carbonate which is slightly soluble in water, separating the precipitate, washing and drying to obtain lithium carbonate crystals.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting and preparing lithium carbonate from sulfate type and chloride type salt lake brine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical; and expands the adaptability of the new extraction technology for lithium extraction in various salt lakes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting and preparing lithium carbonate from sulfate type and chloride type salt lake brine comprises (a) concentrating the brine of the salt lake to a suitable concentration of lithium ion, where the ratio of magnesium to lithium is not greater than 1000:1, adding an alkaline solution to adjust the pH of the brine to neutral or slightly alkaline, and subjecting to no or a small amount of magnesium ions in the raw brine, (2) passing an extractant into the salt lake brine, adding diluent, stirring and mixing well, extracting for 2-8 minutes, and repeating the extraction for 1-5 times to obtain an extracted organic phase containing lithium ions and small amount of magnesium(II) ions, (3) taking the extracted organic phase into a mixer, feeding carbon dioxide and pure water into the mixer as a stripping agent, where the hydrogen ion produced by the hydrolysis of carbon dioxide and water as the stripping agent replaces and extracts lithium ion in the organic phase to obtain lithium bicarbonate and magnesium bicarbonate aqueous solutions, at this time, the magnesium-lithium reduction ratio in the stripping solution is reduced to the level of economic production of lithium carbonate 2:1-4:1, optionally the multi-stage operation of further extraction and stripping can achieve the requirement of low magnesium-lithium ratio by adjusting the pH value of the stripping solution, (4) adding alkali to the stripping solution to precipitate and remove a small amount of magnesium ions in the form of magnesium hydroxide, and (5) heating lithium bicarbonate aqueous solution obtained after magnesium removal and decomposing by heating to produce lithium carbonate which is slightly soluble in water, where lithium carbonate crystallizes out, separating the precipitate, washing and drying to obtain lithium carbonate crystals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline solution is sodium hydroxide solution, potassium hydroxide solution, aqueous ammonia, sodium carbonate solution or calcium hydroxide solution. The slightly alkaline pH in the step (1) is less than or equal to 8. In the step (2) extractant is neutral phosphonate or fluorinated neutral phosphonate.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)
MC M25-A01; M25-B; M25-G04
IP C22B-026/12; C22B-003/38; C22B-003/44

PT P
PN CN110591244-A
TI Preparing environment-friendly polyvinyl chloride foam material for home improvement and packaging products, involves adding polyvinyl chloride resin powder, plasticizer, zinc stearate and barium stearate.
AU LIU C
AE HUAIAN BINGQING CONSTR ENG MANAGEMENT CO (HUAI-Non-standard)
GA 2019A7579C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing environment-friendly polyvinyl chloride (PVC) foam material involves adding 100 pts. wt. of PVC resin powder, 40-48 pts. wt. of plasticizer, 3-5 pts. wt. of zinc stearate, 3-5 pts. wt. of barium stearate, 6-8 pts. wt. of polyester thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer rubber, 7-9 pts. wt. of trioctyl trimellitate, 12-16 pts. wt. of nitrile rubber, 5-6 pts. wt. of aluminum hydroxide and 10-13 pts. wt. of magnesium hydroxide to the high and putting them in low speed mixer for mixing. After mixing for 10 minutes, the mixed PVC material is discharged. The mixed PVC material is put on a two-roll plastic mixer for mixing, the temperature is 175-180 degrees C, the mixing time is 15-20 minutes, and a PVC film with a thickness of 0.5 mm is made. The PVC film is cut into square sheets, put it into the supercritical carbon dioxide pressurized reaction device, and pass carbon dioxide gas into the reaction device. After the preset pressure is reached, the reaction will be maintained for 2 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing environment-friendly polyvinyl chloride foam material used for home improvement and packaging products such as wallpaper, soft bags and handbags.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare environment-friendly polyvinyl chloride foam material, with with excellent indicators such as hardness, foaming rate, and flame retardancy, and the cells in the foam material are closely arranged and uniform in size, which can be used for home improvement and packaging products such as wallpaper, soft bags and handbags.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing environment-friendly polyvinyl chloride (PVC) foam material involves adding 100 pts. wt. of PVC resin powder, 40-48 pts. wt. of plasticizer, 3-5 pts. wt. of zinc stearate, 3-5 pts. wt. of barium stearate, 6-8 pts. wt. of polyester thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer rubber, 7-9 pts. wt. of trioctyl trimellitate, 12-16 pts. wt. of nitrile rubber, 5-6 pts. wt. of aluminum hydroxide and 10-13 pts. wt. of magnesium hydroxide to the high and low speed mixer for mixing. After mixing for 10 minutes, the mixed PVC material is discharged. The mixed PVC material is put on a two-roll plastic mixer for mixing, the temperature is 175-180 degrees C, the mixing time is 15-20 minutes, and a PVC film with a thickness of 0.5 mm is made. The PVC film is cut into square sheets, put it into the supercritical carbon dioxide pressurized reaction device, and pass carbon dioxide gas into the reaction device. After the preset pressure is reached, the reaction will be maintained for 2 hours. After the reaction is completed, release the pressure, and then foam the square sheet in a water bath heater for 6-8 seconds, and finally cool it in cold water to obtain an environmentally friendly PVC foam material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The plasticizer is dioctyl phthalate. Preferred Conditions: The reaction conditions of the holding and holding pressure reaction comprises a reaction temperature of 30 degrees C and a reaction pressure of 15 megapascal. The temperature of foaming in the water bath heater is 80-90 degrees C.

(including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. PT P
PN CN109499273-A; CN109499273-B
TI Method for preparing hexagonal molecular sieve membrane i.e. utilized for separating carbon dioxide/methane, involves cutting ceramic support, followed by polishing, washing, drying and firing and cooling.
AU CHEN W
   WANG P
   ZHOU Y
   ZHU L
   XU H
   ZHANG F
   WEN X
AE SHANGHAI LUQIANG NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (SHNR-C)
   SHANGHAI CHEM IND RES INST CO LTD (SHNR-C)
GA 201928753W
AB    NOVELTY - A hexagonal molecular sieve membrane preparing method involves cutting the ceramic support, polishing, washing, drying and firing in a muffle furnace, after cooling, placing the ceramic support in a solution containing a cationic polymer, performing soaking treatment in water, taking out and drying to obtain pretreated ceramic support, taking aluminum source, silicon source and template which are dissolved in deionized water with stirring to obtain sol liquid. The sol liquid is poured into to reaction vessel to be sealed and crystallized, washed and adjusted the pH to obtain the molecular sieve seed solution. The pretreated ceramic support is immersed in the molecular sieve seed solution, coated the molecular sieve seed crystal on the surface of the pretreated ceramic support, dried and mixed in aluminum oxide, sodium oxide, silicon dioxide, templating agent and water to obtained molecular sieve synthetic liquid, which is crystallized, dried and calcined to obtain finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing hexagonal molecular sieve membrane i.e. utilized for separating carbon dioxide/methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing the hexagonal molecular sieve membrane with strong and not easy to fall off functions, high film finished product rate, high mechanical strength, better continuity, relative high carbon dioxide absorption capacity and high carbon dioxide/methane separation coefficient, small mass transfer resistance, low energy consumption and strong regeneration ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hexagonal molecular sieve membrane preparing method involves cutting the ceramic support, polishing, washing, drying and firing in a muffle furnace at 200-650 degrees C for 0.5-12 hours, after cooling, placing the ceramic support in a solution containing a cationic polymer, performing soaking treatment in water at room temperature to 100 degrees C for 1 minute to 12 hours, taking out to remove excess cationic polymer on the surface and drying to obtain a pretreated ceramic support, taking aluminum source, silicon source and template are fully dissolved in deionized water with stirring according to the ratio of aluminum oxide, silicon dioxide, templating agent and water of 1:1-10:1-15:100-300 to obtain sol liquid. The sol liquid is poured into the reaction vessel to be sealed and crystallized, washed and adjusted the pH to obtain the molecular sieve seed solution. The pretreated ceramic support is immersed in the molecular sieve seed solution, coated the molecular sieve seed crystal on the surface of the pretreated ceramic support by the immersion pulling process and dried at low temperature to obtain the ceramic support coated with the molecular sieve seed crystal. The ceramic support coated with the molecular sieve seed crystal is mixed in aluminum oxide, sodium oxide, silicon dioxide, templating agent and water at molar ratio of 1:0.1-10:1-50:0-1.0:20-400 to obtained molecular sieve synthetic liquid, which is crystallized at 100-160 degrees C for 10-20 days, dried and calcined to obtain the finished product. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a hexagonal molecular sieve membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ceramic support is selected from tubular porous support, single-channel and/or multi-channel structure, that is made from alumina, magnesia, zirconia, titanium oxide and/or silicon oxide, preferably selected from alumina, zirconia and/or titanium oxide. The aluminum source is selected from aluminum hydroxide, hydrated alumina, aluminum sol, sodium aluminate, aluminum sulfate, aluminum isopropoxide, aluminum phosphate and/or alumina. The silicon source is selected from solid silica gel, solid sodium silicate, water glass, silica sol, ethyl orthosilicate, methyl orthosilicate, ethyl orthosilicate, silicate, calcium silicate and/or white carbon black. The template agent is selected from tetramethyl ammonium hydroxide, tetraethyl ammonium hydroxide, tetrapropyl ammonium hydroxide, tetraethyl ammonium bromide, tetrapropyl ammonium hydroxide, diethylamine, ammonia, n- butylamine and/or pyridine. The sodium source is selected from sodium oxide, sodium hydroxide and/or sodium carbonate, preferably sodium hydroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The cationic polymer is selected from diallyl dimethyl ammonium, polyamidine, polyvinylamine, cationic polyacrylamide and/or poly hydroxypropyl dimethyl ammonium chloride. The cationic polymer is selected from dimethyl diallyl ammonium chloride and 3-chlorine-2-hydroxypropyl dimethyl ammonium chloride.

TI Reaction device for producing iron salt, has top cover, heating resistor, drain pipe, grid plate, gas pipe, gas storage device, compression device, air pump, pump pipe, air hole, air hole with fixed net, reaction tank, and electronic valve.
AU ZHU X
AE JIANGSU XIAOCHUN NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201909931A
AB    (CN208395174-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a reaction device for producing iron salt, comprising a top cover, a heating resistor, a drain pipe and a plate, said top cover is installed on the top of reaction pot, and the upper end of the top cover connected with a gas pipe. and the other end of the gas pipe connected to gas storage device, between the gas pipe and the gas storage device is equipped with a compression device, and the storage device of the other side mutually connecting with one end of the air pump, the heating resistor is provided to the decompression tank outside, and the decompression tank are connecting with the pump pipe through the other gas pipe. the pump pipe is opened with an air hole, and the air hole is paved with a fixed net; said discharge pipe connected at the at the centre of the bottom of the reaction tank, and discharging pipe is provided with an electronic valve, said mesh plate mutually connected with the reaction tank through the groove, and the reaction tank is provided with a feeding pipe. the iron salt production reaction device, for maintaining the oxygen-free environment in the reaction tank by carbon dioxide, and realizes recycling use of the carbon dioxide, reduces the energy waste.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the front structure of the reaction device for producing iron salt.

   Drain pipe (12)

EA    NOVELTY - The device has top cover (1) which is mounted on top of reaction tank (2). The upper end surface of top cover is connected with a gas pipe (3). A fixed net is arranged on an air hole (10). A drain pipe (12) is connected to the center of the bottom of the reaction tank. An electronic valve (13) is arranged on the drain pipe. The inner wall of the reaction tank is provided with a groove (14). A grid plate (15) is interconnected with the reaction tank through a groove. A feed tube (16) is mounted on the reaction tank.
   USE - Reaction device for producing iron salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen-free environment in the reaction tank is maintained by carbon dioxide, and recycling of carbon dioxide is realized, so that the production of energy waste is reduced.

PT P
PN CN108977881-A; CN108977881-B
TI Inhibiting polycrystallization of monocrystalline            diamond edges, involves acid-treating substrate,            cleaning, selecting surface with excellent quality as            growth surface, opening chemical vapor deposition            device cavity, and placing substrate.
AU LU C
   WANG Z
   CHEN M
   ZHANG G
   ZHAI D
   DI D
AE ZHONGNAN DIAMOND CO LTD (CHNI-C)
GA 2018A2584A
AB    NOVELTY - The method for inhibiting polycrystallization                of monocrystalline diamond edges involves selecting                single crystal to obtain several substrates,                acid-treating the substrate, cleaning, selecting a                surface with excellent quality as growth surface,                opening cavity of chemical vapor deposition device,                placing substrate on a surface of heat dissipation                device, vacuumizing, introducing hydrogen,                regulating pressure of a reaction chamber, setting                microwave power, starting power supply, increasing                plasma ball, increasing microwave power and cavity                pressure, stabilizing, etching substrate surface,                continuously increasing microwave power and cavity                pressure, raising temperature, introducing methane,                nitrogen and carbon dioxide, adjusting power and                pressure, increasing temperature, maintaining power                and pressure constant for diamond deposition,                improving flow rate of methane, nitrogen and carbon                dioxide, reducing power and pressure, and                maintaining the temperature for stable                growth.
   USE - Method for inhibiting polycrystallization of                monocrystalline diamond edges.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables inhibition                polycrystallization of monocrystalline diamond                edges, and which improves the deposition quality of                the monocrystalline diamond by strictly controlling                the gas flow ratio and the power pressure ratio of                each step in the preparation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for inhibiting polycrystallization                of monocrystalline diamond edges involves (1)                selecting single crystal to obtain several                substrates, where the substrate has a (100)                orientation and thickness of 300-350 mu m, (2)                subjecting the substrate to acid treatment,                ultrasonically cleaning with acetone and alcohol,                and selecting a surface with excellent quality and                without impurity defects as a growth surface, (3)                opening a cavity of a microwave plasma chemical                vapor deposition (MPCVD) device, placing the                substrate after processed in the step (2) on a                surface of a molybdenum substrate heat dissipation                device, vacuumizing, introducing hydrogen,                regulating the pressure of a reaction chamber to                1-20 mbar, setting the power of the microwave power                to 0.8-1.2 kW, starting the microwave power supply,                after the plasma ball is stably formed, increasing                the plasma ball by 0.1 kW at an interval of 10-15                seconds, increasing the microwave power and the                cavity pressure to 1.5-2.5 kW and 30-90 mbar by the                amplitude of 10 mbar, stabilizing when the                substrate temperature reaches 400-700 degrees C,                etching the substrate surface by H plasma,                continuously increasing the microwave power to                2.6-3.5 kW and the cavity pressure to 100-160 mbar                with the same amplitude after H plasma etching,                raising the temperature to 750-900 degrees C,                introducing methane, nitrogen and carbon dioxide,                continuously adjusting the microwave power and the                chamber pressure, increasing the temperature to                900-1050 degrees C, maintaining the microwave power                and the chamber pressure constant for diamond                deposition, improving the flow rate of methane,                nitrogen and carbon dioxide for every 10 hours                after diamond deposition in step (2) for 50 hours,                reducing the microwave power and the chamber                pressure, and maintaining the temperature at                930-990 degrees C for stable growth.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: In step (2), each                  cleaning medium is continuously cleaned 3 times,                  where each washing time is 3 minutes. Preferred                  Component: The acid in step (2) is aqua regia.                  Preferred Process: In step (3), in the diamond                  deposition process of step (2), methane and                  nitrogen are closed for every 12 hours, the                  single crystal growth surface, the edge                  polycrystal and inside of cavity are                  plasma-etched by hydrogen and carbon dioxide for                  30-100 seconds, continuously introducing methane                  and nitrogen after etching is finished,                  recovering to normal growth, and controlling the                  temperature fluctuation within 20 degrees                  C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The purity of the                  hydrogen, nitrogen, and carbon dioxide is greater                  than 99.999%, and the purity of the methane is                  greater than 99.995%.

PN CN108404983-A; CN108404983-B
TI Preparation of ordered mesoporous phenolic resin            polymer-supported silver catalyst used for synthesis of            alkynol of carbon dioxide and terminal alkyne compound            involves subjecting resin to amination and sulfonation,            and reacting with silver.
AU WU H
   MEI Y
   WU P
AE UNIV EAST CHINA NORMAL (UYEN-C)
GA 201866029M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ordered mesoporous phenolic                resin polymer-supported silver catalyst comprises                reacting mesoporous phenolic resin with                chloromethyl methyl in the presence of anhydrous                aluminum trichloride to obtain chloromethylated                mesoporous phenolic resin, reacting obtained resin                with methylamine or dimethylamine and acetonitrile                solution, washing 3 times with acetone and                deionized water, and drying under vacuum to obtain                aminated mesoporous phenolic resin, reacting with                sulfuric acid solution, washing with hot water,                vacuum-drying to obtain sulfonated mesoporous                phenolic resin, mixing obtained resin with silver                nitrate and deionized water at 10:1:1-5 mass ratio,                stirring at room temperature for 10-20 hours,                washing three times with deionized water, reducing                in solution of ethanol and water having 1:1 volume                ratio for 6-15 minutes, washing with deionized                water, and drying under vacuum for 24 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing ordered mesoporous                phenolic resin polymer-supported silver catalyst                used for multi-phase catalytic synthesis of alkynol                of carbon dioxide and terminal alkyne compound                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The obtained catalyst has good catalytic                activity, easy separation, very high catalytic                yield under mild conditions, no pollution to                environment, and simple preparation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of ordered mesoporous phenolic                resin polymer-supported silver catalyst comprises                mixing mesoporous phenolic resin and chloromethyl                methyl ether at 1:3-8 mass ratio under ice water                bath and argon protection, adding 1.2 pts. wt.                mixture of anhydrous aluminum trichloride in three                batches, reacting in ice water bath for 20-30                hours, quenching reaction with deionized water,                suction-filtering, washing filtrate alternately                with deionized water and acetone, drying under                vacuum at 80 degrees C for 24 hours, mixing                obtained resin, methylamine or dimethylamine and 99                wt.% acetonitrile solution at 1:3-10:15-40 mass                ratio, reacting at 70-90 degrees C for 5-10 hours,                filtering, washing filtrate three times with                acetone and deionized water, and drying under                vacuum to obtain aminated mesoporous phenolic                resin; mixing mesoporous phenolic resin with 50                wt.% sulfuric acid solution at 1:3-8 mass ratio,                reacting at 65-85 degrees C for 20-30 hours,                filtering, washing filtrate three times with hot                water, drying in vacuum to obtain sulfonated                mesoporous phenolic resin; and mixing aminated and                sulfonated ordered mesoporous resins with 99.8 wt.%                silver nitrate and deionized water at 1-10:1-5,                stirring at room temperature for 10 to 20 hours,                filtering, washing filtrate three times with                deionized wate, reducing in solution of ethanol and                water having 1:1 volume ratio for 6 to 15 minutes,                washing with deionized water, and drying under                vacuum for 24 hours. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for application of the catalyst for                multi-phase catalytic synthesis of alkynol of                carbon dioxide and terminal alkyne compound                comprising reacting at catalyst amount of 0.1 mol.%                based on nanosilver content relative to terminal                alkyne compound at 50-70 degrees C for 12-24 hours                while charging carbon dioxide at normal                pressure.
