PT P
PN CN110817908-A
TI System used for preparing high-purity lithium            carbonate using lithium-containing waste material,            contains extraction leaching unit, lithium            precipitation reaction unit, hydrogenation reaction            unit, ion exchange unit, and purification unit.
AU KONG J
   JIANG Y
   HUANG W
   LI H
   LI Z
   HE X
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 202018153T
AB    NOVELTY - A system comprises an extraction leaching                unit, which is used for leaching the                lithium-containing waste material with an                extractant to obtain an extraction solution to                separate lithium ions from other nickel, cobalt and                manganese ions; a lithium precipitation reaction                unit, where the enriched lithium solution from the                extraction leaching unit is added to a sodium                carbonate solution to produce a lithium carbonate                precipitate, which is separated by filtration to                obtain a crude lithium carbonate; a hydrogenation                reaction unit, which is used for preparing a crude                product from the lithium precipitation reaction                unit and water into a slurry, and passes carbon                dioxide gas to obtain a clear solution of lithium                bicarbonate; an ion exchange unit, which                ion-exchanges the lithium bicarbonate clear                solution from the hydrogenation reaction unit to                achieve anion and cation deep processing; a                purification unit; and a spray-drying unit.
   USE - The system is useful for preparing high-purity                lithium carbonate using lithium-containing waste                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method of the lithium                carbonate: is continuously controllable, high in                extraction rate and resource utilization degree,                low in production cost and easy to industrialize;                and achieves the goals of green, environmental                protection, energy saving and emission                reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A system comprises an extraction leaching                unit, which is used for leaching the                lithium-containing waste material with an                extractant to obtain an extraction solution to                separate lithium ions from other nickel, cobalt and                manganese ions; a lithium precipitation reaction                unit, where the enriched lithium solution from the                extraction leaching unit is added to a sodium                carbonate solution to produce a lithium carbonate                precipitate, which is separated by filtration to                obtain a crude lithium carbonate; a hydrogenation                reaction unit, which is used for preparing a crude                product from the lithium precipitation reaction                unit and water into a slurry, and passes carbon                dioxide gas to obtain a clear solution of lithium                bicarbonate; an ion exchange unit, which                ion-exchanges the lithium bicarbonate clear                solution from the hydrogenation reaction unit to                achieve anion and cation deep processing; a                purification unit, which is used for purifying the                solution from the ion exchange unit by adding a                complexing agent and a membrane separation                technology; and a spray-drying unit, which                spray-dries the purification liquid from the                purification unit. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included                for a method for preparing the high-purity lithium                carbonate by using lithium-containing waste                material, which involves: (I) adding the                lithium-containing waste material and the                extractant in proportion to an extraction kettle,                controlling the temperature at 20-60 degrees C,                heat preserving and stirring for 10-120 minutes to                obtain an extraction liquid after the reaction is                completed, followed by enriching and separating                lithium ions; (II) adding the extraction liquid                obtained in step (I) to a reaction kettle, heating                at 90-95 degrees C, adding a saturated sodium                carbonate solution in a pulse form, heat preserving                and stirring for 0.5-2 hours, adding seed crystals                to promote the crystallization of lithium carbonate                to form a lithium carbonate slurry, and isolating                the crude lithium carbonate by filtration; (III)                adding water to the crude lithium carbonate                obtained in step (II) to prepare a lithium                carbonate slurry, passing carbon dioxide gas until                the solution changes from turbid to clear, and                reacting to obtain a lithium bicarbonate solution;                (IV) passing the lithium bicarbonate solution                obtained in step (III) through an ion exchange                column loaded with a cation exchange resin, an                anion exchange resin and/or a chelate resin in one                or more stages, and separately removing trace ionic                impurities in the solution; (V) adding a complexing                agent to the solution obtained in step (IV),                complexing a small amount of calcium, magnesium and                iron ions in the filtrate into a complex ion with a                large volume, and separating smaller lithium ions                through ultrafiltration membrane to obtain a                refined lithium-rich solution; and (VI)                spray-drying the purified lithium-rich solution                obtained in step (V).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (I), the                  extraction temperature is 20-80 degrees C, and                  the extraction time is 15-120 minutes. In step                  (II), the pulse frequency of the saturated sodium                  carbonate solution is 10-100 kHz. In step (IV),                  the flow rate of the concentrated solution                  through the ion exchange column is 5-50 bed                  volumes per hour (BV/h). Preferred Components: In                  step (II), the seed crystals are lithium                  carbonate, the particle size is nanometer and                  hundreds of micrometers, and the shape is                  spherical shape, rod shape, flower shape, flake                  shape and/or hollow sphere. In step (III), the                  ratio of crude lithium carbonate to water is                  1:5-1:50, and the flow rate of carbon dioxide gas                  is 0.5-5 l/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (I), the                  extraction agent is pyrrole hexafluorophosphate                  ionic liquid, imidazole hexafluorophosphate ionic                  liquid, pyridine hexafluorophosphate ionic                  liquid, piperidine hexafluorophosphate ionic                  liquid, N,N-dimethylformamide, tributyl                  phosphate, mono-2-ethylhexyl                  (2-ethylhexyl)phosphonate and/or                  N,N-bis(2-ethylhexyl)acrylamide. In step (V), the                  complexing agent is EDTA, crown ether,                  aminotriacetic acid, citric acid, tartaric acid,                  oleic acid, gluconic acid, and/or                  diethylenetriaminepentaacetic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In step (IV), the                  cation exchange resin is styrene resin, acrylic                  resin and/or phenolic resin. The anion exchange                  resin is styrene resin, acrylic resin and/or                  epoxy resin. The chelating resin is D110, D113,                  D152, D401, D403, D418 and/or D564. In step (V),                  the material of the ultrafiltration membrane                  includes ceramic, polysulfone,                  polyetheretherketone, polyvinylidene fluoride or                  PTFE. The filtration accuracy of ultrafiltration                  membrane is 10-100 nm. The module mode of the                  ultrafiltration membrane is hollow fiber, roll                  type, plate type or tube type, and the filtration                  mode of the ultrafiltration membrane is                  cross-flow or counter-current filtration.

PN CN109678809-A; CN109678809-B
TI Preparing tetrazole functionalized UIO-67, comprises e.g. reacting 2-(((1H-tetrazol-5-yl)methylamino)methyl)biphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylic acid and zirconium(IV) chloride with N,N-dimethylformamide, concentrated hydrochloric acid by sonication.
AU WANG J
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV SHANXI (UYSX-C)
GA 2019411275
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing tetrazole functionalized UIO-67 (I), comprises e.g. (i) dissolving dimethyl 2-(((1H-tetrazol-5-yl)methylamino)methyl)biphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylate (A4) in tetrahydrofuran, adding sodium hydroxide solution into the reaction system, refluxing, cooling the mixture to room temperature, distilling off tetrahydrofuran, adjusting the pH of the system to be acidic, precipitating, filtering, washing and drying to obtain 2-(((1H-tetrazol-5-yl)methylamino)methyl)biphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylic acid (A5), and (ii) adding zirconium(IV) chloride to a reaction vessel containing N,N-dimethylformamide, adding concentrated hydrochloric acid, sonicating, continuously adding N,N-dimethylformamide into the reaction system, adding (A5), sonicating until the (A5) is completely dissolved, heating the reaction system, filtering the reaction mixture, washing the precipitate with N,N-dimethylformamide until the supernatant is colorless, washing with acetone, and drying to final product.
   USE - (I) is useful for selective capture and storage of carbon dioxide in mixed gases (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple, cost low and easy to popularize and apply. (I): has high crystallinity, high thermal stability and chemical stability, and utilizes solves the problem of low adsorption selectivity to carbon dioxide/nitrogen and low adsorption amount of carbon dioxide in the vicinity of low pressure zone.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing tetrazole functionalized UIO-67 of formula (I), comprises (i) (a) adding 2-methylbiphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylic acid into a reaction vessel containing methanol, slowly adding concentrated sulfuric acid into the reaction system, heating and refluxing the reaction, cooling to room temperature, steaming until large amount of solid precipitates, filtering, washing, and drying to dimethyl 2-methylbiphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylate of formula (A1) as white solid, (b) dissolving (A1) in carbon tetrachloride as solvent, introducing argon gas at room temperature, adding N-bromosuccinimide and azobisisobutyronitrile into the reaction system under an inert atmosphere, heating and refluxing, cooling the reaction mixture to room temperature, filtering, washing the filtrate with saturated sodium sulfate solution, saturated sodium bicarbonate solution, saturated sodium chloride solution and water, separating the organic layer, drying over anhydrous sodium sulfate, filtering and evaporating, adding methanol to reflux, filtering and drying to obtain dimethyl 2-(dibromomethyl)biphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylate of formula (A2) as white solid, (c) adding (A2) into an acetone/water mixed solvent, adding silver nitrate into the reaction system under absence of light, reacting at room temperature, distilling off the acetone, adding dichloromethane into the reaction system for extraction, separating organic layer, washing the organic layer with saturated sodium hydrogen carbonate solution, drying over anhydrous sodium sulfate and evaporating to dryness to obtain dimethyl 2-formylbiphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylate of formula (A3), (d) dissolving (1H-tetrazol-5-yl)methanamine in methanol, adding (A3) at room temperature, refluxing, cooling the reaction to room temperature, adding sodium cyanoborohydride, heating the reaction to complete the reaction, adding saturated sodium carbonate solution into the reaction system, distilling off methanol, adding water into the reaction system, extracting the mixture with diethyl ether, washing the organic layer with water, drying over anhydrous sodium sulfate, evaporating to dryness, filtering the precipitate to obtain white solid as dimethyl 2-(((1H-tetrazol-5-yl)methylamino)methyl)biphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylate of formula (A4), and (e) dissolving (A4) in tetrahydrofuran, adding 1 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution into the reaction system, refluxing, cooling the mixture to room temperature, distilling off tetrahydrofuran, adjusting the pH of the system to be acidic with 1 mol/l hydrochloric acid solution, precipitating large amount of white precipitate, filtering, washing the precipitate with water and drying to obtain 2-(((1H-tetrazol-5-yl)methylamino)methyl)biphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylic acid of formula (A5), and (ii) adding zirconium(IV) chloride to a reaction vessel containing N,N-dimethylformamide, adding concentrated hydrochloric acid, sonicating, continuously adding N,N-dimethylformamide into the reaction system, adding (A5), sonicating until the (A5) is completely dissolved, heating the reaction system, filtering the reaction mixture, washing the precipitate with N,N-dimethylformamide until the supernatant is colorless, washing with acetone, and drying to final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (a) of the step (i), the ratio between 2-methylbiphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylic acid and methanol is 0.1-0.2 mmol: 1 ml, the ratio between concentrated sulfuric acid and methyl terephthalic acid is 0.07-0.08 ml: 1 mmol, the temperature of the refluxing reaction is 75-85 degrees C, and the reflux reaction time is 11-13 hours. In step (b) of the step (i), the ratio between (A1) and carbon tetrachloride is 0.1-0.3 mmol: 1 ml, the argon gas time at room temperature is 25-35 minutes, and the amount of N-bromosuccinimide added under an inert atmosphere is 1-3 times that of (A1), the amount of azobisisobutyronitrile added is 0.6-0.7 wt.% of (A1), the heating reaction temperature is 75-85 degrees C, and the reaction time is 9-11 hours, the ratio between amount of methanol and (A1) is 3-4 ml: 1 mmol, and the reflux time is 1-3 hours. In step (c) of the step (i), the volume ratio between acetone/water mixed solvent is 4:1, the molar ratio between (A2) and acetone/water mixed solvent is 1 mmol: 10 ml, the molar amount of silver nitrate added to the reaction system is 1.2-3.2 times that of (A2), the reaction time is 24 hours at room temperature, the extraction ratio between dichloromethane and (A2) is 10 ml: 1 mmol. In step (iv) of the step (i), the ratio between methanol and (1H-tetrazol-5-yl)methanamine is 6 ml: 1 mmol, the molar amount of the added (1H-tetrazol-5-yl)methanamine is 1-2 times that of the (A3), and the reflux reaction time is 4-6 hours, the molar amount of sodium cyanoborohydride added is 1.5-2.5 times that of the (1H-tetrazol-5-yl)methanamine, the temperature of the heating reaction is 45-55 degrees C, and the heating reaction time is 4-6 hours, after the reaction the ratio between saturated sodium carbonate solution and the (1H-tetrazol-5-yl)methanamine added into the system is 1.5-2.5 ml: 1 mmol, the ratio between water to the (1H-tetrazol-5-yl)methanamine is 3.5-4.5 ml: 1 mmol, the number of extractions with diethyl ether was 3 times, and the ratio between amount of diethyl ether and amount of (1H-tetrazol-5-yl)methanamine is 5.5-6.5 ml: 1 mmol. In step (e) of the step (i), the ratio amount between the (A4) and tetrahydrofuran is 0.2-0.4 mmol: 1 ml, the ratio between 1 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution and (A4) is 6-8 ml: 1 mmol, the reflux reaction time is 4.5-5.5 hours, and the pH is adjusted to 2-5 with 1 mol/l hydrochloric acid. In the step (2), adding zirconium(IV) chloride into a reaction vessel containing N,N-dimethylformamide, where the ratio between zirconium(IV) chloride and N,N-dimethylformamide in the reaction vessel is 0.07-0.09 mmol: 1 ml, the ratio amount between concentrated hydrochloric acid and of zirconium(IV) chloride is 1.2-1.3 ml: 1 mmol, the sonication time is 18-22 minutes, after zirconium(IV) chloride is completely dissolved, the ratio amount between N,N-dimethylformamide and zirconium(IV) chloride is 24.5-25.5 ml: 1 mmol, the molar amount of the (A5) added is equivalent to zirconium(IV) chloride, and the sonication time is 8-12 minutes, the temperature of the reaction is 75-85 degrees C, the reaction time is 23-25 hours, and the number of times of acetone washing is 3 times.

TI Working medium used in composition for thermal            cycle system, comprises trifluoroethylene,            difluoromethane, and compound e.g. fluoroethane, and            has preset temperature glide when refrigeration cycle            system is operated at preset condition.
AU ONO H
   UENO K
   OTSUKA T
AE ASAHI GLASS CO LTD (ASAG-C)
   AGC INC (ASAG-C)
   AGC INC (ASAG-C)
   AGC INC (ASAG-C)
   AGC INC (ASAG-C)
   AGC KK (ASAG-C)
GA 201821623K
AB    NOVELTY - A working medium comprises trifluoroethylene,                difluoromethane, and compound(s) (c1) e.g.                1,1-difluoroethane, fluoroethane, propane,                propylene, carbon dioxide,                2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene and                (E)-1,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene. The working medium                has a temperature glide of 10 degrees C or less,                when refrigeration cycle system (10) is operated at                preset condition. The average temperature of                evaporation start temperature and evaporation                completion temperature (when working medium is a                non-azeotropic mixture), and the evaporation                temperature (when working medium is an azeotropic                mixture), are 0 degrees C.
   USE - Working medium is used in composition for                thermal cycle system e.g. freezing/refrigerating                system, air conditioning system, electric                power-generating system, heat-transporting system                and secondary cooling system (all claimed). Uses                include but are not limited to built-in-type                showcase, separate placement-type showcase,                business use freezer-refrigerator, automatic                vending machine, ice-making machine, room air                conditioner, package air conditioner for store,                package air conditioner for building, package air                conditioner for equipment, air conditioner for                train, air conditioner for automobile, waste heat                recovery power generation system, and heat                pipe.
   ADVANTAGE - The working medium has excellent thermal cycle                performance capable of being substituted for                near-azeotropic mixture of difluoromethane and                pentafluoroethane, low flammability, suppressed                self-decomposability, and low influence on global                warming.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A working medium comprises trifluoroethylene,                difluoromethane, and compound(s) (c1) chosen from                1,1-difluoroethane, fluoroethane, propane,                propylene, carbon dioxide,                2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene and                (E)-1,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene. The total content                of trifluoroethylene and difluoromethane is 90-100                wt.% with respect to total content of compound (a).                The working medium has a temperature glide of 10                degrees C or less, when the reference refrigeration                cycle system is operated at a condensation                temperature of 40 degrees C, a supercooling degree                of 5 degrees C, and a superheat degree of 5 degrees                C. The average temperature of evaporation start                temperature and evaporation completion temperature                (when the working medium is a non-azeotropic                mixture), and the evaporation temperature (when the                working medium is an azeotropic mixture), are 0                degrees C. The average temperature of condensation                start temperature and condensation completion                temperature (when the working medium is a                non-azeotropic mixture), and the condensation                temperature (when the working medium is an                azeotropic mixture), are 40 degrees C. The content                (%mass) of trifluoroethylene (HFO-1123), content                (%mass) of difluoromethane (R32), content (%mass)                of 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene (1234yf), content                (%mass) of 1,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene (1234ze),                content (%mass) of carbon dioxide (CO2), content                (%mass) of 1,1-difluoroethane (R152a), content                (%mass) of fluoroethane (R161), content (%mass) of                propane, and content (%mass) of propylene satisfy                the relation: -1.000x (HFO-1123)+1.179x                (R32)+1.316x (1234yf)+1.316x (1234ze(E))+3.831x                (CO2)+2.632x (R152a)+2.390x (R161)+6.262x                (propane)+2.237x (propylene)=more than 0 (I),                3.426x (HFO-1123)+5.673x (R32)+2.193x                (1234yf)-0.596x (1234ze(E))-0.768x (CO2)+29.897x                (R152a)+64.400x (R161) +118.965x (propane)+94.943x                (propylene)=less than 10 (II), 1.293x                (HFO-1123)+1.029x (R32)+0.369x (1234yf)+0.354x                (1234ze(E))+3.807x (CO2)+0.229x (R152a)+0.406x                (R161)+0.568x (propane)+0.719x (propylene)=less                than 1.78 (III), 1.214x (HFO-1123)+1.133x                (R32)+0.402x (1234yf)+0.346x (1234ze(E))+3.359x                (CO2)+0.323x (R152a)+0.548x (R161)+0.588x                (propane)+0.725x (propylene)=more than 0.91 (IV),                and 0.3x (HFO-1123)+675x (R32)+4x (1234yf)+6x                (1234ze(E))+1x (CO2)+124x (R152a)+12x (R161)+3.3x                (propane)+1.8x (propylene)=less than 160. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for composition for                thermal cycle system, which comprises the working                medium and a lubricating oil.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of the reference refrigerating cycle system.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content (%mass)                  of trifluoroethylene (HFO-1123), content (%mass)                  of difluoromethane (R32), content (%mass) of                  2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene (1234yf), content                  (%mass) of 1,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene (1234ze),                  content (%mass) of carbon dioxide (CO2), content                  (%mass) of 1,1-difluoroethane (R152a), content                  (%mass) of fluoroethane (R161), content (%mass)                  of propane, and content (%mass) of propylene                  satisfy the relation: 3.426x (HFO-1123)+5.673x                  (R32)+2.193x (1234yf)-0.596x (1234ze(E))-0.768x                  (CO2)+29.897x (R152a)+64.400x (R161)+118.965x                  (propane)+94.943x (propylene)=less than 8 (II-1),                  3.426x (HFO-1123)+5.673x (R32)+2.193x                  (1234yf)-0.596x (1234ze(E))-0.768x (CO2)+29.897x                  (R152a)+64.400x (R161)+118.965x (propane)+94.943x                  (propylene)=less than 6 (II-2), 1.293x                  (HFO-1123)+1.029x (R32)+0.369x (1234yf)+0.354x                  (1234ze(E))+3.807x (CO2)+0.229x (R152a)+0.406x                  (R161)+0.568x (propane)+0.719x (propylene)=less                  than 1.65 (III-1), 1.214x (HFO-1123)+1.133x                  (R32)+0.402x (1234yf)+0.346x (1234ze(E))+3.359x                  (CO2)+0.323x (R152a)+0.548x (R161)+0.588x                  (propane)+0.725x (propylene)=more than 1 (IV-1),                  and 0.3x (HFO-1123)+675x (R32)+4x (1234yf)+6x                  (1234ze(E))+1x (CO2)+124x (R152a)+12x (R161)+3.3x                  (propane)+1.8x (propylene)=less than 120 (V-1). Preferred Properties: The working medium                  has temperature glide of 6 degrees C or less, and                  a relative coefficient of performance of more                  than 0.9, preferably more than 0.95 or                  more.

PT P
PN CN114705666-A
TI Device used for detecting carbon dioxide            concentration based on electronic resonance enhanced            coherent Raman spectroscopy, comprises near-infrared            femtosecond laser, quarter-wave plate, focusing lens,            air cavity, collimating lens, filter sheet, collecting            lens, and grating spectrometer.
AU CHENG Y
   WU Y
   CHEN Y
   LU X
   YU S
   FU B
   ZHANG F
   XIE H
   YAO J
   ZHANG N
AE CAS SHANGHAI INST OPTICS & FINE MECHANIC (CAOF-C)
GA 202290862Q
AB    NOVELTY - A detection device comprises a near-infrared                femtosecond laser, a quarter-wave plate, a focusing                lens, an air cavity, a collimating lens, a filter                sheet, a collecting lens, and a grating                spectrometer. The air cavity is filled with 1                atmospheric pressure standard air (80% nitrogen,                20% oxygen) and preset concentration of carbon                dioxide gas. The laser outputs a linearly polarized                femtosecond laser, which is transformed into a                circularly polarized femtosecond laser through the                quarter-wave plate, and is incident into an air                cavity through the focusing lens. The circularly                polarized femtosecond laser ionizes carbon dioxide                molecules in the air cavity to establish                vibrational coherence of carbon dioxide ion.                Nitrogen molecules are excited to generate an air                laser, which is used as probe light to resonate                with coherently excited electronic state transition                of carbon dioxide ion to induce vibrational Raman                scattering with enhanced electronic                resonance.
   USE - The device is useful for detecting carbon                dioxide concentration based on electronic resonance                enhanced coherent Raman spectroscopy (claimed), and                for rapid recognization of carbon dioxide gas                isotopes.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has high sensitivity, is simple and                suitable for remote characteristics, and not only                utilizes the advantages of long-distance                transmission of femtosecond laser filament                formation, but also fully utilizes the advantages                of nitrogen laser and carbon dioxide ion electronic                transition resonance induced by femtosecond laser                filament formation. Because the spectrum width of                nitrogen laser is narrow, it can only be achieved                electron resonance, but cannot be achieved                resonance, so it also provides an effective unit                for quickly recognizing carbon dioxide isotope gas.                The filter sheet used for obtaining air laser                signal or a narrow-band filter sheet with high                transmission for the electronic resonance enhanced                Raman scattering signal wave band has high                transmission to the air laser.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A detection device comprises a near-infrared                femtosecond laser, a quarter-wave plate, a focusing                lens, an air cavity, a collimating lens, a filter                sheet, a collecting lens, and a grating                spectrometer. The air cavity is filled with 1                atmospheric pressure standard air (80% nitrogen,                20% oxygen) and preset concentration of carbon                dioxide gas. The laser outputs a linearly polarized                femtosecond laser, which is transformed into a                circularly polarized femtosecond laser through the                quarter-wave plate, and is incident into an air                cavity through the focusing lens. The circularly                polarized femtosecond laser ionizes carbon dioxide                molecules in the air cavity to establish                vibrational coherence of carbon dioxide ion.                Nitrogen molecules are excited to generate an air                laser with a wavelength of 337.1 nm. The air laser                is used as probe light to resonate with coherently                excited electronic state transition of carbon                dioxide ion to induce vibrational Raman scattering                with enhanced electronic resonance. The light                emitted from the air cavity is focused on the                grating spectrometer after passing through the                collimating lens, the filter sheet and the                collecting lens.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for detection                of carbon dioxide concentration using the detection                device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The preset                  concentration of carbon dioxide gas is 0-6.25% of                  the total gas volume of the carbon dioxide                  gas.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); V08 (Lasers and Masers)
MC E11-Q03C; E31-N05C; J04-C04A; J04-C02; S03-E04D1; S03-E04A; V08-A01A1; V08-A04B; V08-A02; V08-A01D; V08-A01A2

PT P
PN CN111591957-A; CN111591957-B
TI Coal bed methane combined cycle power generation            and carbon dioxide capture system comprises coal-bed            methane hydrogen production unit including partial            oxidation reactor and oxygen permeable membrane reactor            and decarbonization unit.
AU CHI J
   HAO X
   SUN L
   MAO L
   SHA Y
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2020857839
AB    NOVELTY - Coal bed methane combined cycle power                generation and carbon dioxide capture system                comprises coal-bed methane hydrogen production                unit, decarbonization unit and combined cycle power                generation unit. The coal-bed methane hydrogen                production unit includes a partial oxidation                reactor (1) and an oxygen permeable membrane                reactor (2). The oxygen permeable membrane reactor                includes an oxidation zone and a hydrogen                production zone. The feeding port of the oxidation                zone is connected to the feeding port of the                partial oxidation reactor and middle-low                concentration coal bed methane (101) and super                heated steam can enter coal bed methane hydrogen                production unit to obtain fuel gas and reformed                tail gas through reaction and fuel gas is fed into                the combined cycle power generation unit. The                converted tail gas enters the decarbonization unit                to separate carbon dioxide.
   USE - Used as coal bed methane combined cycle power                generation and carbon dioxide capture system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes zero carbon dioxide                emission while realizing high coal bed methane                utilization rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for coal                bed methane combined cycle power generation and                carbon dioxide capture method, comprising (i)                taking the coal bed methane combined cycle power                generation and carbon dioxide capture system,                entering medium and low concentration coal bed                methane into the partial oxidation reactor for                partial oxidation reaction to form reducing                atmosphere mixed gas; (ii) entering the reducing                atmosphere mixed gas into the oxidation zone of the                oxygen permeable membrane reactor to perform                oxidation reaction, decomposing the super heated                steam for hydrogen production into hydrogen and                oxygen in the hydrogen production zone, and coming                the decomposition heat from the reaction heat in                the oxidation zone, transporting the oxygen                produced in the hydrogen production zone into the                oxidation zone through the oxygen permeable                membrane, generating the tail gas in the oxidation                zone by the oxidation reaction and recovering its                heat through the tail gas waste heat recovery unit,                cooling the dehydration unit, separating the                condensed water, entering the obtained dehydrated                tail gas into the carbon dioxide separation unit                for carbon dioxide separation, entering the                separated gaseous carbon dioxide into the carbon                dioxide compression unit for compression to produce                liquid carbon dioxide, where the gas at the                discharge port of the hydrogen production zone and                is a high-temperature hydrogen-rich fuel gas, and                the high-temperature hydrogen-rich fuel gas                provides fuel for the power output unit, recovering                the heat of the tail gas of the power output unit                by the waste heat boiler and generating the waste                heat boiler power steam to drive the steam turbine                to perform work, in the step (i), the main                components of the reducing atmosphere mixed gas                includes carbon monoxide, hydrogen, nitrogen,                carbon dioxide and unreacted methane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation                of the overall structure of coal bed methane                combined cycle power generation and carbon dioxide                capture system.
   Partial oxidation reactor (1)
   Oxygen permeable membrane reactor (2)
   Middle-low concentration coal bed methane                (101)

MC E11-Q02C; E31-A02C; E31-N05C; H06-A02; H06-P; J01-E02; J01-E03; Q51-D07A; Q51-H01; Q51-J02F; X11-A01C; X11-A08; X11-C02; X11-C03; X11-C08; X11-U01A

PT P
PN WO2019175731-A1; CA3102596-A1; US2020407289-A1; US11447434-B2
TI Converting alkanes to alkenes for making olefin            derivatives by converting a portion of alkanes to            alkenes in oxidative dehydrogenation reactor and            reacting resulting stream in presence of catalyst            comprising group 11 metal and promoter.
AU SIMANZHENKOV V
   GOODARZNIA S
   SERHAL K
   GAO X
   KIM Y
   SERHAL K E
AE NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM CORP (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
GA 201980798C
AB    NOVELTY - Alkanes are converted to alkenes by (a)                providing 1st stream comprising alkanes and oxygen                to oxidative dehydrogenation reactor; (b)                converting at least a portion of alkanes to alkenes                in the oxidative dehydrogenation reactor to provide                2nd stream exiting the oxidative dehydrogenation                reactor comprising alkanes, alkenes, and oxygen,                carbon monoxide, and/or carbon dioxide; and (c)                providing 2nd stream to 2nd reactor containing                catalyst comprising group 11 metal and optionally,                a promoter comprising cerium(IV) oxide (CeO2)                and/or zirconium(IV) oxide (ZrO2) supported on                silica (SiO2) to convert at least a portion of                carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide.
   USE - Conversion of alkanes to alkenes, such as                ethylene used for making olefin derivatives                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The degree to which carbon monoxide is                produced during the oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH)                process can be mitigated by converting it to carbon                dioxide, which can then act as an oxidizing agent.                The process can be manipulated so as to control the                output of carbon dioxide from the process to a                desired level. Using the methods, a user may choose                to operate in carbon dioxide neutral conditions                such that surplus carbon dioxide need not be flared                or released into the atmosphere. The method can                reduce the amount of carbon dioxide produced in the                ODH process in combination with other processes,                such as thermal cracking. In addition, oxidation of                carbon dioxide is endothermic and by increasing the                degree to which carbon dioxide acts as an oxidizing                agent, heat produced from ODH of ethane is                partially offset by oxidation of carbon dioxide,                reducing the degree to which heat must be removed                from the reactor. When acting as an oxidizing                agent, carbon dioxide can produce carbon monoxide,                which can be captured and used as an intermediate                in production of other chemical products, such as                methanol or formic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) chemical complex for oxidative                dehydrogenation of lower alkanes comprising, in                cooperative arrangement, oxidative dehydrogenation                reactor comprising oxidative dehydrogenation                catalyst and designed to accept, optionally in the                presence of inert diluent, oxygen containing gas                and lower alkane containing gas, and to produce                product stream comprising the corresponding alkene                and possibly, unreacted lower alkane, oxygen, inert                diluent, carbon oxides, including carbon dioxide                and carbon monoxide, oxygenates, including acetic                acid, acrylic acid, maleic acid, and maleic                anhydride, and water, quench tower for quenching                the product stream and for removing water and                soluble oxygenates from product stream, carbon                monoxide oxidation reactor for oxidizing carbon                monoxide to carbon dioxide, amine wash for removing                carbon dioxide from product stream, dryer for                removal of water from product stream, distillation                tower for removing C2/C2+ hydrocarbons from product                stream to produce overhead stream enriched with C1                hydrocarbons, optionally, a unit for introducing a                combustible fuel into overhead stream, and oxygen                separation module comprising oxygen transport                membrane housed inside a sealed vessel and having a                retentate side and a permeate side, 1st inlet for                introducing overhead stream and/or combustible fuel                into retentate side, 2nd inlet for introducing                overhead stream and/or combustible fuel into                permeate side, air inlet for introducing air into                retentate side, exhaust for discharge of oxygen                depleted air and combustion products from retentate                side, and outlet for removing oxygen enriched gas                and combustion products from permeate side;                and
   (2) alkene produced using the chemical                complex.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Oxidative                  dehydrogenation reactor contains oxidative                  dehydrogenation catalyst comprising greater than                  or equal to 1 mixed metal oxides selected from:                  (i) catalysts of formula MoaVbTecNbdPdeOf; (ii)                  catalysts formula NigAhTiDjOf; (iii) catalysts of                  formula MoaEkGlO; (iv) catalyst of formula                  VmMonNboTepMeqOf; (v) catalysts of formula                  MoaVrXsY1tZuMvOf; (vi) mixed metal oxide of                  formula Mo6.5-7V3Od; or (vii) mixed metal oxide                  of formula Mo6.25-7.25V3Od. Group 11 metal is                  selected from copper, silver, and/or gold,                  preferably silver. Catalyst in the 2nd reactor                  comprises Ag/SiO2, AgCeO2/SiO2, AgZrO2/SiO2,                  Cu/SiO2, CuCeO2/SiO2, CuZrO2/SiO2, and/or                  CuCO3O4/SiO2. The stream entering the carbon                  monoxide oxidation reactor comprises 100 ppm to 5                  wt.% oxygen. The stream exiting the carbon                  monoxide oxidation reactor is essentially free of                  oxygen.    TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Alkanes comprise                  ethane. Alkenes comprise ethylene. The 1st stream                  comprises inert diluents, oxygen containing gas,                  and gas containing lower alkanes. The 2nd stream                  comprises unreacted lower alkanes; lower alkenes;                  oxygen; inert diluents; carbon dioxide; carbon                  monoxide; acetic acid; and water. Alkene                  comprises ethylene and optionally alpha -olefins.                  Preferred Devices: Oxidative dehydrogenation                  reactor comprises a single fixed bed type                  reactor. Oxidative dehydrogenation reactor                  comprises a single fluidized bed type reactor                  and/or a moving bed reactor. Oxidative                  dehydrogenation reactor comprises a swing bed                  type reactor arrangement. Acetic acid scrubber is                  placed between the oxidative dehydrogenation                  reactor and the 2nd reactor. Preferred                  Conditions: Temperature in the 2nd reactor is                  40-100 degrees C. Temperature in the carbon                  monoxide oxidation reactor is 40-100 degrees                  C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Olefin derivatives are                  selected from polyethylene, polypropylene,                  ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, polyethylene                  oxide, polypropylene oxide, thermoplastic                  elastomers, thermoplastic olefins, and/or their                  blends. Ethylene and optional alpha -olefins                  produced are used to make polyethylene.                  Polyethylene is selected from homopolymers of                  ethylene, copolymers of ethylene and alpha                  -olefins, high density polyethylene (HDPE),                  medium density PE (MDPE), low density PE (LDPE),                  linear LDPE (LLDPE), and/or very low density PE                  (VLDPE). Preferred Processes: Polyethylene is                  produced using gas phase polyethylene processes,                  high pressure polyethylene processes, low                  pressure polyethylene processes, solution                  polyethylene processes, and/or slurry                  polyethylene processes in parallel and/or series                  reactor configurations.

PN JP2022076883-A
TI Method for storing grape, involves introducing carbon dioxide into package to adjust concentration of carbon dioxide in package to specific value, and storing package in order.
AU NAKAYAMA N
   NAKATA R
   HOKARI K
   ARATAKI M
   SUGAWARA A
   MATSUMOTO J M
AE MITSUI CHEM INC (MITA-C)
   NAT FEDERATION AGRIC COOP (NAFE-Non-standard)
GA 202272675R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for storing a grape involves (a) sealing grape with at least a packaging member to prepare a package, where the packaging member has a carbon dioxide permeability of 7000-60000 cm3/m2.day.atm, and oxygen permeability of 2000-20000 cm3/m2/day/atm, (b) introducing carbon dioxide into the package to adjust the carbon dioxide concentration in package to 10-40%, and (c) storing the package.
   USE - The method is useful for storing grape.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively suppresses the growth of fungi in grapes and suppresses aging of grapes and browning of spike of grapes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a package, which comprises grape and packaging member.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (c) involves maintaining carbon dioxide concentration in the package to 1-20% 5 days after the completion of the step (b), and storing package to have carbon dioxide concentration in the package to 1-15% when the environment inside the package is stable. The environmental conditions outside the package are 1-5 degrees C and humidity is 70-90% RH. In step (c), the package is stored, and carbon dioxide concentration in the package decreases with time until the environment inside the package becomes stable. The volume of the package is 1x 102 cm3 to 3x 106 cm3. The ratio of the volume of the package to the total volume of grapes in the package is 100:5-100:30. The package is a pallet, where several fruit and vegetable containers are arranged on pallet and containing grapes, packaging member and member (A), the member (A) forms the upper part of the package, the packaging member forms a side portion of the package, the member (A) and the packaging member are joined to each other by joining a part of the packaging member with another part of the packaging member, and several fruit and vegetable containers are sealed. The area of the portion where the packaging members overlap on the side portion of main packaging material is 3000 cm2 or less.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The packaging member comprises a polymer having a structural unit derived from at least one chosen from 4-methyl-1-pentene and 1-butene.

TI Integrated system for capturing carbon dioxide and            producing sodium bicarbonate comprises system that            captures carbon dioxide through dry carbonation            process, converts trona in sodium carbonate, and            generates sodium bicarbonate.
AU CHACARTEGUI RAMIREZ R
   BECERRA VILLANUEVA J A
   VALVERDE MILLAN J M
   BONAVENTURA D
   RAMIREZ R
   VILLANUEVA J A

   BECERRA V J A
   VALVERDE M J M
AE UNIV SEVILLA (USEV-C)
   UNIV SEVILLA (USEV-C)
GA 201807435P
AB    NOVELTY - An integrated system for capturing carbon                dioxide (CO2) and producing sodium bicarbonate                comprises system that captures CO2 through dry                carbonation process, converts trona                (Na2CO3.NaHCO3.2H2O) in sodium carbonate, and                generates sodium bicarbonate from generated sodium                carbonate (Na2CO3) and captured CO2.
   USE - An integrated system for capturing carbon                dioxide (CO2) and producing sodium                bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - Overall energy requirement is reduced. Energy                and economic cost of integration of system are                reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                different currents from solids and gases and                interaction between subsystems of capture of carbon                dioxide and regeneration of sodium bicarbonate.                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: System is integrated into                  output stream of fossil fuel thermal plants and                  in carbon dioxide-emitting industrial facilities.                  Dry carbonation process uses carbon dioxide                  capture subsystem. Regeneration of sorbent and                  dissociation of trona is performed at 140-230                  degrees C by renewable energies and solar thermal                  technology of medium temperature or biomass.                  Preferred Parameters: Process has capture                  efficiency of greater than or equal to 90% based                  on fossil fuel by supporting renewable energies.                  Temperature of stream in sodium carbonate                  regenerator and calcinator of trona is 150-220                  degrees C and in both carbonation towers is 60                  degrees C.

PN CN114810005-A
TI Coal seam fracturing method based on synergistic            effect of horizontal slits in coal roadway and carbon            dioxide blasting involves blasting coal layer, placing            carbon dioxide induced cracking device in blasting            drilling hole, and injecting liquid carbon dioxide into            cracking device.
AU LAN H
   LI X
   LI B
   LIU D
   ZHOU B
   HU J
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2022A2117S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves avoiding a coal seam area                with complex geology according to the geological                survey of coal mines, and the coal roadway                excavation working face with high gas and low                permeability is selected. The coal seam pressure                relief moving space is constructed. The horizontal                slitting technology is used near the coal seam                floor to cut the coal seam, and the formed                horizontal slit space is used as the coal seam                pressure relief moving space. The parallel drilling                method is adopted, and the drilling device is used                to continuously construct multiple blasting holes                in the coal seam with low permeability and                difficult to extract along the horizontal                direction, and the blasting holes are located in                the upper portion of the coal seam. A carbon                dioxide cracker is placed in the blasting hole, and                liquid carbon dioxide is injected into the carbon                dioxide cracker. The carbon dioxide cracker is                detonated after the opening of the blasting hole is                sealed. The gas is extracted.
   USE - Coal seam fracturing method based on                synergistic effect of horizontal slits in coal                roadway and carbon dioxide blasting used in field                of coal seam gas drainage prevention and                control.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving gas extraction                process, increasing coal body crack, increasing                extraction radius so as to accelerate underground                coal roadway heading speed, reducing coal mine                production cost and reducing safety accident.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of                drilling and distributing holes and cracks in coal                seam fracturing method. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)

PT P
PN CN113957270-A
TI Smelting antimony sulfide ore for absorbing carbon dioxide and avoiding sulfur dioxide emission, involves mixing antimony sulfide material, sulfur fixing agent and molten salt in crucible, and performing carbon fixing reduction smelting.
AU ZHU Q
   NAN T
   ZHOU Y
   DING R
   YANG J
AE JIANGXI ENVIRONMENTAL ENG VOCATIONAL COL (JIAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 2022192145
AB    NOVELTY - Smelting antimony sulfide ore involves mixing antimony-sulfide material, sulfur fixing agent and molten salt in a crucible, carbon-fixing and sulfur-fixing reduction smelting reactions are carried out under certain conditions during the smelting process, carbon dioxide is blown into molten salt to stir molten salt. The product in the crucible is divided into three layers after the smelting is completed, where bottom layer is metal antimony, middle layer is solid sulfur product MeS, and upper layer is inert molten salt.
   USE - Method for smelting antimony sulfide ore for absorbing carbon dioxide and avoiding sulfur dioxide emission at same time in metallurgy, chemical industry and other important industrial fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to effectively capture and fix carbon dioxide, and introduced carbon dioxide effectively stirs the molten pool, promotes reaction, and avoids the pollution of low concentration sulfur dioxide in traditional antimony sulfide ore smelting. The high-grade refined antimony is refined in one step, and direct yield of antimony is improved. The method enables to provide technical support for the emission reduction of carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide in the smelting of non-ferrous metal sulfide ore in the metallurgical field. The molten salt absorbs and converts carbon dioxide into carbonate under a certain reaction condition, so as to realize the capture of carbon dioxide. The method solves the problem of high energy consumption, complex device and so on in the current pyrotechnic antimony smelting process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reaction conditions for carbon fixation and sulfur fixation reduction smelting are temperature of 800-950degrees Celsius, time of 1-5 hours, dosage of sulfur-fixing metal oxide MeO is 1-1.5 times of theoretical amount required for converting sulfur in antimony sulfide ore into MeS, and metal oxide used as sulfur-fixing agent is selected from zinc oxide or ferric oxide. The inert fused salt is mixed fused salt of sodium carbonate and sodium sulfide, where mass content of sodium sulfide in mixed fused salt is 10-50 wt.%, mass consumption of molten salt is 2-4 times of that of antimony sulfide ore, and mass usage of powdered coal or coke powder reducing agent is 4-10 wt.% of antimony sulfide ore. The carbon dioxide is blown in from the bottom or top or side of molten pool. The carbon dioxide gas is preheated before blowing, and the preheating temperature is 600-1000degrees Celsius. The pressure of the blown carbon dioxide gas is 0.1-0.5 megapascal (Mpa).

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)

PN CN112889554-A
TI Seedling culture box used for automatically adjusting carbon dioxide concentration wall has one-way air suction pipe, driving mechanism, magnetic sliding plug, cavity, gas supply mechanism, illuminating lamp, and strip-shaped cavity.
AU ZHAO Y
AE HUZHOU RUIXUN ELECTROMECHANICAL EQUIP CO (HUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202167045D
AB    NOVELTY - A seedling culture box has a sliding plug cavity (12) whose inner wall is fixedly connected with a one-way air suction pipe (21). A box body (1) is provided with a driving mechanism for driving a magnetic sliding plug (13) to slide back and forth in the sliding plug cavity. A side wall of the box body far away from a strip cavity is provided with a cavity (4). The cavity is provided with a gas supply mechanism for adding carbon dioxide to the box body. The culture box further comprises illuminating lamp, strip-shaped cavity, magnetic plate, first spring, strip-shaped cavity, one-way air inlet pipe, and one-way air suction pipe.
   USE - The seedling culture box is used for automatically adjusting carbon dioxide concentration in modern agriculture.
   ADVANTAGE - The absorption amount of the carbon dioxide absorption liquid to the carbon dioxide is reduced when the concentration of carbon dioxide in the box body is low, and the sliding displacement of the magnetic plate is small, so that the inductor is supplied with power to the electromagnet, so as to push the permanent magnet plate to move downwards to extrude the hydrochloric acid solution in the liquid storage cavity to the reaction cavity. The hydrochloric acid solution and calcium carbonate particles in the reaction cavity react to generate carbon dioxide gas, so as to automatically add carbon dioxide in the box body.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the seedling culture box.

PT P
PN KR2021058578-A; KR2359490-B1
TI Carbon dioxide reduction catalyst for producing hydrocarbon compounds, comprises metal oxide having spinel structure with skeleton made of divalent metal and trivalent metal.
AU NAKYUNGSU
   KIM Y
AE UNIV CHONNAM NAT IND FOUND (UCHO-C)
GA 2021589421
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide reduction catalyst comprises a metal oxide having a spinel structure with a skeleton made of a divalent metal and a trivalent metal and having mesopores in the range of 2-50 nm.
   USE - The carbon dioxide reduction catalyst is useful for producing hydrocarbon compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: is simple and economical to manufacture; increases the conversion rate in the hydrogenation reaction of carbon dioxide and produces hydrocarbon compound with high industrial utility value of at least two carbons with high selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing the carbon dioxide reduction catalyst, comprising dissolving and dispersing each of a divalent metal precursor and a trivalent metal precursor in a solvent, adding the divalent metal precursor dispersion into the trivalent metal precursor dispersion, adjusting the pH by adding a basic solution to the mixed dispersion, removing the solvent contained in the mixed dispersion and obtaining a metal oxide having a spinel structure by calcining the solid product remaining after the solvent is removed;
   (2) producing hydrocarbon compounds, comprising pre-treating the carbon dioxide reduction catalyst in a continuous flow fixed bed reactor, injecting a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen into the reactor to control to maintain a constant pressure, adjusting the reactor to a constant temperature and a constant pressure and inducing hydrogenation reaction of carbon dioxide by flowing the mixed gas at a constant flow rate to the reactor adjusted to the constant pressure and constant temperature; and
   (3) hydrocarbon compounds.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide of the spinel structure has BET specific surface area of 50-200 m2/g and the volume of the mesopores is 0.13-0.47 ml/g. The divalent metal includes copper, zinc, cobalt, iron, nickel, manganese and/or magnesium. The trivalent metal includes aluminum, scandium, chromium, iron and/or cobalt. The metal oxide of the spinel structure includes copper-aluminum spinel, zinc-aluminum spinel, cobalt-aluminum spinel and/or magnesium-aluminum spinel. At least one of a monovalent metal and a divalent metal is supported on the mesopores formed in the metal oxide of the spinel structure, or an accelerating layer is formed by coating the surface of the metal oxide of the spinel structure. The accelerating layer includes 0.5-10 wt.% monovalent metal and 9-50 wt.% divalent metal. The volume ratio of carbon dioxide and hydrogen contained in the mixed gas is 1:2-1:5. The mixed gas further comprises an inert gas in an amount less than the content of carbon dioxide. The hydrocarbon compound is 1-20C compound. Preferred Method: The production of the carbon dioxide reduction catalyst comprises: adjusting the pH of mixture to 9-10; removing the solvent by filtering the solvent from the mixed dispersion and drying the filtered and remaining solid product; calcining the solid product at 700-900 degrees C for 2-6 hours; further forming an accelerating layer on the metal oxide of the spinel structure by dissolving and dispersing the metal oxide having the spinel structure and at least one metal precursor among 0.5-10 wt.% monovalent and 9-50 wt.% divalent metal precursors forming the facilitation layer in a solvent, removing the solvent contained in the dispersion and calcining the solid product at 500-600 degrees C for 2-6 hours. The constant pressure and constant temperature during the preparation of hydrocarbon compound is greater than or equal to 20 bar and 250-500 degrees C, respectively.
   EXAMPLE - 80 g isopropyl alcohol and 12.255 g aluminum isopropoxide (0.06 mol ) were placed in a 250 ml round bottom two-necked flask and refluxed by stirring at 110 degrees C for 2 hours to obtain a trivalent metal precursor dispersion as a transparent solution. 10 ml distilled water and 6.977 g copper nitrate (0.03 mol) and were dissolved in a polypropylene bottle to obtain a divalent metal precursor dispersion. Divalent metal precursor dispersion was slowly added to the trivalent metal precursor dispersion using a syringe. The mixture was stirred vigorously (at 1000 rotations/minute) to prevent solidification and refluxed for 3 hours. The refluxed mixed dispersion was cooled to room temperature and titrated (13 ml) with aqueous ammonia until pH is 9.5, then stirred for 3 hours. The resultant solution was filtered, and the solid product was dried at 100 degrees C for 12 hours. The dried solid product was calcined at 800 degrees C for 4 hours to obtain carbon dioxide reduction catalyst (copper-aluminum spinel).
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Preparation of p-n type in-situ symbiotic            heterojunction materials by pyrolytic nickel-manganese            hydrotalcite topological transformation used for            photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide includes            pyrolyzing hydrotalcite material.
AU LU J
   ZHAO X
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2020A42283
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of p-n type in-situ symbiotic                heterojunction materials by pyrolytic                nickel-manganese hydrotalcite topological                transformation comprises preparing mixed solution                of soluble divalent nickel salt, soluble manganese                salt and hydrogen peroxide solution, preparing                mixed alkali solution of 0.5-0.6 mol/l sodium                carbonate and sodium hydroxide, placing in constant                temperature water bath at 60-80 degrees C, adding                mixed solution and mixed alkali solution, adjusting                pH of mixed solution in reaction bottle at 8-9,                sending slurry to high-pressure reactor,                crystallizing at 100-120 degrees C for 20-30 hours,                cooling to obtain nickel-manganese hydrotalcite,                washing, centrifuging, drying, grinding, placing                nickel-manganese hydrotalcite material in magnetic                crucible, pyrolyzing at 300-800 degrees C for 4-8                hours in muffle furnace and performing topological                transformation.
   USE - The preparation of p-n type in-situ symbiotic                heterojunction materials by pyrolytic                nickel-manganese hydrotalcite topological                transformation is used as catalyst for                photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material has good carbon dioxide reduction                performance and cycle stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                comprising weighing 20 mg catalyst, injecting 1 mL                triethanolamine and 19 mL water, using water as                reducing substance and triethanolamine as                cavitation sacrificial agent, performing ultrasonic                dispersion for 15 minutes, introducing high purity                carbon dioxide gas for 15 minutes to reach carbon                dioxide in high-purity airtight carbon dioxide                environment and performing catalytic carbon dioxide                reduction experiment that simulates sunlight                irradiation.

TI Device used for reducing carbon dioxide, comprises            porous electrode containing porous carbon comprising at            least one metallic-nonmetallic element bond, and at            least one of electrolyte solution and ion-carrying            film.

The electrode (11) is a                porous electrode containing porous carbon                comprising at least one metallic-nonmetallic                element bond (I).

TI Assembly for distributing flow of carbon dioxide, has elongated duct having openings that are formed on lower surface of duct, where ducts extend adjacent to each other from filter housing such that assembly is positioned upon rack.
AU BOGNER M L
   CUNNINGHAM J A
AE VERTICAL AIR SOLUTIONS LLC (VERT-Non-standard)
GA 2019197133
AB    NOVELTY - The assembly has first elongated duct fluidly coupled to first outlet portion of a filter housing (250) and having first set of openings defined on a lower surface of the first duct. Second elongated duct is fluidly coupled to second outlet portion of the filter housing adjacent to the first outlet portion, and has second set of openings formed on lower surface of the second duct, where the first and second elongated ducts extend adjacent to each other from the filter housing such that the assembly is positioned upon a rack.
   USE - Assembly for distributing flow of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The assembly includes braces for supporting the rack to be configured to facilitate placement of the ducts with minimal interference to shelf platforms, so that access to the shelves remains unhindered. The assembly facilitates less space to be used and lower energy cost, and changes disposable filters at lower cost than can filters. The assembly eliminates warm pockets of air by providing concentrated air movement, carbon dioxide dispersion and filtration, and can maintain predetermined carbon dioxide level by decreasing carbon dioxide flow when the detected carbon dioxide level is too high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for distributing flow of carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a carbon dioxide distribution system on a rack system that includes shelves holding plants.

PT P
PN US2018362426-A1; US10858302-B2
TI Converting carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas into methanol in reaction vessel, comprises e.g. multimetallic nanoparticulate catalyst supported on metal oxide placed, feeding carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas into vessel and heating vessel.
AU CHEN C
   KOTYK J K
   SHEEHAN S W
AE CATALYTIC INNOVATIONS INC (CATA-Non-standard)
   CHEN C (CHEN-Individual)
   KOTYK J K (KOTY-Individual)
   SHEEHAN S W (SHEE-Individual)
GA 2018A1704N
AB    NOVELTY - Converting (M1) carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas into methanol in a reaction vessel, comprises: (a) a multimetallic nanoparticulate catalyst supported on a metal oxide placed in the reaction vessel; (b) feeding carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas into the reaction vessel under pressure greater than 300 psi but less than 1500 psi ; (c) heating the reaction vessel to 150-350 degrees C; and (d) possessing a selectivity for methanol over other products greater than 90%.
   USE - The method is useful for converting carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas into methanol in a reaction vessel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides methanol with high selectivity, while optimizing rate and conversion efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) converting (M2) carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas into methanol in a reaction vessel, comprising a trimetallic nanoparticulate catalyst comprised of copper, zinc, and a third transition metal supported on a metal oxide and placed in the reaction vessel, and the steps (b)-(d) as above se;
   (2) purifying (M3) a gaseous stream of greater than 90% carbon monoxide with less than 10% hydrogen gas impurities by hydrogenation of carbon monoxide to a liquid hydrocarbon;
   (3) purifying (M4) a gaseous stream of greater than 90% carbon dioxide with less than 10% hydrogen gas impurities by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to a liquid hydrocarbon; and
   (4) purifying (M5) a gaseous stream of greater than 90% hydrogen gas with less than 10% carbon monoxide impurities by hydrogenation of carbon monoxide to a liquid hydrocarbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The methods (M1) and (M2) take place in a fixed bed reactor or a fluidized bed reactor. Preferred Components: In the method (M1): the catalyst is a bimetallic nanoparticle comprised of zinc or palladium and copper; the ratio of zinc or palladium to copper in the catalyst is 2:1; the selectivity of the catalyst is greater than 99%; the partial pressure of carbon dioxide is 80-500 psi ; the partial pressure of hydrogen is 250-1000 psi ; the catalyst contains stoichiometric amounts or is doped with platinum, ruthenium, rhodium, iridium, gold, nickel, cobalt and/or iron, lithium, sodium, zinc, potassium, selenium, calcium, magnesium and/or manganese, or strontium, barium, silver, samarium, lanthanum, titanium, vanadium, zirconium, niobium, molybdenum, rhenium, tin and/or cerium; and the hydrogen gas supplied to the reactor is made by water electrolysis. In the method (M2), the selectivity of the catalyst is greater than 95%. In the method (M4), the gaseous stream is comprised of greater than 95% carbon dioxide with less than 5% hydrogen gas impurities. In the method (M5), the gaseous stream is comprised of greater than 95%, preferably 99% hydrogen gas with less than 5%, preferably less than 1% carbon monoxide impurities.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid hydrocarbon is methanol.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN WO2022174195-A1; US2022259042-A1
TI Production of hydrogen involves producing            unshifted synthesis gas using feed natural gas, feed            steam and fuel, producing shifted syngas using            unshifted syngas, removing carbon dioxide to produce            carbon dioxide depleted syngas, and producing hydrogen            product.
AU RAVIKUMAR S
   SINGHAL S
   RAVIKUMAR R
AE FLUOR TECHNOLOGIES CORP (FLUO-C)
   FLUOR TECHNOLOGIES CORP (FLUO-C)
GA 2022A6638G
AB    NOVELTY - Production of hydrogen from natural gas                involves introducing a feed natural gas, a feed                steam, and a fuel to a steam methane reformer (110)                to produce unshifted synthesis gas (syngas),                introducing the unshifted syngas to a water gas                shift unit (120) to produce a shifted syngas,                removing carbon dioxide from the shifted syngas to                produce a carbon dioxide depleted syngas and a                carbon dioxide product, and introducing the carbon                dioxide depleted syngas to a pressure swing                absorption unit (140) to produce a hydrogen product                and an off-gas comprising carbon monoxide, carbon                dioxide, unreacted methane, and at least 25 mol%                hydrogen based on a total moles of components in                the off-gas. The fuel that is introduced to the                steam methane reformer comprises the off-gas.
   USE - Production of hydrogen from natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides hydrogen with high                purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for hydrogen                production system (100).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view                illustrating process and system for producing                hydrogen from natural gas.
   Hydrogen production system (100)
   Steam methane reformer (110)
   Water gas shift unit (120)
   Shifted syngas cooling unit (130)
   Absorption unit (140)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The off-gas comprises                  less than 60 mol% carbon dioxide based on a total                  moles of components in the off-gas. The amount of                  carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide product is                  more than 60 mol% of a sum amount that is the                  amount of carbon dioxide that is introduced into                  the process plus the amount of carbon dioxide                  that is generated in the process. Amount of                  carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide product plus                  the amount of carbon dioxide in the residual                  carbon dioxide is 60-95 mol% of the amount of                  carbon dioxide that is introduced into the                  process plus the amount of carbon dioxide that is                  generated in the process. Preferred Components:                  The steam methane reformer produces a flue                  gas.

PT P
PN CN108339383-A; CN108339383-B
TI Carbon dioxide-carbon and oxygen separation method comprises passing photolysis water into a dedicated device sequentially, then adding carbon dioxide to obtain carbon monoxide and elemental carbon.
AU XU M
   YU Q
   GAO R
   ZHAO J
   XIA J
   ZHANG M
   SONG Y
   MA X
   DONG S
   YU M
AE XU M (XUMM-Individual)
GA 201861932E
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-carbon and oxygen separation method comprises passing photolysis water into a dedicated device sequentially, then adding carbon dioxide to obtain carbon monoxide and elemental carbon.
   USE - The method is useful for separating carbon dioxide-carbon and oxygen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has achieved outstanding substantive features and significant progress in resource utilization; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for special device for carbon dioxide carbon and oxygen separation method is a cylindrical tank made of a metal material, there is a carbon dioxide inlet below it, an air outlet is arranged above and an induced draft fan is installed, there are at least two carbon-oxygen separation units inside the tank, each of the carbon-oxygen separation units is internally provided with a blue light tube and a red light tube, mesh carrier, the mesh carrier carries a photosynthetic composite; a sprinkler is arranged above the mesh carrier, and the sprinkler is connected to the spray pipe outside the tank, the bottom of the tank has a sink, install a water level gauge and a water temperature gauge outside the tank, a pump that lifts the electrolyte to the sprinkler is mounted on the outside of the tank.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The carbon-oxygen separation unit is internally equipped with a blue light tube, red light tube, mesh carrier, the mesh carrier carries a photosynthetic complex; and a carbon-oxygen separation chamber of the shower head is mounted on the upper end of the carrier.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photolyzing water is photo-cleared into hydrogen protons, electrons and oxygen by a series of electron transporters under the action of red, blue and photosynthetic complexes. Preferred Method: The method comprises treating carbon dioxide with hydrogen protons and electrons generated by photolysis water in a dedicated device at 5-50 degrees C under atmospheric pressure with a concentration of not greater than 150000 mg/m3 to form carbon monoxide and water. The formation of elemental carbon is based on the formation of carbon monoxide comprises reacting carbon monoxide with hydrogen protons and electrons produced by photolysis water, and eventually produce elemental carbon, and the entire reaction time is not less than 15 seconds. The method comprises condensing pigment on the surface of the photosynthetic composite absorbs the red and blue light emitted by the lamp and converts it into an excited state, rapid transferring of membrane protein complexes and pigments to the reaction center, exciting the pigment in the reaction center by light to cause charge separation, carrying out photochemical reaction, the pigment of the reaction center, the membrane protein complex absorbs the sprayed aqueous solution and the potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, manganese ions in the solution, after a series of electron transfer bodies, cracking the water into oxygen, hydrogen protons and electrons; releasing the generated oxygen into the container space, receiving the generated free electrons by the carbon-containing fibers in the carrier, receiving the generated protons of hydrogen by the gold-containing fibers in the support, then carbon dioxide gas passes through the mesh carrier, hydrogen protons accumulating on the surface of gold-containing fibers combine with a negatively charged oxygen of carbon dioxide molecules to form a water molecule, the carbon element of carbon dioxide receives two electrons from the surface of the carbon fiber to form carbon monoxide, the generated carbon monoxide continues to pass through the mesh carrier, the hydrogen protons on the surface of the gold-containing fiber and the oxygen of carbon monoxide combine to form a water molecule, and the carbon element of carbon monoxide receives two electrons on the surface of the carbon-containing fiber and is converted into elemental carbon. Preferred Components: The mesh carrier is chemical fiber, carbon fiber, porous network composite composed of gold fibers; the mesh carrier is breathable, light transmission, absorption of electrolyte and function of carrying photosynthetic complex, its carbon-containing fibers absorb and carry free electrons, and the gold-containing fibers absorb the hydrogen-bearing protons. The photosynthetic complex on the reticulated carrier is composed of soluble protein, lipids, pigments and potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, zinc, copper, manganese, phosphorus organic and inorganic materials, processed and synthesized by 20-50 nm long, 15-40 nm wide, and long strip film material with a thickness of 2-3 nm. The diameter and height of the dedicated device are set by the volume of carbon dioxide separated per hour, the temperature is 5-50 degrees C, the concentration is not greater than 150,000 mg/m3, and the gas is in the device with a residence time of not less than 15 seconds.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
