PN CN114893987-A; CN217541461-U
TI Method for smelting fused magnesia in negative            pressure state, involves stop returning carbon dioxide            when pressure in closed tank is too high and exceeds            upper limit of negative pressure standard value.
AU DOU Y
   SHANG D
   JIN L
AE ANSHAN YINGFENG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (ANSH-Non-standard)
GA 2022A7870N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves carrying out smelting                production of fused magnesia in a closed tank (2)                using a fused magnesia smelting furnace (11). A                smoke dust containing carbon dioxide produced in                the closed tank is discharged to a dust removal                device (4) through negative pressure for dust                removal, and a clean carbon dioxide after dust                removal is collected. The negative pressure in the                closed tank is controlled to be in preset value,                the carbon dioxide is returned to the closed tank                to maintain a negative pressure environment in the                closed tank when pressure in the closed tank is too                low and exceeds a lower limit of a negative                pressure standard value, and the carbon dioxide is                stopped returning when the pressure in the closed                tank is too high and exceeds an upper limit of the                negative pressure standard value.
   USE - Method for smelting fused magnesia in negative                pressure state for industrial production of                magnesia carbon brick in steel industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not need a furnace platform                operator, adopts the spiral feeder to automatically                feed materials, has no secondary dust during the                feeding process, and is opened and closed according                to rated values of the smelting set current and                power of the smelting furnace. 1-2 workers are                saved in each smelting furnace, and the potential                safety hazard of operators is greatly reduced. The                negative pressure closed tank can effectively                inhibit heat loss of the smelting furnace housing,                and the smoke and dust in the closed tank are                discharged, which is green clean production, and                reaches environmental protection emission standard.                The negative pressure closed tank can reduce the                air content of smelting smoke dust, increase the                concentration of carbon dioxide and improve the                recovery efficiency of the carbon dioxide capture                facility. The carbon dioxide capture facility can                effectively recover carbon dioxide, and the                recovered carbon dioxide can create benefits and                meet the environmental protection requirement of                carbon dioxide emission reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device                for smelting fused magnesia in negative pressure                state.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the                structure of the device for smelting fused magnesia                in negative pressure state.
   2Closed tank
   4Dust removal device
   11Fused magnesia smelting furnace

   14Lifting device

PN CN114805132-A
TI Preparing methyl carbamate in medicine and organic            synthesis applications, involves adding methanol and            urea to high-pressure reaction kettle with magnetic            stirrer, controlling temperature system, adding            catalyst fluorapatite, reducing reaction system,            filtering catalyst and evaporating methanol.
AU ZHANG L
   MA H
   XIE Y
   DU W
   WANG W
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN TECHNOLOGY (UYGL-C)
GA 2022A2237K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing methyl carbamate,                involves (a) adding methanol and urea into a                high-pressure reaction kettle with a magnetic                stirrer, heating and controlling temperature                system, stirring at 30&#8451; for 20 minutes                for fully mixing, and adding catalyst fluorapatite                passing carbon dioxide, replacing air in the                kettle, continuously passing carbon dioxide to make                the system pressure constant, and adjusting the                reaction temperature and reaction time, where the                mass ratio of the reactants to the total mass of                the reactants urea and methanol is 1-(5:100), and                the molar ratio of methanol and urea is 15:1, and                (b) reducing the reaction system to room                temperature, filtering to remove the catalyst,                rotating and evaporating the methanol to obtain the                product.
   USE - The method is used for preparing methyl                carbamate in medicine and organic synthesis                applications (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures simple preparation process,                mild reaction conditions, high yield of the target                product, and clean preparation process of methyl                carbamate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises                  a fluorapatite compound of formula: XCa9(PO4)6                  F2.X = metals comprising Zn, Cu, Ni, Mn, Mg                  and/or Fe.Preferred Composition: The raw materials                  methanol and urea are analytically pure, and the                  addition ratio of methanol and urea is                  (5:1)-(15:1). The mass of catalyst fluorapatite                  to the total mass of reactants urea and methanol                  is (1-5):100.Preferred Method: The reaction kettle is                  made of stainless steel and with the pressure of                  5 MPa, and is resistant to high temperature of                  200&#8451;. For the first time, passing                  carbon dioxide to air inlet, opening the outlet                  system, carbon dioxide passing is discontinued,                  the batch method is adopted, the time for filling                  is 1-4 times, and depending on the amount of                  reaction raw materials, until the air in the                  kettle is completely replaced and closing the air                  outlet. In order to adjust the reaction pressure                  of the reaction system for the second time, the                  reaction pressure is controlled at 0.5-4 MPa, and                  the reaction is carried out at a temperature of                  80-180&#8451; at 0.5-10 hours. The target                  methyl carbamate is purified by a rotary                  evaporator.

AB    NOVELTY - Deep learning-based non-feature constructive metal organic framework (MOF) carbon capture performance prediction method, comprises (i) obtaining the importance of each atom in carbon dioxide adsorption performance through machine learning and calculating the atomic projection value, (ii) calculating the side length of its circumscribed square, (iii) converting the relative coordinates of each atom into Cartesian coordinates and projecting into the circumscribed square of the corresponding surface to obtain three feature maps, (iv) using ResNet to train the feature map to obtain a prediction model and using the prediction model to make predictions, training the training model on the training set, and adjusting the over-parameter according to the verification set, and evaluating the final effect of the model in the test set after the model training is finished.

PN WO2022054297-A1
TI Manufacturing technique for producing paper useful            for producing mask, involves using waste parts of            plants as raw material for paper i.e. material            classified as next-generation paper shaving, made from            all plants and agricultural products.
AU NAKAMURA N
AE NAKAMURA N (NAKA-Individual)
GA 2022413309
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing technique that replaces Japanese                paper, involves using waste parts of plants as raw                material for paper i.e. a material classified as a                next-generation paper shaving, made from all plants                and agricultural products.
   USE - The method is useful for producing paper which                is useful for producing mask (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective since it is                preferable to use parts that are not considered                necessary and are discarded when harvesting plants                and collect and use unnecessary waste parts during                post-harvest processing, reduces transportation                costs and leads to a reduction in carbon dioxide                since generated large amount of waste is collected                at a factory or farmer. The mask prevents sea and                river pollution without using gasoline-based                materials, and reduces carbon dioxide without                incurring transportation costs by purchasing                materials locally produced for local consumption                and replaces corrugated cardboard and containers                for fast food. The method uses raw material for                paper that replaces pulp and thus deforestation is                reduced and the global environment is                improved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Method: The technique involves                  treating plants by heat treatment and/or drying,                  where plants that do not require heat treatment                  or drying can be used as such and the treatment                  method differs depending on nature of plant and                  when rice husks such as rice, wheat and barley                  are used, drying may not be basically required.                  The method uses banana peels, tangerine peels,                  chestnuts, fallen leaves, weeds, tomato flowers                  and stems, sugarcane stems, rice ears, pineapple                  skins and stems and bamboo or thinned tree                  branches. The waste parts of corn are stems,                  skins, silk threads, etc, where stems requires                  heat treatment or drying and after proper                  treatment, crushing also depends on the plant,                  but in the case of corn, after crushing the stem                  and skin (pressure may be applied depending on                  the condition of the stem), the paper came out                  stably when recycled paper was mixed in specific                  ratio when making from the waste part of corn.                  The plants are all unfamiliar i.e. mulberry,                  mulberry bark, Abelmoschus manihot root, Aogiri                  root, Hydrangea skin and Trollius hondoensis. In                  United States, corn, rice husks and orange peel,                  in Italy, tomato stalks, rice husks and wheat                  husks, in Japan, rice husks, bamboo, fallen                  leaves, tea, black tea and oolong tea can be used                  as plant materials. The plant material such as                  coffee bean powder is made into paper by mixing                  recycled paper with other fibers and fixing it.                  The paper cannot said to be Japanese paper                  without using existing materials, and since it is                  not paper because it does not use pulp directly,                  so it is named as "washi" from Japanese paper,                  which is called "paper". The mixing ratio for                  mixing recycled paper, etc. varies depending on                  the nature of the plant and recycled paper, etc.,                  i.e. 70% for plants, and 30% for recycled paper,                  etc. The method produces waste paper by putting                  raw material of paper into raw material before                  making recycled paper and toilet paper from the                  waste paper collected by any method.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN112745060-A
TI Producing environmentally-friendly hot pressing composite plate comprises e.g. drying paper-making white mud, crushing, sieving to obtain white mud powder, adding into sealed container, introducing carbon dioxide gas and drying.
AU LIU Z
AE WUXUE HONGCHANGYUAN DECORATION CO LTD (WUXU-Non-standard)
GA 2021528175
AB    NOVELTY - Producing environmentally-friendly hot pressing composite plate comprises (i) processing modified treatment by (i-a) drying the paper-making white mud to constant weight and crushing and sieving to obtain white mud powder with average grain diameter of 15 mu m, adding the white mud powder into a sealed container, introducing carbon dioxide gas with the concentration of 99.7% and 78.9% carbon monoxide gas, keeping the pressure in the sealed container at normal temperature of 0.15 -0.75 MPa, keeping the pressure for 1-4 hours, drying alkali-removed white mud powder for 7-24 hours under the environment of 110 plus minus 4 degrees C to obtain the alkali-removing white mud powder.
   USE - The method is useful for producing environmentally-friendly hot pressing composite plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can solves the pollution problem of the paper-making white mud to the environment and provides a way for recycling the white mud; is economical, energy-saving and environmentally-friendly, convenient and practical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the materials further includes 2-7 pts. wt. inorganic pigments and 8-25 pts. wt. other auxiliary materials, where the inorganic pigments are inorganic pigments of various colors, other auxiliary materials are broken glass particles that can play a decorative role, colored stones or metal particles. Preferred Method: The method further comprises (i-b) pre-treating the glass fiber and modifying the phenolic resin, mixing the water and absolute ethyl alcohol according to the volume ratio of 1:3 to form ethanol aqueous solution; at room temperature, dissolving KH550 according to volume ratio of 1:5 in the ethanol aqueous solution, allowing to stand for 30-120 minutes, soaking the glass fiber in the KH550 solution for 60-120 minutes, taking out and drying at room temperature to obtain modified glass fiber, preparing 100 pts. wt. phenolic resin, preparing 100 pts. wt. phenolic resin, 1-5 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol aqueous solution with a mass fraction of 8-10%, stirring the two in a constant temperature water bath at 30-60 degrees C for 20-40 minutes, the stirring speed is 250 rpm to obtain modified phenolic resin; (ii) removing 20-80 pts. wt. alkali white mud powder, 10-45 pts. wt. modified glass fiber, 1-30 pts. wt. modified phenolic resin, 0.1-3 pts. wt. curing agent, orderly placing the alkali-removing white mud powder and modified phenolic resin in the stirrer to stir for 15-30 minutes to obtain the premix A, then adding curing agent into the premix A, stirring for 10-30 seconds to obtain the premix B; and (iii) uniformly paving the premix B on the lower template, paving a layer of modified glass fiber with thickness of 0.2-1.5 mu m, when the thickness is 3-8 mm thickness, when the whole thickness reaches the needed thickness, closing the mold, pressurizing and heating, keeping pressure, the pressurizing pressure is 1-18 MPa, the heat preservation temperature is 150-350 degrees C, the pressure maintaining time is 3-18 minutes, in the pressure maintaining process, deflating for 4 times, each time for 1-5 seconds, then opening the mold, taking out the plate, cutting, and grinding.

   (2) reaction device comprising reactor, pressurizing device, carbon dioxide cylinder and precipitation filtering device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The pressurizing device includes housing, mounting frame is mounted on one side of the housing, cylinder is mounted on the mounting frame, and the output shaft of the cylinder extends into the housing and is connected with a piston, an air inlet pipe and an air outlet pipe are connected to the shell, the air inlet pipe is connected to the carbon dioxide cylinder, and the air outlet pipe is connected to the reaction kettle. A heater is installed in the drying cavity, a ventilating fan is installed on the inner wall of the drying cavity on one side away from the filter cavity, and a third valve is installed on the liquid outlet pipe. The reactor is provided with a feed pipe, a fourth valve and a fifth valve are installed on the feed pipe, a return pipe is connected to the feed pipe, and a water pump is installed on the return pipe, the bottom end of the return pipe is connected to the outlet pipe, and the connection between the return pipe and the feed pipe is located between the fourth valve and the fifth valve and the connection between the return pipe and the liquid outlet pipe is located above the third valve. The top side of the reaction kettle is provided with a gas release port, and a pressure relief valve is installed on the gas release port. The filter plate is rotatably connected with the bottom inner wall of the through hole, and a through hole is opened on the inner wall of the filter cavity on the side away from the drying cavity, a connecting plate is installed in the through hole, and a side of the connecting plate close to the filter cavity extends into the filter cavity and is fixedly connected with the filter plate, the other side of the connecting plate extends outside the through hole and is installed with a vibration motor, and a plurality of springs are fixedly installed on the bottom side of the connecting plate, and the bottom end of the spring is fixedly connected with the bottom inner wall of the through hole.

PN CN111389242-A; CN111389242-B
TI Dual-phase oxygen permeable membrane material used            for selectively separating oxygen from            oxygen-containing mixed gas, comprises            samarium-strontium-copper-iron oxide and            cerium-samarium oxide dual-phase material            composition.
AU WU C
   TIAN Q
   ZHANG X
   QUAN W
AE UNIV SHANGHAI (USHN-C)
GA 202066807D
AB    NOVELTY - A dual-phase oxygen-permeable membrane                material comprises samarium-strontium-copper-iron                oxide and cerium-samarium oxide dual-phase material                composition.
   USE - Dual-phase oxygen permeable membrane material                is used for selectively separating oxygen from                oxygen-containing mixed gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The dual-phase oxygen permeable membrane                material is economically prepared with compact                structure, high mechanical strength and oxygen                permeability, excellent compatibility of the two                phases, and resistance to carbon dioxide poisoning,                and no generation of impurity, by simple process,                and is able to operate stably for a long period of                time in an atmosphere containing carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A dual-phase oxygen-permeable membrane                material (SmxSr1-xCuyFe1-yO3-~d-50-70 wt.%                Ce0.8Sm0.2O2-~d, where x is 0-0.5, y is 0.1-0.3 and                delta is not defined) comprises                samarium-strontium-copper-iron oxide                (SmxSr1-xCuyFe1-yO3-~d) and cerium-samarium oxide                (Ce0.8Sm0.2O2-~d) dual-phase material composition.                An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of                dual-phase oxygen-permeable membrane                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The                  samarium-strontium-copper-iron oxide material is                  prepared by dissolving samarium oxide in an acid                  solvent, obtaining a samarium solution,                  dissolving strontium salt, copper salt and iron                  salt in water, obtaining an intermediate                  solution, then mixing the samarium solution and                  the intermediate solution, obtaining a mixed salt                  solution, adding ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid                  (EDTA) and citric acid based on the metal ion,                  EDTA and citric acid weight ratio of 1:1:1.5,                  heating, stirring the reactant solution,                  adjusting pH to 7-8 using aqueous ammonia,                  heating and stirring until the reactant solution                  forms sol, drying the obtained sol-like                  substance, calcining to remove the organic                  substance, and sintering obtained precursor. The                  cerium-samarium oxide material is prepared by                  dissolving samarium oxide in acid solvent,                  obtaining samarium solution, dissolving cerium                  salt in water, obtaining cerium salt solution,                  mixing the samarium solution and cerium salt                  solution, obtaining mixed salt solution, adding                  EDTA and citric acid based on the metal ion, EDTA                  and citric acid weight ratio of 1:1:1.5, heating,                  stirring the reactant solution, adjusting pH to                  7-8 using aqueous ammonia, heating and stirring                  until the reactant solution forms sol, drying the                  obtained sol-like substance, calcining to remove                  the organic substance, and sintering obtained                  precursor. Preferred Composition: The                  samarium-strontium-copper-iron oxide material has                  cubic perovskite phase. The cerium-samarium oxide                  material has fluorite phase. The                  samarium-strontium-copper-iron oxide material                  perovskite phase B site contains copper and does                  not contain cobalt. The acid solvent is 68 %mass                  or more concentrated nitric acid solution. The                  strontium salt, copper salt, iron salt and cerium                  salt are nitrate.

PT P
PN CN111298784-A
TI Catalyst used for electrocatalytic reduction of            carbon dioxide to oxalic acid, comprises lead tin            oxide.
AU KANG P
   CHENG Y
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2020581895
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises leadxtinyoxide3.
   USE - The catalyst is used for electrocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide to oxalic                acid(claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst obtains lead-tin oxide catalyst                by a simple hydrothermal method, produces oxalic                acid with excellent performance. The method is                simple, the reaction conditions are mild, is                controllable, and the repeatability is                strong.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst comprises leadxtinyoxide3,
   x = 0.5-1.5;and
   y = 0.5-1.5.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing catalyst involves (a) weighing                soluble lead salt and tin salt to dissolve in water                to form precursor solution (A), dissolving alkali                in water to form precursor solution (B), (b) adding                the precursor solution (A) in step (a) to the                reactor, dripping the precursor solution (B) into                it with stirring, adding carbon powder, heating in                a water bath, and performing reflux reaction at 90                degrees C, (c) separating, washing and drying in an                oven after the reaction, and (d) obtaining the                sample after drying in step (c), roasting in a tube                furnace at a heating rate of 2-4 degrees C/minutes                at 350 degrees C, baking in an inert gas atmosphere                for 2-4 hours to obtain the lead-tin oxide                catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The soluble lead salt                  comprises lead acetate trihydrate, lead acetate                  or lead nitrate, the soluble tin salt comprises                  stannous chloride or sodium stannate, and the                  base in the precursor solution (B) is sodium                  hydroxide or potassium hydroxide. Preferred                  Composition: The ratio of the total molar amount                  of lead and tin salts in the precursor solution                  (A) to the molar amount of the precursor solution                  (B) is 1:2. The molar ratio of lead to tin is                  3:1-1:3. Preferred Property: The concentration of                  the precursor solution (A) is 0.05-0.2 mol/l, and                  the concentration of the precursor solution (B)                  is 0.5-2 mol/l. The dropping speed of the                  precursor solution (B) is 1-3 drop/second.                  Preferred Method: The method comprises adding                  1/15-1/3 carbon powder. The method for preparing                  lead-tin oxide catalyst electrocatalyst electrode                  in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                  to oxalic acid comprises (i) weighing lead-tin                  oxide in a volatile solvent, adding naphthol to                  it, and ultrasonically processing to obtain an                  electrode dispersion, and (b) applying 5-50 mu l                  of electrode dispersion to the glassy carbon                  electrode and carbon paper electrode respectively                  for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                  to oxalic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volatile solvent                  comprises N,N-dimethylformamide, ethanol or                  isopropanol, where the catalyst concentration in                  the electrode dispersion is 10 mg/ml, the mass                  concentration of naphthol is 0.25 wt.%, and the                  condition of electrocatalytic reduction of carbon                  dioxide to oxalic acid is to use a non-aqueous                  silver/silver chloride (0.01 mol/L                  AgNO3/acetonitrile) as the reference electrode,                  and the platinum wire is a counter electrode, and                  is carried out in a 0.1 mol/l propylene carbonate                  solution of tetrabutylammonium                  hexafluorophosphate.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)
MC E10-C02D1; E11-D02; E11-F03; E11-N; E35-H; E35-J; J03-B01; J04-E04A; J04-E04D; J04-E11; N07-G

PT P
PN US2020188565-A1; US11433164-B2
TI System for prolonged anaerobic storage of red            blood cells, comprises white blood cell-removing            device, oxygen/carbon dioxide-removing device, storage            bag comprising oxygen sorbent, and red blood            cell-reoxygenating, and tubings.
AU YOSHIDA T
   VERNUCCI P
AE HEMANEXT INC (HEMA-Non-standard)
GA 202054861U
AB    NOVELTY - A red blood cell storage system comprises a                device for removing white blood cells (WBCs) from                red blood cells (RBCs); a device for removing                oxygen (O2) and/or carbon dioxide (CO2) from RBCs;                a storage bag for storing RBCs and comprising                oxygen sorbent for maintaining RBCs in anaerobic                condition; a device for reoxygenating RBCs; and                tubings connecting the WBC-removing device,                O2/CO2-removing device, storage bag and                RBC-reoxygenating device.
   USE - The system is used for prolonged anaerobic                storage of red blood cells from collection to                transfusion.
   ADVANTAGE - The system optimizes red blood cells prior to                transfusion to minimize morbidity associated with                transfusion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of RBCs by obtaining whole blood;                separating RBCs from whole blood to form packed                RBCs; optionally adding additive solution to the                packed RBCs to form suspension of packed RBCs;                depleting O2 and/or CO2 from the packed RBCs or the                suspension of packed RBCs to form O2/CO2-depleted                RBCs; and storing the O2/CO2-depleted RBCs in                anaerobic storage environment to maintain in                O2/CO2-depleted condition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The O2/CO2-depleted RBCs                  are stored in anaerobic storage environment at                  1-6 degrees C. The method further includes: (1)                  irradiating the RBCs before or after removing                  oxygen and/or carbon dioxide from the packed                  RBCs; editing the whole blood, packed RBCs, or                  suspension of packed RBCs before storage in                  anaerobic environment, or editing the stored                  O2/CO2-depleted RBCs after storage in anaerobic                  environment; reducing volume of stored                  O2/CO2-depleted RBCs to concentrate the RBCs                  prior to reoxygenation; adding nitric oxide to                  stored O2/CO2-depleted RBCs when the RBCs are                  stored in anaerobic environment; and                  reoxygenating the reduced volume O2/CO2-depleted                  RBCs to form oxygenated RBCs for transfusion; or                  (2) removing WBCs from whole blood, packed RBCs,                  or suspension of packed RBCs before removing                  oxygen and/or carbon dioxide; irradiating the                  RBCs before or after removing oxygen and/or                  carbon dioxide; inactivating pathogens in RBCs                  before or after removing oxygen and/or carbon                  dioxide; editing the whole blood, packed RBCs, or                  suspension of packed RBCs before storage in                  anaerobic environment, or editing the stored                  O2/CO2-depleted RBCs after storage in anaerobic                  environment; reducing the volume of stored                  O2/CO2-depleted RBCs to concentrate the RBCs                  prior to reoxygenation; adding nitric oxide to                  the stored O2/CO2-depleted RBCs when RBCs are                  stored in anaerobic environment; and                  reoxygenating the reduced volume O2/CO2-depleted                  RBCs to form oxygenated RBCs for transfusion. The                  oxygen is depleted from RBCs in 24 hours of                  gamma- or X-ray irradiation, when RBCs are                  irradiated before oxygen is depleted. The editing                  step comprises identifying moribund RBCs by                  filtering 10% of most dense RBCs, exposing RBCs                  to osmotic shocking using hypotonic solution and                  eliminating RBCs damaged by the shock, or using                  filter to trap moribund RBCs and applying                  high-affinity ligand to the filter to trap RBCs                  having desired surface marker suitable for                  identifying moribund or distressed RBCs, or                  separating RBC with reduced deformability by                  processing through bump array device. The method                  further includes adding nutrients or metabolic                  supplements to O2/CO2-depleted RBCs during                  storage in anaerobic environment.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The O2/CO2-removing                  device includes hollow fibers or gas-permeable                  films or fibers for receiving and conveying RBCs;                  and oxygen scavenger and/or carbon dioxide                  scavenger adjacent to and between the hollow                  fibers and/or gas-permeable films or fibers. The                  storage bag includes port(s) for receiving fluids                  comprising nitric oxide (NO) or NO precursors,                  nutrients and metabolic supplements. The                  RBC-reoxygenating device includes hollow fibers                  and/or gas-permeable films or fibers between                  inlet and outlet for passage of RBCs and oxygen                  or air.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The additive solution                  has pH of 5-9 and comprises Additive Solution 1                  (AS-1), AS-3, AS-5,                  saline-adenine-glucose-mannitol (SAGM),                  phosphate-adenine-glucose-guanosine-saline-mannitol                  (PAGGSM),                  phosphate-adenine-glucose-guanosine-gluconate-mannitol                  (PAGGGM), mannitol-adenine-phosphate (MAP), SOLX                  (RTM: alkaline RBC storage solution containing                  bicarbonate, adenine, glucose, mannitol and                  phosphate), ESOL (RTM: RBC additive solution),                  Experimental Additive Solution 61 (EAS61),                  Oxygen-Free Additive Solution 1 (OFAS1), and/or                  OFAS3. The additive solution further comprises                  antioxidant, preferably quercetin, alpha                  -tocopherol, ascorbic acid, and/or oxidase                  inhibitor. The desired surface marker is                  clustered band-3 or phosphatidylserine.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P34 (Sterilising, syringes, electrotherapy (A61L, M, N).)
MC B04-B01B; B04-F04A; B05-A01B; B05-B01P; B05-B02A3; B06-A01; B06-D09; B07-A02B; B10-A07A; B10-A07B; B11-B05; B11-C06; B12-M12D; B15-C00; B15-E00; D05-H13; P34-A02

TI Marine fuel blend for decreasing sulphur emissions of a marine vessel, and for decreasing carbon dioxide footprint of marine traffic, comprises a marine fuel, and renewable hydrotreated fuel, and marine fuel blend has a pour point.
AU KIISKI U
   KOUVA M
   KURONEN M
   NORTIO J
AE NESTE OYJ (NESO-C)
   NESTE OYJ (NESO-C)
   NESTE OYJ (NESO-C)
GA 202049217E
AB    NOVELTY - Marine fuel blend comprises a marine fuel having a density between 860 and 960 kg/m3 at 15 degrees C, and 0.5-50 vol.% renewable hydrotreated fuel. The marine fuel blend has a pour point which is lower than the calculated pour point expressed as the weighted average of the pour points of the marine fuel and the renewable hydrotreated fuel.
   USE - Marine fuel blend used for decreasing sulfur emissions of a marine vessel, and for decreasing carbon dioxide footprint of marine traffic (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The marine fuel blend is environmentally friendly and has good pour point and storage stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of manufacturing the marine fuel blend, which involves providing marine fuel having a density between 860 and 960 kg/m3 at 15 degrees C, and selecting an amount of renewable hydrotreated fuel which provides a higher than calculated improvement of the pour point of the marine fuel blend, and blending the marine fuel with the amount of renewable hydrotreated fuel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The renewable hydrotreated fuel comprises paraffinic components in the carbon number range 15-18 carbons, and paraffinic components comprising at least 70 vol.% of the renewable hydrotreated fuel. The marine fuel blend is having a sulfur content of 0.5 wt.% or less. The marine fuel blend is having a density of max 900 kg/m3 and a pour point of 6 degrees C or less. The marine fuel blend is comprising 0.5-30 vol.% renewable hydrotreated fuel, preferably comprising 0.5-10 vol.% renewable hydrotreated fuel. The marine fuel blend has an oxidation stability which is higher than the calculated oxidation stability expressed as the weighted average of the oxidation stability values of the marine fuel and the renewable hydrotreated fuel, and where the oxidation stability is calculated as defined in EN16091 (2011).
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111108943-A
TI Ecological greenhouse cultivation method comprises e.g. installing interconnected vegetation climbing frames in planting shed, pumping water containing carbon dioxide into fish pond by water pipe, and spraying vegetation.
AU YANG M
   LIN F
AE QUANZHOU QUANGANG PINRAN TEA IND CO LTD (QUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202042181V
AB    NOVELTY - Ecological greenhouse cultivation method comprises (i) installing a multiple of interconnected vegetation climbing frames in a planting shed, where the vegetation climbing frames include a hollow connecting member fixedly provided at lower end of a hollow connecting base and a spraying pipe connected to the cavity forming a space between the connecting member; (ii) starting the water pump, and pumping the water containing carbon dioxide into a fish pond by a water pipe, spraying the vegetation through a shower head and allowing photosynthesis to produce oxygen; (iii) melting oxygen into the spray water and collecting by the collecting tray into the connecting pipe and flowing into the fish pond to supply oxygen to the fish; (iv) using the sludge generated from the fish pond clearing ponds as waste material to fertilize the vegetation; adn (v) picking the vegetation fruits, and placing the stems and leaves of the vegetation into the fish pond as fish food.
   USE - The method is useful for ecological greenhouse cultivation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of vegetation climbing frame utilized in the above mentioned method.

AE ESCAMA S S (ESCA-Individual)
GA 202037450J

The reformer receives a fuel from the fuel supply unit to cause a reforming reaction for generating a mixed gas containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen. PT P
PN CN111019975-A
TI Anaerobic conversion of excess sludge to produce            methane and organic acids comprises e.g. suspending            sludge, adding culture medium, culturing, inoculating            sludge polysaccharide-hydrolyzed bacteria and            culturing.
AU ZHANG F
   ZENG J
   ZHANG W
   DAI K
AE UNIV FUJIAN AGRIC & FORESTRY (UYFN-C)
GA 2020339235
AB    NOVELTY - Anaerobic conversion of excess sludge to                produce methane and organic acids comprises (i)                taking sludge, passing through an iron sieve,                suspending with culture medium, shaking and mixing                uniformly, centrifuging to remove the supernatant,                and repeating the above operation for many times,                (ii) inoculating sludge in culture flask, adding                culture medium, and adding sodium alginate to the                culture medium, infuse mixture of excess nitrogen                and carbon dioxide, sealing the flask, continue                cultivating for 280-320 days at temperature of                34-36 degrees C to enrich the sludge polysaccharide                hydrolysis flora, (iii) inoculating sludge                polysaccharide-hydrolyzed bacteria group enriched                in step (ii) in an anaerobic reactor, using the                remaining sludge as a substrate, and the                inoculation amount is 80-125 ml/l, introducing the                mixed gas of excess nitrogen and carbon dioxide,                sealing the anaerobic reactor, culturing at                temperature of 34-36 degrees C for 2-7 days to                obtain the metabolite methane and organic                acid.
   USE - The method is useful for anaerobic conversion                of excess sludge to produce methane and organic                acids.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical; and has mild                condition conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sludge is residual                  sludge. The iron screen is a 200 mesh iron                  screen. The culture medium comprises 450-550 mg/l                  ammonium chloride, 180-220 mg/l monopotassium                  phosphate, 45-55 mg/l sodium sulfate, 45-55 mg/l                  potassium chloride, 8-12 mg/l calcium chloride,                  65-75 mg/l magnesium chloride hexahydrate, 0.6-1                  mg/l manganese (II) chloride tetrahydrate, 1-1.4                  mg/l cobalt (II) chlorate dehydrate, 3-3.4 mg/l                  ferrous sulfate heptahydrate, 0.4-0.6 mg/l                  aluminum chloride, 0.05-0.15 mg/l sodium                  molybdate dehydrate, 0.1-0.25 mg/l boric acid,                  0.4-0.6 mg/l nickel (II) chloride, 1-1.2 mg/l                  copper (II) chloride dehydrate, 3-3.5 mg/l zinc                  sulfate dehydrate, and 2.8-3.2 mg/l                  ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid disodium salt.                  The culture medium pH is adjusted to 7-7.4 by                  acid or alkali. The concentration of the sodium                  alginate in the medium is 1-3 g/l. The volume                  ratio of nitrogen and carbon dioxide in the mixed                  gas of nitrogen and carbon dioxide is                  70-85:15-25%. The organic acid is acetic acid,                  propionic acid, butyric acid and/or hexanoic                  acid. Preferred Method: The centrifugation is                  carried out at 5500-6500 revolutions/minute for                  4-6 minutes.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN110589864-A
TI Preparing oyster nano calcium carbonate used in preparation of calcium supplement, by dissolving oyster shell in lactic acid, extracting calcium ions, adding trypsin, introducing carbon dioxide gas, magnetically stirring and drying crystals.
AU XIE X
   WENG W
   SUN L
   GAO P
AE UNIV QUANZHOU NORMAL (UQZN-C)
GA 2019A76127
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing oyster nano calcium carbonate using trypsin as a crystal form control agent involves (a) dissolving the oyster shell in lactic acid, extracting the calcium ions in the oyster shell, and then filtering the lactic acid solution to obtain the filtrate and (b) adjusting the pH of the filtrate to 11-12 with aqueous ammonia, then adding trypsin to the filtrate, stirring until complete dissolution, then introducing carbon dioxide gas, magnetically stirring in a water bath, collecting the crystals, and washing and drying the crystals to obtain nano calcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing oyster nano calcium carbonate nano-calcium carbonate which is used in preparation of calcium supplement (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, environmentally-friendly and cost-effective, recycles waste, removes the original fishy smell of oyster shells, improves taste and ensures that the prepared nano calcium carbonate is smaller, has improved crystal form and absorption, and higher bioavailability compared with the calcium carbonate obtained through current mainstream production process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves washing the oyster shell to remove impurity, crushing and sieving through 100 mesh sieve to obtain oyster shell powder, adding 1.75-2.25 mol/l, preferably 2 mol/l lactic acid solution to the oyster shell powder in a beaker, reacting at 50-60 degrees C, preferably 50 degrees C for 25-35 minutes, preferably 30 minutes while magnetically stirring at 250-350 revolutions per minute (rpm), preferably 350 rpm, when the oyster shell powder is completely dissolved in lactic acid, terminating the reaction and filtering the lactic acid solution to obtain a filtrate. In step (b), the mass ratio of calcium in the filtrate and trypsin is 0.4065:0.0041-0.813, preferably 0.4065:0.0203-0.061. The step (b) involves introducing carbon dioxide gas at a flow rate of 1 l/minute and magnetically stirring in a water bath at 23-27 degrees C.

PN CN110483517-A
TI Preparing matrine comprises cleaning matrine-making raw materials, placing in sodium chloride solution, stimulating pulse current, grinding primary raw material liquid, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and spray-drying.
AU SUN W
AE SUN W (SUNW-Individual)
GA 2019A0405R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing matrine comprises (a) cleaning the matrine-making raw materials, placing into a glass vessel containing sodium chloride solution, where the cathode and anode sheets of the pulse stimulator are respectively located in the sodium chloride solution at both ends of the glass vessel, turning off the pulse stimulator after pulse current stimulation, taking out the raw materials for washing and drying, (b) crushing the dried raw materials to 20-30 mesh, mixing with water to obtain a primary raw material liquid having a dry-wet ratio of 1-2:0.6-3.5, (c) grinding the primary raw material liquid to a particle size of less than 100 microns to obtain an intermediate raw material liquid and (d) subjecting the intermediate raw material liquid to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, then separating twice by a separator and spray-drying to obtain a raw matrine powder having a content of 50-75%.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing matrine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and economical; has convenient operation, low consumption, high efficiency and quick production of high-purity matrine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (e) mixing the original radix sophorae flavescentis powder with water to obtain a radix sophorae flavescentis lye with a dry-wet ratio of 1:4-8, grinding the lye to a particle diameter of less than 30 microns, extracting the ground lye to obtain the matrine lye whose average particle diameter is less than 1000 nm, where the particles with a particle size less than 900 nm is more than 95% of all particles and (f) placing the extracted slurry in a low-temperature vacuum concentration device to concentrate to a moisture content of less than 80%. The extraction in the step (e) includes supercritical extraction, microwave extraction, ultrasonic extraction, ultrahigh pressure extraction and/or nano-grinding extraction. The supercritical extraction uses a pressure of 25-80 MPa, a power of 5-25 KW, a temperature of greater than or equal to 40 degrees C and a liquid flow of 1000-1500 l/hour. The frequency of microwave extraction is 300-900 MHz, the power is 5-25 KW and the temperature is greater than or equal to 40 degrees C. The frequency of ultrasonic extraction is 20-50 KHz, the power is 5-25 KW and the temperature is greater than or equal to 40 degrees C. The pressure used for ultra-high pressure extraction is 200-300 MPa, the power is 5-25 KW, the temperature is greater than or equal to 40 degrees C and the liquid flow is 1000-1500 l/hour. The pressure for nano-grinding extraction is 50-80 MPa, the power is 5-25 KW, the temperature is greater than or equal to 40 degrees C and the liquid flow is 1000-5000 l/hour. The step (f) comprises concentrating the slurry to a water content of less than 15% by a low-temperature vacuum drying device to obtain solid high-purity nanomatrine. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is performed under a pressure of 20-40 MPa, a temperature of 25-40 degrees C and a time of 30-60 minutes. Preferred Components: The current of the cathode and anode sheets is provided by the pulse stimulator via a 20 megaohm resistor. The grinding adopts a colloid mill or a cone mill. The raw materials in the step (a) comprise plants radix sophorae flavescentis, Sophora alopecuroides and Sophora subprostrata.

PN EP3495346-A1
TI Preparing calcium methioninate useful as feed additive comprises e.g. hydrolyzing 5-(2-methylmercaptoethyl)-hydantoin with ammonia and water, producing defrosted hydrolyzate, hydrolyzing with calcium oxide and/or calcium hydroxide and water.
AU FISCHER D
AE EVONIK DEGUSSA GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 201950703R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium methioninate, comprises: (a) hydrolyzing 5-(2-methylmercaptoethyl)-hydantoin with ammonia and water to obtain a hydrolyzate- containing ammonium methioninate and carbon dioxide, (b) producing a defrosted hydrolyzate by at least partially separating off existing ammonia and carbon dioxide from the hydrolyzate, preferably by distilling off and/or stripping off, (c) hydrolyzing the defrosted hydrolyzate from (b) with calcium oxide and/or calcium hydroxide and water to a second hydrolyzate containing calcium methioninate and ammonia, (d) optionally producing a calcium methioninate-containing crude product by separating existing ammonia from the second hydrolyzate of (c), by distillation and/or stripping, and (e) producing solid calcium methioninate by spray granulation of the crude product from (d) or optionally by direct spray granulation of the calcium methioninate and ammonia-containing hydrolyzate from (c).
   USE - The calcium methioninate is useful as a feed additive in animal feed or as a feed additive for the production of animal feed (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method directly produces calcium salt of methionine, such that the final calcium methionate product contains more than 53 wt.% methionine content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) is carried out at temperatures of 140-200 degrees C, preferably 160-180 degrees C and pressures of 10-30 bar. In step (a) 1-20 moleq, preferably 2-10 moleq ammonia per moleq methionine equivalente is used. The step (b) is carried out at temperatures of 80-180 degrees C, preferably 100-120 degrees C and pressures of 0-1 bar. The step (c) is carried out at temperatures of 150-190 degrees C, preferably 160-185 degrees C and pressures of 5-20 bar. In step (c) 0.5-1.0 moleq, preferably 0.6-0.8 moleq of calcium hydroxide per moleq of methionine equivalents is used. The step (d) is carried out at temperatures of 70-100 degrees C, preferably 80-95 degrees C and pressures of 0.5-0.9 bar. The step (e) is carried out at temperatures of 50-130 degrees C, preferably 55-110 degrees C and pressures of 0.9-1.2 bar is performed. An additional concentration of the crude product from step (d), takes place by evaporation, before step (e). The 5-(2-methylmercaptoethyl)-hydantoin is used in step (a) in the form of 5-(2-methylmercaptoethyl)-hydantoin-containing reaction mixture which is produced by reacting methylmercaptopropionaldehyde (MMP) with hydrocyanic acid, ammonia and carbon dioxide and/or from corresponding MMP cyanohydrin (MMP-CH) generated with ammonia and carbon dioxide. The residual ammonia from the reaction mixture is used and carbon dioxide is previously at least partially removed by distillation and/or stripping off. The removed ammonia and carbon dioxide are reused to produce 5-(2-methylmercaptoethyl)-hydantoin-containing reaction mixture. Ammonia is separated from the mixture of ammonia and carbon dioxide and is reused for the hydrolysis of 5-(2-methylmercaptoethyl)-hydantoin.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 2019196230
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an expander (12) for generating power by expanding circulating fluid. A power generator generates electricity using the power generated by the expander. A heat exchanger allows the expanded circulating fluid passing through the expander and the compressed circulating fluid passing through the compressor to exchange heat with each other. A power transmitter comprises a driving shaft and rotates a driven shaft, which comprises shafts of the compressor and the pump to transmit the power generated by the expander to the compressor and the pump.
   USE - Power generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has high efficiency and less carbon dioxide emission, and reduces mechanical energy loss and improves energy efficiency since power is directly transmitted from the expander to the compressor and the pump without passing through a power generator and a motor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a power generation system to which supercritical carbon dioxide cycle is applied.
   Combustor (11)
   Expander (12)
   Compressor (13)
   Pump (14)

PN CN109319814-A
TI Preparing activated nanometer calcium carbonate by wet method comprises e.g. calcining the limestone, crushing, pulverizing, adding water, heating, passing carbon dioxide, adding activator, filtrating and drying.
AU WANG A
AE NANJING AIFENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 201917296J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing activated nanometer calcium carbonate by wet method comprises (i) calcining the limestone, crushing by a crusher and pulverizing particle size of 1-2 cm, then adding into a calcining furnace for calcination at 900 degrees C for 120-360 minutes to obtain quicklime, In the calcination process, a fluidized bed is arranged in the calcining furnace, and the limestone particles are fluidized and calcined in the fluidized bed, (ii) adding the quicklime obtained in the first step with water, and stirring to obtain slaked lime, stirring for 30-60 minutes, (iii) adding the slaked lime into the activation reactor, continuing adding water, heating at 45-65 degrees C under stirring, passing carbon dioxide into the activation device, adding activator while stirring to accelerate activation, and (iv) preparing wet calcium carbonate using calcium carbonate obtained in step (iii) by wet method, carrying out sedimentation, and filtrating by a filter press, and (v) drying the wet calcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing activated nanometer calcium carbonate by wet method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: excellent calcining effect; is save energy; and increases the activation rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pressure in the activated reactor is 0.45-0.5 MPa during the activation of slaked lime. The drying temperature in the fifth step is 40-60 degrees C and is dried by hot air. The activator is regulator, coupling agent, lubricant. The regulator is acrylate modifier. The coupling agent is aluminate coupling agent, titanate coupling agent and/or silane coupling agent. The lubricant is polyethylene wax, stearic acid, stearic acid monoglyceride and/or paraffin wax.

PT P
PN CN108946751-A; CN108946751-B
TI Preparing boron hexaoxide used for photo catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide involves utilizing the amorphous boron powder or boron powder with crystallinity as the raw material, when amorphous boron powder is used.
AU LIU J
   YAN D
   LUO H
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 2018A0554Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing boron hexaoxide involves utilizing amorphous boron powder or boron powder with crystallinity as raw material, when amorphous boron powder is used, the amorphous boron powder is first placed in a tube furnace and fired at 900-1300 degrees C in an argon atmosphere for 120 to 600 min, converted into boron powder with crystallinity. The boron powder with crystallinity is added to the oxidizing inorganic acid solution, and then ultrasonically dispersing it for 5 to 30 minutes with an ultrasonic machine at 5 to 40 degrees C, and then reacting for 30 to 240 minutes in an oil bath at 25 to 100 degrees C. The solution obtained is diluted and centrifuged, washed separately with deionized water and ethanol, and then the obtained powder is dried in an oven at 50 to 80 degrees C for 4 to 8 hours to obtain boron hexaoxide.
   USE - Method for preparing boron hexaoxide used for photo catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The methid enables to prepare boron hexaoxide with high stability and good visible lightcatalytic activity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The argon atmosphere is high purity argon having a purity of greater than 99%, the flow rate is 30-100 mL/min. The heating speed is 2-10 degrees C/min. Preferred Components: The oxidizing inorganic acid isnitric acid, sulphuric acid or hydrogen peroxide. The aqueous solution of the alcohol is an aqueous solution of one or both of methanol and ethanol. The xenon lamp is provided with a cut filter of 400 nm, and the light source is visible light. Preferred Process: The method involves adding an alcohol having a volume fraction of 0 to 50% in a ratio of 10 to 200 mg: 50 to 120 ml. he aqueous solution is used as a solvent, and the magnetron is placed in a vacuum. The vacuum pump is used to pump the photocatalytic reaction system to a vacuum. Under the magnetic stirring, a xenon lamp is used as a light source to carry out a photocatalytic decomposition of the hydrogen production experiment, and the atmosphere in the photocatalytic reaction system is at the same time interval. Sampling and quantifying is performed by gas chromatography to determine the content of the product. The boron hexaoxide is added in the photocatalytic reaction system, adding deionized water in a ratio of 10 to 200 mg: 1 to 3 ml, and replacing the light with carbon dioxide gas. The air in the catalytic reaction system is 2-3 times, then carbon dioxide is introduced to normal pressure, and a xenon lamp is used as a light source to perform photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide. The atmosphere in the photocatalytic reaction system is sampled at the same time interval, and gas chromatography is performed.

PN CN108927206-A
TI Preparing metal doped mesoporous molecular sieve material useful for catalyzing the cyclo addition reaction of carbon dioxide with an epoxide comprises mixing template agent 1, alkali source, synthesizing hydrothermally and removing.
AU LI G
   LIU D
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201899537L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal doped mesoporous molecular sieve material comprises mixing template agent 1, alkali source, template agent 2 and silicon source, hydrolyzing, adding metal salt, continuously stirring and synthesizing hydrothermally at 20-130 degrees C and removing the template agent 1, where the template agent 1 comprises cetyl trimethyl ammonium bromide, cetyl trimethyl ammonium chloride, dodecyl amine, hexadecyl amine, polyethylene oxide-polypropylene oxide-polyethylene oxide triblock copolymer or polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene ether block copolymer. The template agent 2 comprises polydimethyl diallyl ammonium chloride or a copolymer of dimethyl diallyl ammonium chloride and acrylamide. The mass ratio of template agent 2 and template agent 1 is (0.01-1):1 and the molar ratio of silicon source and metal salt is (10-500):1.
   USE - The metal doped mesoporous molecular sieve material is useful for catalyzing the cyclo addition reaction of carbon dioxide with an epoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The metal doped mesoporous molecular sieve material has high yield, high selectivity and the method is simple, economical, highly efficient and convenient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for metal doped mesoporous molecular sieve material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent comprises deionized water, ethanol, methanol or isopropanol. The silicon source comprises tetramethyl orthosilicate, tetraethyl orthosilicate, tetrabutyl orthosilicate, silica sol and/or silicate. The alkali source comprises dodecyl amine, ethylenediamine, butylamine, aqueous ammonia, sodium hydroxide and/or potassium hydroxide. The metal salt comprises iron, zinc, chromium, copper, cobalt, aluminum, nickel, titanium, vanadium, nitrate-salt, chlorine-salt, sulfate-salt or acetate-salt. The acid comprises nitric acid, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid or acetic acid. Preferred Method: The method for preparing metal doped mesoporous molecular sieve material comprises (i) adding template agent 1 into solvent, completely dissolving template agent 1, adding alkali source, adding template 2 into the solution, adding a silicon source under stirring and hydrolyzing, adding the metal salt, continuously stirring, performing hydrothermal synthesis at 20-130 degrees C; and (ii) removing template agent 1 with an acid solution using a mild solvent extraction method to generate mesopores and template agent 2 remains in the molecular sieve. The method for catalyzing the cyclo addition reaction of carbon dioxide with an epoxide comprises adding a metal-doped mesoporous molecular sieve material into a cyclo addition reaction system of carbon dioxide and an epoxide, reacting at 80-140 degrees C for 0.5-8 hours with pressure of 0.2-2 MPa.

PN CN108840362-A
TI Preparing light calcium carbonate using steel slag comprises e.g. grinding steel slag, irradiating under microwave field, crushing, placing in ammonium chloride solution, extracting, filtering, injecting carbon dioxide, and reacting.
AU TONG Z
AE UNIV YANGTZE NORMAL (UYYN-C)
GA 201894354J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing light calcium carbonate using steel slag comprises (i) grinding steel slag, irradiating under microwave field, crushing and sieving through 200 meshes sieve; (ii) placing the irradiated steel slag particles in 2-4 mol/l ammonium chloride solution, extracting for 2-4 hours by stirring once for every 10 minutes, filtering to obtain filtrate and filter residue, washing the filter residue with water and filtering to obtain washing liquid; and (iii) mixing the washing liquid with filtrate to obtain a leachate, blowing air into the leachate for 10-30 minutes, allowing to stand for 30-60 minutes, passing upper static solution into a carbonization reactor, turning on the stirring device, continuously injecting carbon dioxide mixed gas, stopping the reaction when the pH of the leachate reduced to 7, allowing to stand and press-filtering.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing light calcium carbonate using steel slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, economical and easy to control; produces high purity product and meets industrial standard requirements; and reduces energy consumption and realizes comprehensive utilization of steel slag.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The microwave irradiation power is 2-5 kw, the frequency is 2.45 GHz and the time is 2-4 hours. In the step (ii), the extraction temperature is 40-60 degrees C. Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the solid-liquid ratio of the steel slag to ammonium chloride solution is 1:20-50 g/ml. The volume ratio of carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide mixed gas is 10-35%.

   LI P
   LI Z
   WEI X
   LIU J
AE UNIV CHINA GEOSCIENCES BEIJING (UYGJ-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Beverage dispensing valve (10) comprises inlet                (12) which is configured to receive carbonated                water and nozzle shell, where nozzle shell (110)                comprises aperture (16) which is open to ambient                pressure environment and outlet down stream of                nozzle shell which is configured to dispense                finished beverage (14).
   USE - Beverage dispensing valve.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage dispensing valve increases                standard nozzle cavity, allows incremental volume,                and controls carbonation level.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for dispensing beverage, which involves:
   (A) flowing carbonated water through inlet of                beverage dispenser valve;
   (B) lowering pressure of carbonated water to                level sufficient which promotes carbon dioxide                nucleation;
   (C) flowing carbonated water through chamber                vented to ambient pressure environment such that                portion of nucleated carbon dioxide is off-gassed;                and
   (D) introducing beverage ingredient when                carbonated water reaches sufficiently low                carbonation level.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                beverage dispensing valve. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
   Beverage dispensing valve (10)
   Inlet (12)
   Finished beverage (14)
   Aperture (16)
   Nozzle shell (110)

Preferred Process: The                  lowering pressure of carbonated water is effected                  by passing carbonated water through diffuser and                  into chamber vented to ambient pressure                  environment. The carbon dioxide nucleated is                  off-gassed through aperture which is disposed in                  portion of beverage dispensing nozzle. Preferred                  Components: The nozzle shell further comprises                  nozzle shell chamber which is configured to                  receive carbonated water from inlet and allow                  carbon dioxide to vent to ambient pressure                  environment through the aperture and interface                  region which is configured to couple to a                  beverage dispensing valve.

TI Catalyst used in production of oil by carbon dioxide hydrogenation, comprises iron nitrate nonahydrate, tartaric acid, water, palladium and potassium.
AU NING W
   DAI H
   WANG B
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201866903V
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises iron nitrate nonahydrate, tartaric acid and water as raw materials for preparing catalyst precursor and palladium and potassium as co-catalysts. The mass ratio of iron nitrate nonahydrate, tartaric acid and water is 1:0.4-0.5:0.35-0.40.
   USE - Catalyst used in production of oil by carbon dioxide hydrogenation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has high activity and selectivity to 5C hydrocarbons at less than 250 degrees C; and can synthesize more 2-4C olefins. The method is simple and has less operation steps.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of catalyst comprising dissolving tartaric acid in water to form tartaric acid solution, pouring iron nitrate nonahydrate, stirring to obtain mixed solution, drying mixed solution in air-blast oven at 80-120 degrees C overnight to obtain solid, placing solid in muffle furnace, calcining at 300-450 degrees C for 9-48 hours to obtain iron oxide particles, loading palladium and potassium on iron oxide particles by equal volume impregnation method, drying overnight in air blast oven at 80-120 degrees C and baking in muffle furnace at 300-450 degrees C for 6-12 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The added amount of palladium is 1-5 wt.%, preferably 1.5-4 wt.%. The added amount of potassium is 1-6 wt.%, preferably 1.5-5 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of iron nitrate nonahydrate, tartaric acid and water is 1:0.46:0.38. Preferred Components: The tartaric acid is D-tartaric acid and/or L-tartaric acid.

PN CN108041157-A
TI Preserving bamboo shoots, involves harvesting bamboo shoots, immersing in ice water, then immersing in preservation solution, drying, immersing bamboo shoots in coating agent, drying to form film, storing and packing product.
AU LIAO Y
AE CHENGDU XINGFANG NIUWA FOOD CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2018401880
AB    NOVELTY - Method of preserving bamboo shoots, involves (a) harvesting bamboo shoots, immersing in 7-9 times ice water within 30 minutes after harvesting, stirring for 5 minutes and cooling to 3 degrees C within half an hour, (b) immersing the roots of bamboo shoots in preservation solution for 5-10 minutes, gently shaking residues and drying in cold air, where the preservation solution is a mixed solution comprising 0.45-0.65 wt.% carbendazim and 0.1-0.2 wt. sodium propionate, (d) dissolving 10-45 g chitosan in 2% citric acid solution (1 l) to obtain a coating agent, immersing bamboo shoots in the coating agent for 10-25 seconds, gently shaking the residues, allowing to stand and drying to form a film, (e) mixing oxygen and carbon dioxide gas, then doping with 5-30 vol.% nitrogen to prepare a mixture of oxygen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas in a volume ratio of 1.5:(8.5-2):3 for controlled atmosphere storage of bamboo shoots and packaging in food-grade foil bags.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving bamboo shoots.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The packaged bamboo shoots are stored in a cold storage at 1-4 degrees C.

GA 202260915P
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction, separation and analysis of nitrosamine compounds involves performing supercritical fluid extraction-supercritical fluid chromatography-mass spectrometry. The supercritical fluid extraction is performed under the conditions: an extraction solvent is carbon dioxide and modifier in a volume ratio of 100:0-95:5, an extraction solvent flow rate is 1-5 mL/minute, a static extraction time is 2-7 minutes, a dynamic extraction time is 1-4 minutes, a pressure of a pressure regulator during extraction is 15-18 MPa, and an extraction vessel temperature is 40-70 degrees C. The supercritical fluid chromatography is performed under the conditions: a mobile phase is carbon dioxide and modifier in a volume ratio of 95:5-75:25, a mobile phase flow rate of 1-3 mL/minute, a column comprising a functional group which is amide or amine, and a column temperature is 40-60 degrees C. The mass spectrometry is performed under the conditions: ionization method is atmospheric pressure chemical ionization.
   USE - Extraction, separation and analysis of nitrosamine compounds such as nitrosodimethylamine and nitrosodiethylamine used in pharmaceutical raw material and preparation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly, and minimizes amount of harmful organic solvents used in extraction and analysis using supercritical fluid.

The supercritical fluid extraction is performed under the conditions: an extraction solvent is carbon dioxide and modifier in a volume ratio of 100:0-95:5, an extraction solvent flow rate is 1-5 mL/minute, a static extraction time is 2-7 minutes, a dynamic extraction time is 1-4 minutes, a pressure of a pressure regulator during extraction is 15-18 MPa, and an extraction vessel temperature is 40-70 degrees C. The supercritical fluid chromatography is performed under the conditions: a mobile phase is carbon dioxide and modifier in a volume ratio of 95:5-75:25, a mobile phase flow rate of 1-3 mL/minute, a column comprising a functional group which is amide or amine, and a column temperature is 40-60 degrees C. The mass spectrometry is performed under the conditions: ionization method is atmospheric pressure chemical ionization, an atomization gas flow rate is 1-4 L/minute, an interface temperature is 200-500 degrees C, a desolvation line temperature is 100-300 degrees C, and a heating block temperature is 100-300 degrees C.

TI Cell-cultivation apparatus has gas permeable chamber with open end to accommodate culture medium, sealing member detachably mounted at open end, and growth substrate (e.g. porous matrices) disposed in gas permeable chamber.
AU CHANG K
   HUANG M
   ZHANG J
AE CESCO BIOENGINEERING CO LTD (CESC-Non-standard)
   CESCO BIOENGINEERING CO LTD (CESC-Non-standard)
GA 2021D9010C
AB    NOVELTY - Cell-cultivation apparatus (11) comprises a gas permeable chamber (101) with at least one open end to accommodate culture medium (12); a sealing member detachably mounted at the open end; and a growth substrate (105) disposed in the gas permeable chamber.
   USE - The apparatus is useful for culturing cells (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Provided is a simple but efficient cell culture method by slowly rotating the cell culture apparatus horizontally combining with a partially filled culture media in the gas permeable container to be able to expose the carriers and submerge the carriers intermittently, to achieve an optimal cell culture environment through the efficient oxygen transfer, carbon dioxide (CO2) balance, with sufficient nutrient supply, without vigorous agitation to achieve oxygen transfer. The horizontally rotation movement with the present cell growth apparatus is efficient for the nutrition, carbon dioxide and oxygen transfer during cultured process for production of cellular protein products, viruses or harvesting the cells. The method reduces waste accumulation by providing sufficient oxygen during culture, helps removing excess carbon dioxide during culture, helps stabilizing culture environment with a simple, reliable, inexpensive and efficient tool, and helps preventing detrimental effects on cells caused by air bubbles and gases. The method can reduce initial seeding density that is usually required in animal cell culture, and also can eliminate lag phase during initial growth period originally due to low inoculum density. Moreover, the method can efficiently remove carbon dioxide and stabilize pH during culture. It provides a method for an easier and more convenient way to produce and harvest secreted cellular products, such as protein, and/or antibiotics, and/or any cellular and/or tissue products from cell or tissue cultures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a device for rotating the cell-cultivation apparatus comprising a motor; a holder (104) disposed to hold the cell-cultivation apparatus; and a shaft connected between the motor and the holder to be driven by the motor to rotate the cell-cultivation apparatus; and
   (2) culturing cells with the cell-cultivation apparatus which involves partially filling the culture medium into the gas permeable chamber to cover the growth substrate; rotating the gas permeable chamber upside-down to expose the growth substrate from the culture medium; and rotating the gas permeable chamber upside-down to submerge the growth substrate in the culture medium.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of cell-cultivation apparatus.

PN CN112871220-A
TI Regenerating catalyst for preparing ethylbenzene comprises pressurizing the reactor with nitrogen, blowing heated nitrogen, incorporating the purified air, continuing heating the reactor, and completing the regeneration of catalyst.
AU SUN C
   LUAN B
AE SHANDONG CHAMBROAD PETROCHEMICALS CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202164136C
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating catalyst for preparing ethylbenzene comprises pressurizing the reactor with nitrogen for discharging the remaining liquid benzene in the reactor, heating the nitrogen, blowing the reactor with heated nitrogen, stopping the heating of nitrogen, and continuing blowing, incorporating the purified air, blowing with only purified air until the temperature of each bed has no change, passing the heated nitrogen into reactor, heating the reactor, controlling the flow of purified air, and controlling the volume content of oxygen in the reactor to 4-6%, continuing heating the reactor, controlling the volume content of oxygen in the reactor to 19-21% by controlling the flow of purified air, and continuing heating the reactor to 545-555 degrees C when the volume concentration of carbon dioxide in the reactor does not change and volume concentration of carbon dioxide is less than 0.05%, and completing the regeneration of catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for regenerating catalyst for preparing ethylbenzene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: consumes less time; and has better regeneration effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Regenerating catalyst for preparing ethylbenzene comprises (a) pressurizing the reactor with nitrogen for discharging the remaining liquid benzene in the reactor, (b) heating the nitrogen at 200-400 degrees C, blowing the reactor with heated nitrogen until the combustible gas in the exhaust gas discharged from the reactor lowers to less than 300 ppm, stopping the heating of nitrogen, and continuing blowing, (c) incorporating the purified air after the temperature of each bed in the reactor lowers to 40 degrees C, controlling the temperature increase rate in the reactor to not greater than 10 degrees C/hour, and blowing with only purified air until the temperature of each bed has no change, (d) passing the heated nitrogen into reactor again until the temperature of catalyst in each bed in the reactor increases to 295-305 degrees C, then heating the reactor to 445-455 degrees C, controlling the flow of purified air, and controlling the volume content of oxygen in the reactor to 4-6%, (e) continuing heating the reactor to 495-505 degrees C, and controlling the volume content of oxygen in the reactor to 19-21% by controlling the flow of purified air, and (f) continuing heating the reactor to 545-555 degrees C when the volume concentration of carbon dioxide in the reactor does not change and volume concentration of carbon dioxide is less than 0.05%, and completing the regeneration of catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon deposit content of catalyst in the reactor is 10-30 wt.%. The nitrogen flow rate for blowing the reactor with heated nitrogen is 300-1000 Nm3/hour. The flow of purified air when the purified air is incorporated is 40-50 Nm3/hour. The flow rate of the heated nitrogen gas is 400-600 Nm3/hour. Preferred Method: The step (d), (e) and (f), respectively comprises heating the reactor at 445-455 degrees C at a rate of 18-22 degrees C/hour, heating the reactor at 495-505 degrees C at a rate of 8-12 degrees C/hour, and heating the reactor at 545-555 degrees C at a rate of 20-30 degrees C/hour. The step (f) further comprises cooling the reactor to 35-45 degrees C at a rate of 48-52 degrees C/hour. The method comprises cooling the reactor to 35-45 degrees C, and blowing with nitrogen into the reactor until the purity of nitrogen in the reactor is not less than 99%.
