PT P
PN CN112645368-A
TI Resource utilization of waste magnesium oxide catalyst comprises drying waste magnesium oxide catalyst, crushing, sieving, then stirring with deionized water, introducing carbon dioxide gas, removing impurities and drying.
AU YUE Y
   WANG X
AE UNIV HEBEI ENG (UYEG-C)
GA 202143554P
AB    NOVELTY - Resource utilization of waste magnesium oxide catalyst comprises drying waste magnesium oxide catalyst, then crushing and sieving to obtain black waste magnesium oxide catalyst powder A, adding black waste magnesium oxide catalyst powder A to deionized water, and stirring completely to form slurry B, introducing carbon dioxide gas into slurry B, then stirring to obtain slurry C, removing impurities in the slurry C through at least one of vacuum filtration, centrifugal separation or plate and frame filter press to obtain clear solution D, stirring the solution D until there is no bubbles generation, allowing to stand, and cooling to room temperature to obtain white slurry E, filtering the slurry E through vacuum filtration, centrifugal separation or plate and frame pressure filtration to obtain filter cake, then drying the filter cake in an oven to obtain a recovered product of basic magnesium carbonate F.
   USE - The method is useful for resource utilization of waste magnesium oxide catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: saves resources; reuses the waste; does not causes secondary pollution; has high environmental and economic value. The basic magnesium carbonate: has high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Resource utilization of waste magnesium oxide catalyst comprises (a) drying waste magnesium oxide catalyst at 60-120 degrees C, then crushing and sieving the dried magnesium oxide to obtain black waste magnesium oxide catalyst powder A, (b) adding black waste magnesium oxide catalyst powder A to deionized water, and stirring completely at room temperature for 1-5 hours to form slurry B, where the mass ratio of black waste magnesium oxide catalyst powder A to deionized water is 1:5-1:500, (c) introducing carbon dioxide gas into slurry B, then stirring under the condition of high-speed stirring at more than 300 revolutions/minute to obtain slurry C, where the flow rate of carbon dioxide per 100 g waste magnesium oxide catalyst is controlled at 200-1200 ml/minute, (d) removing impurities in the slurry C through at least one of vacuum filtration, centrifugal separation or plate and frame filter press to obtain clear solution D, (e) stirring the solution D at 60-100 degrees C for 2-16 hours until there is no bubbles generation, allowing to stand at a constant temperature for 4-6 hours, and cooling to room temperature to obtain white slurry E, and (f) filtering the slurry E through vacuum filtration, centrifugal separation or plate and frame pressure filtration to obtain filter cake, then drying the filter cake in an oven at 60-150 degrees C for 6-48 hours to obtain a recovered product of basic magnesium carbonate F.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises grinding and sieving the magnesium oxide with 80-130 mesh. The method further comprises grinding the basic magnesium carbonate F after drying, then sieving through 200-280 mesh, vacuum packaging the sieved powder, and storing.

PN CN111097548-A
TI Alumina dry glue prepared by adding reaction liquid at bottom of coupled reactor and heating, introducing alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution into reactor from top of coupled reactor, and spraying carbon dioxide gas.
AU PENG C
   FANG X
   ZENG R
   WU Z
   FAN S
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
GA 202041614D
AB    NOVELTY - Alumina dry glue is claimed. The crystallinity of the alumina dry glue is greater than or equal to 90%. The maximum particle size of alumina dry glue is 70-80 mu m. The minimum particle size range is 1-5 mu m. In its particle size distribution, the amount of less-than20 mu m particle size is 85-90 wt.%. The amount of greater-than 20-40 mu m particle size is 8-13 wt.%. The amount of greater-than 40 mu m particle size is less-than 2 wt.%.
   USE - Used as alumina dry glue.
   ADVANTAGE - The dry glue has large pore size, high crystallinity and ideal pore structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: (i) preparing the alumina dry glue, comprising (i) adding reaction liquid at the bottom of the coupled reactor and heating to specific temperature, and starting the bottom stirring paddle, (ii) introducing alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution into the reactor from the top of the coupled reactor and introducing the carbon dioxide gas flow from the upper middle part of the coupled reactor, (iii) when carbon dioxide gas flow upwardly contacts the alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution in the three-dimensional mass transfer tray area, spraying carbon dioxide gas into fine droplets of alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution through gas cylinder and generating aluminum hydroxide crystal nuclei, (iv) conveying the aluminum hydroxide crystal nucleus obtained in the step (iii) into the cyclone settlement area and settling, (v) passing material through the cyclone settlement area, simultaneously adding acidic aluminum salt aqueous solution and alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution or alkaline precipitant simultaneously with the feed ports at the lower end of the reactor to form three impinging streams, adjusting the pH and conducting neutralization reaction, (vi) reacting the three streams of liquid and liquid hit the reactant stream, conveying the free fall zone and completing the stable phase of the neutralization reaction, (vii) aging, filtering, washing the dried reaction material obtained in the step (vi) to obtain high-crystallinity alumina dry glue; (2) and #use of alumina dry glue, comprising uniformly mixing alumina dry glue, small hole SB powder and Sesbania powder, adding an adhesive to obtain plastic body, extruding into a specific shape, and drying, roasting to obtain alumina carrier, impregnating obtained alumina carrier with a solution containing specific active metals, drying and calcining to obtain hydrotreating catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alumina dry glue has crystallinity of 93-98%. The coupling reactor from top to bottom includes gas-liquid three-dimensional mass transfer reaction zone, and cyclone settling zone and liquid-liquid impact reaction zone. The gas-liquid three-dimensional mass transfer reaction zone has trays, liquid collecting plates, plate holes, gas cylinders, mesh plates, caps and downcomers. The air cylinder is fixed on the liquid collecting plate. The position of the air cylinder corresponds to the position of the plate hole. A mesh plate is provided on the lift cylinder. A cap is provided on the top of the lift cylinder. The liquid collecting plate is connected with the lower end of the lift cylinder and the downcomer. Below the gas-liquid three-dimensional mass transfer reaction zone is the cyclone settlement zone, and below the cyclone settlement zone is the liquid-liquid impact reaction zone. The top of the liquid-liquid impingement reaction zone is directly connected to the bottom of the cyclone settling zone. The liquid-liquid impingement reaction zone is provided with 1-3 pairs of acceleration tubes horizontally symmetrically on both side walls of the top of the reaction zone. The other end is used as a material outlet or gas outlet to extend into the gas-liquid reaction zone. A pressure atomizing nozzle is installed at the material outlet to atomize the liquid. A porous gas nozzle is installed at the gas outlet. The liquid-liquid impact reaction zone also includes a settling drum and agitating paddles. The number of layers of mesh plates provided on each gas cylinder in the gas-liquid three-dimensional mass transfer reaction area is at least one layer. The shape of the mesh plate corresponds to the shape of the cap, and the mesh structure is a circular hole, strip hole or diamond hole. The downward baffles around the mesh plate, angle between the baffle plate and the mesh plate is 15-60 degrees . The gas cylinder is in the shape of a cuboid, a cube, a straight cylinder, or a cone bottom structure. The air cylinder has a number of holes in the cylinder wall above the liquid collecting plate, opening rate is greater than that of the tray. The opening rate of the cylinder wall of the lift cylinder and plate opening rate is greater-than 1.2. In the step (i), the heating temperature is 30-120 degrees C, and the rotation speed of the stirring blade is 1000-3000 revolutions/minutes. In the step (ii), the alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution is sodium aluminate or potassium aluminate aqueous solution. The concentration of the alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution is 8-100 g/l in terms of aluminum oxide. The flow rate of the alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution is 5-30 l/h, and the flow rate of the carbon dioxide is 1-30 l/hour. In the step (v), the acidic aluminum salt aqueous solution is aqueous solution of aluminum(III) chloride, aluminum sulfate or aluminum nitrate. The alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution is sodium aluminate or potassium aluminate aqueous solution. The alkaline precipitant is sodium hydroxide, ammonium carbonate or ammonium hydroxide aqueous solution. The concentration of the acidic aluminum salt solution in terms of aluminum oxide is 5-20 g/100 ml. The concentration of the alkali metal aluminate salt solution in terms of aluminum oxide is 10-35 g/100 ml. The active metal is selected from group VIII and group VIB metals of the periodic table. The VIB group metal component is molybdenum, tungsten or chromium. The group VIII metal is cobalt, nickel and/or iron. The precursors of active metals are water-soluble salt solutions of group VIII and VIB metals. The concentration of molybdenum trioxide and/or tungsten trioxide in the solution is 40-90 g/100 ml. The concentration of nickel oxide and/or cobalt(II) oxide is 10-30 g/100 ml. The hydrotreating catalyst includes active metal components are 1-8 wt.% group VIII including cobalt, nickel or iron, and VIB metal element including chromium, molybdenum and/or tungsten. Preferred Method: In the step (v), the pH range is 6-8. The time of the neutralization reaction is 6-25 minutes. The flow rates of the acidic aluminum salt aqueous solution and the alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution are 0.5-1.5 l/minute. In the step (vi), the aging pH range is 8-10. The stable time range is 5-20 minutes. The aging temperature range is 70-160 degrees C and the aging time is 0.5-2 hours. The method comprises using small SB powder, Sesbania powder and adhesive as addition agent in the art alumina carrier, where the adhesive is nitric acid aqueous solution or citric acid aqueous solution. The drying or firing conditions after forming or loading active metals are as follows drying temperature is 40-200 degrees C and drying for 1-8 hours, firing temperature is 350-900 degrees C, and the firing time is 2-8 hours.

PN CN110921615-A
TI Production of ammonia involves subjecting coal water slurry to gasification reaction to form crude synthesis gas, feeding gas into e.g. shift device to obtain hydrogen and subjecting hydrogen and nitrogen to ammonia synthesis reactor.
AU LI X
   SUN N
   SUN J
   LIU H
   MA H
   YUE J
   GUAN Q
   ZHANG B
AE BEIJING QINGCHUANGJINHUA TECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020259219
AB    NOVELTY - Production of ammonia involves generating superheated steam by boiler device provides the driving power for air separation device to separate air into at least oxygen and nitrogen, subjecting coal water slurry to gasification reaction and heat exchange treatment using separated oxygen as gasification agent to obtain crude synthesis gas and by-product high-pressure steam, feeding crude synthesis gas to shift device to obtain shift gas and by-products medium-pressure saturated steam and low-pressure saturated steam, feeding shift gas into low-temperature methanol washing device to remove at least hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide from shift gas to obtain purified gas, feeding purified gas into liquid nitrogen washing device to remove carbon monoxide and methane in purified gas to obtain hydrogen product, feeding separated nitrogen and hydrogen product into low-pressure ammonia synthesis reactor to carry out ammonia synthesis reaction under action of ZA-5 catalyst and cooling.
   USE - Production of ammonia.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of existing method such as long process, large area, high operating pressure, high risk factor, and high production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of ammonia involves generating superheated steam by boiler device provides the driving power for the air separation device to separate the air into at least oxygen and nitrogen by low-temperature separation technology, subjecting coal water slurry prepared by the coal water slurry preparation device to gasification reaction and heat exchange treatment in a high-pressure coal-water slurry radiation waste-pot gasification device using separated oxygen as gasification agent to obtain crude synthesis gas and by-product high-pressure steam, feeding crude synthesis gas to shift device for converting carbon monoxide in the crude synthesis gas into hydrogen to obtain shift gas and by-products medium-pressure saturated steam and low-pressure saturated steam, feeding shift gas into low-temperature methanol washing device to remove at least hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide from the shift gas to obtain purified gas, feeding purified gas into liquid nitrogen washing device to remove at least a small amount of carbon monoxide and methane in the purified gas to obtain a hydrogen product with carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide content of less than 10 ppm, feeding separated nitrogen and hydrogen product into low-pressure ammonia synthesis reactor to carry out ammonia synthesis reaction under action of ZA-5 catalyst and cooling the generated ammonia gas to produce liquid ammonia.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The reaction pressure of the gasification furnace combustion chamber of coal-water slurry radiation waste-pot type gasification device of 8.0-8.7 MPa. The pressure of the crude synthesis gas is 7.8-8.5 MPa, shift gas is 7.6-8.3 MPa, and purified gas is 7.5-8.2 MPa. The ammonia synthesis is carried out at temperature not more than 460 degrees C under pressure of 7.2-8.0 MPa with the feed molar ratio of hydrogen product and nitrogen is 2.0-2.5. Preferred Process: The superheated steam is obtained by pre-heating high-pressure steam obtained from the high-pressure coal water slurry radiation waste-pot gasification device to form superheated power steam which is used to provide driving power for the air separation device. The method further involves pressurizing purge gas discharged from the low-pressure ammonia synthesis reactor and returning to low-pressure ammonia synthesis tower.

PN CN110560894-A
TI Simultaneous protection of different protective            gases on both sides by using high-nitrogen steel            composite welding method comprises e.g. placing two            welding workpieces on copper plate, fixing on workbench            and forming gas retention space.
AU CUI B
   LIU F
   LIU S
   ZHANG F
   ZHANG H
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYCY-C)
GA 2019A6725N
AB    NOVELTY - Simultaneous protection of different                protective gases on both sides by using                high-nitrogen steel composite welding method,                comprises laser-arc welding high nitrogen steel                plate with argon-carbon dioxide as shielding gas,                placing two welding workpieces on a copper plate                with rectangular through grooves in the form of                butt joints, and fixing on the workbench together                with the copper plate, a joint gap is formed                between two welding workpieces, the laser beam from                the laser welding hair passes vertically through                the welding gap between two high nitrogen steel                plates, providing perpendicular to the front of the                workpiece to be welded, placing the welding gun on                the front of the workpiece to be welded, providing                95% argon and 5% carbon dioxide mixed protective                gas with coaxial output with the welding gun above                the workpiece, and forming a gas retention space by                the lower surface of the welding workpiece and the                rectangular through groove.
   USE - The method is useful for simultaneous                protection of different protective gases on both                sides by using high-nitrogen steel composite                welding method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases the nitrogen activity                outside the molten pool; and suppresses weld                porosity and nitrogen loss and thus droplets are                refined and the welding process is more                stable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Simultaneous protection of different                protective gases on both sides by using                high-nitrogen steel composite welding method,                comprises laser-arc welding high nitrogen steel                plate with argon-carbon dioxide as shielding gas,                placing two welding workpieces on a copper plate                with rectangular through grooves in the form of                butt joints, and fixing on the workbench together                with the copper plate, a joint gap is formed                between two welding workpieces, the laser beam from                the laser welding hair passes vertically through                the welding gap between two high nitrogen steel                plates, providing perpendicular to the front of the                workpiece to be welded, placing the welding gun on                the front of the workpiece to be welded, providing                95% argon and 5% carbon dioxide mixed protective                gas with coaxial output with the welding gun above                the workpiece, where the gas flow is 15-20                l/minutes, locating the rectangular through slot on                the copper plate directly below the welding gap                with the rectangular through slot opening facing                up, introducing nitrogen gas at both ends of the                slot, where the gas flow rate is 8-12 l/minutes,                and forming a gas retention space by the lower                surface of the welding workpiece and the                rectangular through groove.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The angle between the                  axis of the welding gun and the axis of the laser                  beam is 30 degrees . The laser beam emitted by                  the laser is any one of a carbon dioxide gas                  laser beam, a Nd:YAG solid laser beam, a                  semiconductor laser beam, a disc laser beam, or a                  fiber laser beam. Preferred Method: The laser                  power is 2-4 KW, and the defocus amount of the                  laser beam is -2 mm, type of welding torch is MIG                  or MAG, arc welding current of welding torch 180                  is -300 A, arc voltage is 22-30 V, arc length is                  12-15 mm, and the diameter of the welding wire of                  the welding gun is phi 1.2 mm. The heat source of                  composite welding is laser-TIG composite welding,                  laser-MIG composite welding, and laser-MAG                  composite welding.

PN IN201741039775-A
TI Apparatus for regeneration of carbon-rich solvent, comprises microwave chamber which is provided with Wetted Wall Column consists of inner tube connected with rich solution inlet and outer tube which is provided with lean solution outlet.
AU BALRAJ A
AE SRI SIVASUBRAMANIYA NADAR COLLEGE ENG (SRIS-Non-standard)
GA 201997542Y
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus comprises microwave chamber (106) which is provided with a Wetted Wall Column (WWC) consists of an inner tube (104) connected with rich solution inlet (101) and an outer tube which is provided with lean solution outlet (107). The two ports are provided in top of the outer tube (105). The another end of the outer tube is extended up to top of the microwave chamber wall and also connected with a water-cooled condenser (112). The peristaltic pump (102) is used for circulation of carbon dioxide loaded solution. The carbon-rich solvent is regenerated by pumping the carbon-rich solvent through the inner tube of WWC and flows downward on the outer surface of the inner tube as a thin film of liquid in presence of microwave energy and desired vacuum condition. The vapor temperature and column pressure is measured by using two ports of the outer tube. The rich solution (TR) inlet flow rate is measured by using a flow meter.
   USE - Apparatus for regeneration of carbon-rich solvent.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus has minimal energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Apparatus comprises microwave chamber (106) which is provided with a Wetted Wall Column (WWC) consists of an inner tube (104) connected with rich solution inlet (101) and an outer tube which is provided with lean solution outlet (107). The two ports are provided in top of the outer tube (105). The another end of the outer tube is extended up to top of the microwave chamber wall and also connected with a water-cooled condenser (112). The peristaltic pump (102) is used for circulation of carbon dioxide loaded solution. The carbon-rich solvent is regenerated by pumping the carbon-rich solvent through the inner tube of WWC and flows downward on the outer surface of the inner tube as a thin film of liquid in presence of microwave energy and desired vacuum condition. The vapor temperature and column pressure is measured by using two ports of the outer tube. The rich solution (TR) inlet flow rate is measured by using a flow meter. The released carbon dioxide (CO2) and the water vapor (Tc) are passed through the condenser to condensate the condensable water vapor (Tc) and non-condensable carbon dioxide (CO2) which is captured and stored in a container.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of apparatus.
   Rich solution inlet (101)
   Peristaltic pump (102)
   Inner tube (104)
   Outer tube (105)
   Microwave chamber (106)

   Water-cooled condenser (112)

   GALLANDT N
AE ECOLE POLYTECHNIQUE FEDERALE LAUSANNE (EPFL-C)
   GAZNAT SA (GAZN-Non-standard)

   GAZNAT SA (GAZN-Non-standard)
   ECOLE POLYTECHNIQUE FEDERALE LAUSANNE (EPFL-C)
   GAZNAT SA (GAZN-Non-standard)
GA 201971218B
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical reactor comprises a catalyst bed                enclosed in a reactor vessel and at least one                cooling tube placed in the reactor vessel and                passing through the catalyst bed, where the cooling                tubes are disposed within the reactor so as to                generate thermal gradients of at least 20 degrees                C/cm, to generate hot spots throughout the reactor                upon carrying out a reaction.
   USE - The chemical reactor is useful for producing                methane from hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a methanation reactor system (1) including                a reactor vessel (2) comprising a housing (10)                defining a reaction chamber (12) comprising a gas                loading zone (18) and a catalyzed reaction zone, a                cooling system, a gas flow system, a heating                element in the vicinity of the reaction chamber, a                temperature sensing system and a temperature                management system, the catalyzed reaction zone of                the reaction chamber comprises a catalyst bed, the                cooling system comprises many coolant circulation                lines within the catalyst bed, the gas flow system                comprises a reaction gas feeding system configured                to load reaction gases into the loading zone of the                reaction chamber and a reaction gas exhaust system                to exhaust reacted gases through the catalyst bed                which is thermo-regulated by the heating element                and the cooling system under the control of the                temperature management system; and
   (2) producing methane from hydrogen and carbon                dioxide, comprising (a) providing a chemical                reactor comprising a reaction chamber which                comprises a gas loading zone and a catalyzed                reaction zone comprising a catalyst bed, (b)                loading a reaction gas mixture of hydrogen and                carbon dioxide in the gas loading zone of the                reaction chamber, so that the gas pressure in the                reaction chamber is 1-20 bar, (c) heating the                reaction chamber, preferably the catalyst bed at a                temperature of 220-260 degrees C so that the                Sabatier reaction and a gas flow through the                catalyst bed start, (d) creating a temperature                gradient of 100 degrees C within the reaction                chamber and preferably within the catalyst bed by                cooling the catalyst bed with a cooling system                directly integrated in the catalyst bed, and (e)                collecting the resulting gas mixture (methane and                water) flowing through the catalyst bed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of                methanation reactor.
   Methanation reactor system (1)
   Reactor vessel (2)
   Housing (10)
   Reaction chamber (12)
   Gas loading zone (18)

The gas flow system                  allows loading the reaction gases (i.e. hydrogen                  gas and/or carbon dioxide) within the gas loading                  zone of the reaction chamber through a reaction                  gas feeding system comprising pressure regulating                  elements and exhausting the reacted gases (i.e.                  methane and water vapor) from the catalyzed                  reaction zone of the reaction chamber through a                  reaction gas exhaust system comprising pressure                  regulating elements.

PT P
PN CN110106371-A
TI Recycling metal indium material from slag comprises e.g. taking an specific amount of slag for crushing treatment, ball milling, leaching, extracting impurities in the crude indium material and extracting pure indium material.
AU LI P
   XIE Y
   LIANG L
AE LIUZHOU CHENGAO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LIUZ-Non-standard)
GA 201970656U
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling metal indium material from slag comprises e.g.(i) taking an specific amount of slag for crushing treatment, adding zinc oxide into the slag, ball milling, and performing neutral leaching, (ii) mixing ball slag and flocculant to obtain mixed solution of slag and water, (iii) injecting mixed solution of slag and water into absorption tower, injecting carbon dioxide gas into the absorption tower, allowing the mixed solution of slag and water to absorb enough carbon dioxide to form a precipitate of mixed salt, and separating the mother liquor to obtain crude product, (iv) adding the mixed salt into a slurry, adding specific amount of a collector, a foaming agent, inhibitor, flotation mixed salt, separating the mixed salt, and filtering, and (v) immersing crude indium material in water, extracting the impurities in the crude indium material respectively, extracting zinc and iron from the mixed liquid by P204 with the organic extracting agent, and extracting pure indium material.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling metal indium material from slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is energy saving; and the product has high purity and good ductility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling metal indium material from slag comprises (i) taking an specific amount of slag for crushing treatment, adding zinc oxide directly into the slag, ball milling with ball mill, and performing neutral leaching, (ii) mixing the ball slag and the flocculant according to a mass ratio of 1:15, and mixing uniformly to obtain mixed solution of slag and water, (iii) injecting a mixed solution of slag and water into the absorption tower, injecting carbon dioxide gas into the absorption tower at a rate of 6-8 l/minute for reaction, allowing the mixed solution of slag and water to absorb enough carbon dioxide to form a precipitate of mixed salt, and separating the mother liquor by filtration to obtain crude product having a mixed salt as a main component, (iv) adding the mixed salt into a slurry having a concentration of 40-75%, and adding specific amount of a collector, a foaming agent, an inhibitor, a flotation mixed salt, separating the mixed salt from the indium material, and filtering out the mixed salt to obtain crude indium material, and (v) immersing the crude indium material in water, respectively, extracting the impurities in the crude indium material respectively, extracting the zinc and iron from the mixed liquid by P204 with the organic extracting agent, and extracting the pure indium material separately by P204.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The collector is fatty amine. The foaming agent is a mixed alcohol having 5-10 carbon atoms. The P204 is an acidic extractant which is insoluble in water and soluble in acetone and ethanol organic solvents.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); P41 (Crushing: centrifuging, separating solids (B02, B03, B04).)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The plasma generator                  comprises a first second plasma generator. The                  plasma generator and the first plasma generator                  are used for discharge exciting carbon dioxide to                  generate oxygen and carbon monoxide. The pressure is generated by                  high frequency power supply to adjust the energy                  first high according to the monitoring result, to                  make it reach discharge condition of high oxygen                  content. Two cylindrical                  conductor shell are air chamber of outer wall and                  inner wall. The                  catalytic function unit comprises a                  yttria-stabilized zirconia or cerium oxide made                  of ventilate layer. PN CN113430539-A; CN113430539-B
TI Improving carbon dioxide conversion rate when            using electrolysis system to electrolyze and reduce            carbon dioxide, comprises heating electrolytic cell,            adding catalyst to pores, contacting first anode layer            and second anode layer with air, and passing mixed gas            of carbon dioxide and water.
AU GUAN W
   WANG J
   YANG J
   CHEN L
AE NINGBO INST MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY & ENG C (CANM-C)
GA 2021B43705
AB    NOVELTY - Improving carbon dioxide conversion rate when                using electrolysis system to electrolyze and reduce                carbon dioxide, comprises heating the electrolytic                cell by heating unit to make temperature of                electrolytic cell reach above 600degrees Celsius,                adding catalyst to pores, contacting first anode                layer and second anode layer with air, passing                mixed gas of carbon dioxide and water into pores,                turning on direct current power supply, and                electrolytic reduction occurs to obtain CO and H2;                catalyst uses gamma-aluminum oxide as carrier,                copper atoms and iron atoms are adsorbed on surface                of carrier, and are denoted as CuFe/aluminum oxide.                The electrolysis system includes direct current                power supply, electrolysis cell, and heating unit;                electrolytic cell is solid oxide electrolytic cell;                electrolytic cell has cathode as supporting layer                and has up-and-down distribution structure, that                is, in electrolytic cell, anode layer, electrolyte                layer, and active cathode layer are stacked up and                down.
   USE - The method is useful for improving carbon                dioxide conversion rate when using electrolysis                system to electrolyze and reduce carbon dioxide,                used in electrochemical process and high                temperature solid oxide electrolytic process                field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses gamma-aluminium oxide as                carrier, and CuFe/Al2O3 with Cu atoms and Fe atoms                adsorbed on the surface of the carrier as the                catalyst; turns on DC power supply to generate an                electrolytic reduction reaction to obtain CO and                H2, which can ensures normal electrical performance                under the presence of the catalyst, at same time,                greatly improves conversion rate of CO2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The active cathode                  layer includes first active cathode layer and                  second active cathode layer, the first active                  cathode layer is located on upper surface of                  cathode support layer, and second active cathode                  layer is located on the lower surface of the                  cathode support layer; the electrolyte layer                  includes first electrolyte layer and second                  electrolyte layer, the first electrolyte layer is                  located on the upper surface of the first active                  cathode layer, and the second electrolyte layer                  is located on the lower surface of the second                  active cathode layer; the anode layer includes                  first anode layer and second anode layer, the                  first anode layer is located on the upper surface                  of the first electrolyte layer, the second anode                  layer is located on the lower surface of the                  second electrolyte layer; the cathode support                  layer is provided with pores for fuel gas                  circulation; the first anode layer and the second                  anode layer are electrically connected through a                  connector, and the cathode support layer, the                  first active cathode layer and the second active                  cathode layer are electrically connected through                  a current collector; the anode of the DC power                  supply is electrically connected to the                  connecting member, and the cathode is                  electrically connected to the current                  collector.INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The                  preparation of catalyst comprises (i) weighing                  copper nitrate trihydrate and ferric nitrate                  nonahydrate crystal hydrate in beaker, adding                  deionized water and stirring to make it fully                  dissolved to obtain a precursor solution; (ii)                  using porous gamma-aluminum oxide as carrier,                  weighing gamma-aluminum oxide and adding it to                  precursor solution, so that Cu2+ and Fe3+ are                  adsorbed on the surface of gamma-aluminum oxide,                  and drying to evaporate the solvent; and (iii)                  heating the dried product obtained in step (ii),                  and passing in H2 for reduction treatment,                  cooling, taking it out to obtain CuFe/aluminum                  oxide. In the method, reducing protective gas is                  also passed into the pores.Preferred Components: The reducing                  protective gas includes H2 and/or CO; the volume                  content of the reducing protective gas is 5-30                  vol.%. The first barrier layer is arranged                  between the first electrolyte layer and the first                  anode layer, second barrier layer is arranged                  between the second electrolyte layer and the                  second anode layer. The first electrolyte                  material is YSZ, SSZ, and/or LSGM; the second                  electrolyte material is YSZ, SSZ, and/or LSGM;                  the material of the first support layer is                  Ni-YSZ, LSM-YSZ, and/or LSCF-YSZ; the material of                  the second support layer is Ni-YSZ, LSM-YSZ,                  and/or LSCF-YSZ. The first barrier layer is GDC;                  the second barrier layer is GDC; the first anode                  layer material is LSM, LSCF, and/or BSFC; the                  second anode layer material is LSM, LSCF, and/or                  BSFC; the content of water vapor in mixed gas is                  not less than 5%.

PN CN112798364-A
TI Method for accurately detecting ratio of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide on top of blast furnace, involves adjusting blast furnace fuel coke ratio to maintain carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide ratio at preset value.
AU SHI H
   LIANG J
   SHI R
   HE L
   XU C
AE LEIYANG YANXIN NON-FERROUS METAL CO LTD (LEIY-Non-standard)
GA 202157452W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves opening sampling ports around the flue pipe on the top surface of an upper furnace cover of a blast furnace. A sampling pipe is utilized to move the surface of the material block closest to the top of the blast furnace, and to penetrate into the gap of the material block, and to extract the unoxidized flue gas in the gap of the material block. The carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide ratio in the flue gas is detected. The blast furnace fuel coke ratio is adjusted to maintain the carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide ratio at 0.95-1.05 according to the detection result of the carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide ratio. The coke ratio is reduced when carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide ratio is greater than 1.05.
   USE - Method for accurately detecting ratio of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide on top of blast furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is simple, effective, and easy to implement. The productivity is improved by more than 30% and can find out whether there is a lack of coke in the furnace in time, and can prevent the accident of furnace death due to lack of coke. The blast furnace operation is optimized and has tremendous effect in improving and stabilizing productivity.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram illustrating the process for accurately detecting ratio of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide on top of blast furnace.
   Blast furnace hearth (1)
   Melting material in hearth (2)
   Blast furnace tube (3)
   Blast furnace bottom coke layer (4)
   Blast furnace tube water sleeve portion (5)

TI Solid handling vessel comprises separation tank having inlet for tangentially receiving inflow of carbon dioxide enriched hydrocarbon feed stream, heating assembly, gas outlet, and collection tank in fluid communication with separation tank.
AU MD JALIL A A M
   ROSTANI K
   OTHMAN N A
   MD J A A M
AE PETRONAS PETROLIAM NASIONAL BERHAD (PETR-Non-standard)
   PETRONAS PETROLIAM NASIONAL BERHAD (PETR-Non-standard)
   PETRONAS PETROLIAM NASIONAL BERHAD (PETR-Non-standard)
GA 2020C0731B
AB    NOVELTY - Solid handling vessel comprises: a separation tank having an inlet for tangentially receiving an inflow of carbon dioxide enriched hydrocarbon feed stream, where the separation tank is used to facilitate cyclonical flow of the feed stream; a heating assembly within the separation tank for maintaining the feed stream above a temperature for solidification of carbon dioxide; a gas outlet arranged to vent gas from the separation tank; and a collection tank located below, and in fluid communication with, the separation tank, where the collection tank is arranged to receive separated liquid and outflow the liquid from a liquid outlet.
   USE - As solid handling vessel.
   ADVANTAGE - The solid handling vessel balances the efficiency of removing a high proportion of the acidic gas from the original feed stream and managing the solid phase separated flow. The solid handling vessel maintains the efficiency of the separation process, and reduces hydrocarbon loss or enhances carbon dioxide separation in hydrocarbon gas mixture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of separating a gas component from a carbon dioxide enriched hydrocarbon feed stream, comprising tangentially introducing the feed stream into a separation tank, cyclonically flowing of the feed stream so as to separate a portion of carbon dioxide gas, venting the carbon dioxide gas from a gas outlet, heating a separated liquid within the separation tank above a temperature for solidification of carbon dioxide, and flowing the separated liquid from the separation tank to a collection tank, and outflowing the liquid from a liquid outlet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The solid handling vessel further comprises: a baffle plate intermediate the separation tank and collection tank for contacting the separated liquid so as to hinder the cyclonic flow; and a heat exchange assembly contained with the collection tank, where the heat exchange assembly is arranged to maintain a temperature of liquid within the collection tank above a temperature for solidification of carbon dioxide. The heat exchange assembly comprise tubes within a shell, where the shell is arranged to receive the liquid within the collection tank and the tubes are arranged to flow a heat transfer medium on an inside bore of the tubes. The heat transfer medium comprises a portion of the liquid outflow from the collection tank. Preferred Method: The method further comprises: contacting the separated liquid within the separation tank with a baffle plate intermediate the separation tank and collection tank so as to hinder the cyclonic flow; maintaining a temperature of the separated liquid within the collection tank above a temperature for solidification of carbon dioxide using a heat exchange assembly contained with the collection tank; and flowing a heat transfer medium in a bore of tubes of the heat exchange assembly, where the tubes are located within a shell arranged to receive the separated liquid.

   LIAO Z

   YIN T
AE YANTAI INST COASTAL ZONE RES SUSTAINABLE (CNYT-C)
GA 202093587D
AB    NOVELTY - Marine carbon dioxide online monitoring method                based on electrochemical sensing comprises (i)                pumping sea water to the electrode flow cell,                waiting for the timing serial port to feedback, and                collecting the electrode potential signal EXr,                pumping sea water to the pH salinity flow cell, and                detecting the pH value, temperature t, and                conductivity C of the sea water in real time                through the pH sensor and temperature sensor; (ii)                calculating the partial pressure of carbon dioxide;                (a) using international oceanographic tables to                determine the relationship between seawater                conductivity C, temperature t and salinity S, and                calculate seawater salinity S; (b) determining the                functional relationship between pH-T by measuring                the pH at the same interval temperature (c)                determining by Debye-Huckel formula and multiple                curve fitting: (d) calculating seawater carbonate                solubility CXr according to electrode working                curve; and (e) calculating the partial pressure of                seawater carbon dioxide.
   USE - Used as marine carbon dioxide online                monitoring method based on electrochemical                sensing.
   ADVANTAGE - The requires less samples; has simple                operation, precision and stability; ensures the                long-term stability and accurate monitoring of                data; can be equipped with observation platforms                e.g. buoys, stations, and monitoring ships for                long-term marine water monitoring.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                marine carbon dioxide online monitoring device                based on electrochemical sensing.

The ultra-critical carbon dioxide turbine is rotated to drive a generator (2) to generate electricity. The working substance at an outlet of the ultra-critical carbon dioxide enters into a high-temperature regenerator (10). A main compressor (6) and a re-compressor (7) are driven by a gear box (5) for compressing the working substance. A portion of the working substance enters into a cooler (8) to cool and the main compressor to compress according to splitting coefficient. PN WO2019191388-A1; DE112019001660-T5; US2021016231-A1; JP2021518261-W
TI Thin-film composite membrane for separating composition comprising carbon dioxide and non-hydrophilic gas, has porous-layer support, high-diffusion rate layer and gas-separation layer, and has preset carbon dioxide permeance.
AU SHANGGUAN N
   NEMSER S M
   MAJUMDAR S
   FEIRING A E
AE COMPACT MEMBRANE SYSTEMS INC (COMP-Non-standard)
   COMPACT MEMBRANE SYSTEMS INC (COMP-Non-standard)
   COMPACT MEMBRANE SYSTEMS INC (COMP-Non-standard)
GA 201983277H
AB    NOVELTY - A thin-film composite membrane (10) has a porous-layer support (70), a nonporous high-diffusion rate layer (50) that is coplanar and in direct contact with porous-layer support, and a gas-separation layer (30) that is nonporous, hydrophilic and coplanar and in direct contact with nonporous high-diffusion rate layer. The thin-film composite membrane has carbon dioxide permeance of (500x 106)x cm3 (STP)/cm2/second/cmHg (500-GPU) and carbon dioxide to non-hydrophilic gas selectivity that is greater than intrinsic selectivity of the gas-separation layer.
   USE - Thin-film composite membrane is used for separating gaseous composition comprising carbon dioxide and non-hydrophilic gas e.g. methane, ethane, oxygen, nitrogen and hydrogen (all claimed), generated from electrical power plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The economical thin-film composite membrane has excellent durability, chemical resistance, thermal stability, mechanical strength and resistance to degradation from contact with liquid water and long-term permeance and selectivity, and is capable of separating carbon dioxide from gaseous composition comprising carbon dioxide and non-hydrophilic gas with reduced consumption of energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for process of separating gaseous composition comprising carbon dioxide and non-hydrophilic gas using the thin-film composite membrane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of high-permeance and high-selectivity thin-film composite membrane.
   Thin-film composite membrane (10)
   Nonporous gas-separation layer (30)
   Nonporous high-diffusion-rate layer (50)
   Porous-layer support (70)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The gas-separation layer comprises repeating units (A) and (B) in which repeating unit (A) is a polymerized derivative of fluorinated monomer and repeating unit (B) comprises hydrophilic groups. The precursor of repeating unit (B) is fluoromonomer of formula: F2C=CFOCF2CF(CF3)OCF2CF2SO2F (I) or F2C=CFOCF2CF2SO2F (II). The repeating unit (A) is chosen from tetrafluoroethylene, chlorotrifluoroethylene, trifluoroethylene, vinyl fluoride, vinylidene fluoride and perfluoro(2,2-dimethyl-1,3-dioxole). Preferred Property: The gas-separation layer has thickness of less than 1 pm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The nonporous high-diffusion-rate layer comprises a copolymer comprising perfluoro(2,2-dimethyl-1,3-dioxole) or copolymer comprising perfluoro(2,2-dimethyl-1,3-dioxole) and tetrafluoroethylene.

TI Turbine expander for expanding working medium e.g.            supercritical carbon dioxide in thermodynamic cycle            system, has annular rectifying channel defined by            intake rectifying cavity whose cross section decreases            gradually.
AU LIN X
   ZHONG M
   ZHANG X
   LI L
   TIAN H
AE XECA TURBO BEIJING TECHNOLOGIES CO LTD (XECA-Non-standard)
   UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2022B5547T
AB    NOVELTY - The expander (1) has a turbine section (10)                used to convert thermal energy of the working                medium into kinetic energy. A motor section (20) is                used to convert the kinetic energy into electrical                energy. A turbine disc (14) is mounted on a main                shaft. The blades arranged on the outer periphery                of a turbine disc main structure receive the                working medium flowing out from the nozzle flow                channel and drive the turbine disc and the main                shaft to rotate under the action of the working                medium. The exhaust runner is configured to                discharge the expanded working medium. An intake                runner (11) extends along the axial direction of                the main shaft and whose cross-sectional diameter                gradually increases as approaching an intake                rectifying cavity (12). The cross-section of the                annular rectifying channel defined by the intake                rectifying cavity and the rectifying cover                decreases gradually as approaching an intake nozzle                ring (13).
   USE - Turbine expander for expanding working medium                e.g. supercritical carbon dioxide in thermodynamic                cycle system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The blade height of the turbine is increased,                the speed of the expander is effectively reduced,                the processing difficulty is reduced, the                requirements of the expander on bearings and motors                are reduced, and the processing cost of the device                is reduced. The transmission efficiency is ensured,                and the turbine and motor of the entire expander                are fully sealed in the expander casing, which                completely solves the problem of external leakage                of the semi-hermetic expander. The heat generated                by the motor is cooled to improve the use                environment of the motor and prolong the service                life.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of                the supercritical carbon dioxide turbine                expander.

TI Double-wire carbon dioxide gas protection backing            welding method for box-shaped component, involves            removing arc striking plate and arc extinguishing plate            after completing welding of welding seams of box-shaped            component, and lifting box-shaped component to            conveying roller by shifting machine.
AU MA J
   KUANG X
   ZHOU Y
   LIU G
   ZHOU G
AE ZHEJIANG SOUTHEAST SPACE FRAME LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202292967Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves placing a pair of flange                plates on the left and right sides. An upper web is                placed between two flange plates up and down. A                column sealing plate is arranged at the front and                rear ends of flange plate and the web to form a                box-shaped component (7). The assembled box-shaped                component is sent to the side of a mobile welding                assembly (5) through several conveying rollers                (10). The box-shaped component is lifted and                traversed to a supporting platform (8) between the                movable welding assembly and conveying roller by a                steel shifting machine (9) between two adjacent                conveying rollers. The steel moving machine is                descended and retreated to other end of the                conveying roller. An arc striking plate (6) and arc                extinguishing plate are removed after completing                the welding of four welding seams of the box-shaped                component. The box-shaped component is lifted to                the conveying roller by the steel shifting machine.                The box-shaped component is discharged through the                conveying roller.

PN CN114180611-A
TI Preparing light calcium carbonate from industrial waste containing calcium oxide, comprises e.g. crushing and grinding industrial waste to obtain industrial waste powder, adding organic acid solution into powder and stirring and reacting.
AU LAI D
   ZENG H
   XI X
   ZHAN J
   FENG H
AE SICHUAN POFESO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2022436930
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises crushing and grinding the industrial waste to obtain industrial waste powder. An organic acid solution is added into the industrial waste powder and stirred and reacted, then orderly clarified and filtered to obtain a first filter residue and a first filtrate. The pH value of the first filtrate is adjusted to 8-9, then heated, stood, clarified and filtered. The pH value of the obtained filtrate is adjusted to 10-12, then stood, clarified and filtered to obtain a second filtrate and a second filter residue. A carbon dioxide gas with purity not less than 10 wt.% is introduced into the second filtrate. The reaction pressure is 1 kPa to 1MPaG. The carbon dioxide gas introduction is stopped when the pH value of the second filtrate is not more than 7 to separate solid and liquid, and obtain light calcium carbonate and third filtrate solid phase.
   USE - Method for preparing light calcium carbonate. Uses include but are not limited to rubber, plastic, paper making, coating, medical healthcare, pesticide and food.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, uses cheap industrial waste containing calcium dioxide as raw material, which greatly saves the cost, can consume a large amount of industrial waste and industrial carbon dioxide waste gas, effectively solves the problem that the industrial waste pollutions the environment, and the obtained light calcium carbonate completely meets the related industry standard.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for light calcium carbonate prepared by the method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the method of preparing light calcium carbonate. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic acid comprises formic acid, acetic acid or propionic acid.

PN CN113943006-A
TI Preparing high reinforcing white carbon black,            comprises e.g. adding aqueous alkali solution and            surfactant into sodium silicate solution, heat            preservating, adding carbon dioxide, mixing, aging,            performing solid-liquid separation on obtained slurry,            washing, drying, crushing obtained filter cake.
AU SHAO J
   XU L
   YANG C
   SHEN J
AE ANHUI EVOSIL NANO MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2022188296
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high reinforcing white carbon black,                comprises, (a) adding aqueous alkali solution and                surfactant into sodium silicate solution at                40-95degrees Celsius, carrying out heat                preservation for 30-60 minutes, maintaining at                40-95degrees Celsius, introducing carbon dioxide                until the pH of the system drops to 7.5-10.5,                mixing for 30-60 minutes, and then introducing                carbon dioxide until the pH drops to 5-6.5 and                aging to obtain slurry, and (b) performing                solid-liquid separation on obtained slurry and                washing to obtain solid content of 20-40% white                carbon black filter cake, drying and crushing the                white carbon black filter cake to obtain white                carbon black with a particle size of 2-6 microm,                where the preparation of the sodium silicate                solution includes: treating mixture of biomass ash,                sodium hydroxide and water at a pressure of 0.7-1.5                MPa and a temperature of 150-250degrees Celsius for                3-6 hours, filtering to obtain sodium silicate                solution with a modulus of 1.5-3.5.
   USE - The high reinforcing white carbon black is                useful in reinforcing silicone rubber products                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: prepares high reinforcing white                carbon black used for reinforcing silicone rubber                products, and can significantly improve the                hardness and tensile strength of silicone rubber                products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) High reinforcing white carbon black                prepared by above method; and
   (2) Use of high reinforcing white carbon black                in reinforcing silicone rubber, where the amount of                high-reinforcing white carbon black is 20-50 wt.%                of the raw silicone rubber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio between                  biomass ash, sodium hydroxide and water is                  1-5:2-4:3-10, where the biomass ash includes corn                  stalk ash, wheat stalk ash and/or rice stalk ash.                  The alkali includes sodium carbonate, potassium                  carbonate, sodium hydroxide and/or potassium                  hydroxide; the mass concentration of alkali in                  the alkali aqueous solution is 10-12 wt.% and the                  addition amount of the alkali aqueous solution is                  0.1-10 wt.% of the sodium silicate solution. The                  surfactant includes polyethylene glycol 6000,                  sodium dodecyl sulfonate, n-octanol,                  gamma-aminopropyltriethoxysilane,                  gamma-(methacryloyloxy)propyltrimethoxysilane,                  bis-(gamma-triethoxysilylpropyl)tetrasulfide                  and/or cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, where the                  addition amount of surfactant is 0.01-2 wt.%                  sodium silicate solution.Preferred Method: The adding time of the                  aqueous alkali solution is controlled at 10-60                  minutes. The flow rate of carbon dioxide is                  20-200 ml/minute when carbon dioxide is                  introduced for the first time, the flow rate of                  carbon dioxide is 150-250 ml/minute when carbon                  dioxide is introduced for the second time, where                  the flow rate of the second introduction of                  carbon dioxide is greater than the flow rate of                  the first introduction of carbon dioxide. In the                  aging step, aging is performed for 30-60 minutes                  under the condition of maintaining the pressure.                  The drying treatment is pressure spray drying,                  the drying temperature is 450-600degrees Celsius                  and the crushing treatment is airflow crushing                  treatment.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The method further involves adding the fresh feed stream to the SO reactor by adding two or more feed streams separately to the SO reactor, adjusting an amount of the steam feed stream added to the SO reactor to increase a selectivity for acetic acid, using a flue gas stream from a combustion process as the carbon dioxide feed stream, using a natural gas stream from a pipeline as the methane feed stream, separating the recycled water (106) stream from the acetic acid, and combining at least a portion of the recycled water stream with the steam feed stream, forming the acetic acid in the SO reactor by placing the fresh feed stream in contact with an SO catalyst comprising molybdenum-vanadium-tellurium-niobium-palladium oxide of formula: MoaVbTecNbdPdeOf (I), where a, b, c, d, e and f are relative atomic amounts of the elements Mo, V, Te, Nb, Pd, and O, when a is 1, b is 0.01-1.0, c is 0-1.0, d is 0-1.0, e is 0-0.10, and f is a number to at least satisfy the valence state of the metal elements in the SO catalyst, producing the acetic acid product stream in one reactor of several parallel SO reactors, and producing an ethylene product stream in at least one other reactor of the several parallel SO reactors, and combining the acetic acid product stream from the one reactor of several of parallel SO reactors with a feed stream provided to the at least one other reactor of the several of parallel SO reactors. Preferred Apparatus: The reactor system comprises several SO reactors which are selectively coupled to either one of two scrubbers comprising a scrubber (s1) configured to separate the reactor effluent into the acetic acid product stream and the gas stream, and to return the gas stream to an SO reactor coupled to the scrubber (s1), or a scrubber (s2) configured to separate the reactor effluent into a liquid stream and the gas stream and provide the gas stream to a subsequent SO reactor coupled to the scrubber (s2). The acetic acid product stream from the scrubber (s1) is coupled to an SO reactor producing ethylene. The methane feed line is coupled to a natural gas pipeline to provide methane to the SO reactor. The methane feed line is coupled to a fractionator providing ethane. The carbon dioxide feed line is coupled to a carbon dioxide pipeline. The carbon dioxide feed line is coupled to a flue gas line from a combustion process. The reactor system further comprises of a process skid comprising the SO reactor and the scrubber.

PN CN113713635-A
TI Metal organic framework composite film contains            amine ligand used for carbon dioxide/nitrogen dioxide            system separation in carbon capture field, comprises            2-methyl imidazole ligand of film replaced by amino            ligand as 3-amino-1,2,4-triazole.
AU LIU Y
   WU H
   JIANG Z
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2021E6339S
AB    NOVELTY - Metal organic framework ZIF-8 composite film                contains amine ligands comprises 2-methylimidazole                ligand of ZIF-8 composite film replaced by amino                ligand, where amino ligands are                3-amino-1,2,4-triazole. The ZIF-8 composite film is                endowed with amidocyanogen group of affinity carbon                dioxide (CO2), reaching high efficiency separation                of CO2; ZIF-8 composite film is prepared by aqueous                phase electro-deposition ligand post-exchange                two-step preparation, where ZIF-8 composite film                thickness is 300-380 nanometer (nm) and exchange                rate of amino ligand replacing 2-methyl imidazole                ligand is 3-29 %
   USE - Metal organic framework ZIF-8 composite film                contains amine ligand used for carbon                dioxide/nitrogen system separation in carbon                capture field, under condition of 25degrees                Celsius, raw material gas pressure is 1 bar, CO2                permeation rate is 1055-1353 GPU, CO2/N2                selectivity is 19-58 (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, mild and controllable.                The prepared ZIF-8 composite film contains amino                ligand has high throughput, high selectivity,                CO2/nitrogen (N2) separation performance reaches                industrial performance requirement, and has good                application prospect in flue gas                decarburization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing metal organic framework ZIF-8                composite film contains amino ligand, which                involves: (a) soaking anode alumina base film in                ZIF-8 precursor solution as cathode; preparing                ZIF-8 composite film by aqueous phase                electro-deposition under constant current, then                placing ZIF-8 composite film in                3-amino-1,2,4-triazole solution for solvent heat                exchange to realize ligand exchange; (c) washing,                and drying by methanol to obtain ZIF-8 composite                film contains amine ligand.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for preparing                  metal organic framework ZIF-8 composite film                  contains amine group ligand involves taking anode                  alumina as base film and spraying platinum on                  surface, preparing 2-methyl imidazole aqueous                  solution with mol concentration of 1 Molar (M)                  marking as solution A, preparing                  Zn(CH3COO)2asterisk2H2O aqueous solution with                  molar concentration as 0.083 M marking as                  solution B; mixing solution A and solution B                  according to volume ratio of 5:1, stirring                  uniformly to form ZIF-8 precursor solution for                  cathode deposition, taking anode alumina-based                  film after spraying platinum treatment on surface                  as cathode, taking carbon paper as anode, soaking                  in ZIF-8 precursor solution under current density                  of 0.13 megaampere/cm2, depositing for 1 hour,                  fully soaking synthesized ZIF-8 composite film                  with de-ionized water and methanol then placing                  at room temperature for drying; adding                  3-amino-1,2,4-triazole into methanol solvent,                  preparing solution with mol concentration of 0.06                  M as post- exchange ligand solution, soaking                  ZIF-8 composite film in post-exchange ligand                  solution, reacting for 0.5-5 hours at 50degrees                  Celsius, taking out film after reaction in                  methanol solvent for purifying, then drying at                  room temperature, obtaining ZIF-8 composite film                  contains amine ligand, where reaction time is 1                  hour.

TI Energy-storing lithium ion battery thermal runaway warning method for early warning fire-fighting system, involves judging working state of battery, and starting corresponding early warning based on temperature inside battery monomer.
AU HU J
   DENG E
   YANG C
   ZHANG G
   SONG D
   HU X
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
GA 2021C5523V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting a temperature T inside a battery monomer by a temperature sensor. The temperature sensor is sent to a main control unit. A concentration of hydrogen is detected by a gas sensor in a gas collecting tank. The concentration of carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide c2, carbon monoxide c3, methane concentration, ethylene c5, ethane c6 and smoke concentration are detected by the gas sensor. A working state of a lithium ion battery is determined by the main control. A corresponding early warning and fire control measures are started.
   USE - Energy-storing lithium ion battery thermal runaway warning method for early warning fire-fighting system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method monitors an internal temperature information through a temperature sensor set in a battery, and monitors battery external temperature in thermal runaway process of battery internal temperature that is obviously greater than the external temperature, relative to the existing early warning system. The internal temperature of the battery is monitored in advance for the first time early warning time, leaving more reaction time. The dangerous mixed gas generated by the battery suction gas pipeline, after detecting by the gas sensor is discharged into the gas processing device, and the harmful gas is eliminated, discharging the power station, preventing the pollution environment. The method pre-warns before the mixed gas explosion, through the carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, methane, explosion limit of mixed gas of ethylene and ethane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (a)an energy-storing lithium ion battery thermal runaway a characteristic collecting device;
   (b)an energy-storing lithium ion battery thermal runaway pre-warning fire-fighting system.

   CAO H
   BAO J
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 2021B00872
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a photocatalyst with atomic-level dispersed metal sites on surface, comprises (i) placing conductive substrate in mixed solution of cadmium nitrate tetrahydrate, thiourea and reduced glutathione, and heating for hydrothermal reaction to obtain cadmium sulfide catalyst, preferably, the conductive substrate is a cleaned FTO glass sheet, (ii) heating mixed solution of cuprous chloride, diluting hydrochloric acid and hydrazine hydrate, and under protection of argon, immersing cadmium sulfide catalyst for reaction, washing, drying, calcining sequentially in argon and hydrogen to obtain cadmium sulfide catalyst with atomically dispersed copper sites on surface, preferably, reaction time for immersing cadmium sulfide catalyst is 1-10 seconds, preferably 2 seconds, 5 seconds or 10 seconds, and the mixed solution of cuprous chloride, dilute hydrochloric acid and hydrazine hydrate is heated to 80-95 degrees C.

   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, the obtained catalyst has good stability, and has atomic-level dispersed metal sites on the surface thereof, provides a large number of active sites, and further improves the yield of photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, the photocatalytic carbon monoxide generation rate is 8.5 mu mol/g/h, which is three times that of the original cadmium sulfide photocatalyst, and the carbon monoxide selectivity reaches 93%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a photocatalyst with atomically dispersed metal sites on the surface, obtained by the method as mentioned above, comprising a carrier and a metal dispersed on the surface of the carrier, where the carrier is cadmium sulfide and the metal dispersed on the surface of the carrier is copper.

The molar ratio of the added reduced glutathione to cadmium nitrate tetrahydrate is 0.13:1. In step (ii), the volume ratio of the dilute hydrochloric acid to hydrazine hydrate is (24-32): 1.8. The concentration of the dilute hydrochloric acid is 0.5-2 mol/l. In step (ii), the molar ratio of the added cuprous chloride to cadmium nitrate tetrahydrate is (0.01-1):1. Preferred Method: In step (i), the hydrothermal reaction is performed at 180-200 degrees C for 2-4 hours. In step (ii), the calcining temperature of the argon gas is 200-400 degrees C. In step (ii), the hydrogen calcination temperature is 150-200 degrees C, and the hydrogen volume concentration is 10%. The pressure of the photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction is normal pressure. The simulated light source used in the reaction is 250-400W xenon lamp. The simulated light source is a 300W xenon lamp.

AE YANGLING VOCATIONL TECH COLLEGE (YANG-Non-standard)
   UNIV NORTHWEST A & F (UNAF-C)
GA 2020C7964F
AB    NOVELTY - Beer comprises apricot juice from tree-dried apricot, barley malt, wheat malt, brewer's yeast, hops, carbon dioxide and water (suitable quantity). The apricot juice from tree-dried apricot is prepared by using ultrasonic assisted extraction and ultra-high pressure sterilization.
   USE - The beer is useful for processing low-alcohol alcoholic beverages (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The beer has improved economic value, reduced the alcohol content, more comprehensive nutritional value, flavor of beer and also a special flavor of wheat sprouts and tree-dried apricot, and a pleasant taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing the beer, which involves (a) crushing malt by weighing barley malt and wheat malt and crushing together to obtain the first material, (b) dipping by adding water to the first material, immersing at a constant temperature of 40-50 degrees C, and washing to obtain a washed malt material, (c) performing saccharification by saccharifying the washed malt material to obtain a mash, (d) filtering the saccharified mash, and collecting the filtrate to obtain the original wort, (e) boiling the original wort, adding an appropriate amount of hops, and then cooling to 8-10 degrees C within 50-70 minutes to obtain a cooled wort, (f) performing fermentation by adding brewer's yeast to the cooled wort for fermentation, and controlling the fermentation temperature at 6-8 degrees C to obtain the original beer, (g) blending by weighing the apricot juice from tree-dried apricot, adding to the original beer and mixing evenly to obtain a mixed liquid, (h) ultrafiltration by after filtering the mixed liquid with a filter membrane, charging with carbon dioxide to obtain an aerated material, and (i) performing sterilization by sterilizing the aerated material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The apricot juice from tree-dried apricot is prepared by cleaning the fresh apricots, removing the pits, then adding into water at 45-55 degrees C in a ratio of 1:2-3 according to the ratio of material to water for ultrasonic assisted extraction, and performing ultra-high pressure sterilization under a pressure of 400-500 MPa. The extraction time of the ultrasonic assisted extraction is 20-30 minutes, the ultrasonic frequency is 35-45 kHz, and the power is 180-220 W. The weight ratio of apricot juice from tree-dried apricot, barley malt and wheat malt is 4-6:3-4:1.5-2. The saccharification process involves performing a first stage and a second stage. The first stage is performed by adding water (suitable quantity) to the washed malt material and saccharifying at a constant temperature of 72-75 degrees C for 10-20 minutes. The second stage is performed by adding water (suitable quantity) after the end of the first stage and saccharifying at a constant temperature of 78-82 degrees C for 40-50 minutes. The total added amount of the hops is 0.005-0.01 times the weight of the barley malt. The aeration is to flush carbon dioxide gas so that the mass fraction of carbon dioxide in the aerated material is 0.35-0.65%. The sterilization is performed at a temperature of 20-30 degrees C and a pressure of 400-500 MPa.

The light energy and heat absorbed during the day is slowly released at night, which improves the heat preservation effect of the interior of the shed. The growth efficiency is improved. The sunburning plants are avoided that protects plants and promote growth.

TI Storing renewable electrical energy through thermochemical conversion of microalgae biomass, involves e.g. producing microalgae biomass in closed photobioreactors or open pools and concentrating fermented algae suspension by centrifugation.
AU MIZSEY P
   FOZER D
AE MIZSEY P (MIZS-Individual)
   FOZER D (FOZE-Individual)
GA 2019A8507U
AB    NOVELTY - Storing renewable electrical energy through thermochemical conversion of microalgae biomass, comprises: e.g. producing microalgae biomass in suitable closed photobioreactors or open pools, where the medium and inoculum for cultivation are provided; then concentrating the fermented algae suspension by centrifugation and adjusting to a dry matter content of 5-25 wt.%; using the concentrated algae suspension as a starting material in the hydrothermal gasification step; supplying the electric furnace used for hydrothermal gasification and the artificial illumination used for cultivation with electricity from solar and wind power plants; and performing hydrothermal gasification to produce biogas containing hydrogen, methane, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, ethylene and ethane at an internal temperature of at least 700 degrees C and a pressure of 230 bar.
   USE - The method is useful for storing renewable electrical energy through thermochemical conversion of microalgae biomass.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Storing renewable electrical energy through thermochemical conversion of microalgae biomass, comprises: producing microalgae biomass in suitable closed photobioreactors or open pools, where the medium and inoculum for cultivation are provided; then concentrating the fermented algae suspension by centrifugation and adjusting to a dry matter content of 5-25 wt.%; using the concentrated algae suspension as a starting material in the hydrothermal gasification step; supplying the electric furnace used for hydrothermal gasification and the artificial illumination used for cultivation with electricity from solar and wind power plants; performing hydrothermal gasification to produce biogas containing hydrogen, methane, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, ethylene and ethane at an internal temperature of at least 700 degrees C and a pressure of 230 bar; separating methane from the biogas and then compressing the separated high methane stream into suitable natural gas storage; burning methane in a combined cycle gas turbine to generate electricity using generators; and using carbon dioxide from the combustion of methane in microalgae cultivation.
