PN CN110540894-A
TI Peanut oil extraction process involves processing shell of cleaned peanut to obtain peanut kernels, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of peanut powder, and depurifying peanut oil to obtain high-quality peanut oil.
AU SU Q
   LIU D
   YU G
AE SU Q (SUQQ-Individual)
GA 2019A4372G
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves manually sorting and screening (S1) the freshly picked peanuts. The impurities and dirt adhering to the surface of the peanut are screened out to prevent from contaminating the peanut during subsequent processing. The shell of the cleaned peanut is processed (S2) to obtain peanut kernels. The normal peanut kernels are added into the heating device to stir fry and remove the red clothes. Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of peanut powder is obtained (S3). The peanut oil is depurified (S4) to obtain high-quality peanut oil. The heating device is provided with casing, a falling plate, a stir-fry unit and a clothes removing unit. The air bag is fixed on the inner wall of the casing near the cam. The airbag is provided with a gas outlet on the side wall near the opening. The airbag is squeezed by cam, so that the gas in the airbag is sprayed through the gas outlet, so that stir frying and peeling of the peanut kernels are ensured.
   USE - Peanut oil extraction process.
   ADVANTAGE - The original nutritional components such as arachidic acid and vitamin E in peanut oil are preserved to the maximum to ensure the nutrition and safety of peanut oil products.

   Step for processing shell of the cleaned peanut to obtain peanut kernels (S2)
   Step for obtaining supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of peanut powder (S3)
   Step for depurifying peanut oil to obtain high-quality peanut oil (S4)

The                  refrigerant comprises greater than or equal to 97                  wt.% 4 compounds, with each compound consisting                  of 45 plus minus 1 wt.% trifluoroiodomethane                  (CF3I), 40 plus minus 1 wt.%                  1,1,1,2-tetrafluoropropene (HFO-1234yf), 13 plus                  minus 1 wt.% difluoromethane (HFC-32), and 2 plus                  minus 0.5 wt.% carbon dioxide (CO2) where                  refrigerant is nonflammable with Global Warming                  Potential (GWP) of less than or equal to 100. The                  heat transfer composition further comprises a                  stabilizer comprising an alkylated naphthalene                  and/or diene based compound and/or isobutylene.                  The heat transfer composition further comprises                  lubricant selected from group consisting of                  polyol esters (POEs), polyalkylene glycols                  (PAGs), PAG oils, silicone oils, mineral oil,                  alkylbenzenes (ABs), polyvinyl ethers (PVE) and                  poly( alpha -olefin) (PAO). The lubricant is a                  polyol ester (POE). Preferred Parameters: The                  evaporatoring temperature of refrigerant is - 40                  to - 12 degrees C, preferably - 12 to 0 degrees                  C. Preferred Device: The heat transfer system                  further comprises an oil separator located                  downstream of the compressor where sequestration                  material is located inside the oil separator such                  that liquid lubricant contacts the sequestration                  material(s) during operation of the system.

TI Plant network for pig iron production, has mixed-gas line system which is provided for mixed gas occurred during setting of mixing quotient, and chemical plant which is connected to mixed-gas line system.
AU KRUEGER M
   KRUGER M
AE THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
GA 201919694R
AB    NOVELTY - The plant network has a hydrogen-gas line system (4) which is provided for one stream of hydrogen-containing gas emitted from a hydrogen source (3). A mixing device (5) is provided for setting one mixed gas from one stream of furnace gas and one stream of hydrogen-containing gas emitted from the hydrogen source. The mixing device is connected to a furnace-gas line system (2) and to hydrogen-gas line system. The mixed gas is set to comprise a stoichiometric quotient of the mixture from a dividend comprising difference between molar quantities of hydrogen as minuend and carbon dioxide as subtrahend and a divisor comprising the total amount of molar quantities of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. A mixed-gas line system (6) is provided for mixed gas occurred during setting of mixing quotient. A chemical plant (7) is connected to the mixed-gas line system.
   USE - Plant network for pig iron production.
   ADVANTAGE - The plant network facilitates improved influenceability of the economy of the overall process and low investment and operating costs with the possibility of saving gas conditioning.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The plant network has a furnace (1) for pig iron production and a furnace-gas line system for one stream of furnace gas occurred during the pig iron production. The stream of furnace gas is provided with a composition comprising nitrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. The composition of the furnace gas flow rate is additionally comprised of nitrogen. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for operating plant network.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the plant network for pig iron production.
   Furnace (1)
   Furnace-gas line system (2)
   Hydrogen source (3)
   Hydrogen-gas line system (4)
   Mixing device (5)
   Mixed-gas line system (6)
   Chemical plant (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The mixed gas line system is connected to the chemical plant which is selected from a group of a plant for production of methanol, a plant for producing higher alcohol such as ethanol, n-propanol, isopropanol, n-butanol, isobutanol, sec-butanol, tert-butanol, 1,4-butanediol or combination, a plant for production of alkanes such as methane, ethane, propane, n-butane, isobutane, cycle hexane or combination, a plant for the production of alkenes such as ethene, propene, but-1-ene, (Z)-but-2-ene, 2 Methylprop-l-ene, 1,3-butadiene or combination, a plant for the production of alkynes such as ethyne, propyne, 1-butyne, 2-butyne or a combination, a plant for the preparation of ethers such as linear ether, cyclic ether, branched ether, saturated ether, unsaturated ether, dimethyl ether (DME), isopropyl methyl ether, oxacyclohexane, polyoxymethylene dimethyl ether (OME) or a combination, a plant for the preparation of aldehydes such as formaldehyde, acetaldehyde, propanal, butanal or a combination, a plant for producing ketone such as acetone, Butanone, 2-pentanone, 3-pentanone, methyl isopropyl ketone or a combination, and a plant for the production of carboxylic acid such as formic acid, acetic acid, propionic acid, oxalic acid or a combination.

TI Oxygen recycling device, has return hose whose end is connected with upper part of right side of box body and gas jet in box body, where another end of return hose is connected with right side of cylindrical transparent hood.
AU LIU Y
   JING Y
   LIN W
AE WEIHAI WEIGAO HAISHENG MEDICAL EQUIP CO (WEIH-Non-standard)
GA 201901670P
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a secondary oxygen device (13) i.e. Oxygen bottle or medical molecular sieve oxy-generator, located between an expander (6) and a carbon dioxide absorber (11). An outlet of the secondary oxygen device is connected with an oxygen flow regulating valve (15) that is connected with an oxygen pressure reducer (14). An outlet of the oxygen flow regulating valve is connected with a gas jet (12) in an inner side of the carbon dioxide absorber through a pipeline. An end of a return hose (16) is connected with an upper part of a right side of a box body (5) and the gas jet in the box body. Another end of the return hose is connected with a right side of a cylindrical transparent hood (1).
   USE - Oxygen recycling device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device fixes a patient head in a film-type head, supplies oxygen for breathing by a patient, reuses oxygen exhaled by the patient, rapidly reduces problem of an anoxic state of tissues and organs of the patient, increases oxygen utilization rate and extends oxygen inhalation time, and has low oxygen recycling cost, and can avoid oxygen exhaled by the human body in an environment and difficulty of providing oxygen source and reduce environmental safety problems caused by oxygen diffusion.

TI Intelligent classroom management system, has master server connected with sub-server, power source switch and window switch fixed on outer side of classroom, and power source switch and window switch arranged on wall of LED display screen.
AU JIANG B
AE HENAN ENJIU INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 201912422C
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a master server connected with a sub-server through an internet. A light sensor is connected with the sub-server through a wireless network. A wind sensor, a temperature sensor and a carbon dioxide sensor are installed in a classroom. A door and window motor opens a door. A power source switch and a window switch are fixed on an outer side of the classroom. The power source switch and the window switch are arranged on a wall body of a LED display screen. The sub-server is connected with a broadcast device through the wireless network. A motor rotating shaft is connected with a door/window track through a conventional transmission gear.
   USE - Intelligent classroom management system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system utilizes the light sensor to collect light intensity in the classroom, utilizes the temperature sensor collects to temperature data in the classroom, utilizes the carbon dioxide sensor for collecting concentration data in the classroom, sends collected data to the main server and allows the server to start an air conditioner according to collected data, utilizes the door and window motor driving the door and the window switch to perform temperature regulation process, controls the door and window motor to open the door and a window to ventilate the classroom if carbon dioxide concentration is high in the classroom, utilizes the LED display screen to display different information, realizes management of classroom in an intelligent manner and reduces management workload.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of an intelligent classroom management system. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PN WO2018185738-A1; CA3057964-A1; AU2017408431-A1; IN201947044254-A; CN110692108-A; EP3607556-A1; JP2020512919-W; US2020222286-A1; BR112019020663-A2; US10898413-B2; US2021169744-A1; US11185474-B2; US2022079845-A1; EP3607556-B1; US11364180-B2
TI Method for adjusting enteral feeding of patient by controller, involves receiving oxygen measurement by receiving carbon dioxide measurement, and computing estimate of energy expenditure to be performed iteratively for every time interval.
AU ELIA L
   IDDAN G J
   IDDAN G
AE ART MEDICAL LTD (ARTM-Non-standard)
   ART MEDICAL LTD (ARTM-Non-standard)
   ART MEDICAL LTD (ARTM-Non-standard)
   ART MEDICAL LTD (ARTM-Non-standard)
   ART MEDICAL LTD (ARTM-Non-standard)
GA 201878158C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves computing an estimate of energy expenditure of a patient based on an oxygen measurement and a carbon dioxide measurement. A target composition and a target feeding rate are computed for an enteral feeding according to a computed estimate of energy expenditure. Instructions are generated for adjustment of a rate of delivery of the enteral feeding according to the target composition by an enteral feeding controller (204). An oxygen measurement is received by receiving the carbon dioxide measurement, and computing the estimate of the energy expenditure to be performed iteratively for every first time interval, where the generating instructions for adjustment are performed for a second time interval that is larger than the first time interval.
   USE - Method for adjusting an enteral feeding of a patient by a controller.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables quickly adjusting a feeding regimen to reflect changes in a current state of the patient, which cannot be performed by manual processes that are based on outdated states of the patient and not the current state of the patient. The method enables utilizing a graphical user interface (GUI) that presents to user suitable options based on an automatically computed resting energy expenditure of the patient, which aids the user in making correct feeding selections.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a system for adjusting an enteral feeding of a patient by an enteral feeding controller
   (2) a computer program product comprising a set of instructions for adjusting an enteral feeding of a patient by an enteral feeding controller.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of an enteral feeding device.
   Enteral feeding controller (204)
   Standard feed inlet (402)

PT P
PN CN108483950-A
TI High-activity magnesium oxide roasting comprises e.g. mixing magnesium carbonate powder with hot air to absorb heat, preheating the material, calcining and decomposing into a mixture of magnesium oxide and carbon dioxide.
AU LIU J
   ZHANG S
   DONG X
   WANG Y
   TIAN Q
AE ZHENJIANG SOBUTE NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (ZHEN-Non-standard)
   SUBOTE NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (SUBO-Non-standard)
GA 2018713184
AB    NOVELTY - High-activity magnesium oxide roasting comprises mixing magnesium carbonate powder with hot air to absorb heat, preheating the material to 350 degrees C, subjecting to cyclonic calcination, calcining at 800-1000 degrees C, decomposing into a mixture of magnesium oxide and carbon dioxide, allowing the cooled magnesium oxide and carbon dioxide mixture to enters into the separation unit to achieve gas separation, allowing the collected powder to enters inthe finished warehouse for storage, treating the escaping exhaust gas by the exhaust gas treatment device and discharging into the atmosphere through the chimney.
   USE - The method is useful for high-activity magnesium oxide roasting.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for special device for the high-activity magnesium oxide roasting, comprising a raw material storage tank, a gas flow preheating furnace, a swirling calcining furnace, a multi-channel combustion system, a cooling device, a separation device, an exhaust gas treatment device, finished warehouse, chimney and temperature detector. The raw material bin is connected to the bottom feed port of the air preheating furnace through a conveying device. The outlet of the gas preheating furnace is connected to the inlet of the top of the swirling calciner and the outlet of the bottom of the swirling calciner is connected to the hot side inlet of the cooling device. The cold side discharge port of the cooling device is connected to the upper feed port of the separating device. The top gas outlet of the separating device is connected to the air inlet of the exhaust gas treatment device. The gas outlet of the exhaust gas treatment device is connected to the chimney and the bottom discharge port of the separation device is connected to the finished product bin through the conveying device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The magnesium carbonate powder is mixed with hot air and is carried out in a preheating furnace, the magnesium carbonate powder is fed from the bottom of the airflow preheating furnace through the conveying device and is mixed with the hot air to absorb heat, so that the magnesium carbonate powder is preheated in the airflow preheating furnace and the preheated magnesium carbonate powder and hot gas escape from the discharge port at the top of the gas preheating furnace. The cyclonic calcination is carried out in a cyclone calciner, passing through a feed port at the top of the cyclonic calciner into a cyclone calciner, and the magnesium carbonate is calcined and decomposed into a mixture of magnesium oxide and carbon dioxide in a rotary calcination. The temperature control detector is provided at the inlet in order to accurately control the calcination temperature, the middle and the outlet of the cyclone calciner, and at the inlet and outlet of the gas preheating furnace, and the temperature data and the gas of the combustion system of each part are provided. The quantity data is controlled by the computer system to ensure the stability of the calcination temperature. In the preheating furnace and the swirling calcining furnace, the combustion system is heated, and the combustion system is located in four channels, one of which is located in the lower part of the preheating furnace, and mainly functions as a preheated magnesium carbonate raw material powder. The other three channels are distributed in the upper, middle and lower parts of the cyclone calciner, and function as calcined and decomposed magnesium carbonate raw meal. The hot air generated during cooling is sent to the combustion engine fan inlet of the combustion system through the duct for waste heat utilization. The gas mixture is transported by a pipelined air delivery system to reduce dust leakage, and a roof filter is placed at the top of the material bin and the finished product bin to reduce the dust discharge concentration. The screw conveyor, bucket elevator, feeding bin and a quantitative feeding screw machine between the raw material warehouse and the airflow preheating furnace. The magnesium carbonate component in the raw material bin is first sent to the feeding bin through the screw conveyor and the bucket elevator, and then sent to the airflow preheating furnace for preheating through the quantitative feeding screw machine at the bottom of the feeding bin. The bottom of the air preheating furnace is provided with a combustion chamber and the combustion chamber is heated by a combustion system. The cyclone calciner is equipped with three combustion systems, located at the top, middle and bottom of the cyclone calciner, to ensure uniform temperature in the swirling calciner. The cooling device is a sleeve type heat exchanger, and the gas mixture which is calcined and decomposed in the rotary calcining furnace enters the heat exchanger from the bottom discharge port, and the cold air is blown from the cold side of the heat exchanger through the blower. The hot air after heat exchange escapes from the hot side of the heat exchanger and then supplies hot air to the four sets of combustion systems through the air duct for waste heat utilization. The separating device is a cyclone dust collector, and the mixture of the cooled magnesium oxide powder and the carbon dioxide gas is gas-solid separated by a cyclone dust collector, and the collected MgO powder is sent into the finished product warehouse from the bottom of the cyclone dust collector. The gas discharged from the top of the cyclone dust collector is divided into two ways by the system fan. One route is sent back to the heating system of the air preheating furnace for waste heat utilization. After the bag is located at the top of the finished product, the dust collector removes the dust emission through the chimney. In the bag type dust collector, the dust-containing exhaust gas separated from the separation device is first dusted, and then discharged through the chimney to ensure that the temperature of the gas entering the baghouse is less than the temperature of the bag, a cold damper is installed at the inlet duct of the baghouse.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)
MC E11-W; E34-B01; L02-A02; L02-A04; L02-B02; L02-G06; L02-J02C

PN CN108483791-A
TI Miniature intelligent control water quality adjusting device comprises detection and lighting module, biological water tank and water quality adjustment module and intelligent control module, which extracts data of temperature.
AU LOU Y
   GUO L
   JI X
   CHEN R
AE LOU Y (LOUY-Individual)
GA 201871321D
AB    NOVELTY - Miniature intelligent control water quality adjusting device comprises intelligent control module (1), detection and lighting module (2), biological water tank (3) and water quality adjustment module (4). Intelligent control module extracts data of temperature, pH, carbon dioxide content, TDS index, and nitrite content by an electronic sensor. Corresponding control reaction is determined according to water quality requirements of organisms (animals, plants, microorganisms) to be raised, and corresponding data index ranges are determined. The detected indicator exceeds preset range, control host transmits data to mobile terminal through wireless network, and performs an alarm, and requests whether automatic control program is turned on. After user selects to perform or manually operate, control host send instructions to water supply device, temperature control device, the carbon dioxide supply device, and water supply device, and adjusting water quality according to preset procedure.
   USE - Miniature intelligent control water quality adjusting device.
   ADVANTAGE - The miniature intelligent control water quality adjusting device is an open source module, is set by the user to define and manually adjust, provides a modular, fully intelligent, multi-project, and automated remote control device for long-term water quality maintenance in the aquarium field.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The detection and illumination module is composed of an electronic detection device and professional lighting equipment for performing various indicators on the biological water tank body. The electronic detecting device detects the Celsius temperature, pH, carbon dioxide content, nitrite content, and TDS of the water body. The biochemical filtration second filter material is composed of a glass ring, a ceramic ring, a biosphere, a polyamine or a porous material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of miniature intelligent control water quality adjusting device.
   Intelligent control module (1)
   Detection and lighting module (2)
   Biological water tank (3)
   Water quality adjustment module (4)
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); W01 (Telephone and Data Transmission Systems)

PN CN108355063-A
TI Traditional Chinese medicinal cream useful for eliminating blood toxins and waste, contains Iphigenia indica, rhizoma polygonati, radix clematidis, peach kernel, witch hazel, Gentian, glycerol, ethylhexyl palmitate, and carbomer.
AU ZHANG J
AE SICHUAN SHENGMINGYUAN HEALTH MANAGEMENT CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 201863565G
AB    NOVELTY - Traditional Chinese medicinal cream comprises Iphigenia indica, rhizoma polygonati, radix clematidis, peach kernel, witch hazel, Gentian, Aloe, radix sophorae flavescentis, pseudoginseng, Rheum officinale, red ginseng, dried bark of Phellodendron chinense, quantum water, glycerol, ethylhexyl palmitate, polyethylene glycol-40 (PEG-40) hydrogenated castor oil and carbomer.
   USE - The medicinal cream is useful for eliminating blood toxins and waste, and improving and preventing disease risk by promoting metabolism.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of preparing the medicinal cream, which involves (a) crushing I.indica, rhizoma polygonati, radix clematidis, peach kernel, witch hazel, Gentian, Aloe, radix sophorae flavescentis, pseudoginseng, R.officinale, red ginseng, and dried bark of P.chinense using a traditional Chinese medicinal crusher, sieving through a 40 mesh sieve to obtain Chinese medicinal powder, adding to a ball mill, performing batch ball milling, ball milling once every 15 minutes, and ball milling for 15-25 minutes to obtain Chinese medicine powder with a particle size of 1-20 mu m, (b) placing the powder obtained in step (a) in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, extracting by using carbon dioxide, where the carbon dioxide flow rate is 20-25 l/hour, the extraction pressure is 15-25 MPa, the extraction temperature is 30-40 degrees C and the extraction time is 2 hours, centrifuging, and taking the supernatant to obtain Chinese medicinal stock solution, and (c) mixing ethylhexyl palmitate, glycerin, carbomer and quantum water, heating at 85 degrees C in a water bath, stirring uniformly with a homogenizer, cooling to 40 degrees C, adding the Chinese medicinal stock solution, stirring for 10 minutes, then adding PEG-40 hydrogenated castor oil, and stirring at a constant speed to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Product: The cream comprises 30-40 pts. wt. I.indica, 30-40 pts. wt. rhizoma polygonati, 28-38 pts. wt. radix clematidis, 28-38 pts. wt. peach kernel, 25-35 pts. wt. witch hazel, 23-33 pts. wt. Gentian, 20-30 pts. wt. Aloe, 20-30 pts. wt. radix sophorae flavescentis, 15-25 pts. wt. pseudoginseng, 14-24 pts. wt. R.officinale, 11-21 pts. wt. red ginseng, 8-18 pts. wt. dried bark of P.chinense, 5-35 pts. wt. quantum water, 20-30 pts. wt. glycerol, 20-30 pts. wt. ethylhexyl palmitate, 18-28 pts. wt. PEG-40 hydrogenated castor oil and 2-5 pts. wt. carbomer.
   ACTIVITY - Antidote; Metabolic.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)
MC B04-A08; B04-A10; B04-A98; B04-B01C1; B04-B01D; B04-C03B; B04-C03C; B10-E04C; B10-G02; B12-M02B; B14-M01; B14-S13; B14-S18; A04-A03; A04-F04; A10-E07C; A10-E08A; A10-E08B; A11-C07; A12-V01

PN CN108299243-A; CN208362223-U
TI Urea production system for absorbing melamine tail            gas, has carbon dioxide stripping urea unit which            passes urea solution to evaporator through outer tubes            and triamine unit whose tail gas enters evaporator            section through outer tube.
AU CAI C
   YIN D
   ZHANG Y
   SHI J
   CHEN F
   FU Z
   FENG W
AE SHANDONG HONGYUN ENG DESIGN CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   KUITUN JINJIANG CHEM CO LTD (KUIT-Non-standard)
GA 201863355R
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a tail suction tower (22) in                which the discharged liquid from bottom portion is                sequentially re-entered into the tail suction tower                through the tail suction circulation pump (26) and                the tail suction circulation cooler (27). The high                pressure steam is configured to enter the liquid                ammonia preheater (4) and the methylammonium                preheater (6). The tail gas of the triamine unit is                configured to enter the two-pass condenser (20)                through the third outer tube (29). The urea                solution from the carbon dioxide stripping urea                unit is configured to enter a section of the                evaporator through the fourth and fifth outer tubes                (30,31). The tail gas of the triamine unit is                configured to enter the evaporator section through                the sixth outer tube (32).
   USE - Urea production system for absorbing melamine                tail gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The concentration of carbondioxide and ammonia                in the gas phase is increased and the amount of                water entering the synthesis tower is reduced. The                steam is saved and ammonia and carbon dioxide are                recovered from the exhaust gas. The vacuum of the                system remains more stable such that the load on                the subsequent desorption hydrolysis system is                reduced. The situation of leakage of the ordinary                steam trap is avoided and the steam utilization                rate is improved. The gas-liquid mixture is cooled                and absorbed by the external cooler, thus the load                of the suction tower is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an urea                production method for absorbing melamine tail                gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit diagram of the                urea production system.
   Liquid ammonium preheater (4)
   Methylammonium preheater (6)
   Two-pass condenser (20)
   Tail suction tower (22)
   Tail suction circulation pump (26)
   Tail suction circulation cooler (27)
   Outer tubes (29-32)
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); E13 (Heterocyclics.)

PN CN107927199-A
TI High-oleic acid rapeseeds blend oil with low acid value comprises rapeseeds, peanuts, sesame, Camellia seeds, olive oil and Moringa seeds.
AU LIN S
   XIE J
   LIN X
   YU J
AE GUANGDONG JINNIBAO TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201832111C
AB    NOVELTY - High-oleic acid rapeseeds blend oil with low acid value comprises 37-55 wt.% rapeseeds, 5-14 wt.% peanuts, 2-8 wt.% sesame, 20-30 wt.% Camellia seeds, 10-20 wt.% olive oil, and 8-18 wt.% Moringa seeds.
   USE - Used as high-oleic acid rapeseeds blend oil with low acid value.
   ADVANTAGE - The blend oil: has output rate of more than 35%, lower than 0.5 (potassium hydroxide)/(mg/g) acid value, and unsaturated fatty acids of more than 90%, and oleic acid content of 60-80%. The method: quickly refines rapeseeds oil; greatly reduces the acidic value of the blended oil; and provides rich flavor and good mouth feel to rapeseeds oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the blend oil, comprising crushing each raw material, mixing uniformly, adding into a multistage supercritical carbon dioxide equipment extracting kettle, regulating the extraction temperature at 45-55 degrees C for 1.5-4 hours under 30-45 MPa pressure, conveying the mixture of supercritical carbon dioxide and blend oil into a separation kettle for vacuum extracting and separating, adjusting the technique condition of the separating kettle as follows: first separating kettle temperature at 40-60 degrees C, and pressure at 10-20 MPa, separating rapeseed blended oil from the first separating kettle, refining rapeseed blended crude oil by adding the supercritical carbon dioxide extracted rapeseeds blended crude oil into a molecular distilling sample tank, adjusting the molecular distillation technology parameters, where the vacuum degree is 2-9 Pa, distillation temperature is 100-150 degrees C, the rotating speed is 100-300 rotations/minute, the feeding speed is 10-50 ml/minute and the condensing temperature is 20-50 degrees C, refining, and collecting the refined oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The oil comprises 37-40 wt.% rapeseeds, 5-8 wt.% peanuts, 2-8 wt.% sesame, 20-25 wt.% Camellia seeds, 13-15 wt.% olive oil, and 8-10 wt.% Moringa seeds.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D10-A01; D10-A02
IP A23D-009/007; A23D-009/02; A23D-009/04; C11B-001/04; C11B-001/10; C11B-003/00; C11B-003/12

PT P
PN CN107892293-A
TI Preparation of graphene using coal comprises drying low-rank coal powder, microwave absorbers and catalyst, treating with microwave and reacting with sodium alkylbenzene sulfonate dispersant under carbon dioxide and nitrogen reaction medium.
AU HAN X
   JIANG M
   WANG J
   MA L
   LI X
AE HAN X (HANX-Individual)
GA 2018288860
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of graphene using coal comprises drying low-rank coal powder, microwave absorbers and catalyst in vacuum-drying oven, treating in microwave pyrolysis reactor, washing reaction mixture with cleaning solution, drying, ultrasonic-cleaning, reacting with sodium alkylbenzene sulfonate dispersant under mixed reaction medium of carbon dioxide and nitrogen having 2:1 mass ratio at pressure of reactor of 7.38-7.99 MPa, reacting for 20-25 minutes, washing product with ethanol solution, and drying at 90-110 degrees C for 10 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing graphene using coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has reduced production cost of graphene, and has simple process, high product quality, and wide raw material source.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of graphene using coal comprises mixing 50-80 pts. wt. low-rank coal powder, 10-15 pts. wt. microwave absorbers and 10-20 pts. wt. catalyst having particle size of less than 0.221 mm uniformly in vacuum-drying oven, transferring into microwave pyrolysis reactor, treating at microwave power of 400-600 W and 400-650 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, treating further at 800-1000 degrees C and 800-1000 W for 10-13 minutes, cooling reactor to room temperature, washing reaction mixture with cleaning solution, drying, ultrasonic-cleaning at 30-40 kHz and 130 W for 10-15 minutes, mixing with sodium alkylbenzene sulfonate dispersant at 2:1 mass ratio, reacting at 32-37 degrees C, introducing mixed reaction medium of carbon dioxide and nitrogen having 2:1 mass ratio until pressure of reactor is 7.38-7.99 MPa, reacting for 20-25 minutes, washing product with ethanol solution, and drying at 90-110 degrees C for 10 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The low-rank coal powder is brown coal and/or long flame coal. The microwave absorbers are kaolinite, illite, montmorillonite, and/or pyrite. The catalyst is sodium carbonate or sodium bicarbonate. The washing liquid is 0.1-0.2 mol/L potassium permanganate solution.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC J04-E01
IP C01B-032/184
PD CN107892293-A   10 Apr 2018   C01B-032/184   201828   Pages: 9   Chinese

PN WO2018048883-A1; US2018221617-A1; US10252018-B2; US2019232003-A1; US2020390991-A1; US10987476-B2
TI Gas delivery and monitoring apparatus for delivering oxygen and monitoring exhaled carbon dioxide in subject, has conduit extended from inlet port through channel from proximal end to distal end into bite block to terminate in delivery port.
AU CHAUDHRY T
AE MOFFITT CANCER CENT & RES INST INC H LEE (MOFF-Non-standard)
   MOFFITT CANCER CENT & RES INST INC H LEE (MOFF-Non-standard)
GA 2018216000
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (10) has a support unit (50) provided with a channel that extends along a longitudinal axis from proximal end to distal. A bite block (70) is affixed to the support unit sized to be inserted within a mouth of a subject. A first elongated conduit (20) is extended from an exhalation capture manifold (40) through the channel from the distal end to the proximal end to terminate in an outlet port. A second elongated conduit (30) is extended from an inlet port through the channel from the proximal end to the distal end into the bite block to terminate in a gas delivery port.
   USE - Gas delivery and monitoring apparatus for delivering oxygen and monitoring exhaled carbon dioxide in subject such as mammal while under anesthesia.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen is delivered directly to the back of the mouth instead of in front of the face, and the carbon dioxide exhaled from both the nose and mouth is captured. The deformation of the bridge provides the spring tension to secure the bite block within the mouth of the subject. The bite block is modular with adjustable heights to accommodate various size mouth openings. The conduits are coated or sheathed with another material to provide additional properties such as rigidity and deformability. The sizeable bite blocks are added to facilitate the placement of oral-airway, laryngeal mask airway, or any other life-saving airway apparatus in case of emergency, and to facilitate the passage of endoscope in the esophagus or trachea of patient.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the gas delivery and monitoring apparatus.
   Gas delivery and monitoring apparatus (10)
   Elongated conduits (20,30)
   Exhalation capture manifold (40)
   Support unit (50)
   Bite block (70)
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); P31 (Diagnosis, surgery (A61B).); P34 (Sterilising, syringes, electrotherapy (A61L, M, N).)

PT P
PN CN107697930-A; US2018264447-A1; US10258974-B2; CN107697930-B
TI Preparation of SAPO-11 molecular sieve useful as            hydrocarbon isomerization catalyst by dissolving            aluminum source in ethylene glycol, and agitating,            adding structure-directing agent and phosphorus,            crystallizing, drying, and roasting.
AU FAN Y
   HAN S
   WEN C
   WANG S
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
GA 2018154588
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of SAPO-11 molecular sieve                includes dissolving aluminum source in ethylene                glycol, and agitating to obtain solution A; adding                the structure-directing agent and phosphorus into                the solution A, and stirring to obtain solution B;                adding the silicon source into the solution B, and                stirring to obtain solution C; transferring the                solution C into the supercritical carbon dioxide                reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide into                the reaction kettle; crystallizing; separating the                product, drying, and roasting to obtain the SAPO-11                molecular sieve.
   USE - Method for preparing SAPO-11 molecular sieve                useful as hydrocarbon isomerization catalyst                carrier in hydrocarbon hydrogenation isomerization                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not need to add extra template                agent to obtain the small grain size SAPO-11                molecular sieve.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of SAPO-11 molecular sieve                comprises:
   (A) dissolving aluminum source in ethylene                glycol, and agitating to obtain solution A;
   (B) adding the structure-directing agent and                phosphorus into the solution A, and stirring to                obtain solution B;
   (C) adding the silicon source into the                solution B, and stirring to obtain solution                C;
   (D) transferring the solution C into the                supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) reaction kettle,                introducing CO2 into the reaction kettle, and                increasing the pressure in the reaction                kettle;
   (E) increasing the supercritical CO2 reaction                kettle to reaction temperature and crystallizing;                and
   (F) after finishing crystallizing, reducing                the temperature of supercritical CO2 reaction                kettle, taking out the solution in the kettle,                separating the product, drying, and roasting to                obtain the SAPO-11 molecular sieve, preferably the                aluminum source comprises aluminum isopropoxide                and/or pseudoboehmite, preferably the stirring step                is carried out at 10-50 degrees C for 0.5-3                hours.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for SAPO-11                molecular sieve prepared by the above given                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: In step (B), the                  structure-directing agent comprises                  di-n-propylamine and/or diisopropylamine, and the                  source of phosphorus comprises phosphoric acid.                  In step (C), the silicon source comprises silica                  sol, ethyl orthosilicate or propyl orthosilicate.                  The mol ratio of the aluminum source, silicon                  source, phosphorus source, structure-directing                  agent and ethylene glycol is                  0.5-1.5:0.1-0.3:1-3:1.5-5:20-50.

PT P
PN CN107579191-A; CN107579191-B
TI Method for preparing lithium sulfur battery poros composite film, involves obtaining mixed solution, introducing carbon dioxide into mixed solution, pouring mixed solution into vaccum drying box, followed by drying washed composite film.
AU SUN K
   FAN L
   ZHANG N
   WU X
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 2018060096
AB    NOVELTY - A lithium sulfur battery poros composite film preparing method involves obtaining uniform mixed solution. Carbon dioxide gas is introduced into the mixed solution. The mixed solution is poured into a vacuum drying box that is made of membrane and dried at 60- 180 degrees C to obtain a dry film. The dry film is again dried in the vacuum drying box at 60- 180 degrees C to obtain a composite film. The dried composite film is washed by using diluted acid. The washed composite film is dried in an oven for 10-18 hours to obtain poros composite film of lithium- sulfur battery.
   USE - Method for preparing lithium sulfur battery poros composite film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing the composite film with high temperature resistance, anti-tensile property and better air permeability and cycle performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A lithium sulfur battery poros composite film preparing method involves obtaining polyamide solution and poly-para-diformyl para-phenylene diamine solution and adding 100 mg/L - 1g/L calcium salt in to the obtained diamine solution to obtain uniform mixed solution. Carbon dioxide gas is introduced into the mixed solution and polyimide solution. The mixed solution is poured into a vacuum drying box that is made of membrane and dried at 60- 180 degrees C to obtain a dry film. The dry film is again dried in the vacuum drying box at 60- 180 degrees C to obtain a composite film. The dried composite film is washed by using diluted acid. The washed composite film is dried in an oven for 10-18 hours to obtain poros composite film of lithium- sulfur battery. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a lithium sulfur battery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium salt is selected from calcium chloride, calcium sulfate or calcium nitrate. The poly-para-diformyl para-phenylene diamine solution is selected from mixture of dimethyl sulfoxide, sulfolane, dimethyl formamide or nitrogen methyl pyrrolidone.

TI Method for instantly and quickly making soda water involves filling carbon dioxide in soda mixed tank to be dissolved in drinking water to generate soda water when carbon dioxide fills soda mixture tank, where soda water is generated from soda mix tank when soda outlet signal is issued.
AU KUANG J
   CHEN W
   FANG Z
   WANG L
AE GUANGDONG GENTUO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202250253G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves installing a carbon dioxide gas bottle on an air bottle mounting seat of a soda water machine. A water storage tank is filled with drinking water. The drinking water in the water inlet water pump is pumped to the soda water mixing tank when a liquid feeding signal is received. The carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide bottle installed on the air bottle installation seat is filled into the soda mixing tank. A carbon dioxide is filled in the soda mixed tank to be dissolved in the drinking water to generate soda water when carbon dioxide fills the soda mixture tank. The soda water is generated from the soda mix tank when the soda outlet signal is issued.
   USE - Method for instantly and quickly making soda water
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and convenient operation, and can manufacture the needed amount of liquid water, avoiding waste, which is better suitable for daily life. The carbon dioxide gas bottle is pre-placed on the air bottle mounting seat of the soda water machine, pre-filling the water in the water storage tank, and when receiving the liquid sending signal, the liquid inlet water pump pumps the drinking water from the water supply nozzle to the water mixing tank. The air source switch module is opened and the carbon dioxide in the gas bottle installed on the seat is filled in the mixing tank, when the air inlet signal is received.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram illustrating the Method for instantly and quickly making soda water.

PN DE102019127456-A1; EP3805529-A1
TI Method for operating turbo machine, involves passing carbon dioxide through turbo stage as process medium set on high pressure side in supercritical state of aggregation, where slide bearing is acted upon with supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU SCHMIED N
AE ATLAS COPCO ENERGAS GMBH (ATLP-C)
   ATLAS COPCO ENERGAS GMBH (ATLP-C)
GA 202138497X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves rotatably mounting a rotor (3) in the housing (1) by a slide bearing (2a,2b). An impeller of a turbo stage (3b) is attached to a shaft (3a). The turbo stage is set with a high pressure side (4) and a low pressure side (5). The carbon dioxide is passed through the turbo stage as process medium, which is set on the high pressure side in the supercritical state of aggregation. The slide bearing is acted upon with supercritical carbon dioxide. The supercritical carbon dioxide fed to the plain bearing is as pure as the process medium. The slide bearing is designed as a hydrostatic slide bearing with supercritical carbon dioxide as the working fluid.
   USE - Method for operating turbo machine (claimed) such as turbo compressor, turbo pump and turbine or turbo expander.
   ADVANTAGE - The contamination of the process fluid by lubricants such as oils or silicone oils or water vapor components is strictly prevented. The supercritical carbon dioxide forms the fluid film in the slide bearing which surrounds associated contact surface of the rotor. The fluid film prevents direct material contact between the rotor and the plain bearing. The fluid film made of supercritical carbon dioxide creates low level of friction and ensures safe and trouble-free operation of the turbo machine. The supercritical carbon dioxide fed to the plain bearing or the plain bearings is expediently taken from the high pressure side of that turbo stage with the highest pressure. Thus, no significant pressure increase has to be generated on the plain bearing in order to achieve an overpressure with respect to all points of the cycle. Hence, flow of the supercritical carbon dioxide from the slide bearing in the direction of the cycle and the prevention of additional entry of foreign substances are ensured.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a turbo machine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional of the turbo machine.
   Housing (1)
   Slide bearings (2a,2b)
   Rotor (3)
   Shaft (3a)
   Turbo stage (3b)
   High pressure side (4)
   Low pressure side (5)

PT P
PN CN113663540-A; CN113663540-B
TI Carbon dioxide separation membrane for carbon capture, includes support layer, nanoparticle composite layer and polymer layer sequentially arranged, where nanoparticle composite layer is prepared from nanoparticles and polymer, and nanoparticles include surface-modified nanoparticles.
AU TAN Y
AE HUNAN YEZHINENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2021E1773G
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide separation membrane includes a support layer, a nanoparticle composite layer and a polymer layer sequentially arranged. The nanoparticle composite layer is prepared from nanoparticles and a first polymer. The nanoparticles include surface-modified nanoparticles. The surface-modified nanoparticles include at least one of crown ether-modified nanoparticles or amino-modified nanoparticles. The preparation method of the carbon dioxide separation membrane includes (a) dispersing the nanoparticles in an organic solvent (I) to prepare a nanoparticle dispersion, adding the first polymer to the nanoparticle dispersion to prepare the casting solution (I), and adding the second polymer to the organic solvent (II) to prepare the casting solution (II), and (b) coating casting solution (I) on the surface of the support layer, curing for the first time, coating casting solution (II) after drying for the first time, curing again, and drying again to obtain the separation membrane.
   USE - The membrane is used in carbon capture and carbon enrichment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide separation membrane has a three-layer structure, and the nano-particle composite layer is formed by matching the nanoparticles and the first polymer to solve the defect problem caused by the uneven dispersion or agglomeration of the nanoparticles in the mixed matrix membrane in the related technology. The membrane solves the problem of selectivity reduction caused by the mismatch between the polymer component and the inorganic nanoparticle phase interface in the traditional mixed matrix film. The carbon dioxide separation membrane has high selectivity to carbon dioxide and high permeability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide separation membrane includes a support layer, a nanoparticle composite layer and a polymer layer sequentially arranged, where the carbon dioxide permeability of the carbon dioxide separation membrane is greater than 3400 Barrer, and the carbon dioxide/nitrogen selectivity coefficient is greater than 45. The nanoparticle composite layer is prepared from nanoparticles and a first polymer, where the mass percentage of the nanoparticles in the nanoparticle composite layer is 90-99.9%. The nanoparticles include surface-modified nanoparticles. The surface-modified nanoparticles include at least one of crown ether-modified nanoparticles or amino-modified nanoparticles. The preparation method of the carbon dioxide separation membrane includes (a) preparation of a casting solution by dispersing the nanoparticles in an organic solvent (I) to prepare a nanoparticle dispersion, adding the first polymer to the nanoparticle dispersion to prepare the casting solution (I), and adding the second polymer to the organic solvent (II) to prepare the casting solution (II), and (b) coating casting solution by coating casting solution (I) on the surface of the support layer, curing for the first time, coating casting solution (II) after drying for the first time, curing again, and drying again to obtain the carbon dioxide separation membrane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional SEM picture of the prepared carbon dioxide separation membrane.
   Bottom layer (100)
   Intermediate layer (101)
   Top layer (102)

PT P
PN CN109200757-A; CN109200757-B
TI Recovering pressure energy from natural gas            ethylene plant by performing carbon dioxide absorption,            performing carbon dioxide desorption, performing            absorbent regeneration cycle, and performing absorbent            pressure recovery.
AU WU D
   HE K
   LI Z
AE SINOPEC SHANGHAI ENG CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI ENG CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 201909531T
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering pressure energy from natural gas                ethylene plant includes performing carbon dioxide                absorption by compressing compressor, cooling,                separating gas and liquid, feeding oxidative                coupling of methane (OCM) product gas to bottom of                absorption tower, adding pressure-increased                low-temperature lean liquid absorbent to carry out                counter-current contact with OCM product gas to                absorb carbon dioxide, and flowing out purified                product gas from the top of absorption tower;                performing carbon dioxide desorption by flowing out                absorbent from bottom of absorption tower, heating                together with high-temperature rich liquid                absorbent through lean-rich absorbent heat                exchanger, adding low-temperature lean liquid                absorbent to desorption column to desorb carbon                dioxide, flashing low-pressure rich liquid                absorbent to flooded flash tank to flash out                hydrocarbon organics to other units; and adding                low-pressure rich liquid absorbent to gas stripping                section to carry out gas stripping                regeneration.
   USE - Method for recovering pressure energy from                natural gas ethylene plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has relative external energy supply                of up to 55.17-67.33% which saves electricity costs                of up to 2.71-3.01 million yuan/year.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering pressure energy from natural gas                ethylene plant comprises:
   (A) performing carbon dioxide absorption by                compressing compressor, cooling, separating gas and                liquid, feeding OCM product gas at outlet of                methane oxidative coupling reactor to bottom of                absorption tower while lowering temperature, adding                pressure-increased low-temperature lean liquid                absorbent to carry out counter-current contact with                OCM product gas to absorb carbon dioxide by the                absorbent, and flowing out purified product gas                from the top of absorption tower;
   (B) performing carbon dioxide desorption by                flowing out absorbent from bottom of absorption                tower, heating together with high-temperature rich                liquid absorbent through lean-rich absorbent heat                exchanger, adding low-temperature lean liquid                absorbent to desorption column to desorb carbon                dioxide, flashing low-pressure rich liquid                absorbent to flooded flash tank to flash out                hydrocarbon organics to other units; and adding                low-pressure rich liquid absorbent to gas stripping                section to carry out gas stripping regeneration,                flowing out carbon dioxide from top of desorption                column, and flowing out lean liquid absorbent from                the bottom of desorption column;
   (C) adding external steam to the bottom of                desorbing tower to remove carbon dioxide, reduce                the partial pressure and increase the temperature                of low-pressure rich liquid absorbent, regenerating                low-pressure lean liquid absorbent, pressurizing to                obtain high-pressure lean liquid absorbent, heat                exchanging to lower the temperature, cooling,                adding to top of absorption tower, and                recirculating carbon dioxide in OCM product gas;                and
   (D) placing pressure recovery device between                lean-rich absorbent heat exchanger and desorption                column, directly connecting high-pressure side                impeller to low-pressure side impeller through                rotating shaft on same pump body, driving                high-pressure side impeller using rich-liquid                absorbent, and driving low-pressure side impeller                through rotating shaft to increase pressure of lean                liquid absorbent at low-pressure end.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN107758665-A; CN207699201-U
TI Purifying food-grade carbon dioxide device comprises pre-processing module, adsorption module, liquefying module, and purifying module.
AU WU Y
   HAN Y
   YUAN J
   XU Y
   LIU M
   LI B
   LIU L
   CUI Y
   PENG S
AE CHANGZHENG ENG CO LTD (CAER-C)
GA 201819998D
AB    NOVELTY - A purifying food-grade carbon dioxide device comprises pre-processing module for pre-processing raw material gas to obtain first stream, adsorption module for adsorbing first stream to obtain second stream, liquefying module for liquefying second stream to obtain third stream, and purifying module for purifying third stream to obtain food-grade carbon dioxide.
   USE - Purifying food-grade carbon dioxide device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has low energy consumption, low operation cost and no coal gasification discharge, and produces high purity food-grade carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) purification of food-grade carbon dioxide comprising pre-processing of raw material gas to obtain first stream, subjecting first stream to adsorption treatment to obtain a second stream, liquefying second stream to obtain third stream, and purifying third stream to obtain food-grade carbon dioxide;
   (2) pre-processing of raw material gas comprising stabilizing raw materials through buffer tank (V-01), feeding first raw material gas into first compressor for one stage compression at a first predetermined pressure to obtain first stream, and cooling to room temperature;
   (3) absorption treatment of first stream comprising pre-desulfurizing of first stream to obtain fourth stream in desulfurization tower (C-01), hydrolyzing carbonyl sulfide (COS) to hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide in fourth stream to obtain fifth stream in hydrolyzing tower (C-02), desulfurizing fifth stream to obtain sixth stream having total sulfur content less than the first threshold value, controlling sixth stream at room temperature, drying to remove water and methanol to obtain seventh stream having water content lower than the second threshold value and methanol content less than the third threshold value, subjecting seventh stream into second compressor at a second predetermined pressure to obtain second stream;
   (4) liquefaction of second stream comprising dividing second stream into two parts, cooling one part into working medium carbon dioxide cooler (E-01), subjecting other part to reboiler (E-04) to a ninth stream, inputting into rectifying tower for refrigeration, collecting thirteenth stream output from working medium carbon dioxide cooler and fourteenth stream output from reboiler into ammonia cooler (E-02) to obtain third stream, and cooling; and
   (5) purification of third stream comprising subjecting third stream into purification tower to obtain eight stream entering to the bottom of rectifying tower, subjecting ninth stream output of reboiler to rectifying tower to obtain tenth stream, re-subjecting tenth stream to rectifying tower to obtain eleventh stream output from the bottom outlet of reboiler, subjecting eleventh stream output in throttle valve at a third predetermined pressure to obtain twelfth stream, and subjecting twelfth stream into gas-liquid separator (V-02) to obtain liquid, and cooling to obtain food-grade carbon dioxide output.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of purifying food-grade carbon dioxide device (Drawing includes non-English language texts).
   Desulfurization tower (C-01)
   Hydrolyzing tower (C-02)
   Working medium carbon dioxide cooler (E-01)
   Ammonia cooler (E-02)
   Reboiler (E-04)
   Buffer tank (V-01)
   Gas-liquid separator (V-02)

   JI G

   WANG F
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 2018540298
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide absorbed lithium orthosilicate material involves using fumed silica as silicon precursor, and lithium nitrate followed by solid phase reaction to prepare lithium orthosilicate.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon dioxide absorbed positive lithium orthosilicate material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare carbon dioxide absorbed lithium orthosilicate material in cost effective manner which has excellent circulation absorption stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The molar ratio of nitrate and silicon dioxide is 3.95-4.05, preferably 4:1. Preferred Process: The lithium orthosilicate is prepared by dissolving gas-phase silicon dioxide into anhydrous ethanol and lithium nitrate at a certain temperature and stirring until complete evaporation followed by drying in vacuum oven, then carrying out solid phase reaction. Preferred Conditions: The stirring temperature is 50-70 degrees C, preferably 60 degrees C the stirring speed is 200-400 revolutions/minute, preferably 300 revolutions/minute, stirring time is 12-36 hours, preferably 24 hours, vacuum drying box temperature is 50-70 degrees C, preferably 60 degrees C and drying time is 8-12 hours, preferably 10 hours, anhydrous ethanol concentration is 99.8%, and mass ratio of the nitrate and anhydrous ethanol is 1:6-1:8, preferably 1:7 and ultrasonic processing time is 15-30 minutes, preferably 20 minutes. The solid phase reaction is calcined and dried fumed silica and lithium nitrate in muffle furnace. The sintering time in muffle furnace is 1.5-2.5 hours, preferably 2 hours, temperature is 700-800 degrees C, preferably 750 degrees C, heating speed is 8-12 degrees C/minute, preferably 10 degrees C/minute and mortar is agate material.

PN WO2022202069-A1
TI Cultivating microalgae, involves obtaining protein            from microalgae, adding protein to compression residue,            and saccharifying compression residue, fermenting sugar            obtained in saccharification step and sugar contained            in juice obtained by squeezing biomass material to            produce fermented product.
AU YOSHITSUGU H
AE HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
GA 2022C4453Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cultivating microalgae, involves                (a) obtaining protein from the microalgae cultured                in the culture step, (b) adding the protein                obtained in the acquisition step as a saccharifying                enzyme to the compression residue obtained by                pressing the biomass material and containing                cellulose, and (c) saccharifying the compression                residue and fermenting at least one of the sugar                obtained in the saccharification step and the sugar                contained in the juice obtained by squeezing the                biomass material to produce a fermented product,                where in the culturing step, carbon dioxide                generated in the fermentation step is supplied to                the microalgae.
   USE - The method is used for cultivating microalgae,                such as Chlamydomonas reinhardtii.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively uses carbon dioxide                generated when ethanol is produced from biomass                materials, and reduces cost of saccharification                process for saccharifying cellulosic biomass.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the culturing step, the                  microalgae which have been subjected to a                  treatment to increase the expression level of the                  protein are cultured. The method further involves                  distilling the fermented product produced in the                  fermentation step to separate the fermented                  product from the distillation residue. In the                  culturing step, nutrients contained in the                  distillation residue obtained in the distillation                  step are supplied to the microalgae. In the                  culturing step, the electric and thermal energy                  obtained by power generation using at least one                  of the solid components not converted to sugar in                  the saccharification treatment and the                  distillation residue obtained in the distillation                  step is used to obtain the drive microalgae                  culture equipment. In the culturing step, carbon                  dioxide generated by power generation using at                  least one of the solid component and the                  distillation residue is supplied to the                  microalgae. In the culturing step, nutrients                  contained in incineration ash generated by                  burning at least one of the solid component and                  the distillation residue to generate power are                  supplied to the microalgae. In the fermentation                  step, the sugar obtained by saccharifying the                  starch accumulated in the microalgae cultured in                  the culture step is also fermented.

TI Preparation of defective nano fiber carbon carrier            coupled iron single atom catalyst used as electrode            material, involves using iron-doped metal organic            framework, polyacrylonitrile by electrostatic spinning            method for preparing iron single atom anchored on nano            fiber carbon carrier.
AU LI P
   WANG J
   ZHAO L
   CHEN J
   LI Z
   WU R
AE UNIV CHINA ELECTRONIC SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UEST-C)
GA 2022358111
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of defective nano fiber carbon                carrier coupled iron single atom catalyst, involves                using iron-doped metal organic framework,                polyacrylonitrile by electrostatic spinning method                for preparing iron single atom anchored on nano                fiber carbon carrier, under the assistance of                dinitrile by high temperature heat treatment under                protection atmosphere.
   USE - Preparation of defective nano fiber carbon                carrier coupled iron single atom catalyst used as                electrode material for electrocatalytic carbon                dioxide reduction reaction, photoelectrochemical                reduction, biochemical reduction and thermal                chemical reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing defective nano                fiber carbon carrier coupled iron single atom                catalyst solving the problem of low specific                surface area of existing catalysts, and low carbon                dioxide reduction reaction activity, and the                prepared iron single-atom nanofiber material                supported by carbon carrier with intrinsic defects                has high catalytic activity and stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation method                  specifically involves (S1) adding iron-doped                  metal organic framework to N,N-dimethyl formamide                  to prepare dispersion with concentration of                  0.07-0.1 mg/mL, (S2) adding polyacrylonitrile to                  dispersion obtained in step (S1), stirring and                  ultrasonically reacting to obtain mixed solution,                  where the mass ratio of polyacrylonitrile and                  N,N-dimethylformamide solution is                  0.08:(1-0.13):1, (S3) transferring the mixed                  solution to needle tube for electrostatic                  spinning to obtain one-dimensional nano-fiber,                  (S4) pre-oxidizing and calcining one-dimensional                  nano fiber in air to obtain sample (A), (S5)                  carrying out high temperature heat-treatment                  under protection atmosphere obtain the sample                  (B), (S6) acid washing the sample (B) in nitric                  acid solution, removing metal iron elementary                  substance particles remaining in material to                  obtain sample (C), and (S7) carrying out                  secondary heat treatment of the obtained sample                  (C) under protection atmosphere, under the                  assistance of nitrogen-containing                  substance.Preferred Composition: In step (S1), the                  mass concentration of dispersion is 0.08 mg/mL,                  and the solution is utilized used for subsequent                  electrostatic spinning.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: In step (S2), the                  content of polyacrylonitrile is 10 wt.% to obtain                  nano fiber with diameter of 200 nm. The mass                  ratio of iron-doped metal organic framework and                  polyacrylonitrile is 1:1.

PN CN113149055-A
TI Preparing calcium carbonate and sulfate using industrial desulfurized gypsum by slurry treating desulfurization gypsum, reacting by introducing carbon dioxide, solid-liquid separating, removing impurity in sulfate solution and concentrating.
AU WANG D
   WANG J
   CHEN Z
   FU X
   HU Y
   WANG P
AE HUNAN XILIN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION MAT (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2021915250
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing calcium carbonate and sulfate using industrial desulfurized gypsum involves slurry treating desulfurization gypsum to obtain gypsum slurry, adjusting to alkaline by alkaline compound, introducing carbon dioxide gas to react, solid-liquid separating to obtain calcium carbonate product and sulfate solution, removing impurity in sulfate solution, and concentrating and crystallizing to obtain sulfate product.
   USE - The method is used for preparing calcium carbonate and sulfate using industrial desulfurized gypsum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can realize maximum resource comprehensive utilization of desulfurization gypsum, obtains calcium carbonate product with high purity and can be returned to flue gas desulfurization process of enterprises in power, fluorine chemical and metal smelting industries, obtains sulfate (ammonium sulfate) and can be used as fertilizer or industrial raw material for agricultural production, realizes virtuous cycle of whole production process, and promotes comprehensive utilization of sulfur, calcium resource and carbon emission reduction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass percentage concentration of gypsum slurry is 1-60%. The gypsum slurry is adjusted to pH value of 7-11. The alkaline compound is liquid ammonia, ammonia water, urea, sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide. Preferred Method: Reaction is done at room temperature to 80 degrees C for 0.2-4 hours. Reaction involves adding alcohol amine compound as catalyst. Impurity removing treatment uses heavy metal adsorbent and/or defluorination agent to remove heavy metal ion and fluorine ion.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The adding amount of alcohol amine compound is 0.01-1.5% of mass of gypsum slurry. The alcohol amine compound is ethanolamine, diethanolamine or triethanolamine.

AU LEE Y H

   RWEI S P

AE UNIV NAT TAIPEI TECHNOLOGY (UNTT-C)
GA 2021A3812V
AB    NOVELTY - Provided is a method for manufacturing a polyamide foam molded body, including: performing a polymerization reaction of a monomer composition for forming a polyamide copolymer; mixing a supercritical fluid of carbon dioxide foaming agent with the polyamide copolymer under pressure for forming a mixture; and releasing the pressure of the mixture for foaming the polyamide copolymer. The dimeric diamine is a C30-40 aliphatic branched diamine having a molecular weight between 450 and 650 g/mole, a melting point of less than -30 and a boiling point of larger than 350. The method provided by the present invention can be performed under realtively low temperature, has a high foaming yield and the foam molded body therefrom is not likely to collapse.

TI Method for producing liquid nitrogen, involves providing a residual gas stream, the residual gas stream is sourced from a retentate stream of a cold membrane, and the residual gas stream includes nitrogen, argon, oxygen.
AU TURNEY M A
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202158065K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a residual gas stream (10). The residual gas stream is sourced from a retentate stream of a cold membrane, and the residual gas stream includes nitrogen, argon, oxygen, and carbon dioxide. The residual gas stream is purified in a front-end purification unit to remove carbon dioxide by forming a purified residual gas stream. The purified residual gas stream is compressed in a first compressor to form a pressurized residual gas stream. The pressurized residual gas stream is introduced to a cold box, and a first unit of the pressurized residual gas stream is cooled in a main heat exchanger.
   USE - Method for producing liquid nitrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method performs substantially the same function to achieve substantially the same result.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the method for producing liquid nitrogen.

   Purified stream (32)

AU CHEN H
   MIN Y
AE SHENZHEN XING PURIFICATION CISCO PROD CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202116302A
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a mask, comprising a mask main body and connected with the mask main body two sides of the ear band, the mask main body comprises two pieces of bending formed symmetrically set of the protective sheet, two pieces of the protective sheet is covered on the human face of the nose of the side are fixed; the adjacent connection of the protective sheet is arc-shaped. PT P
PN CN111362286-A
TI In-situ preparation of lithium carbonate involves preparing lithium alcohol solution, dip-coating alumina porous foam, passing low-humidity carbon dioxide gas, reacting, soaking in ethanol, ultrasonic peeling, filtering, washing, and drying.
AU HUANG J
AE UNIV SHAOXING (UNSX-C)
GA 202063657T
AB    NOVELTY - In-situ preparation of lithium carbonate comprises dissolving lithium chloride in anhydrous ethanol and dispersed ultrasonic extracting at constant temperature to form lithium alcohol solution; dip-coating alumina porous foam by surface extraction and pulling the lithium alcohol solution, and drying at constant temperature to obtain film-coated foam; putting film-coated foam into reaction kettle, passing low-humidity carbon dioxide gas, and reacting for 2-4 hours at constant temperature and pressure; and soaking foam with lithium carbonate film in anhydrous ethanol solution, ultrasonic peeling for 2-4 hours, removing foam, filtering, soaking in sodium hydroxide solution for 30-40 minutes, filtering, washing with absolute ethanol, and drying.
   USE - The method is used for in-situ preparing lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes in-situ reaction system of solid-gas structure to uniformly distribute lithium carbonate particles on the surface of the foam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ultrasonic dispersing is done at 40-80 kHz and 10-30 degrees C. The dip-coating is done by dipping and drawing at 200-400 mm/minute for 3-6 seconds at 10-20 degrees C. The constant-temperature drying is done at 80-90 degrees C. The alumina is pre-processed by soaking alumina in distilled water and anhydrous ethanol, microwave reacting, and drying; and uniformly spraying on the surface of clean foam diluted hydrochloric acid and fast drying. The third step is done by using 2-5% low-humidity carbon dioxide gas and reacting at 100-120 degrees C and 0.2-0.4 MPa. The fourth step is done by ultrasonic peeling at 40-60 kHz and 40-50 degrees C and drying at 80-90 degrees C.

MC E11-P; E33-G
