PN CN111187307-A
TI Extracting aucubin comprises e.g. coarsely crushing fresh Eucommia ulmoides bark, soaking in water, packing in sieve support, immersing in liquid nitrogen, taking out, drying, crushing, and sieving to obtain Eucommia ulmoides bark powder.
AU YE G
AE NANJINH AIMENG LITE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202049992C
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting aucubin comprises (1) coarsely crushing fresh Eucommia ulmoides bark, soaking in water, packing in sieve support, immersing in liquid nitrogen for 30-60 minutes, taking out and placing at room temperature for 5-10 minutes, immersing sieve support in liquid nitrogen 30-60 minutes, and repeating for 2-3 times, (2) drying Eucommia ulmoidesbark, crushing, and sieving through 5-30 meshes sieve at low temperature to obtain Eucommia ulmoides bark powder, (3) uniformly mixing Eucommia ulmoides bark powder in solvent, placing in extraction kettle, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide to extraction kettle, and extracting at 40-50 degrees C with a pressure of 30-45 MPa, and (4) entering carbon dioxide containing extract to separation kettle, and separating under reduced pressure to obtain aucubin.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting aucubin.
   ADVANTAGE - The aucubin has improved yield and quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (4), the pressure of separation kettle is 7-9 MPa with a temperature of 25-30 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (3), the solvent is low boiling point solvent, preferably acetone, ethanol, ether, methanol, and/or methylene chloride. Preferred Process: In the step (2), drying Eucommia ulmoides is carried out in vacuum drying box at 40-52 degrees C with a pressure of 0.009-0.36 MPa to a moisture content of less than 5%. In step (3), the Eucommia ulmoides bark powder is fully mixed with 0.01-0.08 times the weight of biological enzymes before placing in extraction kettle, and allowing to stand for 30-60 minutes, where the biological enzyme is beta glucosidase, pectinase, and/or cellulase.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E06-A02D; E11-Q01C; J01-C; J01-C01A

GA 202024245N
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst composition comprises 40-70 wt.% copper, 20-35 wt.% zinc, 0.1-15 wt.% aluminum, and 0.5-10 wt.% pore-forming agent.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for synthesizing methanol (claimed) from synthesis gas containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has large specific surface area, finer grains of copper oxide and zinc oxide, reasonable pore size distribution, and good catalyst activity and thermal stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the preparation of catalyst comprising co-precipitating the copper-zinc mixture using alkali as precipitator, controlling process pH and precipitating end pH, washing to obtain catalyst precursor, mixing catalyst precursor and alumina carrier, pulping to obtain catalyst slurry, granulating and drying by spray drying, mixing spray-dried particles with pore-forming agent, calcining, and molded by tableting, where the atomic molar ratio of copper-zinc mixture is 2-3.5.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the method, the precipitator is 1-2 mol/l sodium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate, or sodium hydroxide; the copper-zinc mixture is a mixed solution of copper nitrate and zinc nitrate; the preheating temperature of precipitator before usage is 50-80 degrees C; The process pH is controlled at 7.5-8.5, and precipitating end pH is controlled at 7-7.3; the adding amount of the alumina carrier is 5-10% of the support; the calcining is carried out at 300-400 degrees C for 30-100 minutes. The pore-forming agent is ammonium bicarbonate, urea, and/or ammonium carbonate.

PN CN110860294-A; CN110860294-B
TI Preparation of cooper lead composite metal            catalyst used for electrochemical reduction reaction of            carbon dioxide by dissolving ionic liquid in deionized            water to obtain ionic liquid water solution, adjusting            pH, and adding lead nitrate.
AU ZHAO Y
   CAO D
   ZHANG X
   XU X
   LIANG J
   WEI T
AE UNIV HEBEI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYKJ-C)
GA 202020967M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cooper lead composite metal                catalyst comprises dissolving ionic liquid in                deionized water to obtain ionic liquid water                solution, adjusting the pH of the solution at 1-6,                adding lead nitrate, agitating uniformly to obtain                mixed solution B, introducing current to carry out                constant current deposition reaction on substrate                by the mixed solution B, and after the reaction is                finished, washing and drying a deposition sample to                obtain copper-lead composite metal catalyst, where                the ionic liquid is 1-propyl-3-methylimidazolium                bromine salt, 1-propyl-3-methylimidazolium                tetrafluoroborate or                1-aminoethyl-3-methylimidazolium bromine                salt.
   USE - The method is for preparation of cooper lead                composite metal catalyst used for electrochemical                reduction reaction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and suitable for                industrial production and has convenient operation                and mild condition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The hydrochloric acid is                  concentrated hydrochloric acid. The concentration                  of lead nitrate in the mixed solution B is                  0.030-0.090 mol/L. The washing step is conducted                  using deionized water until neutral, and drying                  is conducted using high purity nitrogen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The substrate is copper                  foam.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The concentration of ionic                  liquid water solution A is 0.005-0.05 mol/L. The                  pH of ionic liquid water solution is adjusted at                  1-3 by adding hydrochloric acid under                  stirring.

AE ZHANG X (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2019A6644T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pepper oil comprises: cleaning fresh pepper after drying at low temperature, taking edible oil and pepper at a weight ratio of 6:1, heating to 80-100 degrees C and keeping the temperature for 15-25 minutes, filtering, crushing the filter residue to 60 meshes, putting the crushed residue in supercritical extraction kettle using food grade carbon dioxide as extracting solvent, extraction pressure of 25-27 MPa, extracting temperature of 45-55 degrees C, carbon dioxide flow rate of 1200-1400 L/hour, and total extraction time of 2-4 hours; separating the extracting liquid and adding into the filtrate to obtain the pepper oil.

   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively extract the effective ingredients in the pepper, avoids waste of the effective ingredients in the pepper, and improves utilization rate of the pepper.

TI Refining cotton black liquor by using residual            alkali heat treatment comprises e.g. adjusting            temperature and pH value of refined cotton black liquor            and enter separator reaction tank, and adding            polystyrene or polymethysilane solution.
AU RAN C
   ZHOU G
   YANG Y
   MA K
   XING Y
   MA L
AE UNIV DALIAN MINZU (UYND-C)
GA 201982188S
AB    NOVELTY - Refining cotton black liquor by using residual                alkali heat treatment comprises adjusting                temperature and pH value of the refined cotton                black liquor and enter the separator reaction tank,                and adding polystyrene (PS) or polymethysilane                (PMS) solution to the front end of the reaction                tank in the reaction, activating PS or PMS solution                in the pool by the refining cotton black liquor                residual heat to generate strong oxidation activity                of sulfate and hydrogen-oxygen reacting with the                refractory macromolecular organic pollutants in the                refined cotton black liquor, and decomposes it into                small molecular organic matter, carbon dioxide and                water.
   USE - The method is useful for refining cotton black                liquor by using residual alkali heat                treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: decomposes macromolecular organic                pollutants into small molecules of organic matter,                carbon dioxide and water to achieve efficient                removal of organic pollutants.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i)                  adjusting the temperature of the refined cotton                  black liquor to 60-80 degrees C, pH value                  adjustment to 10-12, through the pump or liquid                  level difference into the reciprocating diaphragm                  reactor, (ii) adding 40-60 l, 2-4 mmol/l PS or                  PMS solution per 1m3 of refined cotton black                  liquor at the front end of the separator reaction                  cell, and adding PS or PMS solution to the                  separator reaction tank is quickly refined,                  activation of residual heat in the cotton black                  liquor produces strong oxidizing sulfate and                  hydrogen-oxygen, sulfate and hydrogen-oxygen                  fully reacts with macromolecular refractory                  organics in the refined cotton black liquor to                  achieve high efficiency of refined cotton black                  liquor deal, (iii) evaluating the efficiency of                  the method for treating the refined cotton black                  liquor by analyzing the COD and the chromaticity                  values from the water outlet. The optimum                  treatment parameters are 50ml2 - 2.5mmol/L PS or                  PMS solution is added to 1m3 refined cotton black                  liquor, the activation temperature is 70 degrees                  C, pH value 11, and the treatment is 100-150                  minutes. When potassium persulfate was activated                  by using residual heat and residual alkali, the                  potassium persulfate concentration is 2 mmol/L,                  and the temperature of the purified cotton black                  liquor was 70 degrees C, pH value 11, t = 100                  minutes. When potassium peroxymonosulfate was                  activated by using residual heat and residual                  alkali, the concentration of potassium                  peroxymonosulfate is 2.5 mmol/L, and the                  temperature of the purified cotton black liquor                  was 70 degrees C, pH value 11, t = 150 minutes.                  The separator reaction cell is a reciprocating                  separator reaction cell.

PN CN110127625-A
TI Hydrogen sulfide decomposition method using combined carrier gas by mixing raw material gas containing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide as carrier gas, performing hydrogen sulfide decomposition, separating, and alkali liquid absorbing.
AU ZHANG T
   ZHANG J
   ZHU Y
   SUN F
   XU W
   SHI N
   JIN M
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC QINGDAO SAFETY ENG INST (SNPC-C)
GA 2019735723
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrogen sulfide decomposition method using combined carrier gas comprises adding raw material gas containing hydrogen sulfide into gas supply-gas distribution unit, mixing with 0.01-99.99 wt.% carbon dioxide as carrier gas, adding to plasma reactor, and performing hydrogen sulfide decomposition reaction under dielectric barrier discharge condition of 5-30 kV, 200-30000 Hz and 0.1-100 A; and adding to gas-liquid separator, separating, adding first gas-phase material to alkali liquid absorption tower to obtain second gas-phase substance and hydrogen sulfide-containing alkali solution, adding second gas-phase material to carrier gas separator to separate carrier gas, analyzing hydrogen sulfide-containing alkali solution to obtain hydrogen sulfide gas, and recycling hydrogen sulfide gas back to gas supply-gas distribution unit.
   USE - The method is for hydrogen sulfide decomposition using combined carrier gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has higher hydrogen sulfide conversion rate than the existing technology.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide has volume of 0.1-99.9%, preferably 15-95%. The hydrogen sulfide-containing alkali solution has concentration of 2-80 wt.%. The raw material gas containing hydrogen sulfide is exhaust gas from petroleum refining process, hydrogen sulfide-containing natural gas, coal cleaning process hydrogen sulfide exhaust gas or hydrogen sulfide-containing waste gas generated during mineral processing. Preferred Method: The decomposition reaction is done at 0-800 degrees C and at - 0.06-0.6 MPa for 1-120x 10-5 seconds. The analyzing reaction is done at 70-180 degrees C and at 0.01-0.2 MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali absorption tower contains alkaline organic amine solvent comprising monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, diisopropanolamine and/or N-methyldiethanolamine.

PN CN110100943-A
TI Marine collagen powder useful for improving immunity comprises raw fish skin, sodium tripolyphosphate, taurine, and calcium hydroxide.
AU ZHOU Y
AE ZHOU Y (ZHOU-Individual)
GA 201970778Y
AB    NOVELTY - Marine collagen powder comprises 30-40 pts. wt. raw fish skin, 2-5 pts. wt. sodium tripolyphosphate, 10-12 pts. wt. taurine, and 4-8 pts. wt. calcium hydroxide.
   USE - The collagen is useful for improving immunity (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical, convenient to produce, and easy to popularize; and produces product that has an effect of improving human immunity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the marine collagen powder, comprising (i) crushing and washing squid skin, (ii) mixing calcium hydroxide with water, and adding the material obtained in the step (i) and polyethylene glycol-b-poly(N-decyldodecylacrylamide), stirring and reacting under a carbon dioxide atmosphere, freezing and thawing, and performing vacuum treatment, (iii) modifying chitosan, (iv) heating the material obtained in the step (ii) under a carbon dioxide atmosphere, adding taurine, sodium tripolyphosphate and modified chitosan, stirring and reacting, and spraying and drying, and (v) performing index analysis on the material obtained in step (iii).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The collagen powder further comprises 15-20 pts. wt. modified chitosan and 3-6 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol-b-poly(N-decyldodecylacrylamide). The collagen powder comprises 40 pts. wt. squid skin, 10 pts. wt. taurine, 8 pts. wt. calcium hydroxide, 15 pts. wt. modified chitosan, and 6 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol-b-poly(N-decyldodecylacrylamide). Preferred Components: The raw fish skin is one of tilapia skin, squid skin or pufferfish skin. Preferred Method: The method further comprises (i) crushing squid skin by using a crusher, sieving through 4-mesh sieve to obtain a squid skin, firstly washing the squid skin with isopropyl alcohol, secondly washing with a sodium chloride solution, filtering, and drying, (ii) mixing calcium hydroxide with water at a mass ratio of 1:140-1:160, adding a calcium hydroxide with a mass of 4-8 times the mass of the obtained material of the step (i) and calcium hydroxide with a mass of 1-2 times the mass of polyethylene glycol-b-poly(N-decyldodecylacrylamide), stirring in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, performing a freezing and thawing cycle to obtain pretreated squid pieces, pretreating the squid pieces under vacuum, and allowing to stand for 30 minutes, (iii) mixing chitosan with sodium hydroxide solution at a mass ratio of 1:10-1:12, adding chloroacetic acid having a mass of 1-3 times of chitosan, stirring and reacting, adjusting the pH to neutrality, then centrifuging, separating, taking the upper layer liquid, mixing the upper layer liquid and absolute ethanol in a volume ratio of 1:2, filtering, and drying, (4) heating the material obtained in the step (ii) to 20-30 degrees C, where the the mass of the substance obtained in the step (ii) is 0.07-0.1 times the mass of the substance is 0.002-0.008 times the mass of sodium tripolyphosphate, stirring and reacting to obtain a mixed billet liquid, uniformly mixing the mixed billet liquid and taurine at a mass ratio of 20:1-25:1, stirring and reacting, and spraying and drying, and (v) performing index analysis on the substance obtained in the step (iv). In the step (ii), the freezing and thawing cycle is carried out by freezing at - 20 degrees C for 1 hour, then thawing for at 20 degrees C for 1 hour, and repeating the freezing and thawing process.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The modified chitosan is a carboxymethyl modified chitosan. In the step (iii), the chitosan is chitosan having a degree of deacetylation of 70-80%. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the polyethylene glycol-b-poly(N-decyldodecylacrylamide) is prepared by mixing N-decyldodecylacrylamide and polyethylene glycol in a mass ratio of 2:1, and performing free radical polymerization. The N-decyldodecyl acrylamide is prepared by mixing acryloyl chloride and N-hydroxysuccinimide at a mass ratio of 1:4, adding 5-8 times the mass of acryloyl chloride to tetrahydrofuran, stirring and reacting, concentrating by rotary evaporation to obtain a first intermediate, mixing the first intermediate with triethylamine at a mass ratio of 3:1, adding 1-2 times the mass of the first intermediate to dodecanediamine and the first intermediate dimethyl sulfoxide having a mass of 5-8 time, stirring and reacting, concentrating by rotary evaporation to obtain a second intermediate, mixing the second intermediate dimethylacetamide dimethyl acetal at a mass ratio of 2:1, adding a second intermediate mass of 10-12 times of tetrahydrofuran, stirring and reacting, and concentrating by performing rotary evaporation.
   ACTIVITY - Immunostimulant.

PN CN110089655-A; CN110089655-B
TI Kiwi fruit and konjak solid beverage comprises            kiwi fruit juice, konjac gel liquid, kiwi fruit seed            liquid extract and kiwi fruit seed powder.
AU HAN X
   CHEN W
   HAN L
   CHEN G
AE SICHUAN SHIWEITIAN AGRIC CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2019702046
AB    NOVELTY - Kiwi fruit and konjak solid beverage comprises                40-50 pts. wt. kiwi fruit juice, 30-40 pts. wt.                konjac gel liquid, 6-12 pts. wt. kiwi fruit seed                liquid extract and 2-4 pts. wt. kiwi fruit seed                powder.
   USE - Used as kiwi fruit and konjak solid                beverage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing kiwi konjak solid beverage comprising (1)                mixing kiwi juice with kiwi seed extract to obtain                mixture A, (2) mixing mixture A three times with                the konjac gel solution, adding in supercritical                reaction device, sealing system, introducing carbon                dioxide, heating at 45-55 degrees C, stirring for                3-4 hours, relieving pressure, heating at 60-70                degrees C, stirring for 0.6-0.8 hours,                re-introducing carbon dioxide, stirring for 0.5-0.7                hours, releasing pressure to obtain mixed liquid B,                (3) spray drying mixture B, adding kiwi seed powder                to obtain a kiwi konjak solid beverage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The kiwi fruit and                  konjak solid beverage comprises 45 pts. wt. kiwi                  fruit juice, 35 pts. wt. konjac gel liquid, 9                  pts. wt. kiwi fruit seed liquid extract and 3                  pts. wt. kiwi fruit seed powder. Preferred                  Method: The kiwi juice is prepared by peeling                  fresh kiwi fruit, washing, cutting into pieces,                  pulping, separating seeds, adding 4-6 times                  amount of drinking water to the pulp, and beating                  in the beater to obtain kiwi juice. The konjac                  gel is prepared by mixing konjac flour and konjac                  powder, adding 10-12 times of the warm water to                  dissolve, stirring for 6 minutes with a constant                  temperature magnetic stirrer, allowing to stand                  at room temperature for 45-55 minutes to obtain                  konjac gel. The kiwi seed powder is obtained by                  washing kiwifruit seeds, drying, breaking into                  diameter particles of less than 2 mu m to obtain                  kiwi seed powder. The kiwi seed extract is                  prepared by adding 8-12 times of drinking water                  for low-temperature extraction of multi-stage                  countercurrent column. In the step (2),                  introducing carbon dioxide at 20-30 MPa, and re                  introducing carbon dioxide at 15-25 MPa. The                  low-temperature extraction conditions of the                  multi-stage countercurrent column are extraction                  temperature is 30-40 degrees C and extraction                  time is 60-90 minutes. The mass ratio of konjac                  flour and konjac powder is 1:1.2-1.4, the warm                  water temperature is 37-40 degrees C, the                  constant temperature magnetic stirrer stirring                  speed is 120-150 revolutions/minute.

PN CN109354331-A
TI Industrial wastewater treatment involves providing wastewater buffer cooling pool, storing pre-discharged wastewater in wastewater buffer cooling tank and lowering to room temperature, and then controlling flow to mix wastewater.
AU WANG J
   YE Z
   MIAO C
   ZHOU Z
AE LIAONING ZHONGZHOU DESHUI ENVIRONMENTAL (LIAO-Non-standard)
GA 201920671B
AB    NOVELTY - Industrial wastewater treatment involves providing wastewater buffer cooling pool, storing pre-discharged wastewater in a wastewater buffer cooling tank and lowering to room temperature, and then controlling flow to mix wastewater in the buffer cooling pool with newly-discovered wastewater to maintain temperature of the mixed wastewater at 60-70 degrees C. The mixed wastewater is passed through an aeration tank, where excess carbon dioxide is introduced. The aeration tank adopts a closed aeration method, where the carbon dioxide overflowing from the top of the pool is collected and pressurized. The aeration is performed by using micropores. The biochemical system adopts an anaerobic fixed membrane bioreactor, where the bio-filler has a diameter of 80-100 mm. The filler is a modified bio biological filler.
   USE - Method for treating industrial wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method treats industrial wastewater in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The modified bio-filler is prepared washing 50-80 pts. wt. diatomaceous earth three times with acetone and deionized water, air drying, and then mixing with 300-400 pts. wt. sulfuric acid and 0.2-0.7 mol/l L-acetic acid, 1-benzyl-3-ethylpiperidine-1-3-dicarboxylic acid, 0.06-0.14 mol/l mixed acid solution, 3-pyridine boronic acid and 0.04-0.12 pts. wt. pentanediol at 50-80 degrees C with stirring for 200-300 minutes. The resulting mixture is filtered, and washed with water. The filter residue is mixed with 5-12 pts. wt. N-methylol acrylamide and 6-15 pts. wt. 3-chloro-2-hydroxypropyltrimethylamine hydrochloride and 200-300 pts. wt. deionized water at 60-80 degrees C with stirring for 60-120 min, and then mixed with 20-30 pts. wt. molecular sieve, 0.1-0.8 pts. wt. tris(2,2,6,6-tetramethyl-3,5-heptanedionate) hydrazine, and 10-20 pts. wt. silica sol at 250 degrees C for 60-90 minutes. The microbial growth promoter is spermine, spermidine, hydroxylamine hydrochloride, hydroxylamine sulfate or hydroxylamine phosphate.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN CN109315368-A
TI Slow-release mosquito sticky paper comprises silicone oil paper as substrate and covered with synthetic resin layer and the titanium dioxide nano-particles and the formic acid molecules uniformly dispersed in synthetic resin layer.
AU LIU Y
AE FOSHAN EXCELLENT ELECTRICALS MFG CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201916806U
AB    NOVELTY - Slow-release mosquito sticky paper comprises silicone oil paper as substrate. The silicone oil paper layer is covered with a synthetic resin layer. The titanium dioxide nano-particles and the formic acid molecules are uniformly dispersed in the synthetic resin layer.
   USE - Used as slow-release mosquito sticky paper.
   ADVANTAGE - The paper is environmentally friendly and has no toxicity and no harmful chemical molecules.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the slow-release mosquito sticky paper, comprising (i) preparing supercritical carbon dioxide solution of n-butyl titanate, i.e. homogeneous solution A; (ii) preparing supercritical carbon dioxide solution of aqueous formic acid solution, i.e. homogeneous solution B; and (iii) mixing homogeneous solution A with homogeneous solution B under a supercritical state to form mixed reaction solution, drying and spraying the silicone oil paper.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The titanium dioxide nano-particle content is 0.5-4.5 wt.% and the formic acid content is 1-3.5 wt.%. The synthetic resin is an vinyl base synthetic resin and the thickness of synthetic resin layer is 0.3-2 mm. The volume ratio of n-butyl titanate to carbon dioxide supercritical fluid in the homogeneous solution A is 0.1-1 g:10 ml. The volume ratio of the synthetic resin to the carbon dioxide supercritical fluid in the homogeneous solution B is 1-5 g:10 ml. The volume ratio of homogeneous solution A and homogeneous solution B is 0.1-0.7:1. The pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide solution is 15-20 MPa.

PT P
PN CN109232989-A; CN109232989-B
TI Preparing composite nano calcium carbonate for            rubber, involves preparing the calcium            carbonate-containing slurry, heating calcium carbonate            slurry and then maintaining the temperature in the            state of stirring and ultrasonication.
AU WANG P
   XU H
   LI G
   ZHAO Z
   CHEN Z
   SUN B
   ZHANG M
AE HEBEI CHEM & PHARM VOCATIONAL & TECH              COL (HEBE-Non-standard)
   HEBEI LIXIN CHEM CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 201913306F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite nano calcium carbonate                involves preparing the calcium carbonate-containing                slurry of 7-15 wt.%. The calcium carbonate slurry                is heated to 30-80 degrees C and maintaining the                temperature, in the state of stirring and                ultrasonication. The carbon dioxide is introduced                into the calcium carbonate slurry. The obtained                mixture is added with sodium silicate solution. The                concentration of sodium silicate in the sodium                solution is 0.10 to 0.20 mol/l, and the volume of                the sodium silicate solution is 15 to 25% of the                volume of the calcium carbonate slurry. When the pH                reaches 7 to 7.5, continue to pass carbon dioxide                for 3-8 minutes under stirring and ultrasonication.                The mixture is aged, dried and dehydrated.
   USE - Method for preparing composite nano calcium                carbonate for rubber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare composite nano                calcium carbonate in a cost-effective manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The grain diameter of                  the nano calcium carbonate is 35-45nm. The                  reaction pressure is 150-180mpa. The speed of the                  stirring is 10-50rpm. The ultrasonic intensity is                  25-30khz. The aging is stewing for 50-90 minu at                  30-80 degrees C. The drying is 105 to 115 degrees                  C and dried to a weight difference of less than                  0.3mg. Preferred Components: The preparation of                  the nano-calcium carbonate slurry and the                  preparation of the composite nano-calcium                  carbonate for the rubber are carried out in the                  same carbonization tower. Preferred Process: The                  preparation method of the nano calcium carbonate                  slurry is filling carbondioxide in calcium                  hydroxide solution to concentration of 5-11 wt.%,                  carbonizing at 50-70 degrees C, until the pH                  value of the solution is 7-7.5.

GA 201900870X

PN CN108795523-A
TI Device useful for preparing natural gas comprises            e.g. a solar energy and/or wind power generation unit,            an electric storage and conditioning unit, an            electrolysis water hydrogen production unit, and a            methanation unit.
AU WANG X
   HOU J
   LI Y
   SONG P
   YAO H
   HOU H
   ZHANG Y
   MU X
   ZHANG B
   WANG S
   GAO Z
AE CHINA NAT OFFSHORE OIL CORP (CNOO-C)
   CNOOC GAS & POWER GROUP CO LTD (CNOO-C)
GA 201892702L
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises a solar energy and/or wind                power generation unit, an electric storage and                conditioning unit, an electrolysis water hydrogen                production unit, a hydrogen storage tank, a gas                mixing tank, a desulfurization unit and a                methanation unit. The gas mixing tank is connected                to a low temperature methanol washing unit in a                coal natural gas process.
   USE - The device is useful for preparing natural gas                by coupling solar energy and/or wind power hydrogen                production with a coal-to-natural gas process                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device: improves natural gas production;                and reduces the carbon dioxide emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing natural gas comprising preparing                hydrogen-containing mixed gas by solar energy                and/or wind power generation, reacting hydrogen in                the hydrogen-containing gas mixture with a carbon                dioxide-rich gas discharged from coal-based natural                gas to obtain natural gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The device further                  comprises a compression unit and a                  decarburization unit which are sequentially                  connected after the methanation unit. The solar                  and/or wind power generation unit comprises a                  solar and wind power combination unit, a solar                  power unit and/or a wind power unit. The                  electrical storage and conditioning unit is                  provided with a small amount of electrical                  storage and regulation functions through the                  charge and discharge device. Preferred Method:                  The natural gas is specifically prepared by (i)                  carrying out intermittent and/or continuous power                  generation by the solar and/or wind power                  generation unit, (ii) performing adjustment of                  the electric power storage and adjustment unit,                  and supplying electrolyzed water to electrolyze                  water to obtain a gas mixture containing                  hydrogen, (iii) separating gas mixture containing                  hydrogen by membrane to obtain high purity                  hydrogen and a remaining mixed gas, and entering                  high purity hydrogen gas in hydrogen storage tank                  and the gas mixing tank, (iv) dissolving carbon                  dioxide relaxation gas of hydrogen sulfide in                  low-temperature methanol washing unit in the                  coal-based natural gas process into the gas                  mixing tank, mixing with high purity hydrogen to                  obtain mixed gas, (v) desulfurizing mixed gas by                  desulfurization unit, entering in methanation                  unit, and reacting to generate methane gas, and                  (vi) passing methane gas through the compression                  unit, entering in decarburization unit to process                  as carbon dioxide removal gas, and mixing with                  coal natural gas process gas to obtain natural                  gas. The volume ratio of the hydrogen containing                  gas mixture entering in gas mixing tank to purge                  gas of the hydrogen sulfide-removing carbon                  dioxide is 8-2:1, the purity of hydrogen in the                  hydrogen-containing gas mixture is greater than                  50 mol%, the concentration of carbon dioxide in                  purge gas of the hydrogen sulfide removed carbon                  dioxide is greater than 50 mol%. The                  desulfurization unit reduces the sulfide content                  in the mixed gas to below 200 ppb by a                  copper-based desulfurizing agent. The conditions                  for the methanation unit reaction are: the                  temperature is 300-700 degrees C and pressure is                  0.5-9.0 MPaG. The methanation unit reaction                  comprises fixed bed adiabatic cycle methanation                  process, fixed bed adiabatic non-cyclic                  methanation process or fluidized bed methanation                  process. The electrolysis is an alkaline aqueous                  solution electrolysis hydrogen production                  technique, a solid polymer hydrogen production                  technique, or a high temperature solid oxide                  hydrogen production technique. The                  decarburization is an alcohol amine                  decarburization or a molecular sieve                  decarburization.

Preferred Properties: The step (3) comprises pulverizing to particle diameter of 100 mesh. In step (4), the volume of the reaction tank is 10 m3. Preferred Composition: The amount of pectinase is 50 mg. The amount of yeast powder is 10-30 mg.

AU CHEN L
   ZHAO Z
   WANG Y
   CHEN Z
AE CHINA EARTHQUAKE AGENCY ENG MECHANICS RE (CEQA-C)
   CHINA EARTHQUAKE AGENCY ENG MECHANICS (CEQA-C)
GA 201885009Y
AB    NOVELTY - A soil saturation of sand measuring method placing a sand sample into a sample container while evacuating the sand sample and the constant head device, introducing the carbon dioxide into the vacuumed sand sample and the constant head device, repeating the steps multiple times, vacuuming the sand sample and the constant head saturation device, using a constant head device to saturate the sand sample with a constant head until the water is immersed in the surface of the sand sample, exposing the saturated sand sample with the normal head to the atmospheric first pressure, vacuuming the sand sample again to second pressure, measuring the change in the liquid level in the sample container under different pressures and calculating the saturated sand sample represented by the expression as given in the specification.

PN CN108675817-A
TI Preparing iron-red ceramic fiber composite material, where ceramic fiber blanket is used to saturate and absorb the prepolymer and gel under specific conditions, supercritical fluid carbon dioxide is dried to form new composite material.
AU SONG D
   WANG B
   CAO Y
   CAI Y
   TIAN G
AE SINOPEC ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOGY SERVICE CO LTD (SINO-Non-standard)
   GUIZHOU AEROSPACE WUJIANG ELECTRO-MECHAN (CAES-C)
GA 201884593T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing iron-red ceramic fiber composite material, where a ceramic fiber blanket is used to saturate and absorb the prepolymer and gel under specific conditions, the supercritical fluid carbon dioxide (CO2), which is dried to form a new composite material of iron red/ceramic fiber, involves preparing a prepolymer, performing composite molding of new composite materials of iron red/ceramic fiber, aging and modifying surface of new composite materials of iron red/ceramic fiber, drying new composite materials of iron red/ceramic fibers.
   USE - Method for preparing iron oxide red ceramic fiber composite material used for manufacturing industry pipeline (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The iron oxide red ceramic fiber composite material has excellent heat preservation performance, mechanical property, fireproof and waterproof performance, and is convenient for construction, enables to avoid the solvent replacement, has simple operation, which is controllable and can provide continuous production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Conditions: The raw material of the prepolymer includes a silicon source precursor, a hydrolysis catalyst, an additive, and a solvent. The silicon source precursor includes one or a mixture of two or more of tetraethyl orthosilicate, methyl orthosilicate, and trimethylchlorosilane. The hydrolysis catalyst includes one or a mixture of two or more of pure water, hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, sulfuric acid, oxalic acid, acetic acid, ammonia water, ammonium carbonate, ammonium hydrogencarbonate. The additive includes one or more a mixture of iron red, ethyl cellulose, N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, formamide, acetamide and an alkylating agent. The solvent includes one or a mixture of two or more kinds of linear alcohols having a mass fraction of 95% or more. The ceramic fiber blanket is a uniform plate or carpet having a density of not more than 300 kg/m 3 made of ceramic fiber having a diameter of 2-5 mu m. The prepolymer is prepared in the volume ratio of ethyl orthosilicate, 95% absolute ethanol, hydrolysis catalyst, additive which is 10:(40-200):(1-5):(0.1-1.5). The aging is to soak the wet gel in an absolute ethanol solution at 35-45 degrees C for 24 hours. The surface modification is performed by surface-modifying the aged wet gel into an anhydrous ethanol solution with a surface modifier at 50-60 degrees C. The surface modifier includes one of hexamethyldisilazane and hexamethyldisiloxane. The control temperature of the CO2 fluid is 45-55 degrees C, the pressure is 10-18 megapascal (MPa), the flow rate of the CO2 fluid is 1000-3000 kg/hour, and the drying time is 3-8 hours. Preferred Process: The iron-red ceramic fiber composite material is prepared by using ceramic fiber blanket to saturate and absorb the prepolymer and gel under specific conditions, where the supercritical fluid carbon dioxide (CO2) is dried to form a new composite material of iron red/ceramic fiber, preparing a prepolymer, performing composite molding of new composite materials of iron red/ceramic fiber, aging and modifying surface of new composite materials of iron red/ceramic fiber, drying new composite materials of iron red/ceramic fibers. The drying is performed by putting the aged red iron/ceramic fiber new composite material into a supercritical drying device, injecting the carbon dioxide (CO2) fluid into the drying kettle, adjusting the temperature and pressure in the drying kettle to contact the material in the supercritical state of the CO2 fluid, dissolving the solvent in the material in the supercritical fluid, collecting the solvent from the discharge port of the separation vessel, to prepare a novel composite material of hydrophilic iron red/ceramic fiber, where alternatively, the drying is performed by putting a surface-modified iron red/ceramic fiber new composite material into a drying kettle, injecting the carbon dioxide (CO2) fluid into the drying kettle, adjusting the temperature and pressure in the drying kettle to contact the material in the supercritical state of the CO2 fluid, so that the solvent in the material is dissolved in the supercritical fluid, collecting solvent from the outlet of the separation vessel to prepare a novel composite material of hydrophobic iron red/ceramic fiber.
DC A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

   (B) introducing carbon dioxide to a pressure of 1.0-3.0 mega Pascal; and
   (C) carrying out constant temperature and constant pressure reaction at 80-120 degrees C for 0.5-2.5 hours, until reaction is completed to obtain cyclic carbonate.
   ..
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The dicationic ionic liquid and the epoxy compound are added to the reaction vessel in a molar ratio of 1:100, and carbon dioxide is introduced at a pressure of 1.5 mega Pascal. The constant temperature and constant pressure reaction is carried out at 100 degrees C for 2.0 hours.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1=CH3 and group of formulae (II-III).

PN CN108529652-A; CN108529652-B
TI Utilization process of industrial sodium chloride resource comprises e.g. diluting industrial stripping aqueous ammonia, and dissolving industrial sodium chloride salt in diluted industrial stripping aqueous ammonia.
AU LI L
   MA Y
   LI P
   WEI G
   ZHANG Y
   CHANGSHUAI L
   PENG H
   YANG G
   HE Y
   LI F
   LI C
AE CHAMBROAD CHEM IND RES INST CO LTD (CHAM-Non-standard)
GA 201874852Y
AB    NOVELTY - Utilization process of industrial sodium chloride resource comprises (1) diluting industrial stripping aqueous ammonia, dissolving industrial sodium chloride salt in diluted industrial stripping aqueous ammonia to obtain aqueous ammonia salt solution; adding sodium carbonate agent to reaction, allowing to stand, taking supernatant, and decoloring to obtain qualified ammonia brine; (2) heating the qualified ammonia brine to 30-40 degrees C, inletting carbon dioxide to react, reacting carbon dioxide, industrial stripping aqueous ammonia, industrial waste salt sodium chloride to obtain sodium bicarbonate precipitate; filtering, passing mother solution into second unit for processing, washing sodium bicarbonate with washing solution, and calcining to obtain sodium carbonate; and (3) adding concentrated hydrochloric acid to mother liquor to adjust pH to 6-6.5, vigorously stirring the reaction, vaporising and concentrating, cooling and crystallizing, filtering to obtain ammonium chloride product.
   USE - The method is useful for utilization process of industrial sodium chloride resource.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the mass fraction of the industrial stripping aqueous ammonia is 8-12 wt.%. In the step (1), the concentration of sodium chloride in the aqueous solution of ammonia salt is 270 plus minus 5 g/l. In the step (1), added amount of sodium carbonate is 0.13-0.3 wt.% of ammonia brine. In the step (2), the washing liquid is saturated sodium bicarbonate aqueous solution. Preferred Method: In the step (1), decoloring by using activated carbon, added amount of activated carbon is 0.4-0.7 wt.% of mass of ammonia brine. In the step (1), qualified ammonia brine index has hardness of not less than 50 mg/l, suspended substance of not less than 50 mg/l, and water chroma of not less than 200. In the step (2), introducing the carbon dioxide gas at rate of 1-2.5 l/minute, and access time is 12-24 hours. In the method, vaporising and concentrating under pressure of -0.09 MPa and temperature of 80 degrees C, and cooling crystallization temperature is greater than or equal to 10 degrees C.

PN CN108470891-A; CN108470891-B
TI Preparing silicon-carbon negative material involves taking silicon, carbon dioxide source and water, measuring silica, carbon source and water to prepare slurry, using sand grinder by wet grinding, freezing and drying.
AU WU Z
   WU C
   GUO X
   XIANG W
   ZHONG B
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
   NANCHONG ZHONGXIN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (NANC-Non-standard)
GA 201869994P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing silicon-carbon negative material involves taking silicon, carbon dioxide source and water with mass ratio of 30-80:5-15:60-120, measuring 50-100 mu m silica, carbon source and water to prepare slurry, using sand grinder wet grinding for 4-5hours, then freezing and drying to obtain nano-grade silicon oxide, performing carbonization process by high temperature to obtain carbon oxide silicon oxide, using silicon oxide, carbon, magnesium and sodium chloride with mass ratio is 1:1:1-1:1:10 at 600-750 degrees C, treating with acid cleaning, washing and drying to obtain silicon nano particles, mixing oxide graphene solution ultrasonically by spray pyrolysis to prepare silicon-carbon negative material.
   USE - Method for preparing silicon-carbon negative material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare silicon-carbon negative material is simple and cost-effective manner which has good structure stability, strong conductivity and ion transmission ability of material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon source is selected from glucose, phenolic resin, polyethylene pyrrolidone and polyacrylonitrile. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of freezing and drying is -45 degrees C. The carbonization temperature is 450-700 degrees C, and carbonizing time is for 2-4 hours. The acid is washed by using 2 moles/L hydrochloric acid to remove impurities, then using 5% hydrofluoric acid for 30 minutes, remove unreacted silicon dioxide, washed with de-ionized water and ethanol. Preferred Process: The silicon-carbon negative material is characterized by using ultrasonic process to form spray granulation function, mixing spherical particles by using hydrogen/argon mixed gas as carrier gas into vertical tubular furnace at 600-800 degrees C to obtain dried silicon-carbon negative material.

PN CN108408747-A
TI Treating magnesium slag obtained by calcining lithium involves preparing magnesium slag as slurry, and introducing process gas containing carbon dioxide for carbonization reaction, and performing solid-liquid separation.
AU LIU D
   ZHANG S
   LI L
AE ZIBO MEISHENG CHEM CO LTD (ZIBO-Non-standard)
GA 201866834G
AB    NOVELTY - Treating magnesium slag obtained by calcining lithium involves preparing magnesium slag as a slurry, and introducing a process gas containing carbon dioxide for carbonization reaction, and performing solid-liquid separation after completion of the reaction to obtain filter residue and heavy magnesium water, pyrolyzing the heavy magnesium water to obtain a magnesium carbonate slurry, and filtering to obtain a first filter cake and a filtrate, and calcining first filter cake to obtain a light magnesium oxide. The filtrate is concentrated by evaporation and then added with a strong inorganic acid, and the pH is adjusted to 1-2 to precipitate a boric acid precipitate.
   USE - Method for treating magnesium slag obtained by calcining lithium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat magnesium slag obtained by calcining lithium which has low energy consumption, high boron magnesium separation degree, low requirement to equipment, small investment, and has high profit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of magnesium hydroxide in the slurry is 2-10 wt.%, and the volume content of carbon dioxide in the process gas is 3-30 %. The content of boron in the filtrate is periodically analyzed, and when the content of boron oxide reaches 3.5-35 g. L-1, the total filtrate is taken out, concentrated by evaporation from 5-30% by mass to obtain a concentrate. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the carbonization reaction iis 35-90 degrees C, and the reaction time is 5-10 hours. The calcination temperature is 880-950 degrees C. The first filter cake is further dried to obtain basic magnesium carbonate, and drying temperature is 250-300 degrees C. The drying time is 0.8-1.2 hours. The filtrate portion is used to prepare a slurry with the magnesium slag, and remaining portion is used for further evaporation and concentration. The pH is adjusted by using hydrochloric acid or sulfuric acid.

The first laser apparatus generates carbon dioxide laser beam for cutting first resin layer. A second laser apparatus generates ultraviolet laser beam (R2) for cutting second resin layer.
   USE - Laser processing apparatus for cutting resin substrates.
   ADVANTAGE - The inside resin layer/polyimide resin layer is hard to be processed at the time of the process of the polyethylene terephthalate-resin layer of surface side, thus preserving the quality of the polyimide resin layer during laser cutting process.

PN CN108094406-A
TI Cell freezing, quick recovery and culturing method involves adding culture medium into coated porous plate, freezing, collecting cells, adding pre-stored liquid, refrigerating, adding into porous plate, refrigerating, and freezing.
AU ZHAO X
   WANG R
   XIONG H
   QIN L
   SONG C
AE CHONGQING SIDEMU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 2018444970
AB    NOVELTY - Cell freezing, quick recovery and culturing method comprises placing 0.2-0.5% gelatin coated porous plate in constant temperature culture box for 1-2 hours, and removing excess gelatin; adding culture medium into the coated porous plate, freezing for more than 1 day at - 80 degrees C, and storing; collecting cells, adding pre-stored liquid to obtain cell suspension, refrigerating for 0.5 hour at 4 degrees C; adding cell suspension into the porous plate, refrigerating for 2 hours at - 20 degrees C for 2 hours, freezing at - 80 degrees C for more than 1 day forming double-layer, and storing at - 80 degrees C; taking out, and directly putting in 37 degrees C carbon dioxide constant temperature culture box; and standing for 2 hours, removing liquid, adding fresh culture medium, placing in 37 degrees C carbon dioxide constant temperature culture box, and culturing.
   USE - method for cell freezing, quick recovery and culturing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The cell is plant cell or animal cell. The animal cell is tumor cell line, immortalized cell line, continuous cell line, genetically modified cell line, split block cells, stem cells, induced pluripotent stem cells and primary isolated cells. The culture medium comprises 10 vol.% fetal calf serum. The pre-stored liquid comprises dimethyl sulfoxide, glycerol, polyvinylpyrrolidone, ethylene glycol, methanol, methyl acetamide and sugar.

PT P
PN FR3058144-A1; WO2018078114-A1; CN109923620-A; FR3058144-B1; US2019248973-A1; EP3533071-A1; US10487186-B2; EP3533071-B1; CN109923620-B
TI Processing of polyamide-based material used for forming electrically-insulating component, involves impregnating polyamide-based material comprising silica fibers and/or fillers with hydrophobic additive(s) in supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU PONCELET O
   GETTO D
AE COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
   ATOMIC ENERGY & ALTERNATIVE ENERGY COMMI (COMS-C)
   COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
   COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
GA 201833946J
AB    NOVELTY - Processing of polyamide-based material involves impregnating a polyamide-based material comprising silica fibers and/or fillers with hydrophobic additive(s) in supercritical carbon dioxide.
   USE - Processing of polyamide-based material used for forming electrically-insulating component for electrical apparatus e.g. medium-voltage or low-voltage electrical apparatus e.g. gas-insulated electrical transformer, gas-insulated line, or busbar for transport or distribution of electricity, and electrical connection/disconnection device, preferably circuit breaker (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical processing of polyamide-based material. The processed polyamide-based material provides electrically-insulating component having excellent dielectric strength, water resistance, scratch resistance, aging resistance and electrical insulation property, and low dielectric loss.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) electrically-insulating component, which is formed using the polyamide-based material(s) comprising silica fibers and/or fillers and impregnated with hydrophobic additive; and
   (2) electrical apparatus, which comprises the electrically-insulating component.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Component: The electrical apparatus further comprises a sealed chamber, electrical components, and a gaseous medium ensuring the electrical isolation and electric arc-extinction within the sealed chamber.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The hydrophobic fluorinated compound comprises 6C or more partially fluorinated hydrophobic hydrocarbon chain, preferably 8C or more partially fluorinated hydrophobic hydrocarbon chain, and group(s) capable of interacting by hydrogen bonding or by Van der Waals interaction with the polyamide chains, preferably amide group of formula: -NH-C(O)-O-, -NH-C(O)- or -NH-C(O)-NH-. The gaseous medium preferably comprises a mixture of fluoroketone and a carrier gas, such as air.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The material comprises polyamide 6.6 or polyphthalamide loaded with silica fibers and/or fillers, preferably loaded with micrometric silica fibers. The material comprises 20% or more, preferably 30-50% silica fibers and/or fillers, preferably micrometric silica fibers. The hydrophobic additive is chosen from dielectric silicone oil soluble in supercritical carbon dioxide, and hydrophobic fluorinated compound soluble in supercritical carbon dioxide. The dielectric silicone oil is chosen from linear silicone oil of polydimethylsiloxane, and aromatic silicone oil, preferably polyphenylalkylsiloxane-type oil, more preferably poly (dimethylsiloxane-co-methylphenylsiloxane)oil and poly (dimethylsiloxane-co-diphenylsiloxane) oil. The material comprises 0.2-5 wt.%, preferably 0.8-1.2 wt.% hydrophobic additive. Preferred Process: The processing of polyamide-based material further involves reducing the water absorption of the material. Alternately, processing of polyamide-based material involves placing the polyamide-based material(s) loaded with silica fibers and/or fillers and hydrophobic additive(s) in an autoclave, generating supercritical carbon dioxide in the autoclave, maintaining the material in the autoclave in the presence of the supercritical carbon dioxide and the hydrophobic additive in order to ensure impregnation of the material with the hydrophobic additive, recovering the impregnated material by depressurizing the autoclave, and optionally drying the impregnated material. The impregnation is performed at a temperature of 50-150 degrees C, preferably 90-100 degrees C and a pressure of 80-450 bar, preferably 200-350 bar for 30-180 minutes, preferably 60-135 minutes. The material is impregnated with the hydrophobic additive over a thickness extending from the surface of the material to a depth of 200 mu m or more, preferably 400 mu m or more.

PT P
PN WO2018031749-A1; US2019177254-A1; US10730814-B2
TI Ozonolysis of alkane e.g. methane to produce non-combustion products e.g. methanol, involves combining alkane and ozone in liquid phase medium comprising carbon dioxide and free of super acid under specified conditions to oxidize alkane.
AU SUBRAMANIAM B
   DANBY A M
   LUNDIN M D
AE UNIV KANSAS (UNIV-C)
   UNIV KANSAS (UNIV-C)
GA 201813120H
AB    NOVELTY - Ozonolysis of alkane involves combining alkane and ozone in a liquid phase medium comprising carbon dioxide under specified conditions to oxidize the alkane. The liquid phase medium is free of a super acid.
   USE - Ozonolysis of alkane e.g. methane, ethane, propane, isobutane, dimethyl propane, heptane, isooctane and cyclohexane to produce non-combustion products e.g. methanol, 4-heptanone, 3-heptanone, 2-heptanone, cyclohexanone and cyclohexanol (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is safe and economical, and efficiently produces non-combustion products with high yield and selectivity. The method produces 4-heptanone, 3-heptanone and 2-heptanone with equal selectivities, and cyclohexanone with a selectivity of at least 95% or 90%. The method produces methanol with a yield of at least 100 g/hour.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a continuous flow reactor for ozonolysis of alkane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid phase medium is liquid carbon dioxide. The method is carried out in the presence of a catalyst and under the specified conditions that can generate an active species from the ozone. The catalyst is a supported transition metal catalyst comprising transition metals incorporated into a metal oxide substrate. The metal oxide substrate is an oxide of a metal selected from aluminum, silicon, titanium, and magnesium. The supported transition metal catalyst comprises iron, nickel, and/or palladium, incorporated into a mesoporous silicate. The method does not produce water, and carbon dioxide. Preferred Composition: The method uses 1-45 mol% alkane and ozone, and 55-99 mol% carbon dioxide. Preferred Conditions: The method is carried out at a specified temperature and pressure to liquefy the carbon dioxide. The method is carried out for 30 minutes or less.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkane is 1-10C alkane.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An iron complex of formula (I), is new.
   R1-R12 = H, halo, hydroxy or carboxy;and
   X1,X2 = coordination atom.

   (2) electrode, which comprises the catalyst;
   (3) decomposition of water, which involves decomposing water using the electrode; and

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation: No general preparation is given. Preferred Properties: The oxygen evolution in the electrolysis of water has onset potential of 1.23-1.6 V with respect to RHE (reversible hydrogen electrode). The Tafel gradient in the electrolysis of water is 10-100 mV/dec. Preferred Components: The electrode comprises an electroconductive substrate and a catalyst layer containing the catalyst, which is provided on at least a portion of the surface of the substrate.    Preferred Definitions:
   R6,R7 = Cl;and
   R2,R11 = F.
   EXAMPLE - 5 g (0.04 mol) Pyridine-2-carboxylic acid and 2.163 g (0.02 mol) 1,2-diaminobenzene were dissolved in 30 ml pyridine. 12.411 g (0.04 mol) Triphenylphosphine was added to the solution. The mixture was heated at 100 degrees C for 4 hours and cooled to room temperature overnight. The precipitate was filtered and washed with methanol and recrystallized with chloroform to obtain long white needle-shaped 46.2% 1,2-bis (2-pyridinecarboxamide) benzene.

PN CN112979977-A
TI Bimetal metal-organic frameworks material useful for carbon dioxide capture comprises using copper nitrate hydrate and transition metal nitrate hydrate as two metal center ion sources, and trimesic acid as ligand material.
AU LI L
   XIAO F
   ZHAO H
   ZHAO N
   LI F
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 2021719395
AB    NOVELTY - Bimetal metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) material prepared by using copper nitrate hydrate and transition metal nitrate hydrate as two metal center ion sources, and trimesic acid as a ligand material, is claimed.
   USE - The material is useful for carbon dioxide capture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The materials: can significantly increases its carbon dioxide adsorption capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for using of bimetal MOFs materials for carbon dioxide capture, comprising (I) pressing the bimetal MOFs material through the sieve, (II) degassing the bimetal MOFs material under normal pressure, and (III) adsorbing the bimetal MOFs material in an atmosphere containing carbon dioxide gas under normal pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of the two metal center ion sources and trimesic acid as the ligand material is 1.5-2.0:1. The molar ratio of the copper nitrate hydrate to the transition metal nitrate hydrate is 1-9:1. In the step (i), the nitrate hydrate of the second metal comprises nitrate hydrate of a transition metal. In the steps (i) and (ii), the solvents comprises N,N-dimethyl formamide, anhydrous ethanol and/or deionized water prepared in equal volumes. In the step (i), the molar ratio of copper nitrate hydrate to the second metal nitrate hydrate is 1-9:1. The molar ratio of the sum of the copper nitrate hydrate and the nitrate hydrate of the second metal in the step (i) to the trimesic acid in the step (ii) is 1.5-2:1. Preferred Method: The bimetal MOFs material is prepared by (i) mixing the cupric nitrate hydrate and the nitrate hydrate of the second metal with the solvent to prepare A solution, (ii) mixing trimesic acid and solvent to prepare B solution, (iii) adding the solution A obtained in the step (i) to the solution B obtained in the step (ii), uniformly stirring, and transferring to a glass bottle for carrying out hydrothermal reaction, and (iv) centrifuging the product obtained in the step (iii), washing, drying the required bimetal MOFs material and grinding. In the step (iii), the hydrothermal reaction is specifically carried out at 60-120 degrees C for 18-36 hours. In the step (iv), the washing and drying is carried out to obtain the required adsorption material specifically by using N,N-dimethyl formamide and a mixed solution of absolute ethanol/deionized water for completely washing, vacuuming drying to obtain the required bimetal MOFs material. In the step (II), the bimetal MOFs adsorbent material is pressed into a sieving sheet, specifically taking the bimetal MOFs material of 20-40 meshes. In the step (III), the degassing bimetal MOFs material under normal pressure is specifically carried out at 120-200 degrees C in an argon atmosphere under normal pressure with space velocity of 3000-4000 m/hours for 2-3 hours. In the step (III), adsorbing the bimetal MOFs material in an atmosphere containing carbon dioxide gas under normal pressure is specifically carried out at 25-65 degrees C under the space velocity of 3000-4000 ml/hour.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN CN112657518-A
TI Carbon dioxide reduction photocatalytic material comprises molybdenum disulfide/tin sulfide compound.
AU SUN S
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 202151663F
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide reduction photocatalytic material comprises molybdenum disulfide/tin sulfide compound (I).
   USE - As carbon dioxide reduction photocatalytic material.
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalytic material has reduced interface defects, high interface conductivity, small particle size and high catalytic performance, is prepared by simple process and suitable for easy realization of large-scale production, does not require heavy equipment and harsh conditions, and ensures strong controllability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide reduction photocatalytic material comprises molybdenum disulfide/tin sulfide compound of formula (I): MoS2/SnS2-x. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the carbon dioxide reduction photocatalytic material, involving dissolving thiourea in water, and uniformly stirring to obtain solution, adding molybdenum salt and tin salt to obtained solution, and uniformly stirring to form precursor solution, transferring obtained precursor solution to hydrothermal kettle, performing hydrothermal reaction at 100-300 degrees C, washing and drying to obtain the photocatalytic material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide reduction photocatalytic material is prepared by in-situ growth from precursor solution using one-step hydrothermal method. Preferred Components: The molybdenum salt comprises soluble molybdate, molybdenum chloride and/or molybdenum complex. The tin salt comprises tin sulfate, tin chloride and/or tin complex. The soluble molybdate comprises sodium molybdate and/or potassium molybdate. Preferred Composition: The concentration of thiourea in precursor solution is 0.01-10 mol/l, the concentration of molybdenum is 0.005-0.5 mol/l, and the concentration of tin is 0.005-0.5 mol/l. Preferred Conditions: The method involves dissolving thiourea in water, and uniformly stirring at 0-90 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours. The method involves adding molybdenum salt and tin salt to solution, and uniformly stirring for 0.5-2 hours. The filling degree of hydrothermal kettle is 40-80%. The method involves performing hydrothermal reaction at 100-250 degrees C for 2-24 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - A new type of carbon dioxide circulating heat exchange (CO2-EGS) model of high-temperature corrosion-resistant cementing system comprises 75-88 wt.% low hydration heat cement or low calcium-silica ratio cement, 11-20 wt.% high temperature strength enhancer and 1-5 wt.% carbon dioxide corrosion-resistant material.

   ADVANTAGE - The cementing system has excellent corrosion resistance, high temperature resistance and thermal insulation properties. The waste concrete has a silica content of greater than or equal to 70%, and a powder fineness of greater than or equal to 300 mesh.

PT P
PN CN111943807-A
TI Designing four carbon supplement positions for            heat integrated methanol synthesis comprises combining            combination of one-stage steam methane reforming,            adding carbon dioxide, conducting heat integration and            obtaining refined methanol.
AU SHEN W
   WANG H
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2020B7204A
AB    NOVELTY - Designing four carbon supplement positions for                heat integrated methanol synthesis comprises                combining combination of one-stage steam methane                reforming (SMR) and carbon dioxide methane                reforming (DMR), adding carbon dioxide before and                after the SMR reformer, in front of the DMR                reformer and the circulating gas, conducting heat                integration for the entire process, where the                process design is carried out by one stage of                methane steam conversion, carbon dioxide methane                reforming, compression, methanol synthesis,                rectification, and obtaining refined methanol with                a purity of 1 wt.%.
   USE - The method is useful for designing four carbon                supplement positions for heat integrated methanol                synthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problems of high energy                consumption and large carbon dioxide                emission.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method specifically                  comprises (1) dividing methane steam reforming                  (SMR) raw material natural gas into two paths                  after pretreatment, heating 50 vol.% natural gas,                  steam and added carbon dioxide stream, mixing by                  heater HEATER2, entering first stage steam                  reformer, providing heat required by HEATER2 by                  the rear coolers C1 and C8, subjecting materials                  to methane steam reforming, performing water gas                  shift reactions in the SMR high-temperature                  reformer, adding stream of carbon dioxide after                  the SMR reformer to adjust the hydrogen to carbon                  ratio, (2) converting carbon dioxide methane                  reforming (DMR) raw material gas in mixed gas                  composed of hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon                  dioxide and methane, cooling by the cooler,                  entering gas-liquid separator FLASH1 to separate                  excess water, passing gas phase through the                  heater H1, heating, mixing with other natural                  gas, adding carbon dioxide stream, heating by                  heater HEATER3 to enter the dry methane reformer                  (DMR) for reaction, generating methanol synthesis                  gas, heating heater provided by cooler, where                  heaters provides required heat, passing synthesis                  gas through the DMR reformer, cooling by                  condenser, entering in gas-liquid separator                  FLASH2 to separate excess water, and entering the                  compression section, (3) compressing synthesis                  gas obtained in the compression section to the                  pressure required by the methanol synthesis                  section through a compressor, determining                  pressure level according to the performance of                  the synthesis process and where the process                  pressure is 7-10 MPa, (4) compressing synthesis                  gas in methanol synthesis section and added                  carbon dioxide stream, mixing with the                  circulating gas to enter the methanol reactor to                  synthesize methanol through heaters, heating                  heaters, where the reacted substance contains                  methanol, water, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide,                  hydrogen, nitrogen, and methane, passing reacted                  gas through the cooler, reducing gas temperature,                  entering the methanol separator, where the crude                  methanol and unconverted reaction gas are                  separated into gas and liquid, and the liquid                  phase crude methanol component is sent to the                  rectification unit for processing, providing gas                  after gas-liquid separation as recycle gas,                  excepting for part of it as purge gas to control                  the content of inert components in the reaction                  gas, recovering the effective components therein,                  boosting gas to the pressure of the methanol                  synthesis section, mixing with fresh methanol                  synthesis gas and sending to the methanol                  synthesis reactor to continue reaction                  conversion, and (5) adopting two-column                  rectification by rectification unit in the                  rectification section, subjecting crude methanol                  from the synthesis unit for decompression                  operation, entering in the crude distillation                  tower, separating light components, hydrogen,                  carbon monoxide, and dissolved gases e.g. carbon                  dioxide, where the crude methanol in the crude                  tower contains methanol, water and a small amount                  of impurities, entering substance from bottom of                  crude distillation tower in main distillation                  tower, obtaining refined methanol product by top                  of the main distillation tower, where bottom of                  the tower contains water and impurities, and the                  bottom water contains a small amount of methanol,                  the heat of the reboiler at the bottom of the                  main distillation tower can exchange heat with                  the coolers C4, C9, and C13. The temperature                  range of SMR reformer is 600-900&#176; C, and                  the temperature of DMR reformer is                  600-800&#176; C. The pressure of the methanol                  synthesizer is 7-10 MPa, and the temperature of                  the thermal fluid in the reactor is                  220-240&#176; C. The temperature of the                  cooler C6 is 50&#176; C, and the temperature                  of the cooler is 35&#176; C. The crude                  methanol from the synthesis unit enters the                  pressure reduction operation to 5 bar, and then                  enters the crude distillation tower T1, the                  operating pressure of the crude distillation                  column is 2-3 bar, the number of theoretical                  plates is 6-8, the feeding position is the second                  to sixth, and the reflux ratio is 0.1-0.5, the                  operating pressure of the refined methanol tower                  T2 is 1-1.5 bar, the number of theoretical plates                  is 13-18, the feed position is 8-15 and the                  reflux ratio is 1.2-1.7. The ratio of the feed                  flow rate of the raw material water vapor to the                  natural gas is maintained at 3. The feed flow                  ratio of the additional carbon dioxide streams is                  1. Preferred Components: The purity of methanol                  after rectification is 1 wt.%. The                  hydrogen-carbon ratio in the synthesis gas used                  to synthesize methanol is 1.8-2.8.

AU CHOI J
   LEE K
   CHUNG Y H
   LEE K Y
   JEONG Y
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 202045648W
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of heterogeneous zeolite separator                involves forming a seed layer by depositing                chabazite (CHA)-type zeolite particles on a                support, and hydrothermally treating obtained                support in a deca-dodecasil 3-rhombohedral                (DDR)-type zeolite precursor solution.
   USE - Manufacture of heterogeneous zeolite separator                used for separating carbon dioxide from gas mixture                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical manufacture of                heterogeneous zeolite separator having excellent                separation performance, crack resistance, long-term                stability, and reliability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                separation of carbon dioxide from gas mixture,                which involves processing (p1) a gas mixture                comprising methane, nitrogen, oxygen, ethane,                ethylene, propene, propane and carbon dioxide using                the heterogeneous zeolite separator, and separating                carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The support is one or                  more chosen from ceramic, alpha -alumina, gamma                  -alumina, silica, glass, mullite, zirconia,                  titania, yttria, ceria, vanadium pentoxide,                  silicon, stainless steel, carbon, calcium oxide,                  phosphorus oxide, polypropylene, polyethylene,                  polytetrafluoroethylene, polysulfone, and                  polyimide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The manufacture of                  separator optionally involves depositing DDR-type                  zeolite particles on the support and                  hydrothermally treating obtained support in                  CHA-type zeolite precursor solution. Preferred                  Composition: The DDR-type zeolite precursor                  solution comprises silicon dioxide,                  methyltropylium salt, sodium hydroxide, water,                  and aluminum hydroxide in a molar ratio of                  100:1-1000:0-1000:10-100000:0-20. The CHA-type                  zeolite precursor solution comprises an organic                  structure derivative, silicon hydroxide, water,                  sodium hydroxide and aluminum trihydroxide in a                  molar ratio of 1-100:100:1000-20000:1-100:0-20.                  The organic structure derivative is chosen from                  N,N,N-trimethyl-1-adamantylammonium hydroxide,                  N,N,N-trimethyl-1-adamantylammonium bromide,                  N,N,N-trimethyl-1-adamantylammonium fluoride,                  N,N,N-trimethyl-1-adamantylammonium chloride,                  N,N,N-trimethyl-1-adamantylammonium iodide,                  tetraethylammonium hydroxide, tetraethylammonium                  bromide, tetraethylammonium fluoride,                  tetraethylammonium chloride, tetraethylammonium                  iodide, dipropylamine, and cyclohexylamine.                  Preferred Conditions: The hydrothermal reaction                  is performed at 100-200 degrees C for 12-300                  hours. Process (p1) is performed at 25-200                  degrees C. Preferred Process: The manufacture of                  separator further involves drying after                  hydrothermal reaction.

During fertilizing through drip irrigation pipeline outlets, carbon dioxide is fed, which is filled with volume of furrows under the screen.
