PT P
PN CN112028100-A
TI Industrial preparing high dispersible nano calcium carbonate for ink comprises taking calcium hydroxide to produce raw materials, processing, introducing carbon dioxide into the raw materials, and sequentially adding coupling agents.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHANG S
   CHEN N
   SHAO X
   MA J
AE WUHU ZHUOYUE NANO NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (WUHU-Non-standard)
GA 2020C4386H
AB    NOVELTY - Industrial preparing high dispersible nano calcium carbonate comprises taking calcium hydroxide to produce raw materials, dividing into three times, three processing, introducing carbon dioxide into the raw materials, sequentially adding coupling agents to the raw materials, reacting the raw materials and allowing to stand to form nano-calcium carbonate suspension, dehydrating and drying to extracting final product.
   USE - The nano calcium carbonate is useful for ink (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nano calcium carbonate: is convenient to improve the quality of the ink.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Industrial preparing high dispersible nano calcium carbonate comprises taking calcium hydroxide to produce raw materials, dividing into three times, three processing, introducing carbon dioxide into the raw materials, sequentially adding coupling agents to the raw materials, reacting the raw materials and allowing to stand to form nano-calcium carbonate suspension, dehydrating and drying to extracting final product, preferably (i) adding certain amount of water into the inside of calcium hydroxide, thus calcium hydroxide is stratified in water, where the upper layer is clarified lime water, and the lower layer is lime milk, (ii) continuously introducing calcium hydroxide into the mixed solution, the weight of the lower layer of lime milk can be 6%-10% compared with the weight of the original mixed solution under the effect of allowing to stand, and pumping the clarified lime water from the upper layer by pumping, (iii) introducing carbon dioxide for the first time, and introducing carbon dioxide into 6-10 lime milk balance amount in step (ii) to form semi-finished product of suspension, the amount of carbon dioxide introduced is 20%-25% of the volume of the lime milk stock solution, and the carbon dioxide is the emergency flow inlet, (iv) allowing to stand the formed semi-finished suspension product still, and testing, (v) introducing carbon dioxide for the second time, introducing carbon dioxide into the semi-finished product of the suspension after allowing to stand for a period of time, introducing simultaneously into the semi-finished suspension liquid at a normal speed, and the volume of the introduced carbon dioxide is 5-10% of the volume of the semi-finished suspension liquid, (vi) preparing the coupling agent into a coupling agent reagent with a volume of 2%-2.5% of the semi-finished product, introducing carbon dioxide into the semi-finished product of the suspension, adding the prepared coupling agent reagent dropwise into the semi-finished product of the suspension, and allowing to stand for certain period of time, (vii) adding carbon dioxide for the third time, slowly adding carbon dioxide into the suspension semi-finished product, and the amount of carbon dioxide is 10-20% of the volume of the suspension semi-finished product, and while slowly introducing carbon dioxide, introducing certain amount of clarified lime water extracted in step (ii)into the semi-finished suspension liquid, allowing to stand to form suspension slurry, and (viii) formulating the coating agent into a coating agent reagent with a volume of 20-25% of the original slurry of the suspension in step (vii), adding the formulated coating reagent dropwise into the suspension slurry formed in step (vii), and mixing and stirring for 2-4 hours, squeezing and filtering the slurry to form a slurry cake with water content of less than 50%, baking the slurry cake at high temperature to remove the water inside the slurry cake to obtain highly dispersible nano-calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (ii), the allowing to stand time is 2-3 hours, preferably until the lime milk and the clarified lime water are completely separated. The rapid inflow of carbon dioxide in step (iii) is performed by introducing carbon dioxide for the first time at a rate 20-50% faster than the normal carbon dioxide inflow rate, thus the milk of lime in step three quickly reacts with carbon dioxide. In step (iv), the allowing to stand time is 1-2 hours, and the carbon dioxide in the semi-finished product of the suspension in the step (iii) is completely reacted by allowing to stand for 1-2 hours. In step (iv), the semi-finished product of the suspension is detected, the amount of carbonization in the semi-finished product of the suspension is measured, the amount of carbonization in the semi-finished product of the suspension is measured, and the amount of clarified lime water to be passed in step seven later is determined. In step (vi), the coupling agent solution is prepared mixing organic chromium complex, coconut oil, lauric silicic acid, nonanoic acid and oleic acid in ratio of 7:1:1:1:0.5:0.5, the pH value of the coupling agent reagent is adjusted to be the same as the pH value of the suspension semi-finished product in step (v). The allowing to stand time in step (vi) is 2-2.5 hours, thus the coupling agent reagent and the semi-finished suspension liquid are completely mixed. The duration of carbon dioxide access in step (vii)is twice the standard access duration. The PH value of the coating agent is adjusted to match the original liquid phase of the suspension in step (vii), and the coating agent is heated to 80-90 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN114602532-A
TI Method for preparing desulfurization and            denitration active coke catalyst involves utilizing raw            material powder preparation, binder preparation,            kneading molding, pre-curing, carbonization,            activation, the raw material powder includes coal            powder, asphalt powder and copper oxide powder.
AU MA F
   WANG L
   SHEN Y
   YIN D
   LIU J
   LIU Y
AE SHANDONG WANDA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202289221N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing desulfurization and                denitration active coke catalyst involves utilizing                raw material powder preparation, binder                preparation, kneading molding, pre-curing,                carbonization, activation. The raw material powder                includes 40-60 pts. wt. coal powder, 5-12 pts. wt.                asphalt powder, 2-9 pts. wt. copper oxide powder,                3-12 pts. wt. manganese trioxide powder, and 1-4                pts. wt. cerium oxide powder, 1-5 pts. wt.                molybdenum carbide powder, 1-6 pts. wt. molybdenum                nitride powder, 1-4 pts. wt. tungsten phosphide                powder and 1-3 pts. wt. cobalt dioxide powder. The                asphalt powder has a particle size 600-1000 mesh.                The softening point of raw pitch is 150-240                degreesC. The particle sizes of the copper oxide                powder, manganese trioxide powder, cerium oxide                powder, molybdenum carbide powder, molybdenum                nitride powder, tungsten phosphide powder and                cobalt dioxide powder are all 900-1100 mesh.
   USE - Method for preparing desulfurization and                denitration active coke catalyst for use in flue                gas desulfurization and denitration                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The active coke catalyst has the advantages of                large specific surface area, high desulfurization                rate and denitration rate, excellent compressive                strength and wear-resistant strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing desulfurization and                denitration active coke catalyst involves utilizing                raw material powder preparation, binder                preparation, kneading molding, pre-curing,                carbonization, activation. The raw material powder                includes 40-60 pts. wt. coal powder, 5-12 pts. wt.                asphalt powder, 2-9 pts. wt. copper oxide powder,                3-12 pts. wt. manganese trioxide powder, 1-4 pts.                wt. cerium oxide powder, 1-5 pts. wt. molybdenum                carbide powder, 1-6 pts. wt. molybdenum nitride                powder, 1-4 pts. wt. tungsten phosphide powder and                1-3 pts. wt. cobalt dioxide powder. The coal powder                has a particle size of 800-1100 mesh, the asphalt                powder has a particle size 600-1000 mesh. The                softening point of raw pitch is 150-240 degreesC.                The particle sizes of the copper oxide powder,                manganese trioxide powder, cerium oxide powder,                molybdenum carbide powder, molybdenum nitride                powder, tungsten phosphide powder and cobalt                dioxide powder are all 900-1100 mesh. The coal                powder raw material is long flame coal, lignite or                coking coal. The raw material pitch is coal pitch                and petroleum pitch. The method for preparing the                raw material powder involves adding the raw                material powder into a powder mixer and mixing                uniformly to obtain the raw material powder                according to the mass ratio of each raw material                powder in the above-mentioned raw material powder.                The preparation method of the binder involves                adding water-soluble phenolic resin, borax, alkali,                aqueous silicate solution, silicon phosphate,                silane coupling agent, surfactant, and methyl                silane triol into a mixing kettle, after stirring                for 3-5 hours at a rate of 3000-4500 revolutions                per minute (r/min), the material is discharged to                obtain a binder. The mass ratio of the                water-soluble phenolic resin, borax, alkali,                aqueous silicate solution, silicon phosphate,                silane coupling agent, surfactant, and                methylsilanetriol is 35-55:2-5:1-3:10                -15:0.5-1:0.3-1:0.2-0.6:1-1.8. The water-soluble                phenolic resin has a viscosity of 500-1000                millipascal-second and a solid content of 55-60%.                The alkali is potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide                or lithium hydroxide. The concentration of the                silicate is 20-30 wt.%, the modulus of the silicate                is 1.5-3. The silicate is lithium silicate,                potassium silicate or sodium silicate. The silane                coupling agent is KH550 (RTM:                gamma-aminopropyltriethoxysilane), KH560 (RTM:                (3-glycidyloxypropyl)trimethox) or KH570 (RTM:                methacryl-functional silane). The surfactant is                8-10C alkyl glycoside or dodecyl hydroxypropyl                sulfobetaine. The method of kneading and forming is                to put the raw material powder into a kneader,                start the kneader, add a binder, knead it to a                batter shape, and transfer it to a wet granulator                to form into granules; pre-curing, its method is                that particle is placed under 70-95 degreesC of                temperature pre-curing 5-8 hours, obtains particle                diameter 30-100 purpose pre-curing particles.                Carbonization method is that pre-cured particles                are placed under the protection of high-purity                nitrogen, heated at 4-8 degreesC/minutes and                constant temperature to 600-850 degreesC, constant                temperature carbonization 40-80 minutes to obtain                carbonized particles. Activation method is that                under the protection of high-purity nitrogen, the                carbonized particles are activated by the combined                activation method of water vapor and carbon                dioxide, and the concrete operation is to heat up                at a rate of 5-9 degreesC/minutes and be constant                temperature to 800-950 degreesC, firstly. Activated                with water vapor, then activated by introducing                carbon dioxide, and then lowered to room                temperature to obtain an active coke catalyst for                desulfurization and denitration, the water vapor                activation, the water vapor flow rate is 4-9                mL/minutes, and the activation time is 30-45                minutes, the carbon dioxide is activated, the                carbon dioxide flow is 60-110 mL/minutes, and the                activation time is 25-55 minutes. The mass ratio of                the raw material powder to the binder is                19:3-10.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                application method of the active coke catalyst in                flue gas desulfurization and denitration, which                involves working the active coke catalyst in                denitration-desulfurization-regeneration continuous                cycle mode, where the denitration temperature is                130 degreesC and the desulfurization temperature is                85degreesC.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC J04-E09; J04-E04
IP B01D-053/48; B01D-053/50; B01D-053/56; B01D-053/60; B01D-053/86; B01J-027/24; B01J-035/10

PT P
PN KR2052952-B1
TI Emergency escape respiratory respirator comprises e.g. body having accommodating space formed inside and upper opening formed at upper end in communication with accommodating space, filter module, mouthpiece, and protective cover.
AU KIM S
AE GEM VAX & KAEL CO LTD (GEMV-Non-standard)
GA 2020055655
AB    NOVELTY - Emergency escape respiratory respirator comprises a body having an accommodating space formed inside and an upper opening formed at an upper end in communication with the accommodating space; a filter module (20) made of multiple filters disposed inside the body; a mouthpiece (30) disposed on one side of the body; a protective cover detachably coupled to the body to protect the mouthpiece from foreign matter when not in use; a cover (50) which is detachably coupled to the upper opening of the body to block outside air from flowing through the upper opening when not in use; and a nasal membrane for blocking breathing by pressing the user's nose. The body comprises an opening and closing member which is opened or closed in accordance with the inhalation and exhalation of the user breathing, a retention tube (14) disposed in the accommodating space, and a dust collecting body (15) disposed continuously outside one end of the retention tube.
   USE - Used as emergency escape respiratory respirator.
   ADVANTAGE - The emergency escape respiratory respirator is worn to prevent user from smothering smoke or dust in event of disaster i.e. fire or earthquake, improves user convenience by allowing user to breathe with mouthpiece in the mouth, is provided with opening and closing member which opens and closes according to inhalation and exhalation to improve the dust collection efficiency of the filter, prevents accidents caused by smoke or dust through the filter module composed of multiple filters disposed inside to collect harmful pollutants, can maintain dust collection efficiency of filter for long time by separately collecting the carbon dioxide contained in the exhalation, secures user's view to induce smooth escape, and can suppress initial fire by allowing user to throw fire extinguishing liquid or powder at the fire location.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Emergency escape respiratory respirator comprises a body having an accommodating space formed inside and an upper opening formed at an upper end in communication with the accommodating space; a filter module (20) made of multiple filters disposed inside the body; a mouthpiece (30) disposed on one side of the body; a protective cover detachably coupled to the body to protect the mouthpiece from foreign matter when not in use; a cover (50) which is detachably coupled to the upper opening of the body to block outside air from flowing through the upper opening when not in use; and a nasal membrane for blocking breathing by pressing the user's nose. The body comprises an opening and closing member which is opened or closed in accordance with the inhalation and exhalation of the user breathing, a retention tube (14) disposed in the accommodating space, and a dust collecting body (15) disposed continuously outside one end of the retention tube. The opening and closing member comprises an opening and closing body formed inside the opening and closing space; a top hole formed on the opening body to communicate the opening and closing space with the accommodating space side; a bottom hole formed in the opening and closing body and communicating with the opening and closing space and the mouthpiece side; a relay hole formed in the opening and closing body and discharging air transferred from the mouthpiece to the outside of the opening and closing body; and a thin film disposed in the opening and closing space to close the top hole when the user exhales and return to the original position when inhaling. The retention tube comprises a residence space in which the air introduced through the upper opening stays is formed inside, a communication hole communicating the staying space and the accommodating space is formed. The residence space includes a filter member for collecting toxic substances and dust contained in the introduced air. The dust collecting body comprises a second arrangement space formed inside the hollow housing shape, an upper inlet hole which communicates with the second arrangement space and the retention space and is formed at a multiple spaced apart positions, and a lower discharge hole communicating the second arrangement space and the mouthpiece side. The upper opening comprises an upper vent plate having multiple holes formed inside for filtering foreign substances having relatively large diameter from the air introduced from the outside, a lower socket which is disposed such that an outer circumference of the upper vent plate abuts on an inner circumferential surface and supports the upper vent plate, and a first arrangement space formed between the upper vent plate and the lower socket. The filter module comprises a first filter disposed in the first batch space, a second filter disposed in the second batch space, a third filter disposed surrounding the outer circumferential surface of the retention tube, a fourth filter continuously disposed on vertical line of the third filter, and a carbon dioxide collector which collects carbon dioxide contained in the air spouted by the user discharged from the relay hole of the opening and closing body but captures carbon dioxide through carbon scrubbing using an amine, disposed along the inner circumferential surface of the accommodating space of the body. The protective cover comprises an arrangement part on which the mouthpiece is disposed, and a filling part formed to surround the arrangement part and filled with the same extinguishing composition made of extinguishing liquid or powder.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the emergency escape respiratory respirator.
   Retention tube (14)
   Dust collecting body (15)
   Filter module (20)
   Mouthpiece (30)
   Cover (50)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The cover comprises lighting means for irradiating light to the outside by an applied power source, and battery for supplying power to the lighting means.
DC P35 (Life-saving, fire-fighting (A62).); Q71 (Lighting (F21))
MC P35-A03E1; P35-A03E5; P35-C01A8; P35-C03; Q71-T99
IP A62B-018/08; A62B-018/10; A62B-023/02; A62B-009/06; A62D-001/00; F21V-033/00

PN US2018305278-A1; WO2018198015-A1; CA2965062-A1; US10343957-B2; AU2018260408-A1; CN110520400-A; KR2019137919-A; IN201937043016-A; EP3615498-A1; SG11201909881-A1; SG10201912707-A1; BR112019022309-A2; JP2020517680-W; AR113868-A1; EP3615498-B1; JP7008726-B2; ES2912331-T3
TI Chemical complex for oxidative dehydrogenation of            lower alkanes, comprises oxidative dehydrogenation            reactor, quench tower, amine wash, dryer, distillation            tower and oxygen separation module in cooperative            arrangement.
AU SERHAL K
   SIMANZHENKOV V
   GOODARZNIA S
   GENT D
   WILLIAMSON M
AE NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM CORP (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
GA 201883214K
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical complex comprises in cooperative                arrangement: at least one oxidative dehydrogenation                reactor, comprising an oxidative dehydrogenation                catalyst and designed to accept, optionally in the                presence of an inert diluent, an oxygen containing                gas and a lower alkane containing gas, and to                produce a product stream comprising a corresponding                alkene and possibly at least one of e.g. unreacted                lower alkane; quench tower (2) for quenching the                product stream and for removing water and soluble                oxygenates from the product stream; amine wash for                removing carbon dioxide from the product stream;                dryer for removal of water from the product stream;                distillation tower (5) for removing C2/C2+                hydrocarbons from the product stream to produce an                overhead stream enriched with C1 hydrocarbons and                any other compounds lighter than C2/C2+                hydrocarbons; optionally, a means for introducing a                combustible fuel into the overhead stream; and                oxygen separation module.
   USE - The chemical complex is useful for oxidative                dehydrogenation of lower alkanes (claimed) and for                the production of olefins from paraffins.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxidative dehydrogenation chemical complex                reduce costs by including integration of an oxygen                separation module that also addresses safety                concerns and reduces emission of greenhouse gases.                The oxidative dehydrogenation reaction complex with                oxygen separation integration is economically                feasible and amenable to large scale commercial                production of olefins from lower alkanes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Chemical complex comprises in cooperative                arrangement, at least one oxidative dehydrogenation                reactor, comprising an oxidative dehydrogenation                catalyst and designed to accept, optionally in the                presence of an inert diluent, an oxygen containing                gas and a lower alkane containing gas, and to                produce a product stream comprising a corresponding                alkene and possibly at least one of unreacted lower                alkane, oxygen, inert diluent, carbon oxides,                including carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide,                oxygenates, including at least one of acetic acid,                acrylic acid and maleic acid and water; quench                tower (2) for quenching the product stream and for                removing water and soluble oxygenates from the                product stream; amine wash for removing carbon                dioxide from the product stream; dryer for removal                of water from the product stream; distillation                tower for removing C2/C2+ hydrocarbons from the                product stream to produce an overhead stream                enriched with C1 hydrocarbons and any other                compounds lighter than C2/C2+ hydrocarbons;                optionally, a means for introducing a combustible                fuel into the overhead stream; and oxygen                separation module comprising. an oxygen transport                membrane housed inside a sealed vessel and having a                retentate side and a permeate side, first inlet for                introducing the overhead stream, combustible fuel,                or both into the retentate side, second inlet for                introducing the overhead stream, combustible fuel,                or both into the permeate side, air inlet for                introducing air into the retentate side, exhaust                for discharge of oxygen depleted air and combustion                products from the retentate side and outlet for                removing oxygen enriched gas and combustion                products from the permeate side. The components are                connected in series in the sequence described, the                overhead stream from optionally directed into the                retentate side, the permeate side, or both the                retentate side and the permeate side. The oxygen                enriched gas and combustion products from the                permeate side optionally directed back to as or                part of the oxygen containing gas introduced into                the at least one oxidative dehydrogenation reactor,                optionally, the chemical complex further comprises                a flooded gas mixer for premixing the oxygen                containing gas and the lower alkane containing gas                prior to introduction into the at least one                oxidative dehydrogenation reactor. An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is also included for oxidative                dehydrogenation of a lower alkane into a                corresponding alkene, comprising (xiii) contacting,                in at least one oxidative dehydrogenation reactor,                optionally in the presence of an inert diluent, an                oxygen containing gas and a lower alkane containing                gas, with an oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst to                produce a product stream comprising the                corresponding alkene, (xiv) passing the product                stream from dehydrogenation reactor (1)through a                quench tower to remove oxygenates and water from                the product stream, (xv) passing the product stream                from quench tower through an amine wash to remove                carbon dioxide from the product stream, (xvi)                passing the product stream from amine wash through                a dryer to remove water, (xvii) passing the product                stream from dryer through a distillation tower to                separate an overhead stream comprising C1                hydrocarbons and any other compounds lighter than                C2/C2+ hydrocarbons, (xviii) passing the overhead                stream from distillation tower and optionally a                combustible fuel into one or both of a retentate                side and a permeate side of an oxygen separation                module comprising an oxygen transport membrane,                (xix) introducing atmospheric air into the                retentate side allowing for generation of heat by                combustion of the overhead stream and the optional                fuel within the retentate side to increase the                temperature of the oxygen transport membrane to at                least 850 degrees C, permitting unreacted oxygen                from the atmospheric air to cross the oxygen                transport membrane into the permeate side producing                an oxygen enriched gas on the permeate side and                leaving an oxygen depleted air containing                combustion products on the retentate side, (xx)                expelling the oxygen depleted air from the                retentate side through an exhaust on the oxygen                separation module and (xxi) removing the oxygen                enriched gas through an oxygen rich outlet on the                oxygen separation module optionally, the oxygen                enriched gas is directed back to oxidative                dehydrogenation reactor as, or a portion of, the                oxygen containing gas introduced into the oxidative                dehydrogenation reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates schematic view of                chemical complex.
   Oxidative dehydrogenation reactor (1)
   Quench tower (2)
   Amine wash tower (3)
   Drier (4)
   Distillation tower (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidative                  dehydrogenation catalyst comprises a mixed metal                  oxide consisting of: viii) catalysts of formula                  e.g. (MoaVbTecNbdPdeOf) or (NigAhB1iDjOf)a-f = relative atomic amounts of the                  elements Mo, V, Te, Nb, Pd and O, respectively                  and when a is 1, b is 0.01-1, c is 0.01-1, d is                  0.01-1, e is 0-0.1 and f is a number to satisfy                  the valence state of the catalyst;g = 0.1-0.9, preferably 0.6-0.8;h = 0.04-0.9;i = 0-0.5;j = 0-0.5;A = Ti, Ta, V, Nb, Hf, W, Y, Zn, Zr, Si                  and/or Al;B1 = La, Ce, Pr, Nd, Sm, Sb, Sn, Bi, Pb, Tl,                  In, Te, Cr, Mn, Mo, Fe, Co, Cu, Ru, Rh, Pd, Pt,                  Ag, Cd, Os, Ir, Au and/or Hg; andD = Ca, K, Mg, Li, Na, Sr, Ba, Cs and/or                  RbThe chemical complex further comprises at                  least one heat exchanger immediately upstream of                  the quench tower to reduce temperature of the                  product stream and caustic wash tower immediately                  downstream of the amine wash. The C2/C2+                  hydrocarbons exiting the distillation tower are                  directed to a splitter for separation of                  unreacted lower alkane and corresponding alkene                  into an unreacted lower alkane stream and a                  corresponding alkene stream; optionally, the                  unreacted lower alkane stream is directed back to                  the at least one oxidative dehydrogenation                  reactor as part of the lower alkane containing                  gas. The distillation tower further provides for                  separation of the C2/C2+ hydrocarbons portion of                  the product stream into an unreacted lower alkane                  stream and a corresponding alkene stream;                  optionally, the unreacted lower alkane stream is                  directed back to the at least one oxidative                  dehydrogenation reactor as part of the lower                  alkane containing gas. The oxygen separation                  module is tubular and the oxygen transport                  membrane comprises an inner tube that is within                  an outer shell and where the retentate side                  comprises the annular space between the inner                  tube and outer shell and the permeate side is the                  space within the inner tube. The oxygen                  separation module further comprises an additional                  inlet into the retentate side, the permeate side,                  or both, for introduction of combustible fuel                  into the oxygen separation module. The oxygen                  enriched gas comprises at least 20% oxygen,                  preferably at least 80% oxygen. The lower alkane                  is ethane. The ratio of lower alkane containing                  gas to oxygen containing gas is above the upper                  explosive limit. Preferred Process: The lower                  alkane containing gas and the oxygen containing                  gas are mixed in a flooded gas mixer prior to                  entry into the at least one oxidative                  dehydrogenation reactor. The product stream from                  the at least one oxidative dehydrogenation                  reactor is passed through at least one heat                  exchanger immediately upstream of the quench                  tower to reduce temperature of the product                  stream. The process further comprises caustic                  wash tower immediately downstream of the amine                  wash.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The oxidative                  dehydrogenation reactor comprises at least one                  fixed bed type reactor. The oxidative                  dehydrogenation reactor comprises at least one                  fluidized bed type reactor. The oxidative                  dehydrogenation reactor comprises at least one                  swing bed type reactor arrangement. The oxidative                  dehydrogenation reactor comprises more than one                  oxidative dehydrogenation reactor, each                  comprising the same or different oxidative                  dehydrogenation catalyst, connected in series,                  and where the product stream from each oxidative                  dehydrogenation reactor except the last oxidative                  dehydrogenation reactor in the series is fed into                  a downstream oxidative dehydrogenation reactor;                  where at least one of the oxidative                  dehydrogenation reactors comprises a fixed bed                  type reactor, a fluidized bed type reactor or a                  swing bed type reactor arrangement. The oxidative                  dehydrogenation reactor comprises more than one                  oxidative dehydrogenation reactor connected in                  parallel and each comprising the same or                  different oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst                  where at least one of the oxidative                  dehydrogenation reactors comprises a fixed bed                  type reactor, a fluidized bed type reactor or a                  swing bed type reactor arrangement.

PN CN115254879-A
TI Slag soil improving device with carbon fixing            function has carbon dioxide concentration sensor and            pressure sensor that are used for real-time detection            of changes in pressure concentration of carbon            dioxide.
AU HOU J
   YANG J
   ZHU Y
   ZHANG G
   LIN J
AE UNIV HUAQIAO (UYHQ-C)
GA 2022D96158
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an air inlet component and a                water inlet component that are respectively                connected to an air inlet and a water inlet through                pipelines for transporting carbon dioxide and                water. An upper cover of a casing (1) is installed                with a variable frequency motor, and the output end                penetrates the upper cover of the casing and is                located in the casing. The output end is connected                with a stirrer. A detection device is installed at                a detection port. The detection device includes a                carbon dioxide concentration sensor and a pressure                sensor. The carbon dioxide concentration sensor and                the pressure sensor are used for real-time                detection of changes in the pressure concentration                of carbon dioxide.
   USE - Slag soil improving device with carbon fixing                function used in filler for filling of parks or                roads (claimed)
   ADVANTAGE - The device adopts different stirring speed to                reach the best absorption of carbon dioxide for                different stirring stage, so as to improve the                mechanical property of the improved soil. The                device ensures that the carbon dioxide and the raw                material and the hydration product fully occur the                carbonization reaction, before introducing carbon                dioxide, pre-stirring raw material, thus ensuring                that the soil is uniformly carbonized in the                reaction with carbon dioxide. The carbon fixation                can improve the strength of the soil, in the                laboratory test, comparing with improved soil                without solid carbon after solid carbon, the                strength is obviously improved, the solid carbon                also can neutralize and neutralize the solid                dioxide, and the soil can be applied to the actual                working condition, by recycling the slag soil and                so on, reaching the purpose of resource utilization                in the improvement of solid carbon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - AN INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for improving slag soil with carbon fixation                function.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                slag soil improving device with carbon fixing                function.

PN CN114315445-A
TI Device used in degradation plastic for realizing            composting condition control and monitoring carbon            dioxide release, has water split head of gas            pretreatment module connected to aeration water storage            layer of culture tank and gas outlet of water            separation head connected to carbon dioxide            sensor.
AU ZHONG L
   PAN J
   YU K
   QING M
AE GRG METROLOGY & TEST CHONGQING CO LTD (GRGM-Non-standard)
   GUANGZHOU CRG METROLOGY & TEST CO LTD (GRGB-C)
GA 2022558554
AB    NOVELTY - Device has an oxygen supply module, a                composting module, a gas pretreatment module and a                gas testing module. The oxygen supply module                includes an intake carbon dioxide sensor. The                composting module includes a culture tank and a                balance water supply tank, and the culture tank is                composed of an aerated water storage layer, a                cobblestone layer, a gauze layer, and a compost                layer from bottom to top. The gas pretreatment                module includes a condensing water tank, a water                dividing head, and a cooling water circulator. The                gas test module includes a carbon dioxide sensor,                an oxygen sensor, and a mass flow meter. The oxygen                supply module is connected with aeration water                storage layer of culture tank. The top of the                culture tank is connected to condensed water tank                of gas pretreatment module. The liquid outlet of                water split head of gas pretreatment module is                connected to aeration water storage layer of                culture tank. The gas outlet of water separation                head is connected to carbon dioxide sensor.
   USE - The device is useful in degradation                plastic.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: simplifies the design of the                arrangement of sensors on the pipeline and in the                culture tank; and is conducive to the accurate                conversion of carbon dioxide mass results. The                operation method is capable of automatically                testing the concentration and flow of carbon                dioxide and converts a carbon dioxide mass to                provide a trend curve of biological decomposition                rate; and realizes the automatic management of                composting condition control.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for realizing compost condition control and                monitoring carbon dioxide release amount by the                device.

PT P
PN CN111826846-A
TI Performing pulsed supercritical fluid printing and dyeing process involves pressurizing and heating liquid carbon dioxide or liquid nitrogen, and converting into supercritical carbon dioxide or supercritical nitrogen.
AU YANG J
   LI W
   YANG F
AE LUOYANG MAITUOFENG TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (LUOY-Non-standard)
GA 2020A74895
AB    NOVELTY - Performing pulsed supercritical fluid printing and dyeing process involves pressurizing and heating liquid carbon dioxide or liquid nitrogen, and converting into supercritical carbon dioxide or supercritical nitrogen. The supercritical carbon dioxide or supercritical nitrogen is mixed with three primary color dyes in proportion in dye tank to form dye solution. The pulsed syringe is used to inject dye solution from top and bottom of dyeing tank into dyeing tank. The yarn spindle from inlet end is dyed, under action of ultrasonic generator and bidirectional circulating pump. The dyeing is mature and sent out from outlet end. The dye liquor discharged from dyeing tank is returned to dye tank for recycling, separated and purified discharged dye liquor into liquid carbon dioxide or liquid nitrogen, dyed, and recycled or stored as liquid carbon dioxide or liquid nitrogen raw material and dye raw material.
   USE - Method for performing pulsed supercritical fluid printing and dyeing process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform pulsed supercritical fluid printing and dyeing process that has clean production, good economic benefits and significant environmental benefits, achieves best dye liquor conveying condition, improves uniformity and efficiency, and in eco-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a pulsed supercritical fluid printing and dyeing device, which comprises super fluid storage tank, three primary color tank, dye tank, pulse generator, dyeing kettle, dye collection tank, decompression cooling unit and extraction separation unit, superfluid storage tank is connected to dye tank through three primary color tanks through filtering unit, drying unit, heating unit and pressurizing unit sequentially through pipeline, in order to convert carbon dioxide or nitrogen in superfluid storage tank into supercritical carbon dioxide or supercritical nitrogen after pressurized and heated, supercritical carbon dioxide or supercritical nitrogen is mixed with three primary color dyes in three primary color tanks in proportion in dye tank to form dye solution, dye tank is connected to dyeing tank through pipeline by auxiliary tank, flow meter and number of pulse injectors connected in parallel, and each of pulse injectors is respectively connected to number of liquid inlet holes arranged in sequence at top and bottom of each section of dyeing kettle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The pressurizing unit is used to pressurize carbon dioxide pressure to above 16 megapascal. The heating unit is used to heat carbon dioxide and nitrogen temperature to 80 degrees C and 20 degrees C, respectively. The pressurizing unit is used to pressurize nitrogen gas to 3.4 megapascal. The dye assistant is added to dye liquor. The dyeing kettle is divided into section along length. The each section of dyeing kettle is equipped with pulse injector, ultrasonic generator and two-way circulation pump. The impulse injectors are respectively connected to multiple liquid inlet holes arranged sequentially at top and bottom of each section of dyeing kettle. The two ends of two-way circulation pump are respectively connected with pulse syringe and dyeing kettle by Y-shaped connectors. The two Y-shaped connectors are respectively located at liquid inlet holes at both ends of bottom of each section of dyeing kettle. The thermal insulation treatment is carried out on dyeing kettle during dyeing process. Preferred Conditions: The dyeing kettle comprises pipe of 24-25 meters, covered with thermal insulation layer.
DC F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC F03-C01; F03-F01; F03-F29; J04-X

PT P
PN WO2018117947-A1; SE201651681-A1; SE540500-C2; CN110099724-A; KR2019099448-A; IN201917028537-A; EP3554650-A1; US2019329082-A1; EP3554650-A4; HK40005443-A0; US11247088-B2; EP3554650-B1; IN392083-B; ES2909136-T3
TI Fire extinguishing device for extinguishing small-scale fires, for instance in kitchen environments, has carbon dioxide directing unit arranged to direct jet of carbon dioxide flowing out from flask when valve is open.
AU WESTBERG A
AE INVENTI VIDI AB (INVE-Non-standard)
   AM WESTBERG INVEST AB (AMWE-Non-standard)
   AM WESTBERG INVEST AB (AMWE-Non-standard)
   AM WESTBERG INVEST AB (AMWE-Non-standard)
   AM WESTBERG INVEST AB (AMWE-Non-standard)
GA 201851591L
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a flask engagement unit (110) that are arranged to engage with a flask (10) for compressed carbon dioxide and to hold the fire extinguishing device (100) in an operating orientation in relation to such a flask. The fask comprises a valve (13) which is spring-loaded towards a closed state, and is associated with a longitudinal direction (L) extending in a upwards direction from a bottom (11) of a flask to a top (12) of a flask and a downwards direction from top to bottom. A radial direction (R) is perpendicular to longitudinal direction, and an angular direction (A). An actuating means (120) is arranged to apply a pressure on the valve when in operating orientation so that the valve as a result of pressure opens and carbon dioxide flows out from flask. A carbon dioxide directing unit is arranged to direct a jet (20) of carbon dioxide flowing out from the flask when valve is open.
   USE - Fire extinguishing device for extinguishing small-scale fires, for instance in kitchen environments.
   ADVANTAGE - The fire extinguishing capability provided by the carbon dioxide contents of such a flask, while it may not readily compare to purpose-built fire extinguishers, and is generally adequate to extinguish smaller fires, or for delaying fire development sufficiently to save lives. The flasks are readily available in many kitchens, and since they are typically frequently used and replaced, users have a high chance of finding them once needed. By realizing, a source of fire extinguishing medium is already readily available in many kitchens, in the form of pressurized carbon dioxide flasks for producing carbonized beverages. Carbon dioxide does not soil the premises where the fire is extinguished as much as conventional fire extinguishers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for extinguishing a fire.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a partly removed view of the fire extinguishing device.
   Flask (10)
   Bottom (11)
   Top (12)
   Valve (13)
   Carbon dioxide jet (20)
   Device (100)
   Engagement means (110)
   Actuating means (120)
   Angular direction (A)
   Longitudinal direction (L)
   Radial direction (R)

PN CN107828432-A; CN207435384-U
TI Household garbage recycling system has preprocessing device, pyrolysis device, high-frequency pneumatic separator, carbon dioxide capturing device, gasification device, gasification gas treatment device and power generation device.
AU REN H
   LIU L
   ZHANG A
   LONG Y
   ZHOU F
   CHEN Q
   WU D
AE BEIJING SHENYUAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018243291
AB    NOVELTY - A household garbage recycling system comprises a preprocessing device, a pyrolysis device, a high-frequency pneumatic separator, a carbon dioxide capturing device, a gasification device, a gasification gas treatment device, a gasification gas collecting device and a power generation device. The carbon dioxide capturing device comprises an absorption tower, a liquid-rich pump, a desorption tower, a cooler and a gas-liquid separator, and is used for extracting high-purity carbon dioxide. A feeding port of the pyrolysis device is connected with a discharging port of the preprocessing device.
   USE - Household garbage recycling system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A household garbage recycling system comprises a preprocessing device, a pyrolysis device, a high-frequency pneumatic separator, a carbon dioxide capturing device, a gasification device, a gasification gas treatment device, a gasification gas collecting device and a power generation device. The carbon dioxide capturing device comprises an absorption tower, a liquid-rich pump, a desorption tower, a cooler and a gas-liquid separator, and is used for extracting high-purity carbon dioxide. A feeding port of the pyrolysis device is connected with a discharging port of the preprocessing device. A pyrolysis carbon outlet of the pyrolysis device is connected with a feeding port of the high-frequency pneumatic separator. A combustion tail gas outlet of the pyrolysis device is connected with the carbon dioxide capturing device. A high-temperature pyrolysis oil gas outlet of the pyrolysis device is connected with the power generation device. A feeding port of the gasification device is connected with a discharge port of the high-frequency pneumatic separator. A gas outlet of the carbon dioxide capturing device and a low-temperature steam outlet of the power generation device are respectively connected with a gas inlet of the gasification device. The gasification device is sequentially connected with the gasification gas treatment device and the gasification gas collecting device, and is used for degasification and purification treatment of gasification gas for storage and utilization. A flue gas outlet of the power generation device is connected with the carbon dioxide capturing device. The power generation device is connected with a power grid. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the method for recycling household garbage using the above system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The gasification gas treatment device comprises a deacidification device and a gas purification device. The deacidification device is used for realizing waste heat recovery and deacidification of gasified gas. The gas purification device comprises a dust removal tower, a cooler (I), an electric tar catcher and a dry desulfurization tower. The pyrolysis device comprises a rotary bed pyrolysis furnace, a radiant tube burner and a cotton material. The radiant tube burner is arranged on an annular furnace wall of the rotary bed pyrolysis furnace, and provides the heat required for the combustion and heating reaction. A furnace bottom of rotary bed pyrolysis furnace is rotatable annular furnace. The rotary bed pyrolysis furnace comprises a drying zone, a pyrolysis reaction zone (I), a pyrolysis reaction zone (II), and a pyrolysis reaction zone (III). A material is fed from the drying zone to the pyrolysis reaction zone (III). A gas outlet is arranged at the top to collect high temperature pyrolysis gas. A discharging device is arranged at pyrolysis reaction zone (III) to collect pyrolytic carbon. The perforated plate is used as a material plate of the rotary bed pyrolysis furnace. The household garbage recycling system further comprises: a crusher (II) arranged between the pyrolysis device and the high-frequency pneumatic separator and arranged for breaking up pyrolytic carbon coming out of the rotary bed pyrolysis furnace; a high-temperature oil and gas purification device arranged between an oil and gas outlet of the pyrolysis device and a gas-fired direct-fired boiler. The high-frequency pneumatic separator includes a fan, a sorting bed surface, a vibration feeding device and an excitation motor, where rubbish charcoal is evenly transferred to the sorting bed by the vibration feeding device and garbage carbon sorting is realized under the levitation force generated by the excitation motor and the suspended airflow generated by the fan. A gas outlet (I) of the gasification gas collection device is connected with the power generation device. A gas outlet (II) of the gasification gas collection device is connected with the rotary bed pyrolysis furnace to provide raw materials for the pyrolysis of the rotary bed.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); Q52 (Reaction engines: external combustion; gas turbines; rockets (F01D, F02C, F02K, F02M)); X11 (Power Generation and High Power Machines)

PN CN114874117-A
TI Process for preparing thiourea dioxide by carbon            dioxide solvent method comprises adding dry ice or            liquid carbon dioxide in the reaction kettle, and a            small amount of thiourea aqueous solution, dropping            hydrogen peroxide and the rest of thiourea aqueous            solution, aging and drying.
AU XING Y
   LIU Q
   YAN Y
   LAI Y
   ZHAO X
   ZHAO J
   ZHANG S
   CHEN Z
   CHEN W
   WU T
   ZHAO H
AE PUYANG HONGYE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (PUYA-Non-standard)
   PUYANG SHENGKAI ENVIRONMENTAL NEW MATERI (PUYA-Non-standard)
GA 2022A5128Q
AB    NOVELTY - Process for preparing thiourea dioxide by                carbon dioxide solvent method comprises (i) adding                dry ice or liquid carbon dioxide in the reaction                kettle, and a small amount of thiourea aqueous                solution, (ii) under the condition of stirring,                when the system temperature is stable at                3-6&#8451;, dropping hydrogen peroxide with                mass concentration of 70-80 % and the rest of                thiourea aqueous solution, in the dropping process,                controlling the pressure in the reaction kettle and                maintaining at 3.7-4 MPa and (iii) after finishing                dripping, aging for 5-15 minutes and drying to                obtain the product.
   USE - Process for preparing thiourea dioxide by                carbon dioxide solvent method.
   ADVANTAGE - The process uses carbon dioxide dissolved in                water as solvent, prepared by hydrogen peroxide                thiourea, which not only shortens the reaction                time, the prepared thiourea dioxide has high yield,                and good purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The mol ratio of                  thiourea to hydrogen peroxide is 1:2.2-2.5. The                  mass ratio of thiourea to dry ice is 1:20-25. In                  the step (i) and step (ii), the mass                  concentration of the thiourea aqueous solution is                  10-15 %, and the adding amount of the thiourea                  aqueous solution in the step (i) is 8-12 wt.% of                  thiourea aqueous solution. In the step (ii), the                  stirring speed is 100-800 revolution/minute. In                  the step (ii), when the dosage of the raw                  material thiourea is 100 g, the dripping speed of                  the double-oxygen water is controlled to be 6-8                  ml/minute, and the dripping speed of the thiourea                  aqueous solution is controlled to be 40-45                  ml/minute.

PN CN113813766-A
TI Anhydrous phase change absorbent used for carbon dioxide comprises tetramethylammonium glycine and alcohol.
AU GAO G
   WU F
   LUO C
   LI X
   JIANG W
   ZHANG L
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 202209896F
AB    NOVELTY - Anhydrous phase change absorbent comprises tetramethylammonium glycine and alcohol.
   USE - The anhydrous phase change absorbent is used for carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The absorbent after absorbing CO2 generates precipitates, which are easily separated, and most of the carbon dioxide product is concentrated in the lower precipitates i.e. rich phase. When desorbing, the absorbent only needs to heat the rich phase, can reduce the heating amount of solution, reduces the regeneration energy consumption. Alcohol is added to greatly reduce the viscosity of (N1111) (Gly), and at the same time participates in the reaction, improving the theoretical absorption capacity, and the generation of the precipitates promotes the absorption reaction to proceed forward, further promoting absorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) absorption method of carbon dioxide comprising contacting carbon dioxide containing gas and anhydrous phase change absorbent, and obtaining absorbent with precipitates; and
   (2) regeneration of anhydrous phase change absorbent comprising obtaining the absorbent with precipitates after absorbing the carbon dioxide; separating the absorbent with precipitates, obtaining rich-phase precipitates and liquid phase; desorbing the rich-phase precipitates to obtain a desorbent; and mixing the desorbent with the liquid phase to finish regeneration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of tetramethylammonium glycine to alcohol is 1:4-2:1. The tetramethylammonium glycine is at 20-67 mass%. The alcohol comprises at least one of 1-5C alcohol. It comprises ethanol, n-propyl alcohol and/or n-butyl alcohol and is analytically pure.Preferred Method: The temperature of contact absorption is 30-60degrees Celsius. The desorption mode is heating at 118-122degrees Celsius degrees centigrade.

PN US2021155864-A1; WO2022147610-A1
TI Preparing a fuel from biogas involves providing            biogas from a multiple of biogas sources, where the            biogas from each biogas source produced in a process            comprising collecting raw biogas from one of the biogas            sources in the multiple.
AU DECHMAN J
   FOODY B
   FOODY P J
AE IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
   IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
GA 202157241R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a fuel from biogas involves                providing biogas from a multiple of biogas sources,                where the biogas from each biogas source produced                in a process comprising collecting raw biogas from                one of the biogas sources in the multiple, the raw                biogas comprising methane and carbon dioxide. The                raw biogas is subjected to a partial purification                to produce partially purified biogas, where the                partial purification removes less than 75% of the                carbon dioxide from the raw biogas. The fuel                production processing comprising feeding the biogas                provided to a biogas upgrading system, the biogas                upgrading system configured to provide a compressed                stream of carbon dioxide removed from the biogas,                and provided carbon dioxide from the compressed                stream of carbon dioxide for transport by vehicle,                pipeline, or a combination.
   USE - Method for preparing a fuel from biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows the the greenhouse gas                emissions attributed to compressing the biogas for                transport and/or the transport of the biogas is                offset by sequestering carbon dioxide (CO2) removed                from the biogas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method comprises                  subjecting the raw biogas to a partial                  purification to produce partially purified                  biogas, and where the partial purification does                  not include carbon dioxide removal. The fuel                  production process comprises reforming upgraded                  biogas, and wherein the method comprises                  sequestering carbon dioxide produced by the                  reforming. Preferred Components: The fuel is                  renewable natural gas, preferably the fuel is                  renewable hydrogen, renewable methanol, renewable                  ethanol, gasoline having renewable content,                  diesel having renewable content, or dimethyl                  ether. The biogas upgrading system includes a                  carbon dioxide removal unit that provides organic                  physical scrubbing, chemical scrubbing, pressure                  swing adsorption, membrane separation, or                  cryogenic upgrading.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparing dairy-based beverage involves preparing            beverage mix, carbonating beverage mix with carbon            dioxide gas, filling container with beverage mix,            pressurizing container with carbon dioxide gas, and            nitrogenating beverage mix.
AU PREWETT J
   SHOEMAKER A
   KENNEDY G
   DENNIS S
   ANDERSON R
   LOPER E
   NYSSEN A
   LAABS J
   MARKO S
   RESURECCION F
AE TILLAMOOK CREAMERY ASSOC (TILL-Non-standard)
   PREWETT J (PREW-Individual)
   SHOEMAKER A (SHOE-Individual)
   KENNEDY G (KENN-Individual)
   DENNIS S (DENN-Individual)
   ANDERSON R (ANDE-Individual)
   LOPER E (LOPE-Individual)
   NYSSEN A (NYSS-Individual)
   LAABS J (LAAB-Individual)
   MARKO S (MARK-Individual)
   RESURECCION F (RESU-Individual)
GA 202128127L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a dairy-based beverage                involves preparing a beverage mix, carbonating the                beverage mix with carbon dioxide gas, filling a                container with the beverage mix, pressurizing the                container with carbon dioxide gas to a                predetermined pressure, and nitrogenating the                beverage mix with nitrogen gas.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a                dairy-based beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage has light sparkling texture,                velvety smooth finish, creamy mouthfeel and taste                of ice cream.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for dispensing a dairy-based beverage                comprising a carbon dioxide gas tank, a nitrogen                diffuser, a nitrogen gas tank coupled to the                nitrogen diffuser, a beverage mix container coupled                to the carbon dioxide gas tank and the nitrogen                diffuser so that the nitrogen diffuser receives a                carbonated beverage mix stored within the beverage                mix container, and a tap or faucet coupled to the                nitrogen diffuser so that the tap or faucet                receives a nitrogenated and carbonated beverage mix                from the nitrogen diffuser.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method further                  involves dispensing the beverage mix from a tap                  or faucet. The step of nitrogenating the beverage                  mix is carried out with a nitrogen diffuser,                  infuser, or sparger in-line with the tap or                  faucet. The step of filling the container with                  the beverage mix involves filling containers with                  the beverage mix, pressurizing the container with                  carbon dioxide gas comprises pressurizing each of                  the containers to the predetermined pressure, and                  nitrogenating the beverage mix comprises                  nitrogenating the beverage mix in each of the                  containers. The step of nitrogenating the                  beverage mix in each of the containers involves                  inserting a device configured to release nitrogen                  gas into the beverage mix in to each of the                  containers. The step of preparing the beverage                  mix involves mixing ingredients to form the                  beverage mix, pasteurizing the beverage mix, and                  homogenizing the beverage mix. The step of mixing                  the ingredients involves mixing together milk,                  egg product, cream, sucrose, water, and flavor                  base, and further mixing a stabilizer, buffer                  salt, defoaming agent and/or an emulsifier. The                  step of carbonating the beverage mix involves                  carbonating the beverage mix to a pressure of 30                  PSI, +/-10 PSI.Preferred Components: The beverage mix                  container further comprises the carbonated                  beverage mix. The nitrogen diffuser can be                  adjusted to alter a balance between dissolved                  carbon dioxide and dissolved nitrogen in the                  beverage mix.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN112177603-A
TI Method for quantitatively evaluating carbon dioxide extraction crude component content in supercritical state, involves obtaining mass of crude oil sample, followed by obtaining temperature range and pressure range of oil reservoir.
AU YANG S
   LV C
   HE Y
   YU H
   LIAO H
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC EXPLORATION & PRODN RES INST (SNPC-C)
GA 202106173M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining the mass of the crude oil sample. The temperature range and the pressure range of the oil reservoir are obtained. The state of the crude oil component and the carbon dioxide at the target temperature and the target pressure are judged. The crude oil component and carbon dioxide density are calculated under the target temperature and the target pressure. The molecular interaction potential between the crude component is determined.
   USE - The method is useful for quantitatively evaluating content of crude oil component extracted by carbon dioxide under supercritical state.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables quantitatively evaluating the carbon dioxide in the ultra-low permeability oil reservoir so as to improve the oil displacement efficiency, solving the problem that the existing quantitative evaluation carbon dioxide improves the low permeability reservoir oil displacement efficiency method consumption time, cannot form a set of fast evaluation and large scale application technology of defect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crude oil is extra-low permeability oil reservoir crude oil. Preferred Conditions: The mass of the crude oil sample, the molar content of the crude oil component and the mass of the carbon dioxide (CO2) are measured by a gas chromatography analyzer.

   ADVANTAGE - The method is eco-friendly and economical by removing metallic impurities by aeration of carbon dioxide in biodiesel by-products and stirred water. The method contributes to carbon economy by consuming carbon dioxide in the process of removing metallic impurities.

   Injecting carbon dioxide bubbles into water to remove metallic impurities of biodiesel by-products (S20)
   Separating biodiesel by-product (S30)

PN CN111424301-A; CN111424301-B
TI Improving copper oxide photoelectric catalytic carbon dioxide conversion efficiency in pulse potential mode comprises anodizing, annealing, soaking in copper-containing electrolyte, electrodepositing copper and reducing carbon dioxide.
AU ZHANG L
   CAO H
   WANG X
   ZHANG H
   ZHENG G
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202069397J
AB    NOVELTY - Improving copper oxide photoelectric catalytic carbon dioxide conversion efficiency in pulse potential mode comprises (a) using the pre-treated titanium sheet as an anode, performing constant potential anodization to obtain a pre-substrate, annealing the pre-substrate to obtain a substrate, (b) soaking the substrate in a copper-containing electrolyte, electrodepositing copper on the surface of the substrate by pulse electrodeposition, heating to obtain a copper oxide/titanium dioxide nanotubes (TNTs) electrode and (c) using the obtained copper oxide/TNTs electrode as the working electrode and the platinum electrode as the counter electrode, soaking in the electrolyte, continuously introducing carbon dioxide, using the xenon lamp as the light source and reducing carbon dioxide in pulsed potential mode by setting different potentials.
   USE - The method is useful for improving copper oxide photoelectric catalytic carbon dioxide conversion efficiency in pulse potential mode.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the accuracy of electrodeposition; has low requirements for manufacturing equipment, low cost, high efficiency and easy realization and realizes mass production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pretreatment includes chemical polishing. The polishing liquid used in the chemical polishing contains 45-90g/l chromium oxide and 45-90ml/l hydrogen fluoride solution with a concentration of 38-42 wt.% and the balance is water. In the anodic oxidation process: using a graphite electrode or a platinum electrode as a cathode, and using a hydrofluoric acid solution of 1-5 wt.% as an electrolyte. The purity of the titanium sheet is greater than or equal to 99%. The copper-containing electrolyte is a soluble copper salt aqueous solution containing 0.1-1 mol/l divalent copper ions. The soluble copper salt is at least one of copper sulfate, copper chloride and copper nitrate. The electrolyte is an aqueous sodium bicarbonate solution with a concentration of 0.05-0.2 mol/l. A saturated calomel electrode is also provided as a reference electrode when performing carbon dioxide reduction in pulse potential mode.

GA 2020157822

AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)

   M = transition metal;and
   L = monodentate ligand or combined bidentate                ligand.

TI Curing binder composition involves forming a            binder composition, curing the binder composition in a            negative pressure state, water- curing the pre-cured            binder composition in an aqueous carbon dioxide            absorbent solution, and curing the first-cured binder            composition in relative humidity atmosphere.

   HAN S H
   KIM J H
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 2022E05177
AB    NOVELTY - Curing a binder composition involves forming a                binder composition, curing the binder composition                in a negative pressure state (pre-curing step),                water curing the pre-cured binder composition in an                aqueous carbon dioxide absorbent solution, and                curing the first-cured binder composition in a 95%                or more relative humidity atmosphere.
   USE - Method for curing a binder composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method water-curing binder composition in                the aqueous carbon dioxide absorbent solution, and                carbon dioxide dissolved in an aqueous solution                reacts with calcium and silica in the binder                composition to form calcium carbonate and calcium                silicate hydrate, and thus, carbon dioxide                absorption and initial strength may be                improved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The binder composition                  includes Portland cement, Class C fly ash, and                  ground granulated blast furnace slag, or water,                  liquid sodium silicate, an aqueous potassium                  hydroxide solution, and an aqueous sodium                  hydroxide solution, preferably the binder                  composition includes the Portland cement, a                  bottom ash fine aggregate or water. The aqueous                  carbon dioxide absorbent solution includes an                  aqueous alkanolamine solution including                  monoethanolamine (MEA), diethanolamine (DEA), and                  triethanolamine (TEA), or an alkaline aqueous                  solution including potassium hydroxide (KOH),                  sodium hydroxide (NaOH) and sodium silicon oxide                  (Na2SiO3). The aqueous carbon dioxide absorbent                  solution includes a primary alkanolamine.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN112920071-A
TI Preparing carboxamide compound using metal/covalent-organic framework and carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. reacting amine compound and silane substrates in solvent using metal/covalent-organic framework and carbon dioxide.
AU LU J
   HE J
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 202165541P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carboxamide compound using metal/covalent-organic framework and carbon dioxide, comprises reacting amine compound and silane substrates in solvent using metal/covalent-organic framework as catalyst and carbon dioxide as carbon source under normal temperature and pressure to prepare carboxamide compound.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carboxamide compound using metal/covalent-organic framework and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids use of high-pressure hydrogen and toxic carbon monoxide; has mild reaction conditions (normal temperature and normal pressure); has low cost, simple operation; and produces formamide product with yield of 99%, which provides green synthesis method for N-acylation reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for metal/covalent-organic framework as catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silane is phenylsilane; and the solvent is acetonitrile, tetrahydrofuran, dimethylformamide or methanol. The molar ratio between amine compound and phenylsilane is 1:1-30. In metal/covalent-organic framework, the covalent organic framework is prepared by reaction of benzenetriol and phenylenediamine; and the metal ions are zinc, nickel, copper, palladium or iron ions. The amine compound is cyclic amine compound of formula (R1-NH-R2). The metal salt solution is aqueous solution of zinc chloride, palladium(II) chloride, platinum(II) chloride, copper(II) chloride, nickel(II) chloride or ferric chloride.R1, R2 = H, halo, aliphatic substituent or aromatic substituent.Preferred Method: The reaction temperature is 0-60 degrees C, and the reaction time is 5-48 hours. The reaction pressure is 1 atm. The metal/covalent-organic framework is prepared by reacting benzenetriol and phenylenediamine to prepare covalent organic framework, and mixing covalent organic framework with metal salt solution and then drying.

PN CN111333825-A
TI New carbon dioxide-based polyester-polycarbonate quaternary block copolymer compound, prepared by e.g. adding monomer, catalyst and solvent into autoclave, then introducing carbon dioxide and performing ring-opening polymerization reaction.
AU MENG Y
   YE S
   XIAO M
   WANG S
   LIANG J
   HAN D
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 202061150G
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-based polyester-polycarbonate quaternary block copolymer compound (I) is new.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide-based polyester-polycarbonate quaternary block copolymer.
   ADVANTAGE - (I): utilizes Lewis acid-base pair as a catalyst, and introduces phthalic anhydride and epoxycyclohexane into main chain of polymethyl ethylene carbonate (PPC) through one-pot one-step method, which improves the PPC Glass transition temperature, thermal stability and tensile strength, and eliminates residual problems of metal catalysts compared to materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide-based polyester-polycarbonate quaternary block copolymer compound of formula (I) is new.
   a-d = greater than or equal to 1.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises adding monomer, catalyst and solvent into autoclave, then introducing carbon dioxide and placing the autoclave in oil bath for ring-opening polymerization, completing the reaction, then dissolving the obtained product in dichloromethane, and adding small amount of acid to stop the reaction, and then precipitating the product in ethanol, where the monomers are epoxide, phthalic anhydride and carbon dioxide, and the epoxide includes propylene oxide and epoxycyclohexane. Preferred Components: The molar ratio between propylene oxide and (epoxycyclohexane and phthalic anhydride) is 2-10:1; and internal pressure range of carbon dioxide after passing into autoclave is 0.1-4.0 MPa. The catalyst is Lewis acid-base pair composite catalyst; Lewis acid is organic boron compound; and Lewis base is organic amine (salt). The organoboron compound is triethylboron, triphenylboron or tri(pentafluorophenyl)boron; and organic amine (salt) is bis(triphenylphosphoryl) ammonium chloride, tetra-n-butylammonium chloride, tetra-n-butylammonium bromide or 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene. The mass ratio between catalyst and epoxide is 1:100-3000. Preferred Method: The reaction temperature of the ring-opening polymerization reaction is 40-100 degrees C and reaction time is 4-72 hours.

AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 201909307A
