PN CN108918597-A; CN108918597-B
TI Preparing carbon dioxide sensor comprises e.g. using the carbonizable polymer or small molecule carbon source, electrically connecting at least two places of the porous carbon device to form an electrode, and performing surface modification.
AU LIU T
   YAO Y
   LI G
AE BEIJING ORIENT IRRIGATION ECO-TECH CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 201898255Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide sensor comprises (1) using the carbonizable polymer or small molecule carbon source to prepare porous carbon device; (2) electrically connecting at least two places of the porous carbon device to form an electrode, and (3) performing surface modification of the porous carbon device sensitive compound layer, then carrying out heat treatment to obtain the carbon dioxide sensor, where the resistance of the sensitive compound layer changes after contacting with carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide sensor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method and sensor: is economic, has universality, flexible design, simple production, automatic processing means, good process controllability, and is fast and balance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRONICS - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the small molecule carbon source is subjected to pyrolysis deposition to prepare a porous carbon device, and the thermal cracking temperature is 400-1800 degrees C. In the step (3), the heat treatment temperature is 10-150 degrees C. Preferred Components: In the step (1), the carbonizable polymer is carbonized in situ by a laser, and the laser light source used has a wavelength of 10nm - 1 mm and a laser irradiation power is 0.2-10 W.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbonizable polymer is at least one of polyimide, polyacrylonitrile, lignin, cellulose, polyvinyl chloride, phenolic resin and amino group-containing polysiloxane. The small molecule carbon source is at least one of ethylene, carbon monoxide, methane, n-butanol and ethanol. In the step (1), the porous carbon is inter-digital, linear array or dot array pattern. In the step (3), the material of the sensitive compound layer is at least one of polyethyleneimine, polyethyleneimine derivative, polyaniline, polyaniline derivative, and amino group-containing polysiloxane. In the step (3), the sensitive compound layer has a thickness of 0.01-20 mu m.

PN CN108617756-A
TI Quick freezing fruits and vegetables involves            treating fruits and vegetables with high-pressure            carbon dioxide to obtain fruits and vegetables, and            quickly freezing liquid nitrogen of fruits and            vegetables after carbon dioxide treatment.
AU WANG Y
   LIAO X
   HU X
   ZHANG F
AE UNIV CHINA AGRICULTURAL (UCAG-C)
GA 201880051F
AB    NOVELTY - Quick freezing fruits and vegetables involves                treating fruits and vegetables with high-pressure                carbon dioxide to obtain fruits and vegetables, and                quickly freezing liquid nitrogen of fruits and                vegetables after high-pressure carbon dioxide                treatment.
   USE - Method for quick freezing fruits and                vegetables.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to quick freezing fruits                and vegetables which replaces blanching treatment                in the pretreatment of fruits and vegetables with                high-pressure carbon dioxide treatment, and                combines subsequent liquid nitrogen quick freezing,                avoiding problems such as serious deterioration of                quality in the conventional quick-frozen treatment,                large ice crystal formation in quick freezing, and                serious deterioration of quality after thawing,                better protects nutrition, quality and function of                products and produces high-quality frozen fruits                and vegetables.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The high pressure                  carbon dioxide treatment temperature is room                  temperature, the treatment pressure is 5-10 mega                  pascals, the treatment time is 10-20 minutes, and                  the average pressure increase rate and the                  average pressure relief rate are 2-3 mega                  Pascals/minutes and 3-4 mega pascals/minutes,                  respectively. The liquid nitrogen quick freezing                  is performed by a liquid nitrogen spray quick                  freezing machine. The liquid nitrogen quick                  freezing operation is performed by placing                  high-pressure carbon dioxide-treated fruits and                  vegetables in a sample tray matched with a liquid                  nitrogen spray quick-freezing machine so that the                  ambient temperature in the liquid nitrogen spray                  quick-freezing machine is between -40 and 100                  degrees C, placing sample so that the sample                  center temperature drops below -18 degrees C                  within 5-10 minutes. The fruits and vegetables                  are pretreated before the high-pressure carbon                  dioxide treatment of the fruits and vegetables.                  The pretreatment involves cutting the fruit and                  vegetable into pieces and pieces of suitable size                  and then performing the cleaning by placing the                  sliced fruits and vegetables in a container                  containing 20-200 parts per million ClO2                  solution, and placing ultrasonic cleaning In the                  tank, washing for 10-15 minutes, washing, and                  draining cut fruits and vegetables in a sterile                  environment, storing quick-frozen fruits and                  vegetables after packaging, the storage                  temperature being -18 degrees C.

GA 201842767F

   USE - Preparation method of chili oleoresin.

The decompression analysis conditions are separation pressure of 5.5-6 MPa and separation temperature of 50 degrees C.

   GAN B
AE WUXI JINBOCHENG MEDICAL DEVICE TECHNOLOG (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 2021E2646Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for quantitative detection of carbon dioxide involves (1) adding 0.2074 g sodium chloride, 0.0642 g potassium chloride and 2.12 g gelatin at 50degrees Celsius in 20 mL of water to completely dissolve gelatin, weighing 69.7 mg of carbon dioxide ionophore, 24.9 mg cetyl trimethyl ammonium chloride, 306 mg dioctyl sebacate and 181 mg polystyrene, dissolving in 3 mL of tetrahydrofuran, mixing, plating a silver layer with a thickness of 40 mum on the polyethylene terephthalate sheet as a conductive layer, masking conductive layer, immersing the whole-plate product in a potassium chloride solution, demolding the prepared silver/silver chloride electrode, cutting, coating a solid electrolyte layer and a carbon dioxide ion carrier membrane layer on the surface of silver chloride on the reaction electrode, and adding a drop of patient serum and a drop of reference solution to the independent two halves of the carbon dioxide ion dry sheet, and storing the reagents in the shell.
   USE - Method for quantitative detection of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of avoiding the damage of reagent, and avoids the corresponding force deformation caused by various forces in the process of testing, thereby avoiding damage of the reagent in transportation, storage, and deformation of the carbon dioxide ion dry solid electrolyte layer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for quantitative detection of carbon dioxide involves (1) adding 0.2074 g sodium chloride, 0.0642 g potassium chloride and 2.12 g gelatin at 50degrees Celsius in 20 mL of water to completely dissolve gelatin, weighing 69.7 mg of carbon dioxide ionophore, 24.9 mg cetyl trimethyl ammonium chloride, 306 mg dioctyl sebacate and 181 mg polystyrene, dissolving in 3 mL of tetrahydrofuran respectively, placing in refrigerator at 4degrees Celsius for 3 hours to fully dissolve the components, mixing, plating a silver layer with a thickness of 40 mum on the polyethylene terephthalate sheet as a conductive layer, masking conductive layer at intervals of 6 mm by masking technology, immersing the whole-plate product in a potassium chloride solution with a concentration of 0.1 mol/L for anodic oxidation for 20 minutes, reacting the silver layer not covered by the mask with the chloride ions in the solution to form silver chloride, taking out and drying in the dark to obtain layer as reactive electrode, demolding the prepared silver/silver chloride electrode, cutting to a certain size, and coating a solid electrolyte layer and a carbon dioxide ion carrier membrane layer on the surface of silver chloride on the reaction electrode, and adding a drop of patient serum and a drop of reference solution to the independent two halves of the carbon dioxide ion dry sheet, and storing the reagents in the shell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide ion dry sheet is a multi-layer analytical component coated on a polyester substrate. The electrodes comprise carbon dioxide ion carrier layer, reference layer, silver layer and silver chloride layer. The solid electrolyte layer of the carbon dioxide ion dry sheet comprises acetic acid, potassium chloride, sodium chloride and gelatin necessary biochemical reagents.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The carbon dioxide ion carrier membrane layer uses PVC as a three-dimensional network framework.

The electrical resistance of the substrate is higher than the electrical resistance of the first cathode and the electrical resistance of the second cathode. The first and the second catalytic material are composed of the group of the elements such as silver, gold, zinc, tin, bismuth and copper. The substrate is designed as a gas diffusion layer.
   USE - Carbon dioxide electrolyzer for electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide into product of value serve as platform chemical such as methane, ethane, ethene, oxygen-containing hydrocarbons or synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The cathodes are operated permanently and alternately or in a pulsed manner to the other cathode. The high long-term stability and product selectivity of the electrolysis is achieved. The selectivity of the first reaction and the second reaction is advantageously controlled in a targeted manner. The efficiency of the overall reaction of the carbon dioxide electrolyzer is increased. The high selectivity of the overall reaction in the carbon dioxide electrolyzer is enabled. The uniform reaction increases the efficiency of the carbon dioxide electrolyzer. The large number of possible configurations of the separation and the contacting of the first and the second cathode are advantageously made. The efficient reaction is enabled on the catalyst. The use of a gas diffusion electrode has the starting materials can reach the gas diffusion electrode in gaseous form. The improved substance transport of the starting materials is achieved to the electrode compared to a liquid. The electrical short circuit between the first cathode and the second cathode is avoided. The hydrocarbons are advantageously generated in a carbon dioxide electrolyzer on two working electrodes with high selectivity and high efficiency. The product distribution is controlled in a targeted manner over the overall process, so that the selectivity of the overall reaction is increased. The high selectivity increases the overall efficiency of product manufacture. The yield of the product of value is increased by reacting the starting material in the same cathode compartment. The high selectivity for a desired valuable material is made from an electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for operating a carbon dioxide electrolyzer.

   Membrane (9)

TI Device for dosing carbon dioxide snow for use is in cooling or keeping cold of food products, has separating knife arranged at distance from discharge opening, and discharge opening associated with horizontally and vertically movable floor.
AU CLAEYS J
   SCHUDDINCK G
AE MESSER BELGIUM NV (MESG-C)
   MESSER BELGIUM NV (MESG-C)
   MESSER BELGIUM NV (MESG-C)
GA 201923935A
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a storage container (2) into which a supply line (3) that is provided for liquid or snow-like carbon dioxide and a gas discharge line (5) is provided for discharging carbon dioxide gas. A discharge opening (7) is arranged on a bottom side and equipped with a separating knife (9) which is arranged at a distance from the discharge opening. The discharge opening is associated with a horizontally and vertically movable floor (8). The outer cross-section is located between a first position in which the discharge opening is released and the movable floor is arranged laterally on the storage container, a second position in which the movable floor closes the discharge opening and a third position in which the movable floor is limitedly inserted into the discharge opening of the storage container.
   USE - Device for dosing carbon dioxide snow for use is in cooling or keeping cold of food products, such as dough pieces, meat, fish, fruits and vegetables, and other products such as chemical or pharmaceutical products.
   ADVANTAGE - The risk of deterioration of functionality by caking carbon dioxide snow or freezing of moisture is minimized. The cutting blades can be equipped with heaters to prevent caking of carbon dioxide snow. The heating device ensures that no carbon dioxide snow adheres to the underside of the cutting blade. The premature breakup of the snow portion is largely avoided by the compression of the snow. The soil prevents the escape of carbon dioxide snow from the storage container.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The in that the separating knife and/or the movable floor are equipped with drives controlled by a control unit (23) in response to sensor (24, 25) measured parameters is controllable. The first and second drives (13, 14) is equipped with movable floor. The first drive has a horizontal movement of the movable floor and a second drive is adapted to allow vertical movement of the soil. The inner cross section of the discharge opening and the outer cross section of the movable floor are rectangular. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for filling coolant receiving compartments of cooling containers with carbon dioxide snow, in particular for transport cooling of food, has storage container is adapted for portioning the carbon dioxide snow and for feeding the portioned carbon dioxide snow to the coolant receiving compartment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the device for dosing carbon dioxide snow.
   Storage container (2)
   Supply line (3)
   Gas discharge line (5)
   Discharge opening (7)
   Movable floor (8)
   Separating knife (9)
   Drives (13,14)
   Control unit (23)
   Sensors (24,25)

PN WO2022220681-A1
TI Preparing compressed fuel comprising carbon            dioxide as vehicle fuel, comprises providing biogas,            removing components from the biogas to obtain purified            biogas having methane and carbon dioxide, and            compressing the purified biogas.
AU MANS P W J L
   KALFF J R
   EISING J W
AE GASHOUDERS BV (GASH-Non-standard)
GA 2022D22347
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a compressed fuel, comprises:                providing a biogas; removing components from the                biogas to obtain a purified biogas having a methane                content of &#8805; 30 mol.% and a carbon                dioxide content of &#8805; 10 mol.%; and                compressing the purified biogas to a pressure of                &#8805; 70 bara to form a compressed                fuel.
   USE - The process is useful: for preparing the                compressed fuel comprising carbon dioxide which is                useful as engine fuel, preferably a vehicle fuel                (all claimed); and in field of renewable                energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: does not require to remove carbon                dioxide to a level lower than 10 mol.% prior to                pressurising to &#8805; 70 bar; and produces                compressed fuel which is low-cost and low-energy                dense.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMs are also included                for:
   compressed fuel is obtained by the process;                and
   compressed fuel, comprising methane and having                a carbon dioxide content of &#8805; 10 mol.%,                preferably 20-45%, where the compressed biogas is                under a pressure of &#8805; 70 bara, preferably                70-350 bara.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide                  content in the purified biogas is &#8805; 15                  mol.%, preferably 20-45 mol.%. &#8804; 75%,                  preferably &#8804; 25% Carbon dioxide content                  of the biogas is removed. Essentially no carbon                  dioxide is removed from the biogas. The fuel                  comprises methane and carbon dioxide in a molar                  ratio of 90:10-50:50, preferably 80:20-60:40. In                  the process: the components are removed from the                  biogas are halogen compounds, nitrogen compounds,                  silicon compounds, sulfur compounds and water,                  preferably silicon compounds e.g. siloxanes. The                  components further comprise water. The purified                  biogas comprises &#8804; 5 ppm.wt. hydrogen                  sulfide; &#8804; 30 ppm.wt. water;                  &#8804; 10 ppm.wt. siloxanes; &#8805;                  30-60 mol.% carbon dioxide; and/or &#8805; 40                  mol.% methane. The natural process includes the                  breakdown of biogenic material, where the                  biogenic material comprises manure, plant                  material, food material, and/or waste. The                  compressed fuel comprises &#8804; 5 ppm. wt.                  hydrogen sulfide, 80 ppm. wt. water, &#8804;                  10 mg/Nm3siloxanes, &#8805; 30-60 mol.%                  carbon dioxide and/or &#8805; 40 mol.%                  methane. Preferred Process: The removing                  comprises adsorption on activated carbon,                  chilling, temperature swing adsorption, pressure                  swing adsorption, amine swing absorption,                  scrubbing, cryogenic separation, and membrane                  separation, preferably the removing comprises                  adsorption on activated carbon, chilling and                  temperature swing adsorption. The purified biogas                  is compressed to a pressure of 70-350 bara,                  preferably 130-300 bara. The compressed fuel is                  in a supercritical state. At least part of the                  biogas originates from a natural process or an                  industrial process.
   EXAMPLE - Compressed fuel was obtained by passing biogas                over activated carbon bed, drying the biogas in a                chiller and subsequently subjecting the biogas to                temperature swing adsorption. Subsequently, the                purified biogas was pre-pressurized in a                pre-pressure vessel after which it was compressed                to a pressure of 70 bara, and subjected to                filtration. The compressed fuel was analyzed for                its composition, it comprised of 37.3 mol.% carbon                dioxide, 55.1 mol.% methane, hydrogen sulfide,                sulfur dioxide, hexamethyldisiloxane and silicon                (remaining amount). The gas composition was                analyzed in accordance with nitrogen and carbon                dioxide were detected using gas chromatography                (Interscience Thermo Scientific Trace 1300 Gas                chromatograph). The other components were detected                using a temperature programmed gas chromatograph                with a capillary column (Interscience Thermo                Scientific Trace 1300 gas chromatograph) and a mass                selective detector (Interscience Thermo Scientific                ISQ QD SQMS). For hydrogen sulfide and sulfur                dioxide a selective analysis of sulfur was                performed using selective ion monitoring. For                hexamethyldisiloxane and total silicon a selective                analysis of siloxanes was performed using selective                ion monitoring.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. PN CN110615770-A; CN110615770-B
TI Carbon dioxide-responsive derivative used in click reactions, which is prepared by adding melamine and deionized water to the reaction container, and stirring under an ice-water bath until uniformly dispersed.
AU YIN H
   FENG Y
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 202001796T
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-responsive derivative is represented by (I).
   USE - Carbon dioxide-responsive derivative used in click reactions.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-responsive derivative has good stimulus response performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide-responsive derivative is represented by formula (I).
   a and b=1-5, where a and b are integers.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing carbon dioxide-responsive derivative, which involves:
   (A) adding melamine and deionized water to the reaction container, and stirring under an ice-water bath until uniformly dispersed;
   (B) adding N, N-dimethylalkyldiamine dropwise to the reaction solution, and raising temperature to 58-65 degrees C after dropwise addition is completed for 4-8 hours;
   (C) adding propargylamine dropwise to the reaction solution, gradually raising the temperature to 80-95 degrees C, and continuing the reaction for 4-8 hours;
   (D) adding a sodium hydroxide solution to the reaction solution, and continuing the reaction for 0.5 to 1 hour; and
   (E) removing solvent from the reaction solution, and purifying product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The ratio of cyanuric chloride and deionized water is 1: (8-20). The ratio of sodium hydroxide to cyanuric chloride is 3: 1.

PN CN113737218-A
TI Copper-based graphene aerogel composite catalyst used for gas diffusion electrode in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises graphene aerogel and coral-shaped nano-cuprous oxide rods and nano-copper rods loaded on the graphene aerogel.
AU CUI X
   YU S
   LI C
   WANG J
AE SINOPEC ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2021E6505S
AB    NOVELTY - Copper-based graphene aerogel composite catalyst comprises graphene aerogel and coral-shaped nano-cuprous oxide rods and nano-copper rods loaded on the graphene aerogel, where the diameters of the nano cuprous oxide rods and the nano copper rods are both 40-60 nm.
   USE - The copper-based graphene aerogel composite catalyst is used for gas diffusion electrode in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The copper-based graphene aerogel composite catalyst has excellent catalytic activity and selectivity, high porosity, fast electronic transmission rate, and improved catalytic reaction efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a method for preparing the copper-based graphene aerogel composite catalyst, which growing coral-like nano-cuprous oxide rods and nano-copper rods on the graphene aerogel by solvothermal method;
   (2) a gas diffusion electrode comprising a gas diffusion electrode body and the copper-based graphene aerogel composite catalyst, where the copper-based graphene aerogel composite catalyst is attached to the surface of the gas diffusion electrode body, the gas diffusion electrode body is carbon paper, carbon cloth or carbon nanotube sponge, the size of the gas diffusion electrode body is 0.5 cmx0.5 cm-3 cmx3 cm, and the loading amount of the copper-based graphene aerogel composite catalyst on the surface of the gas diffusion electrode body is 0.5-5 mg/cm2;
   (3) a method for preparing the gas diffusion electrode, which involves coating the dispersion of the copper-based graphene aerogel composite catalyst on the surface of the gas diffusion electrode body, where the preparation of the dispersion liquid of the copper-based graphene aerogel composite catalyst involves dispersing the copper-based graphene aerogel composite catalyst in an alcohol aqueous solution, adding Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution and ultrasonicating to obtain a catalyst dispersion liquid, the alcohol in the alcohol aqueous solution comprises isopropanol and ethanol, the volume ratio of isopropanol to ethanol is 1:10-10:1, the volume ratio of alcohol to water is 1:10-10:1, the volume ratio of Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution to water is 1:1-1:10, and the mass concentration of Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution is 5-10 wt.%, coating the obtained dispersion liquid on the gas diffusion electrode body, and drying under an infrared lamp to obtain the gas diffusion electrode; and
   (4) a method for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, which involves using the gas diffusion electrode as a cathode for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, preferably, using a double-chamber electrolytic cell, applying an external voltage, using an aqueous potassium bicarbonate solution as the electrolyte solution, using an H-type double-chamber electrolytic cell, Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) proton exchange membrane in the middle, and using the gas diffusion electrode supported by the copper-based graphene aerogel composite catalyst as the working electrode, where the platinum mesh electrode is the counter electrode, the silver/silver chloride electrode is the reference electrode to form a three-electrode system, where the anode and cathode electrolyte solution is 0.1-0.5 mol/l potassium bicarbonate solution, continuously feeding the H-type double-chamber electrolytic cell with carbon dioxide gas during electrocatalysis, feeding carbon dioxide gas to the electrolyte for 30-60 minutes before the reaction is carried out.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the solution system, using ethylene glycol to reduce copper salt, and coral-like nano-cuprous oxide rods and nano-copper rods grown on the graphene aerogel. The method involves dispersing copper salt and graphene aerogel in ethylene glycol respectively to obtain a copper salt precursor solution and a graphene aerogel dispersion, mixing the copper salt precursor solution and the graphene aerogel dispersion, and using a one-step solvothermal reaction to prepare the copper-based graphene aerogel composite catalyst. The method involves dripping the graphene aerogel dispersion to the copper salt precursor solution and stirring to obtain a mixed solution, and placing the mixed solution in a reactor to carry out a solvothermal reaction. The copper salt precursor solution is prepared by dissolving the copper salt in ethylene glycol, stirring and heating to obtain the copper salt precursor solution, where the copper salt is copper nitrate trihydrate, the concentration of copper nitrate trihydrate in the copper salt precursor solution is 0.005-0.05 mol/l, and the heating is carried out at 80-140degrees Celsius for 0.5-2 hours. The graphene aerogel dispersion is prepared by dispersing the graphene aerogel in ethylene glycol, and ultrasonically treating to obtain a uniform suspension. The preparation method of the graphene aerogel involves ultrasonically dispersing graphene oxide in an aqueous solution, freeze-drying, and reducing to obtain the graphene aerogel, where the graphene aerogel is ground into powder, and dispersed in ethylene glycol, the concentration of graphene aerogel in the graphene aerogel dispersion liquid is 1-10 g/l, and the ultrasonic treatment is carried out for 0.5-2 hours. The method further involves performing suction filtration, washing, drying and grinding after the crude product obtained by the solvothermal reaction is naturally cooled, where the washing solution used for washing comprises absolute ethanol and isopropanol, and the drying is carried out by vacuum-drying at 30-60degrees Celsius.Preferred Conditions: The stirring time is 0.5-2 hours. The solvothermal reaction is carried out at 140-180degrees Celsius for 6-24 hours.Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of copper salt and graphene aerogel in the mixed solution is (1-10):1.

PT P
PN CN113620320-A
TI Preparation of high-purity lithium carbonate for battery, involves hydrogenating crude lithium carbonate slurry, subjecting hydrogenation liquid to thermal decomposition reaction, centrifuging to obtain wet lithium carbonate, carbonizing, drying and pulverizing.
AU YUAN L
   QIU Y
   YUAN S
   LI X
   ZHU Q
   ZHONG L
AE YICHUN YINLI NEW ENERGY CO LTD (YICH-Non-standard)
GA 2021C97680
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high-purity lithium carbonate involves passing crude lithium carbonate slurry to carbonization device, passing in carbon dioxide for hydrogenation reaction, press filtering to obtain clarified hydrogenated liquid, pumping hydrogenation liquid into thermal decomposition kettle, heating for thermal decomposition reaction to obtain lithium carbonate slurry, centrifuging to obtain wet lithium carbonate, returning 80% of mother liquor after centrifugal separation to slurry preparation, recovering remaining primary thermal precipitation mother liquor by evaporation and precipitation, passing the wet lithium carbonate to the slurry tank of carbonization device (B), adding high-purity water and preparing slurry according to solid-liquid ratio of 1:20, repeating above steps to obtain high-purity wet lithium carbonate, drying wet lithium carbonate, pulverizing to D50 particle size of 3-7 mum, and packaging to obtain lithium carbonate with high purity of 99.9%.
   USE - Preparation of high-purity lithium carbonate for battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is environmentally-friendly and economical, and produces lithium carbonate with high purity of more than 99.9% and improved yield, and uses carbonization tower device to prolong carbon dioxide and lithium carbonate contact time to achieve the full effect of reaction, reduces the consumption of carbon dioxide due to absorption of the unreacted carbon dioxide completely through carbonization tower, and can save about 50% of the amount of carbon dioxide per ton and low external emission compared with existing single-kettle hydrogenation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of high-purity lithium carbonate by continuous carbonization method involves (s1) preparing crude lithium carbonate slurry with pure water according to solid-to-liquid ratio of 1:20 in the slurry tank of carbonization unit (A), (s2) passing crude lithium carbonate slurry to two-series carbonization tower device (A), passing in carbon dioxide for 3 hours for hydrogenation reaction, observing the turbidity of the mother liquor through the glass manhole of the carbonization tower to determine the end of the reaction, and reacting until mother liquor is clear, (s3) pumping hydrogenated reaction material into the plate and frame filter press for compression, and recovering the solid residue, filtering the mother liquor obtained after the press filtration through precision filter to obtain clarified hydrogenated liquid, (s4) pumping the clarified hydrogenation liquid into the thermal decomposition kettle, turning on the stirring, passing in steam, heating to 95degrees Celsius while stirring for 1 hour for thermal decomposition reaction to obtain lithium carbonate slurry, (s5) centrifuging lithium carbonate slurry in siphon centrifuge to obtain wet lithium carbonate, returning 80% of the mother liquor after centrifugal separation to step (s1) for slurry preparation, recovering remaining primary thermal precipitation mother liquor by evaporation and precipitation, (s6) passing the wet lithium carbonate to the slurry tank of carbonization device (B) through belt, adding high-purity water and preparing slurry according to the solid-liquid ratio of 1:20, repeating the above steps (s2)-(s5) to obtain high-purity wet lithium carbonate, and (s7) placing high-purity wet lithium carbonate on titanium plate dryer, drying, pulverizing to D50 particle size of 3-7 mum, and packaging to obtain lithium carbonate with high purity of 99.9%, and (s8) returning 80% of the mother liquor obtained after centrifugal separation in step (s6) to the slurry mixing tank of carbonization device (B) for slurry mixing.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system for preparing high-purity lithium carbonate by continuous carbonization method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The precision filter in step (s3) adopts PTFE microporous filter membrane with specification of 0.1 mum.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The slurry in steps (s5) and (s8) is prepared by mixing crude lithium carbonate or wet lithium carbonate and pure water and 80% mother liquor is 1:20.

AB    NOVELTY - A genetically modified hydrogenophilus                bacteria in which a lactate dehydrogenase gene                and/or a malate/lactate dehydrogenase gene being                introduced, and at least one lactate utilization                enzymes on the genome being disrupted, are                new.
   USE - The genetically modified hydrogenophilus                bacteria are useful for producing lactic acid                (claimed) which is used for producing polylactic                acid i.e. a biodegradable plastic, and organic                substances used as food, feed or fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogenophilus bacteria have high growth                rate, and carbonic acid fixation ability compared                to plants and photosynthetic bacteria. The                hydrogenophilus bacteria also have excellent carbon                dioxide fixing ability which suppress increase of                carbon dioxide by reducing carbon dioxide emission                and fixing emitted carbon dioxide. The                hydrogenophilus bacteria convert carbon dioxide                into valuable resource thus reduces global warming                and difficulty of securing food, feed and                fuel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A genetically modified hydrogenophilus                bacteria in which a lactate dehydrogenase gene                and/or a malate/lactate dehydrogenase gene being                introduced, and at least one lactate utilization                enzymes on the genome being disrupted, is new. The                lactate utilization enzymes are chosen from (a1) a                DNA having a 759 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 1)                fully defined in the specification, (a2) a AB    NOVELTY - Multi-compartment unit culture system                comprises a multi-compartment unit automatic                culture system (1), an isolation culture unit (2)                and a control computer. An electrical side cabinet                (3) is provided on the outer side of the                multi-compartment unit automatic culture system.                The multi-compartment unit automatic culture system                is provided with multiple isolated culture units.                The rear side of the isolation culture unit is                connected with a gas supply pipe (4) and a power                supply line (5). A water channel, a carbon dioxide                channel and a hydrogen peroxide channel are                provided inside the isolation culture unit. An                exhaust passage is provided outside the isolation                culture unit near the gas supply pipe. The exhaust                passage is provided with an exhaust valve near the                outside of the isolation culture unit. The control                computer is arranged in the electrical side                cabinet. The driving module is connected with a                valve control module and a temperature control                power electronic module.
   USE - The system is useful for good manufacturing                practices (GMP) compliant automated                production.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: improves the fault tolerance of                the system, which has large number of completely                identical repeating units and improves the safety                of the sample by enabling the quick transfer of the                sample to the idle culture unit, when some culture                unit is invalid during the culture process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Multi-compartment unit culture system                comprises a multi-compartment unit automatic                culture system (1), an isolation culture unit (2)                and a control computer. An electrical side cabinet                (3) is provided on the outer side of the                multi-compartment unit automatic culture system.                The multi-compartment unit automatic culture system                is provided with multiple isolated culture units.                The rear side of the isolation culture unit is                connected with a gas supply pipe (4) and a power                supply line (5). The base surface of the isolation                culture unit is provided with an automatic hatch.                The automatic hatch opening mechanism is connected                to the lower end of the automatic hatch. A top                heating unit and a bottom heating unit are provided                on the upper and lower ends of the isolation                culture unit. An automatic slide rail is provided                inside the isolation culture unit. The upper end of                the automatic slide rail is provided with a culture                container. A water channel, a carbon dioxide                channel and a hydrogen peroxide channel are                provided inside the isolation culture unit near the                gas supply pipeline. The water channel, the carbon                dioxide channel and the hydrogen peroxide channel                are provided with a water valve, a carbon dioxide                valve and an hydrogen peroxide valve outside the                isolation culture unit. A temperature sensor, a                humidity sensor, a water concentration sensor and a                carbon dioxide concentration sensor are arranged                near the upper end of the water channel inside the                isolation culture unit. An exhaust passage is                provided outside the isolation culture unit near                the gas supply pipe. The exhaust passage is                provided with an exhaust valve near the outside of                the isolation culture unit. The control computer is                arranged in the electrical side cabinet. The                control computer is provided with an input/output                (I/O) acquisition module and a driving module. The                driving module is connected with a valve control                module and a temperature control power electronic                module.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the multi-compartment unit culture system for                GMP compliant automated production.

TI Air conditioner fresh air supply system for            building, has air conditioner fresh air unit including            air processor, carbon dioxide detecting unit, building            space number measuring unit and indoor and outdoor air            temperature measuring unit.

GA 2022047859
AB    NOVELTY - The system comprises controller and air                conditioner fresh air unit(s), which is                respectively used for providing fresh air to the                predetermined area in the building. Each air                conditioner fresh air unit comprises a fresh air                pipe, air return pipe (1), air processor (10), and                blast pipe. The first end of the air return pipe is                connected with the inner space of the building. The                first end of the fresh air pipe is connected with                the atmosphere. The second end of the blast pipe is                connected with the inner space of the building. The                fresh air pipe is provided with a fresh air                adjusting valve. The air return pipe is provided                with an air return adjusting valve. The air                processor is located on the fresh air pipe and the                air return pipe. The air processor is provided with                a blower. Each air conditioner fresh air unit is                further provided with a carbon dioxide detecting                unit, building space number measuring unit and                indoor and outdoor air temperature measuring                unit.
   USE - The air conditioner fresh air supply system is                for building, preferably public use building. Uses                include but are not limited to station hall,                hospital, exhibition hall and gymnasium.
   ADVANTAGE - By monitoring the carbon dioxide content                and/or number of air in each area in real-time, the                controller controls the corresponding air                conditioner fresh air unit, so that each air                conditioning unit conveying the fresh air quantity                can be matched with the new air demand in the area                in the real time, so as to meet the requirement of                the national standard, but also can reduce the                energy consumption of the air conditioning system                and improve the energy utilization                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The system comprises controller and air                conditioner fresh air unit(s), which is                respectively used for providing fresh air to the                predetermined area in the building. Each air                conditioner fresh air unit comprises a fresh air                pipe, air return pipe (1), air processor (10), and                blast pipe. The first end of the air return pipe is                connected with the inner space of the building. The                first end of the fresh air pipe is connected with                the atmosphere. The second end of the blast pipe is                connected with the inner space of the building. The                fresh air pipe is provided with a fresh air                adjusting valve. The air return pipe is provided                with an air return adjusting valve. The air                processor is located on the fresh air pipe and the                air return pipe. The air processor is provided with                a blower. Each air conditioner fresh air unit is                further provided with a carbon dioxide detecting                unit, building space number measuring unit and                indoor and outdoor air temperature measuring unit.                The carbon dioxide detecting unit, building space                number measuring unit, indoor and outdoor air                temperature measuring unit, blower, fresh air                adjusting valve, and air return adjusting valve are                connected with the controller, which can be based                on the air conditioner fresh air unit of carbon                dioxide detecting unit and/or building inner space                number measuring unit and/or indoor and outdoor air                temperature measuring unit of data control                corresponding to the blower and/or fresh air                adjusting valve and/or air adjusting valve.0

TI Preparing immobilized magnetase of            1,3-dioleoyl-2-palmitoyl triglyceride grease in            supercritical state comprises e.g. wrapping magnetic            nanoparticles by adhering cross-linking agent, adding            palmitic acid, glycerol, and reacting.
AU YU D
   YANG F
   CHEN J
   YU C
   TANG H
   GAO J
   WANG Y
   LI Z
   WANG L
   JIANG L
AE UNIV NORTHEAST AGRIC (UNEA-C)
GA 202085759X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing immobilized magnetase of                1,3-dioleoyl-2-palmitoyl triglyceride grease in                supercritical state comprises e.g. (i) wrapping                magnetic nanoparticles by adhesion of cross-linking                agent, adhering esterification synthetase and                Sn-1,3 position-specific lipase to magnetic carrier                to obtain immobilized esterified synthetic lipase                and immobilized Sn-1,3 position-specific lipase,                respectively, (ii) adding palmitic acid into                supercritical extractor, adding glycerol into                palmitic acid, adding 3-8% of palmitic acid moles                of immobilized esterified synthetic lipase, ,                pumping liquid carbon dioxide gradually, reacting,                collecting reaction product, and (iii) adding                palmitic acid triglyceride into supercritical                extractor, adding oleic acid into palmitic acid                triglyceride, adding 2.5-6.5% of palmitic acid                triglyceride moles of immobilized Sn-1,3                position-specific lipase, pumping liquid carbon                dioxide, reacting, collecting                1,3-dioleoyl-2-palmitoyl triglyceride crude oil                product, and distilling.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing immobilized                magnetase of 1,3-dioleoyl-2-palmitoyl triglyceride                grease in supercritical state.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: achieves the purpose of improving                synthesis efficiency and increasing                1,3-dioleoyl-2-palmitoyl triglyceride                content.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing immobilized magnetase of                1,3-dioleoyl-2-palmitoyl triglyceride grease in                supercritical state comprises (i) wrapping the                magnetic nanoparticles using nano-scale                ferrosoferric oxide particles as magnetic matrix,                and edible polysaccharide as carrier by the                adhesion of cross-linking agent to obtain magnetic                carrier, then adhering esterification synthetase                and Sn-1,3 position-specific lipase to magnetic                carrier under action of crosslinking agent to                obtain immobilized esterified synthetic lipase and                immobilized Sn-1,3 position-specific lipase                respectively, (ii) adding 1 mole palmitic acid into                supercritical extractor, then adding glycerol into                palmitic acid in a molar ratio of 1.1-2:1, adding                3-8% of palmitic acid moles of immobilized                esterified synthetic lipase, tightening the sealing                cap of extractor to keep the constant temperature                of extractor at 42-60 degrees C, pumping liquid                carbon dioxide gradually to maintain the constant                pressure of extractor at 24-32 MPa, controlling the                pressure of separator to constant at 2-5 MPa, and                reacting for 4-6 hours, stopping pumping liquid                carbon dioxide, opening the separator valve to                collect the reaction product until the pressure in                separator and extractor is zero, where the                collected product is high-quality palmitic acid                triglyceride, and (iii) adding 1 mole palmitic acid                triglyceride into supercritical extractor, adding                oleic acid into palmitic acid triglyceride in a                molar ratio of 1.1-1.8:1, then adding 2.5-6.5% of                palmitic acid triglyceride moles of immobilized                Sn-1,3 position-specific lipase, tightening the                sealing cap of extractor, keeping the extractor at                constant temperature of 42-60 degrees C, and                gradually pumping liquid carbon dioxide,                maintaining the extractor constant pressure at                24-32 MPa, controlling the constant pressure of                separator at 2-5 MPa, and reacting for 4-6 hours,                stopping the pumping of liquid carbon dioxide,                opening the separator valve to collect reaction                product until the pressure in separator and                extractor is zero to crude oil product of                1,3-dioleoyl-2-palmitoyl triglyceride structure                which is collected product, then placing the                reaction product in vacuum, controlling the                temperature at 115-135 degrees C, and performing                molecular distillation to remove free oleic acid                and palmitic acid in the reaction system to obtain                1,3-dioleoyl-2-palmitoyl triglyceride grease with                content of 65-85%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The edible                  polysaccharide includes sodium alginate,                  dialdehyde starch,and/or carboxymethyl chitosan;                  and the crosslinking agent includes                  glutaraldehyde, polydopamine and/or                  3,4-dihydroxybenzaldehyde.

TI Preparing a highly transparent fluorescent tracer            gas hydrate test material for visualization by            supergravity comprises using solid materials,            assembling autoclave, injecting water-soluble            fluorescent agent, controlling and sealing.

   ZHU B
   CHEN Y
   LIN W
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202056397P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a highly transparent fluorescent                tracer gas hydrate test material for visualization                by supergravity comprises (1) using solid                materials, (2) assembling and sealing the                autoclave, leaving only the top channel and the                bottom channel, (3) injecting water-soluble                fluorescent agent into deionized water at a                specific ratio, evacuating the prepared solution to                prepare air-free deionized water containing                fluorescent agent, (4) injecting the defluorinated                deionized water, (5) closing the bottom channel,                (6) controlling the autoclave to maintain a                predetermined temperature and injecting methane gas                into the autoclave from the top channel until the                predetermined pressure, (7) methane gas injecting                under the condition that the pressure in the                autoclave, (8) sealing the top channel and bottom                channel to maintain constant temperature and                constant volume, (9) constant temperature and                pressure regulation and repeating.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a highly                transparent fluorescent tracer gas hydrate test                material for visualization by supergravity.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: is quick; has high quality, good                transparency and speckle tracing required for the                supergravity visualization test.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a highly transparent fluorescent                tracer gas hydrate test material for visualization                by supergravity comprises (1) using solid materials                in the autoclave to prepare the solid frame                particles deposits required for natural gas hydrate                and laying the permeable stones on top of the solid                frame particles deposits, placing pressure block on                the permeable stone, (2) assembling and sealing the                autoclave, leaving only the top channel and the                bottom channel, injecting carbon dioxide gas into                the autoclave from the bottom channel under the                action of extrusion, air between the solid                framework particles will gradually move upward and                be discharging from the top channel until the air                in the autoclave completely replaced by carbon                dioxide, (3) injecting water-soluble fluorescent                agent into deionized water at a specific ratio,                evacuating the prepared solution to prepare                air-free deionized water containing fluorescent                agent, (4) injecting the defluorinated deionized                water containing fluorescent agent into the                autoclave from the bottom channel under the                squeezing effect of deionized deionized water                containing fluorescent agent, excess carbon dioxide                gas will flow from the top channel discharged and                small amount of carbon dioxide gas will be                dissolved in deionized water until it reaches the                predetermined liquid level, (5) closing the bottom                channel and using the vacuum device to exhaust the                internal gas of the autoclave from the top channel                until the dissolved carbon dioxide in the deionized                water and the residual carbon dioxide in the                autoclave, accumulation of solid framework                particles reaches near saturation, (6) controlling                the autoclave to maintain a predetermined                temperature and injecting methane gas into the                autoclave from the top channel until the                predetermined pressure, (7) methane gas injecting                under the condition that the pressure in the                autoclave, micro-nano bubble generator is used to                inject into the solid framework particles near                saturation in the autoclave from the bottom channel                methane refinement bubbles, which will adhere to                the surface of solid frame particles, (8) sealing                the top channel and bottom channel to maintain                constant temperature and constant volume in the                autoclave, methane gas near the solid skeleton                particles will react with deionized water in the                solid natural gas hydrate is generated in the pores                of the skeleton particles, and the fluorescent                agent dissolved in deionized water is also retained                inside the natural gas hydrate, the test material                composed of natural gas hydrate and transparent                solid skeleton particles has high transparency                characteristics, (9) constant temperature and                pressure regulation and repeating until the                generated natural gas hydrate material with high                transparency characteristics reaches the amount                required for the supergravity visualization test                and finally adjust the upper gas layer in the                autoclave after the reaction pressure to a                predetermined pressure and stable for hours.

PN WO2019145169-A1; AU2019212000-A1; CA3084310-A1; IN202047036072-A; CN111836679-A; EP3743203-A1; BR112020014556-A2; US2021060519-A1; RU2771242-C2; RU2020127640-A
TI Melamine-urea plant used in combined production of            melamine and urea contains melamine plant to produce            stream containing melamine and stream of off-gas            containing ammonia and carbon dioxide, starting from            urea, and off-gas-fed urea plant.
AU SCOTTO A
   FUMAGALLI M
AE CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE CO LTD (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
GA 201967556T
AB    NOVELTY - Melamine-urea plant comprises: a melamine                plant for the production of melamine starting from                urea, obtaining a product stream containing                melamine and a stream of off-gas containing ammonia                and carbon dioxide in gaseous form; and a                off-gas-fed urea plant dedicated to the synthesis                of urea starting from the stream of off-gas, where                the off-gas-fed urea plant is apt to produce urea                starting from ammonia and carbon dioxide, and                comprises at least a high-pressure synthesis                section running at a synthesis pressure and a                recovery section running at a recovery pressure                lower than the synthesis pressure. The stream of                off-gas provides at least 90% of ammonia and carbon                dioxide supply of the off-gas-fed urea plant.
   USE - The melamine-urea plant is used in the field                of combined production of melamine and urea.
   ADVANTAGE - The melamine-urea plant: allows the use, as                raw material, of urea (in solid form or as an                aqueous solution) coming from the outside and not                produced in loco, e.g. solid urea supplied through                special transportation means; allows no worsening                in the performances of the urea synthesis plant due                to the recovery of the off-gas of the melamine                plant; does not require any type of revamping or                action because the off-gas are recovered in the                specially provided plant, in the event of                construction of a melamine plant supplied with urea                coming from an existing plant; is not required to                interrupt the urea production of the main plant for                the operations of integration with the melamine                plant, since the urea synthesis plant dedicated to                the off-gas recovery is constructed together with                the melamine plant; does not disturb the main urea                plant by any downtime or malfunctioning of the                melamine plant which may alter the quantity and/or                composition of the off-gas by-products; and                utilizes urea coming from plants which are not in                the vicinity of the melamine plant as raw                material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) revamping (m1) the urea plant, comprising                (a) adding a melamine synthesis section to the urea                plant, where at least part of the urea produced in                the existing urea plant is intended to supply the                melamine synthesis section, (b) adding a second                urea plant which is an off-gas-fed urea plant for                the production of urea starting from the melamine                off-gas withdrawn from the melamine plant, where a                stream of off-gas extracted from the melamine plant                provides at least 90% of ammonia and carbon dioxide                supply of the second urea plant, and the second                urea plant comprises at least a high-pressure                synthesis section running at a synthesis pressure                and a recovery section running at a recovery                pressure lower than the synthesis pressure;                and
   (2) revamping (m2) the urea/melamine plant                comprising the melamine plant and a tied-in urea                plant, comprising (a1) adding a second urea plant                specifically dedicated to the synthesis of urea                from the off-gas coming from the melamine plant,                where a stream of off-gas extracted from the                melamine plant provides at least 90% of ammonia and                carbon dioxide supply of the second urea plant, and                the second urea plant comprises at least a                high-pressure synthesis section running at a                synthesis pressure and a recovery section running                at a recovery pressure lower than the synthesis                pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The stream of off-gas                  provides the sole supply of ammonia and carbon                  dioxide of the off-gas-fed urea plant. All or                  part of the urea produced by the off-gas-fed urea                  plant supplies the melamine plant. The                  off-gas-fed urea plant further includes a                  concentration section arranged to receive a urea                  solution effluent from the recovery section and                  to produce a concentrated solution or a urea melt                  containing at least 95% urea. The off-gas-fed                  urea plant further includes a finishing section                  to produce solid urea. The melamine-urea plant                  comprises a first urea plant, which is a main                  urea plant and comprises at least a high-pressure                  synthesis section running at a synthesis pressure                  and a recovery section running at a recovery                  pressure lower than the synthesis pressure, a                  second urea plant, which is the off-gas-fed urea                  plant, where the urea supply of the melamine                  plant is produced at least in part by the main                  urea plant. The urea supply of the melamine plant                  is produced in part by the main urea plant and in                  part by the second off-gas-fed urea plant. A part                  of the urea produced by the second urea plant is                  exported from the plant. The added urea plant or                  the second urea plant is suitable to produce a                  urea product which is any of: an aqueous urea                  solution comprising at least 65 wt.% urea; and a                  molten urea comprising at least 95% urea, a solid                  urea product.

PN CN109666522-A; CN209636187-U
TI Biological natural gas high efficient purification system comprises e.g. washing tower set to deoxygenation and denitrification tower through compressor, hydration reactor I pressurized by gas booster II and stripper condensed by condenser.
AU ZHANG X
   LI J
   WANG Y
   WANG J
   ZHEN X
   WANG C
AE UNIV LANZHOU TECHNOLOGY (UYLZ-C)
   UNIV LANZHOU TECHNOLOGY (UYLZ-C)
GA 201939956C
AB    NOVELTY - Biological natural gas high efficient purification system comprises a washing tower (2) is connected to a gas source (1). The washing tower is connected to a deoxygenation and denitrification tower (5) through a compressor (3). The outlet gas of the deoxygenation denitrification tower is pressurized by a gas booster I and enters a hydration reactor I through a valve. The outlet gas of the hydration reactor I is secondarily pressurized by a gas booster II and enters a hydration reactor II through a valve. The gas is treated again by the hydration reactor II, enters a gas storage device through a valve. A washing liquid circulation recovery device includes a flash tank, a stripper, the flash tank is connected to the scrubber through a valve (4). The outlet vapor of the stripper is condensed by the condenser and then passed to the wash liquor recovery tank.
   USE - Used as biological natural gas high efficient purification system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: ensures separation and removal of impurity gases e.g. hydrogen sulfide, nitrogen and oxygen, which improves the purification efficiency of biogas, achieves temperature conditions required for the gas hydration reaction process by a constant temperature air bath; controls the temperature conditions of the hydration reaction process and degree of sub-cooling; achieves rapid production of carbon dioxide gas hydrates; and improves the purification efficiency of biological natural gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for biological natural gas high efficient purifying comprising (i) passing bio-natural gas to the scrubber through the gas source, removing of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide gas from biological natural gas, compressing the outlet gas in the scrubber by the compressor to obtain compressed biological natural gas, and passing to the deoxygenation and denitrification tower for removing nitrogen and a small amount of oxygen in the biological natural gas, (ii) passing the outlet gas of the deoxygenation and denitrification column through the gas booster I, pressurizing, passing to hydration reactor I through the valve and producing carbon dioxide hydrate to remove carbon dioxide gas, (iii) passing the outlet gas of the hydration reactor I through a gas booster II, pressurizing, passing to hydration reactor II through the valve, removing carbon dioxide gas from biological natural gas by hydration reaction to generate carbon dioxide hydrate, passing the biological natural gas to the gas storage device through the valve, and realizing the high-efficiency purification of the biological natural gas through multi-stage treatment, (iv) absorbing the impurity gas in the washing tower enters the flash tank through the valve by washing liquid to obtain a purified rich liquid, introducing the washing liquid into the stripper for removing the impurity gas in the washing liquid and condensing through the condenser to obtain a washing and recovering liquid, passing washing and recovering liquid to the washing tower for recycling, (v) passing the natural gas through the washing tower through steps (i), (ii), (iii), and (iv), (vi) absorbing impurity by washing liquid gas passes through the flash tank, the stripper, the condenser and to obtain the washing and recovering liquid, realizing the recycling of the washing liquid, and further improving the economy of the biological natural gas purification system and (vii) according to the composition of the bio-natural gas, the multi-stage treatment of the above steps can realize the high-efficiency purification of the bio-natural gas to obtain high-purity methane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the biological natural gas high efficient purification system.
   Gas source (1)
   Washing tower (2)
   Compressor (3)
   Valve (4)
   Deoxygenation and denitrification tower (5)

PN CN110344076-A; CN110344076-B
TI Porphyrin-based organic skeleton supporting copper            cobalt sulfide heterojunction-reductionCO2 method, by            performing super cleaning reaction process and            controlling adsorption sites of central copper atom of            porphyrin-based organic framework.
AU CHENG J
   LIU J
   YANG W
   CEN K
   ZHOU J
   WANG Z
   ZHANG Y
   ZHOU Z
   HE Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201989123M
AB    NOVELTY - A porphyrin-based organic skeleton supporting                copper cobalt sulfide heterojunction-reductionCO2                method, comprises taking graphitic carbon nitride                (g-C3N4) into absolute ethyl alcohol, stirring and                ultrasonic treatment, pouring copper oxide/iron                oxide (CuO/Fe2O3) catalyst, stirring for to form                uniform suspending solution, transferring                suspension solution into stainless steel                high-pressure kettle, and heating to obtain                CuO/Fe2O3.g-C3N4 catalyst; taking CuO/Fe2O3.g-C3N4                catalyst, deionized water-Nafion solution and                performing ultrasonic processing, mixing and                coating on carbon cloth, placing in vacuum drying                box, and drying to obtain anode electrode;                addinghydrochloric acid aqueous solution, adding                potassium carbonate solution into cathode chamber,                putting carbon dioxide into cathode cavity in                double-illumination reduction reaction, performing                super cleaning reaction process, and controlling                adsorption sites of central copper atom of the                porphyrin-based organic framework.
   USE - Porphyrin-based organic skeleton supporting                copper cobalt sulfide heterojunction-reduction                carbon dioxide method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves carbon conversion                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Porphyrin-based organic skeleton supporting                copper cobalt sulfide heterojunction-reductionCO2                method, comprises (A) taking 4mL anhydrous ethanol                solution containing 0.5 mg/mL copper                porphyrin-based organic frame worknano-sheet, 4mL                thioacetamide aqueous solution with 1 mg/mL mass                concentration, 150 mu L Co(NO3)26H2O aqueous                solution with 0.5 mg/mL mass concentration, and                8.0m L absolute ethanol, and performing ultrasonic                treatment to obtain uniformly mixed solution; (B)                adding mixed solution in the reaction kettle,                heating to 75 degrees C, storing for 2-6 hours to                obtain red porphyrin-based organic skeleton                supporting copper catalyst and cobalt dual metal                sulfide catalyst; (C) taking 10 mg catalyst,                de-ionized water, and 100-200 mu l Nafion solution,                uniformly mixing, coating pore diameter of 200                meshes on the dimensional foam copper, placing in                60 degrees C vacuum drying box and dryng for 12                hours,and obtaining cathode electrode; (D) taking                10.7 g iron nitrate nonahydrate and 10.7 g copper                nitrate hexahydrate, pouring into 30 mL deionized                water to prepare mixed solution, and stirring for                30 minutes; (E) taking mixed solution, heating for                30 minutes in water bath at 90 degrees C, until                water is completely evaporated to obtain solid,                placing solid in mortar to grind, putting into                crucible, and placing in he muffle furnace at                constant temperature of 550 degrees C for 5 hours                to obtain copper oxide/iron oxide catalyst; (F)                taking 20.3 g graphitic carbon nitride (g-C3N4)                into 25 mL absolute ethyl alcohol, stirring and                ultrasonic treatment for 0.5 hour pouring 8 g                copper oxide/iron oxide (CuO/Fe2O3) catalyst,                continuously stirring for 24 hours to form uniform                suspending solution, transferring suspension                solution into stainless steel high-pressure kettle,                heating for 4 hours at 150 degrees C, naturally                cooling and filtering, drying at 60 degrees C to                obtain CuO/Fe2O3-g-C3N4 catalyst; (G) taking 20 mg                CuO/Fe2O3.g-C3N4 catalyst, 200 mu l deionized                water-Nafion solution and performing ultrasonic                processing 200 L, uniformly mixing, uniformly                coating on carbon cloth, placing in 60 degrees C,                vacuum drying box, and drying for 12 hours to                obtain anode electrode; (H) using double H-shaped                cavity of illumination reactor quartz glass,                installing reactor at one side of cathode electrode                and the other side of mounting anode electrode,                separating H-shaped cavity in middle of reactor by                cation exchange membrane, using quartz glass                sealing; (I) into anode chamber of the double                illumination reactor, adding 0.5M hydrochloric acid                aqueous solution, adding 0.5M potassium carbonate                solution into cathode chamber, putting carbon                dioxide into cathode cavity in double-illumination                reduction reaction, performing super cleaning                reaction process, and controlling adsorption sites                of central copper atom of the porphyrin-based                organic framework; and (J) reacting carbon dioxide                double-illumination reduction for 4 hours, and                collecting the liquid product in the cathode                cavity.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic diagram                illustrating a Porphyrin-based organic skeleton                supporting copper cobalt sulfide                heterojunction-reduction carbon dioxide method                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The transverse size of                  copper-containing porphyrin-based organic                  framework nano-sheet is 1.2 mu m with average                  thickness of 5.3 nm. The mass concentration of                  Nafion solution is 10%.
