PT P
PN US2018272312-A1; US11135564-B2
TI Porous co-polymeric composition comprising gels and/or aerogels obtained by mixing polyisocyanate and polycarboxylic acid at room temperature in solvent used e.g. to separate carbon dioxide gas from mixtures of gases.
AU LEVENTIS N
   SOTIRIOU-LEVENTIS C
   SAEED M A
AE UNIV MISSOURI (UMOR-C)
   ASPEN AEROGELS INC (ASPE-Non-standard)
GA 201875461T
AB    NOVELTY - Porous co-polymeric composition (C1) (Ia) comprising gels and/or aerogels, and obtained by mixing together at room temperature in a solvent at least one polyisocyanate compound and at least one polycarboxylic acid compound is claimed, where the polyisocyanate compound includes at least 2 isocyanate functional groups, and the polycarboxylic acid compound includes at least 3 carboxylic acid functional groups at least 2 of which are positioned relative to each other so as to be able to form an intramolecular anhydride.
   USE - The porous co-polymeric composition is useful in porous carbon composition for separating carbon dioxide gas from mixtures of gases and pre-combustion purification of fuel gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compositions possess a good balance of adsorption capacity for carbon dioxide, and selectivity towards other gases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Porous co-polymeric composition (C1) of formula (Ia) comprising gels and/or aerogels, and obtained by mixing together at room temperature in a solvent at least one polyisocyanate compound and at least one polycarboxylic acid compound is claimed, where the polyisocyanate compound includes at least 2 isocyanate functional groups, and the polycarboxylic acid compound includes at least 3 carboxylic acid functional groups at least 2 of which are positioned relative to each other so as to be able to form an intramolecular anhydride.
   G1 = 1-10C straight chain alkyl or branched alkyl or cycloalkyl, alkylaryl, aryl, heteroalkyl, heterocyclylalkyl, or heteroaryl (all optionally substituted);
   G2 = alkyl, cycloalkyl, heteroalkyl, heterocylcoalkyl, alkylaryl, cycloalkylaryl, alkylheteroaryl, cycloalkylheteroaryl, arene ring system, or heteroarene ring system (all optionally substituted);
   L1-L4 = at least one linkage comprising amide, imide, urea or carbamic-anhydride;
   w-z = 0-10; and
   p = 1-500.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the porous co-polymeric composition (C1), comprising the step of mixing together at room temperature in a suitable solvent at least one polyisocyanate compound of formula (II) and at least one polycarboxylic acid compound of formula (III) in such amounts so that the ratio of isocyanate functional groups to carboxylic acid functional groups is 1:1, where the suitable solvent is a single solvent or its mixtures, including ether-type solvents, ester-type solvents, amide-type solvents, ketone-type solvents and/or nitriletype solvents;
   (2) a porous, nanoparticulate co-polymeric composition (C2) of formula (Ib), comprising gels and/or aerogels, and obtained by the reaction step of heating a sample of a co-polymeric composition (Ia) at 100-200 degrees C, resulting in the loss of carbon dioxide;
   (3) a porous carbon composition (C3) comprising monolithic carbon gels and/or aerogels, and obtained by a process comprising the step of pyrolizing a sample of the co-polymeric composition (C1) or (C2) under an inert atmosphere at 700-1100 degrees C, for a sufficient length of time until a constant weight is achieved, the resultant porous carbon composition obtained from the pyrolysis step possesses a nanostructured form in which at least 70% of the surface area measured by the nitrogen gas sorption porosimetry method at 77 K is assigned to micropores, and the inert atmosphere is argon, nitrogen gas or helium;
   (4) a porous carbon composition (C4) comprising monolithic open-pore carbon gels and/or aerogels, and obtained by a process comprising the step of reactive etching of a sample of the composition (C3) via pyrolysis under an atmosphere of an etching gas at a temperature of 900-1100 degrees C for a period of 2-4 hours, the resultant porous carbon composition possesses a nanostructured form in which at least 60% of the surface area measured by the sodium sorption porosimetry method at 77 K is assigned to micropores;
   (5) separation (M1) of carbon dioxide gas from mixtures of gases, comprising passing the mixtures of gases through the carbon composition of either (C3) or (C4); and
   (6) pre-combustion purification (M2) of fuel gas, comprising passing the fuel gas through the carbon composition of either (C3) or (C4).
   q = 2-6;
   r = 3-8;
   L5-L7 = at least one linkage comprising amide, imide, urea or carbamic-anhydride; and
   -u = 0-10.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The etching gas is carbon dioxide (preferred), oxygen gas, air, or water. In method (M1), the mixture of gases comprises methane, hydrogen gas and/or nitrogen gas, in addition to carbon dioxide. In method (M2), the pre-combustion fuel gas includes hydrogen gas in addition to carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The suitable solvent is a single solvent or its mixtures, including ether-type solvents, ester-type solvents, amide-type solvents, ketone-type solvents and/or nitrile-type solvents. Preferred Process: The step of heating is carried out at 150 degrees C. The pyrolysis step is carried out at 800 degrees C for a period of 5 hours. The pyrolysis step is carried out at 1000 degrees C for a period of 3 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The composition (C1) has triphenylmethane compound of formula (VI), where the isocyanate groups of the polyisocyanate compound (IV) are attached to their respective aryl rings at the 4-positions of the aryl rings. The composition (C2) has triphenylmethane compound of formula (VII).W1-W6 = amide, urea, imide or carbamicanhydride;n = 2-500;R1-R4 = at least one substituents comprising alkyl, cycloalkyl, alkoxy, alkylthio, aryl, aryloxy, arylthio (all optionally substituted), H (preferred) or halo, nitro, or CN; andW7-W12 = amide, urea or imide.Preferred Components: The polyisocyanate compound is (II) and the polycarboxylic acid compound is (III). The polyisocyanate compound and the polycarboxylic acid compound are mixed together in such amounts so that the ratio of isocyanate functional groups to carboxylic acid functional groups is 1:1. The polyisocyanate compound is hexamethylene diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate, toluene diisocyanate, methylene diphenyl diisocyanate, cyanate compound of formula (OCN-Ar1-CH(Ar1-NCO)2) (IIa), (1,3,5)triazinane-2,4,6-trione cyanate compound of formula (IIb), 1,3,5-tris-(isocyanato-tetramethyl-cyclohexyl)-(1,3,5)triazinane-2,4,6-trione, cyanate compound of formula (OCN-R1a-NH-C(O)-N(-R1a-NCO)-C(O)-NH-R1a-NCO) (IIc). The polycarboxylic acid compound is e.g. tetrahydro-2,5-dioxa-cyclobutadicyclopentene-1,3,4,6-tetraone, cyclobutane-1,2,3,4-tetracarboxylic acid, tetrahydro-difuro(3,4-b;3',4'-d)furan-1,3,5,7-tetraone, tetrahydro-furan-2,3,4,5-tetracarboxylic acid, isochromeno(6,5,4-def)isochromene-1,3,6,8-tetraone, benzo(1,2-c;4,5-c')difuran-1,3,5,7-tetraone, benzene-1,2,4,5-tetracarboxylic acid, bicyclo(2.2.2)oct-7-ene-2,3,5,6-tetracarboxylic acid, naphthalene-1,4,5,8-tetracarboxylic acid, (5,5')biisobenzofuranyl-1,3,1',3'-tetraone, biphenyl-3,4,3',4'-tetracarboxylic acid, 2,5,8-trioxa-trindene-1,3,4,6,7,9-hexaone or benzene-1,2,3,4,5,6-hexacarboxylic acid.The polyisocyanate compound is isocyanate groups of compound of formula (IV) and the polycarboxylic acid compound is carboxylic acid compound of formula (V), where the (IV) are independently attached to their respective aryl rings at the 2-4-positions of the aryl rings.Ar1 = not defined; andR1a = 1-10C-alkyl or cycloalkyl or -(CH2)6-.
   EXAMPLE - Typical the porous co-polymeric composition comprised of: 0.19 g 1,2,4,5-benzenetetracarboxylic acid (pyromellitic acid); 1.359 g Desmodur RE (RTM: Solution of triphenylmethane-4,4',4''-triisocyanate in ethyl acetate); 0.367 g tris(4-isocyanatophenyl)-methane; and 20.7 g tetrahydrofuran.

AB    NOVELTY - Carbonated beverage charging device comprises e.g. a base, where the pedestal is provided with a movable mounting plate, and the movable mounting plate left and right two sides of the telescopic hydraulic rod is connected with the output end. The bottom part of the telescopic hydraulic rod is connected with the base is fixed on the base of middle position is fixed with a cylinder. The water barrel is set on the lower end of pipe. The water pipe is provided with a water drain valve. The water containing cylinder is further provided with a semiconductor refrigerating sheet, semiconductor refrigerating plate of the refrigerating surface is connected with the water barrel, so as to the water containing cylinder to cool having good solubility to improve the carbon dioxide in the water, the water containing cylinder upper port is provided with a sealing ring. The movable mounting plate is fixed to the upper end of the water containing cylinder corresponding with buffer tank.
   USE - Used as carbonated beverage charging device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is simple and reasonable structure, convenient transportation, good seismic performance, improves carbon dioxide solubility in water by cooling and pressurizing the inside of the water cylinder, and achieves internal circulation stirring and mixing of the gas which contributes to rapid dissolution of carbon dioxide, has processing efficiency of device and improves and the utility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbonated beverage charging device comprises a base (1), where the pedestal is provided with a movable mounting plate (29), and the movable mounting plate left and right two sides of the telescopic hydraulic rod (30) is connected with the output end. The movable mounting plate is fixed to the upper end of the water containing cylinder corresponding with buffer tank, the upper end of the buffer tank is provided with a driving motor, stirring tube, the output end of the driving motor is provided with a stirring tube, the mixing pipe is set with the gas guide cavity, the buffer tank is provided with an air inlet hole, stirring tube outside of the lower end of the stirring pipe is provided with a spiral blade close to the spiral blade is provided with a spraying pipe, the mixing pipe is further provided with a pressure cover under the action of the telescopic hydraulic cylinder, the movable mounting plate to move downwards so that the pressing cover and the water cylinder tightly so as to realize sealing effect of the buffer case at the right side of the air inlet end is connected with the high-pressure air pump the air inlet end of the high-pressure air pump is provided with an air inlet box, an air inlet is provided with a first air inlet pipe and the second air inlet pipe, a second air inlet pipe and the right end is connected with the carbon dioxide gas supply tank, a second air inlet pipe is provided with a second electromagnetic valve, the first air inlet pipe is provided with a first electromagnetic valve, oil return hole, air inlet pipe lower end of the air guide hose and the gland bush is connected with the pressing cover is further provided with a barometer.

   Base (1)
   Water pipe (7)

TI Predicting analyte concentrations via machine learning techniques involves receiving sensor data associated with sensor, utilizing portion of sensor data of respective sensor as input to machine learning network trained on cross-point observation indicating response curve location.
AU FORRESTER G C
   NICOLE L
AE METRE INC (METR-Non-standard)
GA 202251592L
AB    NOVELTY - Predicting analyte concentrations via machine learning techniques involves receiving sensor data associated with sensor; utilizing a portion of the sensor data of a respective sensor as input to a machine learning network trained on a cross-point observation indicating a response curve location at which response curves at the respective sensor intersect, the response curves corresponding to exposure of the respective sensor to multiple training samples of a same amount of an analyte concentration at a same particular temperature, where a variable amount of carbon dioxide concentrations are present across the multiple training samples; and receiving output from the machine learning network based in portion on exposure of the one sensor to a present sample having a given concentration of the analyte and a given concentration carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for predicting analyte concentrations via machine learning techniques.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a system which comprises processors, and a non-transitory computer-readable medium comprising sequences of instructions that, when executed by the processors, cause the system to perform operations comprises:
   i. receiving sensor data associated with sensor exposed to exhaled breath;
   ii. utilizing a portion of the sensor data of a respective sensor as input to a machine learning network trained on a cross-point observation indicating a response curve location at which response curves at the respective sensor intersect, the response curves corresponding to exposure of the respective sensor to multiple training samples of a same amount of analyte concentration at a same particular temperature, where a variable amount of carbon dioxide concentrations are present across the multiple training samples; and
   iii. receiving output from the machine learning network based in portion on a concentration of analyte and a concentration carbon dioxide present in the exhaled breath; and
   (2) a computer program product which comprises a non- transitory computer-readable medium having a computer- readable program code embodied to be executed by processors, the program code comprising instructions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The analyte comprises ethanol, isopropanol, methanol, methane, hydrogen or acetaldehyde. The receiving sensor data comprises receiving sample of exhaled breath at one or more electro-chemical cells that performs at least one of oxidization and reduction of the sample of exhaled breath to create a current that corresponds to the sample of exhaled breath; converting the current into a voltage signal; and utilizing the voltage of the signal to capture time series data based on the sample of exhaled breath, receiving the time series data at a microcontroller, the machine learning network accessible by the microcontroller. The analyte comprises ethanol, isopropanol, methanol, methane, hydrogen or acetaldehyde; defining the cross-point observation based in portion as an intersection of a first analyte response curve and a second analyte response curve; where the first analyte response curve at a given sensor is based on a first test sample that presents a first amount of analyte concentration at a first temperature with first amount of carbon dioxide concentration; and the second analyte response curve at the same given sensor is based on a different second test sample that presents the same first amount of analyte concentration at the same first temperature with a second amount of carbon dioxide concentration, the first amount of carbon dioxide concentration differing from the second amount of carbon dioxide concentration. The receiving output from the machine learning network comprises: wherein the present sample comprises exhaled breath; determining a status of the electro-chemical cell via the machine learning network; and receiving output identifying one or more algorithms to be applied to the electro-chemical cell to modify the status of the electro-chemical cell.

PN CN114130412-A
TI Preparing composite photocatalyst comprises peeling off layer to prepare titanium carbide nanosheets, and placing titanium carbide nanosheets in carbon dioxide atmosphere.
AU YI K
   LIANG H
   YI X
AE GUANGZHOU YOUXI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202238722R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite photocatalyst comprises peeling off the layer to prepare titanium carbide nanosheets, and placing the titanium carbide nanosheets in a carbon dioxide atmosphere for heat treatment to obtain a primary modified titanium carbide nanosheets. Cadmium nitrate and indium nitrate are weighed and dissolved in deionized water under mixing and stirring, added thioacetamide after mixing uniformly, added 0.1-1mol/L hydrochloric acid solution to adjust the pH to be acidic. The one-time modified titanium carbide nanosheets are dispersed and transferred into a high-pressure reaction kettle with a polytetrafluoroethylene lining for 8-12 hours under autogenous pressure of 160-180degrees Celsius. The reaction mixture is cooled to room temperature, filtered the precipitate, washed the precipitate with deionized water, and dried in vacuum to constant weight to obtain a secondary modified titanium carbide nanosheet.
   USE - Method for preparing composite photocatalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the composite photocatalyst in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with system enhanced whole process of electron generation, transfer and utilization, and improved photocatalytic efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing composite photocatalyst comprises peeling off the layer to prepare titanium carbide nanosheets, and placing the titanium carbide nanosheets in a carbon dioxide atmosphere for heat treatment to obtain a primary modified titanium carbide nanosheets. Cadmium nitrate and indium nitrate are weighed and dissolved in deionized water under mixing and stirring, added thioacetamide after mixing uniformly, added 0.1-1mol/L hydrochloric acid solution to adjust the pH to be acidic. The one-time modified titanium carbide nanosheets are dispersed and transferred into a high-pressure reaction kettle with a polytetrafluoroethylene lining for 8-12 hours under autogenous pressure of 160-180degrees Celsius. The reaction mixture is cooled to room temperature, filtered the precipitate, washed the precipitate with deionized water, and dried in vacuum to constant weight to obtain a secondary modified titanium carbide nanosheet. The mass ratio of the cadmium nitrate to the indium nitrate, the thioacetamide, and the primary modified titanium carbide nanosheets is 1:(2-2.2):(2.1-2.4):(0.7-0.8). The secondary modified titanium carbide nanosheets are placed in an inert atmosphere for heat treatment, then switched the atmosphere to a carbon dioxide atmosphere and cooled to obtain the composite photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The titanium carbide nanosheet is obtained respectively weighing titanium, aluminum and titanium carbide powder, taking absolute ethyl alcohol as a solvent, putting the powder into a ball mill, carrying out ball milling and mixing, drying after mixing, carrying out compression molding, carrying out heat preservation and calcination for 2-4 hours at 1300-1500degrees Celsius under argon protection atmosphere, and then crushing and grinding to obtain titanium-aluminum carbide powder. Lithium fluoride and concentrated hydrochloric acid are added into titanium-aluminum carbide powder under stirring for 36-72 hours, separated the precipitate, washed to be neutral, and dried to obtain the titanium carbide nanosheet. The porous carrier material is obtained by weighing the composite photocatalyst, dispersing the composite photocatalyst in a mixed solution of deionized water and ethanol, adding ammonia water to adjust the pH of the solution to be alkaline, adding a silane coupling agent and tetraethoxysilane under stirring for reaction for 10-12 hours for carrying out reduced pressure evaporation to remove the solvent, drying to obtain a composite product, adding the composite product into a mixed solution of sodium metaaluminate and sodium hydroxide under stirring at a low speed for reaction overnight, and transferring the mixture into a high-pressure reaction kettle with a polytetrafluoroethylene lining for carrying out heat preservation reaction for 18-24 hours. The obtained reaction mixture is cooled at room temperature, filtered out precipitates, washed the precipitates with deionized water, and carried out vacuum drying to constant weight to obtain the photocatalyst. The silane coupling agent is trimethoxy (3,3,3-trifluoropropyl) silane.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN113023714-A; CN113023714-B
TI Preparing self-propagating synthetic porous            graphene involves using magnesium powder and other            metal powders, mixing magnesium powder and other metal            powders, mixing to obtain mixed powder, placing mixed            powder in a crucible.
AU LI S
   WEI Z
   WANG C
   ZHAO Q
   HE M
   LI F
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 202178967H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing self-propagating synthetic porous                graphene involves using magnesium powder and other                metal powders as raw materials, mixing magnesium                powder and other metal powders in a molar ratio of                10:1-1:10 and mixing to obtain mixed powder,                placing the obtained mixed powder in a crucible,                and embedding the resistance wire in the mixed                powder, and then placing the crucible in a closed                container, passing carbon dioxide gas into the                crucible, and controlling the flow rate of carbon                dioxide gas to 20-500ml/minutes. The current of the                DC power supply is adjusted to make the resistance                wire heat, and the heated resistance wire is used                to ignite the mixed powder in the crucible. The                combustion reaction of the mixed powder is                completed, added dilute acid solution to the                resulting product to react, and the reaction time                is 10-48 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing self-propagating                synthetic porous graphene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high yield, product graphene                impurity is little, with great specific surface                area, in simple and cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing self-propagating synthetic porous                graphene involves using magnesium powder and other                metal powders as raw materials, mixing magnesium                powder and other metal powders in a molar ratio of                10:1-1:10 and mixing to obtain mixed powder,                placing the obtained mixed powder in a crucible,                and embedding the resistance wire in the mixed                powder, and then placing the crucible in a closed                container, passing carbon dioxide gas into the                crucible, and controlling the flow rate of carbon                dioxide gas to 20-500ml/minutes. The current of the                DC power supply is adjusted to make the resistance                wire heat, and the heated resistance wire is used                to ignite the mixed powder in the crucible. The                combustion reaction of the mixed powder is                completed, added dilute acid solution to the                resulting product to react, and the reaction time                is 10-48 hours, and the oxide is removed through                the reaction, filtered the reactant obtained to                collect the precipitate, and the obtained                precipitate is thoroughly washed, dried, and dried                to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The other metal powder                  is zinc powder, manganese powder, tin powder,                  copper powder and aluminum powder. The crucible                  is cast iron crucible, a quartz crucible, a                  corundum crucible, a graphite crucible and a                  silicon carbide crucible. The material used for                  the airtight container is any one of quartz,                  stainless steel, heat-resistant steel and silicon                  carbide. The resistance wire is tungsten wire,                  molybdenum wire, nickel-chromium wire and silicon                  carbide wire. Preferred Conditions: The average                  particle size of the magnesium powder and other                  metal powders is 1-1000 micrometer. The power of                  the DC power supply is 10-2000W and the magnitude                  of the current is 1-30A. The dilute acid solution                  is any one of dilute hydrochloric acid, dilute                  sulfuric acid, dilute nitric acid, and dilute                  phosphoric acid, and the concentration of the                  dilute acid solution is 1-5 mole/L. The molar                  ratio between the dilute acid solution and the                  raw mixed powder is 1:1-4:1. The deionized water                  is used for washing, and the pH is 6-7. The                  drying is heating drying or liquid nitrogen                  freeze drying.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

TI Termite killing function featured water conservancy construction slope protection structure, has slope body formed with assembling cavity, inducing cylinder fixed with carbon dioxide generator and connecting pipe fixed with shunt pipe.
AU CHEN Z
AE CHEN Z (CHEN-Individual)
GA 2021617822

A termite killing mechanism (200) is provided with a first cylinder (201) that is fixed on a top end of the assembling cavity. An inner wall of the first cylinder is provided with an annular guide block (202). A bottom end of the first cylinder is fixedly provided with a connecting shell (206) that is matched with the guide block. A guide component (300) is connected with bottom of the connecting shell. The guide component is fixed at bottom of the assembling cavity. A top end of a second cylinder (401) is fixedly provided with a supplementary component (405). A top right side of an inducing cylinder is fixed with an output end of a carbon dioxide generator (207). A right side of a connecting pipe (301) is provided with a shunt pipe (302). A hollow vertical pipe (304) and a hollow transverse pipe (303) are formed with a through hole.
   USE - Termite killing function featured water conservancy construction slope protection structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure fills air into the inducing cylinder by opening the carbon dioxide generator, radiates smell from a bait bag through a vent, emits gas to the slope body through the guide component forming a smell inducing net, makes termite in the slope body to enter into the shunt pipe through the hollow transverse pipe and the hollow vertical pipe, passes the termite into the connecting pipe and the cylinder for eliminating termites in the slope body, utilizes the guide component to improve slope protection strength and improves efficiency and reliability of inducing termite.

PT P
PN IN202021040554-A; IN388563-B
TI Acidic amorphous aluminosilicates solid acid used in pyrolytic degradation of plastic and conversion of carbon dioxide to dimethyl ether by hybrid bifunctional catalyst, comprises silicon and aluminum precursors hetero condensation product.
AU POLSHETTIWAR V
   MAITY A
AE TATA FUNDAMENTAL RES INST (TTTA-C)
   TATA INST FUNDAMENTAL RES (TTTA-C)
GA 202162756P
AB    NOVELTY - Acidic amorphous aluminosilicates (AAS) solid acid comprises hetero condensation product of silicon and aluminum precursors including selectively strong acid sites and nanosponze morphology with surface areas of 436-558 m2/g and pore volumes of 0.7-1.5 cm3/g.
   USE - The solid acid is useful for environmentally safe pyrolytic degradation of plastic and conversion of carbon dioxide to dimethyl ether involving the hybrid bifunctional catalyst (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The solid acid: ensures catalysis effect which has strong acidity like zeolites and textural properties; exhibits significant rates, scales; stabilities making process; and is economically competitive.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) synthesis (M1) of solid acid comprising hetero-condensation between silicon and aluminum precursors where rate of hydrolysis of the later is controlled to lesser than the same of the former for generating strongly acidic sites, in presence of porous template for desired high accessible surface area and nanosponge morphology;
   (2) environmentally safe pyrolytic degradation (M2) of plastic involving AAS comprising subjecting the plastic mass for degradation following pyrolysis involving the catalyst where cracking under influence of AAS is activated in stages involving initial kinetics where cracking occur predominantly due to the external surface acid sites and thereafter the degraded polymeric species diffuse into the pores of (AAS) for further degradation for enabling faster completion of pyrolysis at reduced temperature of 250-500 degrees C;
   (3) hybrid bi-functional catalyst comprising synergistic composistion of 0-80 wt.% of copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst and 20-90 wt.% AAS solid acid catalyst comprising hetero-condensation product of silicon and aluminum precursors including selectivly strong acid sites and nanosponze morphology with surface areas of 436-558 m2/g and pore volumes of 0.7-1.5 cm3/g; and
   (4) conversion (M3) of carbon dioxide to dimethyl ether involving the hybrid bifunctional catalyst comprising (a) initially converting carbon dioxide to methanol in the presence of copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst, followed by (b) selective involvement of the AAS solid acid catalyst for desired selective conversion of methanol to dimethyl ether involving selectively the acid sites in AAS solid acid catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the solid acid, the strong acid sites included zeolites type bridging silanol bond with the high mesoporosity like amorphous aluminosilicates; the silicon/aluminum precursor ratio is 0.1-50, preferably 1.3-9 for tunable acidity; the aluminum and silicon are uniformly distributed in the nanosponze texture; and the stronger acidity is based on zeolitic bridging silanols and accessible acid sites based on the high surface area and sponge-like morphology. The solid acid comprises: modified amorphous aluminosilicate having Al MAS NMR peak shapes different compared to amorphous aluminosilicates with the values of quadrupolar coupling constant (CQ) for Al (IV) of the AC-series lying between 12 and 13 MHz like zeolitic type Al (IV) sites which donot resemble conventional amorphous aluminosilicates; and (1H)-27Al HETCOR cross peak at (58-63, 2.8-3.0), as that of zeolite Bronsted acid sites. The solid acid has: tunable acidity based on the aluminum coordination number and presence of proximal silanol and its electronic environment; proton 1D spectra shifts similar with respect to conventional aluminosilicates as well as zeolitic bridging silanol bond similar to zeolites; distinct sharp peak at 2.8 ppm obtained of 1D-NMR similar to 1H(27Al) TRAPDOR experiments where the peak at 2.8 is attributed to H in close proximity less than or equal to 5 Angstrom ) to aluminum confirming the existence of zeolitic bridging silanol bond; two desorption peaks at approximately 400-560 degrees C which correspond to strongly bridging silanol sites; and synergistic combination of strong acidic sites and larger pore sizes favoring wide range of different catalytic processes selected from styrene oxide ring-opening, vesidryl synthesis, Friedel-Crafts alkylation, jasminaldehyde synthesis, and m-Xylene isomerization with better efficacy with respect to conventional zeolites and amorphous aluminosilicates with/without activation. The solid acid is recyclable up to 27th cycle and regeneratable involving simple oxygen treatment for use as catalyst/catalyst support. In the method (M1), the silicon precursor include silicon tetrachloride, tetraethyl orthosilicate (Si(OR)4), preferably tetraethyl orthosilicate; aluminum precursors include aluminum acetylacetonate or aluminum triacetate, preferably aluminum acetylacetonate; the porous template includes cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB), surfactant of formula (CH3(CH2)n-N(Me/Et/Pr)4 X), and N can also be replaced by pyridine (methyl, ethyl or propyl) and mixture, preferably CTAB; and solvent of the reaction include para, ortho or meta xylene, toluene, cyclohexane, benzene preferably p-xylene.R = Me, Et, Pr or Bu;n = 11-20; andX = Cl, Br or I.Preferred Method: The synthesis (M1) of AAS comprises: effective hetero-condensation between silicon and aluminum precursors in a bicontinuous microemulsion template made up of lamellar phases of CTAB and/or 1-pentanol; selectively tuning the acidity based on the selective silicon/aluminum ratio ranges of 0.1-50 where selectively of silicon and aluminum ratio is 1.3-9 provided for acidity in the level of 225 mu mol/g based on desired end utilities including; and (i) mixing CTAB and urea with water, (ii) adding freshly prepared solution of tetraethyl orthosilicate (TEOS) and aluminum precursor in p-xylene dropwise 1-30 minutes preferably 10 minutes and stirring for 10-60 minutes, preferably 30 minutes at room temperature (RT), (iii) optionally dropwise adding further 1-pentanol under stirring and the reaction mixture was further stirring for 10-60 minutes, preferably 30 minutes, (iv) refluxing and subsequent cooling to RT, (v) centrifuging the solid product followed by washing with ethanol, water and finally again washing with ethanol respectively, (vi) drying overnight at 50-120 degrees C, preferably 80 degrees C and finally removing the template by calcination affording the desired AASs. The method (M2) involved includes AAS with silicon/aluminum ratio of 1.3-9, preferably 1.9 and pyrolyzing at 250-500 degrees C, preferably 325 degrees C. The method (M2) comprises: recycling the involved AAS catalyst by burning the carbon deposits in the pores of AAS for reopening of the active acidic pore sites and pyrolyzing upto 27th cycles for desired catalyst activity; and degradation of plastic waste involving the selective involvement of AAS with select silicon/aluminum ratio and related provision of desired acidic sites provides for safe pyrolytic degradation of plastic under reduced temperate including selectively LDPE degradation in the reduced temperatre range of 250-500 degrees C, falcon tubes degradation in the reduced temperatre range of 250-500 degrees C carrier bags degradation in the reduced temperatre range of 250-500 degrees C and plastic bottles degradation in the reduced temperatre range of 250-500 degrees C. The method (M3) comprises carrying out catalysis based conversion involving a flow reactor with carbon dioxide and hydrogen ratio of 1:1-1:10, preferably 1:3 and 00 to 10000 ml/hour/g, preferably 3000 ml/hour/g GHSV at 100-500 degrees C, preferably 260 degrees C under 1-100 bar, preferably 30 bar pressure. The method (M3) comprises selectively controlling the conversion based on the selective combination of copper-zinc-aluminum and AAS with the highest dimethyl ether formation (~ 68%) involving ACasterisk-1.9 and AC-1.9, preferably ACasterisk-1.9, controlling the GHSV from 100-10000 ml/hour/g, preferably 1500 ml/hour/g, selectivity increasing dimethyl ether upto 79% which enable higher residence time, in turn more molecules of methanol to react with AAS to form more dimethyl ether.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN111778568-A
TI Automatic cleaning device for chemical fiber production, has solidification generator that is connected with first air duct and dry ice discharge pipe located in inner cavity of cleaning tank connected with spray top through connecting hose.
AU TAN B
AE GUIZHOU KEHOO ENTERPRISE MANAGEMENT GROUP CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2020A5832Q
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a cleaning tank (1) whose one end is provided with a warehouse door (2). The inside of the door can be pushed and pulled back and forth with a mounting seat (3) for clamping the spinneret. Other end side surface of the cleaning tank is respectively connected with a first air extraction pipe (4), a second air extraction pipe (5) and a dry ice discharge pipe (6). Other end of the first suction pipe is connected with a tail gas collection tank (8) through a first vacuum pump (7). Other end of the second suction pipe is connected with a second vacuum pump (9) for exhausting air. Other end of the dry ice discharge pipe is connected with a solidification generator (10) for converting carbon dioxide gas into dry ice. One side of the solidification generator is connected with a first air duct (11) for introducing carbon dioxide gas. The dry ice discharge pipe located in the inner cavity of the cleaning tank is connected with a spray top through a connecting hose.
   USE - Automatic cleaning device for chemical fiber production.
   ADVANTAGE - In the automatic cleaning device for chemical fiber production, when dry ice is used to clean the spinneret, the vacuuming effect of the second vacuum pump is used. The device can easily ensure that the inner cavity of the cleaning tank is in a vacuum state, and after the cleaning is completed, the dry ice will vaporize into carbon dioxide gas. The gas can be collected and stored by the first vacuum pump, and the carbon dioxide can be reused by connecting the tail gas collection tank and the inlet of the solidification generator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the automatic cleaning device for chemical fiber production.

   Warehouse door (2)
   Mounting seat (3)

   Dry ice discharge pipe (6)
   First vacuum pump (7)
   Tail gas collection tank (8)
   Second vacuum pump (9)
   Solidification generator (10)
   First air duct (11)

PN CN111662793-A; CN212357188-U
TI Automatic hop adding device has main pipeline whose inlet and outlet are connected with shut-off valve and exhaust valve, and pressure transmitter that is connected to hop storage tank and hop metering and adding tank.
AU MA K
   LI L
   YU Z
AE HEFEI OLOGY ENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 202092168X
AB    NOVELTY - The device has CIP cleaning pipeline (3), compressed air intake pipeline (4), carbon dioxide pipeline (5), main pipeline (6) and branch pipelines that are connected to the top of each hop storage tank (1) and the top of the hop metering and adding tank (2). The first pipeline and the second pipeline are connected to the bottom end of the hop metering and adding tank. The inlet of the main pipeline is connected with a shut-off valve, and the outlet of the main pipeline is connected with an exhaust valve. A pressure transmitter is connected to the hop storage tank and the hop metering and adding tank. The pressure transmitters, CIP valves, compressed air valves, carbon dioxide valves, shut-off valves, exhaust valve, top valve of each storage tank, drive motor of screw conveyor, storage tank discharge valve, weighing module, inlet and addition tank valve, addition tank discharge valve and inlet valve are connected to the main controller.
   USE - Automatic hop adding device.
   ADVANTAGE - The automatic hop adding device ensures that the hops are stored in a low-temperature and oxygen-isolated environment, automatically weighs and transmits materials, reduces labor, and improves the accuracy and precision of hop addition. The device realizes independent control of each hop storage tank, prevents the inter-colonization of carbon dioxide gas during feeding, and avoids the possibility of the entire group of feeding stagnation caused by the abnormality of a single hop storage tank. The main controller controls the driving motors of three shaftless screw conveyors through a frequency converter, by reducing the manufacturing cost of the automatic hop adding device. The operator conveniently views the data and better traces the production process analysis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for automatic batch addition of hops by automatic hop adding device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the structure of the automatic hop adding device.
   Hop storage tank (1)
   Hop metering and adding tank (2)
   CIP cleaning pipeline (3)
   Compressed air intake pipeline (4)
   Carbon dioxide pipeline (5)
   Main pipeline (6)

PT P
PN CN111534138-A
TI Preparing water-based paint antifouling additives involve dispersing the cage polysilsesquioxane derivative in an organic solvent to obtain a solution and using copper salt aqueous solution A, and sodium hydroxide aqueous solution B.
AU LI Z
AE QINGDAO YINGHAI PAINT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202081081E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing water-based paint antifouling additives involve dispersing the cage polysilsesquioxane derivative (I) in an organic solvent to obtain a solution with a mass concentration of 20-50%, using copper salt aqueous solution A, sodium hydroxide aqueous solution B, and glucose solution C, and placing carrier emulsion, copper salt aqueous solution A, sodium hydroxide aqueous solution B and glucose solution C in ratio 3: 1: 1: 1 in an autoclave. The supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is injected to 5-10 mega pascal into the autoclave, and then performed magnetic stirring while raising the temperature to 70-90 degrees C, and reacted for 30-50 minutes and cooled to room temperature. The supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is discharged, and the reaction product is washed, filtered, and dried to obtain the antifouling additive for waterborne coatings of cage polysilsesquioxane loaded with cuprous oxide.
   USE - Method for preparing water-based paint antifouling additives.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare water-based paint antifouling additives that utilizes the high diffusibility, strong solubility, excellent surface wettability and strong permeability of supercritical fluid, promotes the penetration of some copper ions into the cylindrical cage structure, and ensures the stability and durability of the anti-fouling additive.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing water-based paint antifouling additives involve dispersing the cage polysilsesquioxane derivative (I) in an organic solvent to obtain a solution with a mass concentration of 20-50%, using copper salt aqueous solution A, sodium hydroxide aqueous solution B, and glucose solution C, and placing carrier emulsion, copper salt aqueous solution A, sodium hydroxide aqueous solution B and glucose solution C in ratio 3: 1: 1: 1 in an autoclave. The supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is injected to 5-10 mega pascal into the autoclave, and then performed magnetic stirring while raising the temperature to 70-90 degrees C, and reacted for 30-50 minutes and cooled to room temperature. The supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is discharged, and the reaction product is washed, filtered, and dried to obtain the antifouling additive for waterborne coatings of cage polysilsesquioxane loaded with cuprous oxide.
   R=alkyl, amino, alkenyl, and aryl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is selected from aliphatic hydrocarbons, alicyclic hydrocarbons, aromatic hydrocarbons, alcohols, ketones, ethers, and alcohol ethers. The alicyclic hydrocarbon organic solvents include but are not limited to cyclohexane, cyclohexanone, toluene cyclohexanone. The aliphatic hydrocarbon organic solvents include but are not limited to pentane, hexane, and octane. The aromatic hydrocarbon organic solvents include but are not limited to benzene, toluene, and xylene. The alcohol organic solvents include but are not limited to methanol, ethanol, and isopropanol. The ketone organic solvent includes but is not limited to acetone, methyl methyl ethyl ketone, methyl isobutyl ketone. The ether organic solvents include, but are not limited to, ethyl ether, butyl ether, and propylene oxide. The alcohol ether organic solvents include but are not limited to ethylene glycol monomethyl ether, ethylene glycol monoethyl ether, and ethylene glycol monobutyl ether.

PN CN111440277-A
TI Material used for indoor building decoration applications, is prepared by reacting atomized sodium metaaluminate aqueous solution and carbon dioxide, modifying with composite surfactant, and reacting and grafting resultant on cinnamic acid.
AU YUE P
   FANG Y
   ZHAO Y
AE JIANGSU ARCHITECURAL TECHNOLOGY VOCATION (JICO-C)
GA 202072449S
AB    NOVELTY - An indoor building decorative material is prepared by reacting atomized sodium metaaluminate aqueous solution with carbon dioxide by carbon method, obtaining aluminum hydroxide aerosol, modifying with a composite surfactant, obtaining surfactant coated with aluminum hydroxide, and reacting resultant with cinnamic acid, then grafting on cinnamic acid, in which double bond in the cinnamic acid structure is subjected to addition polymerization reaction to obtain indoor building decoration material containing polystyrene as main chain, and side chain grafted with surfactant coated with aluminum hydroxide.
   USE - Material is used for indoor building decoration applications (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The indoor building decorative material has excellent flame retardance, with effect of filling low aluminum hydroxide having excellent flame retardance, by avoiding influence of aluminum hydroxide on mechanical properties of polystyrene.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the decorative material, which involves dissolving composite surfactant in an alcohol solvent to obtain a mixed solution, atomizing sodium metaaluminate aqueous solution in a preparation device, adding to the mixed solution, stirring under the condition of continuously feeding carbon dioxide gas and carrier gas for 1-2 hours, terminating passing of carbon dioxide gas and carrier gas, aging, filtering, washing, drying, obtaining material (A), adding the material (A), cinnamic acid and sodium hydride catalyst to solvent, refluxing at 70-150 degrees C for 4-6 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering to obtain material (B), adding potassium persulfate catalyst and benzoyl peroxide initiator, reacting at 70-100 degrees C for 6-8 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering, washing with water, obtaining material (C), adding to a molding machine for compression molding, and drying. The concentration of sodium metaaluminate aqueous solution is 80-110 g/L. The mass ratio of sodium metaaluminate and composite surfactant is 100:3-4.5. The ratio of the sodium hydride catalyst with respect to total amount of material (A) and cinnamic acid is 2-4:100. The ratio of cinnamic acid and material (A) is 1-1.2:1. The usage amount of potassium persulfate catalyst and benzoyl peroxide initiator are equal. The ratio of potassium persulfate catalyst or benzoyl peroxide initiator and material (B) is 0.02-0.04:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carrier gas is air. The flow ratio of carbon dioxide and carrier gas is 1.4-1.7:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composite surfactant includes sodium phosphate and cationic surfactant. The cationic surfactant is dimethylbenzyldodecylammonium bromide, benzyltriethylammonium chloride, octadecyltrimethylammonium chloride or benzalkonium chloride. The composite surfactant includes 6-9 %mass sodium phosphate. The alcohol solvent is ethanol, propanediol, butanol and/or polyvinyl alcohol. The solvent is N,N'-dimethylformamide or dimethyl sulfoxide.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity hydrogen using natural                gas, comprises (i) introducing hydrogen gas into                natural gas raw materials adding hydrogenation                catalyst, adding desulfurization agent and                dechlorination agent to obtain desulfurized natural                gas, (ii) passing desulfurized natural gas and                preheating steam into reformer tube, mixing                desulfurization natural gas and steam in ratio of                carbon and water, hydrocarbon-steam conversion                reaction occurs under the action of high                temperature catalyst, heating, taking natural gas                reformer tube and combusting recovered tail gas and                heating to provide energy for reaction, (iii)                passing reformed gas through the waste heat boiler                and introducing conversion furnace to convert                carbon monoxide and water into hydrogen and carbon                dioxide, condensing through heat exchange and                steam-water separation through the program control,                passing gas through the adsorption tower with three                specific adsorbents in sequence, boosting PSA by                pressure swing adsorption.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-purity                hydrogen using natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high-purity                hydrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high-purity hydrogen using natural                gas, comprises (i) introducing hydrogen gas into                natural gas raw materials adding hydrogenation                catalyst, heating up to 400-450 degrees C, adding                desulfurization agent and dechlorination agent to                obtain desulfurized natural gas, (ii) passing                desulfurized natural gas and preheating steam into                reformer tube, mixing desulfurization natural gas                and steam in ratio of carbon and water is 4-5:1,                hydrocarbon-steam conversion reaction occurs under                the action of high temperature catalyst, heating at                700-800 degrees C, taking natural gas reformer tube                and combusting recovered tail gas and heating to                provide energy for reaction; performing                pre-conversion contains hydrogen to obtain reformed                gas and performing conversion gas of carbon                monoxide and carbon dioxide, (iii) passing reformed                gas through the waste heat boiler at 300-400                degrees C and introducing conversion furnace to                convert carbon monoxide and water into hydrogen and                carbon dioxide, condensing through heat exchange                and steam-water separation through the program                control, passing gas through the adsorption tower                with three specific adsorbents in sequence,                boosting PSA by pressure swing adsorption, and                nitrogen, carbon monoxide, methane, carbon dioxide                extraction product hydrogen , decompression                analysis releases impurities and regenerates the                adsorbent.

The high-temperature catalyst                  for steam pre-conversion is a nickel-based                  catalyst Preferred Method: The natural gas is                  compressed to 2-3MPa, heating to 400-450 degrees                  C and desulfurizing and dechlorinating.

PN IN201721042916-A
TI Treating wastewater such as tannery/textile wastewater and sewage wastewater, involves making wastewater more acidic, adding compositions to adjust pH, adding hydrated lime, adding polyelectrolyte flocculant, and neutralizing treated water.
AU GATTANI R C
AE GATTANI R C (GATT-Individual)
GA 201963483Y
AB    NOVELTY - Process for treating wastewater, involves: (a) preparing compositions (I)-(IV); (b) making wastewater more acidic having a pH of 2; adding the compositions (I)-(IV) to adjust the pH to 4-7 by making all effluent carbonic acid salt which dissolves carbon dioxide to brake toxic gas in effluent; (c) adding hydrated lime to the wastewater to raise the pH of water from 4-4.5 to 8-9 which releases broken toxic gas in water to air; (d) adding a polyelectrolyte flocculant to the treated water as an aid in effecting solid liquid separation by settling out the sludge from the water; and (e) neutralizing the treated water for concomitantly addressing capture and sequestration of both carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide from atmosphere and also reducing other acidic gases from atmosphere to control air pollution and auto generate oxygen in treated water.
   USE - The process is useful for wastewater treatment, preferably for recovering metal from stainless steel effluent water (claimed), and treating tannery/textile wastewater, and sewage wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: solves/reduces world's biggest problem of climate change and global warming; is economical and environmentally-friendly; reduces the air pollution by absorbing carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide and reduces other acidic gases (green house effect) from atmosphere and controls air pollution from the atmosphere; removes the bad odor, color, opaque elements, organic and inorganic metals, and toxic gases, and reduces arsenic and fluoride; reduces the chemical oxygen demand (COD) and biochemical oxygen demand (BOD) within limit; and auto generates oxygen in treated water so that it reduces energy and does not require activated carbon plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process for recovering metal from stainless steel effluent water, comprising (a1) adding a calcium carbonate to the stainless steel effluent water to adjust the pH to 3 to discharge gypsum sludge, (b1) adding additional calcium carbonate to stainless steel effluent water to adjust the pH to 4-4.5 to make all effluent carbonic acid salt which dissolves carbon dioxide and brakes toxic gas in effluent to discharge iron oxide, and (c1) performing the above-mentioned steps (c)-(e).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition (I) comprises calcium carbonate, magnesium carbonate, magnesium sulfate, carbonic acid and ammonium sulfate/urea. The composition (II)/(IV) comprises calcium carbonate, magnesium carbonate, magnesium sulfate, carbonic acid, ammonium sulfate/urea and ferric/iron oxide. The composition (III) comprises calcium chloride, magnesium sulfate, carbonic acid and ferric salt/iron.

PN CN108310032-A
TI Extracting, enriching and purifying total flavonoids, by crushing callus of Saussurea, extracting with ethanol for thrice, combining extract, absorbing using macroporous adsorption resin column, collecting eluate and concentrating eluate.
AU CHU M
AE TONGXIANG BOAO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TONG-Non-standard)
GA 2018605548
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting, enriching and purifying total flavonoids from Saussurea involucrata, involves (a) drying callus of S.involucrata, crushing, placing in a supercritical extraction kettle comprising fluid such as carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide with ethanol (70-95%), pretreating, performing dynamic extraction, separating at 30-50 degrees C and 6-10 MPa for 60-150 minutes, filtering to obtain first extract of flavonoids and remaining residue, (b) adding ethanol to residue, performing microwave extraction and filtering to obtain second extract of flavonoids and residue, (c) adding ethanol to residue, performing ultrasonic treatment and filtering to obtain third extract, (d) mixing all the flavonoid extracts, concentrating the extract to an ethanol concentration of 0-15 vol.% and adsorbing using macroporous adsorption resin column and (e) sequentially eluting with ethanol, collecting 30-50% eluate and concentrating to a flavonoid solid content of 40-50%.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting, enriching and purifying total flavonoids from S.involucrata.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the utilization rate of non-destructive material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting, enriching and purifying total flavonoids from Saussurea involucrata, involves (a) drying callus of S.involucrata, crushing into 100-150 mesh, placing in a supercritical extraction kettle comprising supercritical fluid such as carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide with ethanol (70-95%), pretreating, performing dynamic extraction at 40-60 degrees C and 20-30 MPa, separating at 30-50 degrees C and 6-10 MPa for 60-150 minutes, filtering to obtain first extract of flavonoids and remaining residue, (b) adding ethanol to residue, performing microwave extraction at 200-500 nm and filtering to obtain second extract of flavonoids and residue, (c) adding ethanol to residue obtained in step (b) performing ultrasonic treatment and filtering to obtain third extract, (d) mixing all the flavonoid extracts, concentrating the extract to an ethanol concentration of 0-15 vol.% and adsorbing using macroporous adsorption resin column and (e) sequentially eluting with 0-95% ethanol, collecting 30-50% eluate and concentrating to obtain 40-50% total flavonoid solid content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amount of ethanol in steps (b) and (c) is 10-20 times of residue and its concentration is 70-95%. Preferred Method: In step (b), the microwave extraction is performed for 10-30 minutes. In step (d), the macroporous adsorption resin used is HPD700, HPD400 or HPD500 and the macroporous adsorption resin is sequentially eluted with 0-95% ethanol solution at a flow rate of 2-8 bed volumes (BV)/hour. The step (e) involves sequentially eluting macroporous adsorption resin with 0-95% ethanol and eluting with 30-50% ethanol, collecting 30-50% eluate and concentrating to obtain 40-50% total flavonoid solid content.

   LI Y
AE UNIV JINAN SHANDONG (UNJN-C)
GA 2018402727

   FALKOWSKI J M

GA 2020A59395
AB    NOVELTY - Mixed-metal organic framework having an                empirical or chemical formula of at least two                distinct metallic elements and bridged by a linker                comprises many disalicylate linkers, where each                linker comprises at least one aromatic ring, each                aromatic ring comprising a carboxylate functional                group and an alcohol functional group, the                carboxylate functional group and the alcohol                functional groups are adjacent to one another on                each aromatic ring, and each aromatic ring is                positioned at a greatest distance from the                other.
   USE - The framework is useful in the system, where                the system is useful: in a particle and the                adsorbent material; and for adsorbing carbon                dioxide from a carbon-dioxide containing stream and                tuning the position of a step of a type-V carbon                dioxide isotherm (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The framework: exhibits a type-V isotherm                profile for carbon dioxide; and provides the                adsorbent (adsorbent material) that can bridge the                two approaches through the incorporation of sites                that bind carbon dioxide by chemisorption onto                solid materials and eliminates the need for aqueous                solvents and may have significantly lower                regeneration costs as compared with conventional                amine scrubbers, yet maintain their exceptional                selectivity and high capacity for carbon dioxide at                low pressures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a mixed-metal mixed-organic framework                system comprising the mixed-metal organic framework                and a ligand;
   (2) synthesizing the framework, comprising (1)                contacting a solution comprising at least 2 sources                of at least 2 distinct metallic elements and an                organic linker capable of bridging metal cations,                and (2) heating the mixture to produce the                framework;
   (3) a particle comprising the system;
   (4) an adsorbent material comprising the                system;
   (5) adsorbing carbon dioxide from a                carbon-dioxide containing stream, comprising                contacting the stream with the adsorbent; and
   (6) tuning the position of a step of a type-V                carbon dioxide isotherm, comprising varying                quantity or a type of the metal ions of at least                two distinct metals of the framework or                system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metallic elements                  comprise calcium, magnesium, iron, chromium,                  vanadium, manganese, cobalt, nickel, zinc or                  copper.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The framework has                  substituted mixed-metal organic compounds of                  formula (M1xM2(2-x)(A)) (I). The framework                  provides an X-ray diffraction pattern that can be                  indexed to a hexagonal unit cell. The unit cell                  comprises space groups of 168-194. The framework                  further comprises a metal rod structure. The                  framework has a hexagonal pore oriented parallel                  to the metal rod structure. The framework                  displays a (3,5,7)-c msi net. The framework                  displays a (3,5,7)-c msg net. The framework                  expresses peak maxima in the X-ray diffraction                  pattern at 30 degrees C after drying at 250                  degrees C under nitrogen for 30 minutes at d(                  degrees A) as given in the table of the                  specification. The framework expresses peak                  maxima in the X-ray diffraction pattern at 30?pC                  after drying at 250 degrees C under nitrogen for                  30 minutes at d( degrees A) as given in the table                  of the specification. An a axis of the unit cell                  and a b axis of the unit cell are each greater                  than 18 A, and a c axis is greater than 6 A. The                  ligand comprises an amine, a diamine or a                  tetramine. The diamine is a cyclic diamine. The                  diamine comprises substituted diamines of formula                  (II)-(VI). The diamine ligand comprises one of                  dimethylethylenediamine (mmen) or                  2-(aminomethyl)piperidine (2-ampd). The tetramine                  comprises one of 3-4-3-tetramine (spermine) or                  2-2-2-tetramine. The system further comprises a                  ligand, where the ligand is a triamine. The                  ligand comprises 3-4-3-tetramine, 2-ampd,                  2-2-2-tetramine or mmen. The solution comprises                  an elemental metal or its salts of the metal in                  which the counter anion comprises a nitrate,                  acetate, carbonate, oxide, hydroxide, fluoride,                  chloride, bromide, iodide, phosphate, or                  acetylacetonate.M1, M2 = different metal cations, preferably                  Ca+2, Mg+2, Fe+2, Cr+2, V+2, Mn+2, Co+2, Ni+2,                  Zn+2 or Cu+2;A = disalicylate organic linker;Z = carbon, silicon, germanium, sulfur or                  selenium; andR1-R10 = H, halo, methyl, halo substituted                  methyl or OH.Preferred Method: The method comprises                  contacting the framework with a secondary ligand                  in a gas or liquid medium, where the ligand is an                  amine-containing molecule, a diamine, a triamine                  or a tetramine.
   EXAMPLE - 241.15 mg Manganese(II) chloride tetrahydrate,                312.65 mg magnesium nitrate hexahydrate and 267.15                mg 4,4'-dioxido-3,3'-biphenyl dicarboxylate were                combined in a 3-neck 250 ml round bottom flask with                stir bar. 49 ml Deoxygenated methanol and                N,N'-dimethylformamide (DMF) were transferred to                the metal and ligand-containing solution while                stirring. The solution was stirred for 20 minutes                to ensure all solids were thoroughly dissolved. The                reaction solution was split in 15 ml aliquots and                transferred into 23 ml Teflon (RTM: PTFE)-lined                Parr reactors. All reactors were sealed and heated                at 120 degrees C for 96 hours under static                conditions. Upon cooling naturally to ambient                temperature, the mother liquor was removed by                decantation, and the solid was washed three times                over 24 hours with DMF, then three times over 24                hours with methanol to obtain 40 mg mixed-metal                framework MOF-274 of M1xM2(2-x)(DOBPDC) and the                methanol was removed by slow centrifugation.

TI Integrated process for carbon dioxide capture,            sequestration, and utilization by contacting            carbon-dioxide-containing gas stream with aqueous            slurry, acid-treating magnesium depleted solid residue,            and precipitating magnesium carbonate.
AU BENHELAL E
   BRENT G F
   KENNEDY E M
   RAYSON M S
   STOCKENHUBER M
   BENKHELAL E
   KENNEDI E M
   REISON M S
   STOKENKHUBER M
AE MINERAL CARBONATION INT PTY LTD (MINE-Non-standard)
   MINERAL CARBONATION INT PTY LTD (MINE-Non-standard)
   MINERAL CARBONATION INT PTY LTD (MINE-Non-standard)
   MINERAL CARBONATION INT PTY LTD (MINE-Non-standard)
GA 201970452T
AB    NOVELTY - An integrated process for carbon dioxide (CO2)                capture, sequestration, and utilization involves                (a) providing aqueous slurry comprising aqueous                solution and particulate solid comprising activated                magnesium silicate mineral; (b) in a dissolution                stage, contacting CO2-containing gas stream with                the aqueous slurry to dissolve magnesium from                activated magnesium silicate mineral to provide                magnesium ion enriched aqueous solution and                magnesium depleted solid residue; (c) recovering at                least a portion of magnesium depleted solid                residue; (d) in a separate acid treatment stage,                reacting recovered portion of magnesium depleted                solid residue with solution comprising mineral acid                or acid salt to further dissolve magnesium and                other metals and to provide an acid-treated solid                residue; (e) recovering acid-treated solid residue;                and (f) in a separate precipitation stage,                precipitating magnesium carbonate from magnesium                ion enriched aqueous solution.
   USE - An integrated process for carbon dioxide                capture, sequestration, and utilization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a process for permanent                and safe capture and sequestration of carbon                dioxide gas while producing products of economic                value. The method is particularly concerned with an                efficient and economically viable integrated                process for the chemical conversion of carbon                dioxide and magnesium silicate feedstocks to solid                carbonates and silicates, enabling a reduction in                the amount of carbon dioxide discharged to the                atmosphere while simultaneously producing products                of economic utility and value, in particular                magnesium carbonates and silica. The method may                provide significant improvements over previous                mineral carbonation processes, rendering the                overall process more energetically and economically                favorable than might have been expected and more                competitive than previous processes, particularly                with the production of valuable carbonate, metal                hydroxide, or metal oxide and silica products. The                method may provide a more favorable way of                converting carbon dioxide into stable magnesium                carbonates, enabling a reduction in the amount of                carbon dioxide discharged to the atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for reactor                system adapted to perform the integrated process                comprising dissolution vessel, precipitation                vessel, and acid treatment vessel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Magnesium ion enriched                  aqueous liquid is produced by dissolving                  activated magnesium silicate mineral in greater                  than or equal to 1 reactors fed by input                  CO2-containing gas stream near, at or above                  atmospheric pressure, and, (i) in a separate                  precipitation stage, inducing a pH shift in the                  liquid by the removal of dissolved CO2 through                  the application of a partial vacuum or                  sub-atmospheric pressure to the liquid to                  precipitate magnesium carbonate; or (ii) in                  greater than or equal to 2 separate precipitation                  stages, inducing a pH shift in the solution by                  the removal of dissolved CO2 through the                  application of a partial vacuum or                  sub-atmospheric pressure in greater than or equal                  to 1 of the precipitation stages to precipitate                  magnesium carbonate. Removed carbon dioxide is                  recycled back to the dissolution stage. Magnesium                  depleted solid residue from dissolution stage is                  subjected to wet magnetic separation process to                  extract an iron-rich portion. The pH of acid                  solution from the acid treatment stage is raised                  to 4-8.5 by adding base while stirring at 20-100                  degrees C, and precipitated silica is separated                  from the solution. Dissolved metal species are                  precipitated out of the solution by selectively                  adjusting the solution pH by addition of base to                  recover the metal species as metal hydroxides.                  Metal hydroxides are converted to corresponding                  metal oxides through thermal hydrolysis and/or                  thermal decomposition. The pH of acid solution                  from the acid treatment stage is lowered below 0                  by adding mineral acid, and precipitated silica                  is separated from the solution. Remaining acid                  solution after precipitation of silica is                  recycled to the initial acid treatment step.                  Silicate mineral is activated by grinding and/or                  heating. Preferred Components: CO2-containing gas                  stream comprises flue gas from hydrocarbon                  combustion process. Mineral acid or acid salt                  used in the acid treatment stage is selected from                  nitric acid (HNO3), sulfuric acid (H2SO4),                  hydrochloric acid (HCl), sodium hydrogen sulfate                  (NaHSO4), hydrobromic acid (HBr), hydrofluoric                  acid (HF), hydroiodic acid (HI), perchloric acid                  (HClO4), boric acid (H3BO3), and phosphoric acid                  (H3PO4). Preferred Conditions: Pressure in each                  dissolution stage is 100-20000 kPa, and                  temperature is 20-185 degrees C. Pressure in the                  acid treatment stage is 100-200 kPa, and                  temperature is 20-l20 degrees C. The pH in the                  acid treatment stage is 0-3. Temperature of acid                  solution having a pH below 0 is 20-80 degrees                  C.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN US2019160438-A1
TI Carbonated water dispensing apparatus has bracket that is provided with axis of rotation which is rotated to provide access to threaded aperture for mounting carbon dioxide tank.
AU KNOLL G
   ROOSMALEN K
AE NATURAL CHOICE CORP (NATU-Non-standard)
GA 201946782B
AB    NOVELTY - The carbonated water dispensing apparatus has a bracket (100) that is provided with a manifold (104) including a threaded aperture (102) for accepting a threaded head on a carbon dioxide tank (23) e.g. 60L tank. The bracket is provided with an axis of rotation that is rotated to provide access to the threaded aperture for mounting the carbon dioxide tank. The path of travel of the pressurized gas through the manifold from the carbon dioxide tank is transverse the axis of rotation of the bracket, when attached to the threaded aperture. The axis of rotation is formed from a first rotatable axle.
   USE - Carbonated water dispensing apparatus for dispensing carbonated water to pressurized carbon dioxide tank.
   ADVANTAGE - The water dispensing apparatus is provided such that the individual units, such as the separate heating and carbonating are easily accessible and modular, and can be removed and replaced when necessary is provided. Simple and easily accessible mechanism is provided to allow an end user to adjust back pressure and flow rate of carbonated water dispensed. Hot water expansion chambers in water dispensing apparatus are provided to effectively capture hot water that overflows from a hot water tank and provides effective draining when drawn or when the overflow condition ends. The expansion chamber is provided such that the hot water stream is continuous and smooth without turbulence caused by unwanted air. The expansion chamber is easily replaced in the event of scale buildup or failure. The air can be prevented from entering the outlet due to the air being blocked by the check balls respectively, thus preventing sputtering when the hot water is dispensed through tap.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for attaching carbon dioxide tank to carbonated water dispensing apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side front view of the carbon dioxide tank bracket connector and regulator connected to the carbon dioxide tank.
   Carbon dioxide tank (23)
   Bracket (100)
   Threaded aperture (102)
   Manifold (104)
   Enclosure (110)

PN CN108587682-A
TI Method for electric desalting of carbon dioxide-nitrogen enhanced crude oil, involves mixing of crude oil and water, followed by subjecting, mixing, recovering and discharging.
AU SUN Z
   ZHANG M
   WANG Z
   LIU Z
   LI Q
   ZHU L
   GONG Z
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM (UYPE-C)
GA 201879506N
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide-nitrogen enhanced crude oil electric desalting method involves mixing of crude oil and water, mixing and injecting into the first-stage electric desalination device for primary electric desalting, injecting carbon dioxide foam into the first-stage electric desalination device during the desalination process, the water droplets is fully coalesced and settled, recovering carbon dioxide gas, injecting the crude oil after the first-stage reaction into the secondary electric desalination device and injecting the crude oil in the secondary electric desalination device again with water, subjecting the crude oil and reinjected water to secondary static mixing, carrying out the secondary electric desalting, injecting the nitrogen foam into the secondary electric desalination device during desalination, the water droplets is fully coalesced and settled and recovering nitrogen gas, discharging the crude oil into the preliminary distillation column.
   USE - Method for electric desalting of carbon dioxide-nitrogen enhanced crude oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables electric desalting of carbon dioxide-nitrogen enhanced crude oil with improved water drop coalescence removing rate, effectively remove impurities in crude oil and cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide-nitrogen enhanced crude oil electric desalting method involves mixing of crude oil and water, mixing and injecting into the first-stage electric desalination device for primary electric desalting, and continuously injecting carbon dioxide foam into the first-stage electric desalination device during the desalination process, the water droplets is fully coalesced and settled and recovering the remaining carbon dioxide gas, injecting the crude oil after the first-stage reaction into the secondary electric desalination device and injecting the crude oil in the secondary electric desalination device again with water, subjecting the crude oil and the re-injected water to secondary static mixing, after thorough mixing, carrying out the secondary electric desalting, and injecting the nitrogen foam into the secondary electric desalination device during the desalination process, the water droplets is fully coalesced and settled and recovering the remaining nitrogen gas, discharging the crude oil into the preliminary distillation column after secondary electric desorption.

   ZHOU J
AE HUANENG GROUP TECHNOLOGY INNOVATION CENT (CHHN-C)
