AB    NOVELTY - The structure (100) has a bracket (101)                connected with a storage device (1000). A lifting                driving component drives an evaporator (200) under                a guide (103) of a guide piece. The evaporator                slides out of a shell (300) along a sliding                through-hole when the evaporator is necessary to                disassemble. A supporting piece (104) blocks the                guide through-hole and the sliding through-hole. A                top end of the shell of the storage device is                provided with the guide through-hole. The lifting                driving component is connected with the shell that                is fixed on the supporting piece.
   USE - Evaporator dismounting structure for use in a                carbon dioxide geological storage device (claimed),                for relieving greenhouse effect to control carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure drives the evaporator to enter                and exit the shell through the lifting driving                component so as to realize assembly and disassembly                of evaporator, thus avoiding manual dismounting                evaporator and reducing labor intensity of a                worker. The guide piece provides a guide to sliding                of the evaporation, so that the evaporation and the                storage device are avoided from collision. The                evaporator is slidably mounted on the shell, and                can slide out of the shell along the sliding                through hole under the guide of the guide piece,                thus preventing damage to the shell and evaporator.                The supporting piece is installed at the end of the                guiding piece extending to the guide through hole,                thus reducing the number of components of the                lifting driving component, and hence reducing the                manufacturing cost of the structure and simplifying                the manufacturing process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                carbon dioxide geological storage device.

TI Preparing composite material for capturing carbon            dioxide, involves dissolving polyatomic aldehyde and            nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compound in glacial            acetic acid, preparing into mixed solution, stirring            the mixed solution at room temperature under the            protection gas for a period of time.
AU ZHANG M
   WEI W
   FAN Y
   HU D
   WANG D
   SUN N
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
   SHANXI LUAN MINING GROUP CO LTD (SHLU-C)
GA 202256499X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite material involves                dissolving polyatomic aldehyde and                nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compound in                glacial acetic acid, preparing into mixed solution,                stirring the mixed solution at room temperature                under the protection gas for a period of time,                adding ferric trichloride and continuously stirring                to obtain the organic polymer precursor solution,                placing the organic polymer precursor solution in                the hydrothermal reaction kettle, heating and                reacting to obtain the organic polymer, after                cooling the organic polymer reaction liquid, using                water and organic solvent washing for multiple                times, placing in the vacuum dry box to obtain dry                organic polymer, adding divinylbenzene,                azodiisobutyronitrile into ethyl acetate                solution.
   USE - Method for preparing composite material for                capturing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material prepared has good                hydrophobic performance, for capturing the carbon                dioxide under the water vapour environment,                reducing the influence of water vapour in the                carbon dioxide capturing process in the flue                gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing composite material involves                dissolving polyatomic aldehyde and                nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compound in                glacial acetic acid, preparing into mixed solution,                stirring the mixed solution at room temperature                under the protection gas for a period of time,                adding ferric trichloride and continuously stirring                to obtain the organic polymer precursor solution,                placing the organic polymer precursor solution in                the hydrothermal reaction kettle, heating and                reacting to obtain the organic polymer, after                cooling the organic polymer reaction liquid, using                water and organic solvent washing for multiple                times, placing in the vacuum dry box to obtain dry                organic polymer, adding divinylbenzene,                azodiisobutyronitrile into ethyl acetate solution,                stirring at room temperature to obtain ethyl                acetate mixed solution, grinding the organic                polymer obtained, after grinding, placing in the                hydrothermal reaction kettle, adding the ethyl                acetate mixed solution obtained, heating and                reacting for a period of time, taking out and                cooling and dry to obtain the composite                material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a photograph of a camera of                the composite material prepared in Example 1 of the                present invention with water drops contact.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polyaldehyde is                  m-phthalic aldehyde, terephthalaldehyde or                  triformaldehyde, the nitrogen-containing                  heterocyclic compound is combination of pyrrole,                  pyridine, imidazole or quinoline. The organic                  solvent comprises methanol, ethanol, acetone,                  tetrahydrofuran or chloroform. The divinyl                  benzene is m-phenylene vinylene or ethylene                  diethylene. Preferred Conditions: The ratio of                  the mol number of the ferric trichloride to the                  volume of the glacial acetic acid is 0.01 to                  0.2mol/l.

AE SHAANXI GUOHUA JINJIE ENERGY CO LTD (SHGR-C)
   CHINA ENERGY GUOHUA BEIJING ELECTRIC POW (CHEN-Non-standard)

PN CN113522331-A
TI Carbon-based cobalt-palladium bimetallic nanocatalyst for use in photo-thermal catalytic carbon dioxide methanation, comprises core-shell type substrate with silicon dioxide nanospheres as core and carbon film as shell, and metal nanoparticles of cobalt and palladium loaded on carbon film.
AU HU J
   FU W
AE TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
GA 2021C38602
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-based cobalt-palladium bimetallic nanocatalyst comprises a core-shell type substrate with silicon dioxide nanospheres as a core and a carbon film as a shell, and metal nanoparticles of cobalt and palladium loaded on the carbon film.
   USE - Carbon-based cobalt-palladium bimetallic nanocatalyst for use in photo-thermal catalytic carbon dioxide methanation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The uniform and fine dispersion of the metal nanoparticles on the substrate is ensured. The particle size distribution is between 3.5-5.5 nm. The utilization rate of the metal atoms is improved. The reaction temperature of methanation is significantly reduced, and excellent catalytic performance is obtained at 220degreesC by using the plasma effect of metal palladium atoms and introducing light-assisted thermal catalysis. The methane yield is 207.84 mmol/g metal/hour. The selectivity is 92.57%. The carbon dioxide conversion rate in the sealed reactor reaches 44% for 6 hours.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: 1. a preparation method of the catalyst, which involves (S100) preparing silicon dioxide nanospheres, (S200) using aminosilane coupling agent to modify a surface of the silicon dioxide nanospheres, (S300) adding surface-modified silicon dioxide nanospheres obtained in the step (S200) to glucose solution, hydrothermally reacting, calcining the hydrothermally-reacted product to form a layer of porous carbon film outside the silicon dioxide nanospheres as carbon-doped silicon dioxide, (S400) adding carbon-doped silicon dioxide to water to obtain solution (I), dropping the precursor solution of cobalt and palladium into the solution (I) to obtain solution (II), and heating the solution (II) to a preset temperature until the water is evaporated, and (S500) calcining and reducing the product obtained in the step (S400), and reducing the precursors of the supported cobalt and palladium to nitride to obtain catalyst as nitrogen-cobalt-palladium of formula: N-Co_xPd_y/carbon-doped silicon dioxide, where x and y is the mass ratio of cobalt and palladium; and 2.application of the catalyst in photothermal catalytic carbon dioxide methanation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Properties: The particle size of the metal nanoparticles of cobalt and palladium is less than 10 nm, preferably 3.5-5.5 nm. The carbon film is distributed with micropores and mesopores, where the aperture of the micropores and mesopores is 0.6-4 nm. The particle diameter of the catalyst is 200-240 nm. Preferred Composition: The total loading amount of the two metal elements of cobalt and palladium is 7-9 wt.%. The mass ratio of cobalt and palladium is (1-6):(1-6), preferably 6:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The silicon dioxide nanospheres are prepared by (S101) dripping aqueous ammonia into the mixed solution of ethanol and deionized water, stirring for a predetermined time, dripping tetraethyl orthosilicate, and continuously reacting for a predetermined time after finishing dripping, (S102) dripping mixed solution of tetraethyl orthosilicate and octadecyltrimethoxysilane to the reaction system obtained in (S101), and leaving still for hydrolysis, (S103) centrifuging and separating the reaction system obtained in (S102), removing the lower layer precipitate, washing and drying, and (S104) calcining the dried product obtained in (S103). The step (S200) specifically involves dissolving silicon dioxide nanospheres obtained in (S100) in anhydrous methanol, dripping aminosilane coupling agent, stirring the suspension liquid at room temperature, centrifuging and collecting the solid, washing and drying. The calcination reduction in (S500) is performed by calcining the product obtained in (S400) in a tubular furnace under argon gas flow at 500degreesC for 90 minutes, naturally cooling to room temperature, calcining the sample at 450degreesC in an ammonia gas atmosphere for 2 hours, and naturally cooling to obtain catalyst. Preferred Components: The metal nanoparticles of cobalt and palladium are nitrides of cobalt and palladium. The precursors of cobalt and palladium are cobalt(II) nitrate hexahydrate and palladium(II) nitrate dehydrate, respectively. Preferred Conditions: In (S300), the temperature of the hydrothermal reaction is 180degreesC and the time is 3 hours, and the calcinations is performed at 900degreesC for 1.5 hours under the condition of argon gas flow. The preset temperature in (S400) is 80degreesC.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The aminosilane coupling agent in (S200) is N-(2-aminoethyl)-3-aminopropyl-trimethoxysilane, gamma-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane or gamma-aminopropyltriethoxysilane.

PN JP2021151986-A
TI Manufacture of carbonate ester used for e.g. gasoline additive, involves reacting carbon dioxide with alcohol comprising monohydric alcohol and dihydric alcohol in presence of solid catalyst containing cerium oxide, zirconium oxide, lanthanum oxide, gadolinium oxide and/or europium oxide and solvent.
AU NAKAO K
   SUZUKI K
   TAMURA M
   NAKAGAWA Y
   TOMISHIGE K
AE NIPPON STEEL CORP (YAWA-C)
GA 2021B27762
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of carbonate ester involves (i) reacting carbon dioxide with alcohol(s) comprising monohydric alcohol and dihydric alcohol, and having a boiling point of more than 100 degrees C in the presence of solid catalyst(s) comprising cerium oxide, zirconium oxide, lanthanum oxide, samarium oxide, praseodymium oxide, tin oxide, dysprosium oxide, gadolinium oxide and/or europium oxide and a solvent at a reaction temperature from 100 degrees C or more to less than boiling point of the alcohol and solvent to obtain carbonate ester and byproduct water, and (ii) removing byproduct water from a reaction zone by evaporation.
   USE - Manufacture of carbonate ester. Uses include but are not limited to gasoline additive for improving octane value, diesel fuel additive for reducing particles in exhaust gas, alkylating agent, carbonylating agent and solvent for synthesizing resin/organic compound, such as polycarbonate and polyurethane, pharmaceutical, pesticide, electrolyte solution of lithium battery, raw material for lubricating oil, and deoxidizing agent for rust prevention of boiler pipe.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient manufacture of carbonate ester under mild reaction pressure without using dehydrating agent or chemical wettable powder.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacturing apparatus of carbonate ester, which comprises a reactor (10) filled with the solid catalyst and the solvent, a cooling tower (11) for cooling unreacted carbon dioxide, unreacted alcohol, byproduct water and solvent discharged from the reactor, a distillation column (12) for separating unreacted carbon dioxide as a gaseous phase and unreacted alcohol, byproduct water and solvent as a liquid phase and distilling the liquid phase to obtain byproduct water as gaseous phase and unreacted alcohol and solvent as liquid phase, a cooling tower (13) for cooling carbonate ester, unreacted alcohol and solvent discharged as liquid phase from the reactor, and a distillation column (21) for distilling the liquid phase to obtain unreacted alcohol and solvent as gas phase and carbonate ester as liquid phase.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the manufacturing apparatus of carbonate ester. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Reactor (10)
   Cooling columns (11,13)
   Distillation columns (12,21)
   Separation columns (14,22)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The apparatus further comprises a separation column (14,22) for separating unreacted alcohol and solvent as liquid phase and carbonate ester as solid phase from the reactor, and a catalyst regeneration column for regenerating the solid catalyst and reusing the regenerated solid catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alcohol is 4-10C monohydric alcohol or 2-10C dihydric alcohol, preferably 4-6C dihydric alcohol. The solvent is ether solvent or hydrophobic solvent. Preferred Process: The evaporated byproduct water is removed from the reaction zone by airflow of carbon dioxide. Alternately, the manufacture of carbonate ester involves supplying the alcohol to the reactor filled with the solid catalyst and the solvent, reacting carbon dioxide and alcohol at the reaction temperature to obtain carbonate ester and byproduct water, cooling unreacted carbon dioxide, unreacted alcohol, byproduct water and solvent discharged from the reactor, separating unreacted carbon dioxide as a gaseous phase and unreacted alcohol, byproduct water and solvent as a liquid phase and distilling the liquid phase to obtain byproduct water as gaseous phase and unreacted alcohol and solvent as liquid phase, cooling carbonate ester, unreacted alcohol and solvent discharged as liquid phase from the reactor and distilling the liquid phase to obtain unreacted alcohol and solvent as gas phase and carbonate ester as liquid phase, cooling carbonate ester, unreacted alcohol and solvent discharged as liquid phase from the reactor and separating unreacted alcohol and solvent as liquid phase and carbonate ester as solid phase, reusing unreacted carbon dioxide separated in the cooling process, unreacted carbon dioxide separated in the distillation process, unreacted alcohol and solvent separated in the cooling process, and unreacted alcohol and solvent separated in the distillation process in the reaction, separating the solid catalyst discharged as solid phase with the liquid phase from the reactor, and regenerating the solid catalyst and reusing the regenerated solid catalyst.
   EXAMPLE - To a 200 mL circulation-type eggplant-shaped flask, 0.6 g cerium(IV) oxide as solid catalyst, 10 g 1,6-hexanediol as alcohol and 70 g toluene as solvent were charged. Carbon dioxide was introduced into the flask at a pressure of 0.1 MPa and a flow rate of 200 mL/minute. Carbon dioxide and 1,6-hexanediol were reacted at 110 degrees C for 12 hours. When the flask was air-cooled and cooled to room temperature, the reaction product was collected and analyzed by gas chromatography. As a result, 1.35 mmol linear carbonate ester dimer and 0.01 mmol linear carbonate trimmer were produced. The yield of linear carbonate ester was 3.18%.

PN CN113044869-A
TI Preparing pure nano-calcium carbonate with high dispersibility, by adding amino acid to material tank containing calcium hydroxide slurry, mixing, stimulating carbonized balls, carbonizing, heating, adding surface treatment agent for activation treatment, filtering, drying, crushing, and screening.
AU WANG R
AE WANG R (WANG-Individual)
GA 202178721C
AB    NOVELTY - Process for preparing pure nano-calcium carbonate with high dispersibility, involves (i) adding amino acid to a material tank containing calcium hydroxide slurry, stirring and uniformly mixing, (ii) placing uniformly distributed carbonized balls in material tank, stimulating carbonized balls with strong light, forcing to indirectly adhere to wall to release low-temperature carbon dioxide, and reacting carbon dioxide with calcium hydroxide slurry for carbonization, (iii) detecting pH value of slurry in material tank in real time, and when pH value reaches 7-8, and stopping the strong light stimulation to obtain a calcium carbonate cooked slurry, (iv) heating the cooked calcium carbonate slurry at 60-75 degrees C, adding a surface treatment agent for activation treatment, and filtering to obtain a filter residue, and (v) washing the filter residue, drying and dehydrating, crushing, refining and screening to prepare nano-calcium carbonate.
   USE - The process is used for preparing pure nano-calcium carbonate with high dispersibility.
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-calcium carbonate has high purity and excellent dispersibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for preparing pure nano-calcium carbonate with high dispersibility, involves (i) adding amino acid to a material tank containing calcium hydroxide slurry, where the weight ratio of amino acid and calcium hydroxide slurry is (0.2-2):100, stirring and uniformly mixing, (ii) placing a number of uniformly distributed carbonized balls in the material tank, stimulating the carbonized balls with strong light, forcing to indirectly adhere to the wall to release low-temperature carbon dioxide, and reacting the carbon dioxide with the calcium hydroxide slurry for carbonization, (iii) detecting pH value of the slurry in the material tank in real time, and when the pH value reaches 7-8, and stopping the strong light stimulation to obtain a calcium carbonate cooked slurry, (iv) heating the cooked calcium carbonate slurry at 60-75 degrees C, adding a surface treatment agent for activation treatment, and filtering to obtain a filter residue, and (v) washing the filter residue, drying and dehydrating, crushing, refining and screening to prepare nano-calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The material tank used in step (i) is made of a light-proof heat-insulating material, and the material tank is placed in a non-light environment. The carbonized ball in step (ii) comprises a light-weight thermal insulation ball shell and carbonization reaction capsules, where the carbonization reaction capsule is uniformly embedded on the outer surface of the light insulation spherical shell. The light-weight thermal insulation spherical shell is filled with dry ice. The carbonization reaction capsule comprises a carbon dioxide absorbing material layer, a waterproof and breathable membrane, a black carbon base layer and a combined thermal insulation layer from the outside to the inside in sequence. Preferred Composition: The surface treatment agent in step (iv) comprises a mixture of sodium resinate and cocount oil-based dihydroxyethyl amine gemini quaternary ammonium salt at a weight ratio of (3-7):(7-3). The weight ratio of the surface treatment agent and the calcium carbonate cooked slurry in step (iv) is (3-6):100. Preferred Method: The method involves heating the surface treatment agent at 80-95 degrees C, and mixing and stirring for more than 1 hour for activation treatment. Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the temperature of the slurry in the tank is controlled at 10-30 degrees C, and the carbonization reaction is carried out for 40-60 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred components: The amino acid used in step (I) comprises alanine, glycine, and/or glutamic acid.

PN EP3771901-A1; US2021032087-A1
TI Carbonation device for use in beverages, has second photodiode that generates second electrical signal dependent on radiation portion which is diffused by carbonated beverage along substantially diffusion direction.
AU TESSICINI F
   NORGIA M
   ANDREIS D
AE FLUID-O-TECH SRL (FLUI-Non-standard)
   FLUID-O-TECH SRL (FLUI-Non-standard)
GA 202112714J
AB    NOVELTY - The carbonation device (1) has a carbonation tank (3) that is adapted to receive a beverage to be carbonated and carbon dioxide so as to generate a carbonated beverage. A dispensing device (5) is in fluid communication with carbonation tank and adapted to dispense, through a duct (7). A first photodiode is placed on irradiation direction from a side opposite to radiation source with respect to duct and generating a first electrical signal dependent on the radiation portion which passes through carbonated beverage which flows along duct. A second photodiode is placed along a diffusion direction substantially orthogonal to irradiation direction and generating a second electrical signal dependent on the radiation portion which is diffused by carbonated beverage along substantially diffusion direction.
   USE - Carbonation device for use in beverages such as bottle.
   ADVANTAGE - The device consists of realising a carbonation device for beverages which allows to obtain information on the presence and the type of bubbles in the beverage, in order also to guarantee a stability and a repeatability of the recipe of the carbonated beverage, with reference to the desired carbonation degree. The carbon dioxide source comprises a pressure regulator electrically connected to the processing and control unit, which unit regulates the dispensing of carbon dioxide inside the tank through pressure regulator, on the basis of the first electrical signal and of the second electrical signal detected by the carbonation sensor. The carbonation device reacts to an undesired reduction of the carbonation degree by reducing the introduction of a not yet carbonated beverage into the tank. The device reads the carbonation degree of the beverage that is carbonated directly inside the bottle, and it also allows to provide the same carbonator device with a feedback on carbonation degree, so as to be able to regulate the subsequent carbon dioxide dispensing inside the beverage to be carbonated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a carbonation sensor; and
   (2) a method for measuring carbonation of carbonated beverage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a carbonation device for beverages.
   Carbonation device (1)
   Carbonation tank (3)
   Dispensing device (5)
   Duct (7)
   Carbonation sensor (100)

PN CN110144322-A
TI Rapidly separating and culturing placental mesenchymal stem cells to harvest frozen comprises separating tissue, carrying out tissue block preparation, inoculation bottle laying and carrying out primary cell observation and liquid exchange.
AU XIANG S
   XU C
   WANG Q
   MA H
AE HUNAN NANHUA AMSPRING BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 201974578R
AB    NOVELTY - Rapidly separating and culturing placental mesenchymal stem cells to harvest frozen comprises (i) removing the placenta, soaking in 75% alcohol, and washing with salt water, (ii) taking some of the chorionic tissue, washing, cleaning the tissue block, mechanically cutting tissue block into homogenate by a sterile medical surgical scissors, (iii) using a cell scraper to spread the tissue block in the culture bottle, adding into a carbon dioxide incubator for dry culture, adding the medium and placing it in a carbon dioxide incubator for cultivation, (iv) allowing to stand for bottle laying, absolutely allowing to stand for seven days, and on the eighth day, observing under a microscope and changing the culture medium in the culture bottle by the cell growth state, (v) carrying out passage under the microscope, when the degree of fusion is reached and (vi) carrying out the microscopic observation and carrying out the cryopreservation and when the harvesting degree is reached.
   USE - The method is useful for rapidly separating and culturing placental mesenchymal stem cells to harvest frozen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Rapidly separating and culturing placental mesenchymal stem cells to harvest frozen comprises (i) removing the placenta, soaking in 75% alcohol, and washing with salt water for 3 times, (ii) taking some of the chorionic tissue, washing into a 50 ml centrifuge tube, cleaning the tissue block, mechanically cutting tissue block into homogenate by a sterile medical surgical scissors, (iii) using a cell scraper to spread the tissue block in the culture bottle, adding into a carbon dioxide incubator for dry culture, adding the medium and placing it in a carbon dioxide incubator for cultivation, (iv)allowing to stand for bottle laying, absolutely allowing to stand for seven days, and on the eighth day, observing under a microscope and changing the culture medium in the culture bottle by the cell growth state, (v) carrying out passage under the microscope, when the degree of fusion is reached and (vi) carrying out the microscopic observation and carrying out the cryopreservation and when the harvesting degree is reached
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the only part of the chorionic epithelial tissue is taken for preparation. In the step (ii), the drying time of the carbon dioxide culture box is 1-2 hours. In the steps (ii) and (iii), the temperature of the carbon dioxide culture box is 37 degrees C and the concentration of carbon dioxide is 4.5%. In the (iv), (v) and (vi) steps, the first observation time was on the 8th day of the bottle and the half is changed, and the cells are observed every 3 days and the cells are exchanged, passaged or frozen according to the number of cell clones. In the step (v), the minimum number of cells required for inoculation of each bottle of T175 bottle after the first passage is 6.7x105. In the step (vi), the minimum number of cells required for cryopreserving cells per 1 mL of the cryotube is 8.4x106.

PN CN109867986-A; WO2020199348-A1; CN109867986-B
TI Nano-calcium carbonate useful as a filler, an additive or a modifier for plastics, rubber, paints, sealants, inks and adhesives prepared using synthetic or natural polymer modifier as crystallization control template and in-situ modifier.
AU YIN Y
   HUANG J
   LIAO C
   YE L
   TU S
   ZHAO Y
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
   UNIV NINGBO (UYNB-C)
   BEIJING INSIGHT CHEM ENG TECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
   UNIV NINGBO (UYNB-C)
   TH-UNIS INSIGHT CO LTD (THUN-Non-standard)
GA 201955511B
AB    NOVELTY - Nano-calcium carbonate prepared using synthetic or natural polymer modifier as crystallization control template and in-situ modifier is claimed. The nano calcium carbonate is calcite type nano calcium carbonate or vaterite type nano calcium carbonate, the nano calcium carbonate is a dried powder or a filtered washing paste, the polymer modifier is a water-soluble polymer modifier or a polymer modifier capable of forming an emulsion, the polymer modifier is a hydrophilic polymer or a lipophilic polymer, and styrene-acrylic emulsion, sodium lignin, pure acrylic emulsion, silicon pure acrylic emulsion, fluorine pure acrylic emulsion, polyvinyl alcohol, polyethylene glycol, urea resin, phenolic resin, lignosulfonate and/or cellulose sulfonate.
   USE - The nano-calcium carbonate is useful as a filler, an additive or a modifier for plastics, rubber, paints, sealants, inks, adhesives, asphalt, paper or complex materials (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method controls crystal growth during the preparation of nano calcium carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing nano-velocite calcium carbonate comprising adding polymer modifier to the calcium glycinate solution, carbon dioxide absorbing solution containing carbon dioxide gas or glycinate is introduced under stirring state until pH drops to 7.5 as the end of the reaction, filtering obtained precipitate, washing, drying to obtain polymer surface-modified vaterite calcium carbonate product having single crystal size of less than or equal to 10 nm, where the polymer modifier is lignin sodium or styrene-acrylic emulsion, the styrene-acrylic emulsion is a styrene-acrylate emulsion, when the polymer modifier is lignin sodium, then the amount of sodium lignin is 5-15% of the theoretical mass of calcium carbonate produced, when the polymer modifier is a styrene-acrylic emulsion, then the amount of styrene-acrylic emulsion is 1-5% of the theoretical mass of calcium carbonate, recycling filtrate, providing part of the filtrate to neutralize dissolved calcium carbide slag or calcium hydroxide to prepare calcium glycinate, and providing other part to absorb carbon dioxide in the flue gas to prepare glycinate carbon dioxide absorbing liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nano calcium carbonate is prepared by a glycine method or a direct carbonization method, the glycine method comprises is obtained by in-situ modification of 5% styrene-acrylic emulsion or 10% sodium lignin sodium, where the average grain size is below 10 nm, the oil absorption value is 80 g/100g or 105 g/100 g, the specific surface is above 20 m2/g, the direct carbonization method uses in-situ modification of 1% styrene-acrylic emulsion or 5% lignin sodium to obtain calcite-type calcium carbonate, the average grain size of calcium carbonate is below 25 nm, the specific surface is above 30 m2/g, the oil absorption value is 190 g/100 g or 113 g/100 g, the modified calcium carbonate improves the processing properties of the complex material, the styrene-acrylic emulsion modified calcium carbonate is more suitable for toughening, and lignin-modified calcium carbonate is more suitable for reinforcement. The concentration of the calcium glycinate solution is 1 mol/l, the mol ratio of glycine to calcium raw material is 2:1, where the calcium is based on calcium hydroxide, the concentration of the sodium glycinate solution is 1 mol/l, and it dissolves at normal temperature. Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (1) neutralizing glycine solution, dissolving by calcium carbide slag or slaked lime, and filtering to obtain calcium glycinate solution, (2) reacting glycine with base to obtain glycine salt solution for absorbing carbon dioxide in the flue gas or directly absorbing carbon dioxide to pH of 7.5 to obtain glycine salt carbon dioxide mixture, (3) adding polymer modifier to the calcium glycinate solution, and stirring uniformly, (4) adding the mixed liquid obtained in the step (2) to the solution obtained in the step (3) or directly synthesizing modified nano calcium carbonate by mixing with flue gas or carbon dioxide gas, filtering to obtain nano-velocite calcium carbonate paste product, and drying to obtain powder product, and (5) recycling filtrate filtered in the step (4), providing part of the filtrate to dissolve calcium carbide slag or slaked lime to obtain purified calcium glycinate solution, and other part of the filtrate to absorb carbon dioxide in the flue gas to obtain a carbon dioxide absorption solution of glycine. The nanocalcium-type calcium carbonate preparation using in-situ modification of a polymer specifically comprises adding polymer modifier to the calcium hydroxide slurry droplets, passing in carbon dioxide or flue gas containing carbon dioxide, mixing carbonization until the pH of the solution drops to 7, subjecting precipitate to suction filtration, washing to obtain polymer-modified calcite-type calcium carbonate paste having average crystal grain size of 20-25 nm, and drying to obtain powdered calcium carbonate, where the polymer modifier is a styrene-acrylic emulsion. The method comprises (1) preparing calcium oxide or calcium hydroxide into calcium hydroxide slurry, stirring and mixing, (2) adding polymer modifier to the calcium hydroxide slurry to obtain polymer-containing calcium hydroxide slurry, when the polymer modifier is a styrene-acrylic emulsion, the solid content of the styrene-acrylic emulsion is 50%, and the amount of the styrene-acrylic emulsion is 1% of the theoretically produced calcium carbonate, (3) passing carbon dioxide gas into the calcium hydroxide slurry containing the polymer modifier, stirring to carry out the carbonization reaction until the pH of the solution drops to 7, subjecting precipitate to suction filtration, washing to obtain modified nano-calcite type calcium carbonate paste, and drying to obtain powder calcium carbonate, where the concentration of calcium hydroxide slurry is 7-10%.

PN CN109647400-A; CN109647400-B
TI Catalyst useful for catalyzing dehydrogenation of 1-butene to produce 1,3-butadiene by carbon dioxide oxidation comprises carrier (activated carbon) and active component (e.g. elemental iron).
AU YAN B
   WANG B
   WANG L
   JIANG T
AE UNIV TIANJIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYTC-C)
GA 201939471P
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises carrier and active component. The preparation of catalyst comprises adding oxidative acid-treated carrier, soluble iron salt, metal-soluble salt of the same period as elemental iron to deionized water, uniformly dispersing, removing deionized water to obtain particle sample, heating under the inert protective gas atmosphere form room temperature of 20-25 degrees C to 500-600 degrees C for thermal insulation roasting, naturally cooling to 20-25 degrees C, and heating at a rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute for 3-8 hours, where the metal soluble salt in the same period as the elemental iron is metal chromium soluble salt and metal vanadium soluble salt, and molar ratio of elemental iron, metallic chromium and metal vanadium is (10-6):1:1. The oxidative acid-treated carrier is prepared by uniformly mixing the carrier and oxidizing acid. The carrier is activated carbon and oxidizing acid is nitric acid.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for catalyzing the dehydrogenation of 1-butene to produce 1,3-butadiene by carbon dioxide oxidation.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: uses activated carbon as catalyst carrier, which has large specific surface area, rich pores, good electrical conductivity and rich surface oxygen-containing groups, and can be effectively dispersed compared with the conventional alumina-supported iron-based catalyst; and utilizes active component iron oxide avoids agglomeration and sintering of the active component, and can also improve the electron transfer ability of catalyst, thus improves the activity, selectivity and stability of the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of catalyst for catalyzing the dehydrogenation of 1-butene to produce 1,3-butadiene by carbon dioxide oxidation comprising carrying out reaction at 500-700 degrees C, preferably 550-600 degrees C under normal pressure, where the mass space velocity based on 1-butene is 1.5-7.5 h-1, used amount of catalyst is 0.1-0.2 g, and molar ratio of the raw material 1-butene to carbon dioxide is 1:(2-15), preferably 1:(6-10).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidative acid-treated carrier, soluble iron salt, metal-soluble salt and alkali metal salt of the same period as elemental iron is added to the deionized water to realize the co-impregnation of metal chromium, metal vanadium, metal iron and alkali metal. The alkali metal is lithium metal or potassium metal. The alkali metal salt is soluble alkali metal salt e.g. lithium nitrate, lithium chloride, potassium nitrate, potassium chloride, elemental iron, metallic chromium, metal vanadium and alkali metal in a molar ratio of (10-6):1:1:(0.5-1). The oxidizing acid is 1-10 mol/l, preferably 3-7 mol/l nitric acid. The mass ratio of activated carbon (g) to the volume of nitric acid (ml) is (1-10):100, preferably (4-8):100. The soluble iron salt is soluble ferric salt e.g. ferric nitrate and ferric chloride. The metal chromium soluble salt is chromium nitrate. The metal vanadium soluble salt is ammonium metavanadate. Preferred Method: The method comprises stirring to achieve uniform mixing and treatment at a speed of 200-350 revolutions/minute for 180-240 minutes, preferably 200-240 minutes to achieve complete reaction, filtering the oxidative acid-treated carrier, washing until to neutrality to obtain particle sample, and drying at 80-100 degrees C. The stirring and dispersing process is carried out at a speed of 180-220 revolutions/minute for 160-220 minutes to achieve uniform dispersion, and removing the solvent water using rotary evaporator at 60-80 degrees C for 40-60 minutes. The inert protective gas is nitrogen, helium or argon. The heating process is carried out at a rate of 3-5 degrees C/minute for 3-5 hours at 550-600 degrees C using tube furnace as roasting device.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN109619649-A; CN109619649-B
TI Method for preparing special high-filled tobacco product sheet, involves drying tobacco raw material and pulverizing into tobacco powder, mixing soot powder with adhesive, followed by cooling and drying mixture under normal pressure.
AU SHU H
   LI G
   WANG H
   LIU Z
   YANG X
   YUE X
AE CHINA TOBACCO HUBEI IND LLC (CNTA-C)
GA 201938012Y
AB    NOVELTY - A special high-filled tobacco product sheet preparing method involves drying a tobacco raw material and pulverizing into a tobacco powder, mixing a certain amount of the soot powder with an adhesive, mixing a certain amount of water with a sweetness leavening agent, an acidity adjuster, a flavor and fragrance into a liquid material, passing a sufficient amount of carbon dioxide and pressurizing, weighing a certain amount of additional fiber, utilizing a disc grinder to beat, the slurry concentration after beating is 10-20%, passing carbon dioxide and pressurizing, weighing a certain amount of atomizing agent, weighing the powder, the liquid material, the external fiber and the atomizing agent, thoroughly mixing in a homogenization reactor with a pressure of 2-3 atmospheres, discharging after the mixture, roll forming, drying, shredding, cooling and drying under normal atmospheric pressure to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing special high-filled tobacco product sheet.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A special high-filled tobacco product sheet preparing method involves drying a tobacco raw material and pulverizing into a tobacco powder, mixing a certain amount of the soot powder with an adhesive, the alkaline foaming agent and the acidic foaming agent in a ratio of 100:0.1-5:0.1-5:0.1-1 by weight to a powder, mixing a certain amount of water with a sweetness leavening agent, an acidity adjuster, a flavor and fragrance in a weight ratio of 100:0.1-5:0.1-1:0.1-1 into a liquid material, passing a sufficient amount of carbon dioxide and pressurizing, weighing a certain amount of additional fiber, utilizing a disc grinder to beat at 15-25 SR, the slurry concentration after beating is 10-20%, passing carbon dioxide and pressurizing, weighing a certain amount of atomizing agent, weighing the powder, the liquid material, the external fiber and the atomizing agent in a weight ratio of 1:1-2:0.1-0.5:, thoroughly mixing in a homogenization reactor with a pressure of 2-3 atmospheres, discharging after the mixture, roll forming, drying, shredding, cooling and drying under normal atmospheric pressure to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The tobacco raw material comprises mixture of tobacco leaves, cut tobacco, tobacco stems and stem stems. The flavor and fragrance comprises mixture of tobacco extract and tobacco flavor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The adhesive comprises mixture of sodium alginate, carboxymethyl cellulose and gelatin. The alkaline foaming agent comprises mixture of potassium hydrogencarbonate, sodium hydrogencarbonate, calcium hydrogencarbonate, potassium carbonate and sodium carbonate. The acidic foaming agent is potassium hydrogen tartrate. The acidity regulator comprises mixture of L(+)-tartaric acid, phosphate, citric acid and sodium salt, potassium salt and sodium lactate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sweet leavening agent comprises mixture of D-mannitol, maltitol and D-sorbitol.

PT P
PN CN109091536-A
TI Preparing Ficus microcarpa extract comprises e.g. pretreatment, primary leaching, secondary leaching, supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, mixing and concentrating.
AU LI Y
   LU H
   LI M
AE NAPO KANGZHENG NATURAL PLANT EXTRACTION (NAPO-Non-standard)
GA 2019025676
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Ficus microcarpa extract comprises e.g. washing Ficus microcarpa, removing impurities, carrying out micro-crushing to powder, placing Ficus microcarpa in absolute ethyl alcohol, extracting for 3-6 hours at 40-50 degrees C, extracting obtained extract liquid by ethyl acetate for second time to obtain oleanolic acid crude extract solution, subjecting the oleanolic acid crude extraction liquid to low pressure silica gel column chromatography, eluting using gradient elution to obtain refined oleanolic acid, secondary leaching, supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, mixing obtained refined oleanolic acid and secondary extract and tertiary extract, adjusting pH value of the mixture to 6-7, evaporating and concentrating the mixture for to obtain the Ficus microcarpa extract.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing Ficus microcarpa extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The extract has high volatile oil, flavonoids, phenolic compounds and organic acids, which greatly improves medicinal value of Ficus microcarpa extract.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing Ficus microcarpa extract comprises (1) washing Ficus microcarpa, removing impurities, carrying out micro-crushing to powder, (2) placing Ficus microcarpa in absolute ethyl alcohol, extracting for 3-6 hours at 40-50 degrees C, extracting obtained extract liquid by ethyl acetate for second time to obtain oleanolic acid crude extract solution, subjecting the oleanolic acid crude extraction liquid to low pressure silica gel column chromatography, eluting using gradient elution to obtain refined oleanolic acid, (3) placing the step (1) obtained leaching residue in acid solvent, leaching at 90-100 degrees C for 4-5 hours to obtain secondary leach residue and leach liquor, where the acid solvent is composed of 5-10 wt.% acetic acid, 5-10 wt.% ethanol, 1-5 wt.% toluene, and water (balance), (4) placing the secondary leach residue in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, adding 20 wt.% ethanol as cosolvent, extracting under 40-50 MPa extraction pressure, 20-35 degrees C extraction temperature, and 20 l/minute carbon dioxide flow rate for 30-40 minutes to obtain tertiary extract, (5) mixing the step (1) obtained refined oleanolic acid and secondary extract and tertiary extract, adjusting pH value of the mixture to 6-7, evaporating and concentrating the mixture for to obtain the Ficus microcarpa extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (5), adjusting the mixture pH value using citric acid, acetic acid and/or monopotassium phosphate. In the step (2), the eluent is petroleum ether and ethyl acetate in weight ratio of 4:1-4. The mesh number of the silica gel column is 300 meshes. The pH value of the acidic solvent is 4-5. In the step (4), the mass ratio of leaching residue and cosolvent is 100:2. Preferred Method: In the step (2), when subjecting oleanolic acid crude extraction solution to low pressure silica gel column chromatography, passing carbon dioxide or nitrogen into the silica gel column so that the pressure in the silica gel column is 0.03-0.05 MPa. In the step (2), when subjecting the oleanolic acid crude extraction solution to low pressure silica gel column chromatography, maintaining the flow rate of the eluent at 1-5 ml/minute.

TI Washing wastewater comprehensive recycling technique by adding detergent to wool and wool washing water, discharging, recycling wool sheep fat, entering washing wastewater into adjusting tank, and lifting and feeding to heat exchanger.
AU YAO H
   TANG K
   JIN W
   YAO X
   WANG D
   XIA Z
   LU G
   YAO Z
   YAO P
AE SUZHOU HONGYU WATER TREATMENT ENG SERVIC (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2018883105
AB    NOVELTY - Washing wastewater comprehensive recycling technique involves sending wool and wool washing water into washing machine, adding detergent, washing machine discharging washing wastewater, recycling wool sheep fat, entering washing wastewater into adjusting tank, and lifting and feeding to heat exchanger for cooling; entering washing wastewater into hydrolysis acidification tank, converting chemical oxygen demand into biochemical oxygen demand by facultative bacteria, entering hydrolysis acidification tank effluent into anaerobic tank, decomposing biochemical oxygen demand into methane and carbon dioxide by anaerobic bacteria, and entering anaerobic tank effluent into aerobic tank; and decomposing organic matter in washing wastewater into carbon dioxide by aerobic bacteria, degrading pollutants in wastewater, discharging sludge into sludge tank, entering aerobic tank effluent into dosing reaction sedimentation tank, removing residual bacteria, and discharging sludge into sludge tank.
   USE - Method for washing wastewater comprehensive recycling technique.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes zero discharge, provides nitrogen phosphorus nutrient to printing and dyeing wastewater, supplements boron, manganese and molybdenum microelement, improves dyeing wastewater treatment effect, and reduces wastewater processing cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Washing wastewater comprehensive recycling technique involves:
   (A) sending wool and wool washing water into washing machine, adding detergent, washing machine discharging washing wastewater, recycling wool sheep fat by degreasing device, entering washing wastewater into adjusting tank, and lifting and feeding to heat exchanger for cooling by lifting pump;
   (B) entering washing wastewater into hydrolysis acidification tank, converting chemical oxygen demand into biochemical oxygen demand through facultative bacteria, entering hydrolysis acidification tank effluent into anaerobic tank, decomposing biochemical oxygen demand into methane and carbon dioxide through anaerobic bacteria, and entering anaerobic tank effluent into aerobic tank;
   (C) decomposing organic matter in washing wastewater into carbon dioxide through aerobic bacteria, degrading pollutants in wastewater, discharging sludge into sludge tank, entering aerobic tank effluent into dosing reaction sedimentation tank, removing residual bacteria, and discharging sludge into sludge tank; and
   (D) sending reaction sedimentation tank effluent into wastewater treatment station through draining pump, incorporating into printing and dyeing wastewater treatment system, treating discharged washing water and printing and dyeing wastewater, and reaching wastewater processing standard.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The washing wastewater has organic matter concentration is chemical oxygen demand of not less than 60 mg/L.

PN US2022242728-A1
TI Producing potable water using system, involves            combining fossil fuel with oxygen in combustion device            to produce flue gas including water vapor and carbon            dioxide, condensing water vapor in flue gas and            producing potable water.
AU THEODORE L
AE THEODORE L (THEO-Individual)
GA 202297364A
AB    NOVELTY - Producing potable water involves combining a                fossil fuel with oxygen in a combustion device to                produce a flue gas including water vapor and carbon                dioxide, condensing the water vapor in the flue                gas, and producing potable water as the condensed                water vapor from the flue gas.
   USE - Method for producing potable water using                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces 2,500 pounds of potable                water from sea water or non-potable ground water,                where water is sufficiently free from pathogens,                restricted chemicals, and salts to comply with any                federal drinking water standards, and the standards                of the State of New York.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for producing potable water, which comprises a fuel                inlet adapted to receive a flow of natural gas and                an air inlet adapted to receive a flow of air,                containing a quantity of oxygen; a natural gas                combustion device having the fuel inlet and a flue,                adapted to receive the natural gas and oxygen,                combust the natural gas and produce a flue gas                comprising carbon dioxide and water vapor out the                flue; a first water vapor condensation device                adapted to condense the water vapor in the flue gas                into a first quantity of potable water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of a system                for producing potable water.
   110Flue gas stream
   120Coolant stream
   130Heated coolant stream
   140Cooled flue gas stream
   150Condensed water
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The combustion device                  is a home natural gas furnace. The water vapor is                  condensed with a heat exchange device. The heat                  exchange device is a multi-stage heat exchanger,                  distillation device or multiple heat exchangers.                  The source of oxygen is humidified with a source                  of non-potable water. The non-potable water is                  sprayed into the flue gas before it is condensed.                  The carbon dioxide and/or nitrogen in the flue                  gas is reduced or removed before the condensation                  step. The pressure of the flue gas is increased                  prior to condensation. The flue gas is cooled to                  below 150&#176;F or 112&#176;F to                  condense the water vapor. Preferred Components:                  The source of oxygen comprises a source of pure                  oxygen. The hydrocarbon fuel is natural gas. The                  combustion device is a home heating unit.

AE HUBEI JIANGCHENG LAB (HUBE-Non-standard)
   UNIV HUBEI (UYUB-C)
GA 2022897043
AB    NOVELTY - Processing niobium oxide gate pipe with                hydrogen peroxide as supercritical fluid of                reactant comprises placing niobium oxide gate pipe                in the reaction chamber, adding 450-500 &#956;l                water and 10-50 &#956;l 25-35 wt.% hydrogen                peroxide into the reaction chamber, introducing                supercritical carbon dioxide fluid into the                reaction chamber, controlling pressure in the                reaction chamber to be 2800-3200 psi, and reacting                at 110-130&#8451; for 1-2 hours.
   USE - Processing niobium oxide gate pipe with                hydrogen peroxide as supercritical fluid of                reactant
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses hydrogen peroxide as reactant                of supercritical fluid processing method of niobium                oxide gating pipe, using supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid technology, using hydrogen peroxides                and as deionized water substance. The method                successfully reduces the OFF state current of the                device, greatly improves the gating ratio, further                integrates and provides the powerful support for                the device. Because the hydrogen peroxygen has                better oxidation than that of the deionization                water, compared with single deionizing water as the                reaction substance, using the peroxide and deionize                water as a reactive substance can better passivate                the material and device inner part and different                material interface of the defect.

   2Conversion layer
   3Top electrode

PN KR2022090257-A
TI Method for monitoring volcanic eruption precursor using e.g. volcanic eruption precursor monitoring apparatus, involves acquiring earth atmosphere observation data from earth atmospheric observation satellite.
AU LEE H
   KIM D W
   SERIN K
   HYUN P
AE UNIV PUKYONG NAT (UYPU-Non-standard)
GA 202286328W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves acquiring earth atmosphere observation data from an earth atmospheric observation satellite. The sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide extracting data are extracted from the obtained data. The ratio of sulfur dioxide concentration to carbon dioxide concentration in an expected volcanic eruption area is calculated using the extracted sulfur dioxide data and the carbon dioxide data. A threshold value for the ratio is set using the statistics calculated for a preset long period of time. Prediction is made whether a volcanic eruption occurs in the expected eruption area using the set threshold value.
   USE - Method for monitoring volcanic eruption precursor using volcanic eruption precursor monitoring apparatus (claimed), processor e.g. central processing unit (CPU) or semiconductor device, hard disks, floppy disks and magnetic tapes, optical media e.g. compact disk read only memories (CD-ROMs) and digital versatile disks (DVDs), magnetic media e.g. floppy disks, magneto-optical media, and hardware devices e.g. read only memory (ROM), random access memory (RAM) and flash memory.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables determining whether the volcanic eruption occurs by detecting a volcanic eruption precursor in an expected volcanic eruption area using atmospheric measurement data from artificial satellites.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a flowchart illustrating a method for monitoring a precursor of a volcanic eruption performed by an apparatus for monitoring a precursor of a volcanic eruption (Drawing includes non-English language text).

AU WANG K

GA 202237333S
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical hardening method for high-hardness low-carbonate-alkalinity coal gasification ash water, involves (a) first adding carbon dioxide to ash water, so that the molar amount of inorganic carbon is 0.8-1.2 times that of calcium mole, (b) performing electrochemical treatment on grey water using diaphragm method, (c) adding sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide to water in an electrolytic cathode of diaphragm for improving alkaline of grey water, and (d) introducing alkaline grey water medium into a flocculation settling tank and performing flocculation sedimentation treatment to reduce the hardness of grey water, where the order of electrochemical treatment of step (b) and alkali-supplementing treatment in step (c) is electrochemical treatment after electrochemical treatment, or electrochemical treatment after alkali treatment.
   USE - The electrochemical hardening method is useful for high-hardness low-carbonate-alkalinity coal gasification ash water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes carbon dioxide discharged from coal chemical industry to add inorganic carbon to high-hardness and low-carbonate alkalinity coal gasification ash water, adopts electrochemical hardening removal method in which diaphragm method electrolysis is main method and alkali supplementation is additive method, improves the effect of electrochemical hard removal, reduces energy consumption, alleviates scaling problem of coal gasification system, and realizes water saving and emission reduction of coal gasification system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for determining content of inorganic carbon in gray water medium after carbon dioxide supplementation involves adjusting the pH value by bubbling carbon dioxide to treatment medium, and using total alkalinity method to test the total inorganic carbon content of medium under each pH value, and preparing relationship curve between pH value and inorganic carbon content. The method for determining content of inorganic carbon in grey water medium after carbon dioxide supplementation involves adding organic scale inhibitors to grey water medium with different carbon dioxide supplement amounts to stabilize water quality, adding organic scale inhibitors to grey water medium to which organic scale inhibitor is added, adding sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide for adjusting pH to 8.45-9.5, measuring methyl orange alkalinity and phenolphthalein alkalinity by hydrochloric acid titration, based on two alkalinity values and the non-carbonate alkalinity value of raw water, calculating inorganic carbon content in medium, and preparing pH value and inorganic carbon and relationship curve of content, based on which carbon dioxide supplementation amount is determined. In step (b) of electrochemical treatment, the pH value of effluent is controlled to 4 by adjusting the grey water flow rate in anode chamber, adjusting the pH value of effluent from cathode chamber by adjusting gray water flow rate of cathode chamber by when the alkali supplementation treatment process is after the electrochemical treatment process, the pH value of cathode effluent is controlled to 8-9.5, and when the alkali supplementation treatment process is before electrochemical treatment process, the pH value of cathode effluent water is controlled to more than 10. The cathode chamber of diaphragm electrolysis processes the grey water medium supplemented with carbon dioxide or grey water supplemented with carbon dioxide and sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide, and the anode chamber processes raw grey water. The cathode effluent of grey water after electrochemical treatment and alkali supplementation treatment flows to catholyte post-treatment buffer tank, comprising 10-60 g/l calcium carbonate scale crystals, and the size of scale crystals is less than 1000 microm, after stirring for 20-120 seconds, flowing to flocculation sedimentation tank, after flocculation and precipitation, removing scale residue, and after softening, uniformly mixing clarified gray water and electrochemically treated anode effluent, and returning to gray water system.Preferred Components: The anode material for diaphragm electrolysis is graphite, lead alloy, noble metal electrode or metal oxide coated electrode, the cathode material is carbon steel, stainless steel, copper alloy, graphite, titanium alloy, lead alloy, precious metal electrode or metal oxide coated electrode, and the membrane material is cation exchange membrane, bipolar membrane, microporous ceramic membrane, microporous plastic membrane, wood fiber membrane or asbestos membrane.

AB    NOVELTY - High-efficiency heat exchange system comprises a raw material processing and reaction system (1) for mixing and pressurizing the raw materials e.g. propane and carbon dioxide, and performing oxidation synthesis and dehydration reaction to obtain crude propylene saturated gas. The crude propylene saturated gas comprises propylene, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, unreacted propane and methanol. A propylene purification system (2) rectifies crude propylene saturated gas, separating propylene and propane, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, and methanol. A methyl nitrite regeneration system (3) recovers methanol in the propylene purification system to generate methyl nitrite, which is used to treat carbon monoxide. A dimethyl oxalate reaction system (4) mixes carbon monoxide and methyl nitrite to generate dimethyl oxalate. A by-product refining system (5) rectifies the products in the reaction system, and simultaneously recovers the dimethyl carbonate product and methanol.
   USE - The system is useful for preparing propylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: efficiently treats the by-products in the process of preparing propylene from propane; realizes the thermal energy balance through the cooperation between the systems; and avoids the energy waste.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the high-efficiency heat exchange system for preparing propylene.

   Propylene purification system (2)

   Dimethyl oxalate reaction system (4)
   By-product refining system (5)

PN CN112239391-A
TI Preparing 1,3-butadiene by dehydrogenating 1-butene by using mixed gas of 1-butene and carbon dioxide as raw material, and contacting with 1-butene dehydrogenation catalyst.
AU MIAO C
   WEI C
   FAN Y
   HUA W
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 2021096757
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing 1,3-butadiene by dehydrogenating 1-butene involves using a mixed gas of 1-butene and carbon dioxide as a raw material, and contacting with a 1-butene dehydrogenation catalyst at a reaction temperature of 550-650 degrees C, where the mass space velocity of 1-butene is 2-10 hours-1, the molar ratio of raw material 1-butene to carbon dioxide is 1:(2-10), and 1,3-butadiene is obtained under the conditions, the carrier of the catalyst is chosen from MCM-41, SBA-15, HMS, MCF or silicalite-1 molecular sieve, preferably Silicalite-1 molecular sieve, and the active component in the catalyst is chosen from indium.
   USE - The method is used for preparing 1,3-butadiene by dehydrogenating 1-butene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the technical problems of the carrier that comprise abundant vacant hydroxyl groups, and the active components can interact with the carrier.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing 1-butene dehydrogenation catalyst comprising (a) pre-treating the carrier after contacting the carrier with hydrogen peroxide, (b) contacting the active component with water at 60-90 degrees C, and contacting the pretreatment carrier of step (a), and controlling the pH of the solution to 7.5-9 to obtain a solid catalyst, and (c) further comprises the steps of filtering, washing, drying, and calcining the solid catalyst obtained in step (b) to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves before the reaction, the catalyst is activated under an argon atmosphere at 550-650 degrees C for 1 to 3 hours, and the molar ratio of the raw material 1-butene to carbon dioxide is 1:(4-8). The catalyst carrier is preferably silicalite-1 molecular sieve. The specific surface area of the Silicalite-1 molecular sieve is 380-420 m2/g, and the total pore volume is 0.49-0.55 cm3/g. The method comprises 0.6-25 %mass, preferably 3.5-15 %mass indium. Preferred Conditions: The drying condition in step (c) is 80-120 degrees C for 10-30 hours. The calcination condition in step (c) is calcination at 600-650 degrees C in air atmosphere for 3-8 hours. Preferred Components: The active component is chosen from indium salt, and the raw material of the indium salt is chosen from indium(III) nitrate hydrate, indium(III) chloride or indium(III) sulfate, preferably indium(III) nitrate hydrate. Preferred Properties: The scanning electron microscope morphology of the catalyst is spherical particles, and the crystal grains of the particles are 70 nm to 3 mu m. The concentration of the hydrogen peroxide solution in step (a) is 2-5%, preferably, after the carrier is contacted with hydrogen peroxide, the steps of soaking, washing with deionized water and drying are further comprises, the soaking time is 12-36 hours, and the drying condition is 80-120 degrees C for 12-36 hours. The hydroxyl content of the pretreated carrier obtained in step (a) is 0.4-0.6 mmol/g. In step (b), aqueous ammonia is used to control the pH value of the solution, where the concentration of aqueous ammonia is 5-7 mol/l, and the control of the pH value is preferably 8-8.5.

PN KR2019099951-A
TI Producing disk shaped catalysts for carbon dioxide methanation, involves adding nickel, titanium dioxide, cerium oxide, and yttria-stabilized zirconia in distilled water followed by stirring for about 1 hour, and evaporating water.
AU CHANGSOONWOONG
   KIM S
   MOON L S
   AN J Y
AE UNIV KYONGGI IND & ACAD COOP FOUND (UKYO-C)
GA 201975762F
AB    NOVELTY - Producing disk shaped catalysts involves adding nickel, titanium dioxide, cerium oxide, and yttria-stabilized zirconia (YSZ) in predetermined amount of distilled water followed by stirring for about 1 hour, and evaporating water from the mixed aqueous solution using an evaporator. After drying for more than 24 hours in a drier at 105 degrees C to dry the remaining moisture to obtain uniform disk shaped particles of a certain size by using a sieve of 40-50 mesh and 31 g of disk shaped particles are put into a disk forming mold for molding the catalyst, and molded at a pressure of 1000 pound per square inch. The resulting product is heat treated in air at 950 degrees C for 2 hours. After fixing the disk catalyst in the reactor, the temperature of the reactor is raised, pretreated with nitrogen for 30 minutes, reduced with hydrogen for 1 hour, and then hydrogen (H2) and carbon dioxide (CO2) (H2:CO2=4:1) is injected to stabilize for at least 30 minutes to obtain desired catalyst.
   USE - Method for producing disk shaped catalysts for carbon dioxide methanation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce disk shaped catalysts which has excellent catalytic activity, thermal and mechanical stability, disk-type catalysts do not require secondary processing and facilitate mass transfer and heat transfer.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing hydrogen and co-producing coke involves (a) carrying out pyrolysis treatment of biomass to obtain volatile components and hot coke, (b) removing the coke particles by gas-solid separation of volatile components, (c) cooling the hot coke and the gas-solid separated fine coke to obtain a cold coke, (d) maintaining the gasification temperature of the volatile components and water vapor at 600-700 degrees C and generating hydrogen-rich gas through gasification of a carbon dioxide absorbent and a catalyst, and (e) purifying and drying the hydrogen-rich gas to obtain a dry hydrogen-rich gas.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide absorbent is a calcium-based carbon dioxide absorbent. The catalyst is nickel-based catalyst. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the carbon dioxide absorbent and the catalyst (1-10):1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Device: The biomass pyrolysis reactor is a spiral pyrolysis reactor in a biomass (A) heater provided outside the pyrolysis reactor. The coke cooler is a screw conveyor with a water-cooled jacket. The volatile gasification reactor is an external heat fixed bed reactor.

PN CN109206295-A
TI Method for synthesizing ammonia to analyze gas and coke oven gas to produce methanol involves converting methane in mixed coke oven gas into carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen and synthesizing to obtain crude methanol.
AU WANG C
   FAN H
   XUE J
   FENG B
AE SHAANXI HEIMAO COKING CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2019080843
AB    NOVELTY - The method for synthesizing ammonia to analyze gas and coke oven gas to produce methanol involves depressurizing methane-enriched coke oven gas produced in synthetic ammonia pressure swing adsorption process until the pressure is less than or equal to 15 kPa, mixing the coke oven gas with methane-rich coke oven gas in volume ratio of (3-6):1, and pressurizing to 2.5 MPa by coke oven gas compressor, transferring to methane steam reformer, sending oxygen obtained in air separation process to methane steam reforming furnace, converting methane in mixed coke oven gas into carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen through methane steam reforming furnace to obtain converted coke oven gas, cooling until temperature is 38-42 degrees C, and pressurizing to 5.5 MPa, transferring to methanol synthesis tower, synthesizing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen in coke oven gas through methanol synthesis tower to obtain crude methanol and hydrogen-rich gas, distilling and purifying the crude methanol.
   USE - Method for synthesizing ammonia to analyze gas and coke oven gas to produce methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables synthesis of ammonia to analyze gas and coke oven gas, improves the methane content in the raw material gas, and effectively increases the production of methanol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system for synthesizing ammonia to analyze gas to coke oven gas to produce methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The liquid ammonia is obtained by sending released hydrogen-rich gas to the ammonia synthesis process. The methanol is produced by sending hydrogen-rich coke oven gas produced in synthetic ammonia pressure swing adsorption process to the ammonia synthesis process. Preferred Composition: The methane-enriched coke oven gas comprises 15 vol.% carbon monoxide, 28 vol.% hydrogen, 40 vol.% methane, 8 vol.% carbon dioxide, 6 vol.% nitrogen and 3 vol.% hydrocarbon. The coke oven gas comprises 10 vol.% carbon monoxide, 60 vol.% hydrogen, 20 vol.% methane, 3 vol.% carbon dioxide, 3 vol.% nitrogen and 4 vol.% hydrocarbon. Preferred Conditions: The pressure of the coke oven gas is less than or equal to 15 kPa.

PT P
PN CN109133018-A
TI Comprehensive utilization of refractory low-grade rubber involves performing high-temperature calcination of low-grade phosphate rock to obtain a pyrolysis products and carbon dioxide gas, and the collected for use.
AU TAN G
   TAN S
AE SHIMEN JINZISHAN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (SHIM-Non-standard)
GA 2019055364
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensive utilization of refractory low-grade rubber involves performing high-temperature calcination of low-grade phosphate rock to obtain pyrolysis products and carbon dioxide gas, and collected for use. The pyrolysis product obtained is uniformly mixed with water, the amount of water is 1-2 times the total weight of the pyrolysis product, fully digested, and then filtered to obtain a primary filtrate and a primary filter residue. The filter residue is repeatedly washed with water. The primary filtrate is collected and carbon dioxide is introduced into the primary filtrate, stirring, first adjusting the pH value to 8-9, and filtering after the pH value is stable.
   USE - Method for comprehensive utilization of refractory low-grade rubber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables comprehensive utilization of refractory low-grade rubber in a cost-effective manner with improved utilization rate of phosphorus resource.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - While filtering, the filter residue is washed with water and the filtrate is not washed until neutral, and the secondary filtrate and the secondary filter residue are obtained, and then the pH of the secondary filtrate is adjusted to be more than or equal to 11.2, and the temperature of the secondary filtrate is 70-80 degrees C. After the pH value is stable, it is filtered. While filtering, the filter residue is washed with water and the filtrate is left unwashed to neutrality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The calcination temperature is 850-1000 degrees C, and the calcination time is 50-65 min. The calcination is divided into two stages, that is, the temperature is first raised to 900-950 degrees C for 15-30 min. , then heated up to 970-1000 degrees C for 35min. The temperature of the pyrolysis product is 80-260 degrees C.

PN CN107653520-A
TI Preparing polyacrylonitrile-based carbon fiber comprises e.g. dissolving polyacrylonitrile in solvent to obtain PAN/solvent spinning solution, obtaining PAN/solvent spinning solution and subjecting for rotary evaporation and spinning.
AU YU M
   QIAO M
   KONG H
   DING X
   WU Y
   YU X
   ZHANG L
   TAO L
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
   SHANGHAI FISHERMAN.NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201811465F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyacrylonitrile-based carbon fiber, comprises (i) dissolving polyacrylonitrile (PAN) in solvent to obtain PAN/solvent spinning solution, placing in sealed reaction kettle, filling carbon dioxide, heating, subjecting for an aromatic cyclization reaction and vacuum extracting to obtain an aromatic circulated PAN/solvent spinning solution (ii) subjecting the aromatic cyclized PAN/solvent spinning solution for rotary evaporation, performing spinning, stretching and temperature-stage carbonization under a nitrogen atmosphere to obtain PAN-based carbon fibers.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polyacrylonitrile-based carbon fiber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves production rate, reduces the energy consumption, increases the carbon yield for the preparation of high performance carbon fiber and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing polyacrylonitrile-based carbon fiber, comprises (i) dissolving polyacrylonitrile (PAN) in solvent to obtain PAN/solvent spinning solution of concentration 0.2-0.4 g/dl, placing in a sealed reaction kettle, filling the carbon dioxide, replacing air, heating, filling carbon dioxide again, subjecting for an aromatic cyclization reaction under supercritical conditions and vacuum extracting to normal pressure to obtain an aromatic circulated PAN/solvent spinning solution (ii) subjecting the 10-13 wt.% of the aromatic cyclized PAN / solvent spinning solution obtained in the step (i) for rotary evaporation, performing spinning and stretching to obtain an aromatic cyclized PAN-based carbon fiber precursor and subjecting for temperature-stage carbonization under a nitrogen atmosphere to obtain PAN-based carbon fibers
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the (i), the PAN is a PAN homopolymer or a PAN copolymer and the molecular weight is 5-100. In the step (i), the solvent is dimethylsulfoxide, dimethylacetamide, sodium thiocyanate solution or zinc chloride solution. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the number of times of filling carbon dioxide is 3-5 times. In the step (i) the parameters of aromatic cyclization reaction is as follows: the reaction pressure is 8-18 MPa, the reaction temperature is 100-350 degrees C, the heating speed is 5-100 degrees C/min, the reaction time is 2-40min; time is 2~4 min. In the step (ii), the spinning is wet spinning or dry-wet spinning. In the step (ii), the stretching is hot stretching in a coagulation bath, hot steam stretching, high-pressure steam stretching or supercritical fluid stretching, where the stretching ratio is 2-50 times. In the step (ii), the stage heating technical parameters of carbonization is as follows: the first stage carbonization temperature is 400-800 degrees C, the heating speed is 300 degrees C/minute, the second stage carbonization temperature is 1000-16000 degrees C, the heating speed is 800 degrees C/minute and the total stage heating carbonizing time is 60-90 seconds.
