AB    NOVELTY - Use of cerium-based metal-organic framework                material for adsorbing and separating carbon                dioxide, is claimed.
   USE - The material is useful for adsorbing and                separating carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material: improves synthesis efficiency of                cerium-metal-organic framework; and reduces energy                consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cerium-based                  metal-organic framework structure material is                  under the condition of 1 atmosphere and                  temperature of 0&#176; C. The amount of                  carbon dioxide adsorption reaches to 1.9                  mmol.g1or more, preferably 2.5 mmol.g1. The                  particle size of the cerium-based metal organic                  framework structure material is 0.5-10 &#956;                  m, and preferably 1-5 &#956; m. Preferred                  Method: The method comprises comparing with                  methane and nitrogen, the adsorption and                  separation factor of carbon dioxide gas by the                  cerium-based metal organic framework structure                  material is above 10. The cerium-based metal                  organic framework structure material is prepared                  by hydrothermal method. The cerium-based metal                  organic framework structure material is prepared                  by (i) dissolving ammonium cerium nitrate in                  water and stirring to obtain liquid A, and                  dissolving terephthalic acid in N,                  N-dimethylformamide and stirring to obtain liquid                  B, (ii) mixing liquid A and liquid B, and then                  slowly adding glacial acetic acid to obtain mixed                  liquid and uniformly stirring, mixing the liquid                  A and the solution B and placing in a ice bath                  and stirring, and then slowly adding glacial                  acetic acid, and(iii) pouring the mixed solution                  into a reaction kettle, placing the reaction                  kettle in a microwave reactor, and reacting the                  reaction product for specific time under specific                  temperature condition to obtain reaction product,                  then washing the reaction product, filtering and                  drying to obtain cerium-based metal-organic                  framework powder. In the step (iii), the reaction                  time is 0.5-6 hours, preferably 1-1.5 hours. In                  the step (iii), the microwave reactor has power                  of 500-800 W. In the step (iii), the reaction                  temperature is 80-140&#176; C. In the step                  (i), the concentration of cerium ammonium nitrate                  in liquid A is 0.5-1 mol.l1. The concentration of                  terephthalic acid in the liquid B is 0.05-0.1                  mol.l1. In the step (ii), the ratio of the amount                  of the cerium ammonium nitrate and the glacial                  acetic acid in the mixed liquid is                  1:5-1:50.

PN CN109020781-A
TI Synthesizing methanol by carbon dioxide resource comprises mixing carbon dioxide and hydrogen, purifying mixed gas, reacting under catalyst including iridium yttrium magnesium zinc copper, separating liquid methanol and water and rectifying.
AU WANG Q
   WANG X
AE NINGBO HI-TECH ZONE QUANBAOSHOU NEW MATE (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2018A5186B
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing methanol by carbon dioxide resource comprises uniformly mixing carbon dioxide and hydrogen supplied by a gas station in a gas mixer, supplying to a gas purification pipe, which purifies the mixed gas and supplies to the reactor, reacting under a catalyst including iridium yttrium magnesium zinc copper at 240-270 degrees C at a gas pressure of 3-7 MPa, gas space velocity of 40000-80000/hour, passing the gas passes through a gas-liquid separator after the reactor is completely reacted, separating liquid methanol and water, rectifying to obtain methanol product. The method uses a high pressure stainless steel fixed bed reactor comprising a raw material gas station, a regulator valve, a flow controller, a gas mixer, a gas purification pipe, a reaction tube and a gas-liquid separator. The raw material gas station is divided into a hydrogen gas station and a carbon dioxide gas station for supplying stable pressure hydrogen and carbon dioxide to the gas mixer in a molar ratio of 3-7:1.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing methanol by carbon dioxide resource.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally friendly and energy saving; and uses catalyst having high catalytic activity and selectivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The iridium yttrium magnesium zinc copper doped catalyst is prepared by introducing 15-20 pts. wt. copper nitrate, 7.5-12.5 pts. wt. zinc acetate, 0.01-0.1 pts. wt. acetylacetone iridium, 3-8 pts. wt. urea, 0.01-0.1 pts. wt. yttrium hexafluoroacetylacetonate, 0.1-0.9 pts. wt. magnesium hexafluoroacetylacetonate 1,2-dimethoxyethane complex, 0.01-0.06 pts. wt. (R)-1,1'-Spirobiindane-7,7'-diol, 1-5 pts. wt. hydrazine dihydrochloride and 15-20 pts. wt. glycine to a pulverizer, high speed crushing for 3-10 minutes, placing the mixed powder into an oven at 110-150 degrees C for 5-10 hours, transferring the mixed powder into a ball mill, ball milling for 1-5 hours, calcining the mixed powder in a muffle furnace at 100-180 degrees C for 3-8 hours under a nitrogen atmosphere, and grinding after cooling to obtain iridium yttrium magnesium zinc copper doped catalyst.
